jobs.name: Supply Chain Modeling & Optimization Specialist
jobs.description: Job Description: Objectives of the Position Digitalization will be part of our DNA at GB. We will change processes that will simplify life within but also outside of GB, sometimes even without our BASF colleagues noticing it. Within GB, we will use digital tools to stop us spending too much time on tasks that hinder us from doing the things that are really needed, such as consulting our service recipients. Digital tools will free up our time to help our internal partners and we will be able to actively shape our division’s digital footprint. GB Strategy is to transform Kuala Lumpur Hub into a digital Hub to support BASF. GBC Supply chain team is looking for an exceptional Supply chain professional to support in development and implementation of new digital tools that will help operations. You will focus on designing and implementing (along with counterparts and solution architects) Control Tower solutions for BASF Overseas delivery services. In addition, you will need to understand the customers' requirements and use cases to map to the Control Tower solution. As part of the digital transformation, you will play a key role in change management and communication to stakeholders. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities (50%) Supply chain Tactical Support •    You will be responsible for providing tactical support for door-to-door supply chain delivery process •    You will monitor exceptions at each supply chain stage to allow timely and informed decisions for effective escalation management •    You will drive supply chain disruption monitoring activities including what-if scenarios and help create recommended actions / options •    You will support operations teams during crisis management events by providing impact analysis and recommending corrective / preventive actions •    You will conduct market analysis to identify market trends, new opportunities, alternate ports, carriers, and modes •    You will improve supply chain resilience and mitigate our customer delivery risk exposure •    You will identify improvement opportunities for supply chain process and recommend best fit solutions or tools (20%) Communication and Change Management. •    You will work closely with operations to identify business requirements and structure them into actionable and meaningful objectives •    You will drive change management and help deployment of new tools and processes •    You will work with carriers and service providers identify and resolve issues impacting service delivery •    You will provide timely communication to relevant stakeholders on disruptions, identify impacts, provide recovery options and estimate expected back to normal (20%) Citizen data science •    You will coordinate analytics and data ETL (Extract, Transform and Loading) activities for all deliveries. •    You will work with data science and digitalization experts to implement statistical and machine learning models to create predictive and prescriptive solutions that provide users with key insights. •    You will build prototype models for proof of concept and paving way for new digitalization tools. (10%) Supply chain operations support •    You will create and run monitoring, exception, performance and KPI reports for delivery and shipment status. •    You will help automate and maintain reporting for operations and performance •    You will Identify and evaluate automation and technology opportunities to drive operational improvements. Education Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply chain, Economics, Industrial Engineering, Chemistry, or related field Working Experience At least 4 years of relevant Supply Chain / Logistics / Transportation Management Technical & Professional Knowledge Knowledge of working with SAP and business reporting Knowledge of working with SQL, Azure, and cloud databases Knowledge of supply chain, logistics and shipping industry Advantage for knowledge of working with business intelligence applications like Tableau, Power BI etc Self-driven and takes initiative to learn and experiment Open minded with diversity and strong communication skills Great analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving abilities Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Account Manager Glysantin DACH (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF As a European distribution organization of the BASF Group, BTC Europe sells highly-refined specialty and fine chemicals to international customers in numerous industries. We capitalize on our proximity to our customers, agility, many years of expertise and the accumulated know-how of the BASF Verbund. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We are looking for a field account manager for the DACH sales region to start as soon as possible, with a 100% remote work arrangement. A company car will be provided. In your new position, you manage and grow customer relationships in the Automotive Fluids Independent Aftermarket, focusing on coolant/antifreeze and brake fluid products. Your expertise is asked for when negotiating contracts and steering technical and commercial projects with customers and internal teams. Together with your colleagues, you actively acquire new customers and business opportunities, driving cross-selling and price management initiatives. Not least, you represent BTC at industry events and fairs, ensuring visibility and collaboration with BASF counterparts. Furthermore, you contribute to digitalization initiatives and support the development of industry-specific strategies within local and European teams. WHAT YOU OFFER technical professional training in a relevant area or completed master’s degree in business or STEM fields several years of professional experience in automotive fluids technologies and aftermarket sales strong knowledge in contract negotiations, account planning, and customer relationship management, ideally with Salesforce experience fluent German and advanced English skills, plus confident use of IT tools and MS Office applications proactive team player with excellent communication skills, intercultural competence, and a growth-driven mindset willingness to travel WHAT WE OFFER 100% home office company car inspiring networks for developing and implementing your own ideas (e.g., Women in Business) mentoring programs and learning opportunities for your professional and personal development career paths for professional growth, including opportunities to move into other roles within the company 30 vacation days, plus options for special leave and sabbatical models flexible working time arrangements (e.g., part-time employment) an attractive salary, based on business success and your individual performance bicycle leasing options HOW TO REACH US Svenja Sollmann (Talent Acquisition), svenja.sollmann@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59355 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online (technical questions cannot be answered in this appointment) You can also reach our recruiting team here ABOUT US BTC Europe GmbH is a wholly owned subsidiary of BASF. Learn more about BTC Europe GmbH at on.basf.com/BTCEurope/EN Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. And by the way, we leave it up to our employees to decide if and when they address each other informally.

jobs.name: EHS Specialist III
jobs.description: Now hiring! EHS Specialist III Malcom, IA (On Site) We are looking for an EHS Specialist III to join our Performance Materials team in Malcom. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction.These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. Your primary responsibilities will include ensuring compliance with all federal, state, and local regulations related to environmental protection, hazardous waste management, and spill prevention, as well as adhering to BASF's internal standards, such as Responsible Care®. You will play a crucial role in enhancing our site programs to maintain our license to operate while fostering a culture of safety and compliance among all personnel.  In addition, you will lead our site security initiatives, identifying and mitigating security risks to uphold and optimize our security standards. As a key member of the site leadership team, you will collaborate closely with the site manager and contribute to the overall success of the Malcom site.  You will also be an integral part of the BASF EHS community, working alongside EHS experts across the organization and reporting directly to the EHS Director for Performance Materials North America. As an EHS Specialist III, you create chemistry by... Leveraging your effective communication skills to develop strong working relationships with employees, the EHS community, and regulatory agencies/third parties, serving as a positive change agent within the organization. Utilizing your knowledge of Occupational Safety, Industrial Hygiene, and Environmental disciplines to develop and maintain EHS programs, policies, and procedures for the site. Drawing upon your experience in Occupational Safety, Industrial Hygiene, Environmental management, and Emergency Response program management in a manufacturing environment to lead and participate in risk assessments, accurately addressing identified deficiencies. Providing guidance - both tactically and strategically- to site leadership on critical EHS issues, ensuring adherence to corporate EHS policies and procedures, including the Responsible Care® Management Systems. Creating training content and, where appropriate, delivering training or coaching, facilitating the sharing of programs, procedures, tools, and best practices across the BASF EHS organization. Prioritizing opportunities and issues to drive the continuous improvement of EHS processes and systems. Utilizing your strong organizational skills to identify and monitor key performance indicators within your area of responsibility, making necessary adjustments to improve EHS performance. Leading and participating in project reviews, process hazard analyses, and change management processes, ensuring all internal and external reporting requirements are met through your solid analytical and computer skills. Being a role model and collaborating with hourly employees to reinforce a culture of behavior-based safety and support emergency response activities. Working with and traveling to other sites to evaluate audit readiness and share best practices Leading and/or participating in EHS work teams and/or task forces If you have... A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Science, or a related field. Two (2) years of experience in an industrial/manufacturing environment Ability to work independently under broad goals and objectives with minimal guidance. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Experience with Environmental Reporting and Safety Management Systems strongly preferred Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Production Executive
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: Objectives of the Position: As a designated Shift Supervisor, takes responsibility of Safety, Production, Quality of System House plant in the shifts. Main Tasks: Coordinate and manage production in shift with consistent Quality and Safety as per the target. Manage the manpower planning in the shift. (Employee and Contractor) Continuous improvement of processes in coordination with the Plant Manager and his Deputy & Quality control to reduce batch cycle times, increase in yields, increase operational ease and ultimately leading to cost effective production. Whenever required, Coordinate with Supply chain and logistics team for adherence of production schedule and share the information to the team of change if any. Guiding a group of Operators in the plant continuously to ensure consistent product quality and safety. Implement Safety, Health and Environment system of the plant concerning man and equipment. Update knowledge and share the information of any change through proper channel. Ensure that discharge parameters of all the emissions in the plant are within the statutory limits and health and hygiene of the plant personnel is well maintained in the shifts. Ensuring proper implementation of norms as per the departmental operating procedures of ISO 9001, ISO 14001 & ISO 50001 Energy Management system. Guide shop floor personnel towards SHE (Safety, Health and Environment) /(RC)Responsible care /cleanliness of the plant (5S) /discipline for improving the BASF’s safe work culture in the shifts. This ensures compliance of BASF’s statutory requirements, which also includes the training of the subordinates regarding the safety aspects and handling of the critical hazardous chemicals. Ensuring compliance to TDS requirements while performing activities like tanker loading, Tanker unloading, labelling and packing as and when required. Adherence to the PPE usage by self and plant personnel, ensure work permit system is properly followed in the shifts. Adherence to target agreement and perform additional responsibilities as per given timeline Prepare and share production reports to higher management. Co-ordinate with site functions like Warehouse, Engineering , WWTP, Admin and Quality team. Compliance to process safety and environmental protection code as per BASF Practice. Ensure the documentation of Responsible care requirement on continual basis. Act as EHS representative in the shifts. As a Part of ERT(Emergency Response Team), rush to the incident spot in case of emergency in the site. For emergency in System House plant, take a lead and coordinate with incident controller. Requirements: Bachelor of Science/ Diploma 4 - 5 years of experience in the relevant industry. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Chemical Process and relevant experience. Job Description Footer:

Über 900 engagierte Mitarbeitende arbeiten hier täglich daran, Technik zu verstehen, Prozesse effizient zu gestalten und gemeinsam mit starken Partnern neue Maßstäbe zu setzen. Als Standortbetreibergesellschaft legen wir großen Wert auf kundenorientierte Services und maßgeschneiderte Lösungen – mit dem Ziel, nicht nur den Erfolg unserer Kunden zu sichern, sondern auch Ihre persönliche Weiterentwicklung aktiv zu fördern. Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – werden Sie Teil unseres Teams! WAS SIE ERWARTET Unser interdisziplinäres Team Automation & Digitalization Excellence treibt die digitale Transformation an einem unserer größten Produktionsstandorte voran. Wir implementieren innovative digitale Lösungen, verbessern Prozesse und schaffen so einen erlebbaren Mehrwert für unsere Kunden. Mit Ihnen gehen wir voran: In Ihrer neuen Position gestalten Sie Digitalisierungsprojekte und entwickeln innovative Lösungen, z. B. mit Fokus auf App-Entwicklung, Data Analytics, Robotics oder Virtual Reality. Sie setzen Ihren Schwerpunkt: Sie analysieren entweder verfahrenstechnische Fragestellungen mithilfe datengetriebener und modellbasierter Methoden oder fokussieren sich auf die Entwicklung skalierbarer Applikationen und digitaler Lösungen bis hin zur produktiven Integration moderner Data-Analytics- und KI-Technologien. Sie haben ein Gespür für neue Trends: Insbesondere im Bereich KI und Advanced Analytics verfolgen Sie kontinuierlich technologische und marktseitige Entwicklungen, bewerten deren industriellen Mehrwert und leiten gemeinsam mit Experten und Expertinnen konkrete Projekt- und Anwendungsfälle ab. Dabei überzeugen Sie durch fachliches Know-how, klare Kommunikation und treiben Themen aktiv voran. Alles im Blick: Sie analysieren Geschäfts- und Betriebsprozesse, identifizieren gezielt Digitalisierungspotenziale und überführen diese in maßgeschneiderte, praxisnahe Lösungen – sowohl eigenständig als auch in interdisziplinären Teams. Gemeinsam mehr erreichen: Durch Ihre Neugier und Leidenschaft für Digitalisierung sowie Ihr starkes Netzwerk übernehmen Sie eine zentrale Rolle bei der Mitarbeit und Leitung von standortweiten und -übergreifenden Projekten. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Sie bringen nicht alles mit? Keine Sorge, wir suchen nach Persönlichkeiten, die etwas bewegen und unser gemeinsames Arbeitsumfeld gestalten möchten! abgeschlossenes Studium der Natur-, Ingenieur- oder Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Informatik oder einer vergleichbaren Studienrichtung vertiefte Kenntnisse in Data Analytics und Machine Learning sowie praktische Anwendung von Künstlicher Intelligenz und agentischen Systemen, idealerweise im industriellen oder prozessnahen Umfeld Erfahrung in der Konzeption, Entwicklung und Implementierung von KI-/ML-Lösungen in IT-Umgebungen von Unternehmen Verständnis dafür, komplexe KI-Modelle und Ergebnisse aufzubereiten und zwischen Fachbereich, Engineering und IT zu vermitteln fundierte Kenntnisse in Python oder einer vergleichbaren Programmiersprache idealerweise Erfahrungen sowie Kenntnisse in der (chemischen) Prozessindustrie und im agilen Projektmanagement sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse empathische Persönlichkeit mit einer selbstständigen und zuverlässigen Arbeitsweise sowie einer ausgeprägten Ergebnisorientierung WAS WIR BIETEN Sie sind vom ersten Tag an Teil unseres Teams, in dem MITEINANDER groß geschrieben wird und wir Herausforderungen gemeinsam meistern. Leben Sie Kreativität: Wir fördern außergewöhnliche Ideen und geben Ihnen Raum zu wachsen. Sie erhalten ein attraktives Vergütungspaket (u.a. marktgerechtes Grundgehalt, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Unfallversicherung, 30 Tage Urlaub, Möglichkeit zusätzlicher freier Tage). Ergänzend dazu erwartet Sie ein vom Unternehmensergebnis und von der individuellen Leistung abhängiger Bonus sowie die Möglichkeit zur Unternehmensbeteiligung durch den Erwerb von Aktien. Eine optimale Work-Life-Balance ist durch flexible Arbeitszeiten und anteiligem mobilen Arbeiten möglich. Zusätzlich bieten wir moderne Arbeitsplätze in Co-Working Atmosphäre in Dresden. Sie erhalten Unterstützung bei Ihrer persönlichen Entwicklung entsprechend der Lebensphase in einem kreativen und internationalen Team. Wir halten Sie fit durch umfangreiche Gesundheitsprogramme, die Möglichkeit zur Nutzung eines Jobrades sowie unseren Werksärztlichen Dienst vor Ort. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Nina Schülzke (Talent Acquisition), nina.schuelzke@basf.com , Tel. +49 30 2005-58790 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . Nutzen Sie die Chance und gestalten Sie mit uns zukunftsorientierte Lösungen. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! WAS SIE ERWARTET Die Ziele und Aufgaben der Rechtsabteilung sind die Wahrung, der Schutz und die Durchsetzung der rechtlichen Interessen der BASF Polyurethanes GmbH in Lemförde. jobs.name: Account Representative II - Georgia - Automotive Refinish
jobs.description:
Now hiring! Account Representative II - Georgia - Automotive Refinish Georgia (Remote) **Field role / Company car provided If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. As an Account Representative based in a major city in Georgia with the ability to travel 60% of the time, you will work with automotive collision repair shops and distribution partners to grow our market share. We are looking for an Account Representative to join our Automotive Refinish team in Georgia. As an Account Representative II - Georgia - Automotive Refinish, you create chemistry by... Executing the business strategy and driving sales growth in the local markets through identifying customers and market needs within your assigned market responsibility. Increasing sales growth by developing market potential through forecasting, lead generation, qualification, closing sales and recommending new products and services (70% of the role will be focused on adding new business). Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. Establishing trust and value in our ever-changing industry. Retaining business and driving sales growth in with a home base within a major Georgia city. If you... Have a Bachelor’s Degree (preferred).  HS Diploma/GED is acceptable with relevant work experience Are experienced in field sales, territory management, and customer relations Have experience in the collision industry (preferred), strong interpersonal skills, robust relationship-building and value-based selling skills Are a solid professional who will value our current customer base while also being aggressive to win new Surventis business Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . BASF Group operates this platform solely as a technical service provider under a 
 Transitional Service Agreement following the divestment of the Coatings division, 
 effective July 1st 2026, now Surventis. The employer and contracting party for the advertised positions is exclusively . alone is responsible for the content of the job adverts and the 
 decision regarding applications. Data Protection The processing of your personal data is carried out by as the controller 
 within the meaning of any applicable data privacy laws. BASF Group acts as a technical service provider in this respect and processes 
 data exclusively on behalf of . Further information can be found in the privacy policy coatings privacy policy incl. Contact details .

jobs.name: Lab Technician I
jobs.description: Now hiring! Lab Technician I Savannah, GA. We are looking for a Lab Technician o join our Catalyst team in Savannah, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops andproduces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recyclingand related products and services. As a Lab Technician for our Catalyst business unit, you will start a meaningful career and you will be responsible to Conduct quality control testing on products manufactured by the BASF Savannah site facility in accordance with documented analytical test procedures as directed by the Quality Supervisor. The Savannah plant in particular manufactures and operates the following products: fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) catalysts, co-catalysts and additives used by petroleum refiners to convert crude oil into gasoline, other transportation fuels, heating oil, and petrochemical feed stocks. During your 12-hour rotating shift as a Lab Technician I, you will Set up, calibrate, and operate laboratory instrumentation such as analytical balances, pH and ion meters, microwave moisture analysis ovens, microwave digestion ovens, surface area analysis, atomic absorption, X-ray fluorescence and diffraction and particle size analysis. Operate computer-controlled instrumentation and be able to store and extract data. Prepare solutions, standards, and buffers according to standard formulas used in analytical analysis. Prepare quality control production samples such as microspheres, zeolites, FCC catalysts and alumina. If you... Have a High School Diploma or GED with 2-3 years of laboratory experience. Have an Associate´s Degree/Bachelor ’ s degree in chemistry/biology or related field or any higher education: No experience required. Are familiar with Laboratory Analysis and math calculations. Are willing and able to work 12-hour shifts, rotating from day to night shifts. Can lift up to 50 lbs, carrying up to 25 lbs. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Damit sichern wir die Grundversorgung der Unternehmensbereiche, die dadurch erfolgreich am Markt agieren können. Zusätzlich beraten wir die Betriebe in allen Belangen rund um Umweltschutz, Arbeits- sowie Anlagensicherheit, Gesundheitsschutz und Genehmigungsrecht. WAS SIE ERWARTET Unsere Einheit Montagekoordination Seilzugangs‑ und Positionierungstechnik koordiniert standortweit externe Leistungen in der Seilzugangstechnik und organisiert gleichzeitig Aufträge, die durch BASF‑eigene Höhenarbeiter:innen ausgeführt werden. Ein vielseitiges und spannendes Aufgabenfeld erwartet Sie, in dem Sie die Seilzugangs- und Positionierungstechnik nach der Betriebssicherheitsverordnung sowie den technischen Regeln für Betriebssicherheit verantwortungsvoll koordinieren. Sie führen Aufsicht bei Einsätzen, sichern die fachgerechte Anwendung der Zugangstechniken ab und erstellen Gefährdungsbeurteilungen, Betriebsanweisungen sowie notwendige Schutzmaßnahmen. Die operative Personalverantwortung, welche die Aus- und Weiterbildung sowie die Unterweisung von Mitarbeitenden im Level 1 & 2 und die Personaleinsatzplanung umfasst, zählt ebenfalls zu Ihren Aufgaben. Zudem entwickeln Sie Zugangs- und Rettungskonzepte nach den FISAT-Sicherheits- und Arbeitsrichtlinien für Seilzugangs- und Positionierungstechnik (FISAT-FSR 15.3) und sorgen für deren Umsetzung inklusive Unterweisung vor Ort. Ein weiterer Schwerpunkt Ihres Arbeitsalltags ist die wirtschaftliche und qualitätsgerechte Abwicklung von Aufträgen, die Koordination von Fremdfirmen sowie die Abstimmung mit anderen Gewerken und die Überwachung von Baustelleneinsätzen. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene technische Berufsausbildung erfolgreich absolvierte Weiterbildung zum/zur Höhenarbeiter:in mit FISAT-Zertifizierung Level 3 oder Level 2 mit entsprechender Seilstundenerfahrung und der Bereitschaft, sich zum Level 3 weiterzuqualifizieren mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im industriellen Umfeld Kenntnisse im Sicherheits-, Gesundheits- und Umweltschutz Führungskompetenzen sowie selbständige und verantwortungsbewusste Arbeitsweise WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktive Vergütung nach Chemietarifvertrag sowie eine variable Erfolgsbeteiligung Betriebliche Altersversorgung, firmenfinanzierte Pflegezusatzversicherung, Aktienprogramm für Mitarbeitende sowie Wertkonto Tariflicher Zukunftsbetrag (z.B. zur Finanzierung eines Jobrads oder zusätzlicher freier Tage) Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung (z.B. betriebliche Kinderkrippe, Ferienprogramme) Fitness – und Gesundheitsstudio, Medical Center mit zahlreichen Angeboten für Ihre Gesundheit Inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen (z.B. Women in Business) Mentoringprogramme und Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Karrierewege als fachliche Weiterentwicklung auf andere Stellen im Unternehmen (auch Jobrotation) Deutschlandticket als vergünstigtes Jobticket, Jobrad, Verfügbarkeit von E-Ladesäulen für Privatfahrzeuge Diese Funktion kann sowohl in Teilzeit als auch in Vollzeit ausgeübt werden. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Katja Emich (Talent Acquisition), katja.emich@basf.com, Tel.: +49 30 2005-58810 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Führungskraft der Position: Dr. Susanne Schlegel (Montagezusatzgewerke - Gerüstbau und Seilzugangstechnik), Email: susanne-maria.schlegel@basf.com Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF SE mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größtes arbeitgebendes Unternehmen der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studierende, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Bis zum 1. Juli gehörte unser Geschäft zur BASF. Im Rahmen der aktuellen Übergangsphase werden alle Stellenausschreibungen und der Bewerbungsprozess weiterhin auf der BASF-Plattform gehostet und daher vollständig im BASF-Branding angezeigt. Bitte lassen Sie sich davon nicht beirren: Alle Stellen sind Positionen bei Surventis und Ihre Bewerbung wird an uns übermittelt. Bei Surventis stellen wir die Kundinnen und Kunden in den Mittelpunkt, denken einen Schritt voraus und entwickeln Lösungen, die echten Mehrwert und nachhaltige Partnerschaften schaffen - we ge beyond the surface. Wir inspirieren einander, weiterzudenken, Verantwortung für unsere Arbeit zu übernehmen und ständig danach zu streben, das Mögliche zu erweitern. Wenn Sie Ihre ganze Persönlichkeit einbringen, das Offensichtliche hinterfragen und mit einem Team wachsen möchten, das es ernst meint – dann sind Sie hier richtig. WAS SIE ERWARTET In der Einheit Anwendungstechnik begleiten wir unsere Kundenlabore bei der Entwicklung und Einführung neuer innovativer Wasserbasislacksysteme für die Automobilindustrie. Sie arbeiten selbständig in einem Team von anwendungstechnischen Lackierer:innen für einen Kundenbereich. Dabei lernen sie verschiedene Applikationsprozesse unserer Kunden kennen und prüfen sich in der Entwicklung befindliche Lacke auf ihre Applikationseigenschaften mittels Hand-, Automaten- sowie Roboterapplikation. Ihre Lackierungen bewerten sie anschließend visuell wie auch messtechnisch und dokumentieren die Ergebnisse. Im engen Austausch mit den Entwicklungslaboren optimieren sie nicht nur etablierte Produkte, sondern entwickeln bestehende Prüfmethoden kontinuierlich weiter. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum/zur Fahrzeuglackierer:in oder Verfahrensmechaniker:in im Bereich Beschichtungstechnik; Berufserfahrungen und/oder Weiterbildung von Vorteil Berufserfahrung als Handlackierer:in oder in der Bedienung von industriellen Lackbeschichtungsanlagen ausgeprägtes technisches Verständnis verbunden mit sicheren Softwarekenntnissen Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit und ggf. Reisetätigkeiten im Rahmen von Kundenbesuchen WAS WIR BIETEN Wir bieten ein attraktives Tarifgehalt mit Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld sowie lukrative individuelle Benefits wie leistungsabhängige Erfolgsvergütungen und die Möglichkeit eine Zusatzzahlung in Freizeit umzuwandeln. Mit einer tariflichen Pflegezusatzversicherung bieten wir eine zusätzliche Absicherung. An unserem Standort in Würzburg gibt es ein Betriebsrestaurant. Außerdem sorgen eine Kooperation mit dem good fit Fitnessclub sowie verschiedene weitere Angebote für die Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Privatleben. Wir haben 30 Urlaubstage/Jahr (bei einer 5-Tage-Woche), zusätzliche Freizeitoptionen und eine 37,5 -Stunden-Woche. Mitarbeitende ab dem 57. Lebensjahr erhalten 2,5 Stunden/ Woche zusätzliche Freizeit, für Mitarbeitende im Schichtbetrieb gilt dieser Anspruch ab dem 55. Lebensjahr. Für die fachliche sowie persönliche Weiterentwicklung bieten wir vielfältige lokale und globale Entwicklungs- und Karrieremöglichkeiten. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Design, develop, and maintain robust, scalable backend services that support and enhance our internal and external applications. Work in an agile, self‑organized team and collaborate effectively with colleagues. Develop new backend functionality using Java and Spring Boot, following modern backend engineering principles and best practices. Maintain and improve existing backend services, ensuring performance, reliability, and long‑term maintainability. Design, implement, and evolve RESTful APIs and service interfaces to enable seamless integration with frontend applications and other systems. Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code in line with team coding standards and industry best practices. Contribute actively to planning, refinement, reviews, operations, and continuous improvement activities within the team. Collaborate closely with frontend developers to ensure smooth data flows, clear API contracts, and consistent end‑to‑end behavior. Implement and maintain unit, integration, and component tests to ensure high quality, stability, and regression prevention. Share responsibility for operations and maintenance as part of the team’s DevOps mindset, including monitoring, troubleshooting, and continuous optimization. (Nice to have) Show interest in frontend technologies such as Angular and full‑stack development, with opportunities to collaborate across the stack if desired. WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or comparable practical experience. Around 3-5 years of professional experience with Java (≥ 21) and building backend applications in production environments Strong hands‑on experience with Spring Boot (3.x) and core Spring modules such as Spring Data, Spring Integration, Spring Batch, and Spring Security Solid understanding of persistence technologies, including JPA 2 / Hibernate, and designing robust data access layers. Experience designing, implementing, and maintaining RESTful APIs for microservice‑based systems. Good knowledge of SQL databases, ideally PostgreSQL, including data modeling and query optimization. Experience writing unit and integration tests (e.g., JUnit or similar frameworks) to ensure code quality and stability. Familiarity with microservice and cloud architectures, including service communication and API‑based integration. Experience working with containerization technologies such as Docker; exposure to Kubernetes is a plus. Proficiency with JetBrains IntelliJ IDEA or comparable Java IDEs. Familiarity with agile methodologies (Scrum or Kanban) and working in agile development teams. Strong communication skills in Spanish and English, both verbal and written. WHAT WE OFFER Continuous training and professional development with our internal vacancies. Safe environment where we promote your health. Restaurant ticket. Competitive economic offer with a base salary plus a variable for good performance. Hybrid model of work and adapt your schedule according to your circumstances. Connect outside the office and practice sports with colleagues through company groups. Flexible remuneration that you can voluntarily allocate to transportation services, training, childcare, etc. Pension plans, life and accident insurance, personal loans and participation in company stock programs. Financial aid for schooling and other measures for families. HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE PROCESS Send your CV in English through the job offer so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, we will call you from the Spanish Talent Acquisition team in Germany (prefix +49) and support you throughout the process. With each step, you will be closer to meeting your future team! Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: DBP Enterprise Architect (m/f/d)
jobs.description: ABOUT US At BASF Digital Hub Madrid we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and strongly encourage applications from individuals with disabilities. We value diversity and believe that varied perspectives enhance our team's effectiveness. Our hiring practices are designed to ensure equal opportunity for all candidates. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The DBP Architect is responsible for defining and operationalizing architecture blueprints for Digital Business Platforms (DBPs) R&D&R (Research & Development & Regulatory) within a specific functional area or technology domain. This role ensures alignment with Global Process Owners (GPOs), Digital Offices, and Enterprise Architecture (EA) governance while driving harmonization, innovation, and integration across business and IT landscapes. As a DBP Architect, you will be part of the R&D&R Digital team and report into the DBP Business Lead. You will be part of an enthusiastic team, consisting of IT Solutions Architects, Product and Portfolio teams. In addition, you will also be part of the R&D Digital Leadership Team to ensure full alignment across our matrix. RESPONSIBILITIES Architecture & Design Define DBP-specific domain architecture blueprints for functional areas or technologies in close alignment with R&D&R team including GPOs. Design and document domain architecture solutions, ensuring integration of business processes, value streams, and information flows across DBPs. Maintain an integrated view of business architecture (processes, value streams) within the domain. Act as functional design authority within DBPs, owning business capability mapping aligned with GPOs and Digital Offices. Ensure DBP solutions fit within the broader enterprise architecture and co-create foundational guidance with IT Enterprise Architects. Conduct thorough analyses of current business processes and systems to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities using value stream mapping. Governance & Compliance Ensure adherence to standards, frameworks, tooling, and processes in DBP specific projects. Operationalize architecture principles, standards, and blueprints within the domain. Conduct monthly and quarterly reporting on EA principles, standards, guidelines, and DBP-specific KPIs, including deviations. Performance & Metrics Manage technical performance using KPIs such as Technical Debt, Lifecycle Management, Product and Platform Roadmaps, and EA KPIs. Establish metrics and KPIs to monitor DBP and EA effectiveness and recommend improvements. Identify risks and create mitigation plans leveraging EA frameworks (e.g., TIME analysis, Technical Debt Score). Collaboration & Leadership Work closely with GPOs, DBP leads, and stakeholders across departments to gather requirements, ensure alignment, and drive technology harmonization and innovation. Collaborate with senior management to define strategic goals and objectives. Lead a team of IT Solution Architects to achieve DBP architecture goals while enforcing EA guidelines and standards. Serve as the primary liaison for domain-specific architectural concerns. Develop and implement change management plans to ensure smooth transitions during business transformations. REQUIREMENTS OF THE POSITION You have a relevant university degree in the field of Information Technology. You have at least 10 years of experience in IT or (business) related discipline. R&D Experience preferred. You have experience in leading global cross-cultural and multidisciplinary teams and the associated change management. You are familiar with modern technologies and trends. You are certified in architecture frameworks and modeling languages, TOGAF and/or ArchiMate. Advanced level of English You are familiar with standard business process frameworks, such as APQC, SCOR, Agile or ITIL To be successful in our dynamic, changing environment in which you have the ability to grow with the company’s aspirations, you must be able to operate with a high degree of freedom and independence in working. You have a communicative, innovative and customer-oriented personality and good problem-solving and analytical skills. WHAT WE OFFER A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 23 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) 2 cultural days A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. HOW TO REACH US Do you have any questions about the application process or the position? Please reach out to Felipe Bianco (felipe.bianco@partners.basf.com)

BASF es la primera empresa química del mundo #WeCreateChemistry y uno de los mejores lugares para trabajar y desarrollar tu carrera. Estamos en búsqueda de talento diverso, apasionado y creativo que desee hacer sus prácticas con nosotros/as, aprender de la mano de tutores/as experimentados/as y marcar la diferencia. BASF Española S.L. comercializa más de 5.000 productos para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros clientes y de la sociedad en una gran variedad de sectores. La industria química está cambiando; y BASF también. Descubre nuestra estrategia: #WinningWays . ¿Quieres poner en práctica tus conocimientos y crear química con nuestro equipo? LO QUE HARÁS Durante tus prácticas en Supply Chain tendrás una visión integral del funcionamiento de la cadena de suministro en la unidad de negocio Dispersiones y Resinas, a través de las siguientes experiencias y aprendizajes: Conocerás las distintas áreas clave de la Supply Chain, como Customer Service, planificación de producción, gestión de materias primas y embalajes, transporte y logística (carretera, tren y marítimo) y gestión de no conformidades. Posteriormente, desarrollarás una herramienta (en Power BI), con el acompañamiento del equipo, para recopilar, visualizar y analizar los KPIs necesarios para gestionar la Supply Chain. Contribuirás a la mejora de procesos y adquirirás experiencia práctica en monitorización del rendimiento y toma de decisiones basada en datos. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Estar cursando estudios en Administración de Empresas, Economía o Ingeniería Posibilidad de firmar convenio de prácticas con Universidad / centro de estudios Conocimientos de Excel y Power BI (deseable) Inglés nivel B2 LO QUE OFRECEMOS Nuestro programa de prácticas te ofrece el perfecto inicio en el mundo de BASF. Desde el comienzo acompañaremos tu aprendizaje. Además, contarás con: Tutoría individualizada: con una persona tutora a tu lado para apoyar tu desarrollo profesional, resolver dudas y darte feedback continuo. Proyectos reales: participarás desde el primer día en proyectos y actividades que impactan al negocio y a nuestros clientes. Prácticas remuneradas: contarás con un apoyo económico mensual proporcional a tu dedicación de tiempo durante tu estancia. Inclusión: queremos que seas uno/a más del equipo, y también podrás participar en eventos de empresa durante este periodo. Formación continua: además del aprendizaje diario, podrás realizar interesantes cursos para aprovechar al máximo tu tiempo con nuestro equipo. Flexibilidad: adaptaremos los horarios de las prácticas a tu carga lectiva para que puedas compaginarlas de la mejor manera. Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, contacta con nuestro/a compañero/a: Andrea López (andrea.lopez-gutierrez@basf.com*) *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Master Data is the backbone of any digital enterprise — getting it right is essential to enable automation, enhance decision-making, and deliver seamless customer experiences. As we continue to industrialize our data landscape, we are looking for passionate individuals who want to make a difference by ensuring that our MDM systems are reliable, scalable, and aligned with our global data strategy. Join us in building a connected data enterprise where high-quality master data powers innovation, compliance, and business growth across the agricultural value chain. RESPONSIBILITIES Collaborate with product stakeholders to understand business needs, transform them into technical specifications, and estimate resources and timelines. Develop and maintain features within the Stibo STEP platform, including customization of workflows, business rules (JavaScript), WebUI, and data model configurations. Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code while participating in code reviews to ensure adherence to development standards and maintainability. Use Azure DevOps to manage code repositories, development tasks, and implement CI/CD pipelines. Ensure high system quality and performance by applying continuous testing, monitoring, and optimization strategies, including implementing security and access controls. Support issue resolution, debug and troubleshoot problems within the STEP environment, and document solutions and technical progress. Contribute to data integration activities by identifying, modelling, and linking internal and external data sources within the STEP architecture. Actively engage in BASF development communities, promoting knowledge exchange and innovation around digital product development and data-driven solutions. Solid understanding of PIM and MDM principles, with practical experience in STEP workflows, attributes, WebUI customization, and business rules. Experience in working with shared and customized STEP environments, with knowledge of the tool’s core and advanced functionalities. Familiarity with STIBO reporting/monitoring functions and good awareness of best practices in MDM/PIM development. Proficient in JavaScript for business rules and comfortable with data modeling concepts in product/customer domains. Hands-on experience with agile development tools, especially Azure DevOps, including version control and CI/CD setup. Strong analytical and communication skills, with a collaborative mindset and eagerness to learn. Fluent in English; German is a plus. Stibo Systems certification is a plus. jobs.name: Technical Service Lab Executive
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Technical Service Lab Executive to join our laboratory team. The ideal candidate will support product testing, customer complaint analysis, formulation support, and technical service activities. This role involves hands-on lab work, data interpretation, and collaboration with cross-functional teams including sales, production, and quality. How You Will Do It Conduct routine and non-routine laboratory analyses on raw materials, intermediates, and finished products. Support technical service requests by performing application studies, product evaluations, and performance testing. Assist in troubleshooting customer issues and providing analytical reports and recommendations. Develop and optimize test methods, formulations, and application procedures as required. Maintain accurate documentation of experiments, test results, calibration logs, and lab reports. Ensure compliance with lab safety practices, ISO standards, and internal SOPs. Handle laboratory instruments such as GC, HPLC, UV-Vis, FTIR, viscometers, titrators, pH meters, etc. (Exposure preferred; training will be provided for freshers.) Prepare technical presentations, data summaries, and customer documentation when required. What We Look For Education: B.Tech / M.Sc in Chemistry, Industrial Chemistry, Applied Chemistry, Polymer Science, Chemical Engineering. Experience: 1–5 years in technical service, R&D, QC/QA, or application labs in chemical, polymer, coatings, adhesives, specialty chemicals, or related industries. 1–5 years of experience in Super Absorbent Polymer (SAP) [Must Have] Strong understanding of analytical chemistry, organic chemistry, or polymer chemistry concepts. Hands-on laboratory skills with good attention to detail. Ability to analyze data and interpret results accurately. Good communication skills for interacting with internal teams and customers. Problem-solving mindset with a willingness to learn and adapt. Organized, disciplined, and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Work Location Innovation Campus Mumbai

jobs.name: Alternance - Sécurité des Procédés (h/f) Détails du poste
jobs.description:
Alternance - Sécurité des Procédés (h/f) Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion au profil Connexion employé Description de poste Alternance - Sécurité des Procédés (h/f) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Postuler BIENVENUE CHEZ BASF Le monde a besoin d’innovation et nous avons besoin de vous ! Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Rejoignez Coatings France, où l'innovation rencontre la durabilité pour créer des revêtements de pointe qui transforment l'industrie. Découvrez notre site de Clermont (60) en vidéo . Etes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES Intégré au sein du service HSE sur notre site industriel de Breuil le Sec classé Seveso Seuil Haut, vous apporterez votre support à la Chargée Sécurité des Procédés site. A ce titre, vos principales missions seront de : Participer à la mise à jour de l'étude de danger (MMR, revue des noeuds papillons..); Participer aux revues de risques sécurité des procédés et aider à la mise en place d'un nouveau logiciel; Rédiger en collaboration avec votre tutrice les dossiers de sécurité; Mettre à jour le Document Relatif à la Protection Contre les Explosions (DRPCE); Participer aux revues de risques sécurité des procédés liées aux projets sur site; D'autres sujets pourront vous être confiés en fonction des évolutions de réglementations et projets internes en cours. VOTRE PROFIL Vous êtes étudiant en Master ou cycle ingénieur dans le domaine de la maitrise des risques industriels ou sécurité des procédés et êtes à la recherche d'une alternance; Vous êtes capable de travailler en équipe; Vous êtes reconnus pour vos compétences relationnelles et votre appétence terrain. Vous avez une bonne maîtrise des outils informatiques (Suite Office); Vous êtes curieux, rigoureux, organisé et force de proposition; Une première expérience sur un site industriel serait un plus. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Un package attractif ; Un accompagnement personnalisé favorisant le développement de vos compétences métier ; Une expérience riche et variée sur un site de production au sein du leader de l'industrie chimique ; L’opportunité de rejoindre une entreprise labellisée "Employeur de choix" depuis plus de 7 années consécutives au travers de notre partenariat avec la plateforme Engagement Jeunes , vous permettant de bénéficier d'un accompagnement personnalisé à l’issue de votre contrat d’alternance et ainsi de booster votre employabilité. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Juliette, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Coatings France est spécialisée dans le développement, la production et la commercialisation de différentes solutions de revêtement allant des traitements de surface à la peinture automobile. Nous soutenons l'innovation, le design et de nouvelles possibilités d'applications au sein d'une équipe internationale dont l'objectif est de répondre aux besoins de nos partenaires dans le monde entier. Notre gamme de services comprend également le programme « Innovation Beyond Paint » qui vise le développement de nouveaux marchés et de modèles commerciaux. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Industry Sales Specialist - Metalworking Fluids
jobs.description:
Welcome If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. Company Description Surventis , is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable mobility and refinish coatings, as well as surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass across many industries. We share the skills, knowledge and resources of interdisciplinary global teams through a collaborative network of sites in Europe, North America, South America and Asia Pacific. Roles and Responsibilities Customer Development & Sales Leadership •    Lead customer development strategy in collaboration with the Key Industry Sales Manager, including account segmentation, target setting, and multi-site growth planning. •    Own and support sales forecasting and pipeline accuracy by partnering with local sales teams and actively managing opportunities in Salesforce. •    Proactively identify, qualify, and advance up-sell and cross-sell opportunities across complex, multi-location customer organizations using data-driven account insights. •    Prospect and introduce new products and solutions •    Monitor competitive activity, pricing, and solution positioning; develop and execute competitive response strategies. Account & Relationship Management Objectives •    Support the negotiation, structuring, and closing of complex sales agreements with strategic and multi-site accounts, •    Own and coordinate commercial and technical approval processes, ensuring timely decision-making and seamless execution across internal stakeholders. •    Act as the primary strategic liaison between assigned accounts and all Chemetall functions, •    Develop and sustain executive-level relationships with key customer decision-makers, influencers, and site leaders •    Identify and support key customer business reviews to assess performance and  align on strategic priorities. Other Accountabilities and Administrative •    Advise regional sales team on account(s) activities. •    Share market insights and trends with Regional Marketing management. •    Provides input into segment strategy. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering, chemistry, engineering, or business administration is preferred. Experience in Metal Working Fluids is required. High level of efficiency in sales process. Project management skills. Demonstrated business acumen. Sales acumen (account & relationship management, value-based selling, etc.) Customer focus Organizational awareness Strong written and oral communication skills. Strong collaboration mindset. Flexibility and ability to travel up to 50%, 25% overnight travel. jobs.description:
BASF SE - Berufsorientierungspraktikum 2027 Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profil-Login Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung BASF SE - Berufsorientierungspraktikum 2027 STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) Jetzt bewerben INFORMATIONEN ZUM BERUFSORIENTIERUNGSPRAKTIKUM Ein Berufsorientierungspraktikum bei der BASF SE am Standort Ludwigshafen findet immer von Montag bis Freitag statt. Die Dauer beträgt mindestens eine Woche, maximal zwei Wochen. Schulische Praktika werden ohne Altersbeschränkung ab der 8. Klasse angeboten. Freiwillige Praktika sind laut Jugendarbeitsschutzgesetz erst ab 15 Jahren möglich. Diese sind in den Schulferien ebenfalls möglich - in den Sommerferien allerdings nur, wenn du bereits eine betreuende Einheit im Vorfeld gefunden hast. Ein Berufsorientierungspraktikum ist maximal 12 Monate nach Schulabschluss möglich. Folgende Unternehmen kooperieren beim Thema Berufsorientierungspraktikum: BASF SE in Ludwigshafen, BASF Gastronomie GmbH Ludwigshafen, BASF Digital Solutions GmbH in Ludwigshafen und BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH in Limburgerhof oder Ludwigshafen. Daher könnte es zu einem Praktikum in einem der genannten Unternehmen kommen – solltest du einen bestimmten Wunsch haben, lass es uns im Bemerkungsfeld gerne wissen. Über den Bewerbungsbutton auf dieser Seite kannst du dich für ein Praktikum im Jahr 2027 bewerben. Hinweis: In der Online-Bewerbung sprechen wir manchmal von Ausbildung. Lass dich davon nicht verunsichern! Du bist hier richtig mit deiner Bewerbung für ein Berufsorientierungspraktikum. DAS BRINGST DU MIT Du benötigst für ein Berufsorientierungspraktikum keine Vorkenntnisse. WANN ERHALTE ICH RÜCKMELDUNG Nach sorgfältiger Prüfung deiner Unterlagen erhältst du so schnell wie möglich eine Rückmeldung. Solltest du bereits eine:n Betreuer:in gefunden haben, vermerke das gerne in deiner Bewerbung - dann steht einem Praktikum nichts im Wege. Bitte habe Verständnis dafür, dass die Bearbeitung und Prüfung deiner Bewerbung etwas Zeit beansprucht. DEIN WEG ZU UNS: Richtig bewerben - so geht's! Für deine Bewerbung benötigen wir eine Kopie deines letzten Zeugnisses und einen Lebenslauf . In den nachfolgenden Schritten musst du dir ein Konto in unserem Bewerbungssystem erstellen und deinen Lebenslauf hochladen. Im Anschluss gibst du deine Praktikumsdaten an und lädst die Kopie deines letzten Zeugnisses hoch. Es ist kein Anschreiben erforderlich. Bitte nenne uns deinen Berufswunsch für das Praktikum (sowie mögliche Alternativen) in Schritt 3 der Online-Bewerbung. In folgenden Berufen kann ein Praktikum absolviert werden: Produktion und Technik Anlagenmechaniker:in "plus" ("beinhaltet Bestandteile des Ausbildungsberufes "Industriemechaniker:in") Chemikant:in Elektroniker:in für Automatisierungstechnik Elektroniker:in für Betriebstechnik Mechatroniker:in Kunststoff- und Kautschuktechnologe:in Naturwissenschaftliche Ausbildung Biologielaborant:in Chemielaborant:in Physiklaborant:in Tierpfleger:in - Fachrichtung Forschung und Klinik Kaufmännische und IT-Ausbildung Industriekaufmann:frau Fachinformatiker:in für Anwendungsentwicklung Fachinformatiker:in für Daten- und Prozessanalyse Fachinformatiker:in für Systemintegration Patentanwaltsfachangestellte:r Gastro & More Eisenbahner:in im Betriebsdienst (nur einwöchig möglich) Fachkraft für Schutz und Sicherheit (nur einwöchig möglich)* Fachmann:frau für Systemgastronomie* Hotelfachmann:frau* Koch:Köchin* Fachkraft für Restaurants und Veranstaltungsgastronomie* Hilfstätigkeiten im Integrationsbetrieb** *auch in den Sommerferien möglich **nur für Bewerbende mit Einschränkung möglich Duale Studiengänge Angewandte Informatik International Business Administration International Business Administration and Information Technology Kooperatives Ingenieurstudium Maschinenbau: Schwerpunkt Verfahrenstechnik Schwerpunkt Technische Gebäudeausrüstung Kooperatives Ingenieurstudium Elektrotechnik: Schwerpunkt Automatisierungstechnik Schwerpunkt Energietechnik KONTAKT Du erreichst uns unter der Mailadresse berufsorientierungspraktikum-lu@basf.com .
