jobs.name: Data Security Analyst Specialist - Insider Threats (m/f/d)
jobs.description: ABOUT US At BASF Digital Hub Madrid we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and strongly encourage applications from individuals with disabilities. We value diversity and believe that varied perspectives enhance our team's effectiveness. Our hiring practices are designed to ensure equal opportunity for all candidates. Our global Cyber Security organization, headed by the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO), has the task of protecting BASF's IT and OT systems from hacker attacks and ensuring information security. We reduce the expected threat level with our strategic and operational activities. With dedicated projects we significantly increase the maturity of cyber security in the entire organization. We aim to secure the digital transformation in a sustainable way while supporting our customers and stakeholders as partners. It is our goal to enable and support the business by empowering employees to work in a secure way without unnecessary barriers and by raising awareness on information protection. You will be part of the Device and Data Security Team that proactively designs, implements, continuously improves and automates security solutions across device types and for sensitive business data — reducing cyber risks and attack surfaces according to BASF Cyber Security Strategy and Initiatives, while considering business requirements. RESPONSIBILITIES Design, evaluate, implement and continuously improve technical solutions for data protection. Enhance classification, labeling, protection, and monitoring of sensitive data throughout its lifecycle. Automate security tasks to optimize processes and enhance reporting accuracy and efficiency. Coordinate closely with internal Cyber Security teams to maintain consistent and risk informed data handling procedures. Facilitate collaboration with internal business partners to align their requirements with the technical capabilities of the security landscape. Present technical progress and achievements clearly to management, translating risk mitigation outcomes into meaningful business metrics. Collaborate with cybersecurity teams, product owners, and other stakeholders for guidance and support. Encourage teamwork, support colleagues’ ideas, and foster shared success. Share knowledge and best practices, building consensus between technical and business teams. Focus on operational excellence and bring new perspectives to problem-solving. QUALIFICATIONS A completed bachelor’s degree in computer science, information technology, cybersecurity or related field is required. Candidates should possess familiarity with Microsoft Data Security Tools Purview, DLP, IRM, DSPM and Elastic XDR suite or comparable security platforms. Demonstrated expertise in technical and design development using Python, SQL, and the Microsoft Platform—including Copilot—is essential, accompanied by strong communication and leadership skills. Well-developed conflict resolution abilities and situational awareness are required. Applicants must demonstrate an agile mindset and a commitment to ongoing learning and adaptation within a dynamic technological landscape. In addition to proficiency in the native language, excellent written and spoken English skills are expected. Certifications such as CISSP, Microsoft M365 Security Operations Analyst, or ITIL are considered advantageous BENEFITS A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. CONTACT Do you have any questions about the application process or the position? Please reach out to Felipe Bianco ( felipe.bianco@partners.basf.com )

jobs.name: Deputy Manager- Technical Service - Personal Care
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Technical key account management and key interface of customers R&D Translate the customer needs into technical projects briefing Drive Technical service projects delivery with business impact How You Will Do It 1. Technical Interface & Application Development Serve as the gatekeeper for all R&D and technical exchanges between customers and BASF. Develop appropriate solutions in lab and present technical proposals, new concepts, and application ideas tailored to customer needs. Support implementation of these solutions at customer sites through trials, formulation guidance, and troubleshooting. Provide technical consulting, training, and hands-on support to customers. 2. Technical Service Strategy & Innovation Develop and execute proactive technical service strategies that enhance long-term customer loyalty. Identify customer-driven innovation opportunities and emerging application fields specific to Personal Care. Lead joint development projects with customers, ensuring strong project management and full accountability for deliverables. Manage and mitigate product and process risks, ensuring compliance with technical and regulatory standards. 3. Technical Key Account Management & Stakeholder Influence Influence key decision-makers within customer organizations and internally within BASF—even without direct authority—to drive technical and commercial progress. Build and maintain strong, strategic, long-term relationships with key accounts across R&D, procurement, marketing, operations, and leadership. Leverage in-depth knowledge of Personal Care formulations, applications, and market value chains to anticipate trends and guide customer development efforts. Act as the liaison between customer teams and internal BASF functions to ensure smooth communication and high customer satisfaction. 4. Capability Building Enhance the technical capability of the sales team and distributor teams through structured trainings, coaching, and joint customer visits. Promote strong cross-functional understanding of product performance and application behavior 5. EHS & Compliance Follow and promote BASF’s Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) practices during all technical service activities. Ensure all technical recommendations meet regulatory and safety guidelines relevant to Personal Care products What We Look For Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Cosmetic Science, or related discipline Experience in technical service, formulation development, or application support in Personal Care- 8 to 12 years Strong understanding of Surfactants, UV filters, Bio actives, Emollients and other Personal Care ingredients Hands-on experience with lab formulations and customer trials Strong communication, influencing skills, and project management capabilities Work Location Turbhe, Navi Mumbai

jobs.description:
About The Role As a Technical Sales Representative, you will implement technical, colour and digital customer solutions, support sales acquisition by demonstration and validation of solutions, development of distribution technical teams and supporting new product, digital & colour service launch within the described territory. Provide day‑to‑day technical, colour, and training support to customers and distributors within the assigned territory. Conduct new customer acquisition demos, including digital and colour solutions, showcasing value propositions versus competitors. Support sales teams in presenting technical demo outcomes to target customers. Implement digital and colour solutions (including Refinity) across all assigned customers. Identify painter skill gaps, deliver onsite training, resolve customer complaints promptly, and conduct technical vehicle inspections with reporting. Execute system conversions, customer trainings, and technology transfers while identifying improvement opportunities and demonstrating advantages. Update Customer Skills Assessment and Sales Force databases regularly as required. Maintain required technical competencies, complete all training (including e‑learning), and collaborate with Technical Process Specialists to maximize customer productivity, efficiency, and repair quality. What We Look For Either hold a tertiary qualification in Automotive Refinish or equivalent spray painter qualification. Certificate IV in Workplace Assessment and Training is preferred. At least 6 years of refinish industry working experience. Experienced in coaching and development, delivering and communicating information to a diverse audience. Experience in using modern auto refinish materials preferred but not essential Proficient in Microsoft Office, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. What We Can Offer You You get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a wide range of elements to suit each one of our global team. You can look forward to: A diverse and inclusive environment, with an open and fair internal promotion process A competitive parental leave policy, regardless of gender, and a supportive return to work plan Give back to your community with our corporate volunteering scheme 4 weeks annual leave, with the offer to purchase additional leave throughout the year Opportunities to apply for international assignments – the perks of a global business! jobs.name: Site Controller
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position: This position is required to guide, challenge and advise the organization towards economic decision-making and value generation. Ensure that financial practices align with the overall corporate strategy while also adhering to local regulations and standards. Enforce compliance with financial policies and procedures. To facilitate the site management in steering the business and evaluating the costs in the legal entity. Develops financial competencies, partnering with key site functions and business, systems and reports to help improve performance, in respect of rules & standards to ensure full compliance. This position will be based in Thane and Vikhroli - incumbent is required to travel between these two lcoations as and when required. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Prepare Business Reports (Site Management inclusive) and maintain BASF Applications: Prepare and present accurate financial statements and reports to senior management. Monitor and ensure compliance with accounting principles and standards Reports prepared punctually and accurately Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): Product Costing report Sales Performance Overview: Role as variant BASF application gate keeper on site, e.g. Contract Management Database, COINS (Cost Consolidated Reporting and Analysis), I-Comp (ICTP), MARIS, Ocean, CARAT, SCII, etc. Support Site CAPEX  / OpEx (Small Investment or potential regional/global project): Manage annual Small Investment Planning Analyze Small Investment cost development, number of CAPEX and make sure budget under control. Manage potential regional or global project(s) planning taking place, in alignment with regional / global counterparts. Setup and on-going support to project Application and closing, for example project capital injection, project tax refund coordination, project equipment custom duty-free register, etc. Deliver relevant training, build up site team knowledge on Finance. Supervise BU/FU/Company Operational Planning, Business Analysis: Manage OMP operational planning process, cost budget and production planning in alignment with the region Ensure that standard costing is done in compliance with BASF accounting guidelines Definition, target setting and monitoring of KPIs in the site organization Monthly report fixed cost, variable cost and cost of sales comparison BGT/ACT/FC and information to management Monitor and report operation result of Manufacturing, Maintenance, Infrastructures, WWTP & utilities. Support various stock taking in the site organization Set up company level VBM and KPI and work with plant managers on constant improvement in plant costs Monthly monitoring to ensure performance under control. Implement and monitor cost control measures to enhance profitability and operational efficiency. Analyze cost structures and recommend cost-saving initiatives. Review and approve all site-related expenditures, ensuring alignment with budgetary constraints. Constantly seeking improvement in processes to increase efficiency and effectiveness Responsible for OMP Manufacturing Cost and Allocation Key Analysis: Monitor Process Order settlement rate and close the order in urgent case. Monitor/analyze company manufacturing cost development (e.g. utilities, infrastructure, wastewater, indirect service charge, direct cost, etc.). Monitoring BOM, Recipes, Business Process to make sure Process order properly operated Cross BU/plant cost allocation agreement for shared costs (idle cost, cost difference, etc.) Monitor material price (e.g. PUP price, standard price, make investigation on significant price fluctuation, etc.) Check SAP cost estimate before month end costing run. Site all cost centre gate keeper including cost set up and allocation key update. Support OpEx (Operational Excellence Measures) by validating and challenging benefits provided by site managers for OpEx Compliance and Risk Management: Ensure compliance with local, state, and federal financial regulations and laws. Identify financial risks and implement appropriate risk mitigation strategies. Liaise with external auditors during financial audits. Coordinate both internal and external audits (e.g. CEQ, Project audit A/W, operation audit, FRC audit, KPMG, FX, Tax, Customs, Supplier auditor, including follow up case) Advises Site colleagues on compliance matters Teamwork and Interface Management: Teamwork with other related teams, i.e. SAP team, logistic team. R&D team, maintenance team. To execute as LBM representative on assignments to ensure alignment among regional and local stakeholders to fulfil business objectives Business Support to Development Lab: Support the PU Development Lab with regards to accounting and financial matters including payments, sale of scrap, allocations, process setup Manage operational planning process of Lab, cost budget and monitor costs, recommend cost- saving initiatives, improve efficiency and effectiveness Manage annual capex plan and make sure no budget variance Deliver relevant training, build up site team knowledge on Finance. Projects and Back-up: Be a back-up for other PM site controllers as and when required. Active participation and engage with other Site controllers to share best practices. Lead and participate in ad-hoc projects locally or for the region Fixed Cost planning, forecasting and monitoring for APMA- India Requirements: CA degree in Finance or Accounting Around 5 years working experience in international manufacturing company in manufacturing environment Strong cost accounting and financial analysis capabilities, project management and transversal relations skills Proficient in English communication

jobs.name: Software Engineering & Operations Manager
jobs.description: Now hiring! Software Engineering & Operations Manager Austin, TX We are looking for a Software Engineering & Operations Manager  to join our trinamiX team in Austin. Come create innovative solutions with us! trinamiX , a brand of BASF, is expanding into the United States to develop advanced sensing and biometric security solutions that unlock previously undiscovered opportunities. Our mission is to redefine what’s possible by delivering technologies that empower industries, enhance lives, and drive progress through precision, reliability, and innovation. We are dedicated to improving decision-making and security with cutting-edge technology. By pioneering advanced sensing technologies, we aim to shape a smarter, more connected future where our solutions pave the way for innovation and growth. As part of this vision, we are looking for an experienced Software Developer leader to guide our trinamiX team and ensure smooth local operations of our U.S. entity. As a Software Engineering & Operations Manager, you Foster Innovation by... Proactively identifying the best technical approach to meet strategic goals — we value engineers who think, not just execute. Performing rigorous code reviews and raising the technical bar across the engineering team. Translating technical and business requirements into high‑performance embedded C++ libraries. Designing and implementing robust, efficient, well‑tested, and extensible components across our core biometric authentication platform. Leading, managing, and mentoring a cross‑functional team of 11 Software Engineers and Data Scientists, with full disciplinary responsibility. Fostering a high‑performing, collaborative engineering culture focused on excellence and continuous improvement. Translating business requirements and product needs into clear technical direction for the team. Creating an environment where engineers grow, innovate, and do their best work. serving as the Site Leader of our Austin legal entity for administrative and legal compliance purposes. Overseeing day‑to‑day office operations to ensure a productive and well‑coordinated work environment. Maintaining regular communication and alignment with headquarters in Germany. Note: This Managing Director function is lightweight — no P&L ownership and no strategic business decision-making. If you... Hold a BS degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. Bring 5+ years of professional software development experience. Have 4+ years of leadership experience with direct reports, incluiding disciplinary responsibility. Demonstrate strong, proven expertise in C++ (ideally C++17) and preferably Python 3. Have experience leading or managing a software engineering team. Exhibit excellent analytical thinking and communication skills in English. Are responsible, organized, and an exceptionally fast learner Have experience with Git, Linux, CMake, and/or QML.  (preferred) Are familiar with embedded systems or computer vision domains. (preferred) Have previous collaboration experience with Data Scientists or cross‑functional R&D teams. (preferred) Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Manager-Occupational, Transport & Environment Safety
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: About The Role Provide support to all stake holders of BASF in India for the effective implementation of Organization, Occupational Safety, Environment protection & Transport Safety discipline initiatives to ensure safety of employees in work, prevention of incidents and continuous improvement in EHS performance of the organization Support the organization in effective implementation of Occupational Safety, Environment Protection and Transport safety strategy, rules and regulation to ensure that national requirements, BASF strategies and requirements are fulfilled and high standard of EHS performance is achieved Interpret, translate, and support the mandatory requirements in Occupational safety, Environmental protection, Transport safety and Organization disciplines. Support the HUB and corporate EHS governance in effective implementation of new programs. Follow up OS, EP and TDS targets and KPI to improve EHS performance of the organization. Support the digitalization team in developing EHS tools and support implementation in India. Conduct RCMS audits (internal and external) How You Will Do It Occupational Safety Support the implementation of Organization, Occupational Safety, TDS and Environment protection program in India with collaboration with EHS HUB and corporate governance (COR) Oversee safety related regulatory development in India and facilitate regulatory compliance at sites & companies by coordinating gap analysis and planning for compliance effort with stakeholders at sites. Coordinate on incident reporting with stake holders, e.g. offices, sites, farms to ensure timely and 100% incident reporting. Coordinate and support incident reporting and management process for all injury cases. Support root cause analysis whenever required. Monitor and follow up of incident flash compilation and distribution. Also coordinate the incident learning sharing between the sites Participate in the incident investigation of high potential cases. Support production/ construction sites in India to achieve green rating in the Global RC Audits Conduct internal spot check audits for plants/facilities Arrange and conduct pre-audits with project team, procurement and BU for the production / construction sites, ARS sites, Initiate and coordinate improvement measures with sites and project teams. Conduct pre- assessments for toller selection Carry out RCMS training for suppliers/tollers/customers as per the business requirement. Carry out pre assessment of new offices or co sharing offices. Deliver safety training to respective sites, offices as per requirements. Assessment of logistics service providers (RSA and WSA) and closure of action items post-reviewing the implementation of these items. Support ODs to understand and implement EHS regulations and BASF requirements with respect to Transportation and Distribution Safety of new and existing chemicals and products. Track DG shipments by coordinating with sites and the Supply Chain Management (SCM) team. Providing expert advice to 3rd party emergency response service providers and ODs to manage offsite TDS incidents and accidents Coordinate with ICC for KPI reporting, Aditya Birla award nomination, RC logo recertification. Participate and contribute to the regional and global Occupational Safety and Environment Protection meetings Support in the compilation for Annual report and Business sustainability report Support the team in effective implementation of Plastic Waste management rules. Country Code Leadership Act as Country RC Code Leader on Organization, Occupational Safety, Transport Safety, Environment Protection and General EHS topics (OS) Provide advisory and consultancy services to the sites and interface with HUB. Support country strategy in ensuring legal compliance, corporate conformance, and continuous improvement. Provide On-demand services to Sites on Organization, Occupational Safety, Environment Protection and General EHS topics (OS) Provides leadership and directions of RC code subjects. Performs day-to-day consolidated functional RC code tasks. Establishes and maintain country RC code requirements. What We Look For Education: bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering or master’s degree in science Diploma in Safety would be an additional advantage. Working Experience: At least 10~15 years working experience in production, engineering or EHS English Proficiency: Level 3 Technical & Professional Knowledge:  Excellent communication and management skill, familiar with local legislation system and legal requirements in EHS. Must have good understanding of scope and function of EHS units. Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai Job Description Footer:

jobs.description: Masterand:in Deep Learning mit Graph-Datenbanken für R&I-Fließbilder (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Masterand:in Deep Learning mit Graph-Datenbanken für R&I-Fließbilder (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Die Serviceeinheit Global Engineering Services trägt mit herausragender technischer Expertise dazu bei, eine nachhaltige Zukunft zu gestalten. Mit unseren engagierten, interdisziplinären Teams unterstützen wir die BASF-Unternehmensbereiche dabei, gemeinsam mit unseren Kunden und Märkten zu wachsen. Wir setzen Investitionen wettbewerbsfähig um. Rund um den Globus sorgen wir für sichere und zuverlässige Lösungen, damit die Produktionsanlagen exzellente Leistung erbringen. Dabei verbessern wir uns kontinuierlich und treiben den digitalen Wandel voran. WAS SIE ERWARTET Rohrleitungs‑ und Instrumentierungsdiagramme (R&I‑Fließbilder) stellen verfahrenstechnische Anlagen schematisch und funktional dar. Im Rahmen der Masterarbeit soll untersucht werden, ob sich auf Basis der R&I-Daten ein neuronales Netz mittels Deep Learning trainieren lässt und ob das trainierte, neuronale Netz zur Qualitätsprüfung von R&I-Fließbildern eingesetzt werden kann. Die Aufgabenstellung lässt sich wie folgt gliedern: Sie analysieren die Graph- und Anlagendaten und erstellen einen geeigneten Trainingsdatensatz. Darauf aufbauend entwerfen Sie eine geeignete Architektur für ein neuronales Netzwerk zur Verarbeitung der P&ID-Daten. Sie übernehmen das Training und die Optimierung des neuronalen Netzwerks mit den aufbereiteten Daten. Abschließend validieren Sie die Modellleistung und visualisieren die Ergebnisse. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN laufendes Masterstudium in Informatik, Data Science, Ingenieurwesen oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang kenntnisse in Deep Learning und neuronalen Netzen praktische Erfahrung in Python und mit Frameworks wie PyTorch (oder vergleichbar) idealerweise Erfahrung im Umgang mit Graphdatenbanken (z. B. Neo4j) und Interesse an der Graph-Analyse selbstständige, analytische Arbeitsweise und Interesse daran, innovative Lösungen an einer praxisrelevanten Fragestellung zu erarbeiten WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase - Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy -  Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback Netzwerke - unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Praktikumsvergütung -  eine Anerkennung des Einsatzes, weitere Infos hier: on.basf.com/VerguetungBASFSE Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung - je nach Einsatzort und -gebiet SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Larissa Treiber  (Talent Acquisition), larissa-anna.treiber@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56384 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier ÜBER UNS Die BASF SE mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größter Arbeitgeber der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studenten, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Mehr über die BASF SE erfahren Sie unter: http://on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.description: Aushilfe / Studentenjob in der Produktion - 4-Schicht-Betrieb (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Aushilfe / Studentenjob in der Produktion - 4-Schicht-Betrieb (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS In Freiburg im Breisgau, im Infrarhod-Industriepark, produzieren wir Polyamide für den weltweiten Textil- und Automobilmarkt. Unsere Produkte werden z.B. in der europäischen Textilindustrie zu Garnen weiterverarbeitet, die in Funktionstextilien namhafter Hersteller zum Einsatz kommen. Weiterhin werden anspruchsvolle Bauteile für die Automobilindustrie und die Elektromobilität aus unserem Produkt gefertigt. Darüber hinaus sind wir an der Verwertung und dem Vertrieb unserer Produkte beteiligt und unterstützen unsere Kunden durch maßgeschneiderte Lösungen und innovative Produkte. WAS SIE ERWARTET Mit unseren rund 60 Kolleginnen und Kollegen stellen wir in unserem Betrieb Polyamid-Granulat her, das in der Automobil- und Textilindustrie zum Einsatz kommt. Werden Sie Teil unseres Produktionsteams und unterstützen Sie uns in Vollzeit (4-Schicht-Betrieb) für 6 - 12 Monate. Sie bedienen die Granulieranlagen und stellen einen reibungslosen Ablauf der Produktion sicher. Damit unsere Anlagen optimal laufen, kontrollieren Sie den Betriebszustand und führen Produktkontrollen durch. Darüber hinaus melden und beheben Sie Störungen im Produktionsablauf. Durch Ihre Arbeit unterstützen und gewährleisten Sie die Einhaltung relevanter Qualitäts-, Sicherheits- und Gesundheitsvereinbarungen. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN idealerweise Erfahrung im Produktionsumfeld sowie Kenntnisse in der Betriebs- sowie Verfahrenstechnik Erfahrung in der Arbeit mit chemischen Reaktionen und im Umgang mit Gefahrenstoffen von Vorteil Bereitschaft in Wechselschicht zu arbeiten sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (C1 oder höher) handwerkliche Kenntnisse sowie Teamfähigkeit und Zuverlässigkeit WAS WIR BIETEN Profitieren Sie von einer tarifgebundenen Vergütung nach dem Tarifvertrag der Chemischen Industrie (E01) sowie unseren zusätzlichen betrieblichen Leistungen. Sie erhalten 33 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr und die Beschäftigung erfolgt auf Basis einer 37,5 Stunden-Woche. Ein sicheres Arbeitsumfeld, da wir Sicherheit immer Vorrang geben und Ihre Gesundheit an erster Stelle steht. Die richtige Mischung aus Teamwork und eigenverantwortlichem Arbeiten. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Jenny König (Talent Acquisition), jenny.koenig@basf.com , Tel.: +49 30 2005-56012 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Der Produktionsstandort der Performance Polyamides GmbH befindet sich auf dem Gelände des Cerdia-Industrieparks im Industriegebiet Nord in Freiburg im Breisgau, nahe der Grenze zu Frankreich und der Schweiz. Die Universitätsstadt Freiburg bietet auch als Wohnort eine sehr hohe Lebensqualität und ebenso durch den Schwarzwald, die Vogesen in Frankreich und die benachbarte Schweiz einen hohen Freizeitwert. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Financial Reporting (Senior Analyst) - Mandarin Speaker
jobs.description: ​​​​​​ Objectives of the Position Independently in delivering Financial Reporting services to BSSCKL’s customers in accordance with established Financial Reporting processes and work procedures and in compliance with BASF policies & guidelines, internal control requirements, local company legal and statutory requirements. Ensure that timelines and quality meet the agreed service levels and targets. To support and provide guidance to Analysts in performing Financial Reporting tasks. To back up, assist with and perform Financial Reporting tasks as required. Main Tasks Functional Tasks (including but not limited to): Independently provide Financial Reporting transaction processing support and ensuring delivery quality in terms of accuracy, completeness and timeliness on daily basis. Perform and analyse booking of accounting transactions, fixed asset transactions, master data maintenance and account reconciliation including account clearing. Perform analytical review of profit and loss accounts and balance sheet, trend analysis and transaction posting checks. Prepare and coordinate for month-end, quarter-end, year-end and statutory financial reports for BASF companies across Asia Pacific on timely, accurate and complete basis. This would include reports for group reporting, internal management reporting, statutory reporting as well as any agreed ad hoc reports required e.g. audit schedules, tax schedules etc. Trouble shoot and liaise with relevant stakeholders to resolve issues and queries related to Financial Reporting processes. Contribute or participate in working groups (i.e. Smart Close, Final Sprint, RRM, CbCR & etc.) with specific expertise knowledge. Perform back up and other duties as and when required or delegated. Compliance and Quality: Participate in any compliance audits and respond to audit queries. Responsible in ensuring all work documentations and work processes are updated in compliance to BASF policies & guidelines, Financial Reporting Compliance requirements, local company legal and statutory requirements. Continuous Improvement and Projects: Identify, recommend and implement solutions for continuous improvement opportunities. Contribute and drive the implementation of project initiatives e.g. technology enhancement, internal control, merger and acquisition etc. Lead local projects and participate in regional projects. Knowledge Management: Contribute inputs to maintain and update a set of comprehensive and complete training materials of Financial Reporting functional training. Contribute to Financial Reporting internal control measures. Contribute to knowledge database by updating company specific procedures and issue logs (including resolution and preventive action) to facilitate continuous learning, development and knowledge retention. Support new and existing team members through effective knowledge transfer and sharing of best practice for sustainable development of knowledge within the team. Conduct functional training in accordance with established training structure (when necessary). Frequent collaboration with relevant stakeholders: Counterparts in BASF companies, internal/external auditors, external consultants and BSSCKL cross functional units. Specialists, Experts, Assistant Managers, Managers, Department Head, Analysts and peers. Corporate Finance unit. Global Financial Reporting functional units. Degree of Supervision Required in the job: Work with minimal supervision. Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position Education: University degree in finance and accounting or business studies; and/or Chartered or Certified Professional Accountant or equivalent. Working Experience: Minimum 3 years’ experiences in Financial Reporting or other accounting function. Competent candidates with lesser years of experience are considerable. Preferably experience working in SAP system or other major ERP system’s work environment. Experience in a shared service or business process outsourcing engagement will be an added advantage. Experience working in a MNC or Malaysian Public listed company preferred. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Knowledge and understanding of Financial Reporting and other accounting functions. Knowledge and experience of MS office tools. Proficiency in English and other language required with good communication skill. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Country Controller
jobs.description: In the Philippines, BASF has been supporting manufacturers from various industries since 1963. Today, BASF is a leading supplier of chemicals for industries, including Construction and Coatings, Automotive, Nutrition & Health, Home & Personal Care, and Agriculture. BASF also operates an Agricultural Research Station in the country which conducts research, laboratory studies and field testing of novel technologies. In 2024, BASF posted sales to customers in the Philippines of approximately €100 million and employed 92 people as of the end of the year. Further information is available at www.basf.com/ph . Job Description: Objectives of the Position In charge of financial planning, forecasting, reporting and business analysis and commercial controlling support for APA business in country. Act as financial advisor, challenger and business partner to the APA Local Business Manager (LBM) in all matters of financial relevance including business steering and projects/initiatives. Be the go-to person for Compliance questions. Be member of the country and business area APA management team Main Tasks Financial Reporting and Forecasting & Operational Planning: Responsible for monthly actual reporting and full year forecasting of P&L, specific balance sheet items and sales. Conduct forecasting processes as part of the integrated planning process (supply FC, demand FC, financial FC). Accountable for developing, tracking and reporting financial scenarios (risks & opportunities). Develop business commentary for corporate management. Identify risks/downsides and opportunities/upsides to sales plan. Document and track risks and opportunities including likelihood and decision time and review on a monthly basis. Commercial and Operational Controlling: Collect and review calendarized articles including volumes and prices. Support Country Management in steering pricing and rebate schemes. Develop price/volume scenarios. Check rebate settlement calculation, monitor rebate accruals. Track profitability on product level and initiate countermeasures (e.g. price increases) Track and support steering of expenses, i.e. selling cost, R&D cost, and manufacturing/tolling cost (if applicable) Request for ICTPs of new products, providing relevant information relating to market price and local COGS Monitor development of local COGS development (PUP review, MARS Q&A, material master status, tolling fees). Support operational business steering with adequate reports, ad-hoc analyses and recommendations Drive operational excellence projects Be the interface to the local credit management team. Monitor aging of receivables and support collection of overdues via development of payment plans Support LBM/Marketing/Sales in the design of commercial schemes by providing accurate information and developing comparative models for profit analysis Design SOP for commercial scheme pay-out and ensure proper records are kept for audit purposes Strategic controlling: Support financial evaluation as well as development and implementation of strategic projects (e.g. business models, go-to-market approach, make-or-buy decisions, investment decisions, portfolio management) Support strategy development. Plan, forecast and monitor capital expenditure Drive and train mindset of value based management across the organization Support value based decision making throughout the organization targeting sustainable optimization of absolute Market Performance. Perform ad-hoc analyses based on operational business steering requirements. Execute specific action plan to reduce dysfunctional stock (aging, no & slow mover). Strictly follow the inventory guideline process for inventory write-off and stock taking procedures [SC1] [RB2] . Oversee and coordinate with the Supply Chain team for all SC related tasks. Corporate Governance and Compliance: Go-to-person and increase awareness around compliance, internal guidelines and processes. Support design and/or improvement of internal controls Support internal audits and track follow-ups. Accountable for contract management. Responsible for project mangement specifically with the Differentiated Steering Minimum Education and Qualifications Required Bachelor Degree in Finance / Accounting Master’s Degree in Finance / Accounting is an advantage Working Experience: Minimum 3 years of professional experience in Controlling Technical & Professional Knowledge Advanced finance and accounting skills, proficiency in interpretation and analysis of financial reports. Strong knowledge of demand planning, inventory & distribution planning process APO DP & SAP knowledge is beneficial. Advanced skills in MS Office applications. Good command in English. Good communication skills, team player. All applications will be treated confidentially Only short-listed candidates will be interviewed Job Description Footer:

jobs.name: SAP FI-AP Consultant (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME TO THE TEAM If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT In our unit Digital Solutions & IT Architecture, EC/XD, we develop concepts and IT solutions for our global coatings and surface business. We drive innovative solutions, ensure seamless end-to-end processes, and identify global best practices for our different locations. In this role as SAP Consultant for FI/AP you will: Define or select the technically optimal implementation of FI and AP processes, initially focusing on a global project for invoice processing You also take technical responsibility for interfaces to satellite systems. Work closely with the Global and Regional Process Owners of EC/XD and Controlling & Finance digitalization teams. Support shapping the future standalone FI and AP architecture and implement quality standards. Designing and implementing SAP solutions, including customizing, coding and integration into existing system landscapes, in close collaboration with technical implementation partners. Support as 2nd level our Application Managed Services in analyzing critical Incidents on our production system. WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU Bachelor's Degree in Business Informatics, Computer Science or Business Administration with an IT focus. In-depth knowledge of IT landscapes, especially customizing and coding in the SAP (S/4HANA) FI module with focus on invoice processing. Knowledge in related SAP modules (SD, MM) and Process Director is a plus. Experience with methods and tools for interface description and data architecture. Several years of experience with global IT projects is desirable. Strong communication and presentation skills in English and Spanish. Analytical skills and enjoyment of teamwork to achieve optimal results with internal and external partners. WHAT WE OFFER Continuous training and professional development with our internal vacancies. Safe environment where we promote your health. Restaurant ticket. Competitive economic offer with a base salary plus a variable for good performance. Home office and adapt your schedule according to your circumstances. Connect outside the office and practice sports with colleagues through company groups. Flexible remuneration that you can voluntarily allocate to transportation services, training, childcare, etc. Pension plans, life and accident insurance, personal loans and participation in company stock programs. Financial aid for schooling and other measures for families. HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE PROCESS Send your CV in English through the job offer so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, we will call you from the Spanish Talent Acquisition team in Germany (prefix +49) and support you throughout the process. With each step, you will be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any further questions about this process, please contact: Antonio (antonio.casas-castro@basf.com*) *Please note that we will not be able to accept your resume via this email. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.description:
SAP FI-AP Consultant (m/f/d) Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Buscar ofertas de empleo Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Acceso al perfil Acceso empleado/a Descripción del puesto SAP FI-AP Consultant (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Enviar candidatura ahora TE DAMOS LA BIENVENIDA A NUESTRO EQUIPO Si eres una persona que inspira a los demás, asume la responsabilidad de su impacto y busca superarse constantemente en lo que hace, encajarás perfectamente con nosotros. Hasta el 1 de julio, nuestra empresa formaba parte de BASF. Como parte de la transición actual, todas las ofertas de empleo y el proceso de solicitud siguen alojados en la plataforma de BASF y, por tanto, se presentan íntegramente bajo la imagen corporativa de BASF. Por favor, no te confundas por ello: todas las vacantes corresponden a puestos en Surventis y tu candidatura será enviada directamente a nosotros. En Surventis, vamos más allá de la superficie en todo lo que hacemos. Ponemos a los clientes en el centro, nos anticipamos a las necesidades y desarrollamos soluciones que generan valor real y relaciones duraderas. Nos inspiramos mutuamente para ir más allá, asumir la responsabilidad de nuestro trabajo y nos esforzarnos continuamente por ampliar los límites de lo posible. Si aportas tu mejor versión, cuestionas las respuestas evidentes y quieres crecer junto a un equipo que realmente cree en ello, este es tu lugar. LO QUE PUEDES ESPERAR En nuestra unidad Digital Solutions & IT Architecture, EC/XD, desarrollamos conceptos y soluciones de TI para nuestro negocio global de recubrimientos y superficies. Impulsamos soluciones innovadoras, garantizamos procesos integrales sin interrupciones e identificamos las mejores prácticas globales para nuestras ubicaciones. Tu rol consistirá en: Definir o seleccionar la implementación técnicamente óptima de los procesos de FI y AP, con un enfoque inicial en un proyecto global de procesamiento de facturas. Asumir la responsabilidad técnica de las interfaces con sistemas satélite. Colaborar estrechamente con los Global y Regional Process Owners de EC/XD y con los equipos de digitalización de Controlling y Finanzas. Contribuir al diseño del futuro modelo de arquitectura independiente de FI y AP e implementar estándares de calidad. Diseño e implementación de soluciones SAP, abarcando customizing , desarrollo y integración en paisajes de sistemas existentes, en estrecha colaboración con socios técnicos de implementación. En las operaciones diarias, brindar soporte de segundo nivel a los Application Managed Services, analizando incidentes críticos en el sistema productivo. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Título Universitario en Informática Empresarial, Ciencias de la Computación o Administración de Empresas con enfoque en TI. Conocimientos profundos de paisajes de TI, especialmente personalización y programación en los módulos SAP (S/4HANA) FI & CO. Al menos 2 años de experiencia en un rol similar, usando métodos y herramientas para la descripción de interfaces y arquitectura de datos. Se valorará experiencia de varios años en proyectos globales de TI. Habilidades sólidas de comunicación y presentación en inglés y español. Capacidad analítica y gusto por el trabajo en equipo para lograr resultados óptimos con socios internos y externos. LO QUE OFRECEMOS Formación continua y desarrollo profesional con nuestras vacantes internas. Entorno seguro y donde promovemos tu salud. Ticket restaurante. Oferta económica competitiva con un salario base más un variable por buen desempeño. Trabajar en remoto y adaptar tus horarios, según tus circunstancias. Conectar fuera de la oficina y practicar deportes con compañeros/as mediante Grupos de empresa. Retribución flexible que puedes destinar voluntariamente a servicios de transporte, formación, guardería, etc. Planes de pensiones, seguro de vida y accidentes, préstamos personales y participar en programa de acciones de la compañía. Ayudas económicas para la escolarización y otras medidas para las familias. CÓMO PARTICIPAR EN EL PROCESO Envía tu CV en inglés a través de la oferta para poder conocernos. Si tu perfil encaja, te llamaremos desde el equipo español de Talent Acquisition en Alemania (prefijo +49) y te acompañaremos en el proceso. ¡Con cada paso estarás más cerca de conocer a tu futuro equipo! Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, puedes contactar con: Antonio (antonio.casas-castro@basf.com*) *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. ¡La diversidad es nuestra mayor fortaleza! Conviértete en parte de nuestra fórmula ganadora para el éxito y desarrolla el futuro con nosotros/as, en un equipo global que abraza la inclusión y la igualdad de oportunidades independientemente de la edad, género, orientación sexual, discapacidad o creencias. jobs.name: Compensation & Benefits (Specialist)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position •    Understand business needs and provide best suited support on Compensation and Benefits for specific assigned Country in the APAC region. •    Support respective countries assigned in the region by engaging with the stakeholders, from HRBPs to country C&B teams in Compensation & Benefits matters including data analysis and providing operational support. •    Support in identifying and implementing process improvements and best practices in the C&B space to ensure smooth implementation and engagement of best practice for respective countries. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Operational Excellence •    Responsible in preparation of data and analysis required to support any Compensation & Benefits initiatives such as performance management, salary budgeting and merit increment cycle, annual benefit renewal program and market benchmarking exercise for countries in APAC region •    Supports in collating economic indicators, external market practices and data analysis patterns and translates into meaningful indicators for development of any C&B initiatives and proposals to maintain market competitiveness •    Key person for job family matching and data submission for all Compensation & Benefits relevant market surveys (for eg: Mercer Remuneration Survey) with the guidance of the Expert •    Responsible for renewal and implementation of Compensation & Benefits program such as insurance, Flexible Benefits and Employee Assistance Program by managing, coordinating and following up with vendors, which includes market competitiveness analysis and requirement setting •    Manages and coordinates with vendors in the Compensation & Benefits are for insurance enquiries, market data purchase, employee assistance program vendors •    Supports Country HR for in scope countries in developing communications for Employees and Manager for implementation of Compensation & Benefit programs •    Supports Experts in Global Compensation & Benefits projects and activities by sharing current processes and studying the impact of new programs and processes to BASF HR systems •    Provide advise to Country HR based on existing guidelines, policies and practices BASF Corporate Rule Base, labor laws etc for reference •    Responsible for all recurring C&B operational activities such as insurance employee movement updates, year end benefits provisioning, processing of C&B vendor related payments, job evaluation request •    Supports and ensures any C&B tools and systems (for eg: SuccessFactors Compensation Planner, Gambit) implementation and takes into consideration the requirements and interactions with the Payroll & People Data team •    Provides support to country HR for utilization of C&B tools and systems (for eg: SuccessFactors Bonus and Compensation Planner, Gambit) •    Maintains and updates C&B related knowledge management documents and articles on all knowledge sharing platform for onboarding purpose and employee reference (for eg: HR4U) •    Preparation of salary proposals for permanent transfer, hiring cases and update of C&B tools (proposal templates etc) when required •    Responsible for data maintenance in SAPHR or SuccessFactors for Compensation & Benefits information such as Regional Specialist Track or Global Expert Track status •    Reviews any compensation and performance data upload for salary reviews cycles Service Engagement •    Daily monitoring of inquiry management for Compensation & Benefits enquiries to ensure high level of responsiveness on ticketing system •    Provide Compensation & Benefits inquiry support to employees via HR4U / CRM •    Support the Analysts should there be an escalation of C&B matters that require clarification and further in- depth engagement. •    Stay updated with C&B practices and is able to refer Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to readily available C&B policies and practices within BASF •    Play an active role in supporting the Expert in managing requests from Country HR and Regional HR Business Partner •    Guides local C&B experts in access management through Gambit Team Collaboration •    Works autonomously within established procedures and practices; seeks guidance in the more complex aspects of the process. •    Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously provide guidance and sharing knowledge with others. Guides and trains junior team members. Job Requirements Education •    Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree with HR focus as a bonus. Working Experience •    With 4 years of related HR working experiences in strategic Compensation & Benefits and experience in operational C&B area as well. •    Experience in relevant country statutory preferred •    Working knowledge around Rewards programs and system. Technical & Professional Knowledge •    Demonstrates a high level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location. •    Have an acceptable understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. •    Strong processing skills and analytical abilities •    Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills. •    Functional knowledge and skills in using Microsoft application i.e. Words, Excel, Power Point. About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Senior PKI Engineer (m/f/d)
jobs.description: ABOUT US At BASF Digital Hub Madrid we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Digitalization will be a true part of BASF’s DNA that creates new exciting customer experiences and business growth as well as drives efficiencies in processes. This is our digital VISION for BASF. Global Digital Services (GDS) is supporting BASF business in Digitalization transformation. Our MISSION is to drive forward the digital transformation of BASF, providing innovative, global and high-quality digital products and services through a strong agile culture. The product family Cyber Engineering & IAM Solutions enables the business to operate securely by ensuring consistent security measures and maintaining a unified defense through reliable, standardized engineering practices. As a product team of this family, the Certificate Management team is a key enabler for Information Security by managing cryptographic keys and digital certificates. It operates and continuously enhances the trust anchor systems Public Key Infrastructure (PKI), Hardware Security Module (HSM) and Key Management System (KMS) incl. provisioning of TLS certificates by a Cloud service. RESPONSIBILITIES As a team member of the Certificate Management team, you will be part of a team delivering global IT services in an agile set-up with the following main tasks: Configuration and operation of Windows- and Linux-based certificate lifecycle solutions (PKI). Creation and customization of processes for certificate lifecycle management. Configuration and operation of key management systems and hardware security modules. Focus on automation and standardization of the respective use cases, you are responsible for. Troubleshooting, ticket handling & Change Management QUALIFICATIONS Completed Master’s degree or comparable in the field of IT, e.g. Cyber Security, Informatics/ Computer Science, Mathematics Minimum of 5 years of relevant work experience in the environment of PKI/CA and/or cryptography Powershell-Scripting, Repository Management over Github, General-Scripting (Azure DevOps) Strong commitment, fast comprehension & Innovative thinking skills Agile Development & Growth Mindset Problem Solving & Analytical Thinking English proficiency required Nice to have: Knowledge in working with Thales Safenet HSMs and/or Nexus PKI BENEFITS A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. Contact Do you have any questions about the application process or the position? Please reach out to Felipe Bianco ( felipe.bianco@partners.basf.com )

jobs.name: Ingénieur Agronome Responsable Production - Blé Hybride  (h/f) Détails du poste
jobs.description: Ingénieur Agronome Responsable Production - Blé Hybride (h/f) Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion employé Description de poste Ingénieur Agronome Responsable Production - Blé Hybride (h/f) LOCALISATION ENTREPRISE DOMAINE D'ACTIVITÉ TYPE D’EMPLOI CODE DE RÉFÉRENCE OPTIONS DE TRAVAIL FLEXIBLE LOCALISATION ENTREPRISE DOMAINE D'ACTIVITÉ TYPE D’EMPLOI CODE DE RÉFÉRENCE OPTIONS DE TRAVAIL FLEXIBLE Postuler BIENVENUE CHEZ BASF Nous sommes BASF en Agriculture et tout ce que nous faisons, nous le faisons pour l’amour de l'agriculture . Nos solutions sont conçues spécifiquement pour différents systèmes de culture, en connectant semences et traits semis, protection des cultures, outils numériques et approches de durabilité. Nous nous efforçons d'offrir les meilleurs bénéfices possibles aux agriculteurs, producteurs et autres acteurs de la chaîne de valeur. Nous mettons tout en œuvre pour construire un avenir durable pour l'agriculture en connectant innovation, clients et société. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth. Découvrez nos Solutions et missions en Agriculture! VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITÉS Vous pilotez les plateformes de production d’essais de semences de blé hybride: Vous concevez les plans de champs et supervisez les activités du semis à la récolte (personnel, consommables, logistique) ; Vous assurez le suivi opérationnel et budgétaire en suivant les volumes par dispositif, les ressources nécessaires et optimisez les process et designs expérimentaux ; Vous contribuez à l’expérimentation en mettant en place les essais parents et hybrides selon protocoles, collectez et vérifiez des données phénotypiques de qualité et saisissez-les en bases ; Vous supportez les opérations Europe (DE/UA) et vous alignez outils et pratiques, partagez méthodes et retours d’expérience pour harmoniser et améliorer les process transnationaux ; Vous supervisez l’équipe, le matériel et la conformité ; vous organisez le travail des techniciens et saisonniers, gérez le matériel (suivi, révisions, CAPEX), pilotez les commandes SAP et garantissez le respect des directives Safety, QMS, Biosafety et la traçabilité documentaire. VOTRE PROFIL Vous êtes titulaire d’un diplôme d’ingénieur agronome et justifiez de 2 à 5 ans d’expérience opérationnelle en agronomie de terrain, expérimentation variétale ou production de semences, de préférence sur céréales ; Vous avez une expérience de coordination d’opérations au champs : planification, logistique, suivi d’avancement et exécution de la préparation des semis à la récolte ; Vous avez encadré fonctionnellement des équipes terrain (techniciens, saisonniers) et savez atteindre des objectifs de délais et de qualité en période de pics d’activité ; Vous avez une pratique de la collecte de données phénotypiques, de la vérification et saisie en base, ainsi que de la contribution au reporting (visites d’essais, synthèses) ; Vous maîtrisez l’anglais couramment ; Vous avez le permis B. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Une opportunité stimulante et passionnante dans une équipe engagée et compétente ; Un environnement propice à l'innovation, au partage d'idée et au bien-être ; Un univers qui vous permet de développer votre potentiel dans un groupe international. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Nicolas, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Seules les candidatures soumises via l’offre publiée sur notre site carrière seront étudiées. Toute candidature reçue en dehors du processus prévu (par e-mail, par exemple) ne sera pas considérée. Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Fournisseur majeur de solutions pour l'agriculture, la Division Agro de BASF France a pour ambition d'accompagner les agriculteurs dans la transition agroécologique. Pour répondre à ces enjeux, notre station de sélection de blé hybride à Milly-la-Forêt est spécialisée dans la recherche et le développement de nouvelles variétés de pointe de la semence à la récolte, avec pour objectif premier de permettre à la filière de blé française de répondre aux enjeux de l’agriculture de demain. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. Postuler Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. Milly la Foret, FRA BASF France S.A.S. jobs.name: Manufacturing Operator
jobs.description: Now hiring! Manufacturing Operator Sparks, Georgia Starting pay at $18. You will work 14 out of 28 days in 12-hour rotating shifts. You will get shift differential (increase in pay) when rotating to night shift. $500.00 Signing bonus : $250 after 30 days, $250 after 60 days from hire date. Full benefits package available including 401K. We are looking for a Manufacturing Operator to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Sparks, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF's Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. As a Manufacturing Operator, you will measure and batch raw materials to ensure product quality, safely operate batching equipment, and comply with all regulations. You will maintain batch records, complete daily checklists, and update shift activity boards while adhering to safety policies and participating in safety meetings. During your 12-hour rotating shifts as a Manufacturing Operator, you will Measure and batch raw materials in accordance with precise Manufacturing guidelines and procedures so the product meets customer specifications. Safely operate equipment associated with batching operations including pumps, motors, mills, tanks, ventilation systems, control panels. Operate processing equipment in compliance with company training and all federal, state, and local regulations. Maintain batch records, complete daily checklists, update shift activity boards. Always adhere to established safety, health and environmental policies and procedures, including participating in Safety meetings. If you... Earned a HS Diploma or GED . Have at least 2 years of experience in a Manufacturing facility or have work skills that may transfer to a Manufacturing environment. Are open to a 12-hour rotating day/night shifts. Can perform physical activities such as bending and climbing stairs. Are willing to wear the necessary Personal Protection Equipment, such as, hard hats, gloves, goggles, masks & sometimes respirators to ensure safety precautions are met. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.
