jobs.name: Project Lead Strategy Consulting (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF Welcome to the global management consultancy of BASF! We focus on strategic projects that drive the effective transformation of our businesses. Our teams work on a broad range of topics such as sustainability, organizational development, efficiency projects, M&A, and profitable growth through all types of innovation. We put people first: We are proud of our strong team spirit, and we cherish diversity. Become part of a great international team where your hands-on approach and desire to leave an impact are truly valued. Together, let’s create new ideas and offer the best solutions to our customers. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As Project Lead (m/f/d) you collaborate with colleagues on complex strategy consulting projects and provide impactful solutions for businesses and functions across BASF. You identify potential improvements in each project and advise the management team competently and empathetically. From Day 1, you apply your skills and contribute your ideas to ongoing projects. You communicate clearly and openly with all parties involved throughout all project phases. Our end-to-end consulting services allow you to accompany a project from start to finish. You enjoy top management visibility by working with BASF Executives globally on a wide range of strategic high-priority projects with sustainable impact. Our projects cover the fields of Innovation & Growth, Marketing & Sales, Organizational Excellence, and Post-Merger Integration/Carve Out. For an overview about our practice groups see here . WHAT YOU OFFER professional experience in strategic consulting and project work in a (global) strategy consulting firm depending on your qualifications and professional experience, you start as Project Leader (3-5 years) or Senior Project Leader (5-7 years) proven expertise in project management methods as well as consulting methodologies and approaches excellent communication skills in English; German is a plus convincing and competent behavior in client interactions open-minded and inclusive attitude, reliable team player international work experience is a plus WHAT WE OFFER Attractive location: Your work location is in Monheim (near Düsseldorf), the administrative headquarters of BASF Personal Care & Nutrition GmbH. The Monheim office has a canteen and modern, flexible workspaces designed to accommodate various teams and functions. Hybrid Work: You can combine remote work with up to 3 office days. Regular visits of our headquarters in Ludwigshafen are required, e.g. for monthly team days or unit events. Depending on the project/client, journeys to other BASF sites may be necessary as well. Work-Life-Balance: We offer access to childcare providers, flexible working time models, and cooperations with fitness and health studios, along with numerous other (preventive) offers for your mental and physical health. Personal Growth: We provide a comprehensive training program tailored to your needs. Our unit has a successful track record in employee career development: former team members can be found at all hierarchy levels at BASF globally. Individual support for your professional development is part of our mission. Perks: An attractive salary and a variable bonus, employee stock options, the possibility of special leave, 30 days of paid vacation, canteens with freshly cooked food, job bike, social counselling, and many others. HOW TO REACH US Jennifer Weiz (Talent Acquisition), jennifer.weiz@basf.com, Tel.: +49 30 2005-56454 will be happy to answer your questions for this position You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US Moisturizing creams, mild shampoos, strong styling gels, glossy lipsticks and protective sunscreens – all of these products contain ingredients made by BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH, a BASF subsidiary. Using natural, renewable raw materials, we develop and produce a large range of ingredients for the personal care sector. Learn more about BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH at on.basf.com/ECareandNutrition Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Project Lead Inhouse Consulting - Transformational Change (m/w/d Stellendetails
jobs.job_field: BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH
jobs.description: Project Lead Inhouse Consulting - Transformational Change (m/w/d Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Project Lead Inhouse Consulting - Transformational Change (m/w/d STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Willkommen bei der globalen Managementberatung der BASF! Wir konzentrieren uns auf strategische Projekte, die die effektive Transformation unserer Geschäfte vorantreiben. Unsere Teams arbeiten an einem breiten Spektrum von Themen wie Nachhaltigkeit, Organisationsentwicklung, Effizienzprojekte, M&A und profitables Wachstum durch alle Arten von Innovation. Wir stellen die Menschen in den Mittelpunkt: Wir sind stolz auf unseren starken Teamgeist und schätzen Vielfalt. Werden Sie Teil eines großartigen internationalen Teams, in dem Ihr praxisorientierter Ansatz und der Wunsch, einen Einfluss zu hinterlassen, wirklich geschätzt werden. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam neue Ideen entwickeln und die besten Lösungen für unsere Kunden anbieten. WAS SIE ERWARTET Bei uns können Sie als Project Lead (m/w/d) die Zukunft der BASF beeinflussen, indem Sie effektive Empfehlungen und nachhaltige Lösungen für unsere Führungskräfte bereitstellen. Hands-On-Mentalität: Vom ersten Tag an setzen Sie Ihr Fachwissen in der System- und Managementberatung ein, um Change-Prozesse erfolgreich zu gestalten. Klarheit und Orientierung: Sie spezifizieren die Auswirkungen von Veränderungen und leiten Strategien für organisatorische und kulturelle Projekte ab. Einfluss: Sie unterstützen die Führungskräfte und die Organisation bei der Umsetzung wirksamer Veränderungsmaßnahmen. Auf der Grundlage unseres umfassenden End-to-End-Serviceangebots begleiten Sie ein Projekt von Anfang bis Ende. Sichtbarkeit: Sie konzipieren Change-Management-Maßnahmen und führen Interventionen durch, z. B. große Gruppenveranstaltungen und Workshops mit Managementteams. Wachstum: In Ihren Projekten lernen Sie die vielfältigen Geschäftsfelder der BASF kennen und knüpfen ein Netzwerk auf Managementebene. Wir helfen Ihnen, Ihre Kompetenzen zu erweitern und unterstützen Sie in Ihrer persönlichen Entwicklung. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Erforderlich: mindestens 2 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich Change Management, Unternehmensberatung oder strategische Organisations- und Managemententwicklung nachweisliche Erfahrung in der Konzeption, Durchführung und Implementierung von Veränderungsmaßnahmen Studium der Betriebs- oder Volkswirtschaft, Psychologie oder Sozialwissenschaften sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse strategisches und vorausschauendes Denken sowie Interesse in einem internationalen Team zu arbeiten Fähigkeit, die Perspektive zu wechseln und die Dinge aus verschiedenen Blickwinkeln zu betrachten internationale Arbeitserfahrung ist ein Plus WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktive Lage: Ihr Arbeitsort befindet sich in Monheim (nahe Düsseldorf), dem administrativen Headquarter der BASF Personal Care & Nutrition GmbH. Das Büro in Monheim verfügt über eine Kantine und moderne, flexible Arbeitsplätze, die für verschiedene Teams und Funktionen ausgelegt sind. Hybrides Arbeiten: Sie können Remote-Arbeit mit bis zu 3 Bürotagen kombinieren. Regelmäßige Besuche unseres Headquarters in Ludwigshafen sind erforderlich, z. B. für monatliche Teamtage oder Abteilungsevents. Je nach Projekt/Kunde können Reisen zu anderen BASF-Standorten notwendig sein. Work-Life-Balance: Wir bieten Zugang zu Kinderbetreuungsangeboten, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle und Kooperationen mit Fitness- und Gesundheitsstudios sowie zahlreiche andere (präventive) Angebote für Ihre mentale und körperliche Gesundheit. Persönliche Entwicklung: Wir bieten ein umfassendes Schulungsprogramm, das auf Ihre Bedürfnisse zugeschnitten ist. Unsere Einheit hat eine nachweisliche Erfolgsbilanz in der Karriereentwicklung von Mitarbeitern: Ehemalige Teammitglieder sind auf allen Hierarchieebenen weltweit bei BASF zu finden. Individuelle Unterstützung für Ihre berufliche Entwicklung ist Teil unserer Mission. Benefits: Ein attraktives Gehalt und ein variabler Bonus, Mitarbeiteraktienoptionen, die Möglichkeit von Sonderurlaub, 30 Tage bezahlter Urlaub, Kantinen mit frisch zubereitetem Essen, Jobrad, soziale Beratung und viele weitere. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Jennifer Weiz (Talent Acquisition), jennifer.weiz@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56454 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen für diese Position Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier ÜBER UNS Erfahren Sie mehr über BASF Management Consulting auf BASF Inhouse Consulting Pflegende Cremes, milde Shampoos, starke Stylinggele, glänzende Lippenstifte und schützende Sonnenlotionen - all diese Produkte enthalten Inhaltsstoffe der BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH, einer Tochtergesellschaft der BASF. Auf Basis natürlicher, nachwachsender Rohstoffe entwickeln und produzieren wir ein großes Sortiment an Inhaltsstoffen für die Körperpflegeindustrie. Mehr über die BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH erfahren Sie unter: on.basf.com/CareAndNutrition Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. jobs.name: (Senior) Specialist External Reporting (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in Agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming, and the biggest job on Earth. We are an internationally successful company of the BASF Group. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. With teams in the lab, field, office and in production, we do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Here you can gain more insight into our Vision at BASF Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We in Agricultural Solutions are preparing to be an independent, pure-play agricultural company within BASF group. To be ready for independence, we are looking for you to join us in our new Corporate roles (see here: build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In this role you take ownership of the preparation and publication of the Group’s external financial reports, including annual and interim financial statements, the management report (“Lagebericht”) and regulatory filings, ensuring full compliance with IFRS and internal reporting standards. Your expertise is in demand as you manage and further develop the disclosure tool, prepare and review notes and disclosures, and ensure full alignment with consolidated figures, accounting policies and applicable reporting requirements. You ensure that group accounting policies and IFRS interpretations defined by Accounting Governance / IFRS are consistently applied and accurately reflected throughout all external reporting outputs. You translate complex accounting topics into clear, compliant and decision‑useful disclosures, particularly in areas requiring significant judgment such as business combinations, impairments, provisions and equity‑related matters. You act as the primary point of contact for external auditors on reporting‑related matters, coordinating audit requests, discussions and deliverables while explaining reporting judgments and presentation decisions. You also participate in cross‑functional projects (e.g., reorganizations, carve‑outs, acquisitions) with a focus on accounting, external reporting and governance implications. Last but not least, you collaborate closely with colleagues from Consolidation, Accounting, Legal, Tax, Treasury, ESG and Investor Relations to orchestrate inputs, ensure consistent messaging and deliver high‑quality, complete and transparent external reporting results. WHAT YOU OFFER Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting or a related field at least 5 years of experience in external reporting, audit or related areas, ideally within an IFRS environment dealing with complex accounting topics; leadership experience is an advantage strong knowledge of IFRS‑based external financial reporting and a solid understanding of end‑to‑end reporting processes including closings, disclosure preparation and publication cycles in a group environment strong analytical and drafting skills to translate complex technical accounting matters into clear, accurate and audit‑ready disclosures, with the ability to appropriately address judgment‑heavy topics in the financial statements proven digital and AI focus with the ability to leverage modern technologies and data-driven approaches to enhance efficiency and quality excellent English skills as well as solid familiarity with SAP ERP, disclosure tools and common MS Office applications clear communication and effective stakeholder management skills, acting with confidence when interacting with auditors and cross‑functional finance teams WHAT WE OFFER A dynamic work environment that empowers you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and assume responsibility. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence, enabling us to work in a future‑oriented, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks for the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business). Learning opportunities for your professional and personal development. Career paths as professional development into other roles within the company. We actively support with childcare (e.g. company daycare, holiday programmes) and provide a f itness and health studio as well as a medical center with numerous offers for your health. We offer sustainable mobility benefits — a discounted Deutschlandticket for regional public transport, a job‑bike leasing program, and on‑site e‑charging stations for private electric vehicles to make your commute affordable, healthy, and climate‑friendly. HOW TO REACH US Isabel Maisack (Talent Acquisition), isabel.maisack@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56683 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH is a reliable partner to the agricultural sector, offering innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to a wide range of crop systems. With the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, near the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you’ll find an attractive environment for both your professional life and your leisure time in the Rhine‑Neckar metropolitan region. You can learn more about the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof here . Find out more about our Agricultural Solutions team here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Technician, Production
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About BASF At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com . Objectives of the Position According to the defined production processes and quality requirements, you are required to schedule and plan in line with Company EHS guideline, properly operate the machines/equipment, and handle the chemical raw materials to fabricate the products that meet the requirements in quantity, quality, schedule, and process. How You Will Do It Main Tasks •    Perform on-site control and manual operation in the area of responsibility •    Maintain a clean environment in the area of responsibility •    All activities at work have to comply with the company's administration, safety, environmental protection and health regulations •    Comply and perform all operations in accordance with the statement of work for all production activities to ensure that the product quality meets the cGMP standard •    Undergo the assignment given by the operator in the control room and complete it •    Attend training(s) •    Handling and reporting of abnormal conditions On duty •    Handover: Include the current operation status, abnormal progress, maintenance status and unfinished work in the area of responsibility •    Perform environmental and equipment clean-up work in the area of responsibility •    Daily maintenance of equipment, automatic inspection (SAMACO) and fire safety equipment inspection •    Cooperate with the operator in the control room about sampling and all manual operations •    Manage and organize the tools within the area of responsibility •    Record the batch tables in production and perform on-site anomaly handling and reporting •    Perform the tasks as assigned by the superior/supervisor Inter-relationship with the relevant unit •    Take-out from the warehouse in conjunction with production •    Send samples to the quality control laboratory for testing in conjuction with production •    Support the inspection of equipment maintenance and security EHS Measure •    Ensure the workplace/site is clean, the passage is not blocked and there is no possibility of harm •    Maintain the workplace and working environment •    Ensure safe and efficient production activities •    Ensure compliance with local safety and fire regulations, and the company's EHS policy •    Perform immediate notification of a rapid incident (RIR) •    Supervise new recruits (Mentor system) •    Perform the Safety Observation (AHA program) •    Implement the TDS specifications What we look for •     Candidate possess Junior College or above qualifications, majored in chemistry or engineering related fields •     At least 2 years of working experience in Chemical/Petrochemical Plant •     Good knowledge of ISO 9001 and GMP quality systems •     Basic English read/write capability •     Basic computer literacy (e.g., Word, Excel) •     Have Stacker license and/or Crane operating license

Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. 關於巴斯夫 在巴斯夫，我們創造化學新作用——追求永續發展的未來。我們的宏偉目標是成為客戶首選的化工公司，推動其綠色轉型。我們將經濟上的成功、社會責任和環境保護相結合。巴斯夫在全球約有112,000 名員工，為遍佈全球各類產業客戶的成功做出貢獻。我們的業務中，「核心業務」包括化學品、材料、工業解決方案、營養與護理業務領域，「自主業務」包括表面處理技術和農業解決方案領域。2024 年巴斯夫全球銷售額為 653 億歐元。巴斯夫的股票在法蘭克福證券交易所上市 (交易代號: BAS) ，並在美國發行存託憑證 (交易代號: BASFY)。欲了解更多資訊，請上網: www.basf.com 職位目標 根據定義的生產流程和品質要求，您需要按照公司EHS指南進行安排和計劃，正確操作機器/設備，並處理化學原料，以製造在數量，品質，進度和進程方面符合要求的產品。 職位職責 主要工作 •    執行責任區内現場的控制及手動操 •    維持責任區内環境整潔 •    當班之所有活動遵守公司行政、安全、環保、衛生之規定 •    遵守及執行製程生產中各項操作依照工作説明書規範進行所有生產活動，確保產品品質合cGMP規範 •    接受控制室操作員指派工作，並完成之 •    接受訓練 •    異常狀況處理及報告 當班工作執掌 •    交接班：内容含蓋責任區内的操作現況、異常經過、維修狀況及未完成的工作 •    執行責任區内環境、設備清理工作 •    設備日常保養、執行自動檢查（SAMACO) 及消防安全設備檢查 •    配合控制室操作員交代的取樣及所有的手動操作 •    管理責任區内所屬的工具，并整理之 •    記錄製程生產中的批次表，並執行現場異常狀況處理及報告 •    執行上級主管交辦事項 與相關單位之互相關係 •    配合生產中向倉庫領料 •    配合生產中送樣品至品管化驗室檢測 •    配合設備維修中，工安的檢查 EHS 檢測 •    確保製程區内的整潔、通道無阻塞及無危害的可能 •    保持工作場所和工作環境 •    保障工作安全和高效的生產活動 •    確保符合當地的安全消防相關法規，以及公司的EHS方針 •    執行快速意外事件（事故）RIR之立即通報 •    新進人員之督導 •    執行安全觀察AHA program（危害識別） •    落實執行TDS之規範 職位需求 •   大專及以上學歷，化學或工程相關專業畢 •   2年以上石化或化工廠相關工作經驗 •   瞭解 ISO 9001 和 GMP 質量體系 •   基本的英語讀/寫能力 •   基本的電腦操作功能、文書作業（例如 Word、Excel） •   具備堆高機駕照、起重機操作執照佳 •   三班輪班制 08:00-16:00,16:00-24:00, 24:00-08:00 立即應徵 獨特整體待遇 — you@BASF 巴斯夫不僅提供薪酬。我們所提供的整體待遇包括許多不同面向，在您人生的每一階段發揮最佳作用。我們稱之爲you@BASF。請按此以獲得更多資訊。 獨特整體待遇 — you@BASF 巴斯夫不僅提供薪酬。我們所提供的整體待遇包括許多不同面向，在您人生的每一階段發揮最佳作用。我們稱之爲you@BASF。請按此以獲得更多資訊。 At BASF, we believe that people are the key to our long-term success and that talent is in everyone. jobs.name: Intern, Global Operations Support
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented intern to join our Global Operations Support department, supporting Maritime activities for EMEA Region. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Assist with administrative tasks to support the operations team Where needed, support certain units with daily troubleshooting tasks Generate reports to provide insights and support decision-making. Prepare documentation to support operational processes. Coordinate meetings, including scheduling, preparing agendas, and taking minutes. Manage and track pending operational tasks to ensure timely communication Job Requirements Currently pursuing Bachelors’ Degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Business Information System, Information Technology or any related disciplines. Strong organizational and time management skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Responsable Régional des Ventes - Région Sud Ouest (h/f) Détails du poste
jobs.description:
Responsable Régional des Ventes - Région Sud Ouest (h/f) Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion au profil Connexion employé Description de poste Responsable Régional des Ventes - Région Sud Ouest (h/f) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Postuler BIENVENUE CHEZ BASF Le monde a besoin d’innovation et nous avons besoin de vous ! Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Rejoignez la division Coatings Services, où la réputation de nos marques et notre force d'innovation en matière de produits et services nous positionne en leader de la distribution peinture automobile en France. Etes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES En tant que Responsable Régional des Ventes, vous êtes chargé(e) d’assurer la rentabilité et le développement commercial de votre région (Sud Ouest) à travers la gestion d’une force de vente et l’animation d’un réseau de distribution. A ce titre, vos responsabilités incluront : Assurer le suivi et le développement des ventes auprès des clients de votre région, tout en dynamisant le réseau de distribution ; Participer à la gestion des Grands Comptes en étroite collaboration avec le Key Account Manager et le Directeur Commercial ; Veiller à la bonne exécution des opérations commerciales et promotionnelles sur le terrain ; Gérer efficacement le budget régional qui vous est alloué, en garantissant un bon retour sur investissement ; Accompagner vos équipes directes dans leur développement et l'atteinte de leurs objectifs. VOTRE PROFIL Vous justifiez d’une solide expérience dans l’animation d’équipes commerciales ainsi que dans la gestion et le développement de réseaux de distribution ; Doté(e) d’un fort esprit d’équipe et de réelles capacités de prise de décision, vous faites preuve d’un sens aigu des responsabilités ; La maîtrise des outils bureautiques est indispensable ; Le poste implique de fréquents déplacements au sein de votre région. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Un univers passionnant et exigeant ; Opportunité de développer votre potentiel au sein d'une équipe engagée et compétente ; De nombreuses perspectives de carrière dans un Groupe international. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE? Pour postuler, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Emilie, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et contactera les candidats présélectionnés. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée . Il vous reste des questions? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Coatings Services est spécialisée dans la commercialisation et la distribution de systèmes de peintures éco-efficaces destinées aux professionnels de la carrosserie. Grace à la réputation des marques centenaires et premium R-M et Glasurit du groupe BASF, homologuées par les plus grands acteurs du secteur automobile, et à notre force d'innovation en matière de produits et services, nous sommes le leader français de la distribution peinture automobile en France. L'entreprise est présente sur tout le territoire national à travers plusieurs entrepôts et son siège social basé à Compans (77). La diversité est notre plus grande force! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous -- au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendemment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Provide end-to-end operational support for import and export shipments across APAC, ensuring on-time execution, documentation accuracy, and compliance with global trade regulations. Act as the coordination hub between suppliers, logistics service providers , Business Units, and customs brokers to deliver reliable, compliant, and cost-effective shipping operations. Managing and maintenance of import export and customs operations, handling relevant administrative activities, reporting, and documentation processes for the assigned countries Archiving all applicable documentation in accordance with company and regulatory guidelines Proactively escalate cases of deviations in transportation activities to bring shipment back on track in meeting defined and agreed delivery dates Meet defined service levels and any agreed qualitative requirements pertaining to core transportation and documentation scope of work Support monitoring and evaluation of service provider’s performance referring to agreed KPIs and conduct periodic quality reviews Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Responsible for logistics fulfilment processes (including SAP TM maintenance and process adherence) for all import and export shipments by working closely with the country import export & customs teams and hub-based overseas delivery services community Responsible for analyzing issues and managing where possible, escalating to country and/or Hub import export & customs teams for attention or improvements required Non-conformances management (NCM) and issue log maintenance and ensure reported issues are addressed with corrective action (if applicable) Consolidate, prepare and verify import export & customs operational reports to management and other relevant stakeholders Participate in system and new process flow testing where required Initiate continuous improvement initiatives and issue resolution of supply chain issues pertaining inbound and outbound transportation management on behalf of assigned countries. Participation in import export community meetings / activities where required and support service provider management and service delivery performance in assigned countries from global services hub Other responsibilities All other tasks as assigned relevant to meeting BASF and the objectives as outlined above. Assumes back up responsibility for team member/line manager for service provider management and import export operations as required Requirements Education Degree / Diploma (with relevant work experience) in Logistics/Supply Chain/Business Management or related field Working Experience Minimum 3 years of experience in handling Import/Export logistics with customs and trade compliance background, liaison with logistics service providers. Experience in SAP and chemical industry preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Efficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint and Word Basic understanding on Import & Export regulations. Knowledge in SAP system (TM/ SD/ MM Module) is preferred. Experience on using Cobalt or S4 Hana. Proactive team members who take ownership and show persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations. Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions. Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.description: Chemikant:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Chemikant:in (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir sind BASF in der Landwirtschaft und alles, was wir tun, tun wir aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft. Wir verbinden Saatgut und Traits, Pflanzenschutzprodukte, digitale Werkzeuge und Nachhaltigkeitsansätze, um Landwirten und anderen Stakeholdern entlang der Wertschöpfungskette die bestmöglichen Ergebnisse zu liefern. Wir tun alles in unserer Macht Stehende, um eine nachhaltige Zukunft für die Landwirtschaft zu schaffen, indem wir Innovationen, Kunden und die Gesellschaft miteinander verbinden. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Hier erhältst du nähere Einblicke in unsere Agricultural Solutions Vision & Mission ! WAS DICH ERWARTET Wir werden in Kürze ein eigenständiges Unternehmen, die BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH (BASD), in unserem Geschäftsbereich Agricultural Solutions gründen. In diesem Unternehmen werden unsere Mitarbeitenden dann in einem unbefristeten Arbeitsverhältnis beschäftigt sein. Eine Einstellung erfolgt frühestens zum 01.08.2026. In unseren Einheiten Thiuram/Netzschwefel-Komplex und Xemium-Fabrik werden zahlreiche Zwischenprodukte für Pflanzenschutzmittel, Wirkstoffe sowie deren Formulierungen hergestellt. Mit diesen Produkten verbessern Landwirte den Ertrag ihrer Erzeugnisse. Zur Unterstützung suchen wir mehrere Chemikant:innen im Einsatz auf Wechselschicht. Als engagiertes Mitglied im Team bedienst und überwachst du unsere Produktionsanlagen, sowohl in der Messwarte, als auch vor Ort. Des Weiteren führst du umsichtig und gewissenhaft Wartungsarbeiten und kleinere Reparaturen durch. Bei deiner Arbeit hältst du alle Sicherheits- und Umweltvorschriften ein. Eigenverantwortlich dokumentierst du deine Arbeit. WAS DU MITBRINGST abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung zum/zur Chemikant:in oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung Erfahrung in der Produktion Freude an der Arbeit im Team zuverlässige Arbeitsweise sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeit in Deutsch WAS WIR BIETEN Inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen (z.B. Women in Business) Mentoringprogramme und Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Karrierewege als fachliche Weiterentwicklung auf andere Stellen im Unternehmen (auch Jobrotation) Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung (z.B. betriebliche Kinderkrippe, Ferienprogramme) Fitness – und Gesundheitsstudio, Medical Center mit zahlreichen Angeboten für Ihre Gesundheit Deutschlandticket als vergünstigtes Jobticket, Jobrad, Verfügbarkeit von E-Ladesäulen für Privatfahrzeuge SO ERREICHST DU UNS Valeria Illera (Talent Acquisition), valeria.illera@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-58110 beantwortet gerne deine Fragen zu dieser Position Unser Recruiting-Team erreichst du auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess findest du hier ÜBER UNS Agricultural Solutions bietet als zuverlässiger Partner der Landwirtschaft innovative, nachhaltige Lösungen für Pflanzenschutz und -gesundheit. Mit dem Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof, nahe der BASF Konzernzentrale in Ludwigshafen, erwartet dich in der Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar beruflich wie in deiner Freizeit ein attraktives Umfeld. Mehr über die BASF SE erfährst du unter: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalte mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. Jetzt bewerben Unsere Mitarbeitenden stehen im Mittelpunkt und setzen sich jeden Tag dafür ein, unseren Kunden einen echten Mehrwert zu bieten. Wir legen großen Wert darauf, unsere Mitarbeitenden heute und in Zukunft gezielt zu fördern, damit wir die Talente und Fähigkeiten haben, die für unseren Erfolg entscheidend sind. Vereint durch die Leidenschaft für die Landwirtschaft, entwickeln wir gemeinsam innovative Lösungen, arbeiten partnerschaftlich zusammen und streben nach den besten Ergebnissen – als ein Team. So schaffen wir Wirkung. Aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft und dem wichtigsten Beruf auf der Erde. jobs.name: Global SC Excellence - APAC & NA (Specialist)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Design and optimize GB services (based on BU demands, legal requirements, M&A, investments, strategic projects etc.) Conduct feasibility, impact and cost analysis of prospective new services in SC Service environment Identify any needs for optimization/process changes and lead the implementation drive Ensuring that all service models and service solution designs conform to strategic, architectural, governance and other corporate requirements. Main Tasks Identify required models, methods and tools for Service Design Structure, group and prioritizing business requirements Ensure usage of new information and communication technology to design or re-design supply chain services Evaluate options, make comparative studies and recommend appropriate approaches/models Develop new and extend existing services based on requirements from the business and align with service catalog Test new services prior to implementation Calculate cost of new service Deliver basis for technical concept in cooperation with Functional Experts Drive automation and digitalization of GB services e.g. paperless document flows Drive integration of new businesses into GB service structure e.g. resulting out of M&A Manipulate/mine data from database tables using different business intelligence methods Interface with Global Stakeholders and Business Analysts across time zones to gather requirements by asking right questions, analyzing data, and drawing a conclusion by making and validating appropriate assumptions. Conduct deep dive analyses of business problems and formulate conclusions and recommendations; determine optimized courses of action Produce written recommendations and insights for key stakeholders to help shape solution design. jobs.name: Asst Manager - Customer Service
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: About The Role Manage OTC (Order to Cash) interfaces with internal & external customers efficiently and effectively. How Will You Do It Perform Sales and Delivery Order Management tasks in SAP. Contribute to development of customer relationships through proactive customer communication related to order management e.g. product availability, order status and delivery tracking. Support Credit Management, Billing and Accounts Receivable clearance. Manage Master Data maintenance process to ensure effective order management. Execute orders with accuracy and specific focus on the following fields like incoterms, RETA (requested ETA), Text ID’s & Shipping instruction, LC (Letter of Credit) term, Partner role, Plant code, Arrival Port, etc. Handle customer complaints via Non-conformance Management (NCM) system Actively communicate status, investigation results, corrective actions to customer and ensure on time closure for service / product quality related complaints. Capture customer expectations / requirements and feedback to BU (business unit) sales / marketing. Support implementation of business rules related to customer order management process Provide order status report and track customer orders, transactional report creation & circulation and timely following up for documentation. Contribute to further developments of customer care within CI and fostering a culture of customer focus in the team. Timely collection and submission of notarised agreement (high seal sale & bond sale) and BoE copies. Preparation of business reports, inventory reports, any other reports as per business requirements. Planning Job Support demand planning with Local Sales and RBU coordination for monthly replenishment, based on sales forecasts. Handle import clearance processes and work towards resolution of customs queries. Track aging & slow-moving stocks; drive liquidation in coordination with sales. Manage incident cases such as leakage, pilferage, or damage, ensuring timely reporting and resolution. Digital Transformation & AI-Driven Operations: Knowledge and use of digital & AI tools like Power BI, SAP Analytics Cloud, RPA bots, e-commerce portals, customer self-service platforms, real-time dashboards & CRM tools. Participate in SAP S/4HANA or process automation upgrades, ensuring smooth change management and adoption within the team. Actively contribute to Continuous Improvement initiatives to achieve efficiency with cost reduction. What We Look For Education: Graduate in any stream, MBA / PGDM in SCM Operations preferred. Working Experience: More than 5-7 years’ experience in customer service / supply chain planning. Certification on Supply Chain like APICS etc. will be an added advantage. Good communication & analytical skills. Good understanding of the products, services & processes of relevant business segments. Ability to collaborate within and outside the team to support customers & business. Demonstrate Customer Focus Collaborate for Achievement Communicate Effectively. Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai Job Description Footer:

jobs.name: Business Controller - Performance Materials, India
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: About The Role Lead PM Controlling team in India by steering, providing guidance and inspiring the team to be valued business partners for stakeholders in India. Be responsible for overall controlling, reporting, business analysis activities and ensuring compliance in India. Represent PM interest in discussion with other functions and units in the countries Be responsible for overall PM site controlling and reporting activities in Thane, Dahej and Panoli. Ensuring site operates with clear visibility of its cost structures and strategies. Drive digitalization initiatives to promote AI usage within Controlling and reduce manual task to increase efficiency of the team How You Will Do It Planning & Performance Management Ensure timely submission of monthly analysis and business commentary to A-PMA Controlling share point (e.g. CM1 margin and Fixed Cost comment, manufacturing cost analysis and commentary, capital expenditure report, raw material cost development report). Conduct business analysis; provide self-explanatory comments in business reports, clearly explaining the reasons for deviations. Ensure accurate data in the systems (esp. Business Warehouses and SAP) and proactively follow up with GB in case of discrepancies, ensure that regional controlling and LBMs are informed. Lead the annual Operational planning exercise in India Involve strongly in BASF India Limited public listed activities, ensure cost transparency and balance need of the business plus local requirements Manage project controlling (e.g. capacity expansion project) Fulfil ad hoc requests, questions, reports and analysis from RBU and OD. Business Analysis Conduct CM1 / profitability analysis at Sub-region, product and customer. Provide comments on profitability development. Continuously monitor fixed and variable cost development. Alert regional controlling team and Business Head in case or current or upcoming deviations. Actively suggest improvement measures / cost saving potentials (e.g. proactive follow up on allocated cost development and accurate allocation keys) and highlight risk. Conduct production cost analysis. Highlight potential for improvement/cost saving. Monitor deviations between standard vs. actual cost data and update standard cost data when necessary. Involve actively in credit management and close work relationship with local credit manager, e.g. highlight overdue for follow up Monitor the inventory development and ensure accurate data; highlight issues to plant management and define corrective measures. Involve in regional projects as required e.g support investment evaluation, special analysis, studies etc. Use digital tools and AI for analysis, promote usage of self-service reports within the organization Governance & Compliance Coordinate both internal and external audits (e.g. CEQ, Project audit A/W, operation audit, FRC audit, statutory audit, FX, Tax, Customs, Supplier auditor, including follow up case) Advise Site colleagues on compliance matters Review of sales & cost reports to detect any human errors. Introduce controls to ensure that all provisions, reversals, rebate, intercompany charges and other accounting adjustments are recorded timely to avoid unexpected surprises. Review and timely update cost allocations drivers related to Performance Materials business. Ensure accurate product costing based on standards of BASF group guidelines. Coordinate with country controller to ensure Signed contract are uploaded into contract management tool Teamwork and Interface Management Represent teamwork with other related teams; i.e. GBS team, SCM team, site management team and sales team. Lead country controlling team in India on assignments to ensure alignment among local, regional and global stakeholders to fulfil business objective. Identify, trigger and monitor the realization of cost saving potentials and cross BU synergies through process re-engineering in the BU / benchmarking efforts in line with regional policies and procedures. Conduct BU self-audits within the requirements of local Laws. In addition conduct Self-audit upon request or own judgment to ensure strict compliance for all business related processes. What We Look For Education: Chartered Accountant Working Experience: 10-15 years in Controlling/Finance/ Business Analysis Technical & Professional Knowledge: Proficient knowledge of Cost Accounting, BASF Reporting & Controlling tools., MARIS, BW, SAP Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai Job Description Footer:

jobs.name: Senior Executive, Process & Service Optimization
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position The Process & Service Optimization role is responsible for driving continuous improvement, operational excellence, and service performance optimization across logistics and supply chain processes for GOS & EMEA, The role focuses on identifying inefficiencies, standardizing processes, improving service reliability, and supporting transformation initiatives in collaboration with regional and global stakeholders. This position acts as a key enabler for stable, efficient, and scalable operations across Global Business Services and Overseas Delivery Services. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities •    Analyse end-to-end logistics and operational processes to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities. •    Initiate & support harmonization and standardization of processes across the GOS and ODS units, as well as with other service units. •    Support service recovery initiatives and corrective/preventive action plans to enhance service quality and customer satisfaction. •    Contribute to the design and improvement of service management frameworks and operational governance models. •    Lead & facilitate alignment workshops, process reviews, and improvement discussions with internal and external partners. •    Support regional and global initiatives by providing operational insights and improvement proposals. •    Coordinate and support change management activities related to process or service enhancements. •    Promote best practices and lessons learned across the operations community •    Support operational excellence projects, pilots, and transformation initiatives across logistics and supply chain domains. •    Participate in system enhancements, UAT activities, and implementation of digital or analytical tools supporting process optimization. •    Collaborate with IT and system owners to ensure operational requirements are embedded into process and system designs. •    Support in Audit process for any process/service relevant topics Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in supply chain management, Logistics, Business Administration, or related field. 2-4 years of experience in logistics operations, process improvement, service management, or operational excellence roles. Career starters with passion & basics in supply chain processes are welcome Hands-on experience in process optimization, continuous improvement, or service performance management within a global operations environment. Proficient in MS Office applications such as Word Excel, PowerPoint, MS Teams, and digital tools Strong analytical and conceptual thinking with a structured problem-solving mindset. Ability to translate operational insights into practical, scalable improvement actions. Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across functions and regions. Experience in process mapping, KPI analysis, and performance reporting. Proficient in Microsoft Office tools; experience with SAP or logistics systems is an advantage. High level of ownership, adaptability, and continuous improvement mindset. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Assistant Manager, Technology, Asia Pacific
jobs.description: Objectives: Responsible for supporting implementation of formulation technology transfer to own sites and contract manufacturers (CM) in APA including consistency check and update of technical database. Responsible for providing technical support and troubleshooting to own sites and contract manufacturers, support as technology subject matter expert in terms of chemical handling and process related topics. Support Senior Manager, Technology, Asia Pacific for all FFP (Formulation, Filling & Packaging) projects (both Capital Investment and Operational Excellence) at APA own sites and contract manufacturers Support in updating/ aligning APA Formulants strategy with APA Operations and Global Formulant teams, including implementation of follow up actions on Formulant Risk Management and cost optimization initiative. Main Tasks: Technical Management Facilitate formulation technology transfer to own sites and contract manufacturers in APA in collaboration with APT/TF, APR/D, APT/Q, APA/OP (Site Managers and/or Sub-regional Contract Manufacturing Managers) for both existing and new product introductions, ensure high standard of reliability and integrity for own sites and contract manufacturers. Provide technical input and solutions on APA FFP Asset Strategy development, ensuring alignment with APA Business and AP FFP Asset Strategies. Provide support to own sites and contract manufacturers in terms of root cause analysis and resolving FFP technical problems, reviewing FFP procedures, proactively identify potential capacity issues and propose mitigation measures. Lead development/ update of Formulation Manufacturing Manual (FMM), review operation record, and work with Global Quality Management to ensure consistency between recipes being registered and produced. Review and implement Management of Change (MOC) processes, support continuous improvement activities, and provide timely expertise technical advisory service based on demands. Support relevant FFP best practice and learning sharing within APA Operations team Project Management: Support key APA Technology relevant projects at own sites and contract manufacturers Provide support in terms of technical feed for project design and execution phase, ensure successful technical startup of new formulation and/or refilling trial at contract manufacturers Provide technical support to Site Asset managers in terms of FFP technical management and processes, i.e. specification management, risk assessment, technical plan and selection process. Support Site Asset managers by proposing and justifying needed improvements and typical solutions including design/ selection, installation & test. Monitor project implementation and facilitate technical decision making Responsible Care/ Quality Assurance: Provide support on Quality Assurance and NCM improvement from technical perspective Provide support on EHS audit follow up on technical part including process modification and/ or material management Global Formulant Task Force: Support development of Regional Formulants Strategy in collaboration with APA Technology and Procurement teams. Support in updating formulant Amiga database for APA Support in the implementation of mitigation actions for identified regional formulant risks Responsible for ensuring all formulants changes reflected at Formulant RegDB and all actions followed up timely Regional Development Laboratories and Sample Preparations: Support in aligning with regional development laboratories in collaboration with own facilities (Singapore and China) and tollers to develop laboratory trial process for new product implementation and troubleshooting purposes. Coordinate samples for global samples, country registration and marketing purposes together with own laboratories and Global Sample Coordinator. Management of Change (MOC) Coordinator: Provide technical support on creation of MOC, access rights and know-how on MOC Trackwise process Organize alignment on kick-off meeting between MOC requestor and stakeholders Update regional MOC and its subsequent action items ensuring closure as per project timeline effectively Requirements: University technical degree (Major in Technology or Engineering is preferable) > 5 years’ relevant experience in Technology, Engineering, Manufacturing or Site/ Contract Manufacturing Operations Excellent and proven technology and engineering skills. Strong analytical, communication and presentation skills Good understanding/ experience in Continuous Improvement, LEAN, 5S and/or Operational Excellence Exposure and/or experience of working in a multi-cultural and matrix organization environment

jobs.name: Manager, Sales India
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Develop and implement the sales activities to realize the mid and long-term objectives of the plastic additives business both Up Stream (Polymer Manufacturer) and Down Stream (Converter/ Compounder/ Masterbatcher) in India How Will You Do It Sales development: Develop sales plan and execute towards volume and margin growth Prospect, secure and expedite orders with existing and new accounts Conclude business contracts (sales, distribution, etc.) to maintain business growth Develop new markets, customers and new applications Attend to the customer complaints and provide measures to resolve them Prepare customer visits and other reports Market intelligence: Conduct studies/analysis on market trend, competitors, products’ applications and technology, service needs and initiate appropriate action plan Carry out market research project to develop competitor grid and benchmarking analyses Prepare market, industry trends, competitors, customers’ reports on a regular basis Customer & distributor management: Maintain existing customers / account plans and improve business relationships Manage key accounts and distributors Conduct field visits, seminars, presentation and attend to customer’s inquiries Provide prompt response related to quality, technical applications, inquiries, and other related issues Cooperate with functional team to ensure best product, service quality and good information flow and solve customer’s issues Inventory management Provide data and timely feedback on end use market trends, and monthly sale forecast, to support defining and revising the levels of regional stock and allocation Product and application: Conduct sales presentation, workshop, trade road show to promote products and their applications Arrange product testing and trial runs Credit management: Ensure timely payment of receivables to meet established sales targets and revenue goals Coordinate with credit control to make appropriate credit recommendation Others Prepare sales performance report, and other relevant reports as assigned Perform such other duties, which may be assigned by the supervisor from time to time What Are We Looking For Bachelor’s Degree, preferably in Polymer Chemistry or Chemical Engineering Min 5 years experience in a sales or commercial role preferably in plastic  industries Business development experience is an advantage Good understanding of plastic additives and their applications; plastics market and plastics value chain Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai

jobs.name: Praktikum Prozessoptimierung mit anschließender Abschlussarbeit (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Praktikum Prozessoptimierung mit anschließender Abschlussarbeit (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Praktikum Prozessoptimierung mit anschließender Abschlussarbeit (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir sind eine weltweit führende Forschungsorganisation mit über 3000 Mitarbeiter:innen, die in den Bereichen Materialwissenschaften, Analytik, chemische Synthese, Prozessentwicklung, Biowissenschaften, Katalyse und Digitalisierung arbeiten. Mit unseren kreativen und diversen Teams sowie unserer Spitzenforschung tragen wir dazu bei, unsere Kunden erfolgreicher zu machen und die Nachhaltigkeitstransformation voranzutreiben. WAS SIE ERWARTET Das Process Development TPU Team ist für die Verfahrensentwicklung der thermoplastischen Polyurethanprozesse am BASF-Standort Lemförde verantwortlich. Gemeinsam arbeiten wir an Prozessoptimierungen in der TPU Produktion.  Absolvieren Sie ein drei- bis sechsmonatiges Praktikum bei uns und verfassen im Anschluss Ihre Masterarbeit in einem praxisnahen industriellen Umfeld. Wir mögen es fundiert: Im Rahmen Ihrer Masterarbeit arbeiten Sie wissenschaftlich an der Inbetriebnahme, Methodenentwicklung und Validierung eines optischen Flachfoliendetektionssystems für das TPU-Betriebslabor. Qualität wird bei uns groß geschrieben: Sie entwickeln ein robustes und nachvollziehbares Bewertungskonzept zur Qualitätsbewertung von TPU-Folien und wirken aktiv an der Überführung des Systems in einen industriellen Prüfbetrieb mit. Ein wesentlicher Bestandteil Ihrer Arbeit ist die statistische Absicherung des Messsystems, einschließlich der Planung und Durchführung einer vollständigen Messsystemanalyse. Gemeinsam mehr erreichen: Sie arbeiten stets eng mit relevanten Fachbereichen zusammen, um praxisnahe, nachhaltige und umsetzbare Lösungen zu entwickeln. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN aktuelles Masterstudium im Bereich Ingenieurwesen Grundkenntnisse in Statistik und statistischer Auswertung sowie Kenntnisse über Flachfolienextrusion sind von Vorteil strukturierte, analytische und wissenschaftlich fundierte Arbeitsweise sichere und authentische Kommunikation auf Deutsch sowie Englisch WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase - Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy - Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback Netzwerke - unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Vergütung - eine attraktive Praktikumsvergütung Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung - je nach Einsatzort und -gebiet Informationen zu Praktika finden Sie hier SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Michael Wiesenack-Flick (Talent Acquisition), michael.wiesenack-flick@basf.com , Tel: +49 30 2005-58564 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH mit Sitz in Lemförde entwickelt, produziert und vertreibt maßgeschneiderte Polyurethan-Produkte und Problemlösungen, die das Leben komfortabler und sicherer machen. Unsere innovativen Spezialkunststoffe werden von Herstellern weltweit in nahezu allen Branchen erfolgreich eingesetzt. Mehr über die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH erfahren Sie unter: hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. Nos solutions sont conçues spécifiquement pour différents systèmes de culture, en connectant semences et traits semis, protection des cultures, outils numériques et approches de durabilité. Nous nous efforçons d'offrir les meilleurs bénéfices possibles aux agriculteurs, producteurs et autres acteurs de la chaîne de valeur. Nous mettons tout en œuvre pour construire un avenir durable pour l'agriculture en connectant innovation, clients et société. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth. Découvrez nos Solutions et missions en Agriculture! VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITÉS Au sein du Pôle Communication de la Direction Marketing, rattaché à la Responsable du pôle, vous contribuez à la mise en œuvre des actions de communication internes et externes de BASF Agro, en participant à la création de contenus, au développement des relations presse et à l’information des collaborateurs. À ce titre, vous : Concevez et déclinez des supports de communication visuels statiques et animés (print et digitaux), dans le respect de la charte graphique BASF, en lien avec les équipes internes et les agences créatives et média ; Participez au suivi des relations presse agricoles : pige et diffusion des retombées presse, gestion des demandes journalistes, mise à jour de la base médias et contribution à la rédaction des communiqués de presse ; Contribuez à l’organisation des temps forts presse, notamment la conférence de presse nationale et les points presse en région, ainsi qu’à l’élaboration du bilan annuel des relations presse ; Rédigez et mettez en page la newsletter interne bimensuelle, en assurant la veille, la recherche d’informations, la rédaction d’articles et la réalisation d’interviews ; Concevez et diffusez des contenus de communication interne (textes, visuels, vidéos, présentations) sur les différents canaux internes afin d’informer, mobiliser et fédérer les collaborateurs. VOTRE PROFIL Vous préparez une formation Bac +4 à Bac +5 en école de design graphique, communication des organisations ou relations presse, et recherchez une alternance de 12 ou 24 mois ; Vous justifiez d'une première expérience en communication (stage, alternance) ; Vous maîtrisez les outils de création graphique et avez une bonne compréhension des codes graphiques et des principes de mise en page ; Vous avez d'excellentes capacités rédactionnelles et êtes reconnu pour votre organisation, votre rigueur et votre sens du détail ; La créativité, l'esprit d’équipe et la curiosité sont des qualités qui vous définissent ; Des compétences en montage vidéo et motion design sont appréciées ; La connaissance du milieu agricole constitue un atout. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Emilie, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Seules les candidatures soumises via l’offre publiée sur notre site carrière seront étudiées. Toute candidature reçue en dehors du processus prévu (par e-mail, par exemple) ne sera pas considérée. Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Fournisseur majeur de solutions pour l'agriculture, la Division Agro de BASF France a pour ambition d'accompagner les agriculteurs dans la transition agroécologique au travers d'un portefeuille de solutions faites de produits modernes de protection des cultures, de biocontrôle, de solutions digitales, de semences et d'expertises agronomiques. Plus de 300 collaborateurs basés à Ecully à proximité de Lyon et en régions, répondent aux besoins des agriculteurs et aux enjeux de l'agriculture française. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. Postuler Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. Ecully, FRA BASF France S.A.S. Nos solutions sont conçues spécifiquement pour différents systèmes de culture, en connectant semences et traits semis, protection des cultures, outils numériques et approches de durabilité. Nous nous efforçons d'offrir les meilleurs bénéfices possibles aux agriculteurs, producteurs et autres acteurs de la chaîne de valeur. Nous mettons tout en œuvre pour construire un avenir durable pour l'agriculture en connectant innovation, clients et société. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth. Découvrez nos Solutions et missions en Agriculture! VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITÉS Dans le cadre du lancement d’une innovation herbicide pour les céréales, vous intégrerez notre département Marketing et jouerez un rôle important au sein de notre équipe. Cette alternance vous offre une opportunité de développer vos compétences en agronomie et marketing tout en contribuant à une démarche agroécologique innovante. Enjeux marketing : -    Suivi d'un réseau d'essais: Pour vous familiariser rapidement avec les enjeux agronomiques et notre innovation herbicide, vous aurez l'opportunité de vous appuyer sur un réseau d'essais répartis à travers la France. -    Etude de marché : Vous réaliserez des études de marché approfondies pour identifier les tendances et les besoins du secteur. Vous présenterez vos analyses de manière claire et concise, contribuant ainsi à la stratégie de lancement de notre innovation herbicide. -    Création de supports de présentation : Vous concevrez des brochures et des présentations intégrant notre démarche agroécologique, afin de promouvoir notre produit auprès de différents publics. -    Sondages et enquêtes:  Vous concevrez et administrerez des sondages auprès des agriculteurs, des techniciens agricoles ou de nos équipes commerciales pour recueillir leurs retours d’expérience, leurs besoins et leurs attentes. Vous analyserez les données collectées et rédigerez des rapports détaillés, fournissant des insights précieux pour notre équipe. Enjeu technique : Analyse ponctuelle de données techniques : Vous interpréterez des résultats techniques et aiderez à la création de synthèses agronomiques en fonction des besoins de notre responsable agronomique. VOTRE PROFIL Vous êtes étudiant en école d’ingénieur ou en Master en agronomie avec une spécialité ou un fort intérêt pour le marketing, et vous recherchez une alternance pour 12 ou 24 mois ; Vous possédez de solides connaissances en agronomie, pratiques agricoles et systèmes de culture ; Une première expérience (stage ou alternance) en agronomie, expérimentation, marketing agricole ou environnement similaire est un atout ; Vous faites preuve d’autonomie, de rigueur, d’esprit d’analyse et appréciez le travail en équipe et les intéractions terrain ; Vous êtes à l’aise avec les outils bureautiques (Pack Office) ; La maîtrise de l’anglais est un plus ; Le permis B est requis. Des déplacements ponctuels sont à prévoir sur le territoire français (visites d’essais, rencontres terrain, etc.). CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Un package attractif (rémunération supérieure au minimum légal) ; De nombreux avantages comme une prime de transport ou l'accès aux avantages CSE ; Un accompagnement personnalisé favorisant le développement de vos compétences métier ; Un environnement stimulant et responsabilisant ; L’opportunité de rejoindre une entreprise labellisée "Employeur de choix" depuis plus de 7 années consécutives au travers de notre partenariat avec la plateforme Engagement Jeunes , vous permettant de bénéficier d'un accompagnement personnalisé à l’issue de votre contrat d’alternance et ainsi de booster votre employabilité. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Nicolas, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Seules les candidatures soumises via l’offre publiée sur notre site carrière seront étudiées. Toute candidature reçue en dehors du processus prévu (par e-mail, par exemple) ne sera pas considérée. Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Fournisseur majeur de solutions pour l'agriculture, la Division Agro de BASF France a pour ambition d'accompagner les agriculteurs dans la transition agroécologique au travers d'un portefeuille de solutions faites de produits modernes de protection des cultures, de biocontrôle, de solutions digitales, de semences et d'expertises agronomiques. Plus de 300 collaborateurs basés à Ecully à proximité de Lyon et en régions, répondent aux besoins des agriculteurs et aux enjeux de l'agriculture française. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. Postuler Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. Ecully, FRA BASF France S.A.S.
