jobs.name: Regional Category Buyer  - Tokyo
jobs.description: Regional Category Buyer （Assistant Manager but non-manager Role ） ■ポジション概要 本ポジションは、日本市場向けパフォーマンスケミカル（スチレン、グリコール類など）および原材料およびエネルギー（天然ガス・工業用ガス・電力・再生可能エネルギー等）の調達戦略を担い、競争力ある調達と安定供給の実現に貢献いただく重要な役割です。サプライヤーとの戦略的交渉・契約管理・関係構築を通じてコスト最適化を推進しながら、日本市場における市場の分析（政策、料金体系、インセンティブ、主要プレイヤー、投資動向等）を行い、持続可能かつ効率的な調達体制の構築をリードいただきます。 化学業界特有の市場変動・供給リスク・品質要求に対応しながら、事業価値を最大化する戦略的購買ポジション です。 iBP、製造拠点、グローバル調達組織、サプライヤーと密に連携し、 価格交渉に留まらず、安定供給・リスクマネジメント・プロセス改善まで幅広く関与 していただきます。 このポジションの魅力 ・化学品購買の専門性を活かせる／高められる ・グローバル企業における戦略的調達ポジションで環境エネルギー・サステナビリティ領域に関わる最前線の業務 ・上司が海外、同僚も全員が海外。日本オフィスに籍は置くものの、インターナショナルな環境での勤務が可能 ・SAP、Coupa 等の先進的調達プラットフォームを活用 職務の目的（Objectives） サプライヤーとの交渉を通じ、競争力のある価格および最適な購買条件を実現する 契約条件の交渉、契約締結後の実行・モニタリングを担う iBP の原材料購買要件（品質・数量・供給安定性）に対し、最適なソリューションを提供する 生産拠点・事業部門・サプライヤーと連携し、安定かつ持続可能な供給体制を構築する サプライヤーマネジメント、リスクマネジメント、シングルソース管理を推進する 日本向けパフォーマンスケミカルの購買および市場・サプライヤー分析を実施する 主な職務内容（Key Responsibilities） 1. 調達・契約交渉 担当原材料およびエネルギー関連製品における最適な購買条件の設定・価格交渉 サプライヤーとの契約条件交渉、契約締結および履行管理 日本におけるエネルギー・ユーティリティ契約の交渉・導入 2. サプライヤーマネジメント サプライヤーとの関係構築・維持・改善 サプライヤー評価およびパフォーマンス管理 リスク管理およびシングルソースに対する対応策の実行 3. 市場分析・戦略提案 日本の化学・エネルギー市場の分析およびサプライヤー分析 エネルギー市場（政策、料金制度、投資動向、主要プレイヤー）の調査 コスト効率およびサステナビリティを考慮した供給モデルの提案 4. 社内連携・ソリューション提供 生産拠点・事業部門・関連部門と連携し、安定供給の確保 内部ビジネスパートナーの要件（仕様、物流、需要動向）の把握と最適提案 緊急対応時のサポートおよびカテゴリーチームとの連携 5. カテゴリー戦略およびプロジェクト推進 チームリードおよびリージョナルカテゴリーマネージャーと連携し、調達戦略の立案・検討・実行 「内製 vs 外購」の検討やサプライチェーン最適化の検討への参画 6. オペレーション・プロセス管理 SAPにおける購買申請の承認プロセス管理 監査対応・マスターデータ管理のためのサプライヤー情報収集 品質不良（NCM）発生時のサプライヤー交渉および改善推進 応募資格（Requirements） 必須条件 化学業界における購買・営業部門での実務経験（目安：3年以上） 日本語での業務遂行能力および高い英語力（読み書き・会話） 歓迎条件 化学品（原料・中間体・スペシャリティケミカル）やエネルギーの購買または販売経験 SAP、Coupa 等の購買システム使用経験 求める人物像 社内外ステークホルダーに影響を与えられる交渉力・調整力 問題解決力とビジネス価値創出への意識 自律的に考え、改善提案ができるマインドセット Objectives of the Position · Ensure competitive and reliable procurement of raw materials (chemicals and, where applicable, energy/utilities -defined here as Natural Gas, Industrial Gas, Electricity and Renewable Energy) for BASF Japan through effective negotiation, contract management, and supplier strategy. Perform Energy and Utility market study in Japan, including policy development, tariff systems, incentive schemes, market trading, major equipment manufacturers, developers, utilities, investment fund and project pipelines. · Negotiate with supplier for the most competitive price and/or purchase condition within given boundary · Negotiate terms and conditions for contract, contract implementation & monitoring · Manage supplier relationships and support the needs of internal business partners. · Routinely engage with BASF production sites, business units and suppliers to ensure reliable supply and provide effective solution to iBP meeting their requirements · Execute Supplier Management · Execute Risk Management and Single Source Management · Understand Japanese Chemical Market and make supplier and market analysis of the relevant products Minimum Education and Qualification required for the Position Bachelor’s degree major in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or Economics, Business Management +2 years working experience in procurement Preferred to have experience in chemical purchase/sales management Native or fluent Japanese speaker and excellent level of verbal & written English

jobs.name: Customer Service Officer with French EVO
jobs.job_field: Customer Service Officer with French EVO
jobs.description: Job Description: CUSTOMER SERVICE OFFICER WITH FRENCH EVO In the Performance Chemicals Division, it is all about customized products. The division's portfolio includes plastics additives, fuel and lubricant solutions as well as oilfield chemicals, mining solutions and chemical as well as refinery catalysts. The customers who benefit from our innovative solutions are from various industries, including the chemicals, plastics, consumer goods, energy and resources as well as the automotive and transportation industries. RESPONSIBILITIES Customer Order Management: Act as main interface to customer on order placement and follow up Select and use adequate tools (SAP R/3 SD, e-commerce software) to receive, process, and follow up on orders including cash management Understand and create customer master data as well as customer specific SAP set-up Collaborate with sales accounts, supply chain, transport, warehousing, and finance to provide excellent service to our customer Customer complaint management: from reception, to follow up and conclusion of complaint Development of new business opportunities: identifying and onboarding new customers and business opportunities QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor or Master degree in Sales, Business Studies, Logistics, Supply Chain Relevant professional experience at least 3 years Experience of working with the chemical and automotive industry will be an advantage Experience in SAP (Sales & Distribution) or alternative ERP system will be an advantage Customer orientation and team work abilities Strong communication skills and ability to work in international environment Proficiency in English (written and oral) Proficiency in French (written and oral), German would be a merit BENEFITS We offer a wide benefits package: life insurance, co-financing of MultiSport card, remote work allowance, training & development programs, Company Social Benefits Fund With non-standard items: VIP medical care, lunch card, commuting allowance, EAP Employee Assistance Program  (our total offer can be found here ) You can count on: gaining experience in a leading chemical company in the world, Work Life Balance, the possibility of using remote work in a 3+2 hybrid model, and workation up to 3 weeks per year At work, our values are important to us: innovation, openness, flexibility and attractiveness ABOUT US At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, on sustainable actions, and on connected thinking. And on you. Become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us – in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. Job Description Footer:

jobs.name: Gerente de Ventas - Plásticos de ingeniería Detalles del puesto
jobs.description:
Gerente de Ventas - Plásticos de ingeniería Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Encuentra trabajos Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Ingreso para empleados Descripción del puesto Gerente de Ventas - Plásticos de ingeniería LOCACIÓN COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE POSICIÓN ID DE TRABAJO MODALIDAD DE TRABAJO LOCACIÓN COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE POSICIÓN ID DE TRABAJO MODALIDAD DE TRABAJO Enviar candidatura ahora En BASF, somos una empresa que otorga igualdad de oportunidades. Todas las candidaturas cualificadas que cubran el perfil para una posición se considerarán para nuestras vacantes sin distinción de edad, ciudadanía, tono de piel, religión, sexo, estado civil, nacionalidad, discapacidad, identidad o expresión de género, orientación sexual,  origen étnico, lengua, identidad indígena, apariencia física, condiciones de salud, características genéticas, embarazo, religión, opiniones políticas, académicas o filosóficas, identidad o filiación política, por su forma de pensar, vestir, actuar, gesticular, por tener tatuajes o perforaciones corporales o cualquier otra condición análoga protegida por la ley del país correspondiente. En BASF, estamos comprometidos con la inclusión, proporcionando siempre que sea posible, ajustes razonables a las personas con discapacidad o con alguna necesidad específica, para garantizar su acceso y el uso de servicios.  En caso de requerir algún apoyo, favor de comunicarlo durante tu primer contacto con Atracción de Talento. Estamos buscando un(a) Gerente de Ventas - Plásticos de ingeniería para Ciudad de México. Desarrollar e implementar estrategias y planes de acción para los clientes del segmento de plásticos de ingeniería, alineado a los objetivos y estrategias de la Unidad de Negocio PM y de acuerdo a las Políticas de BASF con el fin de asegurar la operación, crecimiento y rentabilidad de las cuentas a su cargo. En este rol, estarás a cargo de: Negociar precios, ofertas de servicios, acuerdos de confidencialidad y términos para proponer y ejecutar contratos con los clientes para generar el máximo valor para BASF.  Gestionará un enfoque balanceado entre el mantenimiento de clientes existentes y la búsqueda de nuevos clientes. Gestionar y dar seguimiento como Key Account Manager de la cuenta asignada, para alcanzar los objetivos identificados con el cliente estratégico del negocio, siguiendo las políticas de la compañía. Involucrar con éxito a las áreas de la organización necesarias, para permitir la colaboración con el personal de marketing y servicio técnico, así como otras funciones según sea necesario para desarrollar y comercializar nuevos productos y aplicaciones. Aprovechar los productos existentes en cuentas nuevas y actuales para identificar nuevas aplicaciones altamente rentables. Proporcionar la retroalimentación y la información necesaria en las decisiones comerciales estratégicas, impactando positivamente en las cuentas de los clientes a cargo y de nuestros objetivos comerciales futuros para acelerar el crecimiento y la rentabilidad de las cuentas y de la región. Gestionar la comunicación con clientes internos y externos, utilizando su conciencia organizacional. Buscar mejorar la eficiencia del trabajo diario y proponer mejoras en los procesos utilizando su mentalidad de mejora continua. Proporcionar información relevante para la recopilación de datos (por ejemplo, pronósticos y presupuestos) y mantener los registros e informes en los respectivos sistemas (por ejemplo, Salesforce) relacionados con sus cuentas y territorio. Compartir la inteligencia de mercado y de los clientes, con Marketing y otras áreas/personas relevantes de la organización, para ayudar a desafiar y refinar las estrategias del segmento de mercado y la priorización de los recursos de innovación, impulsando también las relaciones y networking entre los líderes senior y los clientes estratégicos. Ser responsable de los clientes al involucrar a miembros de equipos multifuncionales cuando sea necesario, para resolver problemas complejos mientras representa la voz del cliente con un sentido de urgencia. Si cuentas con: título universitario relevante al cargo, experiencia por 5 años o más en roles de liderazgo de ventas de químicos (idealmente en la industria del plástico y/o automotriz), nivel de inglés avanzado oral y escrito (excluyente), destacadas habilidades comerciales, de liderazgo, negociación, planificación y ejecución de estrategias, esta vacante es para ti! Crea tu propia química: Lo que te ofrecemos ... Agregar valor a nuestros clientes comienza por agregarte valor a ti. you@BASF es el paquete de beneficios, desarrollo de carrera, ambiente de trabajo y compensación que ofrecemos para apoyarte en todas las etapas de tu vida y carrera. Las recompensas totales que recibes como empleado de BASF van más allá de tu pago. Desde planes competitivos de salud y seguros, hasta planes de pensiones robustos, creemos que invertir en ti es invertir en nuestro éxito. Trabajando para una gran organización global, tendrás la oportunidad de crecer profesional y personalmente, expandir tu red y construir una carrera profesional gratificante y dinámica. BASF brinda oportunidades interesantes y retadoras para ayudarte a aprovechar y explotar tu talento.  Acquisition Europe team is glad to assist you. Please note that we do not return paper applications including folders. Please submit copies only and no original documents. jobs.name: Business Reporting (Associate Analyst) - 1 Year Contract
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Do you want to work with passionate teams, providing excellent business and digital services to the Asia Pacific region? Do you want an open, supportive, and caring working environment? Do you view your development needs seriously as we do? If YES, then take your career to the #NextLevel with BASF ! O bjecti v es of t h e P osi t ion To deliver Business reporting services to BASF’s internal customers, in accordance with established Business reporting processes and work procedures and in compliance with BASF guidelines and policies, Financial Reporting Compliance requirements, Internal Control requirements and Local Company legal and statutory requirements. To ensure that timelines and quality meet the agreed service levels and targets for enabling country stakeholders drive business/operation with greater transparency and faster To support and back up other analyst in performing Business reporting tasks as required. M ain T as k s Functional Tasks (Inclusive but not limited): To deliver Business reporting services, by ensuring delivery quality in terms of accuracy, completeness and timeliness: To maintain and ensure master data regarding material ledger, inventory valuation, production planning budget, intra-company transfer pricing, cost center accounting, fixed assets and project accounting are performed timely and accurately. To maintain and ensure monthly standard and actual price calculation for all material included in material ledger are performed timely and accurately with correct stock valuation. To maintain and ensure estimation and year-end inventory valuation are performed timely and accurately follow BASF group guideline. To maintain and ensure the revision and year-end production planning budget are performed timely and accurately follow BASF group guideline. To maintain, review and ensure monthly cost center, cycle allocation and FICO reconciliation analysis are performed timely and accurately. Review and analyze of fixed assets, project accounting transactions and Non-Trade Business transactions To perform Month end closing and Year End closing activities timely and accurately. To perform analysis with comment on cost variations, non-distributed price differences for all material included in material ledger timely and accurately. To perform analysis with comment for Plan & Actual comparison for Fixed and Variable manufacturing cost and capacity utilization. To perform business analysis and provide advice to customer and controlling units on all matters regarding material ledger, inventory valuation, production planning budget, intra-company transfer pricing, cost center accounting. To trouble shoot and resolve issues related to Business reporting process by working with relevant stakeholders on the queries and issues. To act as business partner for BASF's internal customer to suggest and discuss workable solution with customers. To act as backup for Manager on daily task as and when required, and to perform other duties as and when assigned. Compliance: To participate in any compliance audits and respond to audit queries. To ensure all work documentation and process are updated to meet the Internal Control Framework on timely basis. To ensure compliance to BASF guidelines and policies, Financial Reporting Compliance requirements, local regulation and statutory requirements. Others: To contribute and support Business reporting Senior Manager, Manager, Regional Process Expert and Subject Matter Expert Lead in the implementation of project initiatives. To support Global Finance Transformation (GFT) initiatives in Business reporting team. To collaborate and support local counterpart team, service management team, regional and country controllers, plant personnel and other Finance service work streams. To drive in transition and migration projects, and support team strategic plan. To act as mentor to Senior Analysts and Analysts to encourage good practice, team spirit and a quality-orientated approach. Service Management: To perform tasks in accordance with the service management framework, by providing a timely and accurate service. To ensure customer queries are attended and resolved timely, responsible and proactive manner. To ensure all tasks are processed within agreed service timeline. Quality and Continuous Improvement: To identify, recommend and implement solutions in consultation with Senior Manager, Manager, Regional Process Expert, Subject Matter expert lead for continuous improvement opportunities. To support standardization across regions and globally by focusing on alignment of end to end business process areas, system and tools. Knowledge Management: To conduct functional training and contribute in training materials preparation in accordance with the established training framework To support in establishing a knowledge database, also of work problems encountered and steps for resolution to facilitate continuous learning, development and cross sharing of knowledge throughout Business reporting team. This is including to document any exceptions to the country's end to end process, carry out post mortem for each month end in preparation of Team or Departmental meeting. To ensure update changes to process documentation as and when required. To support new and existing team members through proper knowledge transfer; and sharing of best practice thereby ensuring quality is maintained through if staff transition. Professional guidance / multiple reporting lines: Assist to delegate work and assign tasks to others in team where requested Assist Manager in day to day supervisory tasks of team Provide guidance and support to the designated Business reporting team Assist managers in providing feedback and reinforcement to team members regarding development needs and opportunities, and secure resources to support development efforts Establishes good interpersonal relationships by helping team members feel valued, appreciated and aware of business performance issues. Assume Manager’s role in the absence of Manager Follow BASF Group guidelines; accounting and reporting guidelines Understand and follow local regulation and statutory requirements Frequent cooperation within BASF Group Counterparts and/or relevant stakeholders in BASF companies, internal auditors and the other BSSCKL functional teams Senior Managers, Managers, Analysts and peer team members Service Centre management team Global/Regional Finance Reporting functional units Frequent cooperation outside BASF Group Work closely with external consultants, auditors etc. Degree of Supervision Required in the job Work with minimal supervision M ini m um E d u cation and Q u alifica t ion R e q ui r ed for t he P osition Education: University degree in finance and accounting or business studies; and/or Chartered or Certified Professional Accountant or equivalent. Working Experience: Minimum 2 years’ experience in finance and accounting functions preferably in Business Reporting. Competent candidates with lesser years of experiences are considerable. Experience working in a MNC, shared service center or Malaysian Public listed company will be an advantage. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Knowledge and understanding of Business Reporting and other accounting functions. Knowledge and understanding of SAP system or other ERP system will be an advantage. Knowledge and experience of MS office tools. Proficiency in English and other languages as required with good communication skills. BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre was established in 2005. Located in Kuala Lumpur, the regional service hub has about 1,200 employees and growing. Focused on being a competency and digitalization driven hub, it also provides quality functional and know-how services in the areas of finance, controlling, human resources, real estate and facilities management, communications, environmental, health and safety, procurement, import-export order management, and information services to BASF companies and affiliates in the Asia Pacific region. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay that cover the Europe and Americas regions. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €68.9 billion in 2023. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the U.S. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Architect Data Products(M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Objective of the position Describe your Product Mission here / objective of the role: At our unit “Data Foundation - Big Data Management” we aim to offer organizations a robust and scalable solution for managing and deriving insights from vast quantities of data. By utilizing Azure's PaaS components, our platform streamlines the deployment and handling of Big Data workloads, empowering our clients to make data-driven decisions and propel business expansion. Our team is accountable for the creation and upkeep of the Big Data platform built on Azure PaaS components EDL (Enterprise Data Lake). We collaborate intensively with stakeholders to comprehend their needs and devise solutions that fulfill their expectations. The team is also in charge of guaranteeing the platform's scalability, dependability, and security, and for staying abreast with the newest technologies and trends in the big data domain About the Job: We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented candidate who will be responsible for all data engineering aspects of the data product layer of our Enterprise Datalake, working with Azure and Databricks, maintaining/developing our framework and CI/CD pipelines and processes in an ambitious team. Main Task Develop, extend and optimize the data product layer of our Enterprise Datalake with high code standards, tests and suitable documentation to meet both user and internal needs. Collaborate with the product owner and architect to understand data requirements and provide suitable solutions. Troubleshoot data and software related issues and optimize pipelines for performance and scalability. Work with Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory/Synapse keeping up to date with the latest features and suggest their implementation if beneficial. Demonstrate fluent communication skills in English (spoken and written) Job Requirements T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (mandatory) Experienc e in Agile way of working with a DevOps mindset In-depth know how with Lakehouse concepts , Databricks and Unity Catalog . jobs.name: Mitarbeiter Abwasseranlage (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Mitarbeiter Abwasseranlage (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Mitarbeiter Abwasseranlage (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Die globale Geschäftseinheit Oberflächentechnik des Unternehmensbereichs Coatings der BASF, die unter der Marke Chemetall operiert, ist ein führender Lieferant angewandter Oberflächentechnik für Metall-, Kunststoff- und Glassubstrate. Diese Spezialchemikalien werden in vielen Industrien und Märkten genutzt, wie zum Beispiel Automobil, Luftfahrt, Aluminiumveredelung, Coil oder in der metallverarbeitenden Industrie – unterstützt von einem großen Netzwerk technischer Experten. WAS SIE ERWARTET In Ihrer Rolle als Mitarbeiter Abwasseranlage (m/w/d) erwarten Sie folgende Aufgaben: Selbständiges Führen und Warten der chemisch-physikalischen Abwasserbehandlungsanlage, der VE-Wasseraufbereitungsanlage und der Umkehr-Osmoseanlage Ansetzung und Bereitstellen von Prozesschemikalien Unterstützung bei der Sonderabfallentsorgung Unterstützung in der Gebäude- und Labortechnik WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Chemisch-technische Ausbildung, z.B. als Chemikant/in, Klärwärter/in, Ver- und Entsorger/in mit dem Schwerpunkt Abwassertechnik oder vergleichbarer Berufsabschluss 2-3 Jahre Berufserfahrung Technisches Verständnis Selbständige, sorgfältige und sicherheitsorientierte Arbeitsweise im Team Gabelstaplerführerschein von Vorteil WAS WIR BIETEN Moderne und umweltfreundliche Verfahren, flache Hierarchien, kurze Entscheidungswege, flexible Arbeitszeiten, vielfältige und herausfordernde Aufgaben, attraktive Sozialleistungen und weltweit engagierte Kolleginnen und Kollegen Im Rahmen unseres Onboardings erhalten unsere neuen Mitarbeitenden eine individuelle, auf ihre Tätigkeit zugeschnittene Einarbeitung, unterstützt durch zahlreiche Schulungsangebote Ein familiäres Miteinander mit wertschätzendem, offenem und vertrauensvollem Umgang sind bei uns gelebte Werte und die Basis für unser gutes Betriebsklima SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Sie haben Fragen zum Ablauf oder zu der Stelle? Für nähere Informationen steht Ihnen Britta Schneider (People and Culture Business Partner, Chemetall GmbH), Tel.: +49 69/7165 -3061, gerne zur Verfügung. ÜBER UNS Das Portfolio besteht aus Lösungen zur Gewährleistung von Korrosionsschutz und Haftung von Beschichtungen, zum Verformen und Bearbeiten von Substraten, für Anwendungen zur Vorbereitung von Oberflächen für den Lackierprozess, sowie Dichtungsmassen für spezifische Anwendungen in der Luftfahrtindustrie. Chemetall hat den Hauptsitz in Frankfurt am Main, mit rund 2.500 Mitarbeitern weltweit und einem globalen Netzwerk an Vertriebs- und Produktionsstandorten. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Full-stack Developer (m/f/d)
jobs.description: ABOUT US At BASF Digital Hub Madrid we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. JOIN THE TEAM We are currently seeking a talented Full Stack Developer (JavaScript/Python/Django) to join our team in BASF's Agricultural Solutions department, Digital Strategy & Delivery. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, debugging, and maintaining innovative digital solutions for lab and study management. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure the successful launch and maintenance of high-quality digital products. We are looking for individuals with a diverse skill set and extensive knowledge of both front-end and back-end components. Our teams deliver digital solutions to a variety of user groups in the domains of regulatory affairs, stewardship, and public affairs related to agronomic products. The applications you will create, extend, and maintain contribute to our research and registration pipeline and support colleagues with scientific and governance backgrounds. You will develop backend services and user-facing features, work with data and databases, apply security best practices, and collaborate closely with DevOps to ensure reliable CI/CD, cloud deployments, and production readiness. RESPONSIBILITIES You will work closely with product stakeholders to understand their requirements, translate them into technical specifications, and estimate the time and resources needed for these work packages. You will be responsible for developing and maintaining digital products, by writing clean, efficient, and well-documented code, as well as participate in code reviews to maintain the code quality and adherence to best practices to be able to adapt the solutions to changing conditions. You will utilize Azure DevOps as the central development tool for managing code repositories and implementing Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines. Through continuous monitoring and testing you will ensure the quality, scalability, and performance of developed products, while you implement security measures and access controls to ensure confidentiality and compliance. You will be responsible for troubleshooting issues, continuously optimizing solutions for faster response times and stability, providing support in resolving any arising issues, and documenting development progress. You will be responsible for identifying, extracting, modelling, integrating, and presenting data from various internal and external sources. You actively participate and contribute to development communities at BASF, proactively sharing experiences and knowledge to foster a culture of innovation and transparency. In this role, your primary responsibility will be to develop and maintain the front-end and back-end components of digital products. You will work in agile digital teams (planning, standups, reviews, retrospectives) and communicate effectively with product, design, QA, and other stakeholders Since your stakeholders will come from different domains and backgrounds, clear and comprehensible communication tailored to each recipient will be an important aspect of your role. The digital solutions are design to support scientific communities, so your ability to understand basic biochemical concepts and Python for Chemistry (e.g., RDKit, SBML) will play a strong role here. REQUIREMENTS University degree in computer science. Minimum of 5 years of experience in Javascript/Python/Django. Solid knowledge of front-end development, with strong skills in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and experience with modern frameworks—particularly HTMX—and ideally one or more of the following: Vue.js, React, Svelte, or Angular. Leadership experience with agile organizations. Quality assurance and testing skills. IT infrastructure/system landscape. Data & function modelling. Data analysis techniques and data transfer formats. Analytical thinking and problem solving. Azure/AWS experience. Advanced proficiency in English, both written and verbal, is required. BENEFITS Responsibility from day one in a challenging work environment and "on-the-job" training as part of a committed team. Adequate compensation according to your qualifications and experience • A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holidays per year Another 5 days (readjustments days) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. CONTACT Do you have any questions about the application process or the position? Please reach out to Felipe Bianco (felipe.bianco@partners.basf.com)

jobs.name: Test Automation Engineer (M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Objective of the position: We are looking for a highly dynamic, motivated, and collaborative person to join our team. We are focused on the modernization of legacy systems and development of new capabilities of digital solutions in the Operational EHS domain. There is a growing global need within BASF to leverage digitalization to continue BASF’s journey of EHS excellence and continuously improving our capabilities especially through digitalization. In this role, you will work alongside a global development team to create and iteratively improve upon a global, internal full stack development solution for the occupational safety domain with a focus on an open source stack and a cloud-based platform. Role : As a test automation engineer for the Operational EHS Platform and Integration team, you will be part of a global, multidisciplinary team responsible for the development of an internal platform for daily use globally at BASF production facilities. You will develop programs that will test the accuracy and reliability of internally developed software solutions for each module during its initial development and on an ongoing basis, especially in advance of major updates/deployments. Drive automation coverage and quality for your modules (prioritization, maintainability, and reducing flaky tests). You will collaborate with members of the Operational EHS Solutions team to define test cases and ensure testing is comprehensive and accurate You will provide feedback to the full-stack developer team and provide recommendations for corrective approaches for any issues identified during the testing process. Own the test approach for your assigned modules, aligned with the globally defined strategy. Requirements: Edu c a t i o n Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, informatics, business informatics or in a comparable field. Work experience More than 5 + years as QA Automator for a large team More than 5 + years as Manual QA for a large team T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (mandatory) Experience in development and testing, with programming skills in JavaScript Experience in test automation UI / API test development, utilizing design patterns and automation framework such as Playwright, Selenium or similar . Experience in Manual testing and exploratory testing Solid understanding of QA methodologies Improve delivery speed by preventing defects and reducing rework through early testing and automation The ability to predict common user workflows in order to design effective tests. Experience in test design, performance testing, and configuration management T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (additional plus/nice to have skills) ISTQB Certifications Load testing knowledge (Locust, Jmeter , etc) Excellent issue isolation, troubleshooting, and debugging skills. Knowledge of DevOps tools for development, release, and monitoring Experience working in an agile scrum based team Experience learning independently and coaching others WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Production Engineer
jobs.description: Now hiring! Production Engineer Regina, SK (Onsite) We are looking for a Production Engineer to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Regina, SK. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. This position will be responsible for the effective planning, implementation, and control of assigned capital and expense projects, ensuring they are executed safely, on schedule, and within budget. The role provides technical support on engineering matters, including consistent review and approval of Management of Change (MOC) activities. It further supports coordination across production, maintenance, technical, capital investment, EHS, and quality functions. . As a Production Engineer, you create chemistry by... Leading capital and expense projects from scoping through execution, applying engineering standards, calculations, and technical specifications to deliver safe, compliant, on-time, and on-budget results. Managing project selection, implementation, and operational readiness by prioritizing requests, controlling budgets, schedules, contractors, change orders, reporting, and invoices, and supporting SOP updates for new installations or process changes. Coordinating production, construction, and outage activities with site leadership, production scheduling, and stakeholders to minimize disruption and maintain throughput, quality, and customer commitments. Supporting safe and reliable operations by troubleshooting process upsets, contributing to process safety and operational readiness reviews, and partnering with Quality Operations to translate requirements into compliant operating and testing practices. Leading engineering governance and compliance processes by owning Management of Change reviews, ensuring approvals before startup, and incorporating environmental requirements into designs and project execution. Maintaining safe, audit-ready engineering systems by supporting Responsible Care programs and keeping drawings and technical documentation, including PFDs, P&IDs, control narratives, and electrical single lines, current and accurate. Providing day-to-day leadership support by coaching engineering students, assigning and reviewing work, and offering feedback to support development and project delivery. Maintaining operational continuity and team alignment by providing production supervision coverage when needed and actively participating in production and engineering meetings to support communication and coordination. If you have... A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering from an accredited institution, preferably Chemical, Mechanical, or Industrial Systems Engineering. Registered membership of APEGS with P.Eng. designation, or Engineer in Training actively progressing toward P.Eng. licensure. 3–5 years of experience in manufacturing processes; chemical process experience or specialization considered an asset. Demonstrated experience in project management and contracting. Awareness of automation and control systems in a production environment (asset). proficiency in Microsoft Office; knowledge of SAP and CAD is an asset. Knowledge of applicable codes and regulations (CSA, API, ASME) and relevant government safety and environmental regulations. Effective verbal and written communication skills, strong organizational and planning abilities. Demonstrated ability to adapt to change and work effectively in dynamic or fluid situations. Ability to influence and manage without formal authority, fostering positive and collaborative working relationships. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF Adding value to our customers begins with adding value to you. you@BASF is the suite of benefits, perks, programs and unique opportunities we offer to support you – the whole you – in all stages of your life and career. With you@BASF, you create your own chemistry. The total rewards that you receive as a BASF employee go way beyond a paycheck. From competitive health and insurance plans to robust retirement benefits that include company-matching contributions, to making sure you never stop learning, we believe investing in you is investing in our success. Working for a large, global organization, you’ll have a chance to grow professionally and personally, expand your network and build a rewarding and dynamic career.  We also provide engaging learning and development opportunities to help you make the most of your talents and your role. About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2025 as one of Canada's Top Employers for eleventh consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the twelfth year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy Statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request personal or financial information of any kind via either text message or direct message on any social media platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never offer a candidate a position and request a cheque for equipment or any other type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment BASF is dedicated to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where our employees can be their authentic selves. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, place of origin, ethnic origin, colour, religion/creed, sex/pregnancy, marital status, family status, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law. We seek to make our recruitment process accessible for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations or support throughout the recruitment process, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com with the subject line “Recruitment Accommodation Request”.

jobs.name: Corporate Real Estate Manager (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in Agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming, and the biggest job on Earth. We are an internationally successful company of the BASF Group. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. With teams in the lab, field, office and in production, we do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Here you can gain more insight into our Vision at BASF Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We in Agricultural Solutions are preparing to be an independent, pure-play agricultural company within BASF group. To be ready for independence, we are looking for you to join us in our new Corporate roles (see here: build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In your new position, you define and implement the global real estate strategy of a leading agricultural solutions company with sales of nearly €10bn doing business in almost all countries worldwide, aligned with business and financial objectives. You lead portfolio planning and optimization, identify cost-saving and value-creation opportunities, establish governance, policies, and performance management across all real estate activities and in your role you own the real estate budget, planning, and reporting. Moreover, you oversee global transactions (leases, acquisitions, disposals) and capital projects while providing senior advisory and building as well as leading a lean global team scaling capabilities over time (transactions, data, asset management). You manage external service providers, drive the transition to an independent operating model and enhance digitalization as well as data transparency in real estate management. Last but not least, you act as a trusted partner to the CFO and senior leadership team supporting strategic initiatives (e.g. M&A, transformation). WHAT YOU OFFER successfully completed degree in Real Estate, Finance, Business, or related field 10+ years of experience in corporate real estate, transactions, or portfolio management, add-on: experience in agriculture / rural areas proven leadership experience in a global or multinational environment as well as strong expertise in real estate strategy, transactions, and financial analysis experience with outsourcing models and service provider management; exposure to transformation or IPO environments is a plus proven digital and AI focus with the ability to leverage modern technologies and data-driven approaches to enhance efficiency and quality excellent stakeholder management and negotiation skills WHAT WE OFFER A dynamic work environment that empowers you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and assume responsibility. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence, enabling us to work in a future‑oriented, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks for the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business). Learning opportunities for your professional and personal development. Career paths as professional development into other roles within the company. We actively support with childcare (e.g. company daycare, holiday programmes) and provide a fitness and health studio as well as a medical center with numerous offers for your health. We offer sustainable mobility benefits — a discounted Deutschlandticket for regional public transport, a job‑bike leasing program, and on‑site e‑charging stations for private electric vehicles to make your commute affordable, healthy, and climate‑friendly. HOW TO REACH US Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH is a reliable partner to the agricultural sector, offering innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to a wide range of crop systems. With the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, near the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you’ll find an attractive environment for both your professional life and your leisure time in the Rhine‑Neckar metropolitan region. You can learn more about the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof here . Find out more about our Agricultural Solutions team here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive) - Japanese Speaker
jobs.job_field: Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive) - Japanese Speaker
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Description: Objectives of the Position Managing and maintenance of all inbound and outbound Transportation management and documentation processes in support of assigned countries Archiving of all applicable documentation in accordance to company and regulatory guidelines Proactively escalate cases of deviations in transportation activities to bring shipment back on track in meeting defined and agreed delivery dates Meet defined service levels and any agreed qualitative requirements pertaining core transportation and documentation scope of work Support monitoring and evaluation of service provider’s performance referring to agreed KPIs and conduct periodic quality reviews Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Responsible on logistics fulfilment processes (including SAP TM maintenance and process adherence) for all import and export shipments by working closely with country import export team and hub-based overseas delivery services community Monitor, analyze, and manage operational issues where possible, and escalate to the appropriate country team, import/export stakeholders, or Team Lead for resolution and process improvement. Manage non-conformances (NCM), maintain issue logs, and ensure corrective actions are implemented where applicable. Coordinate and monitor BASF import and export shipments to ensure timely and accurate execution. Participating in system and new process flow testing where required. Initiate continuous improvement initiatives and issue resolution of supply chain issues pertaining inbound and outbound transportation management on behalf of assigned countries. Participation in import export community meetings / activities where required and support service provider management and service delivery performance in assigned countries from global services hub Other tasks and activities as assigned, relevant to the competency and skillset of this role and for development purposes Manage BASF import export shipment monitoring and coordination. Work closely with service providers and 3PL partners to ensure smooth import and export arrangements, timely customs clearance, and effective information flow. Act as the primary contact point for service providers and CSO, maintaining strong working relationships and ensuring timely updates on any related issues. Responsible to analyze issues and manage where possible, escalating to Team Lead for attention or improvements required Manage all non-trade Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders for related services. Requirements Education Degree / Diploma (with relevant work experience) in Logistics/Supply Chain/Business Management or related field Japanese language study or language proficiency certification preferred Working Experience 1-3 years in handling Import/Export logistics / order management & documentation, liaison with logistics service providers. Experience in SAP and chemical industry preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Efficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint and Word Knowledge in SAP system / S4Hana(Transport Management & Material Management Module) preferred Proactive team member who takes ownership and shows persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English and Japanese is necessity Job Description Footer: BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Senior Software Engineer (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Location Madrid WELCOME As one of the world's leading companies in this field, we develop and manufacture coatings for the automotive and industrial sectors. The success of our team is based on innovative solutions and close cooperation with our customers. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a Senior Software Engineer, you will design, build, and maintain scalable web applications while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality digital solutions aligned with business needs. jobs.name: Accountant Financial Reporting Italian (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Join our international Financial Reporting team and start your career as an Accountant. In this position you are responsible for the general ledger activities and act as an interface between different departments and our group headquarters. You work closely with our group companies and ensure that all accounting processes run smoothly. In doing so, you have the opportunity to continuously expand and apply your financial reporting expertise. In this role, you process business transactions in the general ledger and in asset & lease accounting, as well as reconciliation of sub-ledgers. Moreover, you prepare monthly, quarterly and annual financial statements according to IFRS and HGB/local GaaP standards for European BASF Group companies in a timely manner. You support income tax calculations and provide input to group reporting. In addition, you are the central contact person in accounting matters for our subsidiaries, other finance departments, central finance units of BASF as well as external auditors. You are involved in projects and participate in the development of tools and processes. Join us on our journey to implement SAP S/4HANA in the coming years. Together with your colleagues you actively contribute to our great team spirit and further development of the organization. WHAT YOU OFFER degree in business administration / apprenticeship with a focus on accounting or an equivalent degree good knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel and ideally experience with SAP R/3 and S/4HANA very good knowledge of English and Italian high motivation to achieve joint success in a team independent way of working and analytical thinking characterize your working style you are enthusiastic about automation and digitization of processes WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 40.000 - 46.000 €, depending on qualification and work experience. In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success as well as individual performance Structured onboarding including a buddy to help ease your transition into the BASF world Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Development discussions and programs so that you can reach your full potential Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - here a first impression HOW TO REACH US Jennifer Weiz (Talent Acquisition), jennifer.weiz@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56454 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. Y ou are welcome to book an online appointment . You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here and in our Q&A video ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our location-independent work models at basf.com/en/servicehub-berlin . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Ingenieur:in Automatisierung (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description:
Ingenieur:in Automatisierung (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profil-Login Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Ingenieur:in Automatisierung (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wenn Sie jemand sind, der andere inspiriert, Verantwortung für seine Wirkung übernimmt und das, was er tut, weiterentwickeln möchte, passen Sie perfekt zu uns. Bis zum 1. Juli gehörte unser Geschäft zur BASF. Im Rahmen der aktuellen Übergangsphase werden alle Stellenausschreibungen und der Bewerbungsprozess weiterhin auf der BASF-Plattform gehostet und daher vollständig im BASF-Branding angezeigt. Bitte lassen Sie sich davon nicht beirren: Alle Stellen sind Positionen bei Surventis und Ihre Bewerbung wird an uns übermittelt. Bei Surventis stellen wir die Kundinnen und Kunden in den Mittelpunkt, denken einen Schritt voraus und entwickeln Lösungen, die echten Mehrwert und nachhaltige Partnerschaften schaffen - we ge beyond the surface. Wir inspirieren einander, weiterzudenken, Verantwortung für unsere Arbeit zu übernehmen und ständig danach zu streben, das Mögliche zu erweitern. Wenn Sie Ihre ganze Persönlichkeit einbringen, das Offensichtliche hinterfragen und mit einem Team wachsen möchten, das es ernst meint – dann sind Sie hier richtig. WAS SIE ERWARTET In unserer Einheit Global Automation betreuen wir global unsere Standorte in allen Themen rund um die Prozessautomatisierung. Die aktive Unterstützung von der Projektidee bis hin Lebenszyklussupport zeichnen uns aus. Als Experten in der Automatisierung von Batchprozessen, der vertikalen und horizontalen Integration sowie der funktionalen Sicherheit gestalten wir die Zukunft unserer Produktionsstandorte. In Zusammenarbeit mit unseren Betrieben und Facheinheiten sowie durch eine Beobachtung des Marktes erarbeiten Sie globale Konzepte für die Digitalisierung und Automatisierung unserer Produktionsanlagen. Sie leiten und begleiten globale Automatisierungsprojekte von der Idee bis hin zur Inbetriebnahme und Optimierung. Sie sind das zentrale Bindeglied zwischen dem Asset Team, Engineering und den Auftraggebern. Das breite Tätigkeitsspektrum erstreckt sich vom Projektmanagement bis hin zur Detailprogrammierung auf DCS, SIS und MES-Ebene. Im Rahmen Ihrer Tätigkeit führen Sie zeitlich begrenzte Einsätze im Ausland durch. Global unterstützen Sie unsere Standorte zu Fragen rund um die Themen Automation Security und Funktionale Sicherheit. Sie bringen sich aktiv in Veränderungsprozesse für den nachhaltigen wirtschaftlichen Erfolg des Unternehmens ein. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN überdurchschnittlich abgeschlossenes Masterstudium im Bereich der Elektrotechnik (Bereich Prozessautomatisierung) oder vergleichbare Ausbildung mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im internationalen Umfeld mit dem Schwerpunkt Automatisierung und Digitalisierung im Produktionsumfeld fundierte Kenntnisse zu MES und Automatisierungssystemen (Siemens PCS7, Simatic Batch, ABB 800xA, 800xA Batch, Hima HIMatrix), Automation Security, Netzwerktopologie und Administration Freude an einer Schnittstellenfunktion in einem innovativen und dynamischen Unternehmensumfeld sowie Begeisterung für neue Herausforderungen wirkungsvolle und überzeugende Kommunikation auf Deutsch und Englisch sowie ein hohes Maß an Eigenverantwortung WAS WIR BIETEN Die Attraktivität unserer außertariflichen Gehälter stellen wir durch regelmäßige Benchmarks sicher. Über den Bonus können die Benefits individuell gestaltet werden. Eine firmenfinanzierte Unfall- und Pflegezusatzversicherung sowie eine attraktive Altersvorsorge bieten eine umfassende Absicherung. An unserem Standort in Münster gibt es ein betriebseigenes Fitness-Studio „Freizeit“ mit angeschlossener Physiotherapie-Praxis, festangestellte Werkärzte, ein Betriebsrestaurant „Auszeit“ inklusive einer Café-Bar, eine bilinguale Betriebs-KiTa „LackiKids‟ sowie ein Ferienprogramm „LackiKids on Tour“. Für die fachliche sowie persönliche Weiterentwicklung bieten wir vielfältige lokale und globale Entwicklungs- und Karrieremöglichkeiten. Surventis steht für Vielfalt – wir sind stolz auf unsere Mitarbeiternetzwerke im Bereich Coatings: WomenInCoatings, Color Pride und CultiConnect. Die Feedback-Kultur ist fest in unserer Unternehmenskultur integriert z.B. durch regelmäßige Mitarbeiterbefragungen sowie Führungsfeedback. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Bekim Krasniqi (Talent Acquisition), bekim.krasniqi@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56136 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. ÜBER UNS Mehr über unser Unternehmen erfahren Sie hier. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Solution Consultant System Integration (EDI, Purchase-to-Pay) (M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Objectives : BASF’s Business Process Integration (BPI) team provides and supports digital solutions for B2B integrations with external partners through a variety of systems and processes within the BASF’s architectural landscape.  BPI is considered a strategic tool to digitalize processes and enable seamless exchange of data between applications, businesses, customers and partners in real-time. In this role, you will focus on consulting representatives of BASF businesses and BASF partners on integrating business processes primarily in the Purchase-to-Pay process area, including respective mappings. Requirements : The Solution Consultant provides customized integration solutions for internal partners and external partners via EDI, API, Micro Services, AI Agents, and similar technologies. The Solution Consultant supports our internal (SAP ERP and Satellite Systems) and external partners to specify their requirements and translates those into best-in-class solution designs based on technical and business process knowledge, aligned with process design and architecture. The consultant will be responsible for supplemental tasks in the daily operation, e.g. operational support (2nd/3rd level), issue resolving, quality assurance, and business interaction. The role does also include assignments to support comprehensive business integration projects. The Solution Consultant works in a global, international setting, interacting with team members in all other regions to provide sustainable digital solutions for our partners. Additional work aspects: strategic contribution to IT architecture and cross-process digitalization efforts, interact with connectivity solutions by supporting API, micro services, generative AI and other industries relevant technical developments. The business processes in scope of this role are: Purchase-to-Pay Edu c a t i o n Master's degree in computer science, business or STEM major with 4-6 years of IT or related field experience OR Bachelor's degree in computer science, business or STEM major with 6-8 years of IT or related field experience OR Diploma or comparable training with 6+ years of professional experience in IT and/or business areas Work Experience Minimum 4 years related working experience Experiencing Agile way of working or agile implementation approach Work experience in an international company is a plus T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (mandatory and nice to have) Good business knowledge in various application and process areas, esp. Purchase to Pay (Purchase Orders, Confirmations, Goods Receipts, Migo Movements, Invoices). Consult and support internal and external partners when dealing with complex processes and IT application questions. Conducts and support the technical implementation of the B2B Integration to external partners Showing strong transactional experience in SAP R3 and/or S4/HANA (MM Procurement - SD ) Ability to work and navigate in SAP middleware platforms (PO, CPI Integration Suite) Provides a deep technical understanding of standard connectivity messages EDI-APIs and programming protocols (supporting e.g. iDocs , XML, EDIFACT, JSON, UI etc.) Consult and support IT analysis (process and/or data), mentoring, modelling , solution design and optimization of related business processes and models Execute studies in preliminary stages of projects, including evaluation of existing or new solutions Ability to support strategic planning and realization Can assume roles as project manager and related tasks Coordinate and execute prototyping, configuration and testing of solutions based on BASF requirements, while ensuring quality and security aspects Provide structured acceptance, analysis and description of solutions and/or concepts Support and/or steer the formulation of more complex costs estimates for efforts spend solution design, testing and implementation of business solutions Provides Business support and training (processes, tools, reports, etc.) where required Provide guidance and structure to enable day2day operational support incl , advising external support resources Extensive knowledge of standard connectivity messages, related business processes and applications Familiar with Azure DevOps (or GitHub or similar). Personal traits : You can demonstrate fluent communication skills in English (spoken and written) Well-developed sense of teamwork in an international team with strong interpersonal, written and verbal communication and conflict resolution skills. Business consulting skill with a focus on value generation Ambitious, proactive & entrepreneurial character Highly developed sense of responsibility High customer- and innovation-orientation Time flexibility to work across different time zones Nice-to-Have : Experiences as project manager Experiences in strategic planning and realization Experiences in AI Agents and AI based communication Certifications in Agile, SAP S/4 WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Officer Accounts Payable Team Intercompany (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Accounts Payable and Freight Cost Management Department provides professional invoice management for all affiliated European BASF Group companies. It cooperates with the procurement and local finance departments, actively involved in the creation of an optimized creditor process chain. You are responsible for accounting activities such as checking and posting invoices, clarifying accounts as well as verifying payment runs for our BASF Group Companies. You will be in charge of analyzing automatically booked invoices as well as of advancing the automation of invoice processing. In the optimization of the purchase-to-pay process, you will take an active part and work in close cooperation with the purchasing and finance departments of the BASF Group. WHAT YOU OFFER commercial business training or a degree in Business Administration first experience in accounting and projects communicative team player who is open to changes first experience with SAP fluent in English, German is a plus WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 – 38.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience - In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Team and company events in which we celebrate our achievements and live up our team spirit Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - Here a first impression Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks HOW TO REACH US Viktoria Pohl (Talent Acquisition), viktoria.pohl@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-58390 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team here . First information about our application process can be found here . ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

Responsible for follow up on audit activities. Consulted on risk management process.
