jobs.name: Chemical Plant Operator
jobs.description: Now hiring! Chemical Plant Operator Wyandotte, MI We are looking for a Chemical Plant Operator  to join our Performance Materials team in Wyandotte, MI. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction. These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. As a Chemical Plant Operator for our Wyandotte Polyol Plant, you will be responsible for all aspects of personal safety, product quality, and operating all aspects of the reactors, as well as, filling, loading, and unloading systems and their auxiliary and utility equipment. During your 12-hour rotating shift as a Chemical Plant Operator, you will Operate DCS and PLS systems, as well as environmental control systems (such as the pond, scrubber, or thermal oxidizer) Read, record, and interpret data of different industrial equipment (reactors, tanks, pumps, etc.) and make corrective adjustments as required to keep efficient production on reaction trains. Perform maintenance, i.e., purges, washes, dries, and recharges equipment as conducting visual inspection of operating equipment to  current operating statusdetermine Work from both verbal & written instructions of Shift Supervisor, standard procedures, batch sheet & loading sheets to operate reactors, as well as filling & loading systems Participate in miscellaneous duties including painting, cleanouts, assistance to plant during outages, and maintain operating condition of key equipment **Physical demands: The Chemical Plant Operator will have the ability to work for prolonged periods, on outdoor settings most of the time, while wearing proper PPE. The position works with high activity including standing, walking, climbing ladders/stairs and lifting up to 50 lbs., with reasonable accommodation if needed. If you... Have a High School Diploma/GED Have 6 months of experience in a manufacturing or production environment Are able to maintain facial hair to comply with site respirator policy Are able to work indoors/outdoors in all weather conditions This position is part of a job family with multiple levels.  The successful candidate will be placed into the appropriate grade level commensurate with their qualifications. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Main Tasks : Ensuring Compliance with Security and Regulatory Requirements: Monitor and assess adherence to legal, regulatory, and internal requirements in information security and data protection. Conducting Audits and Assessments: Plan, coordinate, and execute internal and external audits as well as risk and compliance assessments to identify vulnerabilities and areas for improvement. Consulting and Training: Support and raise awareness among business units and employees on compliance and security topics; conduct awareness trainings. Risk Management: Identify, assess, and track risks related to information security and compliance; develop and implement risk mitigation measures. Reporting and Communication: Prepare compliance status reports and communicate results and recommendations to management and relevant stakeholders. Collaboration with Internal and External Partners: Work closely with IT, data protection, legal, internal audit, as well as external auditors and authorities. Preparation and Support of Certifications: Assist in the preparation and execution of certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, TISAX) and ensure ongoing compliance with requirements. Continuous Improvement: Analyze incidents, derive lessons learned, and continuously enhance compliance and security processes. Minimum Education and Qualification Requirements for the Position : Degree in IT, business informatics, engineering, or a comparable qualification. Several years of professional experience in information security, compliance, audit, or risk management. Knowledge of relevant standards and legal requirements (e.g., ISO 27001, GDPR, NIS2). Analytical thinking, strong communication skills, and assertiveness. Certifications such as CISA, CISM, CISSP, or ISO 27001 Lead Auditor are an advantage. Soft Skills: Exceptional collaboration and interpersonal skills with a proven ability to foster cooperation and empower a diverse team. Strong strategic thinking and problem-solving capabilities. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, facilitating effective collaboration with diverse stakeholder groups at all levels. Tools and Technology Skills : Proficient in GRC tools for managing governance, risk, and compliance processes, ensuring effective integration and reporting. Familiarity with security frameworks and compliance standards such as ISO 27001, NIS2, and the Cyber Resilience Act, etc., to ensure proper alignment with regulatory requirements. Proficiency in utilizing collaboration tools such as M365 and SharePoint to streamline communication and documentation within teams. WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

Translate complex business requirements into robust functional concepts and guide stakeholders in process optimization. Conduct fit ‑ gap analyses and provide recommendations for process harmonization and simplification across regions. Ensure legal and policy compliance related to working time, accruals, absences, and data retention. Technical Solution Ownership - Design, configure, and maintain SAP S/4HANA Time Management (PT) , including schemas, rules, time valuation, work schedules, and master data dependencies. Implement technical changes and coordinate development activities with external and internal teams. Evaluate, test, and deploy SAP Notes, transport requests, and configuration adjustments. Oversee system integrations (Payroll, PA/OM, BTP, external workforce onboarding, interfaces). Operational Excellence & DevOps - Manage day ‑ to ‑ day application operations, troubleshoot issues, and ensure system stability. Conduct thorough testing, regression analysis, and quality assurance for changes, upgrades, and releases. Drive continuous improvement initiatives, such as data cleanup, process automation, and S/4HANA simplification adoption. Prepare documentation, guidelines, and training materials for users and stakeholders. Functional Requirements - Strong understanding of HR Time Management processes , including time valuation, working time regulations, absence management, time quotas, and country-specific compliance especially for European Country. Experience advising HR stakeholders and translating technical capabilities into actionable business solutions. Ability to model end ‑ to ‑ end processes and identify optimization and harmonization opportunities. Technical Requirements - Solid knowledge of SAP S/4HANA (ideally H4S4) and the underlying ABAP Platform , Fiori applications, and HANA concepts. Hands ‑ on configuration experience in time schemas, PCRs, valuation rules , and PT-related tables. Understanding of cross ‑ module integration with Payroll, OM, PA, and external systems. Experience with technical troubleshooting, debugging, testing, and transport handling. General Skills - Strong analytical capabilities and structured working style. Ability to communicate clearly and confidently with both IT and business audiences. Proactive mindset, ownership mentality, and willingness to drive topics independently. Fluency in English WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. jobs.name: Coordinador(a) de Customer Service Detalles del puesto
jobs.description:
Coordinador(a) de Customer Service Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Encuentra trabajos Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Ingreso para empleados Descripción del puesto Coordinador(a) de Customer Service LOCACIÓN COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE POSICIÓN ID DE TRABAJO MODALIDAD DE TRABAJO LOCACIÓN COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE POSICIÓN ID DE TRABAJO MODALIDAD DE TRABAJO Enviar candidatura ahora En BASF, somos una empresa que otorga igualdad de oportunidades. Todas las candidaturas cualificadas que cubran el perfil para una posición se considerarán para nuestras vacantes sin distinción de edad, ciudadanía, tono de piel, religión, sexo, estado civil, nacionalidad, discapacidad, identidad o expresión de género, orientación sexual,  origen étnico, lengua, identidad indígena, apariencia física, condiciones de salud, características genéticas, embarazo, religión, opiniones políticas, académicas o filosóficas, identidad o filiación política, por su forma de pensar, vestir, actuar, gesticular, por tener tatuajes o perforaciones corporales o cualquier otra condición análoga protegida por la ley del país correspondiente. En BASF, estamos comprometidos con la inclusión, proporcionando siempre que sea posible, ajustes razonables a las personas con discapacidad o con alguna necesidad específica, para garantizar su acceso y el uso de servicios. En caso de requerir algún apoyo, favor de comunicarlo durante tu primer contacto con Atracción de Talento. Estamos buscando un(a) Coordinador(a) de Customer Service. El objetivo de la posición será coordinar y liderar al equipo de Customer Service Representatives, fortaleciendo las relaciones con clientes bajo la estrategia Customer First para comprender sus necesidades y gestionar internamente respuestas efectivas; actuando como enlace clave con Ventas y Operaciones para asegurar soluciones integrales que impulsen la satisfacción del cliente y el crecimiento del negocio. Adicionalmente, será responsable de administrar la estrategia de comercio electrónico de BASF Mexicana, alineada a las directrices regionales y corporativas, garantizando competitividad en el mercado y el cumplimiento de los objetivos comerciales. Asimismo, gestionará clientes con acuerdos globales, asegurando una atención estratégica que contribuya directamente al crecimiento de las ventas totales del negocio. En este rol estarás a cargo de: Coordinar y dirigir el proceso de atención a clientes, asegurando el cumplimiento de las business rules y políticas de calidad, para atender de manera eficiente los requerimientos de clientes internos y externos. Gestionar la estrategia de recepción de materiales a clientes mediante SAP, de acuerdo con el mapeo de Proceso General de atención a clientes e instructivos del área. Identificar e implementar áreas de oportunidad con procesos de mejora continua, interactuando directamente con el área de Cadena de Suministro, para optimizar la operación y asegurar entregas en tiempo y forma. Diseñar, junto con el área de Ventas, la estrategia de visitas a clientes, con el objetivo de identificar nuevas oportunidades de negocio que contribuyan al crecimiento comercial. Asegurar la implementación de herramientas y metodologías de mejora continua para la resolución de no conformidades, evitando recurrencias y elevando la satisfacción del cliente. Garantizar la participación en proyectos de la Dirección de Administración y Finanzas, así como en iniciativas estratégicas del equipo de Customer Service. Si cuentas con: Formación académica en Administración, Negocios Internacionales, Logística, Ingeniería Industrial, Química o carreras afines. Experiencia sólida en Customer Care, con conocimiento integral de la cadena de suministro, incluyendo gestión de inventarios, procesos de forecast y liderazgo de equipos. Dominio de SAP aplicado a procesos de Customer Care y Supply Chain. Nivel avanzado de inglés. ¡Esta vacante es para ti! Crea tu propia química: Lo que te ofrecemos ... Agregar valor a nuestros clientes comienza por agregarte valor a ti. you@BASF es el paquete de beneficios, desarrollo de carrera, ambiente de trabajo y compensación que ofrecemos para apoyarte en todas las etapas de tu vida y carrera. Las recompensas totales que recibes como empleado de BASF van más allá de tu pago. Desde planes competitivos de salud y seguros, hasta planes de pensiones robustos, creemos que invertir en ti es invertir en nuestro éxito. Trabajando para una gran organización global, tendrás la oportunidad de crecer profesional y personalmente, expandir tu red y construir una carrera profesional gratificante y dinámica. BASF brinda oportunidades interesantes y retadoras para ayudarte a aprovechar y explotar tu talento. Enviar candidatura ahora Nuestra oferta total única: you@BASF En BASF tienes más que una remuneración, nuestra oferta total de beneficios incluye una gran variedad de recursos que te acompañará en tu desarrollo profesional y personal, por eso lo llamamos you@BASF. Para más información click aqui. jobs.name: Yard Management - SAP TM Functional Consultant (M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. Roles and Responsibilities : We are looking for a seasoned SAP Transportation Management (TM) Functional Consultant with deep expertise in S/4HANA TM, a solid understanding of end-to-end supply chain logistics, and proven delivery experience in the chemical industry. The successful candidate will lead the design, configuration, drive cross-functional alignment, act as a bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams and optimise our Yard Management solutions which have a deep integration with TM. A strong background in SAP R/3 to S/4HANA migration projects is required. Functional knowledge : Strong understanding of end-to-end transportation and logistics processes: freight order management, carrier selection, route planning, yard management, and freight settlement, milestone tracking, gate management, and inbound/outbound logistics handoffs. Proficiency in freight order lifecycle management, subcontracting, execution monitoring, and event management (milestones, status updates). Solid knowledge of SAP modules interfacing with TM: MM (Materials Management), EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), SD (Sales & Distribution), and FI/CO. Ability to translate complex business requirements into scalable and maintainable technical solutions. Ability to drive and facilitate cross-team technical discussions, aligning stakeholders from TM, EWM, Manufacturing, Core Systems, and business teams towards a common solution design. Understanding of multi-ERP integration scenarios Familiarity with BASF-relevant logistics processes in the chemical industry: bulk transport, hazardous materials, inbound/outbound logistics Experience supporting User Acceptance Testing (UAT), functional testing, and hypercare phases post go-live. Ability to drive standardisation and reduce non-standard developments by leveraging SAP standard functionalities. Apply a global mindset with a strong focus on reusability, standardisation, and harmonisation when designing and developing new solutions – ensuring scalability across BASF’s global landscape. Technical Knowledge : Strong understanding of ABAP development, including object-oriented ABAP (OO-ABAP). Deep knowledge of API/event-based architecture: Kafka, SAP API Management, SAP Integration Suite (CPI), and event-driven design patterns for real-time logistics data flows Familiarity in SAP S/4HANA migration tools and methodologies (SUM, SPDD/SPAU, custom code adaptation, Simplification Item checks). Familiarity with SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) Relevant Professional experience : Overall, we are looking for an SAP TM Functional Consultant with +4 years of IT industry experience in SAP software implementation with increasing levels of responsibility. We require: Proven track record in at least one full-cycle SAP TM implementation or major upgrade project. Demonstrated experience in SAP R/3 to S/4HANA migration projects Experience working in global, multicultural, and geographically distributed teams, ideally in a shared services or digital hub environment. Background in the chemical, manufacturing, or process industry is a strong advantage, with exposure to multi-site logistics models. Experience delivering IT solutions in an agile product model, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and DevOps practices. Hands-on involvement in system integration projects with third-party logistics providers (3PLs), carriers, or external platforms. Proven experience in driving and coordinating System Integration Testing (SIT) across multiple workstreams, including test planning, defect management, and sign-off in complex multi-system landscapes. Experience with application lifecycle management, change/release management, and quality assurance processes. Experience running IT solutions as products (product ownership mindset) and contributing to continuous improvement initiatives. Relevant Education : Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or a related field from an accredited university. Advanced English skills – written and verbal; additional European languages are a plus. Growth mindset with a genuine curiosity to learn, adapt, and improve in a fast-evolving technology landscape. Strong team player with excellent interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Ability to work efficiently under pressure on non-routine and highly complex tasks. Comfortable working in a multicultural environment across multiple time zones. Nice to Have : Experience with additional programming languages such as Java, Python, or JavaScript. Knowledge of Jira and/or Microsoft Azure DevOps for project and backlog management. SAP certification in TM, S/4HANA Logistics, or related areas. Experience with SAP Yard Logistics (YL) as a complement to TM. Familiarity with core lean practices and IT product management methodologies. jobs.name: Integration Platform Engineer (M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Join our “Data Foundation” team and work on the cutting-edge “Integration” product that leverages innovative technologies to seamlessly connect applications and facilitate data exchange. The Integration Platform is a versatile framework for building and executing integration scenarios—serving both internal and external needs. With the platform and its underlying technologies, you’ll have access to advanced tools and processes designed to connect to any type of data, in any format you require. As part of our mission, you’ll help implement Enterprise Integration Concept capabilities across Data Integration, Thing Integration, Process Integration, and User Integration. If you’re passionate about driving digital transformation and want to make a real impact in Hyderabad, this is your opportunity to be at the forefront of integration technology. Be part of building the future of integration platform in Hyderabad! As an Integration Platform Engineer, you will play a key role in shaping a self-service, governed, and highly visible integration lifecycle for APIs, events, and workflows. Your expertise will help design and develop essential backend services that drive orchestration, governance, discovery, and automation—leveraging the power of Kubernetes. Join us to create robust solutions that accelerate digital transformation and make a real impact on global business operations. Requirements : Create lifecycle workflows with Argo (or Camunda) and connect them to backend services and Kafka events. Include governance and approval steps, and guarantee that processes are auditable and traceable. Develop and deliver platform services using Spring Boot, ensuring robust and scalable solutions for our Hyderabad team. Create and maintain REST APIs to support registry, marketplace backend, lifecycle management, and governance processes. Integrate and manage PostgreSQL databases, optimising performance and reliability for business-critical applications. Implement automation workflows utilising Argo or Camunda to streamline and enhance operational efficiency. Produce and consume Kafka events, enabling seamless communication and data exchange across platform components. Experience in large-scale enterprise environments is highly desirable. Technical & Professional Knowledge Mandatory expertise : Experience with setting up and operating Argo Workflows (or Camunda) in Kubernetes (k8s) Integration of systems using REST APIs Kubernetes (application operation) Knative Knowledge of Kafka Traefik Ingress Securing Microservices Azure Key Vault cert-manager Nice to have : Familiarity with BPMN Expertise in Software Development : Programming Languages and Frameworks: Extensive hands-on experience in Java and Spring Boot, with a robust understanding of building scalable and maintainable backend systems. API Design and Integration: Demonstrated ability to design, develop, and maintain RESTful APIs, ensuring secure and efficient data exchange between services and client applications. Database Management: Proficient in PostgreSQL, including schema design, query optimisation, and integration with enterprise applications. Containerisation and Orchestration: Strong working knowledge of Kubernetes at the application level, including deployment, scaling, and management of containerised microservices. Practical experience with Knative for managing serverless workloads on Kubernetes clusters. Streaming and Messaging: Hands-on expertise with Kafka at the application level, enabling reliable data streaming and event-driven architectures within distributed environments. Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment: Track record of designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines to automate testing, integration, and deployment processes, ensuring rapid and reliable software delivery. Language Proficiency : Strong command of English, both spoken and written WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities : You are part of a global leadership team who is ensuring BASF ‘s business continuity by providing stable platforms for the very large global SAP landscape of BASF group based on innovative technologies and state of the art operation for a leading chemical company. We are in the middle of our S/4 transformation project and are ramping up a significant team in our new Digital Hub in Hyderabad, India. This position offers the opportunity to build something new and become a cornerstone of the long ‑ term operational backbone of our global S/4 landscape. Now is the time to take a new step: Own it. Drive it. Excel in it. Drive continuous improvement of S/4 operational processes— leveraging AI where beneficial—ensuring harmonization, lean practices, and structured product lifecycle management with a strong focus on radical standardization. Develop internal expertise to ensure long-term operational excellence across the S/4 solution landscape. Contribute to the organizational development of our broader team and be an active member of a forward ‑ looking leadership team. While we have a focus on SAP Authorization, we are very much welcoming candidate with a broader SAP experience, mainly with Test & release management , deployment & SAP development management and cross SAP services . Here are some of your key responsibilities, which can be applied to SAP cross topics beyond authorizations: Global SAP Authorization Ownership: Leads SAP authorization implementation and support including delivery of SAP Authorizations for projects across BASF and serves as the primary contact for audits assessments based on Corporate Guidelines. You drive innovation, automation and leverage AI capabilities in day-to-day operations. Leadership of a Distributed Global Team: Leads a worldwide SAP Authorization product team and steers external global service providers to ensure efficient, high quality service delivery. Owns budgeting, resource planning, team development, goal setting, and performance management aligned with corporate standards and strategic transformation goals. End to End DevOps Accountability for SAP Authorizations: Drives all DevOps activities based on agile methodologies for continuous operational improvement of SAP Authorization concepts and the related implementations. Qualifications : Completed university degree in Business Informatics, Business Administration or Computer Science with several years of professional experience Several years of professional experience in various areas in the IT/digital environment, including SAP, as well as experience in application projects Practiced leadership skills (orchestration and management of cross-functional topics), entrepreneurial thinking and organizational awareness Ability to critically question the status quo and openness for new trends and innovations Strong communication skills, effective communication and very good written and spoken English skills Good conflict management skills, high customer orientation and empathy when dealing with stakeholders across the organization WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Senior Agentic AI Engineer (M/F/D)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities: Engineer Core Components: Lead the development of the orchestration service and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) bus, writing high-performance Python code that routes thousands of requests per minute. Build Complex Agents: Implement "Horizon 3/4" autonomous agents (e.g., self-correcting coding agents, complex supply chain optimizers) using LangGraph or AutoGen hosted on AKS. Integrate Data & AI: Build robust integrations between Databricks (Unity Catalog) and our agent runtime, ensuring agents have access to real-time, governed business data. Optimize Performance: Tackle the hard problems of agentic AI—latency reduction, token cost optimization, and managing distributed state across long-running workflows. Mentor & Review: Conduct code reviews for junior engineers and divisional teams, ensuring that all agent code adheres to our Zero-Trust security practices and software engineering standards. Develop Tools (MCP): Build and maintain a library of Model Context Protocol (MCP) compliant tools that allow agents to interact with SAP, ServiceNow, and other enterprise APIs. Qualifications: Engineering Mastery: You are a software engineer first. You write clean, testable, production-grade Python (FastAPI, Pydantic) and are comfortable with async/await patterns and concurrency. Container Native: You don't just run Docker locally; you have experience deploying and debugging complex applications on Kubernetes (AKS). Agentic Frameworks: Deep, hands-on experience with LangChain, LangGraph, or Semantic Kernel. You know how to debug a "looping" agent and how to manage conversation memory effectively. Azure & Databricks: Practical experience with Azure AI Foundry for model serving and Databricks for data engineering. Problem Solver: You enjoy debugging non-deterministic systems and finding creative ways to make LLMs reliable (e.g., retry logic, output parsing, verification steps). Years of experience: 5 WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. jobs.name: Agentic AI Architect (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities: Architect the Agent Backbone: Define and maintain the enterprise reference architecture for autonomous agents, ensuring seamless interoperability between managed services (Azure AI Foundry) and custom open-source runtimes on AKS. Define Agentic Standards: Own the technical standards for the ecosystem, including the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool interoperability and the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication protocol. Design the Orchestration Layer: Lead the architectural design of our central semantic router hosted on AKS, that interprets user intent and orchestrates workflows across distributed, cross-platform agents. Enable Hybrid Innovation: Create "Golden Path" blueprints that leverage Databricks (Unity Catalog) for massive-scale data context while utilizing AKS to host complex, stateful agent swarms using frameworks like LangGraph or AutoGen. Drive Co-Creation & Governance: Partner with other platform teams (SAP, Salesforce) to co-architect complex cross-domain workflows (Tier 3-4) and serve as the primary technical authority for validating high-risk agent deployments. Hybrid Infrastructure Expert: You are fluent in Azure AI Foundry for governance and model management, but you are equally comfortable architecting custom solutions on AKS to run open-source frameworks (LangChain/LangGraph) when managed services fall short. Data-Driven Architecture: Strong experience with Databricks, understanding how to expose complex data pipelines as "Tools" for agents and how to manage agent memory/state at scale. API & Protocol Design: Proven ability to design robust, event-driven architectures (CloudEvents, Kafka) and standard APIs (FastAPI, GraphQL, MCP) for distributed systems. Strategic Leadership: You can communicate complex architectural decisions to stakeholders and mentor senior engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence. Tech Stack You Will Master: Orchestration: LangGraph, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, Azure AI Foundry. Compute & Runtime: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Docker, Azure Functions. Data & Memory: Databricks (Unity Catalog), Vector Databases (Qdrant/Pinecone), Redis. Languages: Python (Expert), TypeScript (Proficient). Standards: Model Context Protocol (MCP), OpenTelemetry. The team will be based in Hyderabad, India, and will form part of our new digital hub launching this year. Its mission: to take over SAP S/4HANA topics transitioning from project mode into operations across multiple business areas—Finance, Procurement, Order Management, Planning, Manufacturing, Quality Management, Warehousing, SAP Business Data Cloud, and more. The team will work in an agile setup, closely connected to our global product organizations and to the ongoing global S/4 transformation program. We are looking for a Product Manager who will shape and lead this new operational area from day one, starting no later than July 1, 2026. In this role, you will manage product activities as S/4 project waves enter steady-state operations, ensure value-driven and reliable delivery, and collaborate with stakeholders and peers across domains and regions. Drive continuous improvement of S/4 operational processes—leveraging AI where beneficial—ensuring harmonization, lean practices, and structured product lifecycle management with a strong focus on radical standardization. Develop internal expertise to ensure long-term operational excellence across the S/4 solution landscape. Contribute to the organizational development of our broader product families and be an active member of a forward‑looking leadership team. Qualifications : Proven experience in S/4HANA application operations, ideally including leadership responsibility in a similar enterprise environment. Strong servant leadership qualities: visionary thinking, motivational ability, and a focus on developing talent. A user‑centric, agile, and open mindset, with hands-on experience in agile delivery models. A solution-oriented approach and solid understanding of core S/4 AMS technical processes. Excellent communication skills in English and a high level of enthusiasm and engagement. jobs.name: Executive, Import Export Malaysia
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position: Ensure effective execution of inbound and outbound transportation (Import & Export) including documentation and system (SAP TM) processes Maintain accurate reporting, cost control, and compliance with company and regulatory requirements Drive timely issue resolution, escalation, and continuous improvement across logistics operations Support monitoring of service provider performance against defined KPIs Main Tasks: 1. Operations Management Manage end-to-end Import & Export shipment processes (Air, LCL, FCL, ISO, tanker, parcel tanker) Coordinate shipment arrangements with LLP, LSP, warehouse, PPC, and stakeholders Prepare and validate shipping documentation and ensure proper system updates (SAP TM) Handle MDB block release and shipment readiness validation Troubleshoot system or operational issues impacting shipment execution 2. Reporting & Analytics Prepare and manage operational reports including: LLP Rework Report LCL/Air KPI Report TCO Report Demurrage & Detention Report Freight Cost Clean-up (weekly & monthly) FCV bi-weekly updates Validate data with LLP/LSP and ensure submission within defined timelines Analyze deviations and drive corrective actions 3. Cost Control & Billing Verify freight invoices and resolve discrepancies with FCV and LSP Manage trailer detention and demurrage/detention validation and approval Perform freight cost clean-up and ensure closure of open items Support PR issuance based on PO balance review and operational requirements 4. Claim & Issue Management Manage Claim Billing and Non-Conformance Management (NCM) Act as coordinator for insurance claims involving Legal, warehouse, and service providers Investigate root causes and implement corrective actions for operational issues Maintain issue logs and ensure closure of all reported cases 5. Spot & Rate Management Manage spot rate requests (FCL, LCL, Air, Flexi, ISO) when not covered under contracts Ensure proper approval prior to shipment execution Maintain spot rates and supporting documentation in systems and SharePoint 6. Compliance & Audit Perform health checks on operations and documentation compliance Ensure all supporting documents are properly archived (SharePoint / TM / systems) Support internal and external audit requirements Ensure compliance with VGM (SOLAS) and other regulatory requirements 7. Knowledge & Process Management Develop and maintain Work Instructions (WI), guidelines, and job aids Upload and maintain documents in KNOWIT and SharePoint Support process improvements and system enhancements 8. Stakeholder & Service Provider Management Liaise with LLP, LSP, procurement, warehouse, PPC, finance (FCV/P2P), and Hub teams Monitor service provider performance against KPIs Support performance reviews and continuous improvement initiatives Handle enquiries related to shipment arrangements and logistics rates 9. System & Data Management Maintain SAP TM master data including route, partner (Z5), and cost elements Support troubleshooting on shipment cost, FOB value, and invoice generation issues Ensure data accuracy and system compliance Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position: • Education: Degree or above in international trade, logistics, supply chain or business management. Advanced diploma to be considered for candidates with more years of experience in customs and international trade. • Working Experience: 1–3 year’s experience in Import/Export operations, logistics coordination, and documentation Exposure to working with logistics service providers (LLP/LSP) Experience in SAP TM / MM and chemical industry is an advantage • Technical & Professional Knowledge: Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) Knowledge of SAP TM processes and transportation management systems Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and operational priorities Good communication skills (written & spoken English) High level of ownership, accountability, and attention to detail The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Personal Protection Officer (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Personal Protection Officer (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Personal Protection Officer (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Unsere Einheit Corporate Legal, Compliance, Insurance & Security berät zu Rechtsfragen und Versicherungen, betreut das Compliance & Integrity Programm der BASF-Gruppe und bündelt alle Themen rund um Schutz und Sicherheit. Mit unserer Expertise auf diesen Gebieten stehen wir unseren Kolleg:innen als aktiv gestaltender Partner zur Seite. Durch unser globales Team stellen wir sicher, dass unsere Beratung stets nah am Kunden erfolgt. WAS SIE ERWARTET Sie führen operative Personenschutzmaßnahmen im In- und Ausland durch und gewährleisten die unmittelbare Betreuung sowie Absicherung schutzbedürftiger Personen bei Terminen, Reisen und Veranstaltungen. Zu Ihren Aufgaben zählen regelmäßige Fahrten im Rahmen des Personenschutzes, einschließlich Bring- und Abholservices sowie lageabhängiger Zusatzfahrten unter konsequenter Berücksichtigung sicherheitsrelevanter Aspekte. Sie planen und steuern Routen unter Sicherheitsgesichtspunkten und passen diese flexibel an veränderte Lagen an. Schutz- und Einsatzkonzepte setzen Sie gemäß internen Vorgaben um und wenden diese situationsgerecht im Einsatz an. Sie erkennen, analysieren und bewerten Risiken sowie Bedrohungslagen, führen strukturierte Lagebeurteilungen durch und leiten geeignete Maßnahmen zur Gefahrenabwehr ein. Auch unter physischer und psychischer Belastung handeln Sie souverän und situationsgerecht. Einsätze dokumentieren Sie strukturiert, übernehmen das Berichts- und Meldewesen und wirken an der Weiterentwicklung von Schutzkonzepten sowie internen Sicherheitsstandards mit. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung auf mindestens DQR-/EQR-Niveau 4, idealerweise mit Sicherheits- oder Einsatzbezug (z. B. Polizei, Bundeswehr, Justiz, Rettungsdienst oder privates Sicherheitsgewerbe) oder vergleichbare höhere Qualifikation alternativ mehrjährige, einschlägige Erfahrung im professionellen Personenschutz sowie ausgeprägtes Einsatzverständnis über reine Fahrertätigkeiten hinaus erfolgreich abgelegte Sachkundeprüfung gemäß § 34a GewO sowie Waffensachkunde nach § 7 WaffG, ergänzt durch fundierte Personenschutzausbildung inklusive professionellem Fahrtraining Erfahrung in der Bewertung von Risiken und Bedrohungslagen sowie Fähigkeit zur strukturierten Informationsauswertung und sicheren Lageentscheidung auch in dynamischen Situationen sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie Englischkenntnisse auf mindestens B1-, idealerweise B2-Niveau für die Kommunikation im internationalen Umfeld hohe persönliche Zuverlässigkeit und Integrität sowie Bereitschaft zur Durchführung von Zuverlässigkeitsüberprüfungen und Background Checks Bereitschaft zur Teilnahme an medizinischen, sportlichen und psychologischen Eignungsüberprüfungen ausgeprägtes Verantwortungsbewusstsein, Eigenständigkeit, Diskretion und Belastbarkeit sowie hohe Reisebereitschaft und zeitliche Flexibilität Führerschein der Klasse B, umfangreiche Fahrpraxis und sicheres Verhalten in dynamischen Fahrsituationen WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktive Vergütung nach Chemietarifvertrag sowie eine variable Erfolgsbeteiligung Betriebliche Altersversorgung, firmenfinanzierte Pflegezusatzversicherung, Aktienprogramm für Mitarbeitende sowie Wertkonto Tariflicher Zukunftsbetrag (z.B. zur Finanzierung eines Jobrads oder zusätzlicher freier Tage) Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung (z.B. betriebliche Kinderkrippe, Ferienprogramme) Fitness – und Gesundheitsstudio, Medical Center mit zahlreichen Angeboten für Ihre Gesundheit Inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen (z.B. Women in BASF) Mentoringprogramme und Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Karrierewege als fachliche Weiterentwicklung auf andere Stellen im Unternehmen (auch Jobrotation) Deutschlandticket als vergünstigtes Jobticket, Jobrad, Verfügbarkeit von E-Ladesäulen für Privatfahrzeuge SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Laura Juliane Mierke (Talent Acquisition), juliane.mierke@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-58922 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF SE mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größtes arbeitgebendes Unternehmen der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studierende, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Mehr über die BASF SE erfahren Sie hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: E-coat Process Engineer I
jobs.description:
Now hiring! E-Coat Process Engineer Southfield, MI - Travel up to 50% (US, Canada and Mexico) Surventis Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinishcoatings, decorative paints as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio issupplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enable new applications with innovative surfaces. The automotive coatings division is one of Surventis most dynamic and fast-growing businesses in the region. We have great people filled with passion to deliver innovative, high quality coatings solutions toour customers. Our team is dynamic, customer focused and growth oriented. We are looking for an E- Coat Process Engineer to join our team based in our Coatings Research Center off Telegraph Rd. in Southfield, MI. You will work in a high-pressure/demanding environment with the goal of meeting very demanding customer expectations in short timelines. This is anexcellent opportunity for you to demonstrate your varied skills, including maintaining, contributing to, and leading EHS and 6S standards and continuousimprovement as well as showcasing your knowledge of E-coat application and systems. Your day-to-day: Providing technical service and conduct customer support activities for electrocoat products while being able to work independently. Working to make and test electrocoat samples to internal and customer specifications. Using your skills to evaluate material performance to ensure they meet customer requirements both in a lab and in a production facility. Travelling to multiple customer sites to support and decide on adjustments when resolving customer issues. Working in partnership with various departments and externally with customers. Utilizing the knowledge you have in electrocoat product and process to provide customer support in the areas of electrocoat system design, process optimization, technology conversion, technology launch, and new system launch including responsibility to lead and manage launches. Developing methods to reproduce customer problems, design experiments to correct the problem, work with manufacturing to implement changes, and reporting to the customer root cause and corrective actions. Assisting internal manufacturing and customer facilities with performance evaluation and provide technical direction when needed. If you... Have a Bachelor’s degree in chemistry, engineering, or related field or equivalent blend of knowledge and experience in E-coat operation and runningsystems; or a high school diploma and at least 7 years of E-coat industry experience, including prior customer line experience. Have at least 5-7 years of experience in E-coat chemistry or process, particularly with layout parameters and system design. Have excellent communication skills and have prior experience with direct customer interaction. (Automotive industry highly preferred.) Strong mechanical aptitude required. Possess proven analytical and problem-solving skills. SAP experience highly preferred. Prior experience with European customers a plus. Bilingual a plus. (German or Spanish) Basic equipment repair skills. Privacy Statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color,religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protectedby law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . BASF Group operates this platform solely as a technical service provider under a 
 Transitional Service Agreement following the divestment of the Coatings division, 
 effective July 1st 2026, now Surventis. The employer and contracting party for the advertised positions is exclusively . alone is responsible for the content of the job adverts and the 
 decision regarding applications. Data Protection The processing of your personal data is carried out by as the controller 
 within the meaning of any applicable data privacy laws. BASF Group acts as a technical service provider in this respect and processes 
 data exclusively on behalf of . Further information can be found in the privacy policy coatings privacy policy incl. Contact details .

Excel ist für Dich kein Vorname und dass man die Buchhaltung nicht in der Bücherei abwickelt, ist Dir schon lange klar? Tausche Deine Schulbücher gegen einen Laptop und erfahre in dieser Ausbildung, was von einem Schreibtisch aus alles in Gang gesetzt werden kann. Lerne unsere wichtigsten Abteilungen kennen und reichere so tagtäglich Dein wirtschaftliches Interesse und Wissen an. Werde zum wahren Allrounder, denn so viele Elemente wie das Periodensystem hat, so facettenreich ist auch Dein Aufgabengebiet. Jahr 1211 € / 2. Jahr: 1275 € / 3. Jahr: 1341 € Jahresprämie (100%), Urlaubsgeld (700 Euro), Zukunftsbetrag (23% einer mtl. Du kannst es nicht abwarten, endlich im Labor zu stehen und den Dingen auf den Grund zu gehen. Bei uns untersuchst du verschiedene Verbindungen mit detektivischer Genauigkeit und modernsten Methoden. Tauche ein in die Grundlagen der Chemie und erlerne dabei organische und anorganische Produktions- und Analyseverfahren. Werde zum Chef von mehreren Tausend Atomen. Unvorstellbar! Das wäre wie Zähneputzen ohne Zahnpasta, Joggen ohne Laufschuhe oder die perfekte Frisur ohne Haarspray. Neugierig, in welchen Produkten die BASF noch enthalten ist? Dann ist dieser Beruf genau das Richtige für Dich, denn Du bekommst nicht nur einen genauen Einblick in die spannende Produktwelt, sondern bist bei der Produktion von vielen chemischen Erzeugnissen mittendrin statt nur dabei. Komm an Bord und nimm die Ruder selbst in die Hand, denn nur mit Dir steuern wir ans Ziel des Endprodukts. Klicke hier für erste Eindrücke. DAS LERNST DU WÄHREND DEINER AUSBILDUNG Erlernen der industriellen Herstellung unterschiedlicher chemischer Erzeugnisse und Produkte Steuern und Überwachen der Produktionsabläufe Mitwirken bei der Verfahrensentwicklung zur Herstellung chemischer Produkte 35 % zentrale Ausbildung für Grundlagen, weiterführende Ausbildungsinhalte und Prüfungsvorbereitungen im eigenen Ausbildungszentrum 65 % praxisorientierte und betriebliche Ausbildung in den verschiedenen Produktionen und Instandhaltung DAS BRINGST DU MIT mindestens einen guten Realschulabschluss Begeisterung für Chemie und Technik Interesse an der Herstellung von chemischen Stoffen technisches Grundverständnis und praktisches Geschick großes Verantwortungsbewusstsein, Genauigkeit und Sorgfalt beim Arbeiten Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit sowie Spaß an Herausforderungen DAS BIETEN WIR IN DEINER AUSBILDUNG eine spannende Ausbildung im weltweit führenden Chemieunternehmen gute Übernahmechancen Ausbildungsvergütung: 1. Jahr 1211 € / 2. Jahr: 1275 € / 3. Jahr: 1341 € / 4. Jahr: 1425 € Jahresprämie (100%), Urlaubsgeld (700 Euro), Zukunftsbetrag (23% einer mtl. Ausbildungsvergütung), sowie 30 Tage Urlaub betriebliche Altersvorsorge Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme am BASF-Aktienprogramm zwei Tage bezahlte Freistellung zur Prüfungsvorbereitung moderne IT Ausstattung maßgeschneiderte Schulungsangebote in Form von Seminaren, Workshops und Veranstaltungen zum Weiterentwickeln Deiner persönlichen Fähigkeiten Freizeit und Fitnessangebote sowie Mitarbeiterevents Azubiprojekte und Buddyprogramm AUSBILDUNG GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN? Als Maschinenmaster und Produktionsprofi lässt Du Dich von keinem Problem mehr aus der Ruhe bringen! Du sorgst dafür, dass unsere Produktion nicht zum Stillstand kommt. jobs.name: Safety Coordinator
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF Bij BASF Heerenveen werken we met een enthousiast team van ruim 230 medewerkers aan de ontwikkeling en productie van innovatieve, watergedragen polymeerproducten en additieven. Deze producten worden wereldwijd toegepast in de verpakkings- en coatingindustrie. We werken in een dynamische omgeving waar passie, innovatie en teamgeest centraal staan. Als Safety Coördinator bij BASF speel jij een sleutelrol in het creëren én borgen van een veilige werkomgeving. Jij zorgt ervoor dat werkzaamheden—zowel tijdens dagelijkse productie als tijdens grote onderhoudsstops en shutdowns—veilig, gecontroleerd en volgens onze procedures worden uitgevoerd. Jij bent zichtbaar op de werkvloer, werkt nauw samen met collega’s en contractors en bent dé schakel tussen Operations, EHS en Techniek. Krijg jij energie van verantwoordelijkheid, duidelijke processen en mensen veilig laten werken? Dan is dit jouw kans. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Je bent het veiligheidskompas van de locatie, dit houdt in dat je verantwoordelijk bent voor het opstellen en beoordelen van Veilig Werkvergunningen (VWG) en Lock & Tag-lijsten (L+T). Je past veiligheidsdocumenten aan wanneer de situatie verandert. Daarnaast bereid en ondersteun je veiligheidsmaatregelen tijdens projecten en shutdowns. Je ziet toe op veilig werken in het veld en grijpt meteen in bij onveilige situaties. Ook voer je controlerondes uit binnen Utilitie installaties en volg je bevindingen op. Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het uitvoeren van gasmetingen (O₂, explosiviteit, toxisch) voor werkplekvrijgave. Je begeleidt contractors naar de werkplek en controleert veiligheidsmaatregelen. Je communiceert met Shift Supervisors, Operators en projectleiders over Risico`s en voortgang. Tenslotte instrueer je put‑ en brandwachten en ondersteunt de Turnaround‑organisatie. WHAT YOU OFFER Je hebt een relevante afgeronde MBO niveau 4 opleiding bijvoorbeeld Procestechniek. Je bent ook in het bezit van een afgeronde Middelbare Veiligheidskunde of soortgelijke opleiding op gelijk niveau. Ervaring met LOTO/Lock & Tag, werkvergunning‑systematiek is van groot voordeel. Je hebt aantoonbare relevante werkervaring in een soortgelijke industriële omgeving. Daarnaast heb je kennis van in- en externe wet- en regelgeving en veiligheidsprocedures. Je communiceert duidelijk, neemt verantwoordelijkheid en blijft kalm in drukke situaties. Je hebt een scherp oog voor veiligheid én het vermogen en zelfvertrouwen om dit goed over te brengen op anderen. WHAT WE OFFER Marktconforme vergoeding met 8,3% vakantietoeslag, een vaste eindejaarsuitkering (1 maandsalaris) en een bonusregeling. Aantrekkelijke aanvullende regelingen zoals verzekeringen voor ziekte en ongevallen, een reisverzekering, fietsplan, employee assistance programma en collectieve zorgverzekering. Het gaat om een fulltime positie. Een sleutelrol binnen een veilige, moderne productieomgeving. Veel verantwoordelijkheid en ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen. Samenwerking met experts uit Operations, Techniek en EHS. HOW TO REACH US Solliciteren kan direct via de sollicitatie button. Voor vragen over de functie en/of het recruitmentproces kun je contact opnemen met ons recruitment team via: talent-acquisition-bene@basf.com. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

Um als Chemiekonzern an der Spitze zu bleiben, braucht es kluge Köpfe wie Dich. Durch die Verbindung von Theorie und Praxis und das Durchlaufen verschiedenster Abteilungen wie zum Beispiel Rechnungswesen, Personal und Einkauf wirst Du zum wahren Allrounder. Dabei lernst Du, wie man ein Unternehmen führt, organisiert und steuert. Auch Deine Sprachkenntnisse sind gefragt, denn Du stehst unseren internationalen Kunden mit Rat und Tat zur Seite. Gestalte die Zukunft von Morgen mit uns und tauche in spannende Digitalisierungsthemen ein. So viele Elemente wie das Periodensystem hat, so facettenreich sind auch Deine Aufgabengebiete. DAS LERNST DU WÄHREND DEINES DUALEN STUDIUMS Studieren an der PHWT (Private Hochschule für Wirtschaft und Technik) am Campus Vechta. DAS BRINGST DU MIT mindestens eine Fachhochschulreife gute Kenntnisse in den Fächern Deutsch, Mathematik und Englisch Interesse an anderen Kulturen und internationalen betriebswirtschaftlichen Zusammenhängen Spaß am Organisieren und Planen sowie am Arbeiten in Projektteams Verantwortungsbewusstsein, Genauigkeit und Sorgfalt beim Arbeiten DAS BIETEN WIR IN DEINEM DUALEN STUDIUM eine spannende Ausbildung im weltweit führenden Chemieunternehmen gute Übernahmechancen Ausbildungsvergütung: 1. Jahr 1211 € / 2. Jahr: 1275 € / 3. Jahr: 1341 € / 4. Jahr: 1425 € Jahresprämie (100%), Urlaubsgeld (700 Euro), Zukunftsbetrag (23% einer mtl. Ausbildungsvergütung), sowie 30 Tage Urlaub betriebliche Altersvorsorge Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme am BASF-Aktienprogramm zwei Tage bezahlte Freistellung zur Prüfungsvorbereitung moderne IT Ausstattung maßgeschneiderte Schulungsangebote in Form von Seminaren, Workshops und Veranstaltungen zum Weiterentwickeln Deiner persönlichen Fähigkeiten Freizeit und Fitnessangebote sowie Mitarbeiterevents Azubiprojekte und Buddyprogramm DUALES STUDIUM GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN? Nachdem Du bei uns in den unterschiedlichsten Bereichen Praxisluft geschnuppert hast, stehen Dir als Zahlenzauberer und Kundenkünstler die Türen zur Wirtschaftswelt und verschiedenen Unternehmensbereichen offen. Egal für welche Richtung Du Dich entscheidest, Deine Aussichten sind exzellent. Wohin soll die Reise gehen? Das genaue Ziel wählst Du! DEIN WEG ZU UNS: Richtig bewerben - so geht's! Neugierde, Offenheit und Motivation. Für uns steht deine Persönlichkeit im Vordergrund. Noten sind nicht alles. Im ersten Schritt des Auswahlverfahrens bleiben die Schulnoten daher erst einmal unberücksichtigt. Für uns ist der Gesamteindruck entscheidend. Daher LET'S MATCH & BEWIRB DICH JETZT: Bewerbungsstart: ab Anfang Juni 2026 DIE FÜNF SCHRITTE UNSERES AUSWAHLVERFAHRENS: 1. Online-Test von zu Hause aus 2. Eignungstest vor Ort bei der BASF 3. jobs.name: Consignment Manager
jobs.description:
Consignment Manager Description A chemical structure is only as strong as the bonds between the molecules. At Surventis, the strength of the bonds lies not just in the chemicals but is also mirrored in the links forged between the global companies and employees of the Surventis world. We are a company with highly qualified talent, who give their contribution each and every day across the different sectors to make the Surventis difference. We demonstrate uncompromising ethics and appreciation for our employees, as well as respect for the environment. Surventis is a leading specialty chemical company whose quality, innovation, and service exceed the expectations of our customers. If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. Overview: The primary function of the Consignment Manager is to work closely with the business leaders and Sales, Finance, Marketing, Supply Chain and suppliers to manage all consignment accounts for Surventis. Job Duties: Facilitate and oversee contract management processes for consignment accounts with support from Legal Implement and manage processes to collect, measure and track relevant metrics in order to assess contract compliance with key contract terms and conditions for all contracts, including product inventory tracking Develop and manage the contract renewal process evaluating account profitability Develop and manage the expiration notification process for all contracts Develop thorough knowledge and understanding of each contract; act as the single point of contact for all contract inquiries regarding terms and conditions, obligations, compliance, products, services, rights and deliverables for each contract Develop contract portfolio metrics, including key usage and profitability Assist in identification of reporting tools/solutions to automate contract management reporting and tracking Reconcile inventory between customer and SAP Perform inventory audit one per year Requirements: Ability to take ownership of and focus on critical initiatives Strong business acumen, organizational, planning, analytical, negotiation and conflict resolution skills Must have proven ability to gather, extract, organize, analyze, and interpret detailed data from a variety of sources Ability to take ownership of and focus on critical initiatives Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, rapidly changing environment with demonstrated project management skills Proven ability to collaborate with employees at all levels of an organization Team player with ability to learn quickly, multi-task, self-direct and appropriately prioritize workload Ability to communicate effectively, both in verbal and written communications Warehouse management experience preferred Bachelor’s degree in business or related majors Salary Range- $95,000-110,000 We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. jobs.name: Applicatie Specialist Commercial Vehicles Functiedetails
jobs.description:
Applicatie Specialist Commercial Vehicles Functiedetails | BASF SE Verdergaan naar primaire inhoud Zoek banen Taal Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profiel login Werknemerslogin Beschrijving van de vacature Applicatie Specialist Commercial Vehicles LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Solliciteer nu WELKOM Als jij iemand bent die anderen inspireert, verantwoordelijkheid neemt voor zijn of haar impact en wil uitblinken in wat je doet, dan ben je de perfecte match voor ons. Tot 1 juli maakte onze organisatie deel uit van BASF. In het kader van de huidige overgang worden alle vacatures en het sollicitatieproces nog steeds gehost op het BASF‑platform en daarom volledig in BASF‑huisstijl weergegeven. Laat je hier niet door verwarren: alle functies zijn posities bij Surventis en jouw sollicitatie wordt bij ons ingediend. Bij Surventis kijken we een stukje verder — in alles wat we doen. We zetten de klant centraal, denken een stap vooruit en ontwikkelen oplossingen die echte waarde en langdurige samenwerkingen creëren. We inspireren elkaar om dieper te denken, verantwoordelijkheid over ons werk en streven er voortdurend naar om het mogelijke te vergroten. Als je jezelf volledig meebrengt, de voor de hand liggende antwoorden uitdaagt en wil groeien met een team dat het meent — dan is dit jouw plek. Als Applicatie Specialist Commercial Vehicles ben je een sleutelpersoon in het ondersteunen van onze klanten op technisch vlak. Je combineert praktijkervaring met commerciële feeling en helpt mee om onze producten en processen succesvol te implementeren en te optimaliseren bij klanten. Je werkt nauw samen met het sales team en draagt actief bij aan klanttevredenheid en business groei. Jouw verantwoordelijkheden Uitvoeren van technische interventies bij klanten Geven van praktische en theoretische opleidingen in het trainingscenter Ondersteunen van sales bij de implementatie van nieuwe producten en processen Verzorgen van product- en procesdemonstraties, zowel intern als bij klanten Voorbereiden en organiseren van trainingssessies en demonstraties in het trainingscenter Actief deelnemen aan trainingen in het moederbedrijf in Münster (of sporadisch elders in Europa) Bij uitzondering ondersteuning bieden aan collega’s in het buitenland Wat verwachten wij van jou? Je bent Nederlandstalig en hebt een goede kennis van het Engels. Je hebt ervaring in het voorbereiden en spuiten van zwaar vervoer (Commercial Vehicles) - ervaring met personenwagens is een extra troef. Je beschikt over basis kleurkennis. Je hebt een basiskennis van MS Office. Je hebt een commerciële mindset en denkt klantgericht. Je hebt een positieve ingesteldheid en bent een echte teamplayer. Je bent flexibel en hebt geen typische 9-to-5 mentaliteit. Je bent bereid om sporadisch te reizen met overnachting. Wat bieden wij jou? Een uitdagende en afwisselende functie binnen een internationale organisatie De kans om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen via interne en externe opleidingen Een dynamische werkomgeving met sterke teamgeest Een competitief salarispakket met aantrekkelijke extralegale voordelen Diversiteit is onze grootste kracht! Maak deel uit van onze succesformule en ontwikkel samen met ons de toekomst -- in een wereldwijd team dat inclusiviteit en gelijke kansen omarmt, ongeacht geslacht, leeftijd, afkomst, seksuele geaardheid, handicap of geloof. jobs.name: Postdoc, Solid Process
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role You will work as an expert in process research and development, focusing on solid process and engineering . How You Will Do It Be responsible for compliance to EHS rules in your area of activity. Initiates safety review for experiments and ensures compliance to measures. Initiate, manage, constantly discuss, and align research projects with other project partners including product development, technical groups, production, universities, etc. to develop solid handling, drying, mixing, extrusion and other relevant processes. Your expertise and results will be applied to both research and operation. Provide technical support for business units in Asian Pacific region on related process development and optimization, troubleshooting, debottlenecking on field of solid characterization, conveying, drying, mixing, extrusion, etc. Design, instruct, and coach lab members for project experiments, closely watching and achieving project milestones, timeline and budget, present and document results, file patents. Join the expert network within the BASF global R&D community, operational units, technologies, and scientific partners to maintain state of the art expertise as well as monitor new practices and market trends What We Look For Education: Ph.D. in chemical engineering, process engineering, mechanical engineering, material science and engineering, physical chemistry or related. Working Experience: at least 1 year relevant work experience. Hands-on experimental skills and in-depth knowledge on solid mechanics, powder technology, conveying, drying, mixing, extrusion, etc. Demonstrated ability to define research problems, design experimental plans, and independently drive a project forward. Excellent data analysis and technical writing skills. Prior industrial experience is a strong plus. Modeling and simulation experience on solid related processes is highly valued English and Chinese: Fluent. Demonstrate a high level of creativity and personal initiative, strong interpersonal and communication skills to work well in global teams 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用。

jobs.name: Senior Officer, New Substance Notification
jobs.description:
About The Role Our EC/ES Product Stewardship APAC unit plays a crucial role in ensuring compliance with national and international chemical legislation. As the first point of contact for Product Stewardship topics, we actively engage with stakeholders, monitor regulatory developments, and promote responsible chemical management throughout the lifecycle of our products. By sharing our knowledge and expertise, we aim to ensure that Product Stewardship is integrated into all aspects of our operations. How You Will Do It • You are together with the Chemical Regulations Manager the primary contact for ECs business units for Asian chemical regulations. In close cooperation with the EC global regulatory team, you stay up to date with the latest regulations and requirements. This helps us to ensure compliance with the legal regulations for EC operations (ECO, ECR, ECT) for finished products in APAC. • Conducting hazard assessments and risk evaluations of our products from a chemical and regulatory perspective is another important task for you. By strategically identifying emerging regulatory issues and opportunities, you assess product and marketing risks and proactively develop risk management measures and product strategies for assigned portfolio and business units. • In Asia several countries require registrations. You will support preparing and submitting New Substance Notifications and (Product) Registration applications in accordance with local and regional regulations for more than 10 countries. This will require collaboration with internal stakeholders, including R&D, Manufacturing, and Quality Assurance, to gather necessary data for submissions. • Monitoring of registered substance and being responsible for the Substance Volume Tracking for the Asian countries is another daily task including SAP maintenance. What We Look For • Completed scientific studies (B.Sc or M. Sc), preferably in chemistry. • Regulatory affairs experience, specifically in Asian Pacific chemical regulations. • Well-versed in IT applications, with at least 3 years working experience in SAP EHS system in product safety or chemical legislation area, coatings, fine chemical industrial relevant experience preferred. • High level of commitment and teamwork skills, flexibility, customer orientation, as well as independent and structured work approach. • Clear and authentic communication skills in English, with the ability to communicate effectively at all levels of the organization, translate regulatory/technical information clearly. • Entrepreneurial thinking, pragmatism, and hands-on mentality.
