jobs.name: Assistant Manager, Finance & Tax
jobs.description:
About The Role • Serve as the legal entity lead in China, accountable for financial compliance of the entity’s statutory financial statements. • Act as the primary contact with local government authorities (e.g., tax authorities) to ensure efficient, accurate and sustainable handling of tax and reporting obligations under PRC accounting and tax laws/regulations. • Build and maintain transparent, trust-based relationships with tax and other government authorities. • Serve as a proactive business partner, delivering tailored tax and finance advice and helping implement processes that support operations and the company’s long term strategy. How You Will Do It • Act as the primary contact for finance and tax service provider and local business functions to ensure efficient communication and fulfilment of internal and external reporting and filing requirements. • Work together with finance and tax service provider to ensure timely and accurate submission of group reporting packages and local statutory reports. • Collaborate with external tax service providers to manage local tax compliance, including tax filings, tax accounting and reporting, tax credit rating management and related matters. • Lead finance related projects, e.g. Tax compliance, employee reimbursement system implementation, etc • Coordinate and support tax inspections and tax audits, including preparation of documentation, responses and remediation planning. • Provide tailored finance and tax advice to business and functional units to support projects, transactions and strategic initiatives. • Deliver finance and tax training and knowledge sharing sessions to internal and (where relevant) external stakeholders to raise compliance and process capability. • Establish, document and continuously optimize operational processes and controls to drive efficiency, compliance and service excellence aligned with business needs. What We Look For • Education: Bachelor degree or above from university, majoring in finance, accounting and taxation related • Certificate: CPA, CTA is a plus • Working Experience: Over 5 years’ working experience with at least 3 years’ tax experience in multinational manufacturing company (experience in Big 4 and domestic leading firms is a plus) • Technical & Professional Knowledge: - In-depth knowledge of PRC accounting regulations (IFRS and PRC) and tax operation and practice - Being familiar with ERP system and program (e.g. SAP), IT expertise and programming experience is plus - Team-oriented and self-motivated, strong customer orientation and drive for innovation - Strong logical analysis and organization skill, excellent communication, interpersonal and leadership skills - Fluent in both verbal and written English - Proficient MS Office skills for Excel, Word and PowerPoint [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: Customs Classification Specialist
jobs.description:
About The Role Join our company as a key expert in customs classification of chemical products. In this role, you will ensure compliance with customs regulations and support efficient processes across Asia Pacific while being connected to a global community. How You Will Do It • Act as the primary contact for customs classification requests in the Asia Pacific region. • Perform accurate and timely customs classification for products, raw materials, and merchandise according to local and international regulations. • Monitor and analyze customs regulations in China and Asia Pacific; implement compliant processes proactively. • Support process improvements with a focus on simplification and digitalization. • Collaborate closely with Supply Chain, Sales, and Procurement teams to ensure seamless operations. • Participate in global customs classification communities and share best practices. • Additional tasks may include handling Dangerous Goods requests, packaging consulting and article release for the Asia Pacific region. What We Look For • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or a related scientific field. • Proven experience in customs classification of chemical products in China; knowledge of other Asian countries is a plus. • Several years of experience in chemical industry or related. • Strong English communication skills (written and spoken). • Familiarity with SAP GTS; knowledge of SAP EHS and other modules (TM, OTC) is advantageous. • Analytical mindset, structured approach, and interest in automation tools and data-driven insights. • Collaborative and proactive attitude. Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Rejoignez Coatings France, où l'innovation rencontre la durabilité pour créer des revêtements de pointe qui transforment l'industrie. Découvrez notre site de Clermont (60) en vidéo . Etes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES En tant qu'Alternant Communication et Branding, vous serez en charge des missions suivantes : Support Plan MarCom global: Création de contenu pour les réseaux sociaux / photos / stories / videos pour toutes nos marques globales : Glasurit, R-M, baslac et Refinity; Gestion projets avec différents pays + traduction de certains supports; Gestion agences / devis / facturation; Customer face / RCC / brand : courts montages vidéo (Camtasia, Première Pro); Création de supports visuels (Adobe Photoshop, Canva); Aide à la gestion et la mise en place d'événements de façon ponctuelle; Support mise à jour les sites web GL, R-M, baslac. VOTRE PROFIL Vous préparez un diplôme Master en Marketing Communication / Digital; Vous êtes doté d'une très bonne capacité à vous exprimer écrite et orale. Vous êtes à l'aise sur les outils digitaux de communication; De plus, la pratique de l'anglais est requis; Par ailleurs, vous êtes en capacité à travailler en équipe; Enfin, vous avez un esprit créatif, vous êtes orienté client et êtes ouvert vers l’extérieur. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Un package attractif ; Un accompagnement personnalisé favorisant le développement de vos compétences métier ; Une expérience riche et variée sur un site de production au sein du leader de l'industrie chimique ; L’opportunité de rejoindre une entreprise labellisée "Employeur de choix" depuis plus de 7 années consécutives au travers de notre partenariat avec la plateforme Engagement Jeunes , vous permettant de bénéficier d'un accompagnement personnalisé à l’issue de votre contrat d’alternance et ainsi de booster votre employabilité. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Bruno, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Coatings France est spécialisée dans le développement, la production et la commercialisation de différentes solutions de revêtement allant des traitements de surface à la peinture automobile. Nous soutenons l'innovation, le design et de nouvelles possibilités d'applications au sein d'une équipe internationale dont l'objectif est de répondre aux besoins de nos partenaires dans le monde entier. Notre gamme de services comprend également le programme « Innovation Beyond Paint » qui vise le développement de nouveaux marchés et de modèles commerciaux. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Assistant Manager, Technical Development (Nansha)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. How You Will Do It Provide on time technical service to customer - Executes and achieve trial (new product introduction) and troubleshooting requests - Performs formulation modifications, evaluations and testing base on customer needs - Follows up development of new customers, applications and relevant processing technologies - Provide necessary technical training/workshop/exchange with customer Technical activities & knowhow documentation - Communicates and documents (internal and externally) all processing technologies & trends - Documentation of all opportunities in salesforce - Document all technical related activities (trial report/trouble shooting report/lab working report etc) - Collects market information (competitors, customers, regulations, etc.) & new ideas for R&D proposal - Supports build up regional technical capability and fosters expertise exchange between countries Support function (PM,QM,MM,EHS, PD etc) on quality/material/product analysis/registration/optimization etc - Initiate New Article registration and ensure compliance to formulation change process & BOM’s consistence - Supports evaluation of alternative raw materials - Create related technical documents (TDS,BOM,BI,Quality documents….) - Support root cause analysis of COPQ - Defines product specifications for product developments/ modifications Compliance to EHS requirements - Ensures compliance to EHS requirements in labs & customer’s site - Be responsible for the cleanness and safety of TS laboratories and workshop areas. What We Look For - Bachelor's Degree in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering or equivalent. - More than 5 years of technical service and team management experience in Polyurethane Industry. - Strong technical knowledge on PU formula design, application development, prefer with PU footwear application background. Good understanding on technical perspective. - Good communication skills in English - Willing to be based at the Nansha location. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用

WAS SIE ERWARTET Fachliche Führung & Verantwortung - Sie leiten zehn Betriebskantinen in Ludwigshafen und führen die Küchenleiter:innen (8) sowie das Team Angebotskonzeption (2). Führung & Entwicklung - Sie coachen und entwickeln Führungskräfte, begleiten Recruiting, Onboarding und Qualifizierungen und bauen ein einheitliches Führungsverständnis auf. Kulinarische Steuerung & Konzepte - Sie verantworten standortübergreifende Speisepläne, Rezepturen, Kalkulationen und Ernährungsstandards und entwickeln gastronomische Konzepte kontinuierlich weiter. Qualität & Standards - Sie stellen die Einhaltung von Qualitäts-, Prozess- und HACCP-Standards sicher und optimieren Abläufe sowie Rezepturen fortlaufend. Organisation & Wirtschaftlichkeit - Sie unterstützen die Küchenleitungen bei Wareneinsatz, Kalkulation, Personaleinsatz und Organisation und stellen effiziente Ressourcennutzung sowie stabile Abläufe sicher. Trendbewusstsein & Praxisnähe - Sie beobachten Trends, entwickeln moderne Angebote und bewerten neue Konzepte und Technologien auf ihre Praxistauglichkeit. Zusammenarbeit & Strategie - Sie arbeiten eng mit internen Schnittstellen zusammen und agieren als Sparringspartner:in der Leitung Gemeinschaftsgastronomie bei strategischen Themen und Projekten. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Qualifikation – Abgeschlossene gastronomische Ausbildung, idealerweise mit Weiterbildung (z. B. Küchenmeister:in, staatl. geprüfte:r Betriebswirt:in, Hotelfachschule oder vergleichbar). Erfahrung – Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in der Gemeinschaftsgastronomie und im Catering-Geschäft sowie fundiertes Know-how in Speiseplankonzeption, Kalkulation und Qualitätsstandards. Führungskompetenz – Erfahrung in der fachlichen Führung von Führungskräften sowie ein wertschätzender, klarer Führungsstil. Arbeitsweise – Strukturiert, verbindlich und lösungsorientiert, mit hohem Qualitäts- und Verantwortungsbewusstsein. Persönlichkeit – Ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Coachingkompetenz, Freude an konzeptioneller Arbeit, Weiterentwicklung und Zusammenarbeit – und der Anspruch, sich und andere jeden Tag ein Stück besser zu machen. jobs.name: Automation Engineer (1 year contract)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Do you want to work with passionate teams, providing excellent business and digital services to the Asia Pacific region? Do you want an open, supportive, and caring working environment? Do you view your development needs seriously as we do? If YES, then take your career to the #NextLevel with BASF ! Objectives of the Position BASF Shared Services Center Kuala Lumpur was established in 2005 to provide functional services in Finance, Controlling, Information Services, Human Resources, Environment, Health & Safety, Procurement, and Import-Export Order Management to BASF companies in the Asia-Pacific region. The center is part of BASF’s Global Business Services (GB) division, with three other sites in Berlin and Ludwigshafen in Germany and in Montevideo, Uruguay. GB’s Global Technology Unit (GTU) is looking for an Automation Engineer with a technical background in in automation technologies. The role works closely with GTU’s Global Business Enablement and solutions delivery teams to implement and support IT solutions for other units of the GB division globally. Additionally, it is envisioned for the role to be part of a global Community of Practice to further the awareness and adoption of Citizen Development technologies across the GTU and GB organization in Kuala Lumpur, Berlin and Montevideo. Main Tasks As an Automation Engineer in the Global Technology Unit team, your main tasks include: Designing, implementing and supporting automated workflows using UiPath RPA, Microsoft Power Automate and other related technologies Integrate automation solutions with enterprise systems and platforms (e.g. SAP, SharePoint, APIs, databases, and cloud services) by leveraging REST APIs and connectors to enable end-to-end process automation across multiple systems Collaborate with business stakeholders to assist in analyzing and mapping end-to-end business processes, identifying automation opportunities and optimization potential Define automation scope, requirements, and success criteria, ensuring alignment with business objectives Design scalable and maintainable automation solutions, including exception handling strategies and human-in-the-loop considerations where required Participating in testing activities, including any applicable unit testing, SIT, UAT, FAT etc. Creating and managing Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment pipelines to automate the software application release process in-line with BASF best practices Monitoring and optimization of implemented solutions and the relevant infrastructure environments they run on, to minimize service disruption risks by analyzing metrics related to system/application health Supporting implemented solutions through troubleshooting and rectification Produce and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including design documents, runbooks, and support materials Collaborate with technical teams across BASF’s technology landscape within the automation domain Ensure automation solutions adhere to BASF technology standards, security policies, and integration guidelines Apply governance practices for Power Platform and RPA environments, including access control, auditability, and compliance requirements Requirements Education: Bachelor Degree in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / Information Technology or business-technology related discipline. Working experience 3-5 years’ experience in automation development using UiPath and Microsoft Power Automate, including workflow design, reusable component development, exception handling, and application of RPA best practices (e.g. REFramework, orchestrated execution, scalable design patterns) Technical Skills (mandatory): Proven ability to integrate automation solutions with enterprise systems (e.g. SAP, SharePoint, databases, cloud services) using REST APIs, connectors, and data exchange mechanisms Solid understanding of data structures and processing (e.g. JSON, XML, SQL) to ensure reliable end-to-end automation Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines, version control, and automated deployment practices Ability to design and execute testing strategies (unit, integration, UAT) to ensure quality and stability of automation solutions Strong understanding of automation governance and security principles, including access control, environment management, and compliance with enterprise IT standards Ability to design and implement solutions aligned with Power Platform and enterprise governance models, ensuring auditability and controlled scalability Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ecosystem (e.g. Databricks, Azure SQL, Data Factory) and broader data services to support automation use cases Demonstrated strength in producing and maintaining clear, structured technical documentation (e.g. solution design, process documentation, runbooks) to support maintainability, auditability, and knowledge transfer Working experience in an MNCs setup, with cross-collaboration between multi-disciplinary teams virtually across multiple time zones Technical and Professional Knowledge (nice-to-have) Exposure to AI development using Microsoft Copilot Studio Soft-skill required: Digitally-savvy, innovative, highly passionate on new technology offerings, highly perceptive of what’s new in the technology space and its relevance to bring business value Strong analytical skills to steer engineering requirements and can drive high code standards Strong communication & team building skills, combined with an ability to manage and resolve conflict, change management skill in matrixed and virtual teams Enthusiastic, confident, entrepreneurial with sound business judgement within the given broad guidelines Ability to maintain customer-oriented focus even when dealing with technical subject matter Demonstrate a high discipline to complete tasks within project team timelines Appreciation on the importance of connecting tasks to the bigger picture BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre was established in 2005. Located in Kuala Lumpur, the regional service hub has about 1,200 employees and growing. Focused on being a competency and digitalization driven hub, it also provides quality functional and know-how services in the areas of finance, controlling, human resources, real estate and facilities management, communications, environmental, health and safety, procurement, import-export order management, and information services to BASF companies and affiliates in the Asia Pacific region. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay that cover the Europe and Americas regions. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €68.9 billion in 2023. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the U.S. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Sales Manager - Animal Nutrition, Vitamins and Carotenoids
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role To drive profitable growth of business in India. How You Will Do It Plan and direct sales activities in India to achieve agreed sales volume, pricing and selling cost objectives by implementing consistently the overall sales strategy, using technical, organizational and customer knowledge. Be responsible for business top line, bottom line and volume growth as per annual targets. Develop and maintain strategic long-term business relationships with respective accounts (all decision-makers) to promote brand value. Handle and improve sales of Vitamins and Carotenoids products in Animal Nutrition Market in India. Support Business Head, South Asia with inputs to efficiently steer business operations. Provide inputs and participate in the marketing, market planning and technical development of products and services. Update customer data, account plans and market concept of assigned customers and market. Provide accurate sales forecasts, supporting efficient planning of products and services. Work with the R&D, customer service and logistics personnel to establish a communication path with the customer to ensure sales opportunities for product and services. Develop market intelligence / Network to pursue market growth and deepen market penetration Nurture, grow and develop direct key account as well as Distributor Management to Optimize & strengthen distribution network and ensure effective distributors management. Collect and provide market information/requirements to the organization. Ensure proper cash flow and working capital management by achieving defined organizational KPIs such as DSO and DIV for ENV Animal. Develop lasting customer relationships to identify business trends in and beyond the customer scope or respective portfolio. Utilize product application knowledge as well as thorough knowledge of entire customer and market value chain to anticipate market trends. Develop a strong opportunity pipeline for existing / new products by identifying new customers, pursuing new applications, and interacting with corresponding process and product developers of the potential customers, leading to profitable future business. Identify customers to develop new concepts for our niche products (like Carotenoids) working together with our technical and marketing team. What We Look For B V Sc & AH with Specialization in Animal Nutrition / Bachelor of Science with experience in the relevant industry is preferred. Vet Dr. + MBA with relevant experience will be added advantage. 8 to 12 years’ experience in feed supplement & animal nutrition industry is preferred. Techno commercial. Techno- commercial individual with market & business orientation with excellent competencies of customer focus, communication skills, entrepreneurial drive & collaborating for success and ability to use MS Office, CRM systems (like Salesforce) and virtual meeting tools effectively. Work Location Any of the work location: Mumbai / Hyderabad / Delhi / Pune

jobs.name: Account Manager
jobs.description: Now hiring! Account Manager Houston, TX (Remote) Company vehicle offered We are looking for an Account Manager to join our Performance Chemicals team in Houston, TX. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. As an Key Account Manager – Refinery Catalysts, you will serve as the primary commercial and technical interface between BASF and refinery customers. You will be responsible for driving profitable growth, strengthening strategic partnerships, and delivering measurable value through catalyst solutions and services. This role blends technical expertise, commercial leadership, and relationship management to support refinery performance, reliability, and profitability As a Account Manager, you create chemistry by... Acting as the face of BASF to assigned refinery clients, building multi-level relationships across operations, technical, procurement, and leadership teams, while developing and executing account strategies focused on growth, retention, and value creation. Achieving annual sales targets, margin goals, and market share objectives through contract negotiations, pricing strategy, commercial agreements, accurate sales forecasting, pipeline management, and CRM maintenance. Applying knowledge of FCC, hydroprocessing, and refining catalyst technologies to support customer operations through catalyst performance reviews, economic value assessments, technical presentations, and recommendations that translate technical results into economic and operational benefits. Overseeing implementation and performance of catalyst programs, including trials, new product introductions, performance tracking and reporting, troubleshooting, optimization, and coordination of internal and external stakeholders to ensure high-quality execution. Leading regular business reviews with customers to review KPIs, performance, and value delivered; align on customer priorities and operational goals; identify risks and growth opportunities; and develop joint continuous improvement plans that strengthen long-term partnerships. Working closely with BASF teams including Technical Service/Engineering, Supply Chain, and Marketing & Product Management to coordinate resources and deliver integrated customer-focused solutions. Monitoring refinery market trends, customer strategies, and competitive activity to provide insights that support pricing, product positioning, and growth strategy. If you... Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, with Chemical Engineering preferred, or a related field. Bring a minimum of 5 years of experience in chemical engineering, preferably within the refinery or chemical industry, with a proven track record managing large accounts and driving business growth. Preferred: Have experience within catalysts or related industries to support customer engagement and technical understanding, as well as 3+ years in account management or sales. Demonstrate a strong technical understanding of refinery processes. Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships with clients and internal teams. Have strong analytical, negotiation, problem-solving, and organizational skills, including the ability to analyze market trends and customer needs, develop strategic sales plans, and manage multiple accounts simultaneously. Are proficient with MS Office, CRM software, and other sales management tools. Are willing to travel up to 50% for customer meetings and industry events as needed. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $150000 - $190000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Industry Manager, ASEAN
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Objectives : Industry Manager, ASEAN is responsible for leading and driving the overall sales strategy, business growth, and market expansion across the ASEAN sub-region for Dispersion Business Unit (Fiber Bonding & Adhesive, Paper & Carpet Dispersion Industry). This role oversee sub-regional sales operations, key account management, revenue growth, and customer relationship development while ensuring alignment with the company’s business objectives and profitability targets. The role requires strong leadership capabilities, strategic planning, and hands-on execution to achieve sustainable growth and strengthen the company’s market presence across ASEAN countries. Key Responsibilities Sales Strategy & Business Growth Develop and implement sub-region sales strategies to achieve revenue and profitability targets across ASEAN markets. Identify new business opportunities, market trends, and customer needs to expand the company’s market share. Drive sales growth through strategic partnerships, channel development, and key account acquisition. Monitor market conditions, competitor activities, and pricing strategies to maintain competitiveness. Sub-Region Sales Management Lead, coach, and manage country sales teams to ensure high performance and target achievement. Establish sales objectives, KPIs, and performance metrics for sub-region and country-level teams. Conduct regular sales reviews, forecasting, pipeline management, and business performance analysis. Ensure effective coordination and alignment among ASEAN sales teams. Key Account & Customer Relationship Management Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers and strategic partners. Support negotiations for major contracts and commercial agreements. Ensure high customer satisfaction and long-term business retention. Operational & Financial Management Take responsibility for financial performance to achieve agreed on targets regarding net sales, contribution margin and fixed costs Manage sub-regional sales budgets and ensure efficient resource utilization. Prepare sales forecasts, reports, and business performance presentations for management. Ensure compliance with company policies, commercial procedures, and sub-region regulations. Collaborate with operations, finance, marketing, and supply chain teams to support business objectives. Leadership & Team Development Provide leadership, mentorship, and capability development for sub-region sales teams. Foster a high-performance culture focused on accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Identify talent development and succession planning opportunities within the sales organization. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s Degree in Chemical Engineering or Chemistry Minimum 10 - 15 years of sales and business development experience in the chemical industry within multinational environments. Strong exposure with Polymer Dispersions product Proven experience managing sales teams. Strong track record in achieving sales growth and managing key accounts in Fiber Bonding & Adhesive, Paper & Carpet Dispersion Industry is a plus Experience in strategic planning, budgeting, and commercial negotiations. Skills & Competencies Strong leadership and people management skills. Excellent business acumen and strategic thinking. Strong negotiation and stakeholder management capabilities. Excellent communication and presentation skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced, multicultural environment. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Working Conditions Frequent regional travel across ASEAN countries is required. Ability to work across different time zones and multicultural environments.

jobs.name: Representante Técnico(a) de Vendas Senior (AGRO) - Campo Novo do Parecis/MT
jobs.description: Job Description: Representante Técnico(a) de Vendas Senior (AGRO) – Campo Novo do Parecis/MT Negócio “Juntos pelo seu legado”. Esse é o compromisso da BASF com o agricultor! Aqui, o campo está em nosso DNA e, por isso, estamos conectados às mais novas tendências do agronegócio mundial, oferecendo um amplo portfólio de produtos e serviços, com foco na rentabilidade e sustentabilidade dos negócios. Área Esta é uma área de vendas para o atendimento da venda direta de defensivos, sementes de soja Credenz e sementes de algodão Fibermax na região do Parecis. O foco é no sistema produtivo Algodão. A carteira é composta por  clientes ELO, clientes com grandes áreas cultivadas, com diversas unidades produtivas e estrutura organizacional complexa. Quer conhecer mais? Se inscreva no processo de seleção! Candidato(a) Você será responsável pela negociação, geração de demanda e relacionamento com esses clientes, sendo fundamental alto conhecimento técnico no cultivo de algodão. É importante possuir formação completa em Engenharia Agrônoma ou Agronomia, habilidade em negociações e experiência em geração de demanda/valor. Buscamos uma pessoa  proativa, com habilidade em fazer relacionamentos, empática, organizada e comunicativa. A área de abrangência é extensa e flexibilidade a viagens durante a semana é importante. Nossa química valoriza o talento e a diversidade de gênero, raça, idade, nacionalidade, crenças, orientação sexual, identidade e expressão de gênero, deficiências e todas as demais características, diferenças e semelhanças que fazem parte da diversidade humana. Convidamos você a se candidatar em nossas vagas!  #BASF_EuRecomendo Job Description Footer:

Bis zum 1. Juli gehörte unser Geschäft zur BASF. Im Rahmen der aktuellen Übergangsphase werden alle Stellenausschreibungen und der Bewerbungsprozess weiterhin auf der BASF-Plattform gehostet und daher vollständig im BASF-Branding angezeigt. Bitte lassen Sie sich davon nicht beirren: Alle Stellen sind Positionen bei Surventis und Ihre Bewerbung wird an uns übermittelt. Bei Surventis stellen wir die Kundinnen und Kunden in den Mittelpunkt, denken einen Schritt voraus und entwickeln Lösungen, die echten Mehrwert und nachhaltige Partnerschaften schaffen - we ge beyond the surface. Wir inspirieren einander, weiterzudenken, Verantwortung für unsere Arbeit zu übernehmen und ständig danach zu streben, das Mögliche zu erweitern. Wenn Sie Ihre ganze Persönlichkeit einbringen, das Offensichtliche hinterfragen und mit einem Team wachsen möchten, das es ernst meint – dann sind Sie hier richtig. WAS DICH ERWARTET Mittendrin statt nur dabei! In deinem Praktikum ab September für bis zu 6 Monate unterstützt du aktiv die Inventur unseres Containerbestandes von rund 15.000 Mehrwegcontainern am Standort. Alles auf einen Blick! Du erfasst systematisch alle Container im Werk und sorgst für eine vollständige und korrekte Bestandsaufnahme. Gemeinsam mit deinen Kolleg:innen kontaktierst du unsere Kunden, holst Bestandsmeldungen ein und stimmst die Containerbestände ab. Des Weiteren prüfst du Abweichungen zwischen System- und Ist-Beständen und unterstützt bei der Bereinigung des Inventarverzeichnisses. Nicht zuletzt erhältst du spannende Einblicke in das Containermanagement und logistische Prozesse eines industriellen Umfelds. WAS DU MITBRINGST laufendes Studium im Bereich Betriebswirtschaft, Logistik, Supply Chain Management oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang idealerweise erste praktische Erfahrungen, z. B. durch Praktika oder Werkstudententätigkeiten im kaufmännischen oder logistischen Umfeld zuverlässige, kommunikative und teamorientierte Persönlichkeit mit Eigeninitiative und Verantwortungsbewusstsein sorgfältige und strukturierte Arbeitsweise sowie Interesse an Bestandsprozessen und organisatorischen Abläufen gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse sowie sicherer Umgang mit MS Office, SAP-Kenntnisse von Vorteil WAS WIR BIETEN Du bist vom ersten Tag an Teil des Teams. Wir bieten eine attraktive Praktikumsvergütung sowie die Möglichkeit, teils mobil zu arbeiten. Du kannst unser modernes Fitnessstudio sowie weitere Fitnessangebote vergünstigt nutzen und Deine Pausen in unserem Betriebsrestaurant verbringen. Bei uns steigst Du in anspruchsvolle Arbeitsgebiete ein und übernimmst spannende Aufgaben und nach Möglichkeit eigenverantwortliche Teilprojekte in einem interdisziplinären Arbeitsumfeld. Deine Weiterentwicklung ist uns wichtig: Deine neuen Kolleginnen und Kollegen unterstützen Dich in Deiner Entwicklung, so gewinnst Du vielfältige Einblicke und Erfahrungen bei dem größten Chemieunternehmen weltweit. SO ERREICHST DU UNS Melissa Amina Rahmani (Talent Acquisition), melissa.rahmani@basf.com, Tel.:+4930200558073 beantwortet gerne deine Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne kannst du auch einen Online-Termin buchen. ÜBER UNS Mehr über unser Unternehmen erfahren Sie hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Ausbildung Chemikant:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description:
Ausbildung Chemikant:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profil-Login Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Ausbildung Chemikant:in (m/w/d) STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) Jetzt bewerben ELEMENTE DEINER AUSBILDUNG Ein Chemieunternehmen ohne seine Chemikant:innen? Unvorstellbar! Das wäre wie Zähneputzen ohne Zahnpasta, Joggen ohne Laufschuhe oder die perfekte Frisur ohne Haarspray. Neugierig, in welchen Produkten die BASF noch enthalten ist? Dann ist dieser Beruf genau das Richtige für Dich, denn Du bekommst nicht nur einen genauen Einblick in die spannende Produktwelt, sondern steuerst und überwachst die Produktion von vielen chemischen Erzeugnissen. Komm an Bord und nimm Fahrt auf bei einem der Weltmarktführer der chemischen Industrie. DAS LERNST DU WÄHREND DEINER AUSBILDUNG Erlernen der industriellen Herstellung unterschiedlicher chemischer Erzeugnisse und Produkte Steuern und Überwachen von Produktionsanlagen Mitwirken an der Weiterentwicklung von Produktionsvorgängen Entnahme und Überprüfung von Produktproben zur Sicherstellung gleichbleibend hoher Qualität Wartung, Kontrolle und Verantwortung für Produktionsanlagen Arbeiten nach höchsten Standards für Umwelt und Sicherheit DAS BRINGST DU MIT mindestens eine Berufsreife / einen Hauptschulabschluss Interesse an Naturwissenschaft, Mathematik und Technik körperliche Belastbarkeit für Tätigkeiten in der Anlage und im Produktionsbetrieb keine Angst vor größeren Höhen, z. B. bei Arbeiten an technischen Anlagen Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit sorgfältiges, eigenverantwortliches und teamorientiertes Arbeiten DAS BIETEN WIR IN DEINER AUSBILDUNG eine spannende Ausbildung im weltweit führenden Chemieunternehmen in Kooperation mit der BBS Naturwissenschaft in Ludwigshafen maßgeschneiderte Schulungsangebote sowie die Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme an EU-Austauschprojekten attraktive Vergütung – du startest mit 1147 Euro pro Monat im 1. Ausbildungsjahr zahlreichen Zusatzleistungen: Jahresprämie, Erfolgsbeteiligung, 700 Euro Urlaubsgeld, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Teilnahme am BASF Aktienprogramm, Tablet, Essensgeldzuschuss, Zuschuss Jobticket 30 Tage Urlaub und 37,5 Stunden-Woche Freizeit- und Fitnessangebote sowie Mitarbeiterevents Wohnmöglichkeiten für Azubis von weiter weg Verkürzung der Ausbildung um ein halbes Jahr bei besonders guten Leistungen möglich gute Übernahmechancen bei entsprechenden Leistungen Besonderheit an unserem Standort: Durch die Ausbildungskooperation mit der BASF SE in Ludwigshafen lernst Du schon während der Ausbildung zwei moderne BASF-Standorte kennen AUSBILDUNG GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN ? Du hältst die Produktion am Laufen – auch dann, wenn es darauf ankommt. Im Team entwickelst du dich mit jeder Schicht weiter. Dabei stehen dir vielfältige Entwicklungs- und Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten offen – fachlich, persönlich und perspektivisch auch über deine aktuelle Rolle hinaus. DEIN WEG ZU UNS: Richtig bewerben - so geht's! Neugierde, Offenheit und Motivation. Für uns steht deine Persönlichkeit im Vordergrund. Noten sind nicht alles. Im ersten Schritt des Auswahlverfahrens bleiben die Schulnoten daher erst einmal unberücksichtigt. Für uns ist der Gesamteindruck entscheidend. Daher LET´S MATCH & BEWIRB DICH JETZT: Bewerbungsstart: ab April 2026 Bewerbungsende: Es gibt keine Frist, du kannst dich bewerben, solange Plätze frei sind! DIE FÜNF SCHRITTE UNSERES AUSWAHLVERFAHRENS: Online-Test von zu Hause aus Eignungstest vor Ort bei der BASF Vorstellungsgespräch oder Video-Interview ggf. werksärztliche Untersuchung Zusage und Vertrag Der gesamte Auswahlprozess bei BASF am Standort Ludwigshafen dauert ungefähr 50 Tage bis zur Zusage. Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung! jobs.name: Sales Manager PU - Footwear, Sports & Leisure 1
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjecti v es of t h e P osi t ion To implement PU systems for Footwear,Sports & Leisure, Performance Materials India sales. To implement marketing strategy in new business development Initiate, develop & grow customer relationships with existing and potential customers. Execute PU system sales in Footwear,Sports & Leisure & develop business according to the country & regional strategy and objectives. Provide input for account plans, and update them periodically Implement pricing strategy according to regional & local strategic guidelines. Provide inputs for market intelligence. Strong collaboration with GKAMs. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Sales Development : Understand customer needs, industry drivers, unique selling points deeply to recommend the best solutions, leading to the achievement of value creation for the customers and BASF. Formulate strategy to develop and implement segment, application, product and customer strategies to achieve sales targets. Conclude business contracts and maintain business growth at existing/potential customers. Explore, identify, and develop new businesses. Implement pricing strategy and support launch of new products at key customers. Provide accurate sales forecasting and regular feedback from time to time. Support new product development activities for customers, follow up and collaborate with lab team to ensure timely development/Customization of the products meeting customer requirement. Collaborate with respective team (local and regional technical Service, Supply Chain, Customer Service, Marketing, etc.) to provide prompt response related to quality, technical and application’s inquiries and other customer related queries. Responsible for managing margins at product and customer level, Identifying and managing channel partners, implement pricing policy, trading terms, rebates regularly to meet sales targets. Responsible to effectively manage distribution channel/partners. Coordinate with Global / Regional Key Account Management team to manage local activities with OEM’S and Tiers wherever required. Strategic Tasks and Implementation: Conduct studies and analysis on market trend, competitors, product applications and technology and provide appropriate market strategies. Develop key account management profiles wherever applicable. Monitors product, customer and segment profitability and initiate improvement opportunities. Lead product/application launches within regional marketing strategy. Adopt and utilize digital tools and technologies in rolling out dashboards to allow for real-time business and operations excellence analysis Customer Management: Supports in providing the response related to quality, technical applications, inquiries, and other related issues; conducts customers’ assessments and analysis. Coordinate with BASF experts to conduct field visits, seminars, presentations and attend to customers’ inquiries. Conduct customers’ assessments and analyses to align and ensure the strategic focus in the right place. Ensure all supporting functions involved in the delivery of agreed customer’s needs; resolve problems/ expedite matters where appropriate Share Market Intelligence, strategy & communicate internally via visit reports Credit Management: Monitor the growth of the customers’ business, review and coordinate with credit controlling team to make appropriate credit recommendations. Follow-up on payment collections and ensure on-time payment from the customers. Innovation, Sustainability, Embrace Digitalization: Accelerates Innovation to achieve innovation sales target. Hunt/explore customer specific projects. Live Sustainability: supports the Implementation of A-PMA/E Sustainability roadmap in country with projects acquired which includes chemcycling, mechanical recycling, BMB solutions, Bio based Materials. Embrace Digitalization: Enhances aligned use of salesforce Implementation and enhances the use of social media available to PM (Wechat, LinkedIn etc.) for promoting more new applications, technical webinar, and customer interaction. Support e portal sales. Job Requirements Edu c a t i o n: Bachelor’s / Engineering / Polymer Science / Chemical degree; MBA degree is preferred. W o r k in g E x pe r i e n c e: Over 8-12 years technical sales working experience preferably with Polyurethane industry. T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge: Experience in Customer management, Negotiations and in handling Sales Operations, Business Development, in PU Products in footwear/polyurethanes Industry Working knowledge of polyurethanes chemistry, applications and processing. Good English and computer skills (MS. Office).

jobs.name: SAP Developer (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. RESPONSIBILITIES: Support Analyst with foundational knowledge in SAP S/4HANA Order-to-Invoice processes and a strong aptitude for structured troubleshooting across application, integration, and process layers. The role is part of the growing S/4 AMS team in Hyderabad and is designed for professionals who bring analytical thinking, process understanding, and a willingness to strengthen expertise in an ITIL-aligned support environment. The focus is on incident analysis, root-cause identification, and reliable issue resolution. Candidates are expected to work within defined support processes, escalate where needed, and continuously build knowledge on SAP Standard and Clean Core principles. Core competencies: SAP S/4HANA SD / Order-to-Cash, ITIL-aligned incident handling, structured problem analysis, ticket documentation, and escalation support Functional focus: Sales orders, deliveries, billing, pricing, output, integration aspects with other SAP modules (MM, FI/CO, TM) and basic understanding of EDI flows and intercompany or STO scenarios Technical understanding: Ability to read and interpret system logs, interface knowledge with focus on monitoring (CPI flows, API, Kafka), orientation in ABAP traces or debugging output; analysis capability is key Working style: Methodical, service-oriented, reliable in documentation, committed to following SAP Standard and avoiding custom workarounds. WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Scrum Master (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities : • Guiding and coaching the Scrum Team and DevHub in the effective use of Agile practices, creating an environment that enables teams to become self-managed and sustainably high performing. • Supporting and coaching the Product Owner by providing techniques to refine and prioritize the backlog, ensuring alignment with broader product and organizational priorities. • Assessing the agile maturity of teams and the platform and actively guiding them toward higher levels of effectiveness and continuous improvement. • Translating product priorities into clear team goals and actionable improvements. • Helping teams focus on high-impact work by reducing waste, limiting unnecessary complexity, and improving flow. • Adapting agile practices to the team context to ensure pragmatism, effectiveness, and continuous improvement. • Promoting strong communication and collaboration across the platform to support sound decision-making, shared ownership, and a common understanding of goals and agile ways of working. • Accompanying the Product Manager and key stakeholders in shaping the platform and supporting decision-making within an agile and, where applicable, scaled framework. • Identifying improvement opportunities and contributing to an agile roadmap together with the product family. • Improving meeting effectiveness through clear facilitation, purpose-driven collaboration, and a focus on outcomes. • Building a trusting and safe environment where challenges can be raised openly, with an emphasis on constructive problem-solving and continuous learning. • Supporting teams in resolving impediments, blockers, and conflicts autonomously wherever possible. • Facilitating discussions, decision-making, and conflict resolution, while supporting transparent and effective communication within and beyond the team. • Collaborating closely with other Scrum Masters and Agile Masters to share learnings, align practices, and improve consistency across teams. • Supporting agile transformation at team and product-family level. Requirements : Edu c a t i o n and work experience Bachelor Degree in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / Information Technology or business-technology related discipline Min. 3 years of professional experience in IT and / or IT Consulting C ertification in agile methodologies Scrum , LESs and scaled frameworks (additionally Kanban, SaFe valuated ) Plus: Professional coaching certification Plus: Certification in full stack tools ( preferably GitHub ) T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (mandatory) Relevant experience as an Agile leader (similar position as Agility/ Scrum Master) Experience as Scrum Master for a team/s that was/were diligently applying Scrum principles, practices, and theory. Excellent coaching skills - high level of empathy and experience in conflict resolution Workshops/meetings/events facilitation. Experienced in international working contexts. English language by default . T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge ( additional plus/nice to have skills) Trainer skills being capable to teach Agile, Scrum , and Sales Agile Frameworks content (Values, Principles, User Stories, Estimations, STATIK, Continuous Improvement, etc.). Additional knowledge in Kanban and OKRs highly appreciated Work experience in IT; specifically in development platforms D i r ect S ubo r dinate Position(s) Optional: Only for leadership positions Functional/Operational: NA Disciplinary/Local: NA WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Technical Sales Rep PA
jobs.description: Where the Chemistry Happens: The Technical Sales Representative will contact established and prospective customers in an assigned territory for the purpose of selling Chemetall surface treatment chemicals. This individual will provide complete sales and service which ensures safe quality product performance while maximizing profitability within their assigned territory. Formula for Success: Sales Analysis and Planning Understands the business activities of accounts in the territory and applies knowledge of the customer’s internal operation to tailor sales presentations and sales proposals to the account’s specific needs. Manages territory profitability by reviewing territory compensation margins, providing margin improvement by product selection and optimum pricing.  Assures that technology provided within the territory strengthens the company’s competitive position within the accounts. Plans a weekly itinerary of calls to allocate time effectively and to ensure proper territory coverage by industry, geography and account potential. Sales Execution Calls on established and prospective customers, determines their needs, and sells appropriate products to markets, targeted by the business units of the company. Makes proper number of calls per day based on territory requirements. Must be able to physically service and call on existing and prospective customers. Selects the process and the right material to solve customer cleaning, sanitation or surface treatment problems. Makes sales presentations, both orally and in written. Answers customers questions concerning product use. Price negotiation: Provide pricing quotes, price and delivery policy within established limits, subject to approval. Attain sales plan by securing new customers, increasing sales to current customers and maintaining up to date opportunity pipeline in CRM (Salesforces) Customer Service Works closely with customers focusing on building strong business relationships. Maintains contacts with customers in relation to their service requirements. Provides information on new products, methods, techniques and other general information such as safety and disposal. Trains customers and insures proper and safe use of Chemetall materials. Reports competitive activities, that impact sales in the territory, to the supervisor (District Manager) Prepares and submits call reports, exception reports, and in-plant service reports via CRM (Salesforce) Use of the CRM ( Salesforce) for management of the accounts. Share reports with appropriate with colleagues to ensure collaboration with different functions to keep all necessary information on all accounts Sales Support Activities Improves technical knowledge of Chemetall materials through self-study and participation in professional Association. Participates in team selling activities in cooperation with other TSR’s, technical support personnel and application engineers. Requests samples according to company policy. Exercises proper caution in handling company materials and equipment. Operates automobile (company car) safely and maintains it in proper operating condition. Complete all necessary reports in due time ( expenses, mileage report, etc.) Ingredients for Success: BS Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Engineering, or Business is required. Minimum of 2 years of relatable experience is required. B2B/outside industrial sales experience, in specialty chemical sales (surface treatment-paint pretreatments, wastewater treatments, metalworking fluids, etc.) preferred. Ability to make sales presentations and follow up with written proposals and manage customer contracts. Well organized with sales planning. Managing customer accounts within the assigned territory. Strong selling skills for customer acquisition and continuous up-/cross-selling opportunities (customer support/account management) Build and strengthen customer relationships. Technical consulting capabilities regarding products and processes; will learn and understand Chemetall product portfolio. Strong business acumen. Strong Negotiation skills Problem solving skills Time management Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. Travel up to 50-60%, with occasional overnight travel. Valid driver's license in good standing .

jobs.name: Officer, Procurement Operations & Process Excellence (1 Year Contract)
jobs.description:
About The Role - Execute and monitor purchase-to-pay process, to ensure delivery reliability targets and efficient end-to-end process - Assist internal departments (e.g. Supply Chain, Engineering, HR, Legal, etc.) in following procurement guidelines and purchase-to-pay procedures. - Actively steer buying channel usage to target high degree of automation - Ensure compliance with quality standards, documentation requirements, and procurement policies How You Will Do It - Conduct purchase-to-pay tasks (e.g. process Purchase Requisitions, generate Purchase orders), collaborating with various stakeholders (requisitioners, buyers, vendors, …) - Solve supply-related issues efficiently by monitoring order confirmations and clarify changes - Steer exception handling for purchase requisitions or coordinate order intake, further complete master data maintenance including document collection (e.g. certificates) - Train internal stakeholders to follow procurement guidelines and to use proper platform for request raising - Support in the implementation of procurement strategies - Coordinate the non-conformity investigation with internal and external stakeholders What We Look For - This role offers a 1-year fixed-term contract. - Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain - Min. 3 years working experience with procurement, sales or supply chain. Fresh Graduates are welcomed. - Skilled with ERP system. SAP purchase-to-pay module experience is a plus. - Good English skills in both writing and speaking. Additional language is a plus (Japanese, Thai) - Strong communicator with a service-oriented mindset, able to independently resolve inquiries and collaborate effectively with colleagues globally. - Open minded and passionate on taking challenging tasks. Als HR Officer übernehmen Sie eine zentrale Rolle entlang des gesamten Employee Lifecycles und tragen aktiv dazu bei, unsere HR‑Prozesse effizient und rechtssicher ablaufen. Dabei gestalten Sie den weiteren Aufbau der BASF Gastronomie GmbH aktiv mit. WAS SIE ERWARTET Beratung & Betreuung – Sie unterstützen Führungskräfte bei personalrelevanten Fragestellungen Recruiting & Onboarding – Sie wirken bei Recruiting‑Prozessen mit (z. B. Ausschreibungen, Onboarding) Vertragsmanagement & Administration – Sie unterstützen bei Ein- und Austritten sowie Vertragsänderungen unter Berücksichtigung arbeits- und tarifrechtlicher Vorgaben HR‑Projekte & Weiterentwicklung – Sie bringen sich in HR‑Projekte ein und treiben die Weiterentwicklung von Prozessen aktiv voran Schnittstellenmanagement – Sie arbeiten eng mit internen Partnern wie Payroll, Controlling und Betriebsrat zusammen Struktur & Compliance – Sie stellen eine strukturierte und rechtssichere HR‑Administration sicher WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Ausbildung – Abgeschlossene kaufmännische Ausbildung Erfahrung – Erste Berufserfahrung im HR‑Bereich von Vorteil, gerne auch Berufseinsteiger mit relevanter Praxiserfahrung IT‑Affinität – Sicherer Umgang mit MS Office sowie Interesse an digitalen HR‑Prozessen Struktur & Arbeitsweise – Sie arbeiten zuverlässig, eigenständig und behalten auch bei mehreren Themen den Überblick Kommunikationsstärke – Sie arbeiten gerne mit unterschiedlichen Zielgruppen zusammen und kommunizieren klar und wertschätzend Vertrauenswürdigkeit & Diskretion – Ein hohes Maß an Sensibilität im Umgang mit personenbezogenen Daten zeichnet Sie aus WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktives Gehalt – 12 Monatsgehälter über Gastroniveau Zusätzliche Extras – 13. Monatsgehalt als Jahresprämie, Urlaubsgeld und Zuschuss zum Jobticket Mehr Freizeit & Flexibilität – 30 Tage Urlaub, 5 Zukunftstage, Gleitzeit und mobiles Arbeiten Betriebliche Altersvorsorge – 100% Zuschuss: Wir verdoppeln Ihre Einzahlung Gesundheit & Wohlbefinden – Zugang zu Medical Center und Gesundheitsangeboten Karriere & Entwicklung – Individuelle Förderung, interne Schulungen und Weiterbildung in Digitalisierung & KI Gestaltungsspielraum & Innovation – Bringe eigene Ideen ein und gestalte die Transformation aktiv mit Starkes Miteinander & Diversität – Respekt, Toleranz und ein Team, das zusammenhält. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Melden Sie sich bei Katrin Statnick (Talent Acquisition): katrin.statnick@basf.com +49 30 2005-58118 Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier. Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier. ÜBER UNS Wir gestalten die Zukunft der BASF Gastronomie – verantwortungsvoll, nachhaltig und innovativ. Ob Qualitätssicherung, Arbeitssicherheit oder Personalentwicklung: Wir schaffen die Basis für Genussmomente und ein starkes Team. Bei uns zählt nicht nur, was auf dem Teller liegt, sondern auch, wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Sie oder Du? – Wir richten uns nach Ihnen Unsere Bewerbungsplattform verwendet in der schriftlichen Kommunikation automatisch die „Sie“-Form. In den Bewerbungsgesprächen können Sie uns aber jederzeit sagen, wenn Sie lieber geduzt werden möchten – wir richten uns da gerne nach Ihnen. jobs.name: Ferienhelfer:in Produktion/Logistik (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Ferienhelfer:in Produktion/Logistik (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Ferienhelfer:in Produktion/Logistik (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Im Bereich Performance Materials entwickeln und produzieren wir innovative Kunststoffe und Schäume für verschiedenste Anwendungen. Ob für Autos, Gebäude oder Sportartikel - wir suchen immer die ideale Materiallösung. Dafür arbeiten wir eng mit unseren Kunden zusammen: von Produktion und Logistik über Forschung und Vertrieb bis hin zu Bauteilprüfung, Designberatung und Simulation. WAS SIE ERWARTET Als Ferienhelfer:in unterstützen Sie während der vorlesungsfreien Zeit von Juni bis September für bis zu maximal 15 Wochen die Mitarbeiter:innen der BASF Polyurethans GmbH in der Produktion oder in der Logistik. In unseren Produktionseinheiten unterstützen Sie die Anlagenfahrer:innen an halbautomatischen Anlagen und tragen zu einem reibungslosen Produktionsablauf bei. Zudem übernehmen Sie die Fertigteilbearbeitung sowie einfache Kontrolltätigkeiten und stellen Produktionsmittel bereit. In unserem Logistikbereich übernehmen Sie die eigenständige Bereitstellung sowie Etikettierung der Warensendungen, kontrollieren den Warenausgang und stellen so den reibungslosen Versand der Sendungen sicher. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Sie möchten gern während Ihrer Semesterferien als Ferienhelfer:in in unserer Produktion und Logistik tätig werden? Außerdem sind Sie mindestens 18 Jahre alt und an einer deutschen Hochschule / Universität immatrikuliert? Zudem können Sie sicher in deutscher Sprache kommunizieren, sind zeitlich flexibel und besitzen vorzugsweise einen in Deutschland gültigen PKW-Führerschein. Nicht zuletzt bringen Sie die Bereitschaft mit im vollkontinuierlichem Schichtbetrieb zu arbeiten in der Produktion. Dann bewerben Sie sich ausschließlich online mit folgenden Unterlagen: aktueller Lebenslauf Immatrikulationsbescheinigung aktuelle Notenübersicht Angabe des Einsatzzeitraumes Angehörige von Staaten außerhalb des Europäischen Wirtschaftsraums und der Schweiz schicken bitte ihre Aufenthalts-/Arbeitsgenehmigung inkl. zugehöriges Zusatzblatt mit. WAS WIR BIETEN Wir bieten Ihnen die Mitarbeit in einem kompetenten Team mit attraktiven monatlichen Verdienstmöglichkeiten von mindestens 3.084,- Euro als Bruttoentgelt. Bei einer Tätigkeit in Wechselschicht erhalten Sie zusätzlich entsprechend anfallende Sonn-, Feiertags- und Nachtzuschläge gemäß dem Tarif der chemischen Industrie. Bitte achten Sie auf die Aktualität und Vollständigkeit Ihrer Bewerbungsunterlagen. Ohne aktuelle Dokumente können wir Ihre Bewerbung leider nicht berücksichtigen. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Michael Wiesenack-Flick (Talent Acquisition), michael.wiesenack-flick@basf.com , Tel.: +49 30 2005-58564 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH mit Sitz in Lemförde entwickelt, produziert und vertreibt maßgeschneiderte Polyurethan-Produkte und Problemlösungen, die das Leben komfortabler und sicherer machen. Unsere innovativen Spezialkunststoffe werden von Herstellern weltweit in nahezu allen Branchen erfolgreich eingesetzt. Mehr über die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH erfahren Sie unter: hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen.
