jobs.name: Executive - Customer Service, Demand Planner
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role To service the customers of the assigned Business Unit and order management Driving Demand Planning and Service Excellence Proactive Customer Orientation Communication Effectiveness Continuous Improvement How Will  You Do It Strategic- Key Activities: Perform sales and delivery order management according to SOP Monitor orders and communicate to customers & Sales team on order status Perform business rules and manage data related to order-to-cash process. Responsible to conduct Monthly S&OP meetings & provide key inputs to management for operations during meeting. Conduct regular analysis of supply chain performance metrics and identify areas for improvement. Coordinate with cross-functional teams i.e. operations, product management & controlling to optimize supply chain processes and resolve any issues or bottlenecks. Stay updated on industry trends, emerging technologies, and regulatory changes impacting the customer service operations or supply chain operations & taking necessary actions wherever required. Prepare reports and presentations to communicate supply chain performance and improvement initiatives to stakeholders. Develop & implement strategies for the disposition of obsolete inventory Analysing & improving supply chain processes & Operations to optimize the cost Operational: Inventory monitoring – local key user of SCII to report DIV, OIV, aging stock Forecast Accuracy – DP FCA reports (external and internal) Logistics Costs – monitor monthly development (filling, packaging, shipping, distribution, demurrage) NCM monthly report – local key user of NCM, report NCM KPIs monthly Delivery Services – local key user of DSC, report DC/DR monthly Respond to verbal (or written) requests in a timely and professional manner, ensuring any promises made are fulfilled Determine the appropriate solution by following operating procedures. Resolve issues without unnecessary delay and escalate more complex problems as required Consistently deliver work tasks on time and to agreed standards. Alert colleagues or manager in advance when timeline or quality of the deliverables is likely to be affected Proactively seek guidance and asks questions to understand structures, processes, systems and responsibilities, and how own role contributes to the broader team goals Creation of Sales Orders based on customer PO or Sales Forecast Tracking of orders, Sales & Customer Receivables on daily basis and highlighting issues if any Connect with Customer on periodic basis Customer AR follow up for payment purpose review credit limit with sales team Follow up with Customer for Call ups against the tender qty and to keep a track of the supplies to avoid oversupply. Forecast and plan the orders  to avoid / minimise delay penalty. Align with Sales colleague and transmit the orders accordingly Focus on digitalisation and utilization of digital tools Co-ordinate with transportation team for vehicle placement & reduced cost for the BU APO forecasting and updating the same in system Inventory control & review of aged inventory What Are We Looking For Education: Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Supply Chain or Logistics Mgt. Working Experience: 5-7 years of relevant experience in Logistics or Supply Chain function Soft Skills: MS Office (Power BI, Excel & Power Point), Proficiency in supply chain management software and tools (e.g., ERP systems, SAP R3, APO DP, SNP). Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret data and make informed decisions. Excellent organizational and time management abilities to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. Proficiency in supply chain management software and tools (e.g., ERP systems, SAP R3, APO DP, SNP). Effective communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with internal teams and external partners. Knowledge of industry best practices, regulations, and compliance standards in supply chain management. Ability to adapt to changing business needs and work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong attention to detail and a focus on accuracy in planning and execution. Professional certifications such as Certified Supply Chain Professional (CSCP) or APICS certification are a plus. Well versed with SAP Order To Cash (OTC) processes Good understanding of the products/services of relevant business segments Good knowledge of the business processes Service excellence competencies Ability to collaborate within and outside the team for servicing the customers Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai

jobs.name: Senior Officer, Order to Cash (Japanese Speaker)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position The primary function of this role is to provide efficient order management, manage interfaces with customers for order to cash processes and provide improvements to supply chain management. Effectively communicate and resolve order issues with customers and sales. Responsibility of placing replenishment purchase order to supply source and monitor the delivery schedule Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage Master Data maintenance process to ensure effective order management and the End-to-End order process by proactively collaborating with multiple functions. Capture market intelligence on the supply chain related topics from daily interaction with customers and share with the business as appropriate and be an integral part of business management. Contribute to development of customer relationships through proactive customer communication related to order management e.g. product availability, order status and delivery tracking. Support Credit Management, Billing and Accounts Receivable Follow-up. Issuing tax invoices for month end closing. Handle customer complaints in BASF’s available system (Non-Conformance Management, tNPS) and actively communicate status, investigation results, corrective actions to customers to ensure on time closure for non-product quality related complaints. Capture customer expectations / requirement and feedback to the business team. Ensure that all operations are carried out in compliant with business rules, internal standards, and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Provide order status report and monitor customers’ order placement against sales forecast and Joint-customer call to customers in case of request by sales team as sales supporter. Job Requirements Bachelor degree in business administration or familiar areas Experience in customer service in an international company for more than 2 years or relevant work experience in SCM/sales/controlling Chemical industry background preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Experience in using ERP systems such as SAP (at least intermediate level) Experience with data analytics or reporting systems (e.g. PowerBI) is a plus. Understanding of end-to-end OTC process Possess excellent communication, and interpersonal skills Good proficiency in Japanese language and English Proactive, can work independently and in teams About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.description: = Objectives = • Global Category Buyer of chemical raw materials in the global team Procurement Functional Intermediates for Direct Procurement department. • Ensure a competitive, sustainable and secured supply of raw materials to the internal business partners within the defined area of responsibility. Negotiate high-spend contracts and generate savings for BASFs divisions. • Responsible for contributing to develop, implement and monitor strategy for regional categories • Prepare and execute Request for Quotation (RfQ), Contract implementation & monitoring • Category Management • Lead sourcing projects and contributes in projects • Execute Supplier Management • Execute Category Risk Management = Main Tasks = Procurement Plan to Strategy: • Responsible for contributing to procurement strategies for Global Categories: • Provide input of Integrated Business Planning (iBP)’s needs and market knowledge (e.g. supply market structure and dynamics, strategies of suppliers, pricing structures, opportunities and threats) to Regional Category Manager • Support Global Category Manager on strategy development (e.g. strategic goals, strategic options, implementation plan) • Strategy implementation & follow-up: tackle action items Procurement Source to Contract: • Prepare and execute Request for X (RfX) within boundaries set by Global Category Manager (scope , RfX type and approach, iBP requirements, roles for negotiation, list of suppliers, alternatives to negotiated agreement • Evaluate incoming offers • Negotiation & contract award: determine optimal negotiation / award alternative, award goods to supplier(s) • Contract implementation & monitoring: manage contracts and decide on appropriate actions; jobs.name: Intern, Customs Operation
jobs.description: The main objective of this internship program is to provide students with comprehensive exposure to customs operations within the supply chain, specifically for the chemistry industry. Interns will gain practical understanding of customs-related topics, including HS code classification, analyzing the impact of duties and taxes in international trade, and exploring trade facilities such as free trade agreements, Authorised Economic Operator (AEO), duty drawback etc. Interning with the customs operation team offers valuable insights into international business practices and supply chain dynamics. This experience equips interns with specialized skills that are highly sought after in global trade and logistics, fostering professional growth and enhancing future career opportunities. Learn the basics of customs operations and their critical role in the supply chain. Study foundational customs knowledge and terminology relevant to the chemistry industry. Observe and map out the overview process flow of customs procedures. Understand HS code classification and its importance for accurate documentation and tax calculation. Explore the effects of duties and taxes on international business transactions in the chemistry sector. Gain knowledge about trade facilities, such as free trade agreements, Authorised Economic Operator (AEO), duty drawbacks etc. that benefit global commerce. Participate in improvement initiatives discussion to work on improvement plans and timelines. Participate in small assignments to apply gained knowledge and reinforce learning. About BASF in MalaysiaBASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my BASF – Malaysia

Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives: To support Production, Utility, QC, PPIC, and QC-Lab Department Main Responsibility: EHS & Process Safety related: Comply and follow all EHS regulations Work safely in accordance with the occupational safety rules applicable at PT BASF Indonesia, Merak plant. jobs.name: GB/G Head of Finance Services
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Head of Finance India will lead all finance activities for the Service Delivery Unit (SDU) in India, ensuring strong financial control, insightful business partnering, and effective delivery of finance services that support the SDU’s growth and operational excellence. This role combines strategic leadership, operational finance management, process optimization, and people leadership to enable data-driven decisions and alignment with global finance standards. Key Responsibilities Financial leadership & strategy Develop and execute the SDU’s strategy in alignment with global objectives. Serve as the primary finance business partner to the global Finance GBS community, translating financial implications of strategic and operational decisions. Responsible for budgeting, forecasting, long-range planning, and scenario analysis; drive financial discipline and performance. Operational finance & process delivery Manage end-to-end finance processes delivered by the SDU (e.g., accounts payable/receivable, closing cycles, management accounting), ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and high service levels. Identify and implement process improvements, automation, and standardization Manage capacity planning, workload balancing, and operational resilience for peak cycles Performance management & reporting Define and monitor KPIs and SLAs (closing time, accuracy, payments on time, etc.) Use metrics to drive improvement plans and communicate performance trends and risks Talent & team leadership Build, coach, and retain a high-performing finance team Set clear objectives, development plans, and performance expectations; foster a collaborative, continuous-improvement mindset. Ensure proper delegation, succession planning, and capability development. Governance & stakeholder management Maintain strong relationships with Regional/Global Finance, Tax, Legal, and business stakeholders. Ensure timely escalation of financial risks and provide recommended mitigation actions. Represent the SDU in global finance forums and ensure alignment with corporate policies. Qualifications & Experience Chartered Accountant / MBA Finance / CIMA or equivalent professional qualification preferred. 12+ years of progressive finance experience, with at least 5 years in leadership roles; experience in SDU, shared services, or business unit finance preferred. Solid exposure to FP&A, accounting, controllership, and finance operations in an international or matrixed organization. Proven track record of process improvement and implementing finance automation/ERP systems. Strong business partnering skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate strategy into financial plans. Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Demonstrated people leadership, team building, and talent development capabilities.

jobs.name: GB/G Head of HR Services
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Head of HR Services India will lead all HR Services activities for the Service Delivery Unit (SDU) in India, strategic and operational HR services owning delivery of transactional, strategic and platform-based HR services (HR operations, HRIS, payroll interfaces, service management, case resolution, and vendor management). This role combines strategic leadership, operational HR management, process optimization, and people leadership to enable data-driven decisions and alignment with global HR standards. Key Responsibilities Service delivery leadership Own end-to-end HR Services delivery for the SDU Ensure consistent, high-quality service through defined SLAs, KPIs, and service-level reporting. Operations & process excellence Design, implement, and optimize standardized, scalable HR processes and operating procedures aligned with global HR policies. Drive continuous improvement initiatives (automation, RPA, workflow redesign) to increase efficiency, reduce error rates, and shorten turnaround times. Manage capacity planning, workload balancing, and operational resilience for peak cycles (e.g., payroll, year-end processes). HRIS & technology stewardship Champion adoption of HR technology, self-service portals, and automation to improve employee and manager experience. Oversee reporting and analytics for service performance, data quality, and business insights. Vendor & partner management Manage relationships with external service providers (payroll vendors, benefits administrators, IT vendors, recruitment shared services) and ensure contractual SLAs are met. Coordinate with global HR Centers of Excellence and regional HR Business Partners to ensure seamless handoffs and aligned service models. Performance management & reporting Define and monitor KPIs and SLAs (case resolution times, accuracy, NPS/, time-to-hire handoff metrics, etc.) Use metrics to drive improvement plans and communicate performance trends and risks. Change & transition management Lead service transition projects (new processes, vendor transitions, system rollouts) and ensure minimal disruption to operations. Support global HR transformation programs by providing delivery expertise, implementation planning, and stakeholder coordination. Team leadership & capability building Build, lead, and retain a high-performing HR Services team focused on operational excellence and customer service. Set objectives, manage performance, and invest in upskilling for HR operations, HRIS, and service management competencies. Establish clear roles, escalation paths, and succession plans within the SDU HR Services function. Qualifications & Experience Advanced degree in HR, Business, or related field; HR certifications (e.g., SHRM, CIPD) a plus. 10+ years of progressive HR operations, HRIS, or shared services experience; 5+ years in leadership of HR service delivery or SSC/SDU operations. Proven experience managing HRIS landscapes, service desks, payroll/vendor relationships, and large-scale HR process delivery. Strong knowledge of HR service management best practices, process automation tools (RPA), and service reporting/analytics. Experience working in global/matrixed environments and coordinating with Centers of Excellence and business stakeholders. Demonstrated success in driving process standardization, technology adoption, and measurable service improvements. Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and vendor negotiation skills.

Local de publicação: Morumbi País: Brasil N° Requisição: 135852 Área de atuação: Marketing e Vendas Tipo de contrato: Efetivo Modelo de trabalho: Remoto Data de início da publicação: 14/07/2026 Concatenates: Cabeçalho da descrição do cargo: Descrição do cargo: Representante Técnico(a) de Vendas Senior (AGRO) – Campo Novo do Parecis/MT Negócio “Juntos pelo seu legado”. Esse é o compromisso da BASF com o agricultor! Aqui, o campo está em nosso DNA e, por isso, estamos conectados às mais novas tendências do agronegócio mundial, oferecendo um amplo portfólio de produtos e serviços, com foco na rentabilidade e sustentabilidade dos negócios. Área Esta é uma área de vendas para o atendimento da venda direta de defensivos, sementes de soja Credenz e sementes de algodão Fibermax na região do Parecis. O foco é no sistema produtivo Algodão. A carteira é composta por  clientes ELO, clientes com grandes áreas cultivadas, com diversas unidades produtivas e estrutura organizacional complexa. Quer conhecer mais? Se inscreva no processo de seleção! Candidato(a) Você será responsável pela negociação, geração de demanda e relacionamento com esses clientes, sendo fundamental alto conhecimento técnico no cultivo de algodão. É importante possuir formação completa em Engenharia Agrônoma ou Agronomia, habilidade em negociações e experiência em geração de demanda/valor. Buscamos uma pessoa  proativa, com habilidade em fazer relacionamentos, empática, organizada e comunicativa. A área de abrangência é extensa e flexibilidade a viagens durante a semana é importante. Nossa química valoriza o talento e a diversidade de gênero, raça, idade, nacionalidade, crenças, orientação sexual, identidade e expressão de gênero, deficiências e todas as demais características, diferenças e semelhanças que fazem parte da diversidade humana. jobs.name: Production Operator生产操作员
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. 主要工作职责 ： -     安全环保：  严格遵守BPCH的安环保制度    及时指出任何在BPCH内违反安环保制度的行为并汇报确保负责区域环境卫生状况持续改进和提高，确保工完, 料尽,场地清正确处理突发意外事件并在***时间汇报准时参加5分钟安全教育认真进行现场交接班正确使用并维护劳防用品，防护设施和器械 -     生产：  按时完成下达的生产任务和工作量，严格按照操作规程、作业指导书准确操作，实现岗位操作规范化对本人负责的所有与反应或后反应相关的原料配制及投料和转料过程全面负责， 投料前复核原料名称及数量；巡回检查反应状况和物料状况，如有问题及时提出，并寻求解决的方法包装或放料时应做到份量准确、标签准确，整齐划一，桶外表清洁，并确保放料进度及时如实地做好操作记录认真完成相关的工艺清洗及设备的高压清洗，以及设备恢复投料前的密封和检测工作 -     质量和成本：  确保操作卡严格执行并及时准确填写确保及时取样并送分析及时如实汇报质量问题，降低不合格率以及质量有关的客户投诉。生产过程中，巡回检查控制参数合理节约成本，避免资源浪费（原料，公用工程，包装材料，辅助材料）；严格按规定排放废水和其他废弃物。主动积极提出合理化建议 -     设备：  巡回检查设备，及时提出问题并在领班/班长协助下解决检查、保养，整洁和报修所负责的设备根据设备操作程序正确使用设备在领班安排下担当维修监护/协调人,严格执行许可证制度 -     技术:  接受公司组织的各项培训熟悉本岗位各项操作规程与技术，进行一岗多能的学习；能够独立完成本岗位工作学习并拓展本生产线的生产及设备使用技术具备操作与本岗位相关的一些特种设备操作资质 -     纪律:  遵守公司的各项劳动纪律规章制度保持高出勤率，确保完成工作定额。服从 主管/领班的工作安排。在完成本职工作的前提下，主动对其他同事给予工作支持做到与同事有良好地工作关系 。 学历/资质要求: 教育背景:  大专或以上。 专业要求： 化学工程与工艺, 高分子材料与工程, 化工工艺, 应用化工技术,精细化工, 石油化工生产技术等相关专业 工作及经历要求: 化工行业1年以上工作经验。 Competency 要求: 具备相应化工生产操作的技能和知识，能独立进行聚合现场的操作。 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用。

jobs.name: Counsel, Legal & Compliance Services Hub - Thai Speaker
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main Tasks You will draft, review, and negotiate local or international legal and intellectual property contracts, using templates and playbooks within defined limits. You will provide legal and compliance support on various matters, including due diligence, merger and acquisition projects, audits, and legal investigations. You will manage the contract lifecycle, ensure process efficiency, and contribute to the maintenance and improvement of contract templates and knowledge management systems. You will interact with internal clients and external customers as required for your assigned tasks. You will perform ancillary tasks such as preparing presentations, using legal tech tools, and handling translations. Requirements University degree in Law, preferably with a focus on commercial law Experience in draft and legal review of contracts, preferably with commercial law emphasis At least 3 years of professional experience in legal and commercial contract management, preferably wit h knowledge or practice of both common-law and civil-law systems Experienced in drafting and performing legal review of contracts, preferably with an emphasis on commercial law Fluent in English (and Thai and/or Korean language as an advantage), enabling you to successfully contribute to an international environment. Experienced in working with legal databases and standard IT applications Able to communicate effectively and tailor messages to the target audience, while leveraging value-creating opportunities for collaboration both in-house and across units Strong analytical skills, a goal-oriented working style, and a pronounced customer orientation make you a valuable member of our team

jobs.name: Payroll & People Data Analyst - Mandarin Speaker (Leasing, 12 month contract)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Seek to understand business needs and provide support, services and solution on payroll, HR data, HR systems and processes. Offer day-to-day support to service recipients on HR inquiries, work collaboratively with others and perform research on payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislations in local countries. Participate in process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Main Tasks Operational Excellence Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data processes. Receive, analyze, process and follow-up on personnel administration requests via multiple request channels, ensure accurate and timely interface to the respective internal/ external processing chains i.e. payroll, finance, data analytics, external service providers, local authorities etc. Manage entire termination workflow for all termination types which includes leaves calculations, information checks, letters preparation, systems updates, payment calculations and exit clearance. Responsible for time and attendance administrations, including manual calculations on time allowances/deductions, maintenance of work schedules, leaves, overtimes, and shift substitutions. Support the delivery of payroll inputs and administration i.e. employee subsidies, fleet management, long service awards etc. Support the salary review exercises on employee data preparation, letter generations and new salary processing. Responsible for the local labor administrative activities and scheduled statutory HR reporting, ensure timely and compliant deliveries as per legislation requirements. Responds to recurring or ad-hoc business needs to design and to deliver country specific or customized reports which could not be auto-generated by the analytics/reporting systems. Work closely with other teams and external service providers to ensure all service requests are completed accurately and timely. Service Engagement Providing advisory on employee enquiries, policy/guidelines clarifications, HR processes, HR systems guides, legislation processes etc. Monitor multiples service requests to ensure services delivered by the team are in accordance with established Service Level Agreement (SLA) and internal Key Performance Indicators (KPI). Perform research and information gathering on local market HR news, legislation updates and guidelines to maintain internal knowledge database. Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and People data to develop the formation of competence hub. Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case, and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target. Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience Identify deviations from the established blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes and escalate to Assistant Manager and Manager in ensuring the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time. Team Collaboration Works with moderate supervision within established procedures and practices. Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously sharing knowledge with others. Assist in orientation training and act as buddy to new team member. Job Requirements Education Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce or Business Administration and Finance Work Experience 1–2 years of experience in Payroll and HR Operations, or fresh graduates with relevant internship experience Proficient in Mandarin to support China-based customers Preferably experience in relevant HR procedures, practices, and country statutory Preferably working knowledge of integrated payroll systems preferably on SAP. Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrates a good level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location. Preferably understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. Strong processing skills and analytical abilities (inclination for Mathematics/Finance/Problem solving). Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills.

jobs.name: Customer Site Service Manager
jobs.description:
Now Hiring! Customer Site Service Manager Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) Ford Michigan Assembly Plant Location: Wayne, MI We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! This position supports the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The role responds to customer requirements, enforces product quality/performance specification, and supports new product/application development. The Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) will provide on-site expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. S/he will develop and support customer relationships. This role supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis and resolution. The position assures product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. The incumbent will demonstrate technical and professional expertise to enhance Surventis reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. As Customer Site Service Manager, you will be responsible for... Supporting the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. If you have... Bachelor’s Degree or High School Diploma (or GED) plus Equivalent Experience 6+ years of Automotive Coatings Industry experience preferred. Leveraging your education and extensive background in paint applications and manufacturing you will provide on-site expertise of products, their application and performance. Your superior communication abilities will allow you to influence all levels of the customer organization, implement action plans and develop and achieve customer metrics. Your strong entrepreneurial drive and business acumen will allow you to identify improvement opportunities, develop and execute a plan to achieve them in a cost-effective and timely manner as well as manage future plant production and product launches. Your strong leadership ability as well as your priority management skills will allow you to lead and coach a team and identify development opportunities and execute development plans. Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Pay transparency Surventis is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $113,680-$128,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. jobs.name: HR Administration & Payroll Specialist mit Französisch (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: HR Administration & Payroll Specialist mit Französisch (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung HR Administration & Payroll Specialist mit Französisch (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN IM TEAM Die Einheit "Global Business Services" unterstützt die Geschäftsbereiche der BASF mit einem funktionsübergreifenden Netzwerk und bietet dafür moderne und bedarfsgerechte Lösungen. Als spezialisierte Serviceeinheit stehen wir für eine hohe Kundenorientierung und gestalten unsere Dienstleistungen partnerschaftlich, effizient und zukunftsweisend. IHRE AUFGABEN Im HR Services Team France erwartet Sie ein vielfältiges und inklusives Team aus Expertinnen, Experten und jungen Talenten. Gemeinsam setzen wir uns dafür ein, unseren BASF Partnern in Frankreich einen exzellenten Service zu bieten. Zur Verstärkung unseres Administration & Payroll Teams suchen wir einen HR Specialist (m/w/d) mit Interesse an Payroll, HR-Prozessen und Kundenservice. Sie bearbeiten variable Daten zur Vorbereitung der monatlichen Gehaltsabrechnung und tragen zur Optimierung administrativer HR-Prozesse in unseren Systemen bei. Sie erstellen monatliche und jährliche HR-Reports. Sie stellen sicher, dass alle gesetzlichen und regulatorischen Anforderungen in unseren HR-Prozessen eingehalten werden. Sie beraten Mitarbeitende in Frankreich zu HR-relevanten Fragestellungen. Sie arbeiten eng mit unseren lokalen HR-Teams in Frankreich sowie mit internen Abteilungen in Berlin und externen Dienstleistern zusammen. Sie wirken aktiv an internen Projekten mit, identifizieren Verbesserungspotenziale und fördern den Austausch von Best Practices in unseren europäischen HR-Teams. Sie unterstützen die Digitalisierung unserer HR-Services durch das Einbringen von Ideen und den Austausch mit internationalen Kolleginnen und Kollegen. QUALIFIKATIONEN Sie haben eine kaufmännische Ausbildung oder ein Studium im Bereich Personalwesen, Payroll oder einem vergleichbaren Bereich abgeschlossen und verfügen über erste Berufserfahrung im HR-Umfeld, einschließlich erster Erfahrungen in der Entgeltabrechnung (Payroll). Aufbauend auf dieser ersten Erfahrung möchten Sie Ihre Kenntnisse im Bereich Payroll weiter vertiefen und Ihr Wissen zu gesetzlichen Regelungen (Sozialversicherung, Steuern, Zeitwirtschaft, Compensation & Benefits) ausbauen. Sie arbeiten sicher mit HR-Systemen (idealerweise SAP HR) und sind im Umgang mit Excel routiniert. Sie verfügen über fließende Französischkenntnisse (zwingend erforderlich) sowie gute Englischkenntnisse. Deutschkenntnisse sind von Vorteil. Sie arbeiten gerne mit Menschen zusammen und überzeugen durch eine serviceorientierte und freundliche Kommunikation. Sie zeichnen sich durch eine strukturierte Arbeitsweise sowie ausgeprägte analytische und organisatorische Fähigkeiten aus, auch bei engen Zeitvorgaben. WAS WIR BIETEN Jahresbruttogehalt (Vollzeit): 40.000 – 46.000 €, abhängig von Qualifikation und Erfahrung, zusätzlich Bonus abhängig vom Unternehmenserfolg 30 Urlaubstage, Sonderurlaub und Sabbatical-Optionen Flexible Arbeitszeiten für eine gute Work-Life-Balance Modernes, nachhaltiges Büro in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg mit flexiblen Arbeitsmöglichkeiten, Dachterrasse und Cafeteria Team- und Unternehmensevents zur Förderung des Zusammenhalts Hybride Arbeitsmodelle mit definierten Präsenztagen in Berlin Zuschuss zum öffentlichen Nahverkehr sowie weitere Mitarbeitervorteile Mobiles Arbeiten aus anderen EU-Ländern für bis zu sechs Wochen SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie hier . Weitere Informationen zum Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . Bei BASF stimmt die Chemie. Denn wir setzen auf Innovation in unseren Lösungen, auf Nachhaltigkeit in unserem Handeln und auf Verbundenheit in unserem Denken. Und auf Sie. Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und entwickeln Sie mit uns die Zukunft - in einem globalen Team, das Vielfalt lebt und sich für Chancengerechtigkeit unabhängig von Alter, Herkunft, Geschlecht, sexueller Identität, Behinderung, Religion oder Weltanschauung einsetzt. jobs.name: Sales Manager, Rigids PU - Performance Materials
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Sales: To plan and direct sales and marketing activities in a specific Zone (major geographic area)/ construction segment in order to achieve agreed sales volume, pricing, margins and selling cost objectives by implementing consistently the overall sales strategy, using technical, organizational and customer knowledge. To ensure timely payment of receivables from customers In addition, provide inputs and participate in the marketing, market planning and technical development of products and services. Specifications and BD: Establishing rapport with Specifiers - architects, consultants and designers in public and private organizations in particular with RDSO, EIL, HPCL, ONGC and know about all their upcoming projects. Focusing on Specifiers in chosen segments for specification selling in line with our Segment Strategy, to get our systems specified at the design stage of projects. Generation of sales potential (Volume) meeting quarterly and yearly targets through specification of our systems and through breaking competitors’ specifications in our favour. Assisting in organising technical seminars, etc to key Specifiers organisations. Main Tasks Sales: Stimulate and develop market intelligence to pursue market growth and deepen market penetration, e.g. attracting new customers (including distributors), applications, and innovations. Take responsibility for the overall sales process and coordination of interfaces to other departments/functions. Monitor & control local DSO and DIV Develop, implement, and monitor agreed sales strategies (including pricing implementation) To maintain strategic long term business relationship with customers To Provide timely Forecast accurately & make close co ordination with SCM to manage the RM and FG stock effectively. Specifications: No. of specifications achieved Vs objective in targeted key organisations and their potential sales value. Knowledge of construction related problems and system solutions. jobs.name: Global IT Process Owner HRIS (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Location Madrid (hybrid) WELCOME TO OUR TEAM If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a Global IT Process Owner HRIS – Time Management systems, you are the application manager and solution architect for the global Coatings HRIS application landscape with focus on Time Management systems. Your main responsibility is the design, development, and implementation of a dedicated and global IT landscape for Human Resources including a greenfield HRIS implementation. This landscape supports current business objectives and ensures compliance with legal and internal regulations, always in close collaboration with the EC HR department. In your role as Global Process Owner, you will also understand closely related systems such as HCM and Payroll. You build the bridge to our internal stakeholders and translate business requirements into IT demands. During projects and ongoing maintenance, you collaborate closely with technical implementation partners and independently lead rollouts and projects. Your consultancy is valued by our internal partners and leads to a lean but innovative future IT set-up. WHAT WE OFFER Job Grade: 4 or 5, depending on qualification and experience. Continuous training and professional development with our internal vacancies. Safe environment where we promote your health. Restaurant ticket. Competitive economic offer with a base salary plus a variable for good performance. Hybrid work model and adapt your schedule according to your circumstances. Connect outside the office and practice sports with colleagues through company groups. Flexible remuneration that you can voluntarily allocate to transportation services, training, childcare, etc. Pension plans, life and accident insurance, personal loans and participation in company stock programs. Financial aid for schooling and other measures for families. Employees losing their job as a result of restructuring projects are given priority when filling positions. WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU Bachelor's Degree in Business Informatics, Computer Science, or Business Administration with a focus on IT and/or HR. Solid knowledge of IT landscapes and IT processes in the Human Resources area, ideally with ADP Workforce Management or SAP Success Factors Time Off or comparable. Proven track of records for project management skills and techniques. Several years of experience with IT projects, in a leading position, in the HR context is desirable. Strong communication and presentation skills in English and Spanish. Further languages are an advantage. Analytical skills and enjoyment of teamwork to achieve optimal results together with internal and external partners. WHAT WE OFFER Continuous training and professional development with our internal vacancies. Safe environment where we promote your health. Restaurant ticket. Competitive economic offer with a base salary plus a variable for good performance. Hybrid work model and adapt your schedule according to your circumstances. Connect outside the office and practice sports with colleagues through company groups. Flexible remuneration that you can voluntarily allocate to transportation services, training, childcare, etc. Pension plans, life and accident insurance, personal loans and participation in company stock programs. Financial aid for schooling and other measures for families. HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE PROCESS Send your CV in English through the job offer so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, we will call you from the Spanish Talent Acquisition team in Germany (prefix +49) and support you throughout the process. With each step, you will be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any further questions about this process, please contact: Antonio (antonio.casas-castro@basf.com*) *Please note that we will not be able to accept your resume via this email. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: Assistant Manager, Sales - Engineering Plastic (Guangzhou)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. How You Will Do It Sales Development - Fully responsible and accountable for profitable business growth for assigned customers - Develops sales plans; secures and expedites orders. - Supervises and consolidates the accurate sales forecast. Strategic Tasks and Implementation - Identifies key accounts and develops key account profiles. - Implements the sales and marketing directions per agreed directions Product/Industry/Business Development - Scouts for new business opportunities in the respective sales areas / key accounts and develops a business plan. - Suggests product developments and requirements to Sales Manager or LBM and support its implementation. Customer Management - Conducts field visits, seminars, presentations and attends to customers’ inquiries. - Provides prompt response related to quality, technical applications, inquiries, and other related issues; conducts customers’ assessments and analysis. - Aligns with Regional Business Unit and functions to solve customers’ issues. Credit Management - Monitors the growth of the customers’ business, reviews and coordinates with credit control to assure appropriate credit recommendations. - Follows up on payment collections and ensures customer’s on-time payment Marketing - Implements pricing policy and supports product launch processes. - Monitors and reviews marketing/promotion campaign’s execution. - Supports public relation and marketing communication campaigns for related business. Market Intelligence - Conducts studies/analysis on market trend, competitors, products’ applications and technology, service needs. Innovations, Sustainability, Embrace Digitalization - Accelerates Innovation to achieve innovation sales target - Live Sustainability: supports the Implementation of A-PMA/E Sustainability Roadmap in country with projects acquired which includes Chemcycling, Mechanical Recycling, BMB solutions, Bio based Materials - Embrace Digitalization: Enhances aligned use of salesforce Implementation and enhances the use of social media available to PM (Wechat, LinkedIn and etc.) for promoting more new applications, technical webinar, and customer interaction What We Look For - Bachelor or Master's Degree in Polymer, Chemistry, Material Science, Chemical Engineering, Material Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or equivalent. - Minimum 5 years technical sales working experience preferably with Engineering Plastics industry. - In-depth knowledge of Engineering Plastics, technical know-how and applications. - Tooling, Plastics Compounding and Application Development experiences would be highly desirable. - Have hands-on experience with plastic / polymer processing, plastic processing equipment experience in aspects of injection molding, extrusion, blow molding, etc. would be highly advantageous. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用

jobs.name: Transport Management (Executive)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position: Responsible for ensuring transport management for inbound or outbound (as assigned) are as per stipulated guidelines, procedures and requirements Support process improvement, knowledge and risk management as per overseas delivery services M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Outbound Processing transports for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our customers in compliance with the required customer deadlines at cost-optimal conditions Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and customer-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation of all necessary transport documents, control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Inbound Processing transport for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our sites within EMEA Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and site-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates, and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation and monitoring of all necessary transport documents (e.g verifying draft documents), control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Job Requirements: Edu c a t i o n: Fresh graduates from programs such as Transport Management, IT, Business Studies, or other relevant fields who are interested in exploring a career in supply chain. W o r k in g Experience: 1-2 years in logistics Career starters or first professional experience in transport management T e c hn i c al & Professional Knowledge: Experience in supply chain operations, distribution and warehousing processes Knowledge of the processes of filling and shipping units Experience in handling of MS Office products and, ideally, SAP R/3 quality management and transport management Structured, independent and process-oriented working width High degree of customer orientation High proficiency in spoken and written English The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com.

Objectives of the Position The Purchase To Pay provides invoice verification services for Inbound / Outbound supply chain activities. It is the focal point for the relevant invoice verification activities before payment is initiated to the service providers. The analyst, will execute the process with a consistent high level of service to be able to meet the growing needs of the businesses in Asia Pacific Main Tasks Operations and Performance: Check and verify inbound and outbound invoices against contracted / approved Spot rates from service providers according to guidelines Coordinate with service providers / local country Supply Chain / Finance teams for invoice clarifications and corrections to avoid service interruptions Provide full and accurate supporting documents to Finance team for vendor payment Understand charging mechanism and process for relevant Supply Chain services Participate proactively in new workflows or process improvements for on time and accurate verification services Identify risk related to the process and propose mitigations Troubleshooting and problem solving Support and provide documents for internal and external audit purpose Requirements Bachelor degree in Business or Supply Chain Management or Finance and Accounting. 2-4 years relevant experience in Purchase to pay, supply chain management/ shipping management, including hands-on experience in dealing with logistics service provider and shipping invoice handling. Supply Chain Management or Finance and Accounting /process knowledge, SAP Transportation Management experience, including hands-on experience in operational shipping / logistics management, good communication skills. BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre was established in 2005. Located in Kuala Lumpur, the regional service hub has approximately 1,100 employees and growing. Focused on being a competency and digitalization driven hub, it also provides quality functional and know-how services in the areas of finance, controlling, human resources, real estate and facilities management, communications, environmental, health and safety, procurement, import-export order management, and information services to BASF companies and affiliates in the Asia Pacific region. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay that cover the Europe and Americas regions. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 111,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €87.3 billion in 2022. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the U.S. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: QA Engineer (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Ubicación Madrid (híbrido) WELCOME TO OUR TEAM As one of the world's leading companies in this field, we develop and manufacture coating solutions for the automotive and industrial sectors. The success of our team is based on innovative solutions and close cooperation with our customers. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT You help deliver high-quality web applications that improve BASF Coatings services for internal and external users. You work in an agile, cross-functional DevOps team and support the product lifecycle from concept to go-live, with a strong focus on quality assurance and test automation. You help define risk-based test approaches and design automated testing solutions to ensure applications meet their functional and non-functional quality goals. You build a strong understanding of business processes, user journeys, and application workflows to create meaningful, risk-based test coverage. You collaborate closely with software developers to validate new features, identify defects early, and improve overall testability. You design, build, and maintain automated tests for functional, regression, and integration scenarios, and integrate them into CI/CD pipelines to support reliable quality checks across multiple applications. You use modern QA tools and frameworks, such as Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Postman, or SOAP UI, to test web applications and APIs. You align closely with product owners, designers, and developers on acceptance criteria, test coverage, and shared quality expectations. You contribute to a reliable automated QA process by reviewing test results, supporting quality gates, and helping development teams identify and resolve issues early in close collaboration with an international QA team. You document test approaches, test cases, and quality results in a structured way to support transparency, maintainability, and continuous improvement. WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU 3+ years of professional experience in software quality assurance, software testing, or quality engineering in an IT environment. Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a comparable qualification gained through practical experience. Experience creating and maintaining test plans, test cases, test reports, and structured testing documentation. Experience identifying, documenting, and validating defects, as well as testing web applications and APIs; exposure to cloud-based environments is a plus. Hands-on experience with at least one automated testing framework such as Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, or Cucumber/Gherkin. Practical experience with common QA tools for API and functional testing, such as Postman or SOAP UI. Practical experience with at least one programming language used for test automation, such as JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python. Familiarity with service-oriented or microservice-based architectures. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines or interest in working with automated quality checks in delivery pipelines. Experience working across multiple applications or projects and the ability to balance testing priorities in a dynamic environment. A structured, analytical, and collaborative way of working, with strong problem-solving and self-organization skills. Strong communication skills in Spanish and English, both written and spoken, to collaborate effectively across locations and time zones. Familiarity with agile methodologies such as Scrum or Kanban and experience working in agile development teams. WHAT WE OFFER Continuous training and professional development with our internal vacancies. Safe environment where we promote your health. Restaurant ticket. Competitive economic offer with a base salary plus a variable for good performance. Hybrid model of work and adapt your schedule according to your circumstances. Connect outside the office and practice sports with colleagues through company groups. Flexible remuneration that you can voluntarily allocate to transportation services, training, childcare, etc. Pension plans, life and accident insurance, personal loans and participation in company stock programs. Financial aid for schooling and other measures for families. HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE PROCESS Send your CV in English through the job offer so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, we will call you from the Spanish Talent Acquisition team in Germany (prefix +49) and support you throughout the process. With each step, you will be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any further questions about this process, please contact: Vanessa (vanessa.morales-soto@basf.com*) *Please note that we will not be able to accept your resume via this email. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.description:
QA Engineer (m/f/d) Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Buscar ofertas de empleo Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Acceso al perfil Acceso empleado/a Descripción del puesto QA Engineer (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Enviar candidatura ahora Ubicación Madrid (híbrido) TE DAMOS LA BIENVENIDA A BASF Como una de las empresas líderes del sector a escala mundial, desarrollamos y fabricamos pinturas para los sectores del automóvil e industrial. El éxito de nuestro equipo se basa en soluciones innovadoras y una estrecha colaboración con nuestros clientes. LO QUE PUEDES ESPERAR Ayudas a ofrecer aplicaciones web de alta calidad que mejoran los servicios de BASF Coatings para usuarios internos y externos. Trabajas en un equipo DevOps ágil y multifuncional y apoyas el ciclo de vida del producto desde el concepto hasta la puesta en marcha, con un fuerte enfoque en el aseguramiento de la calidad y la automatización de pruebas. Ayudas a definir enfoques de prueba basados en riesgos y a diseñar soluciones de pruebas automatizadas para garantizar que las aplicaciones cumplan sus objetivos de calidad funcional y no funcional. Desarrollas una sólida comprensión de los procesos de negocio, los recorridos de usuario y los flujos de trabajo de las aplicaciones para crear una cobertura de pruebas significativa y basada en riesgos. Colaboras estrechamente con desarrolladores de software para validar nuevas funcionalidades, identificar defectos de forma temprana y mejorar la capacidad de prueba general. Diseñas, desarrollas y mantienes pruebas automatizadas para escenarios funcionales, de regresión e integración, y las integras en pipelines de CI/CD para respaldar controles de calidad fiables en múltiples aplicaciones. Utilizas herramientas y frameworks modernos de QA, como Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Postman o SOAP UI, para probar aplicaciones web y APIs. Te alineas estrechamente con product owners, diseñadores y desarrolladores en cuanto a criterios de aceptación, cobertura de pruebas y expectativas compartidas de calidad. Contribuyes a un proceso fiable de QA automatizado revisando resultados de pruebas, apoyando puertas de calidad y ayudando a los equipos de desarrollo a identificar y resolver incidencias de forma temprana en estrecha colaboración con un equipo internacional de QA. Documentas enfoques de prueba, casos de prueba y resultados de calidad de forma estructurada para apoyar la transparencia, la mantenibilidad y la mejora continua. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Más de 3 años de experiencia profesional en aseguramiento de la calidad del software, testing de software o ingeniería de calidad en un entorno IT. Grado universitario en Informática, Ingeniería de Software o una cualificación comparable adquirida mediante experiencia práctica. Experiencia en la creación y mantenimiento de planes de prueba, casos de prueba, informes de prueba y documentación estructurada de testing. Experiencia en la identificación, documentación y validación de defectos, así como en pruebas de aplicaciones web y APIs; la experiencia en entornos cloud se valorará positivamente. Experiencia práctica con al menos un framework de automatización de pruebas como Playwright, Cypress, Selenium o Cucumber/Gherkin. Experiencia práctica con herramientas comunes de QA para pruebas de APIs y pruebas funcionales, como Postman o SOAP UI. Experiencia práctica con al menos un lenguaje de programación utilizado para la automatización de pruebas, como JavaScript, TypeScript o Python. Familiaridad con arquitecturas orientadas a servicios o basadas en microservicios. Familiaridad con pipelines de CI/CD o interés en trabajar con controles de calidad automatizados en pipelines de entrega. Experiencia trabajando en múltiples aplicaciones o proyectos y capacidad para equilibrar prioridades de testing en un entorno dinámico. Una forma de trabajar estructurada, analítica y colaborativa, con sólidas habilidades de resolución de problemas y autoorganización. Sólidas habilidades de comunicación en español e inglés, tanto escritas como orales, para colaborar eficazmente entre distintas ubicaciones y zonas horarias. Familiaridad con metodologías ágiles como Scrum o Kanban y experiencia trabajando en equipos de desarrollo ágiles. LO QUE OFRECEMOS Formación continua y desarrollo profesional con nuestras vacantes internas. Entorno seguro y donde promovemos tu salud. Ticket restaurante. Oferta económica competitiva con un salario base más un variable por buen desempeño. Modelo híbrido de trabajo y adaptar tus horarios, según tus circunstancias. Conectar fuera de la oficina y practicar deportes con compañeros/as mediante Grupos de empresa. Retribución flexible que puedes destinar voluntariamente a servicios de transporte, formación, guardería, etc. Planes de pensiones, seguro de vida y accidentes, préstamos personales y participar en programa de acciones de la compañía. Ayudas económicas para la escolarización y otras medidas para las familias. CÓMO PARTICIPAR EN EL PROCESO Envía tu CV en inglés a través de la oferta para poder conocernos. Si tu perfil encaja, te llamaremos desde el equipo español de Talent Acquisition en Alemania (prefijo +49) y te acompañaremos en el proceso. ¡Con cada paso estarás más cerca de conocer a tu futuro equipo! Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, puedes contactar con: Vanessa (vanessa.morales-soto@basf.com*) *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. ¡La diversidad es nuestra mayor fortaleza! jobs.name: Operatør med erfaring indenfor fødevare- eller pharmaproduktion Joboplysninger
jobs.description: Operatør med erfaring indenfor fødevare- eller pharmaproduktion Joboplysninger | BASF SE Gå til hovedindhold Find jobs Sprog Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Medarbejder Login Jobbeskrivelse Operatør med erfaring indenfor fødevare- eller pharmaproduktion BELIGGENHED VIRKSOMHED JOBOMRÅDE JOBTYPE JOB-ID FLEKSIBLE ARBEJDSVALG BELIGGENHED VIRKSOMHED JOBOMRÅDE JOBTYPE JOB-ID FLEKSIBLE ARBEJDSVALG Ansøg nu VELKOMMEN TIL BASF Hos BASF A/S i Ballerup har vi gennem mange år udviklet en helt unik og højteknologisk proces inden for mikroindkapslede formuleringer af vitaminer og carotenoider. Vores produkter anvendes globalt i fødevare-, kosttilskuds- og medicinalindustrien, og vi beskæftiger ca. 130 engagerede medarbejdere. For at styrke vores produktionsteam søger vi nu en operatør, der med både faglighed og personlighed kan være med til at holde vores høje kvalitet og sikre en stabil produktion. Vores værdisæt, Winning Behaviors, handler om ejerskab, fremdrift og et mindset præget af forbedringer – og vi leder efter en kollega, der deler disse værdier. HVAD DU KAN FORVENTE Som operatør bliver du en central del af vores team, der har ansvaret for at sikre optimal drift af vores vitaminproduktion. Efter oplæring forventer vi, at du på sigt kan varetage alle arbejdsområder i produktionsprocessen – herunder også lager, blanding og pakning. Dine primære opgaver vil være: •    Drift, overvågning og betjening af processen •    Klargøring af udstyr og procesområder •    Prøveudtagning og proceskontrol •    Skriftlig dokumentation og rapportering •    Rengøring af anlæg, udstyr og lokaler •    Tværgående samarbejde med kolleger om drift og optimering Arbejdstiden er på skiftehold, typisk med vagter på 8, 12 eller 12,5 timer placeret over hele ugen. Der vil være både dag- og nattevagter HVAD DU TILBYDER Vi forestiller os, at du har en baggrund som: •    Operatør eller lignende job med erfaring indenfor fødevare – eller pharmaproduktion •    Uddannet procesoperatør, industrioperatør, bager, slagter, kok eller lignende Derudover lægger vi vægt på: •    Fleksibilitet, teknisk flair og god hygiejneforståelse •    Gode samarbejdsevner og en ansvarsbevidst tilgang •    Ordenssans samt lysten til at følge procedurer •    Gode danskkundskaber i skrift og tale •    Kørekort B •    Du trives i et aktivt job og er i god fysisk form, da en del af opgaverne kræver fysisk arbejde, f.eks løft, bevægelse, opgaver både i højder og mindre rum (confined space) •    Kendskab til GMP er en fordel og at du har et truckkort er et stort pluss HVAD VI TILBYDER Hos BASF får du: •    Ansvar fra første dag og mulighed for udvikling i jobbet •    Et engageret team, hvor vidensdeling og samarbejde er centralt •    Konkurrencedygtig løn •    Sundhedsordning, sundhedstjek, fysioterapi og adgang til træningsfaciliteter •    Bruttolønsordning og DSB erhvervskort •    Kantineordning med varieret og lækker buffet •    En røgfri arbejdsplads SÅDAN SØGER OG KONTAKTER DU OS Vi har kun brug for dit CV og gerne et par linjer om dig selv og din motivation. Af hensyn til en fair og objektiv proces ønsker vi ikke billede i CV’et. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte vores rekrutteringsteam: https://www.basf.com/global/en/careers/contact ANSØGNINGSFRIST Vi gennemgår ansøgninger løbende og lukker opslaget, når vi har fundet den rigtige kandidat. OM OS Hos BASF skaber vi kemi for en bæredygtig fremtid. Vi kombinerer økonomisk succes med miljøbeskyttelse og social ansvarlighed. De ca. 110.000 ansatte i BASF-koncernen arbejder for at bidrage til succes for vores kunder, som man finder i de fleste brancher og i næsten ethvert land over hele verden. Mangfoldighed er vores største styrke! Vi tror på, at succes skabes gennem diversitet. Derfor opfordrer vi alle uanset køn, alder, oprindelse, seksuel orientering, religion eller funktionsnedsættelse til at søge stillingen. Ansøg nu Et unikt samlet tilbud: dig@BASF Hos BASF får du mere end bare kompensation. Vores samlede tilbud omfatter en bred vifte af elementer, som du skal bruge til at yde dit bedste i alle faser af dit liv. Det er det, vi kalder you@BASF. Klik her for at læse mere.
