jobs.name: Global Bulk Parcel Operations, Manager
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position This position will assist bulk parcel operation team lead to perform, execute and facilitate all chartering operation activities  in handling bulk transportation of BASF group of companies’ products by chemical tankers in Asia Pacific region in a way that is cost efficient and in compliance to all the rules, regulations and standards set by BASF as well as the industry. High standard services aim for on-time delivery, cost efficient, seamless operation, compliance, and safety are key objectives of this position. Responsibilities Execute contract of affreightment (COA) and Spot chartering activities, including cargo demand consolidation, demand planning, vessel nomination, cargo nomination and fixture execution in accordance with the applicable charter party clauses. Develop and optimize shipment strategies through cargo bunding and efficient allocation of COA volumes to maximize cost efficiency. Identify suitable vessels by evaluating technical and commercial fit-for-purpose criteria such as vessel space, laycan, tank coating, last three cargo, ship vetting status and jetty acceptance. Work closely with all relevant stakeholders covering shipowners, brokers, shippers, receivers, terminals, and related BASF entities in ensure seamless bulk parcel shipping charactering operations and where necessary lead the coordination of issue resolution where required. Manage critical issues and claims with brokers/ shipowners and affected internal & external business partners, escalate issues further if required and ensure that effective measures are implemented timely to improve customer satisfaction. Monitor and review performance of shipowners to develop KPIs with the objective of having continuous improvement of the operations. Providing Support and collaboration with other Global Bulk Parcel Operations team members in matters pertaining to shipping operations and other initiatives. Work together with BASF stakeholders on projects related to shipping improvements and system related projects. Ensure adherence and compliance to all Distribution Safety Codes of bulk cargoes. Job Requirements A degree/professional qualification (e.g. ICS/CILT), BS or BA degree in business management, supply chain management or equivalent. Minimum of 5 years working experience in Parcel tanker operations covering fixture, port fixture, demurrage computation and bulk tank terminal operations. Be familiar with chemical tanker shipping market resources and have excellent knowledge and ability in handling the vessel’s stowage and planning to execute shipment, optimize freight and reduce demurrage cost. Have basic knowledge of environment (MARPOL regulation), health & safety aspect on bulk parcel product transportation.  Have basic knowledge of IBC code, cargo quantity and quality control. Possess excellent problem solving, analytical, negotiation and interpersonal skills with can-do attitude. Self-motivated, independent, result-oriented team player and able to work under high pressure in adynamic growing environment. Possesses excellent communication management skills (written and spoken), analytically inclined and able to work with minimum supervision, proactive, result-oriented and a good team player Applicant must be willing to work in Kuala Lumpur. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Nematologist Scientist (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. Come join us on our journey to create solutions for sustainable agriculture at the Innovation Center of BASF Belgium Coordination Center CommV in Ghent, Belgium. We are seeking people who thrive in a culture of creativity, openness, responsibility, and entrepreneurial spirit. In our organization, we encourage and celebrate innovative thinking, where ideas flow freely and are nurtured. We value individuals who take ownership of their work and are accountable for their actions. We foster an entrepreneurial mindset, where taking calculated risks and seizing opportunities are encouraged to drive growth and success. Join our team and be part of an environment that values and rewards creative thinking, collaboration, and a can-do attitude. Within BASF Seed and Traits R&D, Trait Research we focus on the design, creation and testing of candidate leads as gene-based solutions for herbicide tolerance, pest and disease control in key crops including soybean, oilseed rape, wheat and cotton. We are currently seeking a talented, motivated, and inspiring Nematologist to join our Trait Research team at the R&D Innovation Center in Ghent, Belgium. In this role, you will develop and execute research strategies aligned with business goals to successfully commercialize innovative nematode resistant traits and germplasm. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions: Vision & Mission WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT You propose innovative nematode trait concepts and develop and implement nematode control research strategies using state-of-the-art know-how and technologies including suitable third-party collaborations to ascertain scientific competitiveness of the nematode program. You evaluate scientific progress of nematode control programs with the project leader and with support of team members (technicians and scientists in the lab and GH) and take decisions to ensure the achievement of scientific objectives and critical milestones with optimal use of available resources. You design and oversee the execution of nematode control assays to support program goals. You articulate a clear, innovative and competitive testing scheme to meet these goals and to ensure the efficient delivery of high-quality nematode traits. You proactively report on your scientific progress and new opportunities to all relevant stakeholders as requested. You develop the innovative ability and competence of research teams, and you act as a mentor and give scientific guidance across the organization. You support the program leader to ensure strategy competitiveness by working closely with Intellectual Property colleagues to build a proactive patenting (and, if needed, licensing-) strategy and ensure a competitor IP analysis in the program field. WHAT YOU OFFER You hold a PhD in Nematology with 12-15 years of relevant experience or equivalent. You are internationally acknowledged as a consistent innovator and leading authority in the field of Plant Nematology and have a profound knowledge of GM trait development. You provide innovative solutions for plant parasitic nematode issues and demonstrate knowledge and expertise from working directly with plant parasitic nematodes. You are familiar with crop business strategies and the principles of value creation for growers. You understand the connection between the lab and the field. You have a track record of providing scientific leadership for internal and external program work packages, effectively managing collaborations with partners, and excel in developing innovative research proposals that incorporate novel technology platforms to advance plant parasitic nematode control project objectives. You possess leadership and team coaching skills, motivating and inspiring others, while fostering collaboration in diverse environments through a multi-cultural mindset. You demonstrate proactivity, with well-developed communication skills that enable effective interactions with stakeholders. You build a culture of innovation by creating an environment that supports creativity and by coaching and developing people. You are fluent in English. WHAT WE OFFER A permanent contract within an attractive working environment in a professional and motivated team. Interesting and challenging learning and development opportunities to help you make the most of your talents and your job. An attractive base salary in relation to your diploma and competencies, an annual bonus and extra-legal benefits. HOW TO REACH US If you need more information about the process or the vacancy, you can reach our recruiting team here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Maintenance Planner
jobs.description:
Now hiring! Maintenance Planner Greenville, OH (On-site) Surventis Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinish coatings as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio is supplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enable new applications with innovative surfaces. We are seeking a professional like you to join our team.  In this role, you will be responsible for planning and scheduling of maintenance, repair, and construction work to ensure 24/7 operation of the chemical manufacturing process. This position will also serve as a backup to the maintenance supervisor at various times throughout the year. Your day-to-day: Troubleshooting operations problems, order repair parts, manage the work order backlog, perform long-range and short-term planning of in-house and contractor labor, as well as cost estimating. Ensuring we have all critical spare parts and materials in inventory, and to ensure that expedited repairs are executed as efficiently as possible. Having a keen focus on sustainability that allows you to support and adhere to the principles of the American Chemistry Council's Responsible Care®, EPA, and OSHA initiatives by protecting the environment, health, safety, and security of our employees, contractors, carriers, distributors, visitors, customers, and neighbors.  Knowledge of OSHA PSM regulations, ASME codes, and API codes is important. Demonstrating the competency of continuous improvement, you will analyze cost trends, use the techniques of Total Productive Maintenance, track key performance indicators, and maintain equipment histories.  The result will be equipment reliability that allows the production department to meet its goals. Coordinating with the procurement department, in-house and contractor labor, suppliers and technical representatives, and the plant production groups. Using SAP for all the maintenance work order and procurement processes as well as maintaining critical SAP data such as functional locations, material masters, and maintenance plans.  The Microsoft Office suite, including MS Project, is also a key tool for e-mails, databases, and reporting. Planning and scheduling the annual turnarounds of the various production plants. You will also use these skills to coordinate all resources from repair parts to contract labor to rental equipment. Expertise needed includes: High School Diploma/GED required; advanced degree preferred. Experience with chemical plant production equipment highly preferred. At least 3+ years related expertise using SAP PM and MM. In depth knowledge of maintenance systems and equipment, planning, and scheduling. Are able to read piping & instrumentation diagrams, engineering specifications, engineering drawings, wiring diagrams, loop diagrams, and vendor-supplied technical information. SAP knowledge and understanding required.  MSProject, and Avetta software preferred. Understand multiple crafts (mechanical, I/E, scaffolding, rigging, excavation) preferred. Possess a good working knowledge of all crafts and a solid understanding of MPI. Deal effectively with the unknown and unexpected and possess the ability to handle non-routine and emergency activities, such as schedule breakers, plant shutdowns, start-up, commissioning, etc. Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Now hiring! Regional Digitalization Lead Surventis’ Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinish coatings, decorative paints as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio is supplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enable new applications with innovative surfaces. We are looking for a Regional Digitalization Lead to join our Coatings team. You lead regional digitalization efforts across North American manufacturing sites. You translate global digital strategy into execution. You guide site Digital Process Optimization roles and align initiatives across Greenville, Windsor, and Tultitlán. You focus on turning data into decisions. You enable sites to use digital tools, data, and analytics to improve performance, cost, and asset utilization. You work in close partnership with the Regional Automation Lead. Digitalization defines decisions. Automation converts those decisions into execution. This role sits at the intersection of operations, data, and digital technology. You will have a working relationship with the following: Direct collaboration with Regional Automation Lead Oversight of site Digital Process Optimization roles Interface with global digital and automation teams Digitalization Lead: Defines insights and decisions from data Automation Lead: Converts decisions into system execution As a Regional Digitalization Lead you will be responsible for... Driving the regional digitalization roadmap across assigned sites Translating global digital standards into regional execution Aligning digital initiatives across Greenville, Windsor, and Tultitlán Providing steering and direction to site Digital Process Optimization roles Ensuring consistency in solutions, KPIs, and tools across sites Coaching and guiding site Digital Process Optimization leads Standardizing KPI frameworks and dashboards across sites Promoting adoption of digital tools for shop-floor and business decisions Supporting development of scalable and reusable digital solutions Enabling data-driven decision-making across operations Defining how data is structured, visualized, and used for decisions Supporting deployment of decision-support tools across operations Driving standardization of reporting and performance tracking Promoting use of statistical analysis and data modeling Ensuring measurable impact on cost, efficiency, and asset utilization Partnering with operations, engineering, IT, and automation teams Aligning closely with Regional Automation Lead on execution strategy Acting as regional interface between sites and global digital teams Supporting digital transformation initiatives across functions Leading regional digital projects from concept to stabilization Ensuring projects deliver measurable business value Driving adoption and sustainment of digital solutions Promoting continuous improvement using digital-first methods Ensuring Digital roadmap is defined, aligned, and executed across all sites Ensuring Standard KPIs and dashboards are used consistently for decisions Ensuring Sites independently use digital tools to drive performance Ensuring Digital initiatives deliver measurable cost and efficiency improvements Ensuring Strong alignment between Digitalization (decisions) and Automation (execution) If you have... Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in engineering, IT, data science, or related field Experience in manufacturing, production, or process industries Several years of experience in digitalization, analytics, or process optimization Experience working across multiple sites or regions preferred Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate) Data analytics and visualization Statistical analysis and modeling Experience with MES and ERP systems (e.g., SAP) Understanding of manufacturing processes and operations Strong analytical thinking and data-driven decision-making Critical thinking and structured problem solving Project management and execution discipline Strong communication and influencing skills Ability to work across functions and cultures Ownership and results-oriented mindset Regular travel across North American sites as required Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. jobs.name: Officer / Senior Officer, Order Management (Mandarin Speaker)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position This position aims to provide end-to-end delivery services by efficiently handling customer service and logistics management to maintain business continuity, enhance service level and safety continuity in business. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage end-to-end daily order process and operation By collaborating with multiple functions (internal and external) including but not limited to customers, sales, shared services, suppliers, finance, controlling. The operation includes but not limited to: Sales order creation PO transaction. Payment following up and order release. Delivery schedule coordination. Delivery order creation. Shipment creation. Transportation arrangement. Billing and invoice management. Documents collection. Month end reconciliation. Import/export related coordination and operation. Monitor shipping and import/export status for overseas supply & delivery. Escalate any issues for on-time fulfillment. Capture market intelligence on supply chain related topics From daily interaction with customers and share with the business as appropriate, includes but not limited to: Provide best possible service to customers and promote the establishment of long-term customer relationships to ensure a sound profitable future customers base. Ensure best logistic cost execution through daily transportation management operation. Continuously improve end-to-end efficiency with creative ideas and digital tools’ support. Complete any unstated end-to-end officer-related tasks assigned by the manager. Manage customers’ complaints NCM operation and communication according to defined procedures / system. Escalate critical NCMs when needed for appropriate resolution. Operational Compliance Ensure that all operations (also at customer premises) are carried out in accordance with internal standards and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree or higher in International Trade, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field. Work Experience: Minimum 2–3 years of experience in order-to-cash processes; experience in the chemical industry is an advantage. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Language Skills: Proficient in Mandarin (both written and spoken) for communication with customers in China, as well as fluency in English. Technical Skills: Strong SAP competency is required. Personal Attributes: Thrives in a dynamic environment, maintains a positive and solutions‑oriented mindset, adapts well to change, collaborates effectively across teams, and consistently demonstrates strong professionalism and work ethic.

jobs.name: Global Supply Chain Excellence - APAC & NA (Specialist)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Design and optimize SC services (based on GB demand, legal requirements, M&A, investments, strategic projects etc.) Conduct impact and cost analysis of new services in SC Service environment Identify needs for optimization/process changes/automation and drive implementation Provide input for tender strategies and ensure continuous improvement Support the customs related topics and provide expert support for Customs Management Main Tasks Identify required models, methods and tools for Service Design Structure, group and prioritize business requirements Ensure usage of new information and communication technology to design or re-design supply chain services Develop new and extend existing services based on requirements from the business and align with service catalog Test new services prior to implementation and calculate cost Deliver basis for technical concepts in cooperation with DevOp Team and Business Experts Drive automation and digitalization for GB Drive continuous improvement and change management in the operation Create customer-specific and cross-regional distribution concepts in cooperation with respective SDU, Purchase to Pay, Procurement, Custom and Finance team. Drive as Expert to integrate new businesses into GB service structure In-depth knowledge of business and SAP processes and their implementation in foreign trade and other related areas like Supply Chain, Transport Management, and Import Export Management. Proficient in the creation of concepts and process design, with expertise in SAP GTS in the module of Customs Management Requirements Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain management, Logistics, Information System with SAP Certification, or equivalent Minimum 5 years in SCM / Logistics area Supply Chain Management / process knowledge, strong in SAP R/3 and SAP S4/HANA(OTC & TM module) experience, Business Intelligence Tools like SCI , Import Export Operations, foreign documentary trade,customs management, analytical thinking, leading with key performance indicators, and good in communication skills. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Officer Accounts Payable with French (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Accounts Payable and Freight Cost Management Department (GBE/VA-FA) provides professional invoice management services for all affiliated BASF Group companies in Europe. It works closely with the procurement and local finance departments and plays an active role in creating an optimized creditor process chain. You are responsible for accounting activities, including checking and posting invoices, reconciling accounts, and verifying payment runs for BASF Group companies. You will be responsible for analyzing automatically booked invoices and driving the advancement of invoice processing automation. In the optimization of the purchase-to-pay process, you will take an active part and work in close cooperation with the purchasing and finance departments of the BASF Group. WHAT YOU OFFER commercial business training or a degree in Business Administration first experience in accounting and projects communicative team player who is open to changes fluent in French and English WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 - 39.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience - In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Team and company events in which we celebrate our achievements and live up our team spirit Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - Here a first impression Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks HOW TO REACH US Viktoria Pohl (Talent Acquisition), viktoria.pohl@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-58390 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team on.basf.com/contact First information about our application process can be found here on.basf.com/application ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities at basf.com/servicehub-berlin . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: DevOps Engineer
jobs.description: Digital Hub Kuala Lumpur attracts, inspire and develop diverse talents who will elevate BASF’s digital journey. As one of the world’s largest chemical companies, BASF is on a mission to create chemistry with a sustainable future. The Digital Hub Kuala Lumpur, alongside four other Digital Hubs across the globe are part of the technology powerhouse driving our vision. Join our organization and make an impact in an inclusive and diverse work environment. We value individual differences and provide opportunities for you to contribute your unique perspective towards our collective goals. Digital Hub Kuala Lumpur attracts, grows, and develops passionate people who will meaningfully impact the digital future of BASF. Come join us and be a part of our digitalization journey. As part of the Data Transparency team, you will contribute to the development and operation of the technical foundations that enable finding, discovering and consuming data in BASF. Your focus will be on building and evolving workflows of the Data Catalog and on developing the APIs that integrate with other data platforms. Responsibilities Design, implement and evolve workflows in the Data Catalog solution with strong focus on maintainability, testability and faster change cycles Modernize workflow logic by moving it into API-based implementations where it accelerates delivery, debugging and reuse across the data catalog ecosystem Develop, maintain and support backend APIs in Python to enable scalable integrations between different systems Build and improve CI/CD pipelines and release procedures for the APIs and data-related automation components, with emphasis on safe deployments, environment promotion and rollbacks Job Requirements Bachelor's or higher degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or similar Hands-on experience designing and implementing workflows using BPMN 2.0 and Groovy scripting Strong Python development experience, specifically with Django and Django REST Framework, building production-grade RESTful APIs Solid experience deploying and operating applications on Microsoft Azure (App Service / AKS / Container Apps, Azure Storage / ADLS Gen2, Azure AD & OAuth2, Key Vault, Application Insights) Strong problem-solving mindset and ability to work in a collaborative, cross-functional environment Fluent English, both written and spoken At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €68.9 billion in 2023. BASF is committed to empowering its employees to pursue their interests and explore emerging technologies. By investing in top-quality tools and promoting cross-functional collaboration, we encourage our team members to drive positive change within our organization. Come join us and be a part of our digitalization journey . Further Information at https://go.basf.net/careers_digitalhubkl

jobs.name: Senior Specialist Digitalization (m/f/d)
jobs.description: ABOUT US At BASF Digital Hub Madrid we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. RESPONSIBILITIES You will take on global responsibility for the ongoing development and design of processes and associated digital solutions within the area Market to Order (e.g. Customer Relationship Management Platform and Digital Commerce Platform) as a key member of a global team You will work with the regional business units, focus on increasing the user adoption of our CRM solution Salesforce and drive the implementation of the Platform for Customer Service within the Petrochemicals division CP In addition, you will manage corresponding projects and assume budget responsibility You will represent the interests of the division BASF wide expert communities and BASF projects. QUALIFICATIONS University degree in economics or computer science Experience in driving digital solutions and managing data (e.g. Power BI, programming languages) Experienced in global project and change management, demonstrating your capability to drive initiatives across diverse teams Excellent communication skills in English, with the confidence to engage stakeholders at all levels Aptitude for quickly understanding new topics, combined with a structured and conceptual approach to problem-solving Previous experience in commercial roles or experience with Salesforce is a plus, as is a strong collaborative spirit to thrive in a team environment. BENEFITS A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided SALARY RANGE Competitive salary between €45,000 and €55,000 gross per year, depending on experience and fit for the role. At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines. We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance. We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities. Contact Do you have any questions about the application process or the position? Please reach out to Felipe Bianco ( felipe.bianco@partners.basf.com )

By balancing economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility, we are building a more sustainable future through chemistry. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. We provide a challenging and rewarding work environment with a strong emphasis on process safety, as well as the safety of our employees and the communities in which we operate. The Maintenance Scheduler is responsible for planning, scheduling, and coordinating maintenance activities at the Greenville, Ohio site. This role ensures maintenance work is completed safely, correctly, and timely to support 24/7 production assets. Maintenance is critical to the site meeting business and financial obligations. The maintenance scheduler serves as a key integrator between maintenance, operations, engineering, and contractors to align maintenance execution with operational goals. Your day-to-day: Coordinating all contractor maintenance activities, including scheduling, access, and execution. Developing weekly and long-term maintenance schedules aligned with production and operational needs. Ensuring work orders are fully prepared with required materials, labor, tools, and permits. Monitoring schedule adherence and adjusting plans for emergent or high-priority work. Maintaining accurate and up-to-date data in SAP. Communicating schedules, priorities, and plant impacts effectively to stakeholders. Identifying and executing improvement opportunities in maintenance workflow and execution to improve safety, cost, and efficiency. Reducing waste and driving continuous improvement in the maintenance department. If you... Are a High school graduate Have strong organizational, planning, and multitasking skills Have 3+ years in maintenance coordination or scheduling Have an understanding of chemical process equipment and maintenance practices Have effective communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills Have strong SAP maintenance module knowledge Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. jobs.name: Deputy Manager, Technical Sales- PU Footwear
jobs.description: About BASF in Vietnam BASF has been active in Vietnam since the establishment of its representative office in 1994 and celebrates its 30-year presence in the country this year. BASF Vietnam Limited was set up in 2009. The company maintains two sales offices in Hồ Chí Minh City and Hà Nội, offering solutions for Vietnam’s key industries, including Agriculture, Construction, Automotive, Footwear, Paints & Coatings and Electrical & Electronics. BASF’s sales to customers in Vietnam were approximately €238 million in 2023. BASF employed 110 people in Vietnam as of the end of the year. Further information on BASF in Vietnam is available at www.basf.com/vn . Objectives of the Position This role serves as the sales or local key account manager responsible for implementing the Value Chain hunting strategy, engaging with the VN team to identify potential polyurethane (PU) Footwear projects and secure new approvals. It involves establishing relationships with key shoe factories including Taiwanese, Korean and Chinese transplants (TTT/KTT/CTT) and acting as the local contact in VN to drive the PU business growth alongside the global and regional PU key account team. Manage existing sales and develop new business opportunities in the Footwear segments of the Polyurethane (PU) business in Vietnam. Develop & execute sales activities to achieve mid/long-term targets in alignment with SBU strategy. Provides preliminary technical support, troubleshooting, and identify new application for existing product lines for both existing and new customers. MAIN AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITIES AND KEY ACTIVITIES Business/Sales Development: Contribute to profitable growth of Footwear PU business in Vietnam via direct account management Develop sales strategy in alignment with business direction and actively hunt new customers Plan, organize and manage sales activities related to Footwear segments Promote new products and applications to existing or potential customers, drive new business acquisition Organize events (Seminar/Tech Day/Workshop) for strategic customers to gain business opportunities. Define & implement local pricing strategy to reach optimal profitability. Establish relationships with key stakeholders of strategic customers. Ensure customers’ credit management is aligned to company's guidelines and risk management. Organise visits from RBU colleagues to drive business growth. Provide ad hoc and regular management reporting timely. Drive commercialization of projects and document through Sales Force. Support customers’ sustainability initiatives with our portfolio of solutions. Market and Competitor Intelligence: Have in-depth understanding of footwear market and customer needs, industry drivers, unique selling points to recommend the best solution(s), leading to the achievement of value creation for the customers and BASF. Identify and develop new market potential for new applications across product portfolio in coordination with RBU. Conduct studies on market dynamics, industry trends, competitors, products, applications, and technology. Establish database for target market segment, customer, and applications. Technical service: Provide preliminary troubleshooting to support customer, while escalating to Technical Development (TD) team any persisting or more complicated technical issues. Follow-up business development of new customers, applications and relevant processing technologies. Provide necessary technical training/workshop/exchange with customer along with Technical Development team. Job Requirement Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering/Science/Chemical/Chemistry/Materials/Polymer Min 5 years as Technical Sales or Technical Service preferably in the PU and chemical industries. Chinese speaking is required. In depth knowledge of PU products and applications Understanding of key industries (e.g. footwear)

jobs.name: Officer Accounts Payable - Team Travel Expenses (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Accounts Payable and Freight Cost Management Department provides professional invoice management for all affiliated European BASF Group companies. It cooperates with the procurement and local finance departments, actively involved in the creation of an optimized creditor process chain. You are responsible for accounting activities such as checking and posting invoices, clarifying accounts as well as verifying payment runs for our BASF Group Companies. You will be in charge of analyzing automatically booked invoices as well as of advancing the automation of invoice processing. In the optimization of the purchase-to-pay process, you will take an active part and work in close cooperation with the purchasing and finance departments of the BASF Group. WHAT YOU OFFER commercial business training or a degree in Business Administration first experience in accounting and projects communicative team player who is open to changes first experience with SAP and Chrome River fluent in English and German WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 - 37.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience - In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Team and company events in which we celebrate our achievements and live up our team spirit Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - Here a first impression Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks HOW TO REACH US Viktoria Pohl (Talent Acquisition), viktoria.pohl@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-58390 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team here . First information about our application process can be found here . ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Regional Data & AI Engineer, Operations, Asia Pacific
jobs.description:
About the Role Regional Data & AI Engineer builds and advances a scalable data and AI backbone for smart manufacturing across Asia sites within Coatings business unit. It combines the development of a standardized production data architecture across sites with the ability to translate data into business value through advanced analytics, AI-ML, and agentic AI solutions. The role ensures that production data is consistently leveraged to improve performance, enable better decisions, and drive continuous optimization across the regional manufacturing network. How You Will Do It Production Data Architecture Define and implement the target architecture for the capture, integration, and provision of production and process data across multiple sites, ensuring scalability and alignment with global standards. IT/OT Integration Develop and implement standards and concepts for the integration of production-relevant data sources, including sensors, PLC, SCADA, historian, MES, LIMS, quality, and ERP systems, with a focus on data quality, availability, and cybersecurity. Data Modeling, Analytics and AI Enablement Design and structure production data models to support reporting, root cause analysis, process optimization, as well as advanced analytics and AI applications, establishing the foundation for scalable data products. Technical Leadership and Consulting Provide technical guidance and advisory support to business functions, production sites, and project teams; translate business requirements into robust architectural solutions and support the implementation of data-driven and AI-enabled initiatives. Standards and Governance Establish and maintain regional standards for data models, interfaces, documentation, and governance to ensure consistency, traceability, compliance, and reusability across the organization. Global Collaboration and Continuous Development Collaborate within an international network of sites, experts, and stakeholders; contribute to and drive global roadmaps; continuously advance the production data architecture in line with business and technology developments. AI and Agentic AI Solution Development Drive the design, development, and industrialization of AI-ML based and agentic AI-based solutions tailored to manufacturing operations, including predictive analytics, intelligent process control, and decision-support systems. External Innovation and Partnerships Identify, evaluate, and manage partnerships with external vendors, startups, and technology providers to incorporate advanced, manufacturing-specific AI and digital solutions into the operational ecosystem. What We Look For Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data engineering, Data Science / Artificial Intelligence or a comparable scientific or technical field. Working Experience: Min. 5 years of professional experience in data architecture, data integration, industrial data platforms or advanced analytics, ideally in a global production-related environment. Experience in designing scalable data architectures and a good understanding of data quality, governance, information security and standardization. Technical & Professional Knowledge / Others: Strong understanding of production processes and industrial system landscapes as well as solid knowledge of integrating IT and OT systems and relevant standards such as ISA-95, OPC or MQTT. Strong knowledge of data processing, data modeling and data analysis as well as technologies such as Python, SQL, ETL/ELT concepts and modern data platforms. Knowledge of Databricks platform, Agentic AI, Machine Learning techniques, including decision tree learning, clustering, artificial neural networks, etc., and their pros and cons. Strong communication and facilitation skills, confidence in working with global stakeholders, a strong team orientation, as well as a high degree of initiative and structuring capability. Very good written and spoken Chinese and English skills. jobs.name: Regional Automation Engineer, Operations, Asia Pacific
jobs.description:
About the Role Regional Automation Engineer enables efficient and consistent manufacturing operations by deploying automation standards, delivering hands-on execution, and driving continuous improvement in system performance and reliability. The role ensures stable execution, data availability from production systems, and continuous improvement in performance, reliability, and scalability across sites. How You Will Do It Automation Standards & System Deployment Translate global automation and digitalization standards into regional implementation and ensure consistent adoption across sites. Automation Opportunities & Roadmap Alignment Partner with operations and specialist teams to identify, prioritize, and deliver automation opportunities aligned to regional and global roadmaps. End-to-End Automation Project Execution Lead or support automation projects from concept through commissioning, optimization, and lifecycle management. Cross-Functional Interface Management Act as the regional interface between manufacturing sites, global automation, engineering, and IT/OT teams. Automation Systems Engineering & Support Provide hands-on technical support across MES, DCS, and SIS, including troubleshooting, integration, and commissioning. Data Connectivity for Automation Systems Enable data-driven manufacturing by ensuring reliable system integration and data readiness from OT environment for analytics and AI use cases. Reliability, Performance & Continuous Improvement Drive continuous improvement in reliability, scalability, and operability, while supporting change initiatives and sharing best practices. Robotics & Advanced Automation Scouting Scout and assess robotics and automation solutions for manufacturing operations, evaluating applicability, feasibility, and value potential across sites. Automation Security Implement cybersecurity measures for control systems Conduct risk assessments and develop improvement actions in accordance with global standards What We Look For Education: Degree in electrical engineering, automation, or equivalent practical experience. Working Experience: 5+ years of experience in automation within a production environment. Strong communication skills in English and in Chinese. Technical & Professional Knowledge/Others Production management systems and MES Process automation systems (ABB 800xA, 800xA Batch, Freelance, Control Builder M) Configuration, commissioning, and troubleshooting experience OT Infrastructure Automation security fundamentals IEC 61511, functional safety management Network topology and system integration concepts [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: Technicien de Ligne 1 - basé à Sandouville (h/f) Détails du poste
jobs.description:
Technicien de Ligne 1 - basé à Sandouville (h/f) Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion au profil Connexion employé Description de poste Technicien de Ligne 1 - basé à Sandouville (h/f) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Postuler BIENVENUE Si vous êtes une personne qui inspire les autres, assume l’impact de ses actions et souhaite constamment élever la qualité de ce qu’elle accomplit, alors vous trouverez pleinement votre place parmi nous. Jusqu’au 1er juillet, notre activité faisait partie de BASF. Dans le cadre de cette transition, toutes les offres d’emploi ainsi que le processus de candidature sont encore hébergés sur la plateforme BASF et présentés sous l’identité visuelle de BASF. Merci de ne pas vous laisser dérouter par cette situation : tous les postes proposés sont des opportunités au sein de Surventis, et votre candidature nous sera directement transmise. Chez Surventis, nous allons au-delà des apparences dans tout ce que nous entreprenons. Nous plaçons nos clients au cœur de nos décisions, anticipons leurs besoins et développons des solutions qui créent une véritable valeur ajoutée ainsi que des partenariats durables. Nous nous inspirons mutuellement à voir plus loin, à prendre pleinement la responsabilité de notre travail et à repousser sans cesse les limites du possible. Si vous venez avec toute votre authenticité, osez remettre en question les évidences et souhaitez évoluer au sein d’une équipe qui met réellement ses valeurs en pratique, alors vous êtes au bon endroit. VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES Rattaché au Responsable de Site, vous assurer l'assistance au client au quotidien sur la ligne de produit dans le respect des exigences de qualité, sécurité, environnement. A ce titre, vous : Garantissez l'assistance du client sur ligne de produit : analyse des problèmes, traitement des problèmes simples, alerte en cas de difficultés ; Garantissez la réalisation des essais de nouveaux produits suivant le planning et les réglages définis (paramétrage des machines, réalisations des 1ères mesures, des 1ères corrections...etc) ; Garantissez la collecte et la remontée des informations en provenance de l'atelier, notamment lors des points fabrication ; Garantissez le renseignement, la mise à jour des indicateurs de suivi de l'activité (Indicateurs PAD, non PAD, aspects, plans de surveillance ligne ...etc) ; Réalisez l'inventaire, assurez le fréquentiel de suivi des stocks et gérez les dates de péremption des produits ; Contribuez à la réalisation de plans d'actions et/ou études suites aux demandes clients ; Contribuez au développement d'un climat relationnel favorable avec le client ; Contribuez à l'amélioration permanente au sein de la sociéte ; Vous contribuez à l’assistance technique Cataphorèse, en l'absence du titulaire au poste ; Vous assurez également, en l'absence du coloriste, le suivi colorimétrique des véhicules. VOTRE PROFIL Vous avez minimum un BAC+2 et disposez d'une expérience confirmée dans un domaine industriel, chimique et idéalement dans la peinture ; Vous êtes orienté client, adaptable, proactif et savez faire preuve de rigueur ; Vous maitrisez les outils informatiques (Excel, SAP) ; Horaires variables de journées. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Un univers passionnant et exigeant ; Coatings vous permet de développer votre potentiel au sein d'une équipe engagée et compétente ; De nombreuses perspectives de carrière dans un Groupe international. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Emilie, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Seules les candidatures soumises via l’offre publiée sur notre site carrière seront étudiées. Toute candidature reçue en dehors du processus prévu (par e-mail, par exemple) ne sera pas considérée. Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. Il vous reste des questions ? Vous pouvez nous contacter ici. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Coatings France est spécialisée dans le développement, la production et la commercialisation de différentes solutions de revêtement allant des traitements de surface à la peinture automobile. Nous soutenons l'innovation, le design et de nouvelles possibilités d'applications au sein d'une équipe internationale dont l'objectif est de répondre aux besoins de nos partenaires dans le monde entier. Notre gamme de services comprend également le programme « Innovation Beyond Paint » qui vise le développement de nouveaux marchés et de modèles commerciaux. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Account Manager - Pharma Solutions
jobs.description: Now hiring! Account Manager - Pharma Solutions Research Triangle Park area (Remote) We are looking for an Account Manager - Pharma Solutions to join our Nutrition & Health team in the Research Triangle Park area. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF Nutrition and Health, we develop, produce and market ingredients and solutions for the nutrition and health industries that fulfill the highest safety, regulatory and sustainability standards. We bring our passion and energy to work every day, knowing that through our cutting-edge research and global collaboration, we deliver products that support the health and wellbeing of humans and animals alike across the globe. Our areas of impact include Human Nutrition, Animal Nutrition, Aroma Ingredients, Pharma Solutions and Enzymes. Working in Nutrition and Health offers opportunities for you to develop and grow at every stage of your career. If you enjoy working in a truly inclusive, and highly collaborative environment where you will be valued, recognized, and rewarded for your contributions, we look forward to meeting you! As an Account Manager within BASF Pharma Solutions, you will play a key role in expanding our business and strengthening relationships across the pharmaceutical and biopharma market. You are a proactive, customer-focused professional who enjoys identifying new opportunities, building long-term partnerships, and driving sustainable growth. Guided by BASF's CORE Values and Winning Behaviors, you bring an entrepreneurial mindset, take ownership of your business, and continuously look for ways to create value for customers and BASF. You collaborate effectively across functions, embrace learning, and contribute to a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. This position requires approximately 50% travel. As an Account Manager - Pharma Solutions, you create chemistry by… Driving Business Growth Through New Opportunities Identify and develop new business opportunities within the assigned U.S. territory, with a focus on pharmaceutical and biopharma customers. Build and nurture relationships with prospective customers, helping them understand the value BASF can bring to their business. Develop and execute territory and account plans that support strategic growth objectives. Maintain a healthy sales pipeline and guide opportunities through the commercial process to successful outcomes. Delivering Commercial Excellence Lead customer discussions and negotiations with confidence, professionalism, and a solution-oriented mindset. Work closely with customers to understand their needs and develop mutually beneficial business solutions. Position BASF as a trusted partner by bringing industry insights, technical expertise, and value-added recommendations. Drive profitable growth while maintaining sound commercial judgment and pricing discipline. Building Strong Partnerships and Collaboration Establish trusted relationships with stakeholders across procurement, technical, R&D, manufacturing, and leadership teams. Collaborate effectively with Marketing, Technical Service, Supply Chain, Quality, Regulatory, and other internal partners to support customer success. Encourage open dialogue, share insights, and help identify opportunities to improve processes and outcomes. Foster a collaborative approach that supports both customer needs and business objectives. Driving Results Through Discipline and Continuous Improvement Manage account plans, opportunity pipelines, forecasting, customer visits, and sample requests using Salesforce and related tools. Use market intelligence, customer feedback, and data-driven insights to prioritize opportunities and refine strategies. Continuously develop your knowledge of the market, customers, and competitive landscape. Contribute insights that help strengthen BASF's commercial strategy and support long-term growth. If you… Hold a bachelors degree in chemistry, pharmaceutical chemistry, chemical engineering or related field. Advanced degree preferred. Preferably have 5–7 years of experience in sales, account management, or a related commercial role, ideally within the pharmaceutical, biopharma, or life sciences industry. Bring proven success in new business development or account management within pharmaceuticals, biopharma, life sciences, or specialty chemicals. Have demonstrated strength in competitive selling and negotiation, including winning business against entrenched incumbents. Possess strong executive presence and the ability to influence without authority. Are comfortable operating in ambiguity with the ability to make fast, informed decisions. Are willing to travel within the U.S. territory to win and grow business. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive pay range for this role is $128,000 – $160,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Counsel
jobs.description: Who we are: We are BASF, the largest chemical company in the world, with more than 150 years of experience. In Montevideo, we operate as a HUB providing services to all BASF companies across the Americas and Europe. We offer professional solutions in finance, procurement, reporting, logistics, technology, human resources, projects, communications, legal affairs, environment, safety, health, and quality. Area: The Corporate Legal team provides various types of legal and contractual services for both the Hub and all business units in North America and South America, in the areas of Intellectual Property and Corporate Law. Main Objective of the Position: Support BASF divisions in legal and compliance matters and offer modern solutions tailored to business needs, ensuring a high level of customer orientation with cooperative, efficient, and future-oriented services. Main Tasks: Support BASF businesses across the Americas, Europe, and Asia in legal and compliance matters, delivering efficient, customer ‑ oriented, and future ‑ ready services aligned with internal standards. Support and conduct highly complex contractual analysis and Compliance and Legal matters and operations, and offer modern solutions tailored to business needs, ensuring a high level of customer orientation with cooperative, efficient, and future-oriented services Supporting legal and compliance tools related activities (e.g. in Trust Base, One Trust, ICM). Provisioning services related to local or international standard compliance and legal inquiries based on playbooks. Prepare reports on contract status, key metrics, and performance indicators for management review. Maintain a centralized repository of contracts and related documents for easy access. Create and maintain training materials for legal & compliance tools (e.g. CLM-platform, Trust Base), conduct user training sessions, provide user support and develop support functionalities. Provide guidance and support to the business in the drafting and negotiation of highly complex contracts and support the business in selecting the appropriate high ‑ complexity contractual vehicles for each specific situation. Implementation and maintenance of the existing and newly created model contracts and clauses for BASF standard contracts Maintain continuous and proactive communication with multiple clients and stakeholders, working closely with different areas of the organization. Understand end ‑ to ‑ end (E2E) processes and their impact on workflow and evaluate and propose process improvements. Lead projects involving the management of large volumes of legal contracts within tight deadlines. Minimum Education and Qualification Requirements for the Position: Education: University degree in Law. Working Experience: Minimum of 3 years of experience. Knowledge of common law systems and Brazilian law is a plus. Admission to practice law in the U.S. is a strong plus. Language Skills: Professional proficiency in English and Spanish; Portuguese is a plus. Technical Skills: Experience in the legal, compliance, or commercial contract management fields. Working knowledge of legal databases and standard IT applications. Soft Skills: Strong communication skills to maintain effective interaction with clients, stakeholders and cross-functional teams within the Hub and BASF. What we offer: Living BASF means being part of a culture where the focus is on people and on their professional and personal development. Our diversity, collaboration, and leadership style make the difference. By joining our team, we offer you: • A hybrid and flexible work model • Access to a learning ecosystem with on‑demand resources and tools • Private medical insurance for you and your family from day one, as well as life insurance • Additional flexible leave days, in addition to your statutory vacation days • A day off on your birthday • A day off for moving • Annual bonus (subject to meeting the requirements established by internal policies) • Well‑being benefits, such as on‑site nutrition services and massages BASF considers Law No. 19.691 and Decree No. 73/019 in all its hiring processes. Any qualified candidate who meets the requirements outlined in the job description will be considered in this hiring process without distinction of any kind. In addition, BASF is an equal opportunity employer. We enthusiastically embrace our responsibility to make employment decisions without regard to race, age, marital or social status, national origin, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic or group not related to the candidate’s suitability and qualifications for the position. Our management is committed to upholding this policy in hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, demotion, dismissal, termination, recruitment, compensation and other forms of remuneration, training, and general treatment throughout employment.

jobs.name: Senior Software QA Engineer
jobs.description:
About The Role Within Global Digital Factory ECO (G ‑ ECO/MD), you will be responsible for ensuring the quality of a modern web application as part of an agile DevOps team. The team builds and operates solutions jointly used by BASF Coatings and external customers, and you will contribute to delivering reliable, performant, and user ‑ focused digital products through systematic testing and continuous quality improvements. How You Will Do It -    You contribute to delivering high ‑ quality Intranet and Internet applications that improve BASF Coatings services for internal and external customers. -    You work in an agile and self ‑ organized DevOps team, supporting the full product lifecycle — from ideation to go ‑ live and ongoing operations. -    You define test strategies and design automated testing solutions to ensure the product meets its functional and non ‑ functional quality goals. -    You develop a deep understanding of business logic, workflows, and user scenarios to ensure comprehensive test coverage. -    You collaborate closely with software developers to validate new features, identify defects early, and support automated testing against their delivered functionalities. -    You design, build, and maintain automated test suites (functional, regression, performance) and ensure their integration into CI/CD pipelines. -    You use appropriate tools (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Postman, JMeter) to test modern web applications, cloud solutions, and REST APIs. -    You work closely with the product owner, product manager, designers, and developers to ensure shared understanding of acceptance criteria and quality expectations. -    You contribute to monitoring, performance testing, and quality ‑ related DevOps activities to ensure reliable operations in production. -    You document test strategies, test cases, results, and quality insights to support transparency and maintainability of the testing framework. What We Look For -    Degree or comparable education in computer or information science. -    Minimum 3+ years of professional experience in software quality assurance or software testing in an IT environment. -    Experience creating and maintaining test plans, test cases, and structured testing documentation. -    Strong experience in identifying and reporting defects, and testing web applications, cloud solutions, and REST APIs. -    Hands ‑ on experience with at least one automated testing framework (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Cucumber/Gherkin). -    Proficiency with testing tools such as Postman, SOAP UI, Gatling, or JMeter. -    Practical experience with at least one programming language for automation (JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python). -    Basic knowledge of SQL databases and understanding of microservice architecture. -    Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, Azure DevOps testing integrations, and automated deployment workflows. -    Strong self ‑ organization, analytical skills, problem ‑ solving ability, and collaborative mindset. -    Fluent English for effective communication across locations and time zones. -    Ideally, experience testing distributed systems and microservice ‑ based solutions. -    Ideally, experience with performance testing and load ‑ testing strategies. -    Ideally, familiarity with accessibility testing (WCAG) and UI/UX collaboration. -    Ideally, exposure to containerized environments (Docker) and cloud platforms (Azure).
