jobs.name: Integrated Facilities Manager, South East Asia
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position The Regional Integrated Facilities Manager (IFM) oversees end‑to‑end Integrated Facilities Management operations in South East Asia. This role ensures consistent service delivery, compliance, operational excellence, and continuous improvement across both hard and soft facility management (FM) services. The manager acts as the regional point of accountability for performance, governance, stakeholder management, and commercial outcomes. Responsibilities 1. Regional Operations Management Lead and manage IFM service delivery across multiple countries/sites. Ensure consistent execution of Hard FM, Soft FM, EHS, security, workspace, and vendor management. Implement standardized IFM processes, SOPs, and service delivery frameworks. Oversee asset management, preventive maintenance, reliability engineering, and life-cycle planning. Monitor operational risks and ensure business continuity readiness. 2. Governance & Compliance Ensure all sites meet statutory, regulatory, and internal compliance requirements. Conduct regular audits, site inspections, and compliance reviews. Lead HSE culture and ensure adherence to global standards. Report performance, risks, and escalations through regional governance channels. 3. Performance & Service Excellence Drive KPI/SLA achievement across the regional portfolio. Introduce continuous improvement, lean FM, and efficiency programs. Implement CAFM/CMMS governance, data accuracy, and performance analytics. Use benchmarking and best practices to elevate service quality. 4. Financial & Commercial Management Manage regional FM budgets, forecasts, and cost controls. Manage contract management, procurement strategy, and vendor governance. Track savings, cost avoidance, and financial performance. Support tendering, sourcing, and negotiation for regional FM contracts. 5. Stakeholder & Client Management Serve as the primary FM point of contact for regional leadership. Build strong relationships with country managers, landlords, vendors, and functional teams. Manage escalations and drive positive workplace experience outcomes. Support strategic projects, expansions, and transitions. 6. People Leadership Lead and mentor country-level FM teams and site leads. Drive capability building, succession planning, and talent development. Promote engagement, collaboration, and high-performance culture. Requirements Education Bachelor’s degree in Facilities Management, Building Services, or related field. Experience 5–7 years in Facilities Management, with at least 3–5 years in a regional or multi-country leadership role. Strong background in both Hard FM and Soft FM. Knowledge in EHS. Experience with large portfolios in corporate offices environment. Proven ability in financial management, commercial negotiation, and vendor governance. Skills & Competencies Strong leadership and communication skills. Excellent stakeholder management across senior executive levels. Data-driven, analytical, and proficient in CAFM/CMMS systems. Ability to work in complex, culturally diverse environments. Strong project management and transition management capability.

jobs.name: Assistant Manager - Sales and Sub-Regional Accounts
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives Accountable for achieving key financial performance indicators (KPIs), including Net Sales and cCM1, for distributors across the sub-region and designated accounts, with primary focus on Thailand, Singapore, and Malaysia. Build, expand, and develop direct key accounts and distributor relationships to optimize and strengthen the distribution network, ensuring effective and efficient distributor management. Maintaining up-to-date customer data and account plans and providing accurate sales forecasts to support effective planning. Develop project pipeline with customers partnering with marketing and technical team to achieve future business growth. Main responsibilities Sustainably achieve sales targets encompassing ccM1, volume & sales, costs, working capital and other business targets such as delivery reliability and customer satisfaction. Be accountable and responsible for pricing execution and financials for the assigned accounts. Coordinate the collection of market insights by developing a deep understanding of customer needs, analyzing market trends and competitors, supporting pricing strategies and product launches, and monitoring the execution of marketing and promotional campaigns. Provide accurate sales forecast, supporting efficient planning of products and services. Respond to customer inquiries, provide timely support on quality and technical matters, perform customer assessments and analysis, and collaborate with regional functions to resolve customer issues. Analyze market trends, competitors, product applications, and service needs; execute sales and marketing plans for target customers in alignment with the market development team and collaborate closely with regional marketing on promotional activities and customer projects. Handle and collaborate with other parties to respond to customer enquiries in all dimensions such as document, regulatory, agreement and quality issues. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Science and/or Business Administration or related discipline. Minimum 5 years’ relevant working experience, preferably in the cosmetic ingredient industry. Excellent command of the English language, both in verbal and written communication. Works independently with strong ownership and minimal supervision. A strong comprehension of the personal care industry. Possesses a valid driver's license and owns a personal vehicle. Ability to communicate to international teams and influence at all levels with strong presentation skills. Work location Emporium office, Bangkok BASF has been present in Thailand for more than 50 years. The company offers a wide range of products and solutions to support almost all the key industries in the country, including Agricultural Solutions, Intermediates and Process Catalysts, Monomers, Petrochemicals, Coatings, Dispersions and Resins, Performance Chemicals, Care Chemicals, Nutrition and Health, and Performance Materials. BASF in Thailand was established in 1966 and maintains its head office in Bangkok. It operates three production sites in Samutprakarn, Chonburi and Rayong, an Automotive Coatings Technical Competence Center, and a Performance Materials Technical Development Center in Samutprakarn. In 2024, BASF posted sales of €531 million to customers in Thailand and had 623 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/th .

jobs.name: Product Safety Manager (Project Management)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position This role serves as a Regional PMO Lead and key execution partner to the Head of Product Safety APAC, driving disciplined execution of strategic priorities across the region. The incumbent will manage multiple cross-functional initiatives across Asia Pacific, ensuring strong governance, delivery discipline, and alignment across stakeholders. Acting as an extension of the Head of Unit, the role enforces milestone adherence, escalates risks with clear actions, and drives timely decision-making in a complex, matrix environment. Key Responsibilities Programme & Portfolio Management Lead and manage multiple strategic programmes across APAC Establish and drive PMO governance frameworks (prioritisation, reporting cadence, decision gates) Consolidate and manage portfolio view (milestones, dependencies, risks, resources) Ensure on-time, on-scope delivery of global and/or region initiatives Resolve cross-project dependencies and execution gaps Strategic Execution Support Act as a trusted execution partner to the Head of Product Safety Asia Pacific Translate strategic priorities into clear execution roadmaps and milestones Prepare executive-level updates, briefings, and decision materials Drive follow-up and ensure closure of key action items across teams Stakeholder Management & Governance Drive accountability across regional and global stakeholders Facilitate decision-making across functions and seniority levels Identify, escalate, and resolve risks, delays, and resource constraints Project Governance, Reporting & Analytics Establish standardised dashboards (KPIs, milestones, risks) Lead regular programme reviews with leadership Ensure transparent, data-driven reporting and insights Track performance and identify continuous improvement opportunities Change Management & Implementation Drive end-to-end change execution across APAC initiatives Ensure effective communication, training, and adoption of changes Support organisational transformations and operating model enhancements Requirements Education University degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related science field Working Experience At least 10 years in Programme management / PMO roles in a corporate environment Track record managing complex, cross-functional or global programmes Experience supporting senior leadership / executive stakeholders Strong exposure to governance frameworks and portfolio management Technical & Professional Knowledge Knowledge of chemical regulations and product stewardship Strong analytical and data-driven decision-making capability Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills in matrix organisations Experience with regulatory systems and data analytics tools (e.g. Excel, Power BI) Strong communication skills, including executive-level presentations The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024.

jobs.name: Werkstudent:in Dokumentation Assetmanagement (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Werkstudent:in Dokumentation Assetmanagement (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Werkstudent:in Dokumentation Assetmanagement (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Das European Site & Verbund Management entwickelt die großen, europäischen Standorte der BASF stetig weiter und sichert damit deren Wettbewerbsfähigkeit: Wir managen an den Standorten die perfekte Verzahnung von Infrastruktur, Betriebsservices, Personalservices und Logistik mit der Produktion. Damit sichern wir die Grundversorgung der Unternehmensbereiche, die dadurch erfolgreich am Markt agieren können. Zusätzlich beraten wir die Betriebe in allen Belangen rund um Umweltschutz, Arbeits- sowie Anlagensicherheit, Gesundheitsschutz und Genehmigungsrecht. WAS SIE ERWARTET In unserem Betrieb Hafen- und Tanklager Ludwigshafen arbeiten wir an einer nachhaltigen und an den Verfügbarkeitsanforderungen des gesamten Standorts Ludwigshafen ausgerichteten Prozess- und Produktionstechnik. Sie unterstützen das Team am Einsatzort in Mannheim auf der Friesenheimer Insel ab September 2026 für einen Zeitraum von mindestens 6 Monaten. Als Werkstudent:in in diesem Bereich können Sie Ihr erlerntes Wissen anwenden und einen Einblick in betriebliche Prozesse, Strukturen und standortweite Zusammenhänge erhalten. Durch Ihre eigenverantwortliche Arbeit tragen Sie zur Aufnahme des IST-Zustands der Produktionsanlage bei. Wir freuen uns hier über neue Ideen zur Verbesserung unserer Prozesse. Sie erhalten die Möglichkeit bei der Abwicklung themenbezogener Projekte zur Digitalisierung mitzuwirken, wie beispielsweise der Optimierung unserer technischen Know-How Datenbank. Sie unterstützen uns im laufenden Tagesgeschäft z.B. durch das selbständige Sichten und Aufarbeiten verschiedener Unterlagen und bekommen so detaillierte Einblicke in den ingenieurstechnischen Alltag. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN laufendes Studium der Ingenieurwissenschaften mit der Spezialisierung Verfahrens-, Prozess-, Chemie-, Umwelt- oder Automatisierungstechnik idealerweise erste Fachkenntnisse durch relevante Praktika fundierte Kenntnisse mit den MS-Office Standardanwendungen selbständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Einsatzbereitschaft sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse Angehörige von Staaten außerhalb des europäischen Wirtschaftsraums und der Schweiz schicken bitte ihre Aufenthalts-/Arbeitsgenehmigung inkl. zugehöriges Zusatzblatt sowie den ausgefüllten Fragebogen mit. WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase - Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy - Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback, eine persönliche und individuelle Betreuung sowie Einarbeitung durch unsere Fachexperten Netzwerke - unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Vergütung - Die Vergütung erfolgt gemäß des Tarifvertrages der chemischen Industrie. Bei einer wöchentlichen Arbeitszeit von 20 Stunden beträgt das monatliche Brutto-Tarifentgelt derzeit 1.784 Euro. Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung - je nach Einsatzeinheit ist in Absprache, eine befristete Vollzeitbeschäftigung in der vorlesungsfreien Zeit (Semesterferien), möglich. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Silke Pudicke (Talent Acquisition), silke.pudicke@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-58449 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier ( https://www.basf.com/global/de/careers/contact) Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier: Bewerbung ÜBER UNS Die BASF Jobmarkt GmbH ist eine Gruppengesellschaft der BASF in Ludwigshafen am Rhein und besteht aus den Geschäftsfeldern Zeitarbeit und Ausbildungsverbund. Sie werden während Ihres Einsatzes in einer Einheit der BASF SE eingesetzt. Die BASF SE ebenfalls mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größtes arbeitgebendes Unternehmen der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studierende, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Mehr über die BASF SE erfahren Sie unter: Ludwigshafen Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Paste  Engineer
jobs.description:
About The Role Support the local Pigment & Paste Owner in managing and executing pigment and paste-related activities in China . The role focuses on operational support, coordination, data transparency, and implementation of agreed actions across production, C-Labs, procurement, and suppliers, contributing to robust quality, cost competitiveness, and sustainable local operations. How You Will Do It Support to Pigment & Paste Ownership - Support the local Pigment & Paste Owner in day-to-day pigment and paste activities in China including root causes investigation. - Prepare analyses, reports, and documentation to support decision-making within the PSM framework Operational & Cross ‑ functional Coordination - Support alignment of Chinese pigment and paste activities with global PSM processes and standards - Coordinate with production, C-Labs, procurement, and suppliers on agreed activities and follow ‑ ups - Follow action items, milestone related to pigment and paste topics Pigment Paste Localization & optimization (Support Role) - Support pigment paste localization projects, including testing coordination, data collection, and documentation - Support implementation and follow-up of agreed optimization measures together with production and lab teams Consolidation & Transparency - Support pigment and paste consolidation activities in China based on defined transparency frameworks - Maintain and update local overviews of pigment toolbox, paste portfolio, and intermediates - Identify consolidation opportunities and escalate improvement proposals to the Pigment & Paste Owner EHS & Compliance - Support adherence to EHS requirements in labs and operational activities within the scope of responsibility - Assist in audit preparation and follow-up actions to ensure compliance with BASF standards Main KPIs - #Pigment pastes in China / Sales   - annual tracking - R&D Budget/Spend (Pigment and Paste Team China) - Q-Cost (Pigment intermediates China) - Safety (hsWPRIR) -    Ensure all activities meet BASF standards of EHS and 5S. What We Look For -    Education: Master’s degree or above -    Working Experience: At least 3 years in coating industry -    Technical & Professional Knowledge: Solid Chemistry knowledge and skills; conversational English [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: Group Lead, US Tax - Ag Solutions
jobs.description: Now hiring! Group Lead, US Tax - Ag Solutions We are looking for a Group Lead US Tax to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Research Triangle Park, NC. Come create chemistry with us! We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems, connecting seeds and traits, crop protection, digital tools and sustainability approaches. We strive to deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth In this role, you will lead the U.S. Tax function for Agricultural Solutions, with full accountability for U.S. direct and indirect tax matters in a complex, multi-national environment. We in Agricultural Solutions are preparing to be an independent, pure-play agricultural company within the BASF Group. To be ready for independence, we are looking for you to join us in our new Corporate roles (see here: Build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). As a Group Lead, US Tax - Ag Solutions, you create chemistry by... Leading the timely and accurate preparation, review, and submission of all U.S. federal, state, and local tax filings, including corporate income tax and indirect taxes. Overseeing tax accounting, tax payments, and reporting processes, ensuring alignment with statutory and management reporting. Owning and managing the U.S. tax compliance calendar, ensuring deadlines are met with strong controls and governance. Leading and developing the U.S. tax organization, driving performance, capability building, and a high standard of execution. Acting as the primary advisor to senior leadership on U.S. tax matters, providing clear guidance on risks, opportunities, and compliance requirements. Establishing and maintaining a robust tax control framework, ensuring audit readiness and leading all tax audits and authority interactions. Partnering closely with Finance, Accounting, Legal, and business leaders, as well as global tax teams and external advisors. Supporting key business activities including transactions, restructurings, and strategic initiatives with clear and practical tax guidance. Driving continuous improvement through standardization, automation, and use of tax technology. If you... Have a Master’s degree in Tax, Accounting, Finance, Law, or a related field, with a CPA or equivalent professional certification required. Bring 10–15 years of progressive experience in U.S. corporate taxation, within a multinational corporation and/or leading advisory firm. Have proven experience in a senior tax leadership role with responsibility for U.S. tax operations in a complex organization. Demonstrate deep expertise in U.S. corporate income tax, state and local tax, indirect taxes, and tax accounting. Lead and develop high-performing teams through demonstrated people leadership experience. Work effectively in a global, matrixed environment while partnering across functions and geographies. Bring exposure to large-scale transformation, restructuring, or IPO-readiness environments, which is strongly preferred. Apply strong business acumen to translate complex tax topics into clear, actionable insights for senior stakeholders. Communicate effectively and manage stakeholders with executive presence and sound judgment. Use ERP systems, such as SAP, and tax technology tools with a focus on process efficiency and data quality. Demonstrate a high level of ownership, integrity, and a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Real Estate Expert - Capital Projects & Transactions EMEA (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Real Estate Expert - Capital Projects & Transactions EMEA (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Real Estate Expert - Capital Projects & Transactions EMEA (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir sind BASF in der Landwirtschaft und alles, was wir tun, tun wir aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft und dem wichtigsten Beruf auf der Erde. Wir sind ein international erfolgreiches Unternehmen der BASF-Gruppe. Unsere Lösungen sind speziell für verschiedene Anbausysteme entwickelt. Sie verbinden Saatgut und speziell gezüchtete Pflanzeneigenschaften, Pflanzenschutzprodukte, digitale Tools und Nachhaltigkeitsansätze, um Landwirten, Züchtern und anderen Interessengruppen entlang der Wertschöpfungskette bestmögliche Ergebnisse zu bieten. Mit Teams im Labor, auf dem Feld, im Büro und in der Produktion tun wir alles, was in unserer Macht steht, um eine nachhaltige Zukunft für die Landwirtschaft zu schaffen. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Hier erfährst du mehr über unsere Vision bei BASF Agricultural Solutions! WAS DICH ERWARTET Wir bei Agricultural Solutions bereiten uns darauf vor, ein selbständiges Unternehmen innerhalb der BASF-Gruppe zu werden. Um für diese eigenständige Zukunft bestens aufgestellt zu sein, suchen wir dich als Unterstützung für unser neues Corporate Center ( siehe hier: build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In deiner neuen Position leitest und realisierst du nationale und internationale Immobilientransaktionen in der gesamten EMEA-Region. Darunter fallen Akquisitionen, Veräußerungen, Mietvertragsverhandlungen, Vermietungen und Immobilienbewertungen, und stellst dabei die vollständige Übereinstimmung mit der Immobilienstrategie des Unternehmens sicher. Gemeinsam im Team leitest und koordinierst du regionenübergreifende Immobilienprojekte mit einem starken Fokus auf die Anforderungen an den Hauptsitz, überwachst die Umsetzung und steuerst externe Berater, Makler, Immobilienverwalter und weitere Dienstleister. Darüber hinaus verhandelst du Handels-, Miet-, Kauf- und Dienstleistungsverträge, führst fundierte Finanzanalysen und Immobilienbewertungen durch und erstellst auf der Grundlage etablierter Methoden hochwertige Entscheidungsunterlagen und Empfehlungen für die Geschäftsleitung. Du sorgst für ein qualitativ hochwertiges Immobilien-Datenmanagement, pflegst eine präzise Berichterstattung und Portfoliodokumentation, überwachst relevante KPIs und trägst zur Einhaltung interner Governance-Standards bei. Nicht zuletzt unterstützt du die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung und Optimierung des Unternehmensimmobilienportfolios, indem du Wertschöpfungsmöglichkeiten identifizierst und zu langfristigen Belegungs-, Investitions- und Arbeitsplatzstrategien beiträgst, einschließlich moderner Bürokonzepte. WAS DU MITRBINGST abgeschlossener Master-Abschluss in Immobilienmanagement oder einem verwandten Fachgebiet; alternativ ein Bachelor-Abschluss in Ingenieurwesen oder Betriebswirtschaftslehre mit zusätzlichen Qualifikationen im Immobilienbereich mindestens 7 Jahre einschlägige Berufserfahrung in den Bereichen Unternehmens- oder Gewerbeimmobilien, Vermögensverwaltung, Transaktionen oder Investitionsprojekte in einem internationalen Umfeld fundierte Fachkenntnisse in den Bereichen Immobilientransaktionen, Portfoliomanagement, Immobilienbewertung, Finanzanalyse, Mietvertragsverwaltung, Vertragsverhandlungen und Projektmanagement fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse; weitere europäische Sprachen sind von Vorteil fundierte Kenntnissen in SAP (z. B. SAP RE-FX, SAP FI/CO) und Microsoft Office-Anwendungen, insbesondere Excel und PowerPoint ausgeprägte analytische und strategische Denkfähigkeiten, gepaart mit hervorragenden Fähigkeiten im Stakeholder-Management, in der Kommunikation und im Verhandeln sowie einem proaktiven, lösungsorientierten und strukturierten Arbeitsstil WAS WIR BIETEN Eine dynamische Arbeitsumgebung, die dich dazu ermutigt, deine Ideen einzubringen, Verantwortung zu übernehmen und aktiv mitzugestalten. Ein starker Fokus auf kontinuierliches Lernen, moderne Technologien und den verantwortungsvollen Einsatz von künstlicher Intelligenz. So arbeiten wir zukunftsorientiert, effizient und nachhaltig – und treiben Innovation aktiv voran. Inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung deiner Ideen (z. B. Women in Business). Vielfältige Lernmöglichkeiten für deine fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung. Karrierepfade für deine berufliche Entwicklung in andere Rollen innerhalb des Unternehmens. Wir unterstützen dich aktiv bei der Kinderbetreuung (z. B. Betrieblicher Kindergarten, Ferienprogramme) und bieten ein Fitness- und Gesundheitsstudio sowie ein medizinisches Zentrum mit zahlreichen Gesundheitsangeboten. Wir bieten nachhaltige Mobilitätsvorteile – ein vergünstigtes Deutschlandticket für den regionalen Nahverkehr, ein Jobrad-Leasingprogramm sowie E‑Ladestationen vor Ort für private Elektrofahrzeuge. So wird dein Arbeitsweg kostengünstig, gesund und klimafreundlich. SO ERREICHST DU UNS Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 beantwortet gerne deine Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne kannst du dir auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichst du auch hier Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess findest du hier ÜBER UNS Die BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH bietet als zuverlässiger Partner der Landwirtschaft innovative, nachhaltige Lösungen speziell für verschiedene Anbausysteme. Unser Unternehmenssitz ist das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof, nahe der BASF-Konzernzentrale in Ludwigshafen. In der Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar erwartet dich beruflich wie auch privat ein attraktives Umfeld. Mehr über das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof erfährst du unter: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LI Mehr über unser Team bei BASF Agricultural Solutions findest du hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalte mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Wir duzen dich gerne – das gehört bei uns zur Kultur. Wenn du lieber gesiezt werden möchtest, sag einfach Bescheid. Jetzt bewerben Unsere Mitarbeitenden stehen im Mittelpunkt und setzen sich jeden Tag dafür ein, unseren Kunden einen echten Mehrwert zu bieten. Wir legen großen Wert darauf, unsere Mitarbeitenden heute und in Zukunft gezielt zu fördern, damit wir die Talente und Fähigkeiten haben, die für unseren Erfolg entscheidend sind. Vereint durch die Leidenschaft für die Landwirtschaft, entwickeln wir gemeinsam innovative Lösungen, arbeiten partnerschaftlich zusammen und streben nach den besten Ergebnissen – als ein Team. So schaffen wir Wirkung. Aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft und dem wichtigsten Beruf auf der Erde. jobs.name: Real Estate Expert - Capital Projects & Transactions EMEA (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in Agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming, and the biggest job on Earth. We are an internationally successful company of the BASF Group. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. With teams in the lab, field, office and in production, we do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Here you can gain more insight into our Vision at BASF Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We in Agricultural Solutions are preparing for a partial IPO. Therefore, we are looking for you to join us in our new Corporate roles ( see here: build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In your new position, you lead and execute national and international real estate transactions across the EMEA region, including acquisitions, disposals, lease negotiations, lettings, and property valuations, while ensuring full alignment with the corporate real estate strategy. Together with your colleagues, you manage and coordinate cross-regional real estate projects with a strong focus on headquarters requirements, overseeing implementation and steering external consultants, brokers, property managers, and additional service providers. Furthermore, you negotiate commercial, lease, purchase, and service agreements, conduct in-depth financial analyses and property valuations, and prepare high-quality decision papers and recommendations for senior management based on established methodologies. Your expertise is in demand! You ensure high-quality real estate data management, maintain accurate reporting and portfolio documentation, monitor relevant KPIs, and contribute to compliance with internal governance standards. Not least, you support the continuous development and optimization of the corporate real estate portfolio by identifying value-creation opportunities and contributing to long-term occupancy, investment, and workplace strategies, including modern office concepts. WHAT YOU OFFER completed Master’s degree in real estate management or a related field; alternatively, a bachelor’s degree in engineering or business administration with additional real estate qualifications at least 7 years of relevant professional experience in corporate or commercial real estate, asset management, transactions, or capital projects in an international environment strong expertise in real estate transactions, portfolio management, property valuation, financial analysis, lease administration, contract negotiations, and project management fluent German and English skills, additional european languages are a plus, combined with solid experience in sap (e.g. sap re-fx, sap fi/co) and Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel and PowerPoint strong analytical and strategic thinking skills, paired with excellent stakeholder management, communication, negotiation skills, and a proactive, solution-oriented as well as structured working style WHAT WE OFFER A unique opportunity to shape a pivotal function within a company that prepares for the IPO. A dynamic work environment that empowers you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and assume responsibility. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence, enabling us to work in a future‑oriented, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks for the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business). Learning opportunities for your professional and personal development. Career paths as professional development into other roles within the company. We actively support with childcare (e.g. company daycare, holiday programmes) and provide a fitness and health studio as well as a medical center with numerous offers for your health. We offer sustainable mobility benefits — a discounted Deutschlandticket for regional public transport, a job‑bike leasing program, and on‑site e‑charging stations for private electric vehicles to make your commute affordable, healthy, and climate‑friendly. HOW TO REACH US Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH is a reliable partner to the agricultural sector, offering innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to a wide range of crop systems. With the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, near the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you’ll find an attractive environment for both your professional life and your leisure time in the Rhine‑Neckar metropolitan region. You can learn more about the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof here . Find out more about our Agricultural Solutions team here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Transport Management (Executive) - Contract
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position This person will be responsible for transport operations for a country. The person will also take ownership of all transport management processes for BASF operations. Main Tasks Manage transport operation (inbound, outbound) for bulk and packaged with different modes of transportation Evaluate, monitor and improve the performance of 3rd party service providers, handle non-conformance service issues; participate and support 3rd party service provider selection; Optimize transportation processes based on market surveys and business feedback to meet customer and company requirements Coordination and management of service provider for vehicle placement Tracking delivery of products to customer (monitoring of in transit vehicles, shipments) Ensure transportation safety Evaluate service provider performance on monthly basis in line with BASF standards Coordination and management of order management Ensuring timely delivery of goods to customer Coordination with EHS, insurance and service provider in a case of accidents Ensuring utilization of dedicated vehicles Insurance management (raising insurance claim and recovery) incl. management of recovery from service providers Requirements Education: diploma or degree in supply chain or equivalent Open to fresh graduate, or minimum 1 year of experience in transport management Experience in SAP Must be able to work in India timezone (11AM-8PM MYT) and on Saturday (Sunday and 1 weekday off day) Only Hindi/Tamil speakers

jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position This person will be responsible for transport operations and distribution network management for a country. Main Task Transport Management: Manage transport operation (inbound, outbound) for bulk and packaged with different modes of transportation, perform SAP transactions eg. Delivery Order processing, TM Order processing, etc. Evaluate, monitor and improve the performance of 3rd party service providers, handle non-conformance service issues; participate and support 3rd party service provider selection; Tracking delivery of products to customer (monitoring of in transit vehicles, shipments) Coordination and management of order management Ensuring timely delivery of goods to customer Coordination with EHS, insurance and service provider in a case of accidents Insurance management (raising insurance claim and recovery) incl. management of recovery from service providers Distribution Network Management: Manage distribution network operations including warehouse, tank terminal etc. Perform SAP transactions eg. GR/GI, stock status change, COA, Label, etc. Interact with business management, BU SCM and sales to gain knowledge of business and provide Warehouse operations solution to business Offer guidance to Logistics Service Provider team members in capturing BU/ Customer expectation / reduction of repeat NCM;s, by analyzing the root causes, propose corrective measures and ensure on-time closure of the end-to-end process in coordination with BUs and Functions for all Logistics Operations complaints. Provide on the job coaching & training in matters related to Warehouse operations, process efficiency and foster customer focus culture Ensure Zero Medium and Major observation during internal and External audits of Distribution Network  Operations processes. Regular and timely monitoring of Warehousing LSP via defined KPIs, analysis of data from SAP system and / or developing / implementing new KPIs relevant to the business. Conduct regular service provider performance reviews. Continuous process and Cost improvement for the Business Units in the assigned geography Safety/Compliance : Ensure high safety/compliance standards are maintained by all LSP at all times. Conduct internal audits and visits. Communication : Ensure timely and effective communication with all stakeholders (Business Units, 3rd party LSP, Logistics procurement, Customer Service, BU Planners etc) Requirements Education: Degree in supply chain or equivalent Must 5-7 years of experience in the field of Transport Management and Warehouse Knowledge of SAP processes & digitalization of logistics operations Must be able to work in India timezone (11AM-8PM MYT) and on Saturday (Sunday and 1 weekday off day) Only Hindi/Tamil speakers

jobs.name: Auditor, Corporate Audit
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Why does this position exist?  With what permanent objectives? Corporate Audit supports the BASF Board of Executive Directors in its responsibility to perform supervisory, steering and control activities. It is an integral part of BASF’s internal control and risk management system. It has teams spread over 3 continents of the globe. This position will be a part of the Asia-Pacific hub of the global Corporate Audit function. How Will You Do It We are seeking for an experienced Internal Auditor who possesses strong set of internal audit skills including resilience and agility, business acumen, and critical thinking to join our team. Your role will involve conducting audits with a resilient and agile approach to assess the effectiveness of our organization's risk management, control, and governance processes and recommend improvements. You will also be responsible for conducting audits of our organization's financial and operational processes to identify areas for improvement and provide recommendations to enhance efficiency and effectiveness of the process. It is also expected that you actively collaborate with Global experts (Domain Owners/ SPOCs) as regional subject matter expert for specific audit topic/ risk aspect. Main Task : Execute the internal audit plan in accordance with established audit standards and procedures. Evaluate the effectiveness of the organization's risk management, control, and governance processes. Conduct audits of the organization's financial and operational processes to identify areas for improvement. Provide value adding recommendations to enhance efficiency, effectiveness, and compliance with policies and procedures. Communicate audit observation and recommendations to management and other stakeholders. Strong communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate and engage with stakeholders at all levels. Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and provide guidance as needed. Stay up to date with changes in laws, regulations, and industry best practices. Maintain integrity and ethical behaviour, conduct unbiased audits, ensuring transparency, compliance, confidentiality, professionalism, and teamwork mindset in the role. What We Look For Education: Chartered Accountant, Chemical Engineer, or Data Scientist. Preferable Candidate should have Professional Certifications such as: required CIA (Certified Internal Audit) – or work in progress, optional CFE (Certified Fraud Examiner), CISA (Certified Information System Audit) Working Experience 4 – 7 Years experience. Preferably worked on international assignments and working in diverse cultures of team members. Exposure to industries that are manufacturing based preferably chemical companies. Travel Requirement Travel requirement would be ~30%. Approx. 6 to 7 audits per annum with fieldwork for each audit ~3 to 4 weeks. Depending on the audit scope, a site fieldwork, may require travel between 1 - 3 weeks. Orther Proven experience in internal auditing, demonstrating resilience, agility, and critical thinking. Strong understanding of business dynamics that comes with complex organization, financial principles, and internal control frameworks. You will need to possess the aptitude to understand the intricacies of business dynamics, enabling you to provide valuable insights that strengthen the organization's management processes. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify risks and propose effective solutions. Ability to embracing technology and data analytics. With the increasing volume of data and the complexity of business operations, you will leverage data analytics tools to enhance audit efficiency and effectiveness. Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Work Location Godrej One - Vikroli (Mumbai)

jobs.name: Operator, EPC Plant
jobs.description: Main Tasks: 遵照翻班主管的指示，安全和有效的工作。 保持安全，质量和生产力的最高水平。 确保生产计划及时完成。 在轮班期间操作生产工艺并且采取控制措施确保产品质量在规定的范围之内。 排除工艺故障和采取必须的纠正措施。 联系维修和工程人员进行车间内的维修活动，包括亲自参与。 确保良好的卫生状况，依照程序和当地政府规章正确的处理废料。 Requirements: 学历要求： 技术学校化学工程/工艺及化学分析专业或以上学历。 工作经验： 至少 2 年化工或材料加工行业工作经验。 技术与专业知识： 具备基础聚合物知识和批次经验；英语能力为优势；持有叉车操作证者优先。

jobs.name: Administrative Assistant,Industrial Petrochemicals Asia (3rd party one-year contract renewable)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group's success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability - the economy, environment and society - into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About the Role Reporting to Vice President, Business Management, Industrial Petrochemicals, Petrochemicals Asia Pacific, you will provide high level quality secretarial and administrative support to Vice President and the team, ensure smooth daily office operations and interactions in accordance with company policies, procedures, and guidelines. How You Will Do It Support with business meeting coordination, calendar management, travel arrangements and expense claims, including taking necessary or emergency actions where appropriate. Responsible for organizing and maintaining the Vice President’s correspondence, records and filing system, while ensuring proper handling of confidential information and materials. Cascade the Vice President’s messages and communications to relevant individuals or departments, and obtaining timely feedback from internal and external stakeholders. Responsible for arranging and coordinating internal and external meetings, including customer meetings, leadership discussions and regional engagements. Support with organizing team engagement activities and related logistics. Responsible for supporting evolving business needs, priorities and ad hoc tasks with flexibility and responsiveness. What We Look For Diploma or above in Business Management or related disciplines Minimum 8 years of relevant working experience, preferably from a multinational environment Proficiencies in MS Office applications such as MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Attention to details with maturity in handling business correspondences, and the ability to work independently under pressure and collaboratively as part of a team Excellent command of written and spoken English BASF has a rich history in Hong Kong, dating back to the 1880s when it began selling textile dyes in the region. Over the years, the company has grown and expanded its presence, establishing its East Asia Regional Headquarters in Hong Kong in 1995. As a multinational company with a long-standing presence in Hong Kong, BASF is committed to the city's continued success as an internationally competitive business and financial center. By the end of 2025, BASF East Asia Regional Headquarters Limited had 240 employees.

jobs.description: Qualitätsmanager Reklamationsbearbeitung/Customer Quality Representative Langelsheim (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Qualitätsmanager Reklamationsbearbeitung/Customer Quality Representative Langelsheim (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Die globale Geschäftseinheit Oberflächentechnik des Unternehmensbereichs Coatings der BASF, die unter der Marke Chemetall operiert, ist ein führender Lieferant angewandter Oberflächentechnik für Metall-, Kunststoff- und Glassubstrate. Diese Spezialchemikalien werden in vielen Industrien und Märkten genutzt, wie zum Beispiel Automobil, Luftfahrt, Aluminiumveredelung, Coil oder in der metallverarbeitenden Industrie – unterstützt von einem großen Netzwerk technischer Experten. WAS SIE ERWARTET In Ihrer Rolle als Qualitätsmanager Reklamationsbearbeitung/ Customer Quality Representative (m/w/d) am Standort Langelsheim erwarten Sie folgende Aufgaben: Durchführung und Steuerung von Kundenreklamationen, inkl. schneller Eindämmung und nachhaltiger Problemlösung Anwendung strukturierter Problemlösungsmethoden (8D, Ursachenanalyse) mit Fokus auf wirksame Ergebnisse Übernahme der Eskalationsverantwortung für Kundenfälle Hauptansprechpartner für Kunden zu Qualitätsthemen Sicherstellung klarer/fristgerechter Kommunikation mit Kunden Vertretung der „Voice of the Customer“ im Unternehmen Sicherstellung der Einhaltung kundenspezifischer Qualitätsanforderungen und Standards Planung/Koordination/Freigabe von PPAP-/PPA-Prozessen Erstellung, Prüfung und Freigabe von Qualitätsdokumenten (z. B. PPAP, ISIR, FAI) Definition, Überwachung,Analyse von kundenbezogenen KPIs und Scorecards Reduktion von Kundenreklamationen sowie Verbesserung der Kunden-Scorecard-Performance Zusammenarbeit mit funktionsübergreifenden Teams sowie Unterstützung bei FMEA und Lieferantenaudits WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Abgeschlossene technische Ausbildung oder Studium (z. B. Ingenieurwesen, Chemie oder vergleichbar) Mindestens 2 Jahre Erfahrung im Qualitätsmanagement oder Customer Quality und Erfahrung in einem industriellen Umfeld (Automotive, Luft- und Raumfahrt oder Chemie von Vorteil) Gute Kenntnisse in Qualitätsmethoden (PPAP/PPA, FMEA, 8D) Verständnis für kundenspezifische Anforderungen und Qualitätsstandards Kenntnisse der Normen ISO 9001, IATF 16949 und EN 9100 Analytische und strukturierte Arbeitsweise Eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten und Belastbarkeit Gute Kommunikations- und Teamfähigkeit Gute MS Office-Kenntnisse, SAP von Vorteil Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse WAS WIR BIETEN Moderne und umweltfreundliche Verfahren, flache Hierarchien, kurze Entscheidungswege, flexible Arbeitszeiten, vielfältige und herausfordernde Aufgaben, attraktive Sozialleistungen und weltweit engagierte Kolleginnen und Kollegen Im Rahmen unseres Onboardings erhalten unsere neuen Mitarbeitenden eine individuelle, auf ihre Tätigkeit zugeschnittene Einarbeitung, unterstützt durch zahlreiche Schulungsangebote Ein familiäres Miteinander mit wertschätzendem, offenem und vertrauensvollem Umgang sind bei uns gelebte Werte und die Basis für unser gutes Betriebsklima SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Sie haben Fragen zum Ablauf oder zu der Stelle? Für nähere Informationen steht Ihnen Marcel Blaue (Human Resources Business Partner, Chemetall GmbH), Tel.: +49 5326 51-1430 gerne zur Verfügung. ÜBER UNS Das Portfolio besteht aus Lösungen zur Gewährleistung von Korrosionsschutz und Haftung von Beschichtungen, zum Verformen und Bearbeiten von Substraten, für Anwendungen zur Vorbereitung von Oberflächen für den Lackierprozess, sowie Dichtungsmassen für spezifische Anwendungen in der Luftfahrtindustrie. Chemetall hat den Hauptsitz in Frankfurt am Main, mit rund 2.500 Mitarbeitern weltweit und einem globalen Netzwerk an Vertriebs- und Produktionsstandorten. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: APAC Digital Solution Specialist
jobs.description:
About The Role This role is responsible for end ‑ to ‑ end digital solution implementation for Japan business, as well as APAC regional and global level business initiatives. The role will act as the key contact person between end users and global digital product owners, translate local business needs into clear digital requirements, manage the overall project timeline and milestones, and drive the successful roll ‑ out and continuous improvement of global digital solutions. How You Will Do It Digital Transformation & Implementation Planning - Translate global OEM Technology digital strategy and roadmap into actionable implementation plans for Japan, identify local digital opportunities, lead change management, and ensure digital solutions are rolled out on scope and on time Cross ‑ regional Stakeholder & Requirement Management - Serve as the primary point of contact between Japanese users and global digital product owners. Collect, analyze and translate Japanese business requirements into structured digital requirements, responsible for data definition, proactively communicate development progress and manage user expectations - Be responsible for not only local Japan-related business, but also participant in and drive various regional and global level projects and topics Product Ownership & Continuous Improvement - Take ownership of digital solution modules throughout the lifecycle, lead UAT, and feedback collection, and drive continuous optimization of digital solutions to meet business needs. What We Look For - Bachelor’s degree and above in chemical engineering, materials science, or information technology related field - Minimum 3–5 years working experience in digital transformation, well understood Agile / Scrum approach and digital product management - Fluent Japanese (written & spoken) is mandatory for communication with Japanese stakeholders. - Proven experience in managing project timelines, milestones and implementation plans independently, preferably with cross ‑ border project delivery experience. - Strong stakeholder management skills, able to proactively update progress and manage user expectations. - High sense of ownership, proactive mindset and strong problem ‑ solving skills under cross ‑ regional complex environments. - Fluent English for global collaboration with regional teams [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . Ze helpen je in de eerste plaats om je studiekeuze richting 7 Chemische procestechnieken (7CPT) te verkennen. Tijdens onze oriëntatiedagen krijg je een unieke kans om deze studierichting beter te leren kennen én te ontdekken hoe een job als procesoperator eruitziet in de praktijk. We laten je proeven van de wereld van de chemie via echte verhalen van collega’s, een blik in onze installaties en een realistische inkijk in het beroep. JOUW ERVARING TIJDENS DE ORIENTATIEDAGEN Dit mag je verwachten: Een kennismaking met de opleiding 7CPT, aangeboden door secundaire scholen. Een praktijkgerichte opleiding met mogelijkheid tot stage bij BASF Antwerpen. Een realistische inkijk in de job van procesoperator. Verhalen van medewerkers over hun job en parcours. Een bezoek aan (of inkijk) in een installatie. De mogelijkheid om vragen te stellen en zelf te ontdekken We zorgen voor een warme ontvangst en een interactieve ervaring. VOOR WIE? Je bent bereid om bij één van onze externe onderwijspartners de opleiding 7 Chemische procestechnieken te volgen. Je behaalde een diploma secundair onderwijs. Voorkennis is niet noodzakelijk. Je hebt interesse in chemie, techniek en productieprocessen. Je bent nieuwsgierig naar hoe het er aan toe gaat in een chemisch bedrijf en wat de richting 7CPT inhoudt. MEER OVER ONS BASF Antwerpen is het meest toonaangevende chemiebedrijf ter wereld, dankzij onze diverse teams die intelligente oplossingen ontwikkelen voor een duurzame toekomst. Zo draait onze productiesite volledig op groene stroom en investeren we in oplossingen om onze uitstoot van morgen te neutraliseren. Met meer dan 3.000 collega's met verschillende achtergronden, bieden we een inspirerende werkomgeving waar iedereen zichzelf kan zijn en talenten maximaal kunnen ontwikkelen. Voel je de chemie? Grijp je kans en solliciteer vandaag nog. We kijken ernaar uit om jou te verwelkomen in ons team! #destrafsteproductiesite Solliciteer nu Een uniek totaalpakket: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. jobs.name: Global Production Data Architect (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As part of our unit, you play a key role in advancing the digital transformation of Surventis production sites. In the role of Global Production Data Architect, you contribute to building a scalable foundation for the effective use of production and process data across sites and enable data-driven decision-making in manufacturing. In your new role, you define and steer the target architecture for the capture, integration and provision of production as well as process data across multiple sites. You develop standards and concepts for integrating production-relevant data sources such as sensors, PLC, SCADA, historian, MES, LIMS, quality and ERP systems while ensuring data quality, availability and cyber security. Furthermore, you structure production data to enable reporting, performance steering, root cause analysis, process optimization as well as advanced analytics and AI applications, thereby creating the foundation for scalable data products. In close collaboration with business functions, production sites and project teams, you act as a trusted advisor, translate requirements into robust architecture decisions and support the implementation of data-driven solutions. Additionally, you establish global standards for data models, interfaces, documentation and governance to ensure consistency, traceability as well as reusability across sites. Lastly, you collaborate within an international network of sites, experts and stakeholders, drive roadmaps and strategically advance the production data architecture. WHAT YOU OFFER master’s degree in computer science, engineering, business informatics or a comparable scientific or technical field minimum 5 years of professional experience in data architecture, data integration, industrial data platforms or advanced analytics, ideally in a global production environment strong understanding of production processes and industrial system landscapes as well as solid knowledge of IT/OT integration and standards such as ISA-95, OPC or MQTT strong knowledge of data processing, data modeling and data analysis as well as technologies such as Python, SQL, ETL/ELT concepts and modern data platforms experience in designing scalable data architectures and sound understanding of data quality, governance, information security and standardization strong communication skills in both German and English and confidence in collaborating with global stakeholders willingness to travel approximately 10–20% as required by the role WHAT WE OFFER We offer a competitive salary with attractive individual benefits, such as performance-based bonuses. Company-sponsored supplemental long-term care insurance, accident insurance (subject to certain conditions), and an attractive retirement plan provide comprehensive coverage. At our location in Münster, we have our own “Freizeit” fitness studio with an attached physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, an “Auszeit” company restaurant including a café bar, a bilingual ‘LackiKids’ company daycare center, and a “LackiKids on Tour” vacation program. We offer 30 days of vacation per year (full-time), additional leisure options, and a 37.5-hour work week. Employees aged 57 and over receive 2.5 hours/week of additional free time; for employees working shifts, this entitlement applies from the age of 55. We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development. We offer mobile working within Germany and flexible working time models, such as part-time employment or job sharing. HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As part of our unit, you play a key role in advancing the digital transformation of Surventis production sites. In the role of Global Production Data Architect, you contribute to building a scalable foundation for the effective use of production and process data across sites and enable data-driven decision-making in manufacturing. In your new role, you define and steer the target architecture for the capture, integration and provision of production as well as process data across multiple sites. You develop standards and concepts for integrating production-relevant data sources such as sensors, PLC, SCADA, historian, MES, LIMS, quality and ERP systems while ensuring data quality, availability and cyber security. Furthermore, you structure production data to enable reporting, performance steering, root cause analysis, process optimization as well as advanced analytics and AI applications, thereby creating the foundation for scalable data products. In close collaboration with business functions, production sites and project teams, you act as a trusted advisor, translate requirements into robust architecture decisions and support the implementation of data-driven solutions. Additionally, you establish global standards for data models, interfaces, documentation and governance to ensure consistency, traceability as well as reusability across sites. Lastly, you collaborate within an international network of sites, experts and stakeholders, drive roadmaps and strategically advance the production data architecture.
