jobs.name: Global Supply Planner
jobs.description: Now hiring! Global Supply Planner Parma, ID (Hybrid) We are looking for a Global Supply Planner to join our Agricultural Solutions team for our BASF | Nunhems Vegetable Seed business in Parma, ID. Domestic relocation assistance is available, if applicable. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. The Global Supply Planner is responsible for managing the availability to match the supply versus demand on short term, managing the global inventory to achieve our inventory targets, supporting the global production planning process, and coaching and supporting the Regional Supply Planners. As a Global Supply Planner, you operate at the center of the action, maintaining a clear overview of key parameters such as inventory, forecasts, and ongoing productions. You take a pro-active approach in communicating with stakeholders,  enabling you to effectively manage and ensure the Global availability of the crops within your responsibility. Next to managing short-term availability, you play a crucial role in the mid- to long-term, by ensuring that all regions receive seeds on time, enabling them to deliver the right end-products to our customers. As a Global Supply Planner, you create chemistry by... Supporting Global Supply Chain Management in tactical S&OP (production planning), including preparing and analyzing relevant data Executing stock seed planning to meet production and processing needs Driving availability for main processing entities by ensuring timely seed movement between production areas and processing facilities Owning Operational S&OP for regional and global availability Conducting monthly availability follow-ups and communicating status to stakeholders Monitoring usability data closely to keep information current and actionable Chairing the Operational Supply Review Meeting, covering global availability (regional and SF/ST levels), financial impacts, red/black stock, seed production plan effects, and safety stock allocation Managing global inventory in accordance with supply chain strategies and policies (e.g., country-of-origin and phytosanitary requirements) Setting and managing global priorities for seed supply and processing Monitoring execution of the Global Trial Flow against the agreed framework to ensure lead times are met Functionally steering and collaborating closely with Regional Supply Planners Completing administrative tasks required to execute drop-shipments and Processing POs If you... Hold a bachelor’s degree in Business, Agronomy, or a related field (master’s degree preferred ) Have 8–10 years of experience in supply chain, preferably with exposure to international operations Possess advanced skills in ERP systems and Microsoft Excel. Demonstrate in-depth knowledge of supply chain processes and supply chain managemen t Understand the financial aspects of supply chain decisions and inventory management Bring a proactive, availability-driven mindset with a strong supply chain orientation Communicate very effectively in English, both written and verbal Work well as a team player and collaborate across functions Are able to travel up to 5%, domestically and internationally. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Senior Executive, Import Export OVM Malaysia
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position Own and drive end-to-end Import & Export operations execution for Malaysia, ensuring seamless coordination across all stakeholders. Lead Operational Vendor Management (OVM) for 4PL and 3PL, ensuring delivery of agreed service levels, KPI performance, and operational excellence. Ensure strict governance and enforcement of SOPs, maintaining high discipline in execution by service providers. Provide strong operational control, visibility, and proactive risk management to ensure uninterrupted shipment flow. Drive performance improvement, operational efficiency, and service provider accountability in line with business expectations M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities End-to-End Import & Export Operations Ownership Take full accountability for execution and coordination of all import and export shipments for Malaysia operations. Act as the central control tower managing shipment flows across 4PL, 3PL, and internal stakeholders. Ensure timely and accurate execution of all shipment activities in line with agreed delivery timelines. Proactively monitor shipment status and anticipate risks, delays, and bottlenecks, ensuring early intervention. Ensure high level of operational discipline, tracking, and follow-through across all shipments. Operational Vendor Management (4PL / 3PL Governance) Lead governance and oversight of 4PL and 3PL service providers, ensuring adherence to BASF operational standards. Drive vendor accountability for execution quality, responsiveness, and service performance. Ensure all vendors operate strictly with agreed SOPs, processes, and performance expectations. Act as escalation point for operational issues and performance gaps, ensuring timely resolution. Lead alignment with vendors on process changes, service expectations, and continuous improvement initiatives. 3. KPI Management & Performance Control Own and drive end-to-end KPI monitoring and performance tracking for all IE operations. Ensure timely, accurate, and complete submission of KPIs and operational reports from 4PL and 3PL. Validate data integrity and ensure consistent reporting standards across stakeholders. Analyze performance trends and identify gaps, risks, and improvement opportunities. Lead performance review discussions with service providers and ensure closure of performance gaps. 4. Reporting, Visibility & Management Updates Ensure strong operational visibility across all shipments and performance metrics. Consolidate and deliver clear, structured, and management-ready reports (weekly/monthly). Provide insight-driven updates, highlighting key risks, issues, and mitigation actions. Establish and maintain transparent reporting frameworks and dashboards for operational tracking. 5. Issue Management, Escalation & Risk Control Take ownership of critical operational issues and disruptions, ensuring coordinated resolution. Drive structured root cause analysis and corrective action implementation with 4PL/3PL. Escalate major risks and issues to management with clear impact assessment and mitigation plans. Maintain robust tracking of issues, actions, and resolution effectiveness. 6. SOP Compliance & Operational Governance Enforce strict adherence to BASF SOPs and operational frameworks across all service providers. Perform regular monitoring to ensure process discipline and consistency in execution. Identify gaps in SOP adherence and drive corrective actions with vendors. Support continuous improvement and refinement of SOPs and operational governance frameworks. 7. Continuous Improvement & Operational Excellence Identify and drive process improvements to enhance efficiency, reliability, and execution quality. Lead initiatives to reduce operational delays, errors, and rework across the shipment lifecycle. Promote best practices, standardization, and process optimization. Support digitalization and automation initiatives to improve operational visibility and reporting efficiency. Requirements Education: Min Diploman in Logistics, Supply Chain, Business Management, or related field Minimum 3–5 years of experience in: Import & Export operations Logistics coordination and end-to-end shipment execution 4PL / 3PL vendor management Proven experience in: Managing complex operational environments with multiple stakeholders Driving performance management and service provider accountability Strong expertise in: End-to-end Import & Export operations Logistics execution and coordination Vendor management and performance governance Strong capability in: Operational control and execution management KPI tracking, reporting, and performance analysis Issue resolution and escalation management Experience in SAP or equivalent logistics systems preferred

jobs.name: Operator, Packaging II
jobs.description: Objective of the Position Strictly comply with company regulations on environmental protection, occupational health and safety (EHS). Working on alternate shifts carrying out the jobs assigned by the Production Supervisor or Foreman according to work instructions, in safe manner and according to ISO related systems subscribed by the company. Follow the instructions of the production supervisor and report abnormal situations. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities EHS Strictly comply with and implement the company's EHS policies and ISO 14001 certification requirements. Strictly follow SOPs regarding safety, environment, and occupational health in operations. Take appropriate measures for any safety, environmental, or occupational health incidents and report to the Production Supervisor. Correctly wear and use PPE as required. Carry out emergency actions as required by procedures during emergency response events. Operation Ensure the required packaging material for each product run is sufficient; empty bags, labels, pallets and paper tray. Ensure all packaging materials are in good condition, clean and not contaminated. Conduct briefing for packaging personnel according to packaging work requirement. Monitor individual label and master label system for smooth operation. Escalate to superior for any abnormalities. Check and update stock levels for materials, tools and PPE into the record on weekly basis. Perform necessary tasks to start up the packaging equipment for Compounding process such Auto Packaging, Big Bag Station, Semi Auto Sealing Machine, including Fiber Optic Cable, FOC packaging. Strictly follow process sheets and SOPs, check quality control points during production, and compliance with quality requirements. Perform routine production activities for smooth operations including process troubleshooting. Report any equipment abnormalities to Production Supervisor for TSS troubleshooting. Perform verification of line flushing & cleaning for product changeover and ensure no risk on quality of product. Drained material shall be labelled and returned according to requirement. Monitor process conditions according to process log sheets and quality requirements. To support packaging with implementation audit Integrated Management System (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001) and IATF 16949 Quality Management System for the automotive industry. Ensure good housekeeping and proper handovers among shift team members. Other packaging-related tasks assigned by Production Supervisor. Job Requirements At least SPM certificate, Certificate in Engineering, or equivalent Minimum 2–5 years of experience in chemical-based manufacturing plants Must possess a valid Forklift Driving License Familiar with 5S system (My Area & My Machine requirements) Basic knowledge of ISO standards (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, IATF 16949, ISO 45001) Basic knowledge on ISO 9001, ISO 14001, IATF 16949 and ISO 45001 requirements.

jobs.name: Category Buyer ( Site and Business services )
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Support procurement activities for BASF Global Digital (GD) and Global Business (GB) Service Centres being established in Hyderabad. Manage procurement for large and complex offices and sites with multiple production units, businesses, and functions, including Site and Business Services across BASF locations beyond Hyderabad. Contribute to the development, implementation, and monitoring of regional category strategies covering Site and Business Services (e.g., Facility Management, Fleet, HR Services, Marketing & Communications, and Legal). Oversee contract implementation and performance monitoring. Drive category management initiatives to achieve cost efficiencies and value creation. Execute supplier management, including performance evaluation and relationship management. Ensure vendor compliance with human rights practices in line with applicable country laws and regulations. Manage Procure-to-Pay (P2P) processes effectively. How Will You Do It Supplier Management: Monitor supplier performance to ensure alignment with BASF standards on quality, quantity, and service responsiveness. Maintain strong supplier relationships through regular communication and drive corrective actions using established performance management tools when required. Provide regular updates to internal stakeholders on market dynamics, including pricing trends, supply risks, supplier performance, and insights. Identify and develop new suppliers through market research and analysis to support business growth, including supplier evaluation, commercial negotiations, contracting, and onboarding. Supplier Qualification: Identify and develop new supplier sources, with a strong focus on local vendors in Hyderabad. Lead supplier audits and qualification processes in collaboration with technical teams. Source-to-Contract (S2C) Management: Manage the end-to-end S2C process using BASF procurement tools (e.g., SAP Ariba, Mercanis). Take full ownership of bidding processes for medium- to high-value purchases, including documentation, evaluation, and award. Procure-to-Pay (P2P) Management: Oversee the complete P2P cycle, including purchase orders (via SAP), contract execution (including paper-based agreements where applicable), and vendor payments. Negotiation & Cost Management: Develop and execute negotiation strategies for high-value contracts, with a strong focus on achieving EBIT savings in line with BASF policies. Category Management – Business & Site Services: Lead S2C activities for Business Services categories, including: HR services (manpower outsourcing, recruitment) Marketing services (events, promotions, corporate communications) Legal and professional consulting services Maintain deep category knowledge across site services (facility management, leasing, fleet management) and business support functions. Compliance & Governance: Ensure adherence to human rights, governance, and regulatory requirements, particularly in manpower outsourcing for manufacturing and business operations. Manage internal and external audits and ensure compliance across all assigned procurement activities. Market Intelligence & Strategy: Continuously monitor market trends, cost developments, and supplier landscapes through data analysis and external engagement. Translate insights into procurement strategies and provide forecasts on cost and supply impacts to relevant stakeholders. Stakeholder & Supplier Communication: Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders and external suppliers to ensure effective implementation of BASF procurement policies and procedures. Additional Responsibilities & Requirements Operate under the functional guidance of the Regional Category Manager. Execute supplier and risk management strategies as defined, including performance evaluation, innovation initiatives, and mitigation plans. Report negotiation outcomes and procurement performance metrics. Collaborate closely with headquarters in Mumbai and regional teams in Shanghai. Preferred Experience Strong knowledge of the Hyderabad market is an advantage. Experience managing procurement for large multinational corporate offices (GCCs, GSCs, shared services centers). Exposure to large-scale office environments (approx. 100,000 sq. ft., 2,000–3,000 headcount). Experience in office interiors procurement is an added advantage. What We Look For Education: Master’s degree with a minimum of 8 years of relevant experience, or Bachelor’s degree with at least 10 years of experience across two relevant roles. An MBA / CPSM/CIPS is an added advantage. Professional Experience: Proven experience in Procurement, preferably within a corporate or multinational environment. Core Competencies: Strong negotiation and stakeholder management skills In-depth knowledge of suppliers, market dynamics, products, and technologies within relevant categories Good understanding of Business Services, including HR, Marketing, Corporate Communications, Legal, and engagement of professional consultants Language Proficiency: Fluency in English and Hindi Knowledge of regional language (Telugu) is an added advantage Technical Skills: Proficiency in SAP systems (e.g., SAP R/3, Cobalt, SAP Business Warehouse, Strategic Procurement Suite) Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint Familiarity with digital tools such as Copilot and Power BI

jobs.name: Product Safety (Specialist 1)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position Deliver Product Safety services in specific Chemical Regulations, and/or Product Stewardship skillset. Interphase with various stakeholders such as supply chain, customer services and technical experts to enable the implementation of compliance policies and BASF minimum requirements. Continuous improvement for designated work processes and communication processes, including change management. Ensure right for the first time for services deliverable including evaluation and execution of corrective action Main Areas of Responsibilities Execute related Product Safety services deliverables, including chemical regulation operational compliance measures through close collaboration among team and cross-team through SAP module Monitor product report and review compliance with relevant areas against business processes predefined criteria Evaluate and analyze product regulatory data for system and database maintenance Verify compliance documentation required for related business processes including import, export and domestic trade Perform necessarily follow up action to ensure deliverables executed on timely basis Contribute to productivity, efficiency and Right First Time to achieve operation excellence Effective communication with stakeholders as required in daily operation and timely escalation to leader on critical topics where relevant Execute discussion independently intrateam and collaborate for achievement Participate in continuous improvement projects by contributing to efficiency study, analysis and implementation phase Supports internal and external audit, including Quality Checks for potential improvements. Review, update and improve related work documentation such as process description, work guides, etc. to ensure sustainable knowledge retention and standardization within department. Job Requirements Bachelor degree in scientific area, preferably in Chemistry, or related Science discipline Minimum 2 years in chemical regulation, product regulatory affairs or relevant work experience in Product Safety and/or Stewardship activities. Technical & Professional Knowledge Sound knowledge and experience in Chemistry related field. Proficiency in MS Office is preferred and experience in SAP application is an advantage. Fluent in written and spoken in English. Experience in technology or digital related skills or tool is an advantage, e.g. Robotic Process Automation, Power BI, etc. Excellent analytical, organizational, communication and interpersonal skills. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Product Safety (Senior Specialist I)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Do you want to work with passionate teams, providing excellent business and digital services to the Asia Pacific region? Do you want an open, supportive, and caring working environment? Do you view your development needs seriously as we do? If YES, then take your career to the #NextLevel with BASF ! O bjectives of the Position Deliver Product Safety services, demonstrating intermediate to advance competencies in the specific Chemical Regulations, and/or Product Stewardship skillset Lead discussion for the designated services with various stakeholders such as supply chain, customer services and technical experts for execution and/or improvement actions of related compliance measures Continuous improvement for designated work processes and communication processes, including change management Guide related member(s) in performing routine tasks for designated services Main Areas of Responsibilities Product Safety Asia Pacific is an integrated expertise platform for steering compliant and safe product across the entire product value chain for BASF. We provide effective & efficient standard services and customized professional expertise services for various business and cross regions. As Senior Specialist with designated expertise on BASF Product Safety compliance framework, to execute chemical regulation and product safety related compliance measures for BASF companies. Collaborate closely with team for excellence services and active participation in operation excellence activities and initiatives. Execute related Product Safety services deliverables, including chemical regulation operational compliance measures through close collaboration among team and cross sub-team, including timely addressing non-conformance management. Monitor related regulatory reporting and review compliance relevant areas against business processes predefined criteria Evaluate, analyze and verify product regulatory data and compliance documentation for system and database compliance monitoring and maintenance Assist team members in learning and issues troubleshooting related to the service delivery including communication with internal stakeholder and other business partners As part of region specialist on BASF Product Safety Compliance framework, to assess risk and ensure related regulatory activities are executed timely and in accordance to the requirements. Active execution of knowledge exchange on best practices and training within sub-team and cross team. Lead quality checks, including non-conformance monitoring and active participation in improvement initiatives. Take part as key focal to support internal and external audit, including execution of improvement measures. Ensure timely review, update and improvement related work documentation by team, such as work guide, process description, etc. Job Requirements University degree, Master’s degree in scientific area, preferably in Chemistry, or related Science discipline At least 3-4 years of relevant experience in Chemical Regulation, Product Regulatory Affairs, or related fields focused on Product Safety and Stewardship activities. Technical & Professional Knowledge Excellent analytical abilities with a keen attention to detail. Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with a result-oriented mindset. Sound knowledge in Chemistry and regulations Good understanding on global and local chemical legislation system, master data system, supply chain processes and business processes of BASF Fluent in written and spoken English Proactive and be able to work under pressure and independently The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: Technical Product Manager (w/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME TO OUR TEAM Como una de las empresas líderes del sector a escala mundial, desarrollamos y fabricamos pinturas para los sectores del automóvil e industrial. El éxito de nuestro equipo se basa en soluciones innovadoras y una estrecha colaboración con nuestros clientes. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Technical lead responsible for managing the products and projects under their scope. Conducts laboratory tests in accordance with control plans and/or customer specifications, following the company’s established testing methods. Provides technical support to customer accounts, as well as general and occasional direct support to clients, offering appropriate solutions to product-related issues within their area of responsibility. Develops and adapts new products. Prepares, reviews, adjusts, and modifies product formulas, colour formulations, and/or processes when required, in coordination with colour specialists, applicators, production plants, and the production technology laboratory. Designs base formulations, including all required phases and documentation such as feasibility studies, formulation, product and process FMEA, and product control plans, in accordance with applicable standards. Supports and monitors products throughout their lifecycle, making manufacturing corrections or product adjustments when needed, with support from the Team Leader if required. WHAT YOU OFFER Degree in a chemical/technical field or an equivalent academic qualification. Previous experience in a chemical laboratory is an advantage, ideally within the automotive industry. Proficiency in MS Office tools; SAP user-level knowledge is a plus. Advanced level of Spanish and fluent in English (B2). Knowledge of German will be considered an asset. Strong creativity, organizational skills, ability to work in an international team, and motivation to take on new challenges. WHAT WE OFFER Continuous learning and career development through our internal job opportunities. A safe work environment where we actively promote your health and well-being. Subsidized canteen on site.Free company shuttle service between Alcalá and Marchamalo. Competitive compensation package including a fixed salary and performance-based variable pay. Opportunities to connect outside the office and stay active through company sports groups. Flexible benefits package, which can be allocated voluntarily to transportation, training, childcare, and more. Financial support for schooling and additional benefits for families. HOW TO REACH US Submit your CV in English through the job posting so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, our Spanish Talent Acquisition team based in Germany will contact you (phone prefix +49) and guide you through the process. With each step, you’ll be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any questions about the process, feel free to contact Liliana (liliana-marcela.quiroga-duarte@basf.com*). *Please note that we will not be able to accept applications via this email address. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: IAM Architect - Identity & Access Management (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME TO OUR TEAM In the Coatings division, we focus on developing, producing, and marketing innovative coating solutions – from applied surface technologies to automotive paints. We drive innovation, design, and new applications to meet the needs of our partners worldwide in globally connected teams. This portfolio is complemented by “Beyond Paint Solutions,” enabling new surfaces and advanced materials WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As an IAM Architect, you are responsible for designing, evolving, and ensuring the effective implementation of our global Identity & Access Management (IAM) landscape. You combine strategic architecture ownership with hands-on expertise, acting as the IT-Infrastructure key interface between internal teams (esp. Cybersecurity, SAP, Applications, business units) and Managed Service Providers (MSP). In cooperation with the MSP, you ensure that IAM solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned with business and compliance requirements. You will take ownership across the following IAM domains: Entra ID / Azure AD, Access Governance, SSO, MFA, PIM/PAM, Joiner–Leaver–Mover Integration, Password Management Vault, PKI & Key Management. Your key responsibilities include: Define and evolve the global IAM strategy together with security and infrastructure experts Design end-to-end IAM architecture across all identity lifecycle processes (Joiner–Mover–Leaver) Act as professional counterpart towards MSPs: steer and govern external service delivery Ensure stable, secure, and compliant IAM operations as well as integration with enterprise architecture (I&O Platform, applications, cloud) Provide hands-on expertise in solution design, integrations, and complex troubleshooting scenarios Support and advise projects and business stakeholders on IAM-related topics WHAT YOU OFFER Degree in computer science, business informatics, or comparable qualification Several years of practical experience in IAM, ideally in an international industrial environment with strong SAP footprint Broad expertise as an IAM generalist across multiple domains Hands-on experience across core IAM domains, leveraging solutions such as Entra ID, SailPoint, CyberArk, SuccessFactors & ServiceNow, Keeper and DigiCert Strong expertise in: Microsoft Entra ID / Azure AD Identity Governance solutions (e.g., SailPoint, One Identity) Authentication protocols (SAML, OAuth, OpenID Connect) Directory services (LDAP, Active Directory) Cloud platforms (Azure, & AWS) Experience working with and steering Managed Service Providers Solid understanding of IT infrastructure and hybrid cloud environments Strong collaboration mindset with willingness to work closely with international teams and travel to sites esp. headquarter Münster, Germany Ability to translate business requirements into pragmatic, scalable solutions Structured, self-driven, and solution-oriented working style Strong communication skills and experience working in global teams Fluent in Spanish and English WHAT WE OFFER Continuous learning and career development through our internal job opportunities. A safe work environment where we actively promote your health and well-being. Subsidized canteen on site. Free company shuttle service between Alcalá and Marchamalo. Competitive compensation package including a fixed salary and performance-based variable pay. Opportunities to connect outside the office and stay active through company sports groups. Flexible benefits package, which can be allocated voluntarily to transportation, training, childcare, and more. Financial support for schooling and additional benefits for families. HOW TO REACH US Submit your CV in English through the job posting so we can get to know each other. If your profile matches, our Spanish Talent Acquisition team based in Germany will contact you (phone prefix +49) and guide you through the process. With each step, you’ll be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any questions about the process, feel free to contact Liliana (liliana-marcela.quiroga-duarte@basf.com*). *Please note that we will not be able to accept applications via this email address. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. IAM Architect - Identity & Access Management (m/f/d) Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Buscar ofertas de empleo Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Acceso al perfil Acceso empleado/a Descripción del puesto IAM Architect - Identity & Access Management (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Enviar candidatura ahora TE DAMOS LA BIENVENIDA A NUESTRO EQUIPO En la división de Coatings , nos centramos en el desarrollo, la producción y la comercialización de soluciones innovadoras de recubrimiento, desde tecnologías de superficie aplicadas hasta pinturas para la industria de la automoción. Impulsamos la innovación, el diseño y nuevas aplicaciones para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros clientes y socios en todo el mundo, trabajando en equipos globales e interconectados. Nuestra cartera se complementa con “Beyond Paint Solutions” , una propuesta que va más allá de la pintura tradicional para crear nuevas superficies y materiales avanzados. LO QUE PUEDES ESPERAR Como IAM Architect, serás responsable de diseñar, evolucionar y garantizar la correcta implementación de nuestro entorno global de Identity & Access Management (IAM). Combinarás la visión estratégica de arquitectura con conocimientos técnicos prácticos, actuando como interlocutor clave de IT Infrastructure entre los equipos internos —especialmente Cybersecurity, SAP, Applications y las unidades de negocio— y los proveedores de servicios gestionados (MSP). En colaboración con el MSP, asegurarás que las soluciones IAM sean seguras, escalables y estén alineadas con los requisitos del negocio y de cumplimiento normativo. Asumirás la responsabilidad en los siguientes ámbitos de IAM: Entra ID / Azure AD, Access Governance, SSO, MFA, PIM/PAM, integración Joiner–Leaver–Mover, Password Management Vault, PKI y Key Management. Tus principales responsabilidades incluirán: Definir y evolucionar la estrategia global de IAM junto con los expertos de seguridad e infraestructura. Diseñar la arquitectura IAM end-to-end en todos los procesos del ciclo de vida de identidades: Joiner–Mover–Leaver. Actuar como contraparte profesional frente a los MSP, coordinando y supervisando la prestación de servicios externos. Garantizar operaciones IAM estables, seguras y conformes, así como su integración con la arquitectura empresarial, incluyendo I&O Platform, aplicaciones y cloud. Aportar experiencia práctica en el diseño de soluciones, integraciones y resolución de incidencias complejas. Dar soporte y asesoramiento a proyectos y stakeholders de negocio en temas relacionados con IAM. LO QUE OFRECES Titulación universitaria en Informática, Informática de Gestión o una cualificación equivalente. Varios años de experiencia práctica en Identity & Access Management (IAM), idealmente en un entorno industrial internacional con una fuerte presencia de SAP. Amplios conocimientos como profesional generalista de IAM, abarcando múltiples áreas de especialización. Experiencia práctica en los principales ámbitos de IAM, utilizando soluciones como Entra ID, SailPoint, CyberArk, SuccessFactors, ServiceNow, Keeper y DigiCert. Sólidos conocimientos en: Soluciones de Identity Governance (por ejemplo, SailPoint o One Identity) Microsoft Entra ID / Azure AD Protocolos de autenticación (SAML, OAuth, OpenID Connect) Servicios de directorio (LDAP, Active Directory) Plataformas cloud (Azure y AWS) Experiencia trabajando con y coordinando Managed Service Providers (MSP). Conocimientos sólidos de infraestructura IT y entornos híbridos en la nube. Alta capacidad de colaboración y disposición para trabajar estrechamente con equipos internacionales y viajar a diferentes centros de trabajo, especialmente a la sede central en Münster (Alemania). Capacidad para transformar requisitos de negocio en soluciones prácticas, escalables y sostenibles. Estilo de trabajo estructurado, autónomo y orientado a la búsqueda de soluciones.Excelentes habilidades de comunicación y experiencia trabajando en equipos globales. Nivel fluido de Español e inglés. LO QUE OFRECEMOS Formación continua y desarrollo profesional con nuestras vacantes internas. Entorno seguro y donde promovemos tu salud. Ticket restaurante. Oferta económica competitiva con un salario base más un variable por buen desempeño. Modelo híbrido de trabajo y adaptar tus horarios, según tus circunstancias. Conectar fuera de la oficina y practicar deportes con compañeros/as mediante Grupos de empresa. Retribución flexible que puedes destinar voluntariamente a servicios de transporte, formación, guardería, etc. Planes de pensiones, seguro de vida y accidentes, préstamos personales y participar en programa de acciones de la compañía. jobs.name: Intern, Regional Projects & Value Chain Analysis, Petrochemicals Asia Pacific
jobs.description: At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group's success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability - the economy, environment and society - into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About The Role Reporting to the Senior Manager, Regional Strategic Marketing Petrochemicals Asia Pacific, you will be responsible to support the team on key strategic regional business unit (RBU) initiatives and drive market analyses to identify additional opportunities in Asia-Pacific How You Will Do It RBU Initiatives: Examine data to identify mid- to long-term market opportunities and develop hypotheses to support business growth Facilitate workshops and strategic discussions with the Petrochemicals Asia Pacific Leadership Team by preparing clear and compelling materials Contribute ideas on workshop structure, objective and documentation Market Analysis: Review and analyse existing market intelligence, highlighting insights relevant to the current organization and business context Provide objective perspectives on market gaps and recommend potential target opportunities for the organization What We Look For Students of Bachelor’s Degree in Chemicals, Science or related disciplines Commitment to a 3-month of internship Proficiency in MS Word, Excel & PowerPoint Excellent communication, interpersonal and organization skills Fluent in English, with proficiency in Mandarin, will be helpful BASF has a rich history in Hong Kong, dating back to the 1880s when it began selling textile dyes in the region. Over the years, the company has grown and expanded its presence, establishing its East Asia Regional Headquarters in Hong Kong in 1995. As a multinational company with a long-standing presence in Hong Kong, BASF is committed to the city's continued success as an internationally competitive business and financial center. By the end of 2025, BASF East Asia Regional Headquarters Limited had 240 employees.

jobs.name: Assistant Manager - EHS
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main responsibilities EHS Management System Gap analysis and implementation for the new EHS regulation and requirement. Maintains EHS management system (IMS) and documentations. Support to implement EHS standards to compliance according local and BASF requirement. Define training needs, coordinate/arranges/delivery EHS training. Introduce and initiate country EHS TA together with site TA. Work with the country EHS to deliver competitive, reliable and high quality EHS services according to site needs. Benchmarks the EHS performances in the site (i.e. Initiates measures and proposals to contain the potential issues and continuously improves EHS standards of the Sites). Stakeholder Management Interfaces with site management & Business unit. Interfaces with local industry groups on behalf of BASF for issue management and advocacy. Represents BASF to participant local industry organization. Dialogues regularly with site managements to evaluate level of service, prioritizes & allocates resources and improves effectively and efficiency of the EHS service. Implement service catalogue and conducts service dialogue with site and business unit to fine-tune the service scopes. TDS (Transportation & Distribution Safety) Ensure safety and security standards for all modes of transportation are implementing and compliance. Nomination as Country/Site Dangerous Goods Safety Advisor. Training and Guidance to TDS related personnel involved in distribution safety processes. Conduct TDS Reviews for all Responsible person. Conduct Safety and Quality Assessments of suppliers, service providers and customers that transport or store our raw materials or goods. Incorporation of Transportation and Distribution Safety Performance in the Procurement Process. Carrier/Trucker Qualification Checks and Performance Evaluation. Incident Investigation, Corrective Actions and Reporting. ER (Emergency Response) Nomination as Country Emergency Expert. Assist to develop, review On-site Emergency Response Plans and scenario, fire prevention concept and compliance to legal and BASF requirement. Conduct Fire prevention assessment and fire prevention concept to meet as per requirement. Provide expertise to support and review for fire prevention design and equipment installation. Crisis and Security Management Nomination as Country/Local Security Manager as per BASF requirement. Provide expertise to support Site Security concept. Conduct self-assessment site security and security risk assessment. Coordinate with regional team to provide security review. Lead to investigate security incident. Develop and prepare readiness Country Incident Management. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in EHS, Science, Engineering, Production, Chemical Engineering, or a related field is required. A Master’s degree or PhD is an advantage. At least 5 years of experience in production, engineering, or EHS is required. Leadership experience is an advantage. A professional-level Safety Officer must hold at least a Bachelor’s degree in Occupational Health and Safety or a related field. Proficiency in both spoken and written English is required. Strong communication and management skills, familiarity with local EHS legislation and legal requirements, and a solid understanding of EHS unit scope and functions are required. Work location Emporium office, Bangkok BASF has been present in Thailand for more than 50 years. The company offers a wide range of products and solutions to support almost all the key industries in the country, including Agricultural Solutions, Intermediates and Process Catalysts, Monomers, Petrochemicals, Coatings, Dispersions and Resins, Performance Chemicals, Care Chemicals, Nutrition and Health, and Performance Materials. BASF in Thailand was established in 1966 and maintains its head office in Bangkok. It operates three production sites in Samutprakarn, Chonburi and Rayong, an Automotive Coatings Technical Competence Center, and a Performance Materials Technical Development Center in Samutprakarn. In 2024, BASF posted sales of €531 million to customers in Thailand and had 623 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/th .

Als spezialisierte Serviceeinheit stehen wir für eine hohe Kundenorientierung und gestalten unsere Dienstleistungen partnerschaftlich, effizient und zukunftsweisend. WAS SIE ERWARTET In unserem Payroll Team beraten und betreuen wir die Mitarbeitenden der BASF SE sowie weitere Gesellschaften rund um das Thema Entgelt. Sie erstellen eigenverantwortlich Lohn- und Gehaltsabrechnungen und beraten die Mitarbeitenden Ihres Betreuungskreises bezüglich ihrer Fragen zu Abrechnungsthemen. jobs.name: Technical Sales Manager- Advanced Metallization
jobs.description: Now hiring! Technical Sales Manager- Advanced Metallization Hillsboro, OR (Onsite) Come create chemistry with us! The Technical Sales Manager – Advanced Metallization will join BASF’s Electronic Materials team, focusing on driving growth and innovation in metallization solutions for advanced semiconductor manufacturing. This role is responsible for managing customer and equipment supplier projects, identifying new business opportunities, and supporting the development and commercialization of next-generation metallization products (e.g., copper, cobalt, ruthenium, and related chemistries for sub-10nm nodes and advanced packaging). At BASF Electronic Materials, we uphold a relentless passion to innovate the future of electronics with our customers by equipping them with the essential solutions and technologies to continuously succeed in the competitive electronics industry. BASF has been supplying electronic materials for more than 30 years. The experience we have earned and the chemical expertise we possess enables us to offer outstanding products and solutions to our customers. At BASF, we offer high purity and quality process chemicals and specialized solutions for semiconductors, flat panel displays, and lighting such as LED. With our team of passionate, dedicated experts from all facets of the industry fields working closely with our customers worldwide, we are steadfast in our philosophy to deliver the quality excellence, reliable services, and innovative solutions that exceed the highest standards. As a Technical Sales Manager- Advanced Metallization, you create chemistry by... As a Technical Sales Manager - Advanced Metallization, you create chemistry by... Identify industry trends, unmet customer needs, and key growth drivers in advanced metallization for semiconductor devices. Lead cross-functional projects with US customers and equipment suppliers (e.g., deposition tool vendors), ensuring timely execution and alignment with global technical teams. Evaluate business potential and secure new opportunities by launching new products or expanding existing product lines. Initiate and foster strategic collaborations with customers and tool suppliers to accelerate innovation and adoption of BASF’s metallization solutions. Gather and analyze market intelligence on emerging technologies, customer requirements, and competitive landscape. Provide technical support for new applications and product development, working closely with R&D, sales, and manufacturing teams. Translate customer and market requirements into actionable product specifications and project plans. Inspire creativity and manage abstract concepts in a highly collaborative, global environment. If you have... Experience with semiconductor industry standards, advanced node requirements (sub-10nm) and/or advanced packaging. Familiarity with process integration and qualification of metallization materials Management experience in directing, coordinating and managing team activities to accomplish highly technical tasks. Bachelor’s degree or higher in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field. Minimum 10 years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a focus on metallization, film deposition (CVD, PVD, ALD), electrochemical plating, or advanced packaging. Proven track record of managing complex technical projects and collaborating with leading semiconductor manufacturers and/or equipment suppliers. Strong technical communication skills, with the ability to convey detailed requirements and results to both technical and non-technical audiences. Demonstrated leadership and influencing skills in cross-functional, multicultural teams. Self-starter with excellent organizational skills and the ability to work independently. Willingness to travel up to 25%. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Unsere Mitarbeiter:innen sind unser wertvollstes Gut und wir pflegen eine Kultur der Empathie, der Unterstützung und des Wachstums. Auf diese Weise befähigen wir unser Team, sich persönlich und beruflich weiterzuentwickeln. Mit vereinten Kräften engagieren wir uns für die Entwicklung nachhaltiger, digitaler und innovativer Lösungen für eine bessere Zukunft. Pflegende Hautcremes, milde Shampoos, Sonnenschutzmittel, schonende und umweltfreundliche Waschmittel und leistungsstarke Reiniger für Haushalt und Industrie – all diese Produkte nutzen Inhaltsstoffe aus unserem Bereich Care Chemicals. Darüber hinaus bieten wir beispielsweise Lösungen auf dem Gebiet des Pflanzenschutzes, für technische Anwendungen in der Bauchemie oder Rohstoffe für die Lackindustrie sowie digitale Services für unsere Kunden. Weltweit arbeiten wir gemeinsam mit unseren Kunden an innovativen Lösungen, die Endverbraucher:innen und industrielle Formulierer überzeugen – und das zunehmend auf Basis von natürlichen und nachhaltig gewonnenen Rohstoffen. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam eine bessere Zukunft entwerfen! WAS SIE ERWARTET Unser Cluster EMO stellt Tenside und Fettalkohole für Hygiene, Kosmetik, Wasch- und Reinigungsmittel, sowie für technische Anwendungen für den Unternehmensbereich Care Chemicals her. Als Maintenance Manager führen Sie das Instandhaltungsteam fachlich und disziplinarisch und gewährleisten die Durchführung der Zielvereinbarungs- sowie Entwicklungsgespräche und das Qualifikationsmanagement. Dazu übernehmen Sie Verantwortung für das Kostencontrolling sowie das Auftragsmanagement von beauftragten Instandhaltungsmaßnahmen und treffen operative Make-or-buy-Entscheidungen (Steuerung Eigen-Fremd-Mix). Sie unterstützen und beraten den Asset Manager bei Maßnahmen zur Optimierung der IH-Effektivität sowie der Anlagen und bringen IH-Aspekte bei Anlagenänderungen und Projekten ein. Zu Ihren Aufgaben zählt weiterhin die Wahrnehmung von (delegierten) Betreiberpflichten im verantworteten Aufgabenbereich sowie die Umsetzung von Vorgaben aus Gesetzen, Vorschriften, Verordnungen und Richtlinien. Sie tragen zur Weiterentwicklung des IH-Leistungsportfolios sowie der IH-Prozesse, -Methoden und -Werkzeuge und Steigerung der IH-Effizienz bei. Bei Ihrer Arbeit kooperieren Sie aktiv mit anderen Instandhaltungsmanagern am Standort. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN akademischer Abschluss im Bereich Ingenieurwissenschaften, vorzugsweise Maschinenbau oder Maschinenbautechniker mindestens fünf Jahre einschlägige Berufserfahrung, Personalverantwortung von Vorteil umfassende Fachkenntnisse in Reparatur- und Wartungsprozessen sowie im operativen Controlling vertraut mit Einkaufsprozessen und -richtlinien sowie den tariflichen und betrieblichen Regelungen in HR fundierte Kenntnisse in SAP R/3 PM Moderations- und Präsentationsgeschick sowie sehr gute Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse WAS WIR BIETEN Bruttojahresgehalt (Vollzeit): 80.000 – 110.000 €, je nach Qualifikation und Berufserfahrung. inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen Mentoringprogramme und Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Karrierewege als fachliche Weiterentwicklung auf andere Stellen im Unternehmen (auch Jobrotation) Kooperationen mit Fitness – und Gesundheitsstudio und zahlreiche weitere (Vorsorge-) Angebote für Ihre Gesundheit 30 Urlaubstage attraktive Vergütung sowie ein variabler Bonus Sozialberatung, Unterstützung bei der Altersvorsorge und Aktienprogramm für Mitarbeitende SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Valeria Illera (Talent Acquisition), valeria.illera@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-58110 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. (Fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden) Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier ÜBER UNS Pflegende Cremes, milde Shampoos, starke Stylinggele, glänzende Lippenstifte und schützende Sonnenlotionen - all diese Produkte enthalten Inhaltsstoffe der BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH, einer Tochtergesellschaft der BASF. Auf Basis natürlicher, nachwachsender Rohstoffe entwickeln und produzieren wir ein großes Sortiment an Inhaltsstoffen für die Körperpflegeindustrie. Mehr über die BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH erfahren Sie unter: on.basf.com/CareAndNutrition Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Intern, Laboratory
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Responsibility: Routine maintenance of lab equipment & Accessory consumable (parts and standard solution inventory management). Assist in sample Management (Routine samples & Retaining samples) Operation of lab instruments, routine sample analysis, data documentation & initiate action whenever required Report to supervisor/ Manager immediately of observe any abnormal findings. Maintaining and backup of instrument data. Implements EHS regulation based on BASF guideline (including Maintenance of QC_Lab Safety and Housekeeping). Special assignment given by superior. Primary EHS responsibilities: Comply with all established procedures as provided. Use the required personal protective equipment (PPE) provided for the task. Provide feedback on EHS matters to immediate supervisor. Immediate reporting of all near-miss including unsafe act/condition and incident to immediate supervisor. Participate in hazard identification, risk assessments and determination of controls, if appropriate. Participate in incident investigation, if appropriate. Requirement: Educational background in a relevant field with a strong willingness to learn.

jobs.name: Assistant Sample Logistics (m/f/d)
jobs.description: Welkom bij BASF Bij BASF heeft chemie gelijk. Omdat we vertrouwen op innovatie in onze oplossingen, op duurzaamheid in onze acties en op onze verbondenheid in ons denken. En in jou. Word onderdeel van onze succesformule en ontwikkel de toekomst met ons - in een wereldwijd en divers team, dat staat voor gelijke kansen, ongeacht leeftijd, achtergrond, geslacht, seksuele identiteit, handicap, geloof of overtuigingen. In Heerenveen (ca. 230 medewerkers) ontwikkelen en produceren wij watergedragen polymeerproducten en formulation additives voor de Verpakkings- en Coatingindustrie. Met een multidisciplinair team (Marketing, Technical Service, Application & Syntheses Lab, Engineering en Production) werken wij aan marktvragen van de Printing & Packaging industry. Wat je kunt verwachten De afdeling Sample Logistics, als onderdeel van Supply Chain, verzorgt het (her)verpakken en verzenden van ± 50.000 commerciële samples per jaar wereldwijd. Als Medewerker Samples draag je bij aan de klanttevredenheid en kom je tegemoet aan de wensen van de business (snelheid, nauwkeurigheid, flexibiliteit en betrouwbaarheid). Als onderdeel van een klein team zorg je voor de administratieve en fysieke verwerking van orders in samenwerking met in- en externe partijen. Dit betekent: Je zorgt voor tijdige en juiste voorbereiding en uitgifte van materialen om tegemoet te komen aan de benodigdheden van de business. Je bent verantwoordelijk voor alle operationele en administratieve activiteiten met betrekking tot samples. Daarna zorg je voor de juiste ontvangst, opslag en verzending van goederen volgens de wettelijke eisen voor opslag en transport. Bovendien sta je garant voor een hoge kwaliteit van de ERP-data en interne voorraden. Je zorgt voor veilige en efficiënte (her)verpakking naar samplemateriaal. Wat wij verwachten Je hebt minimaal een diploma mbo-3, bij voorkeur in een logistieke richting. Je hebt relevante werkervaring binnen de logistiek en enige aantoonbare ervaring met administratieve werkzaamheden. Je bent een goede netwerker en onderhoudt contact met externe partijen binnen de BASF vestigingen en internationale vervoerders. Je hebt kennis van SAP (ERP systeem). Kennis van het werken met gevaarlijke stoffen en het vervoer daarvan per weg of lucht is een groot pluspunt. Je hebt goede communicatieve vaardigheden in het Nederlands en Engels. Je bent een teamplayer. Aanbod Verantwoordelijkheid vanaf dag één in een uitdagende (internationale) werkomgeving en "on-the-job" opleiding als onderdeel van een betrokken team. Daarnaast ontvang je: Een leuke baan voor langere tijd voor 40 uren per week 13e maand, 8,3% vakantiegeld en een variabele bonus 25 vakantiedagen + 6 adv dagen (op basis van een 40-urige werkweek) Uitstekende carrièremogelijkheden in een internationaal bedrijf

Als Medewerker Samples draag je bij aan de klanttevredenheid en kom je tegemoet aan de wensen van de business (snelheid, nauwkeurigheid, flexibiliteit en betrouwbaarheid). Als onderdeel van een klein team zorg je voor de administratieve en fysieke verwerking van orders in samenwerking met in- en externe partijen. Dit betekent: Je zorgt voor tijdige en juiste voorbereiding en uitgifte van materialen om tegemoet te komen aan de benodigdheden van de business. Je bent verantwoordelijk voor alle operationele en administratieve activiteiten met betrekking tot samples. Daarna zorg je voor de juiste ontvangst, opslag en verzending van goederen volgens de wettelijke eisen voor opslag en transport. Bovendien sta je garant voor een hoge kwaliteit van de ERP-data en interne voorraden. Je zorgt voor veilige en efficiënte (her)verpakking naar samplemateriaal. Wat wij verwachten Je hebt minimaal een diploma mbo-3, bij voorkeur in een logistieke richting. Je hebt relevante werkervaring binnen de logistiek en enige aantoonbare ervaring met administratieve werkzaamheden. Je bent een goede netwerker en onderhoudt contact met externe partijen binnen de BASF vestigingen en internationale vervoerders. Je hebt kennis van SAP (ERP systeem). Kennis van het werken met gevaarlijke stoffen en het vervoer daarvan per weg of lucht is een groot pluspunt. Je hebt goede communicatieve vaardigheden in het Nederlands en Engels. Je bent een teamplayer. Solliciteren Klaar om deel uit te maken van ons succesverhaal? Solliciteer nu! Je kunt direct solliciteren via de link in de vacature of stuur je cv en motivatie per mail naar Talent-Acquisition Bene@basf.com. Veel succes! Solliciteer nu Een uniek totaalpakket: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a
 wide range of elements you need to be your best in every stage of your life. That’s what we call you@BASF. Click
 here to learn more. A
 unique

Dat noemen we you@BASF. Klik hier voor meer informatie. jobs.name: Senior Specialist Digitalization Digital Plant (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF Our GDD/D – Digitalization of Operations unit implements digital technologies and applications in production plants. This enables us to increase production effectiveness. At the same time, processes become more efficient and safer. RESPONSIBILITIES Our goal is the implementation of digital technologies and applications in chemical plants, to improve work processes and increase the production efficiency. As a part of the global Digital Plant team you will coordinate the creation of a digital, intelligently linked representation of a real asset – the so-called digital twin. The digital twin will therefore enable many future use cases in other products like augmented reality or artificial intelligence. Your activities will include the digitalization of a real plant, by following a standardized and proven procedure create validated 2D- and 3D-data. The implementation of the necessary technology, digital workflows and roles within the whole organization of a plant and the respective service providers will be part of your activities, too. You will guide users how to use digital methods and processes during the asset lifecycle, to raise the potentials of cost and lead time reduction combined with quality increase in engineering, construction, brownfield projects, plant modifications, maintenance, TARM and operations. On the way to the digital asset lifecycle, you will offer change management support, accompanied by training and coaching on easy-to-use tools and enhanced digital processes. QUALIFICATIONS Master Degree in Chemical/Process Engineering or Mechanical Engineering Minimum 3+ years’ experience in planning or construction of or in chemical plants Experience in usage of digital tools and methods in data driven engineering projects Very good project management and coordination skills Ideally working Experience in international projects and agil methods The will and ability to drive change and an entrepreneurial spirit Creative and motivated working style with a strong focus on innovation Ability to work in a multicultural and virtual team Excellent communication skills in English WHAT WE OFFER A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines.We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance.We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities
