jobs.name: Praktikum Sustainability Relations (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Praktikum Sustainability Relations (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Praktikum Sustainability Relations (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir gestalten die Zukunft der BASF, in dem wir den strategischen Rahmen definieren. Als Teil des Corporate Centers unterstützen wir unseren Vorstand in allen Portfolio- und Ressourcenallokationsentscheidungen. Wir entwickeln Strategien, identifizieren Chancen und Risiken und analysieren Trends in Nachhaltigkeit und Technologie. Gemeinsam mit den Unternehmensbereichen erarbeiten wir Strategien für die Geschäftsbereiche und begleiten deren Umsetzung. WAS SIE ERWARTET Für unsere Einheit Sustainability Relations & Trends suchen wir Sie für 3 bis 6 Monate ab September 2026 . Für uns als weltweit größtes Chemieunternehmen ist Nachhaltigkeit Kerngeschäft und danach handeln wir - mit weltweiten Standards für unsere Wertschöpfungsketten und mit Produkten, die Lösungen für nachhaltige Entwicklung ermöglichen. Sie unterstützen die Vorbereitung und Durchführung von Stakeholder-Dialogen sowie weiteren Austauschformaten durch die Erstellung von Analysen, Briefings und Hintergrundmaterialien und leisten damit einen Beitrag zum nachhaltigen Dialog mit unseren Anspruchsgruppen. Außerdem führen Sie Recherchen, Benchmarks und Trendanalysen zu relevanten ESG- und Nachhaltigkeitsthemen durch – auch unter Einsatz von KI – und unterstützen damit fundierte strategische Entscheidungen. Darüber hinaus tragen Sie zur Positionierung zentraler Nachhaltigkeitsthemen wie Biodiversität, verantwortungsvoller Unternehmensführung und gesellschaftlichem Engagement bei, indem Sie Präsentationen und Kommunikationsmaterialien erstellen und deren interne sowie externe Kommunikation begleiten. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN naturwissenschaftlicher, geistes-, wirtschafts- oder sozialwissenschaftlicher Studiengang mit dem Schwerpunkt Nachhaltigkeit großes Interesse an Nachhaltigkeitsthemen und idealerweise erste praktische Erfahrung auf diesem Gebiet sicherer Umgang mit MS Office-Anwendungen sehr gute Kommunikation in Deutsch und Englisch selbstständige Arbeitsweise sowie ausgeprägtes strategisches Denkvermögen Welche Bewerbungsunterlagen werden benötigt? Schauen Sie sich hierzu unser Video an! WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase - Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy - Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback Netzwerke - unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Praktikumsvergütung - eine Anerkennung des Einsatzes, weitere Infos finden Sie hier Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung - je nach Einsatzort und -gebiet SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Yasemin Koparan (Talent Acquisition), yasemin.koparan@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-58540 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF SE mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größtes arbeitgebendes Unternehmen der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studierende, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Mehr über die BASF SE erfahren Sie hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Chemical Plant Operator (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems, connecting seeds and traits, crop protection, digital tools and sustainability approaches. We strive to deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions vision & mission ! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT In our Thiuram/Sulfur Complex and Xemium production units, a wide range of intermediates, active ingredients, and formulations for crop protection products are manufactured. These products help farmers improve the yield of their crops. To support our operations, we are looking for several Chemical Plant Operators to work in a rotating shift system. As a committed member of the team, you will operate and monitor our production plants, both from the control room and directly on site. In addition, you will carry out maintenance work and minor repairs carefully and conscientiously. In your daily work, you will strictly adhere to all safety and environmental regulations. You will independently document your work and ensure accurate record-keeping. WHAT YOU OFFER completed vocational training as a Chemical Plant Operator or a comparable qualification previous experience in a manufacturing or production environment enjoy working as part of a team reliable and conscientious way of working excellent communication skills in German WHAT WE OFFER A dynamic working environment that encourages you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and actively help shape the future. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence. This enables us to work in a forward-looking, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks to support the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business). A wide range of learning opportunities for your professional and personal development. Career paths that support your development into different roles within the company. We actively support childcare (e.g. company daycare and holiday programs) and offer access to a fitness and health center as well as a medical center with a wide range of health services. We offer sustainable mobility benefits, including a discounted Germany Ticket for regional public transportation, a bike leasing program, and on-site charging stations for private electric vehicles. This makes your commute more affordable, healthier, and environmentally friendly. Actively drive our transformation as part of this unique opportunity within AP. Further information about Differentiated Steering at AP can be found here. HOW TO REACH US Valeria Illera (Talent Acquisition), valeria.illera@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-58110 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US Agricultural Solutions, a trusted partner to the agricultural industry, provides innovative and sustainable solutions for crop protection and plant health. With its Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, located near BASF’s headquarters in Ludwigshafen, the Rhine-Neckar metropolitan region offers an attractive environment for both your professional career and your leisure time. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Laboratory Assistant - Ecotoxicology (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems, connecting seeds and traits, crop protection, digital tools and sustainability approaches. We strive to deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions vision & mission ! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Support our Global Ecotoxicology unit at the earliest possible date in planning and organizing biological studies with terrestrial invertebrates, plants, or insects for a fixed-term period of two years. In your new role, you will be responsible for planning and organizing biological studies involving invertebrates or plants in close coordination with the laboratory lead. You will prepare test systems and active substances based on study plans using a variety of techniques and contribute your expertise in handling test organisms both in the laboratory and in field trials. Your responsibilities will also include documenting and evaluating study results in various IT systems, as well as presenting or communicating findings graphically when required. There is always room for improvement! You will optimize existing study methods and continuously develop them further in line with our research strategy. Safety comes first! You will carry out all activities in compliance with relevant safety regulations and the Ecotoxicology safety philosophy. Together with us, you will foster a corporate culture that embraces the responsible use of AI and integrates it firmly into everyday collaboration. WHAT YOU OFFER successfully completed scientific vocational training (e.g., in biology, environmental sciences, chemistry, or a related field) initial to several years of experience in conducting ecotoxicological studies in either the terrestrial or aquatic environment, ideally including experience working under GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) conditions sound knowledge of experimental design, including relevant analytical and testing methods, data evaluation, as well as occupational safety, health, and environmental protection requirements good German and English language skills, along with proficiency in common IT applications (MS Office and ideally laboratory or data management systems) enjoyment of conducting work in field environments in addition to laboratory-based activities openness to using AI in your daily work and a willingness to explore and effectively apply new digital tools and technologies within your area of responsibility WHAT WE OFFER A dynamic work environment that empowers you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and assume responsibility. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence, enabling us to work in a future‑oriented, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks for the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business). Learning opportunities for your professional and personal development. Career paths as professional development into other roles within the company. We actively support with childcare (e.g. company daycare, holiday programmes) and provide a fitness and health studio as well as a medical center with numerous offers for your health. We offer sustainable mobility benefits — a discounted Deutschlandticket for regional public transport, a job‑bike leasing program, and on‑site e‑charging stations for private electric vehicles to make your commute affordable, healthy, and climate‑friendly. HOW TO REACH US Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US As a reliable partner of the rural economy, Agricultural Solutions offers innovative, sustainable solutions for plant protection and health. With the agricultural center in Limburgerhof, close to the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you can expect an attractive place for both work and play in the Rhine-Neckar region. Learn more about BASF SE at: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LI Find out more about our team in Agricultural Solutions here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Sales Consultant for Plant Protection Products - Field Sales Saxony-Anhalt (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in Agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming, and the biggest job on Earth. We are an internationally successful company of the BASF Group. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. With teams in the lab, field, office and in production, we do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Here you can gain more insight into our Vision at BASF Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Within our unit, the Sales Team Central is responsible for sales for BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH in the Saxony-Anhalt sales region. As a sales consultant, you manage the customers assigned to you in your area of responsibility independently, including organising and running customer events, whilst making targeted use of defined sales tools (e.g. prepared specialist topics). Your responsibilities also include maintaining customer data and keeping further documentation (e.g. visit reports) on customers within the CRM system. In addition, you draw up and maintain target agreements with resellers and end users. You act as an additional regional point of contact for the wholesale sector, which is managed by the regional sales team leader. Last but not least, you analyse and evaluate the crop protection market within your own area of responsibility and across the team’s territory, and use this to develop well-founded sales strategies. You develop your expertise independently and continuously in sales-related areas and implement specified strategic approaches in regional market development. WHAT YOU OFFER completed commercial or agricultural training, or an equivalent qualification experience in sales within the agricultural or crop protection sector knowledge of complaints management, customer concepts and customer management proficiency in using CRM systems and digital sales tools an independent, structured and customer-focused approach to work a team player with strong communication skills, as well as negotiation and sales skills WHAT WE OFFER Inspiring networks to help you develop and put your own ideas into action (e.g. Women in Business) Mentoring programs and training opportunities to support your professional and personal development Career paths offering professional development into other roles within the company (including job rotation) Company car, the Deutschlandticket as a discounted travel pass, and the Jobrad scheme HOW TO REACH US Lea Sophie Guenther (Talent Acquisition), lea-sophie.guenther@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56150 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment . You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH is a reliable partner to the agricultural sector, offering innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to a wide range of crop systems. With the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, near the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you’ll find an attractive environment for both your professional life and your leisure time in the Rhine‑Neckar metropolitan region. You can learn more about the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof here . Find out more about our Agricultural Solutions team here Diversity is our greatest strength! Join us in shaping the future as part of a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunity – regardless of gender, age, background, sexual orientation, disability, or personal beliefs.

jobs.name: Global Strategic Marketing Manager
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Global Business Management Hydrogenation & Specialty Catalysts, we are promoting and selling catalysts based on a profound market research. We are developing and advertising new products to drive sustainable innovations together with our customers . How Will You Do It Strategic Market & Portfolio Management Define and drive the global market strategy for the assigned market segment, ensuring alignment with overall business objectives and long-term growth ambitions. Manage and continuously optimize the product portfolio, including prioritization of strategic focus areas and lifecycle decisions. Identify and evaluate new applications, adjacent markets, and growth opportunities to expand the business footprint. Market Intelligence, Data Management & Customer Insights Establish, maintain, and continuously enhance a structured market intelligence database for the assigned segments, ensuring availability of reliable and up-to-date market data. Develop a deep understanding of market dynamics by analyzing market size, growth trends, demand drivers, and value chains. Monitor competitor activities, including pricing strategies, capacity developments, and market positioning. Derive actionable insights from industry trends and customer needs to inform strategic decision-making. Value Proposition & Pricing Strategy Develop differentiated, customer-centric global value propositions that clearly position products in target markets. Define and implement pricing strategies, including value-based pricing concepts and regional adaptations, to maximize profitability and competitiveness. Ensure consistent positioning and messaging across regions and applications. Go-to-Market & Demand Generation Design and lead global go-to-market strategies, including the development of impactful marketing campaigns and coherent messaging frameworks. Coordinate and manage global product launches, ensuring cross-functional alignment and successful market introduction. Support demand generation by strengthening market visibility and customer engagement. Sales Enablement & Cross-Functional Collaboration Equip regional sales teams with effective tools and materials, such as pitch decks, use-case driven content, and application-focused messaging. Collaborate closely with sales, technology, and other relevant functions to ensure alignment between market needs, product capabilities, and commercial strategies. Act as a central interface between global and regional teams to ensure consistent execution of strategies. What We Look For Master's degree in economics or business administration or chemistry, chemical engineering. 5-8 year professional experience within business environment Good knowledge of commercial marketing or product marketing mandatory Strong entrepreneurial drive and willingness to take difficult decisions. Deep P&L and business expertise required. Innovative and solution-oriented mindset to drive and implement creative, out-of-the-box ideas. Excellent communication, negotiation and influencing skills (including English). Strong change management, interpersonal skills, and deep organizational awareness. Strong project steering and management Work Location Godrej One - Vikhorli

In unseren Häusern (Gesellschaftshaus, Feierabendhaus, Hotel René Bohn, Studienhaus St. Johann, Aktivhotel Haus Breitnau) sorgen wir für Vielfalt, Qualität und echte Genussmomente. Jetzt fehlen nur noch Sie! WAS SIE ERWARTET Desserts kreieren – Sie verwandeln hochwertige Zutaten in kunstvolle Desserts und sorgen für eine ansprechende Präsentation im Einklang mit unseren Qualitätsstandards Team führen & Portfolio verantworten – Sie leiten den gesamten Pâtisserie-Bereich über unser vielfältiges gastronomisches Portfolio hinweg – von à la carte über Bankett bis hin zu Events – und arbeiten eng mit der Küchenleitung zusammen Abläufe gestalten Abläufe gestalten & Bestellwesen steuern – Sie strukturieren Arbeitsprozesse, optimieren Abläufe und verantworten das Bestellwesen inklusive Wareneinsatz im Pâtisserie-Bereich Nachhaltigkeit leben – Sie achten auf ressourcenschonende Produktion, smarte Resteverwertung und pflanzenbasierte Alternativen – Hygiene und Umweltschutz sind für Sie selbstverständlich Mise en Place mit Handschrift – Ihre Vorbereitung ist durchdacht, effizient und Teil Ihrer kulinarischen Handschrift – Sie schaffen Ordnung und Klarheit, wo andere nur Zutaten sehen WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Background – Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Konditor:in oder Köchin/Koch Erfahrung – Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung als Pâtissier/Pâtissière in der gehobenen Gastronomie Flexibilität – Ausgeprägte Kundenorientierung sowie Anpassungsfähigkeit im Arbeitsalltag Know-how – Fundierte Kenntnisse der Infektionsbestimmungen und Hygienevorschriften Mindset – Kreativität, Innovationsfreude und echte Leidenschaft für Ihren Beruf WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktives Gehalt – 12 Monatsgehälter über Gastroniveau Zusätzliche Extras – 13. Monatsgehalt als Jahresprämie, Urlaubsgeld, Zuschuss zum Jobticket sowie Sonn-/Feiertags- und Nachtzuschläge Mehr Freizeit & Flexibilität – 30 Tage Urlaub, 5 zusätzliche Zukunftstage und ein Gleitzeitkonto Betriebliche Altersvorsorge – 100% Zuschuss: Wir verdoppeln Ihre Einzahlung Gesundheit & Wohlbefinden – Zugang zu Medical Center und Gesundheitsangeboten Top-Ausstattung – Arbeitskleidung wird gestellt und gereinigt Karriere & Entwicklung – Individuelle Förderung und Aufstiegschancen Starkes Miteinander & Diversität – Respekt, Toleranz und ein Team, das zusammenhält SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Melden Sie sich bei Susanne Reddmann (Talent Acquisition): susanne.reddmann@basf.com +49 30 2005-56097 Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier. Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier. ÜBER UNS Ob Businesshotel René Bohn, Gesellschaftshaus oder exklusive Events – wir stehen für Gastlichkeit auf höchstem Niveau. Unser Team sorgt dafür, dass jedes Detail stimmt und unsere Gäste besondere Momente erleben. Qualität, Service und Leidenschaft sind unsere DNA – vielleicht bald auch Ihre? Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Sie oder Du? – Wir richten uns nach Ihnen Unsere Bewerbungsplattform verwendet in der schriftlichen Kommunikation automatisch die „Sie“-Form. In den Bewerbungsgesprächen können Sie uns aber jederzeit sagen, wenn Sie lieber geduzt werden möchten – wir richten uns da gerne nach Ihnen. jobs.name: Technology Manager- Hydrogenation
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Global Business Management Hydrogenation & Specialty Catalysts, we are promoting and selling catalysts based on a profound market research. We are developing and advertising new products to drive sustainable innovations together with our customers . How Will You Do It Business Development and Technical Leadership Lead the technical development of customer proposals, including process and design concepts, performance calculations, and warranty considerations. Identify new industry trends, represent the technical value proposition in business development activities, customer discussions, and external presentations. Business Execution and Technical Service Ensure high levels of customer satisfaction by supporting start-up activities, monitoring operating data, and delivering training related to catalyst performance. Serve as the primary technical contact for customers on catalyst / performance questions, operational issues, and troubleshooting. Technical Expertise and Product Ownership Act as the technical owner for the assigned catalyst and technology portfolio. Maintain deep technical expertise in product performance, applications, and limitations to support internal and external stakeholders. Lead market/Customer driven continuous innovation, optimization, and product differentiation in collaboration with research and development teams. Cross-Functional Interface Management Collaborate closely with Research and Development, Marketing, Regional Sales, and Production/Operations to align technical and business priorities. Translate market and customer needs into technical requirements and actionable development priorities. What We  Look For Education: preferred PhD in Chemistry / Chemical Engineering / Master in Chemistry / Chemical Engineering Working Experience: Min. 5 years of technical experience in the field of chemical catalysts and processes, sound understanding of chemical reactions and processes Technical & Professional Knowledge: Experience with customer interaction; Good communication skills, team player able to work across organizational boundaries Innovative and solution-oriented mindset to drive and implement creative, out-of-the-box ideas. Excellent communication, negotiation and influencing skills (including English). Strong change management, interpersonal skills, and deep organizational awareness. Strong project steering and management. Work Loction Godrej One - Vikhroli (Mumbai)

jobs.name: Production Engineer
jobs.description: Objective of the Position Responsible for ensuring day-to-day plant operations run smoothly, with a strong focus on daily production safety, process reliability, quality, and regulatory compliance, while optimizing asset utilization and production efficiency. Assist the Production Manager in ensuring the operation of the production section is carried out on time according to EHS, quality and other agreed requirements. Key in driving continuous improvement, cross-shift standardization, and effective problem-solving to sustain high-performance operations. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activies EHS Strictly comply with and implement the company's EHS policies and ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certification requirements. Guide and ensure shift operations to strictly follow SOPs regarding safety, environment, and occupational health in their operations. Take appropriate measures for any safety, environmental, or occupational health incidents and report to the Production Manager as per procedures. Using personal protective equipment (PPE) according to the Safe Work Guidelines or PPE Matrix Carry out emergency actions as required by procedures during emergency response events. Operations Ensure all environmental, health and safety rules and guidelines are being adhered to and maintained by the production personnel. Providing technical and operational leadership to support day-to-day plant operations, ensuring safe execution of production plans in full compliance with BASF Process Safety standards, and applicable environmental regulation. Acting as a technical authority and trusted advisor to Operations, leading complex troubleshooting efforts and guiding Daily Operations teams toward effective, sustainable solutions for process, production, quality, and EHS challenges. Driving continuous improvement initiatives using Lean Six Sigma methodologies to improve asset efficiency, optimize equipment cycle times, reduce waste, and sustain nameplate production capacity. Influencing cross shift standardization, cost optimization, and asset performance by working collaboratively with Operations, Maintenance, and Asset Effectiveness teams to align priorities and maximize plant utilization. Leading cross functional coordination and technical input for inspections, outages, capital projects, and investigations (RCA, NCM), partnering with Engineering, Maintenance, Quality, and Regulatory teams to prevent repeat incidents and improve first pass quality. Lead production team in trial production of new products and internal test products (PTO). To check and revise if necessary; HIRA, Procedures, Working Instructions, and Work Checklists. Manage the inventory of materials and tools, including staging materials for production and packaging by conducting periodic stock counts. Support on evaluating training needs for the operation, to ensure they are provided the adequate training for operation and continuous improvement. Responsible as 5S leader coordinator for Production Department. Review process condition and downtime report, GAP reporting and propose for improvement. Represent the Production Manager on production matter during the Production Manager absence. Ensure effectiveness of the implementation audit for Integrated Management System (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001) and IATF 16949 Quality Management System for the automotive industry. Ensure good housekeeping and proper handovers among shift team members. Other production-related tasks assigned by superiors. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry, Polymers Science or Chemical Engineering. At least 5 - 8 years’ of experience in delivering technical expertise in a plastics compounding manufacturing plant. Posses demonstrated track record of leading through influence, providing expert technical guidance, and partnering with Operations, Maintenance, Engineering, and EHS teams to achieve safe and reliable plant performance. Proficient in production management software (e.g., ERP systems, MES). Hold strong production and operations background with the ability to independently support troubleshooting, root cause failure analysis, and investigations across Production, EHS and Quality. Have proven continuous improvement capability, including practical application of Lean Six Sigma methodologies to drive efficiency, waste reduction, asset utilization, and sustained nameplate performance. Highly effective collaborator with strong attention to detail and communication skills, able to coordinate complex activities and influence priorities across cross functional teams without formal supervisory authority. Knowledgeable on ISO 9001, ISO 14001, TS 16949 and ISO 45001 requirements. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Account Manager Personal Care (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF As a European distribution organization of the BASF Group, BTC Europe sells highly-refined specialty and fine chemicals to international customers in numerous industries. We capitalize on our proximity to our customers, agility, many years of expertise and the accumulated know-how of the BASF Verbund. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Account Manager establishes and develops profitable customer relationships, achieves revenue as well as margin targets, and identifies growth opportunities. As primary customer contact, he/she coordinates internal resources, negotiates contractual terms, and ensures target achievement through regular reporting. The Account Manager lives the BTC PACE values and works in line with local law, BASF compliance guidelines, and business-critical processes (e.g. PULs). You develop and execute account plans in line with the overall strategy, actively drive new business, and leverage cross-selling opportunities. Analyzing market and competitor trends, translating insights into growth opportunities, and supporting the launch of new products and applications is part of your responsibilities. You manage your customer portfolio with a focus on growth and financial targets, build strong relationships with key stakeholders, and ensure transparent documentation in Salesforce. In addition, you take ownership of price management, promote the full portfolio, and lead customer negotiations. As a central interface, you coordinate internal and external stakeholders, ensure smooth customer-facing processes, and collaborate closely with local and European teams while contributing to digitalization and continuously developing your expertise. WHAT YOU OFFER master’s degree in business or life science professional experience in relevant application technologies and industry know-how several years of professional experience in application technology or in sales of products for the cosmetics industry strong knowledge in contract negotiations, account planning, and customer relationship management, ideally with Salesforce experience fluent German and advanced English skills, plus confident use of IT tools and MS Office applications proactive team player with excellent communication skills, intercultural competence, and a growth-driven mindset willingness to travel, mainly in Germany WHAT WE OFFER 100% home office company car inspiring networks for developing and implementing your own ideas (e.g., Women in Business) mentoring programs and learning opportunities for your professional and personal development career paths for professional growth, including opportunities to move into other roles within the company 30 vacation days, plus options for special leave and sabbatical models flexible working time arrangements (e.g., part-time employment) an attractive salary, based on business success and your individual performance bicycle leasing options HOW TO REACH US Svenja Sollmann (Talent Acquisition), svenja.sollmann@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59355 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online (technical questions cannot be answered in this appointment) You can also reach our recruiting team here ABOUT US BTC Europe GmbH is a wholly owned subsidiary of BASF. Learn more about BTC Europe GmbH at on.basf.com/BTCEurope/EN Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. And by the way, we leave it up to our employees to decide if and when they address each other informally.

jobs.job_field: BASF Battery Materials and Recycling Schwarzheide GmbH
jobs.description: Praktikum & Abschlussarbeit Verfahrens- und Umwelttechnik/Chemieingenieurwesen (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Praktikum & Abschlussarbeit Verfahrens- und Umwelttechnik/Chemieingenieurwesen (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Der Lausitzer Produktionsstandort ist auf Wachstum ausgerichtet. Mit dem Bau der Anlage für Batteriematerialien legt die BASF in Schwarzheide den Grundstein für eine europäische Wertschöpfungskette der Elektromobilität. Um unseren CO2-Fußabdruck zu verbessern, setzen wir auf effiziente Produktionsverfahren, innovatives Kreislaufmanagement und eine nachhaltige Energiestrategie. Dazu zählt auch die Rückgewinnung der Rohstoffe durch Recycling um damit den Kreislauf zu schließen. Werden Sie Teil dieser Reise und entdecken Sie das vielseitige Produktportfolio von BASF. WAS SIE ERWARTET Ab Oktober 2026 suchen wir für einen Zeitraum von 6 bis 9 Monaten Unterstützung für unser Team. Lernen Sie im Rahmen eines Praktikums den Aufgabenbereich Batterierecycling kennen und schreiben Sie anschließend bei uns Ihre Abschlussarbeit. Unser Team Schwarzmasseproduktion bündelt Fachwissen in den Bereichen Verfahrenstechnik, Maschinenbau, nachhaltige Produktion und Kreislaufwirtschaft, um flexibel und ressourceneffizient Stoffkreisläufe zu schließen. Sie arbeiten sich in das Recycling von Lithium-Ionen-Batterien ein und lernen unsere Prozessanlagen kennen. Gemeinsam mit dem Team bereiten Sie Daten der Anlage auf. Dabei leiten Sie zusammen mit uns Strategien zur Senkung des Hilfsstoffverbrauchs, des Instandhaltungsaufwands, zur Durchsatzoptimierung sowie zur Erhöhung der Produktqualität ab. Zudem unterstützen Sie bei der Umsetzung dieser Strategien und bei der Bewertung der Wirksamkeit. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN aktuelles Studium in Verfahrenstechnik, Umwelttechnik, Chemieingenieurwesen, Maschinenbau oder vergleichbar erste Erfahrung in der Versuchsdurchführung und -auswertung sowie Dokumentation Kenntnisse in verfahrenstechnischen Grundlagen wünschenswert sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie ein versierter Umgang mit den gängigen MS Office-Programmen Freude daran, gemeinsam im Team Erfolge zu erzielen und sich weiterzuentwickeln WAS WIR BIETEN Sie sind vom ersten Tag an Teil des BASF-Teams. Bei uns steigen Sie in anspruchsvolle Arbeitsgebiete ein und übernehmen spannende Aufgaben und nach Möglichkeit Teilprojekte in einem interdisziplinären Arbeitsumfeld. Ergänzend bieten die BASF-Standorte unterschiedliche Möglichkeiten wie Führungen oder Workshops an, um BASF näher kennen zu lernen. So gewinnen Sie vielfältige Einblicke und Erfahrungen bei dem größten Chemieunternehmen weltweit. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Nina Schülzke (Talent Acquisition), nina.schuelzke@basf.com , Tel. +49 30 2005-58790 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF in Schwarzheide, zwischen der Hauptstadt Berlin und der Metropole Dresden gelegen, produziert funktionale Materialien und entwickelt intelligente Systemlösungen für globale Kunden. Wir stehen für Innovation, digitale Transformation und Nachhaltigkeit. Mit unseren neuen Technologien und Arbeitsweisen übernehmen wir eine Vorreiterrolle innerhalb der BASF-Gruppe. Nutzen Sie die Chance und gestalten Sie mit uns zukunftsorientierte Lösungen. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Assistant Administration des Ventes (h/f) Détails du poste
jobs.description:
Assistant Administration des Ventes (h/f) Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion au profil Connexion employé Description de poste Assistant Administration des Ventes (h/f) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Postuler BIENVENUE Le monde a besoin d’innovation et nous avons besoin de vous ! Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Rejoignez Coatings Services France, où la réputation de nos marques et notre force d'innovation en matière de produits et services nous positionne en leader de la distribution peinture automobile en France. Etes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES En tant qu'Assistant ADV, vous gérez le traitement des commandes dans le souci permanent du service client et en respectant les standards définis par la société avec les autres membres de l'équipe ADV. A ce titre, vos responsabilités incluront notamment: La veille au bon enregistrement et au traitement des commandes, dans le respect des modes opératoires en vigueur (dont le circuit constructeur) ; La garantie du service client par une information adaptée et le traitement des demandes des clients (état d'avancement des commandes, facturation, livraison, réclamation, paiement...) ; La garantie de la bonne gestion des éléments de facturation afférents aux grands comptes, notamment au travers la gestion des bases de données clients et des tarifs ; La participation à la mise en place de projets informatiques ou à l’élaboration de nouveaux processus ; La contribution à la formation des utilisateurs et à la maintenance des systèmes d'information internes. VOTRE PROFIL Vous possédez un diplôme Bac+2 (ou équivalent) en gestion ou administration ; Vous possédez une expérience significative (au moins 3 ans) en Administration des ventes ; Vous avez une très bonne maîtrise des outils informatiques et de SAP ; CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Une opportunité stimulante et passionnante dans une équipe engagée et compétente ; Un environnement propice à l'innovation, au partage d'idée et au bien-être ; Un univers qui vous permet de développer votre potentiel dans un groupe international. CE POSTE VOUS INTERESSE ? Pour candidater, il suffit de cliquer sur le bouton « postuler » sur cette fiche de poste. Vous devrez alors créer votre profil et ajouter vos documents de candidatures. Cela ne vous prendra que quelques minutes. Emilie, en charge de ce recrutement, reçoit et analyse toutes les candidatures et enverra un courriel aux candidats présélectionnés pour les inviter à un entretien vidéo MS Teams de 30 minutes. Nous vous souhaitons bonne chance et sommes impatients de recevoir votre candidature ! Seules les candidatures soumises via l’offre publiée sur notre site carrière seront étudiées. Toute candidature reçue en dehors du processus prévu (par e-mail, par exemple) ne sera pas considérée. Pour bien comprendre le processus, vous pouvez vous rendre sur cette page dédiée. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Coatings Services est spécialisée dans la commercialisation et la distribution de systèmes de peintures éco-efficaces destinées aux professionnels de la carrosserie. Grace à la réputation des marques centenaires et premium R-M et Glasurit, homologuées par les plus grands acteurs du secteur automobile, et à notre force d'innovation en matière de produits et services, nous sommes le leader français de la distribution peinture automobile en France. L'entreprise est présente sur tout le territoire national à travers plusieurs entrepôts et son siège social basé à Compans (77). La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Quality Management Lead
jobs.description: Now hiring! Quality Management Lead Hillsboro, OR (Onsite) Come create chemistry with us! The purpose of this role is to be the first contact for EDM North America (NA) customers to handle quality relevant topics with including NC handling, quality inquiry, customer audit coordination, CoA upload, and white paper/PCN communication. Providing local QM support in NA for business growth and customer satisfaction enhancement. Being local quality contact in NA for 3rd party QC lab evaluation and qualification and providing support for supplier management. As a Quality Management Lead, you create chemistry by... Maintain internal/external contact window for EDM NA customers on quality related inquiries/survey/quality audit/complaints / troubleshooting, etc… Customer relationship management with EDM US customers and take the responsibility to provide initial response to EDM US customers Collaborate with NA business team to meet quality requirements from EDM NA customers and maintain customer satisfaction Collect correct and necessary information to coordinate customer quality request internal/external communication, and implementation follow-up Coordinate customer inquiry/complaint investigation with internal cross function team and provide on-time response to customer including Kuanyin site in Taiwan and Ludwigshafen in Germany Process Change Notice (PCN) report and white paper preparation, communication and follow-up for on-time implementation Collaborate with production site CQM team to finish OCAP, worse chart, BKM, related quality requirements investigation, etc… Collaborate with production site CQM team in preparing regular PCS review, customer audit, and quality inquiring. Take the ownership for CoA and production resume upload for customer system and collaborate with Taiwan/LU team for COA/Production resume upload working model optimization for all batches shipped Being local quality staff in NA to evaluate/qualify 3rd party QC lab by closely working with the production site QC lab. Provide local support in NA for supplier management like supplier audit, visit.. If you have... 5 + years of experience in semiconductor or quality field (customer quality experience or supplier quality experience is preferred) Strong interpersonal/customer dealing skill/coordination, open-minded and positive thinking, value selling, and hands-on skill Master degree in Engineering, Industry Engineering, Chemical engineering, Chemical, Statistic, MBA..etc. Strong English speaking/writing/listening/reading skills are essential and Mandarin is nice to have Proficient at Microsoft Office, Statistic Software (JMP, statistic..), Digitalization, Database Programing is plus Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Unser Schwerpunkt liegt auf strategischen Projekten, die die effektive Transformation unserer Geschäftsbereiche vorantreiben. Unsere Teams befassen sich mit einem breiten Spektrum an Themen wie Nachhaltigkeit, Organisationsentwicklung, Effizienzprojekte, M&A sowie profitables Wachstum durch Innovationen aller Art.  Bei uns stehen die Menschen im Mittelpunkt: Wir sind stolz auf unseren starken Teamgeist und schätzen Vielfalt. Werden Sie Teil eines großartigen internationalen Teams, in dem Ihr praxisorientierter Ansatz und Ihr Wunsch, etwas zu bewirken, wirklich geschätzt werden. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam neue Ideen entwickeln und unseren Kunden die besten Lösungen anbieten. WAS SIE ERWARTET Als Project Lead (m/w/d) arbeiten Sie gemeinsam mit Kollegen an komplexen Strategieberatungsprojekten und entwickeln wirkungsvolle Lösungen für Unternehmen und Funktionsbereiche innerhalb der BASF. Sie identifizieren in jedem Projekt Verbesserungspotenziale und beraten das Managementteam kompetent und einfühlsam. Vom ersten Tag an setzen Sie Ihre Fähigkeiten ein und bringen Ihre Ideen in laufende Projekte ein. Sie kommunizieren während aller Projektphasen klar und offen mit allen Beteiligten. Unsere ganzheitlichen Beratungsleistungen ermöglichen es Ihnen, ein Projekt von Anfang bis Ende zu begleiten. Sie genießen hohe Sichtbarkeit im Top-Management, indem Sie weltweit mit Führungskräften der BASF an einer Vielzahl strategischer Projekte mit hoher Priorität und nachhaltiger Wirkung zusammenarbeiten. Unsere Projekte decken die Bereiche Innovation & Growth, Marketing & Sales, Organizational Excellence, and Post-Merger Integration/Carve Out ab. Einen Überblick über unsere Fachgruppen finden Sie hier . WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Berufserfahrung in der strategischen Beratung und Projektarbeit in einer (globalen) Strategieberatung je nach Qualifikation und Berufserfahrung steigen Sie als Projektleiter (3–5 Jahre) oder Senior-Projektleiter (5–7 Jahre) ein nachgewiesene Fachkenntnisse in Projektmanagementmethoden sowie in Beratungsmethodiken und -ansätzen hervorragende Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Englisch; Deutsch ist von Vorteil überzeugendes und kompetentes Auftreten im Umgang mit Kunden aufgeschlossene und integrative Einstellung, zuverlässiger Teamplayer internationale Berufserfahrung ist von Vorteil WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktiver Standort: Ihr Arbeitsort ist Monheim (in der Nähe von Düsseldorf), der Verwaltungssitz der BASF Personal Care & Nutrition GmbH. Die Niederlassung in Monheim verfügt über eine Kantine sowie moderne, flexible Arbeitsbereiche, die für verschiedene Teams und Funktionen ausgelegt sind. Hybrides Arbeiten: Sie können Homeoffice mit bis zu drei Bürotagen kombinieren. Regelmäßige Besuche in unserer Zentrale in Ludwigshafen sind erforderlich, z. B. für monatliche Teamtage oder Veranstaltungen der Einheit. Je nach Projekt/Kunde können auch Reisen zu anderen BASF-Standorten erforderlich sein. Work-Life-Balance: Wir bieten Zugang zu Kinderbetreuungsangeboten, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle und Kooperationen mit Fitness- und Gesundheitsstudios sowie zahlreiche weitere (präventive) Angebote für Ihre geistige und körperliche Gesundheit. Persönliche Weiterentwicklung: Wir bieten ein umfassendes, auf Ihre Bedürfnisse zugeschnittenes Schulungsprogramm. Unser Bereich kann auf eine erfolgreiche Bilanz bei der Karriereförderung unserer Mitarbeiter zurückblicken: Ehemalige Teammitglieder sind weltweit auf allen Hierarchieebenen bei BASF zu finden. Die individuelle Unterstützung Ihrer beruflichen Entwicklung ist Teil unserer Mission. Vorteile: Ein attraktives Gehalt und ein variabler Bonus, Mitarbeiteraktienoptionen, die Möglichkeit von Sonderurlaub, 30 Tage bezahlter Urlaub, Kantinen mit frisch zubereiteten Speisen, ein Dienstfahrrad, Sozialberatung und vieles mehr. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Jennifer Weiz (Talent Acquisition), jennifer.weiz@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56454 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen für diese Position. Gerne können Sie auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier ÜBER UNS Pflegende Cremes, milde Shampoos, starke Stylinggele, glänzende Lippenstifte und schützende Sonnenlotionen - all diese Produkte enthalten Inhaltsstoffe der BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH, einer Tochtergesellschaft der BASF. Auf Basis natürlicher, nachwachsender Rohstoffe entwickeln und produzieren wir ein großes Sortiment an Inhaltsstoffen für die Körperpflegeindustrie. Mehr über die BASF Personal Care and Nutrition GmbH erfahren Sie hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. jobs.name: Customer Site Service Rep II (CSSR II)
jobs.description:
Job Title: Customer Site Service Rep II ( Magna, Decostar Industries) Customer Site Service Representative II Location: Carrollton, GA Surventis Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinish coatings, decorative paints as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio is supplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enables new applications with innovative surfaces. We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! As a Customer Site Service Representative, you will support the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The role responds to customer requirements, enforces product quality/performance specification, and supports new product/application development. You will provide on-site expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. S/he will develop and support customer relationships. This role supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis and resolution. The position assures product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. The incumbent will demonstrate technical and professional expertise to enhance Surventis' reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. As a Customer Site Service Rep II, you will be responsible for... Supporting the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. Acting as a capable leader and mentor to coach and develop team members as well as providing ready support for other facilities and customer locations. Developing monthly scorecards and identifying opportunities for continuous improvement for presentation to management. Being a subject matter expert in at least one technology of the coating process i.e.. Ecoat, prime, basecoat or clearcoat. Maintaining the database for smart chart and color care, inventory, and forecasting. Available 24/7 as you will be the sole representative for this account If you have... High school diploma required, advanced degree preferred. 2+ years of automotive coatings or equivalent industry expertise; ability to utilize your advanced knowledge of the painting process and paint applications. Ability to develop and grow customer relationships, focusing on building a positive rapport with customers. Utilizing your inventory control/ERP knowledge you will collect and analyze data and apply results to make material and/or process recommendations. Your strong attention to detail will allow you to continuously inspect and evaluate topcoat defects. Your excellent interpersonal skills will help you with interactions with the team and management at our customer facility. Your strong leadership ability will allow you to run a shift on your own with minimal supervision. Prior experience in a manufacturing environment will help you complete instrument updates and calibrations as well as ancillary paint processes such as spot repair and workability trial processes. Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Pay transparency Surventis is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $78,000-$82,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . BASF Group operates this platform solely as a technical service provider under a 
 Transitional Service Agreement following the divestment of the Coatings division, 
 effective July 1st 2026, now Surventis. The employer and contracting party for the advertised positions is exclusively . alone is responsible for the content of the job adverts and the 
 decision regarding applications. Data Protection The processing of your personal data is carried out by as the controller 
 within the meaning of any applicable data privacy laws. BASF Group acts as a technical service provider in this respect and processes 
 data exclusively on behalf of . Further information can be found in the privacy policy coatings privacy policy incl. Contact details .

jobs.name: Global Customs Operations (Executive)
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Global Customs Operations (Executive) is responsible for customs related activities served out via Kuala Lumpur Asia Pacific Service Centre (Hub). The role’s objectives: To enable business focus and delivery of service excellence from the hub to support business needs. To support hub customs operations related service requests. To support compliance within BASF supply chain operational processes regarding Foreign Trade (Customs and Trade Control). To support any improvement and process implementation to ensure an agreed service level and in compliance with governmental regulations and BASF company policies. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities . Delivery of Customs service to business Collaborate cross-functionally with internal working partners in support of trade compliance, supply chain, and general company objectives and projects Support business unit queries related to customs relevant activities. Respond promptly to requests and urgent issues. Hub Customs operational responsibilities: Support hub operational service activities including customs import/ export related documentation, issuance and verification, and duty rate maintenance in SAP or customized database Perform Customs Documentation review and validate all import and export documentations to ensure data accuracy and comply with customs laws and regulations regardless of whether from global or local country perspective Support process improvement projects Monitor regulatory changes and take prompt action to comply with the latest requirements. Support the data preparation required for dashboards Support the standardization of reporting of departmental and organizational measurements Support the automation of routine data collection Support the process documentations (Standard Operating Procedure, Work Guide, Work Instruction) for customs hub operations Proactively address and resolve any discrepancies or customs-related issues (e.g., delayed shipments, missing documentation, tariff classification mismatch, etc.) by troubleshooting the root cause and communicating clearly with relevant parties Customs Compliance responsibilities: Support Customs Compliance related assessment, health checks, audit and its documentation Support operational and service documentation including Standard Operating Procedure, Work Instructions, Work Guide Ensure all activities comply with relevant customs regulations, trade laws and tariff schedules Process and system improvement responsibilities Support any digitalization initiatives or automation for customs process and operations Support any system upgrade or enhancement related review and testing Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in any technical discipline, preferably Supply Chain Management Minimum 1-2 years relevant experience in Customs and Foreign Trade; familiar with Supply Chain operations Experience in customs, trade law and practices of chemical and non-chemical products is desirable Must be able to be flexible with EMEA working hours

jobs.name: Global Customs Performance Management (Senior Executive)
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position The Global Customs Performance Management (Senior Executive) monitors operational performance of global Customs Operations via Kuala Lumpur Asia Pacific Service Centre (Hub). The role’s objectives include: Measuring service execution, quality of service as well as adherence to standard processes as per global Guidance documentation to ensure adherence to local, national or global regulations. Support Export & Import customs declarations, Inward processing or similar saving programs, handbook procedures, Preferential Origin declaration and all other customs processes. Closely collaborate with the global Steering unit to initiate process-oriented risk assessments to check compliance with the agreed strand procedures and initiates mitigation measures if applicable. Incorporates the ability for deep diving process analysis and deep understanding about Customs & Foreign Trade regulations. To support any improvement to ensure an agreed service level with cost control and in compliance with governmental regulations and BASF company policies. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Initiates and defines global, regional and local monitoring processes on operational Customs Performance as defined Support organizing meeting with countries and any business review as necessary Deep dive into and analyses of BASF internal & external customs data and presentation in a visual standardized format Prepare monthly statistics and KPI report for service recipient Support the data preparation required for dashboards, including alerts and analysis Support the standardization of reporting of departmental and organizational measurements Support the automation of routine data collection and develop reporting processes that aid operational performance management Job Requirements Must have degree/diploma in supply chain or related field Must have experience in customs, foreign trade and performance management in customs related topics Strong communication and argumentation skills SAP is preferred Must be flexible to work in EMEA timezone Excellent analytical thinking

jobs.name: Global Customs Operations & Performance Management (Manager)
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position Lead and oversee the team in delivering customs services, including customs verification and documentation review, ensuring alignment with service expectations. Foster strong relationships with internal business partners by understanding their requirements and delivering high-quality, collaborative, and responsive support. Coordinate effectively with external service providers to ensure seamless operations and alignment with service standards through proactive engagement and collaboration. Manage quality assurance related to overseas delivery services, ensuring timely resolution and continuous improvement. Oversee risk management activities for the team, including access control, data protection, business continuity planning, and regular risk assessments. Drive knowledge management initiatives by maintaining up-to-date documentation, facilitating structured reviews, and assigning clear process ownership to ensure knowledge retention. Monitor team performance, provide regular coaching and guidance, and support professional development to achieve operational excellence. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities 1.  Customs Services Define goals and target for the team to support business as usual Stay updated on changes to country-specific regulations and requirements to ensure compliance with all services. Collaborate with Functional Expert team to promote automation and process enhancement Ensure all import/export activities comply with local customs laws and international trade agreements. Maintain up-to-date knowledge of regional customs regulations and free trade agreements (FTAs). 2. Project Management Lead/ Support and coordinate customs-related projects such as system implementations, process improvements, and regulatory change initiatives. Develop project plans, timelines, and deliverables in collaboration with cross-functional teams. Monitor project progress, manage risks, and ensure timely completion of milestones. Report on project outcomes and recommend continuous improvement strategies. 3. Risk Management & Enforcement Identify and mitigate risks related to miscalculations, undervaluation, or non-compliance. Support internal audits and investigations related to customs activities. Implement corrective, preventive actions and process improvements as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement Act as the point of contact to support customs authorities’ inquiries. Provide training and guidance to internal teams on customs procedures and regulatory updates. Collaborate with cross functional team, business unit, supply chain, legal entity, and order management teams to ensure end-to-end compliance. 5. Reporting & Analysis Prepare regular reports on customs performance (quantitative & qualitative) and compliance metrics Analyze data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for optimization. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in international Trade, Customs Administration, Supply Chain, or related field. Minimum 5 years of experience in customs operations or trade compliance and customs performance management Proven experience in managing cross-functional projects or process improvement initiatives. Good understanding of Preferential Rules of Origin, COO processes, and FTA frameworks. Familiarity with HS classification, customs valuation, and import/export documentation. Excellent project management, analytical, and communication skills. Must be able to work in EMEA timezone

jobs.name: Technische:r Anlagenfahrer:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Technische:r Anlagenfahrer:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Technische:r Anlagenfahrer:in (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Ob Einbauteile für Smartphones, Hochleistungsanwendungen für E-Mobilität oder Hightech Sportequipment in unterschiedlichen Farben: Technische Kunststoffe sind aus unserem Leben heute nicht mehr wegzudenken und die Nachfrage steigt rasant. In zentraler Lage Deutschlands, in Rudolstadt (Thüringen), entwickeln und produzieren wir als BASF-Tochtergesellschaft nachhaltige hochleistungs-Polyamid-Compounds, die sich in vielen Bereichen des täglichen Lebens wiederfinden. WAS SIE ERWARTET Mit einem Team von etwa 120 Mitarbeitenden produzieren wir an unserem Standort in Rudolstadt ein breit gefächertes Sortiment hochwertiger Kunststoffe. Unsere Einheit Fertigung stellt technische Kunststoffe in Form von Kunststoffgranulaten in vielfältigen Rezepturen her. Um den reibungslosen Ablauf der Produktion zu gewährleisten, bedienen und überwachen Sie unsere Produktionsanlagen. Dabei erkennen Sie Störungen, führen Umrüstungen durch und melden Prozessabweichungen. Darüber hinaus dokumentieren Sie die notwendigen Betriebsparameter und betreiben die Anlagen ordnungsgemäß in Ihrem Bereich. Mit Ihnen werden wir besser! Zur Optimierung unserer Produktionsabläufe arbeiten Sie aktiv am Lean-Prozess mit und bringen Verbesserungsvorschläge ein. Sie berücksichtigen stets relevante Qualitäts-, Sicherheits-, Gesundheits- sowie Umweltvorschriften. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung als Maschinen- undAnlagenführer:in oder eine vergleichbare technische Qualifikation idealerweise erste Berufserfahrung als Anlagenfahrer:in Kenntnisse in Produktionsverfahren und -prozessen ausgeprägtes Qualitätsbewusstsein und Teamfähigkeit Bereitschaft, im vollkontinuierlichen Schichtsystem zu arbeiten WAS WIR BIETEN Profitieren Sie vom starken Tarifvertrag der Chemischen Industrie (attraktives Gehalt, Urlaubsgeld i.H.v. 1.200 €, 13. Monatsgehalt) und unseren zusätzlichen betrieblichen Leistungen, wie eine jährliche Erfolgsbeteiligung, eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge, eine Pflegezusatzversicherung, eine 24 h - Unfallversicherung sowie ein Langzeitkonto für einen flexiblen Renteneinstieg inkl. Zuschuss von 550 € pro Jahr. Nutzen Sie zudem den Zukunftsbetrag in Form von zusätzlichen freien Tagen, einem Jobrad oder Einzahlung in das Langzeitkonto. Standortspezifische Vorsorge- und Fitnessangebote (z.B. Medical Check oder Grippeschutzimpfung), um Ihre Gesundheit zu fördern und einen Ausgleich zur Arbeit zu bieten. Sie erhalten 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, und die Beschäftigung erfolgt auf Basis einer 38,5-Stunden-Woche. Darüber hinaus gibt es die Möglichkeit zu tariflichem Sonderurlaub, Schichturlaub und die Nutzung von 12 Familientagen pro Jahr zur Unterstützung der Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf (bis Ihr Kind das 6. Lebensjahr vollendet hat). Wir bieten Ihnen einen breiten Gestaltungsspielraum und das passende Netzwerk für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen. Lern- und Entwicklungsplattformen, sowie regelmäßige Entwicklungsgespräche mit Ihrer Führungskraft gestalten Ihre individuelle Entwicklung. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Sophie Meyer (Talent Acquisition), Sophie-Maria.Meyer@basf.com , Tel.:+49 30 2005-58071 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF Performance Polymers GmbH, mit Sitz in Rudolstadt, liegt im grünen Herzen von Thüringen. Als Produktionsstandort der BASF und Teil des Unternehmensbereiches Performance Materials stellen wir hochwertige Technische Kunststoffe für den europäischen Markt her. Anwendung finden unsere Produkte unter anderem in der Automobil-, der Elektro-, Elektronik- sowie in der Möbelindustrie. Mehr über die BASF Performance Polymers GmbH erfahren Sie unter: on.basf.com/PerformancePolymers Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen.
