jobs.name: Officer Treasury Back Office - Focus Area Reporting (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Our Regional Treasury EMEA department is responsible for the Treasury and Cash Management activity of BASF entities in the EMEA region. Within our Back Office team, we offer you a chance to learn about corporate finance in the framework of reporting and support other Back Office activities in a diverse, international team. You are responsible for the preparation of the periodic reports to the central banks in European Union countries (AWV for Deutsche Bundesbank, Banque de France and others). You are the direct contact for the respective central banks, responsible for the formal registration of the group companies in the regional reporting platforms as well as processing the technical set-up in our systems. In course of this, you attend various enquiries, e.g., quality control and support the group companies in the preparation, implementation and corresponding follow-up of regular audits. In addition, you contribute to the continuous development and automation of our reporting processes and document the knowledge accordingly. Solution-oriented cooperation, e.g., with interfaces for purchasing and sales as well as accounting, round off your task profile. WHAT YOU OFFER commercial business training or a degree in Business Administration, first knowledge of Cash Management, Treasury and/or Corporate Finance profound user knowledge in MS365, knowledge in AWV reporting and/or SAP FI is advantageous technical affinity as well as enthusiasm for digitalization topics and the implementation of new digital tools good communication skills in English, German language skills are advantageous organized and self-reliant way of working in order to create innovative driving solutions WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 - 39.000€, depending on qualifications and work experience. In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success as well as individual performance. 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - here a first impression Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks HOW TO REACH US Katarina Borisov (Talent Acquisition), Tel.:+49 30 2005-59269 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main Tasks To assist in Lean Bootcamp task and documentations. Support Electrical & Instrumentation (E&I) on developing Know How Management Sharepoint. To assist updating & maintaining Integrated Management System (IMS) documentation for E&I. To assist updating & maintaining E&I document such as schematic diagram & single line diagram. To assist updating & maintaining E&I Piping and Instrumentation. Risk Control preparation – documentation & task list. Requirements Diploma/Degree in Engineering

jobs.name: Head - Legal & Company Secretary
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Role Overview: BASF Agricultural Solutions India Limited is seeking an experienced Legal and Company Secretarial professional to lead corporate governance, regulatory compliance, legal advisory, litigation, contract management and stakeholder engagement, with a strong focus on listing readiness in India. Key Responsibilities: Regulatory Compliance & Corporate Governance Lead compliance with Companies Act, SEBI regulations, Foreign Exchange laws, Secretarial Standards and other applicable corporate, data privacy, ESG and sector-specific regulations. Act as Key Managerial Personnel and provide governance support to the Board, Committees and senior management. Maintain governance frameworks, compliance calendars, internal controls and processes aligned with BASF corporate requirements. Related Party Transactions & Intercompany Governance Oversee Related Party Transactions, including documentation, approvals, disclosures and compliance with applicable laws. Partner with finance, tax and business teams to mitigate governance, conflict-of-interest and intercompany transaction risks. License to Operate (LtO) – Regulatory / Compliance Framework Safeguard BASIL’s regulatory License to Operate through strong legal and compliance risk management, maintaining trust with investors, the Board and group stakeholders. Oversee legal and compliance aspects of environmental, safety and sustainability requirements, including BRSR, in collaboration with EHS and operations teams. Maintain effective compliance systems, checklists and processes for corporate, listing and related regulatory requirements . Legal Advisory, Litigation & Contract Management Provide legal advisory support on applicable laws, regulations, policies and business matters. Manage litigation, contracts, schemes, incentives, offers and legal risks, particularly in agro-chemicals. Support strategic projects and represent the business with regional and central legal teams as required. Stakeholder & Investor Engagement Support investor relations, analyst and investor meetings, minority shareholder engagement and regulatory liaison. Interface with stock exchanges, MCA, ROC, auditors, regulatory authorities and BASF Group companies. Key Qualifications & Experience: Qualified Company Secretary and LLB with more than 15 years’ experience including Company Secretarial exposure in listed companies in India . Strong knowledge of Companies Act, SEBI regulations, Foreign Exchange laws, corporate governance, RPTs and stakeholder management. Experience in MNCs, manufacturing/process industries or agro-chemicals will be an advantage. Strong governance mindset, integrity and sound judgement. Strategic thinking with hands-on execution capability. Excellent communication, influencing skills and stakeholder management skills. Ability to work effectively in a global, matrixed and cross-cultural environment.

WAS SIE ERWARTET Bestellungen & Termintracking – Sie wickeln Bestellungen operativ ab (Anlage, Nachverfolgung, Terminüberwachung) und sorgen für eine reibungslose Material- und Warenverfügbarkeit. Stammdaten & Datenqualität – Sie pflegen Einkaufsstammdaten (Artikel, Lieferanten, Preise, Konditionen) und halten diese in den Systemen aktuell und sauber. Procure-to-Pay im Blick – Sie begleiten den Procure-to-Pay‑Prozess operativ von der Bedarfsmeldung bis zur Rechnungsprüfung. Ausschreibungen & Lieferanten – Sie unterstützen bei Ausschreibungen (Angebote einholen/auswerten, Dokumentation, Vergabevorbereitung) und im Lieferantenmanagement (Abweichungen, Reklamationen, Mengen-/Preisunterschiede). WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Kaufmännische Basis – Abgeschlossene kaufmännische Ausbildung oder vergleichbare Qualifikation; ein Studium ist von Vorteil, aber nicht zwingend erforderlich. Einkaufserfahrung – Erste bis mehrjährige Erfahrung im operativen bzw. transaktionalen Einkauf, idealerweise in einem strukturierten Umfeld. Systeme & Excel – Sicherer Umgang mit ERP‑Systemen (z. B. SAP) sowie MS Office, insbesondere Excel. Arbeitsweise – Strukturiert, zuverlässig und detailorientiert – mit hohem Qualitäts- und Termintreue‑Anspruch. Service & Kommunikation – Ausgeprägte Service-/Prozessorientierung, Teamfähigkeit, Belastbarkeit und klare Kommunikation mit internen und externen Ansprechpartnern. WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktives Gehalt – 12 Monatsgehälter über Gastroniveau. Zusätzliche Extras – 13. Monatsgehalt als Jahresprämie, Urlaubsgeld und Zuschuss zum Jobticket Mehr Freizeit & Flexibilität – 30 Tage Urlaub, 5 Zukunftstage, Gleitzeitkonto und mobiles Arbeiten Betriebliche Altersvorsorge – 100% Zuschuss: Wir verdoppeln Ihre Einzahlung Gesundheit & Wohlbefinden – Zugang zu Medical Center und Gesundheitsangeboten Karriere & Entwicklung – Individuelle Förderung, interne Schulungen und Weiterbildung in Digitalisierung & KI Gestaltungsspielraum & Innovation – Bringe eigene Ideen ein und gestalte die Transformation aktiv mit Starkes Miteinander & Diversität – Respekt, Toleranz und ein Team, das zusammenhält SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Melden Sie sich bei Katrin Statnick (Talent Acquisition): katrin.statnick@basf.com +49 30 2005-58118 Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier. Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier. ÜBER UNS Hinter jedem perfekten Service steckt ein starkes Backoffice. Wir sorgen für Transparenz, Effizienz und solide Prozesse – von Budgetplanung bis Reporting. Mit Zahlenverständnis und Organisationstalent schaffen wir die Grundlage für den Erfolg der BASF Gastronomie. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. In den Bewerbungsgesprächen können Sie uns aber jederzeit sagen, wenn Sie lieber geduzt werden möchten – wir richten uns da gerne nach Ihnen. Als spezialisierte Serviceeinheit stehen wir für eine hohe Kundenorientierung und gestalten unsere Dienstleistungen partnerschaftlich, effizient und zukunftsweisend. WAS SIE ERWARTET Sind Sie eine leidenschaftliche und motivierte Person? Dann verstärken Sie unsere Abteilung Business Reporting als Junior Controller. Diese Position bietet Ihnen die Möglichkeit, Ihre Fähigkeiten in den Bereichen Kostenstellenrechnung, Kostenverrechnung, Produktkostenrechnung, Projektbuchhaltung oder Reporting weiterzuentwickeln. Wir sind ein international erfolgreiches Unternehmen der BASF-Gruppe. Unsere Lösungen sind speziell für verschiedene Anbausysteme entwickelt. Sie verbinden Saatgut und speziell gezüchtete Pflanzeneigenschaften, Pflanzenschutzprodukte, digitale Tools und Nachhaltigkeitsansätze, um Landwirten, Züchtern und anderen Interessengruppen entlang der Wertschöpfungskette bestmögliche Ergebnisse zu bieten. Mit Teams im Labor, auf dem Feld, im Büro und in der Produktion tun wir alles, was in unserer Macht steht, um eine nachhaltige Zukunft für die Landwirtschaft zu schaffen. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Hier erfährst du mehr über unsere Vision bei BASF Agricultural Solutions! WAS DICH ERWARTET In unserem globalen Team „Environmental Fate and Structure“ wirst du ein Jahr (perspektivisch zwei Jahre) lang als Postdoc zur Optimierung des Umweltprofils potenzieller Pflanzenschutzmittel tätig sein. In deiner neuen Rolle konzipierst, implementierst und verwaltest du Laborautomatisierungs-Workflows. Die Entwicklung und Optimierung von Hochdurchsatz-Assays im Bereich Environmental Fate von Pflanzenschutzmitteln gehören dabei zu deinen Aufgaben. Darüber hinaus erhältst du die Möglichkeit, Liquid-Handling-Systeme zu bedienen und zu optimieren. Nicht zuletzt spielst du eine entscheidende Rolle bei der Projektplanung, -koordination sowie -präsentation und arbeitest eng mit interdisziplinären Teams zusammen. Der stetige Zugang zu modernen KI ‑ Tools und digitalen Assistenten wird dir helfen, effizient zu arbeiten und fundierte, datenbasierte Entscheidungen zu treffen. WAS DU MITBRINGST Promotion in Chemie, Umweltwissenschaften oder einem ähnlichen Fachgebiet Expertise in Laborautomatisierung, praktische Erfahrung mit Liquid-Handling-Systemen sowie fundierte Kenntnisse in Datenanalyse und Datenmanagement Hands-on Erfahrung im Bereich Chromatographie wünschenswert, Erfahrung mit Programmiersprachen wie z.B. Python ist von Vorteil Leidenschaft für die Optimierung von Laborprozessen zusammen mit hervorragenden organisatorischen Fähigkeiten (inkl. Projektkoordination, Dokumentenmanagement und Zeitmanagement) ausgezeichnete Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Englisch erforderlich, fortgeschrittene Deutschkenntnisse wünschenswert eine digitale, zukunftsorientierte Denkweise und die Motivation, KI als Chance zur Verbesserung von Prozessen und Arbeitsweisen zu nutzen WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase - Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy - Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback Netzwerke - unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Vergütung - eine Anerkennung des Einsatzes, weitere Infos findest du hier Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung - je nach Einsatzort und -gebiet SO ERREICHST DU UNS Lea Sophie Günther (Talent Acquisition), lea-sophie.guenther@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56150 beantwortet gerne deine Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne kannst du dir auch einen Online-Termin buchen (fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden). Unser Recruiting-Team erreichst du auch hier Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess findest du hier ÜBER UNS Die BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH bietet als zuverlässiger Partner der Landwirtschaft innovative, nachhaltige Lösungen speziell für verschiedene Anbausysteme. Unser Unternehmenssitz ist das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof, nahe der BASF-Konzernzentrale in Ludwigshafen. In der Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar erwartet dich beruflich wie auch privat ein attraktives Umfeld. Mehr über das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof erfährst du unter: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LI Mehr über unser Team bei BASF Agricultural Solutions findest du hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalte mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Menschen mit Behinderungen oder Weltanschauung lebt. Wir duzen dich gerne – das gehört bei uns zur Kultur. Wenn du lieber gesiezt werden möchtest, sag einfach Bescheid. Jetzt bewerben Unsere Mitarbeitenden stehen im Mittelpunkt und setzen sich jeden Tag dafür ein, unseren Kunden einen echten Mehrwert zu bieten. Wir legen großen Wert darauf, unsere Mitarbeitenden heute und in Zukunft gezielt zu fördern, damit wir die Talente und Fähigkeiten haben, die für unseren Erfolg entscheidend sind. Vereint durch die Leidenschaft für die Landwirtschaft, entwickeln wir gemeinsam innovative Lösungen, arbeiten partnerschaftlich zusammen und streben nach den besten Ergebnissen – als ein Team. So schaffen wir Wirkung. Aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft und dem wichtigsten Beruf auf der Erde. jobs.name: PostDoc Lab Automation and Digitalization (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in Agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming, and the biggest job on Earth. We are an internationally successful company of the BASF Group. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. With teams in the lab, field, office and in production, we do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture. #ForTheLoveOfFarming#TheBiggestJobOnEarth#AgEmployerOfChoice Here you can gain more insight into our Vision at BASF Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Within our global team Environmental Fate and Structure you work as a PostDoc for one year (with the prospect of extending to two years) to optimize the environmental profile of potential crop protection agents. In your new role, you design, implement, and manage lab automation workflows. Development and optimization of high throughput assays in the field of environmental fate of small molecules are part of your tasks. Moreover, you get the opportunity to operate and optimize liquid handling systems. Last but not least, you play a crucial role in project planning, coordination as well as presentation and you interact with multidisciplinary teams. With access to modern AI tools and digital assistants, you help us work more efficiently and make data-driven decisions. WHAT YOU OFFER PhD in Chemistry, Environmental Sciences or a related field expertise in laboratory automation, practical experience with liquid-handling systems as well as in-depth knowledge of data analysis and data management hands-on experience in chromatography is desirable; experience with programming languages such as Python is an advantage passion for optimising laboratory processes combined with excellent organisational skills (incl. project coordination, document management and time management) excellent communication skills in English are essential; advanced German language skills are desirable digital mindset and the willingness to see AI as an opportunity to improve processes and ways of working WHAT WE OFFER Practical phase - actively apply theory in challenging and varied tasks/projects Teambuddy - contact person for all questions and regular feedback Networks - get to know different perspectives, ways of thinking and fields of work Compensation - a recognition of the commitment, more information you will find here Flexible working hours - depending on the location and area of work HOW TO REACH US Lea Sophie Guenther (Talent Acquisition), lea-sophie.guenther@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56150 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment (please note that technical questions cannot be answered in this appointment). You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH is a reliable partner to the agricultural sector, offering innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to a wide range of crop systems. With the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof, near the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you’ll find an attractive environment for both your professional life and your leisure time in the Rhine ‑ Neckar metropolitan region. You can learn more about the Agricultural Center in Limburgerhof here . Find out more about our Agricultural Solutions team here Diversity is our greatest strength! Join us in shaping the future as part of a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunity – regardless of gender, age, background, sexual orientation, disability, or personal beliefs.

Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Intégrez la division Industries et Services de BASF où nous commercialisons les quelques 8 000 produits fabriqués par le groupe et destinés à améliorer la vie quotidienne. Êtes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES En tant qu’Account Manager au sein de BTC France (distributeur interne du Groupe BASF en France), vous êtes chargé d’un portefeuille de clients existants et de prospects dans le marché des peintures & vernis, des adhésifs et des produits pour la construction. A ce titre, vous : Analysez les besoins des clients, apportez un premier niveau de support technique et proposez des solutions tant commerciales que techniques répondant aux problématiques que vous avez identifiées ; Développez pro-activement les différentes stratégies « clients » par une connaissance approfondie du marché et des produits ; Menez et optimisez les négociations commerciales en vue de maintenir une croissance continue de nos parts de marchés et résultats ; Prospectez et fidélisez de nouveaux clients ; Etes force de proposition pour intégrer de nouvelles gammes de produits afin d'élargir l'offre pour vos industries, que ce soit des produits groupe, ou bien des produits tiers ; Contribuez à la mise à jour des données relatives au marché et aux clients par un système de reporting. VOTRE PROFIL Vous êtes titulaire d’un Master en Chimie ; Vous disposez d’une expérience d’au moins 3 ans dans une fonction technico-commerciale ; Vous avez déjà travaillé dans le marché des fabricants de peintures & vernis, la connaissance du marché des adhésifs serait un plus ; Vous savez intégrer l'utilisation des outils de Customer Relationship Management dans vos activités quotidiennes ; Vous parlez couramment le français et l’anglais. La connaissance de l’allemand est un atout supplémentaire ; Vous êtes un team player dynamique, flexible et enthousiaste ; Vous êtes orienté résultats qui aimez les taches variées ; Vous considérez les structures organisationnelles complexes comme un défi intéressant et motivant. CE QUE NOUS VOUS OFFRONS Une opportunité stimulante et passionnante dans une équipe engagée et compétente ; Un environnement propice à l'innovation, au partage d'idée et au bien-être ; Un environnement stimulant et responsabilisant. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Situé au coeur de Paris dans le quartier de la bourse, l’établissement Industrie et Services est le siège social de BASF France SAS et compte plus de 200 collaborateurs. Ce site regroupe diverses fonctions support et commerciales qui couvrent différents domaines. Nos principaux clients sont les industriels de l’automobile, la construction, la cosmétique, l’hygiène, la santé, l’agro-alimentaire, l’emballage, ou encore l’électrotechnique. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendemment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. jobs.name: Account Manager (m/f)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF The world needs innovation – and we need you! At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future and are looking for talents who can help answer tomorrow’s challenges. Join BASF’s Industries and Services division, where we market around 8,000 products produced by the Group, all aimed at improving everyday life. Are you ready to take the next step in your career and contribute to a more sustainable future in chemistry within our teams? WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As an Account Manager within BTC France (the internal distributor of the BASF Group in France), you will be responsible for a portfolio of existing and prospective customers in the paints & coatings, adhesives, and construction markets. In this role, you will: Analyze customer needs, provide first-level technical support, and propose both commercial and technical solutions tailored to identified challenges; Proactively develop customer strategies through in-depth market and product knowledge; Lead and optimize commercial negotiations to ensure continuous growth in market share and results; Prospect and retain new customers; Actively contribute ideas to integrate new product ranges to broaden the offering for your industries, whether BASF products or third-party products; Contribute to the updating of market and customer data through reporting systems. WHAT YOU OFFER You hold a Master’s degree in Chemistry; You have at least 3 years of experience in a technical sales role; You have experience in the paints & coatings market; knowledge of adhesives is a plus; You are comfortable using Customer Relationship Management (CRM) tools in your daily work; You are fluent in French and English; knowledge of German is an additional asset; You are a dynamic, flexible, and enthusiastic team player; You are results-oriented and enjoy working on varied tasks; You see complex organizational structures as an interesting and motivating challenge. WHAT WE OFFER An attractive compensation package; Personalized support to help develop your professional skills; A stimulating and empowering work environment. ABOUT US Located in the heart of Paris, in the Bourse district, the Industries and Services site is the headquarters of BASF France SAS and employs more than 200 people. This site brings together various support and commercial functions covering different business areas. Our main customers operate in automotive, construction, cosmetics, hygiene, healthcare, food, packaging, and electrical engineering industries. Diversity is our greatest strength! Join our winning formula and help shape the future with us—within an international team that promotes inclusion and equal opportunities regardless of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability, or beliefs.

jobs.name: Technology Manager Petrochemical Purification
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role The Technology Manager Petrochemical Purification is responsible for providing technical consulting and design proposals to customers for Adsorbent Solutions in Asia/Pacific with focus on South Asia. As part of the global team, the position drives growth in Petrochemicals, supports business development and jointly with the regional sales team ensures customer success. How Will You Do It As part of the global team engaging with project and plant owners, licensors, and EPC’s in Asia/Pacific with focus on South Asia providing Adsorbent Solutions including technical consulting and technical design proposals for petrochemical purification. Providing input on performance warranty offers and technical value proposition to support pricing proposal in alignment with Global Marketing and Sales. Providing customer consultation and on-site support. Managing product quality issues within the non-conformance management system. Attending and presenting at conferences, seminars, and licensor forums for business development and market/competitive intelligence. Building opportunity pipeline in close collaboration with Sales and Marketing by effectively delivering the value proposition on our high-performance portfolio offerings Understanding customer and market needs while identifying the optimal product/technology solution. Providing market/customer driven ideas and projects for incremental innovations. Working with technical team to improve design tools and align with global segment strategy. Interfacing with other business divisions of BASF as needed to provide complete solutions to customers. What We Look For Education: Master`s degree or higher in chemistry / chemical engineering, or a related field. Working Exp: 5+ years of technical experience in a role focused on oil and gas processing. 2+ years of experience within a customer facing role is preferred. Experience in process engineering or process design incl. preparing technical proposals using simulation tools (e.g. Aspen HYSYS). Experience in working as part of a global team Technical & Professional Knowledge : Process understanding of key petrochemical value chains Knowledge in adsorption or catalysis is an asset Strong analytical skills as well as professional written and verbal communication skills. Work Location Godrej One - Vikhroli (Mumbai)

jobs.name: Workplace Specialist, APAC
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position The Workplace Specialist is responsible for delivering an integrated, efficient, and future-ready workplace by combining facilities management, workplace experience, and digital process optimization. This role supports workplace transformation initiatives, including carve-outs, divestment, fit-outs and renovations, while driving improvements in Procure-to-Pay (P2P) processes, vendor performance, and service delivery Responsibilities 1. Workplace Projects (Carve-out, Divestment, Fit-Out, Renovation, Carve-out, etc) Support design, planning, and execution of transformative projects (office fit-outs, renovations, carve-out, divestment, etc). Coordinate with Cluster Leads, project managers, designers, contractors, and internal stakeholders. Ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and aligned with company standards. Support implementation of new workplace concepts (e.g., agile workspace, hybrid working, smart office solutions). 2. P2P Process Optimization & Automation Drive automation and digitalization of P2P processes. Ensure accurate and timely processing of purchase requisitions, purchase orders, and invoices. Identify inefficiencies and implement improvements to reduce manual work, cycle time, and errors. Collaborate with procurement, finance, and digital teams on system enhancements and process standardization. Provide guidance on end-to-end P2P activities related to workplace and facilities services (PR, PO, GR, invoice validation), if necessary. 4. Health, Safety & Compliance Ensure compliance with local regulations, company policies, and safety standards. Conduct workplace safety inspections, risk assessments, and drills. Assist in incident investigations and reports. Support emergency response planning and incident management. 6. Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate with internal stakeholders (HR, IT, Business Units). Gather feedback on workplace services and drive improvements. Communicate workplace initiatives, changes, and updates effectively. 7. Budget & Cost Management Assist in managing facilities budgets and operational costs. Monitor expenses and identify cost-saving opportunities. Support invoice processing and financial tracking. Requirements Qualifications & Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Facilities Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or related field. 3–5 years of experience in Facilities Management or IFM environment. Experience in managing outsourced facility services is preferred. Skills & Competencies Strong knowledge of IFM operations and workplace services. Vendor management and negotiation skills. Good understanding of health & safety standards and compliance. Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. Problem-solving and proactive mindset. Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Technical Skills Familiarity with CAFM/IWMS systems. Basic understanding of building systems (HVAC, electrical, etc.). Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).

jobs.name: Junior Controller Transfer Pricing (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative,efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As part of our unit Business Reporting you take over the position as Junior Controller within our Intercompany Transfer Pricing Team. In compliance with legal and tax regulations, internationally recognized frameworks, and BASF guidelines, you calculate, monitor, and adjust transfer prices to ensure accuracy and compliance. You take ownership of a dedicated business area by analyzing financial statements, calculating entity-level margins, managing ad hoc requests, and conducting regular quality assurance reviews. As a trusted business partner, you collaborate closely with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Supply Chain, and Controlling, while acting as a first-level escalation point to facilitate solutions and support decision-making. You drive the continuous improvement, optimization, and automation of intercompany transfer pricing processes and contribute to effective knowledge management. Last but not least, you support colleagues and the Global Tax Department during audits and compliance activities while continuously developing your expertise within a digitally driven and innovative organization. WHAT YOU OFFER degree in Business Administration or Business Informatics with a focus on Accounting/Finance or Controlling background in tax related topics and a first professional experience is a plus strong understanding of financial statements including profit and loss statements familiarity with analyzing the development of financial KPIs strong command of MS Office Excel is a must, initial experience in the SAP environment (R/3 or S/4HANA) or Business Warehouse systems is welcome good communication skills in English, knowledge of other language is an advantage openness to learn different technology applications (e. g. VBA, Power BI or UiPath) WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 40.000 - 46.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience. In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success as well as individual performance. Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according toyour needs 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Location-independent working models with defined days of presence in Berlin and residence in Germany – here a first impression Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration – get to know our location HOW TO REACH US Katarina Borisov (Talent Acquisition), Tel.:+49 30 2005-59269 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team here. First information about our application process can be found here and in our Q&A video. ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

How You Will Do It Executes and achieve trial (new product introduction) and troubleshooting requests. More than 3 years of experience in Polyurethane of Chemical industry. Industry experience in rigid polyurethane applications is preferred. Proficient in English, with excellent verbal and written communication skills. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用

jobs.name: Talent Acquisition Specialist (1 Year Contract)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Support end-to-end recruitment for the Global Business Services in the Global Hub. You are responsible for managing end-to-end recruitment processes, from building a sourcing strategy, candidates sourcing, interview scheduling, offer management and onboarding stages, while developing and managing strong relationships with key stakeholders. Continuously look for process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Responsibilities Operational Excellence Collaborate with multiple parties to develop and deliver recruitment strategy and framework suited for the service recipients. Support the talent pipeline processes through gathering of local market talent insights, managing sourcing/recruitment channels, creating internal candidate data pools, and supporting recruitment campaigns and marketing activities. Support internal / external candidate relocation (secondment, delegation, domestic relocation, repatriation etc) and coordinate all related activities to ensure smooth transition while in compliance with legal legislation. Build and maintain a pool of potential candidates, managing relationships with them to ensure a strong talent pipeline. Provide market insights and recommendations to improve sourcing strategies. Track and report key metrics, such as the number of candidates sourced, conversion rates, etc. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Identify process improvement, automation and digitalization opportunities, prepare business case and participate in digitalization and process optimization implementation. Continuously review and maintain knowledge documents in areas of expertise to develop the formation of competence hub. Requirements Education Minimum graduate with a Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Human Resources or Psychology Working Experience With 4-5 years working experience in recruitment or candidate sourcing. Preferably experience and familiar working with a world-class HR system (SAP, SuccessFactors, Workday). Proficiency in sourcing tools (LinkedIn Recruiter, Boolean search techniques, job portals). Experience working in large, diverse teams and able to communicate with different levels. Proven ability to resolve process-related issues. Technical & Professional Knowledge Understanding of HR principles/Employment Laws. Functional knowledge and skills in using Microsoft applications, i.e. Words, Excel, PowerPoint. Fluent in spoken and written English.  Knowledge of any other Asian languages will be an added advantage.

jobs.name: Nematologist Scientist (m/f/d) Functiedetails
jobs.description: Nematologist Scientist (m/f/d) Functiedetails | BASF SE Verdergaan naar primaire inhoud Zoek banen Taal Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profiel login Werknemerslogin Beschrijving van de vacature Nematologist Scientist (m/f/d) LOCATIE ONDERNEMING FUNCTIEGEBIED ARBEIDSVOORWAARDEN REFERENTIECODE FLEXIBELE WERKOPTIES LOCATIE ONDERNEMING FUNCTIEGEBIED ARBEIDSVOORWAARDEN REFERENTIECODE FLEXIBELE WERKOPTIES Solliciteer nu WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. Come join us on our journey to create solutions for sustainable agriculture at the Innovation Center of BASF Belgium Coordination Center CommV in Ghent, Belgium. We are seeking people who thrive in a culture of creativity, openness, responsibility, and entrepreneurial spirit. In our organization, we encourage and celebrate innovative thinking, where ideas flow freely and are nurtured. We value individuals who take ownership of their work and are accountable for their actions. We foster an entrepreneurial mindset, where taking calculated risks and seizing opportunities are encouraged to drive growth and success. Join our team and be part of an environment that values and rewards creative thinking, collaboration, and a can-do attitude. Within BASF Seed and Traits R&D, Trait Research we focus on the design, creation and testing of candidate leads as gene-based solutions for herbicide tolerance, pest and disease control in key crops including soybean, oilseed rape, wheat and cotton. We are currently seeking a talented, motivated, and inspiring Nematologist to join our Trait Research team at the R&D Innovation Center in Ghent, Belgium. In this role, you will develop and execute research strategies aligned with business goals to successfully commercialize innovative nematode resistant traits and germplasm. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions: Vision & Mission WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT You propose innovative nematode trait concepts and develop and implement nematode control research strategies using state-of-the-art know-how and technologies including suitable third-party collaborations to ascertain scientific competitiveness of the nematode program. You evaluate scientific progress of nematode control programs with the project leader and with support of team members (technicians and scientists in the lab and GH) and take decisions to ensure the achievement of scientific objectives and critical milestones with optimal use of available resources. You design and oversee the execution of nematode control assays to support program goals. You articulate a clear, innovative and competitive testing scheme to meet these goals and to ensure the efficient delivery of high-quality nematode traits. You proactively report on your scientific progress and new opportunities to all relevant stakeholders as requested. You develop the innovative ability and competence of research teams, and you act as a mentor and give scientific guidance across the organization. You support the program leader to ensure strategy competitiveness by working closely with Intellectual Property colleagues to build a proactive patenting (and, if needed, licensing-) strategy and ensure a competitor IP analysis in the program field. WHAT YOU OFFER You hold a PhD in Nematology with 12-15 years of relevant experience or equivalent. You are internationally acknowledged as a consistent innovator and leading authority in the field of Plant Nematology and have a profound knowledge of GM trait development. You provide innovative solutions for plant parasitic nematode issues and demonstrate knowledge and expertise from working directly with plant parasitic nematodes. You are familiar with crop business strategies and the principles of value creation for growers. You understand the connection between the lab and the field. You have a track record of providing scientific leadership for internal and external program work packages, effectively managing collaborations with partners, and excel in developing innovative research proposals that incorporate novel technology platforms to advance plant parasitic nematode control project objectives. You possess leadership and team coaching skills, motivating and inspiring others, while fostering collaboration in diverse environments through a multi-cultural mindset. You demonstrate proactivity, with well-developed communication skills that enable effective interactions with stakeholders. You build a culture of innovation by creating an environment that supports creativity and by coaching and developing people. You are fluent in English. WHAT WE OFFER A permanent contract within an attractive working environment in a professional and motivated team. Interesting and challenging learning and development opportunities to help you make the most of your talents and your job. An attractive base salary in relation to your diploma and competencies, an annual bonus and extra-legal benefits. HOE JE ONS KUNT BEREIKEN If you need more information about the process or the vacancy, you can reach our recruiting team here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. Solliciteer nu Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. jobs.name: Technical Commercial Manager
jobs.description:
WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Technical Commercial Manager is responsible for supporting customers across the GCC region through technical application expertise, process optimization, and business development activities. The role focuses on driving growth in areas where commercial success is closely linked to technical capability by delivering customer-focused solutions, supporting new application development, improving product performance, and building strong relationships with customers, distributors, and internal stakeholders. Duties will include the following: Drive business growth through technical engagement, customer acquisition, application development, and identification of new market opportunities Conduct customer visits, process audits, product trials, and technical evaluations to improve customer processes and performance Lead the implementation of new products, applications, and process improvements at customer sites Troubleshoot technical challenges, identify root causes, and recommend corrective actions to enhance customer satisfaction Deliver technical training and product-related support to customers, distributors, and internal teams Monitor industry trends, emerging technologies, and regulatory developments relevant to Surface Treatment applications Manage customer accounts and projects through technical consultancy, commercial coordination, pricing management, and relationship development Conduct market and competitive analyses to identify growth opportunities and support commercial strategy Align customer requirements with supply chain activities to ensure reliable product availability and timely delivery Act as the commercial interface with regional marketing and segment management teams Provide ongoing technical and commercial support to drive successful product implementation, customer retention, and business growth Support initiatives to optimize inventory levels and reduce customer overdues Maintain accurate reporting, sales pipeline information, contract databases, and action plans WHAT YOU OFFER Degree in Engineering, Chemistry, Materials Science, or a related technical discipline Minimum 5 years of experience in Surface Treatment, Coatings, Chemical Processing, or a related industry, including customer-facing technical and commercial responsibilities Strong knowledge of Surface Treatment processes, industrial applications, and manufacturing environments, with experience in technical troubleshooting, product trials, process optimization, and customer training Experience in Coil Coatings, Cold Forming, Beverage Cans, Metal Working Fluids, or related applications is advantageous Experience within the Middle East market is preferred Strong commercial mindset with an entrepreneurial, proactive, and customer-focused approach Excellent project management, problem-solving, and conflict-resolution skills Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to engage effectively across all organizational levels and multicultural environments Proficiency in Microsoft Office; experience with SAP and/or Salesforce is advantageous Fluent English communication skills; Arabic is highly preferred. Additional language skills such as Hindi, Malay, or German are advantageous. Willingness to travel extensively across the Middle East Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.description:
WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Commercial Account Manager is responsible for driving profitable growth across the Middle East through business development, channel strategy execution, distributor network expansion, and commercial excellence. The role focuses on strengthening market presence, developing high-performing distribution partnerships, identifying new business opportunities, and optimizing go-to-market models to support sustainable growth across the region. Duties will include the following: Drive business development initiatives and execute growth strategies across the Middle East Surface Treatment market Develop and implement country-specific channel-to-market strategies to maximize market coverage and growth Identify, evaluate, appoint, and develop specialized distributors and channel partners across assigned territories Strengthen distributor capabilities and performance to support short-, medium-, and long-term business objectives Grow market share by identifying new customers, applications, and business opportunities Manage pricing, margins, and customer contracts to achieve revenue and profitability targets Conduct market analysis and monitor competitive activities to identify opportunities and support commercial decision-making Align customer and distributor requirements with supply chain activities to ensure reliable product availability and service levels Act as the commercial interface with regional marketing and segment management teams Provide technical and commercial support to distributors and customers to strengthen relationships and drive repeat business Support initiatives to optimize inventory levels and reduce customer overdues Maintain accurate reporting, sales pipeline information, contract databases, and action plans WHAT YOU OFFER Minimum 5 years of sales and/or business development experience within Surface Treatment, Coatings, Chemicals, or related industrial markets Proven experience in developing distribution networks, channel partners, and go-to-market strategies Experience within the Middle East market is preferred Strong commercial acumen with an entrepreneurial, growth-oriented, and customer-focused mindset Demonstrated ability to build strategic relationships, negotiate effectively, and drive business growth Strong project management, problem-solving, and conflict-resolution capabilities Excellent communication and presentation skills, with jobs.name: Senior Specialist Controlling - Standalone Transformation (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a new standalone business, we are adapting our Controlling organization with a strong focus on value creation. At its core sits a dedicated Value & Cost Controlling team whose primary tasks are to measure progress on the company’s value‑creation initiatives and to increase transparency on their financial impact. In your new role, you ensure transparency across all Transitional Service Agreements (TSAs), covering scope, service levels, costs, charges, budgets, and timelines across all functions and regions. You monitor TSA-related costs, charges, and budgets, perform variance analyses, and provide reliable financial reporting and forecasting for Finance and senior management. In close collaboration with Group Functions, you validate incoming TSA charges against the agreed scope, service levels, and pricing mechanisms while tracking exit costs, dual-run expenses, replacement costs, stranded costs, and the cost step-up into the standalone target operating model. Furthermore, you act as the central contact for TSA service owners, coordinate regular reviews, monitor milestones, and identify risks or deviations at an early stage to support a timely transition into the target setup. You work closely with colleagues from Controlling, Procurement, Legal, IT, operational functions, and their Controlling counterparts to ensure a consistent understanding of TSA terms and support the successful transition into the target setup. In addition, you contribute to the continuous improvement of TSA controlling processes, tools, methodologies, governance standards, dashboards, reporting, and steering committee materials, while supporting ad-hoc analyses and decision papers related to TSA scope changes, extensions, and early exits. WHAT YOU OFFER university degree in finance, controlling, business administration, economics, or a related field several years of professional experience in controlling, FP&A, or comparable finance functions in an international company strong controlling background combined with an analytical mindset and a hands-on, pragmatic approach experience in managing service agreements, cost controlling for shared services, or complex cross-functional projects; experience in business transitions or the chemicals industry is beneficial proficiency in SAP, BI tools (e.g. Power BI, SAC), and advanced MS Excel and PowerPoint skills very good language skills in English and excellent analytical, problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills in a multicultural environment WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio "Freizeit" with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant "Auszeit" including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center "LackiKids" and a vacation program "LackiKids on Tour". HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: Senior Specialist - Value Creation Initiatives (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a new standalone business, we are adapting our Controlling organization with a strong focus on value creation. At its core sits a dedicated Value & Cost Controlling team whose primary tasks are to measure progress on the company’s value‑creation initiatives and to increase transparency on their financial impact. In this role, you support the design, coordination, and tracking of the company’s strategic value creation initiatives, ensuring transparency regarding financial impact, the maturity of measures, and overall progress. You consolidate, analyze, and review initiative business cases together with initiative owners, validate baselines and target ambitions, and translate operational measures into their financial impact on the P&L, cash flow, and balance sheet. Furthermore, you prepare dashboard-driven management reporting and steering committee materials, providing senior leadership with clear insights into the status, risks, and opportunities of the initiative portfolio. You collaborate closely with functions and businesses to ensure alignment between initiative tracking and financial planning and forecasting, including the reconciliation of initiative impacts into budgets and forecasts to avoid double counting. In addition, you support ad-hoc analyses and decision papers related to initiative scope, prioritization, and resource allocation. Moreover, you contribute to the continuous improvement of tools, methodologies, and governance standards for planning, tracking, and reporting strategic initiatives while acting as a sparring partner to initiative owners across the organization. WHAT YOU OFFER university degree in finance, controlling, business administration, economics, industrial engineering, or a related field several years of professional experience in controlling, FP&A, or a comparable finance function in a multinational corporation strong controlling background combined with an analytical mindset and a hands-on, pragmatic approach proven experience in business case development, KPI tracking, and cross-functional project work; experience in the chemicals industry or business transitions is a plus proficiency in SAP, BI tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau, SAC), advanced MS Excel, and affinity for AI-supported solutions and digital transformation excellent analytical, conceptual, and communication skills as well as fluent written and spoken English WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio "Freizeit" with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant "Auszeit" including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center "LackiKids" and a vacation program "LackiKids on Tour". HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . jobs.name: Transport Planner Officer (m/f/d) - Temporal Detalles del puesto
jobs.description: Transport Planner Officer (m/f/d) - Temporal Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Buscar ofertas de empleo Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Acceso empleado/a Descripción del puesto Transport Planner Officer (m/f/d) - Temporal LUGAR COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE TRABAJO N° DE REFERENCIA OPCIONES DE TRABAJO FLEXIBLE LUGAR COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE TRABAJO N° DE REFERENCIA OPCIONES DE TRABAJO FLEXIBLE Enviar candidatura ahora ¡El mundo necesita innovación y nuestro equipo te necesita a ti! BASF es la primera empresa química del mundo #WeCreateChemistry y uno de los mejores lugares para trabajar y desarrollar tu carrera. Estamos en búsqueda de talento diverso, apasionado y creativo que desee hacer sus prácticas con nosotros/as, aprender de la mano de tutores/as experimentados/as y marcar la diferencia. BASF Española S.L. comercializa más de 5.000 productos para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros clientes y de la sociedad en una gran variedad de sectores. La industria química está cambiando; y BASF también. Descubre nuestra estrategia: #WinningWays . ¿Quieres poner en práctica tus conocimientos y crear química con nuestro equipo? LO QUE HARÁS La persona seleccionada deberá dar soporte en las siguientes tareas: Disposición de transporte para la carga de los productos consignados en las entregas a servir desde los Sites asignados. Seguimiento de los transportes hasta su cierre informando de las incidencias/cambios de los pedidos a sus gestores. Negociación de precios Spot con los transportistas cuando se requiera un servicio no cubierto. Soporte en la integración del nuevo sistema SAP. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Grado en Administración y dirección de empresas, Comercio internacional, Supply Chain o titulación académica equivalente Experiencia mínima de 1 año en funciones área de logística/ supply chain o similar en sector industrial Nivel alto de inglés y valorable conocimientos de italiano y/o alemán Conocimientos de SAP a nivel de usuario Habilidades comunicativas, de trabajo en equipo y resolución de problemas LO QUE OFRECEMOS Entorno de trabajo saludable: donde tu seguridad y salud (física y mental) son nuestra prioridad. Paquete salarial atractivo: basado en un salario fijo y un bonus según resultados. Plan de retribución flexible: para beneficiarte de servicios como seguro de salud, escuela infantil, tarjeta transporte y formación. Desarrollo profesional: promovemos la formación continua y el acceso a vacantes internas para seguir creciendo en BASF. Medidas de conciliación personal y familiar, así como una ayuda económica para la escolarización de hijos/as. Y muchos más. CÓMO PARTICIPAR EN EL PROCESO Envía tu CV a través de la oferta para poder conocernos. Si tu perfil encaja, te llamaremos desde el equipo español de Talent Acquisition en Alemania (prefijo +49) y te acompañaremos en el proceso. ¡Con cada paso estarás más cerca de conocer a tu futuro equipo! Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, contacta con nuestro/a compañero/a: Sara Prado (sara.prado-marques@basf.com)* *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. MÁS SOBRE BASF Celebramos la diversidad y activamente promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo donde cada voz cuenta y cada idea es valorada sin importar edad, género, estado civil, etnia, religión, condición física/mental u orientación sexual. #BeYourself Somos personas C reativas, A biertas, R esponsables y E mprendedoras. Estos son nuestros valores, que nos guían y describen cómo somos y actuamos. Si tú también te identificas con ellos, ¡BASF es tu sitio! ¡La diversidad es nuestra mayor fortaleza! Conviértete en parte de nuestra fórmula ganadora para el éxito y desarrolla el futuro con nosotros/as, en un equipo global que abraza la inclusión y la igualdad de oportunidades independientemente de la edad, género, orientación sexual, discapacidad o creencias. Enviar candidatura ahora Nuestra oferta total única: you@BASF En BASF obtendrás algo más que un salario, ya que nuestra oferta total incluye elementos adicionales segun las diferentes etapas de tu vida.
