jobs.name: Technical Account Manager FCC Catalysts
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF The Catalysts Division is the world's leading supplier of environmental and process catalysts. We develop technologies for air protection, fuel production and for efficient manufacturing of chemicals and other products, including battery materials. We help our customers to be even more successful with our knowledge of precious and base metals and our passion for innovation. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Serving as the priary technical expert for FCC catalyst applications at customer sites, providing hands-on technical support, troubleshooting, unit optimization, performance improvement, and advising refinery operations and technical teams on catalyst selection and operational strategy. Conducting FCC technical evaluations, simulations, and modeling to support recommendations, customer proposals, and performance improvement initiatives. Owning full P&L responsibility for assigned FCC accounts, including volume, margin, pricing, receivables, sales forecasting, CRM management, and account planning. Developing and executing account strategies aligned with BASF’s FCC business objectives, including pricing, contract negotiations, and commercial terms with refinery customers. Identifying and capturing new FCC business opportunities within existing and new refinery accounts, expanding account penetration through relationship mapping across technical and commercial stakeholders, and positioning BASF’s FCC catalyst portfolio to maximize market share in the region. Building strong relationships with refinery engineers, operations teams, and decision-makers, acting as the interface between customers and internal BASF technical, R&D, and business teams, and representing BASF in customer meetings, technical reviews, and industry engagements. Location: Fully remote role, ideally based in Southern Europe or the Mediterranean region to ensure proximity to key refinery customers. Territory: Responsible for refinery accounts across Southern Europe and the Mediterranean area, with a dedicated focus on FCC catalyst applications. Travel: Expected travel of approximately 30–50% to visit customer sites, support technical discussions, and strengthen business relationships across the assigned region. WHAT YOU OFFER Degree (BSc/MSc/PhD) in Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, or related discipline. Strong experience in refining with deep expertise in FCC operations and catalysts (mandatory). Proven track record in a customer-facing FCC technical or commercial role. Ability to perform FCC process analysis, modeling, and technical evaluations. Experience managing multiple refinery accounts in an international environment. Strong commercial mindset with demonstrated success in pricing and contract negotiations. Familiarity with Southern Europe / Mediterranean refining landscape. Expertise in FCC technology and refinery processes. Strong analytical, problem-solving capabilities and strategic thinking. Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. Ability to operate independently in a remote, regional role. WHAT WE OFFER CCNL (National Chemical Collective Agreement). Gross annual salary (RAL) between €70,000 and €85,000, to be assessed based on the candidate’s skills and relevant experience. Variable bonus. Corporate welfare program. Company car for both business and personal use. High-impact regional role focused purely on FCC catalysts. Combination of technical leadership and commercial ownership. Flexible remote working within Southern Europe. Opportunity to work with leading refinery customers in the region. HOW TO REACH US Do you have questions about the selection process or the position? You can contact: talent-acquisition-italy@basf.com Initial information about the application process is available here: http://on.basf.com/applicationprocess . ABOUT US Join a global FCC leader and contribute to advancing refinery performance through technical excellence and strong customer partnerships.

jobs.name: Sales Account Manager - Aftermarket and Distribution
jobs.description: Now hiring! Sales Account Manager - Aftermarket and Distribution Remote Pay Range: $113,600 - $153,000 We are looking for a Sales Account Manager to join our Performance Chemicals team remotely. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. We are seeking a Sales Account Manager - Aftermarket and Distribution, to join BASF's Finished Transportation Lubricants team and drive commercial account management for the aftermarket and distribution market channels in the US & Canada. In this role, you will lead customer strategies, manage contract negotiations and pricing, and collaborate closely with cross-functional partners to ensure an exceptional customer experience and profitable, sustainable growth. As a Sales Account Manager - Aftermarket and Distribution, you create chemistry by... Developing and implementing customer strategies that integrate commercial, technical and R&D priorities to drive profitability and strengthen partnerships. Translating customer needs into actionable insights and initiatives that drive sustainable growth with Finished Transportation Lubricant customers; which will include commodity and specialty products operating in a very complex environment Working across a broad portfolio of Transportation Lubricants, while also supporting Coolants, Brake Fluids and other specialty components under BASF Fuel and Lubricant Solutions. Partnering closely with Product Management to continuously develop and optimize pricing strategies aligned with prevailing market conditions and the Finished Transportation Lubricants business strategy. Coordinating closely with Customer Service & Supply Chain to manage new customer setups, resolve account issues, and ensure accurate invoicing and order entry; acting as the primary sales liaison for customer-related inquiries and process improvements, providing regular, data-driven demand forecasts, communicating changes in customer demand, and supporting inventory planning to meet service-level targets. Effectively utilizing business-enabling tools including CDM for forecasting, CPQ for pricing, and Salesforce for documenting customer visits, call reports, opportunities, risks, and account plans, while sharing insights cross-functionally to enhance customer steering and decision-making. Actively monitoring market trends, competitive activity, macroeconomic developments, and governmental, legislative, and regulatory factors to inform key technical and business decisions. Managing senior-level customer interfaces and cultivating long-term, strategic relationships to strengthen value creation, and customer loyalty. Providing regular contributions grounded in customer input and market insights to support a collaborative forum among the Finished Transportation Lubricants team. If you... Hold a Bachelor's degree in business, or a related commercial field; an MBA or technical MS is preferred. Bring 5+ years of industry (lubricants, automotive, and/or chemical) experience with demonstrated success in account management, sales, or commercial leadership roles. Possess strong business acumen, including pricing strategy, forecasting, pipeline management, and regional market dynamics. Communicate effectively, with strong written and verbal skills, to influence customers and cross-functional stakeholders. Demonstrate a customer-oriented mindset, strong problem-solving and negotiation skills, and experience initiating and driving new customer projects. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $113,600 - $153,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Programa de Pasantías BASF Services Americas
jobs.description: Sumate al Programa de Pasantías BASF Abrimos un nuevo período de Pasantías con foco en la formación de talento y el fortalecimiento de la continuidad operativa. Si sos estudiante y querés aprender, asumir nuevos desafíos e incorporarte a un entorno de trabajo multicultural, mientras completás tus estudios, esta oportunidad es para vos. Postulate si tenés estudios o formación en: Administración de Empresas, Recursos Humanos, Relaciones Laborales, Contador, Economía, Negocios/Relaciones Internacionales, Química, Biotecnología, Bioquímica, Ciencias biológicas, Ingeniería en Alimentos, Sistemas, Tecnologías de la Información, Telemática y afines. ¿Qué ofrecemos?: Pasantía de 6 meses de duración con posibilidad de renovación a un año. Modalidad de trabajo: híbrida, con presencialidad de una/dos veces a la semana. Remuneración acorde a las responsabilidades. Requisitos: Tener entre 18 y 25 años, y estar dando tus primeros pasos en el mundo laboral. Contar con manejo intermedio o avanzado de inglés y/o portugués. Disponibilidad para trabajar 30 horas semanales (6 horas diarias de lunes a viernes). Comienzo de Pasantías: lunes 14 de septiembre 2026. Tiempo de postulación: se recibirán postulaciones hasta el 14/08/2026. Las entrevistas se realizarán entre julio y agosto. ¡Postulate! BASF considera la Ley N° 19.691 y el Decreto N° 73/019 en todos sus procesos de contratación. Cada candidato calificado que cumpla con los requisitos establecidos en la descripción del puesto será considerado en este proceso de contratación sin distinción.Además, BASF es un empleador que ofrece igualdad de oportunidades. Aceptamos con entusiasmo nuestra responsabilidad de tomar decisiones de empleo sin tener en cuenta la raza, la edad, el estado civil y social, el origen nacional, la discapacidad, el sexo, la identidad de género o las expresiones, o cualquier otra característica o grupo de candidatos o empleados que no esté relacionado con su aptitud e idoneidad para el puesto. Nuestra dirección está comprometida a seguir esta política en lo que respecta a la contratación, colocación, promoción, transferencia, descenso, despido, terminación, reclutamiento, remuneración y otras formas de compensación, capacitación y trato general durante el empleo.

jobs.name: Representante de Desarrollo Comercial (AGRO) - Los Ríos/Los Lagos
jobs.description: Negocio La innovación aplicada al desarrollo de Soluciones para la Agricultura es parte de nuestro ADN. Con un completo portafolio de soluciones inteligentes y productos de alto valor como semillas, productos químicos y biológicos para la protección de cultivos, higiene ambiental y agricultura digital. Acompañamos al productor agropecuario para potenciar el éxito de cada una de sus campañas agrícolas. Todo esto combinando rentabilidad con sustentabilidad y responsabilidad social para contribuir a un futuro mejor. Área Ventas Agro es un área clave para el éxito de la nueva Estrategia que tiene al Cliente en el centro.  Nuestro desafío es el crecimiento constante del negocio a través de nuestros clientes. El área se encuentra en relación directa con el Productor Agropecuario, así como también con el Distribuidor Agrícola para entender y brindar soluciones integrales a sus necesidades. En Ventas Agro buscamos implementar estrategias y planes de acción a medida para cada cliente que permitan garantizar el éxito y el alcance de los objetivos de la compañía, a través de la negociación y el relacionamiento, tanto con el cliente como con diferentes áreas dentro de BASF. Candidato/a Desde nuestra área buscamos incorporar a una persona responsable de liderar la generación de demanda y el crecimiento rentable del sell out en el territorio asignado, integrando la gestión comercial y el desarrollo técnico de mercado, mediante la gestión estratégica de agricultores, asesores y distribuidores clave, el impulso del portafolio BASF y la implementación de acciones que aceleren su adopción, fortalezcan la fidelización de clientes y maximicen la captura de oportunidades de negocio. Buscamos un Ingeniero Agrónomo con sólida orientación comercial y experiencia comprobada en ventas y promoción de productos agrícolas, para asumir un rol estratégico en el desarrollo de mercado y la generación de nuevos negocios en la zona. El candidato ideal deberá contar con al menos dos años de experiencia en funciones comerciales dentro del sector agrícola, combinando conocimientos técnicos con una marcada capacidad para construir relaciones de largo plazo con productores, asesores y otros actores relevantes del mercado. Será considerado un diferencial poseer experiencia en el establecimiento y seguimiento de parcelas demostrativas, así como un profundo conocimiento de cereales y cultivos extensivos. Además, será valorado tener conocimiento acabado de los principales clientes y asesores de la zona, que le permita identificar oportunidades comerciales y contribuir activamente al crecimiento del negocio. Valoraremos profesionales con formación complementaria en áreas comerciales, agronegocios o protección de cultivos, capacidad para desenvolverse en entornos dinámicos y una actitud proactiva orientada a resultados. La licencia de conducir vigente y la disponibilidad para residir en Valdivia u Osorno son requisitos excluyentes para la posición. Buscamos una persona con excelentes habilidades de comunicación, fuerte orientación al cliente y pasión por el desarrollo de soluciones que generen valor para el productor agrícola. Nuestra química valora el talento y la diversidad de género, edad, raza, color, nacionalidad, creencias, orientación sexual, identidad y expresión de género, discapacidad y todas las demás características, diferencias y semejanzas que son parte de la diversidad humana. ¡Te invitamos a postularte a nuestras vacantes! #BASF_YoRecomiendo

jobs.name: Sr. Analyzer Engineer
jobs.description: Now hiring! Sr. Analyzer Engineer Freeport, TX We are looking for a Sr. Analyzer Engineer to join our NAF team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protecion and social responsibility. We are seeking a professional like you to serve as an Analyzer Engineer/Specialist. Laboratory Services provides the Freeport site with laboratory and process analytics in support of manufacturing and business units.  The process analyzer team is responsible for maintaining, proposing new analyzer systems and driving continuous improvement of analyzer systems.  As the Analyzer Engineer/Specialist, you will be responsible for leading process analyzer projects, identifying new process analytical technology and implementing continuous improvement initiatives. As a Sr. Analyzer Engineer, you create chemistry by... Identifying improvement opportunities and proposing and implementing sustainable solutions for laboratory and field process analytical equipment and assets by leveraging your engineering background and experience within a chemical, petrochemical, or manufacturing environment. Developing and implementing work processes that improve process analytical asset reliability, sustainability, accuracy, and maintenance costs, while providing manufacturing with key performance indicators to maximize product quality and profitability. Developing long-term process analytical improvement strategies and translating them into annual roadmaps to maintain and improve analytical reliability and asset availability for manufacturing. Applying best industry practices for maintenance, reliability activities, and work processes to lead process analytical improvements through the identification of poor technology placement and reliability issues. Providing manufacturing partners with sustainable product quality through the effective utilization of process analyzers and your expert knowledge of process analytical technology. Collaborating and negotiating with Engineering, Maintenance, and Operations personnel by providing design reviews, repair and maintenance support, and recommendations for asset and process optimization. Serving as a key contact for the North American and Global Process Analytical Technology (PAT) teams and driving overall process analytics improvements for the Freeport site. If you... Hold a Bachelor’s degree in a technical discipline with at least 7 years of related experience, an Associate’s degree with at least 10 years of related experience, or a High School Diploma/GED with at least 15 years of related experience in process analytics and instrumentation within the refining or petrochemical industries. Demonstrate a solid understanding of process safety and Instrumentation & Electrical (I&E) safety concepts. Have experience with the implementation, design, and maintenance of process analyzer technologies, including gas chromatographs, mass spectrometers, TOC, pH, viscosity, conductivity, and related analyzer systems. Have experience with Continuous Emissions Monitoring Systems (CEMS). Possess strong technical knowledge, analytical abilities, and computer skills. Demonstrate strong communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams. Are able to obtain and maintain a TWIC (Transportation Worker Identification Credential) card. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. jobs.name: Regional Product Manager - China
jobs.description:
About The Role The position of Regional Product Manager is responsible to ensure that the right product portfolio is available to contribute to our margin targets and enable growth. Main objective is to ensure a competitive advantages by focusing on localization and prioritizing and driving saving measures. Moreover, the position is the key contributor for EC (Coatings) projects. How You Will Do It - Strategic Marketing Projects: Lead marketing initiative for the Chinese automotive market focused on profitable and sustainable growth. Conduct market analysis, optimize technology portfolios, coordinate market launches and develop tailored product offerings - Pricing Management: Define and implement regional pricing policies, propose pricing actions, and ensure adherence to KAM Business Rules for margin management. -Strategy Development: Build regional strategies for different coatings layers, define milestones, and promote collaboration across countries. Drive implementation of layer strategies in China market - Support strategic RFQs and business growth initiatives in China - Cross functional Collaboration with Customization Lab, global /regional Product Management, Technical Project management, Supply Chain, and Key Account Managers to ensure short- and long-term growth and profitability improvements across all coatings layers - Localization - Evaluate & implement localization of raw materials and intermediates to generate cost savings or ensure sustainable supply - Define organic measures for complexity reduction and profitability improvement, e.g. introduction of local sourcing, toll manufacturing of finished goods (external or BCG), raw materials and intermediates, consolidation of RMs/intermediates/Finished goods technologies etc. - Collaborate with Labs & Key Accounts Management for Introduction and Implementation of new products and technology like Golden Gate or Low Bake What We Look For - A degree in Business Management will be an added advantage, preferably a postgraduate (Chemicals or related field) - At least 5 years’ experience in Automotive or Chemicals sector. Experience in Leading teams preferred - Knowledge of financial KPIs, with the ability to analyse P&L data and setup business cases - Knowledge in Coatings product formulations, applications, OEM specifications, raw materials and manufacturing process is preferred - Business travelling across the countries is required, primarily domestic. jobs.name: Global Key Account Manager - Automotive OEM
jobs.description:
Global Key Account Manager- Automotive OEM Global Key Account Manager Company Description Surventis , is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable mobility and refinish coatings, as well as surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass across many industries. We share the skills, knowledge and resources of interdisciplinary global teams through a collaborative network of sites in Europe, North America, South America and Asia Pacific. We are looking for a Global Key Account Manager to join our enthusiastic, supportive, and driven team. The position consists of managing the overall business objectives, goals, strategies, and sales responsibilities of selected strategic accounts. They will also be responsible for focusing on business development. The GKAM will be ensuring strategic and tactical objectives are achieved for the selected customers. This position will establish and maintain favorable customer relations at all engineering design level to design in technology on current and future production parts but will also be involved at the local plant level to help roll out new processes. This position will be the focal point for all customer interaction, global in scope, and will be responsible for total customer relationship management. The GKAM will also maintain high communication and involvement within the organization. Strong relationships with the global internal team (management, RD&E, customer service, plant sales, etc.) are necessary. Roles and Responsibilities: Customer relationship management to include interaction with: Management, Materials and Process Engineering, Purchasing, Design, Q C, Environmental, Etc. Coordinate product specifications globally (Ensure specs are global where relevant) Develop project & product leverage around the world Communicate needs to local directional team for new product ideas and growth Communicate with directional team on active RD&E projects with key accounts as well as, global communication Manage the profitability and growth of the accounts Develop a Value-Added sales model through cost saving program implementation and through new product introduction. Understand global cost issues relating to your account Develop strategy & tactics for key accounts to successfully accomplish financial goals, continuous improvements, and account growth Define, monitor, organize, and communicate competitive methods and other techniques/trends of assembly etc. Conducting technical presentations, Tech Expos, etc., about Chemical technology at key customer locations Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Related Field required. Seven to ten years of experience within a similar environment preferred Experience in managing large, multinational, multimillion-dollar accounts Excellent computer and database management skills Overseas business travel experience is a benefit Project management or team leadership experience Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Coordinate / Support Sales and Operation and supply chain planning activities with guidance by other team members and managers. Execute actions that contribute to achieving sales, profit, and growth targets by offering services that meet them which are in line with regional, country, and business unit(s) strategies, with guidance by other team members and managers. jobs.name: Customer Service Team Lead - SEA & Regional Order-To-Cash (OTC)
jobs.description:
Objectives of the position : 1) To lead and streamline Customer Service (CS) operations across the SEA region to ensure efficient execution of end-to-end Order-To-Cash (OTC) processes, high customer satisfaction and operational excellence. The role is responsible for overseeing customer service activities related to inter-company and third-party orders, export shipment coordination, customer complaint management, and regional process alignment following the legal entity transition. 2) To ensure end-to-end OTC process alignment with global standards while adapting to regional regulatory and operational requirements. To drive continuous improvement and best practice sharing across APAC markets. Core responsibilities : 1) To lead and manage daily Customer Service operations across the SEA region to ensure service targets and business objectives are achieved 2) To drive standardization and streamlining of Customer Service processes and workflows across SEA markets 3) To manage end-to-end order-to-cash processes including order entry, order management, delivery coordination, invoicing support and shipment tracking 4) To oversee both inter-company orders and third-party customer orders to ensure timely and accurate fulfillment 5) To coordinate export shipments and ensure compliance with shipping and trade requirements 6) To act as the key escalation point for customer complaints and ensure timely resolution through Non-Conformance Management (NCM) processes 7) To collaborate closely with Supply Chain, Logistics, Finance, Production and Sales teams to ensure smooth operational execution 8) To support Business Managers and Sales teams through proactive communication regarding order status, product availability, and delivery updates. 9) Monitor customer service KPIs and drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance customer satisfaction and operational efficiency 10) To lead, coach, mentor and develop Customer Service Specialists to strengthen team capability and engagement 11) To manage master data maintenance processes to support effective order management and operational accuracy 12) To provide accurate and timely order status reporting, monitor customers’ order placement trends against sales forecasts and proactively coordinate with Sales and Supply Chain teams to address demand fluctuations, supply risks, and delivery commitments 13) To act as the regional Subject Matter Expert (SME) for OTC processes in SAP S/4HANA, ensuring standardization and compliance across countries 14) To lead regional OTC process improvement initiatives and projects, working closely with local teams to implement, sustain, and continuously enhance processes across markets Job requirements and qualifications : Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or related discipline Minimum five (5) years of experience in Customer Service, Order Management, Supply Chain or Logistics operations Minimum two (2) years of leadership or supervisory experience managing customer service teams within a regional environment Has strong knowledge of OTC processes, export shipment coordination, and intercompany operations Experience with SAP ERP systems (SD/MM modules preferred) and Microsoft Office applications Strong communication skills, stakeholder management and problem-solving skills Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced regional environment and manage multiple priorities Experience supporting SEA regional operations and legal entity transitions will be an added advantage Experience in leading regional process improvement initiatives or system enhancement projects Has a strong analytical mindset with continuous improvement orientation [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: Senior Engineer, EP Material Testing (ID & Failure Analysis)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Take the functional leadership of the sub-team of ID Analysis (FTIR, DSC and TGA) and failure Analysis to assist lab leader of EP Material Testing lab to manage EP material testing lab, and thus to assist head of Materials & Parts Testing to provide ID analysis and failure analysis supports to Research & Development, production, sales and marketing, business development, technical research & development and strategic objectives for company’s Engineering Plastics related PM business in China and Asia Pacific in an effective way. How You Will Do It Be responsible for operation of ID analysis (FTIR, DSC and TGA) and failure analysis related part of EP Material testing lab. Review test results; monitor progress of testing requests; participate in the process changes to be made; contribute to customer on-site audit; participate in the investigation on customer complaints on EP Material mechanical tests. Lead the execution of testing tasks and make the routine maintenance plan of equipment related to ID analysis and failure analysis of EP materials. Devise tests, procedures and apparatus required to achieve experimental results, help to develop new testing methods to meet requirements, and lead the training for new employees on ID analysis and failure analysis of EP materials. Write comprehensive reports and evaluations on the data obtained relating to ID analysis and failure analysis of EP materials. Participate in the CAPEX plan relating to ID analysis and failure analysis of EP material and execute the plan accordingly. Oversee the new equipment purchase for related tests and contact from the negotiation period of the deal. Contribute to the Asia Pacific part of global testbox program and the discussion for improve testing procedures relating to ID analysis and failure analysis. Contribute to the Asia Pacific part of testing harmonization and the global testing harmonization relating to ID analysis and failure analysis of EP materials. Contribute to the management of external resource of EP material testing relating to ID analysis and failure analysis, conduct external resource screening, audit, negotiation and monitoring. Co-operate with development/technical service/manufacturing/sales & marketing in providing technical assistance to demonstrate company products, and to solve field problems. Contribute to safety, health and environmental standards of EP & FR lab. Coordinate to maintain EP Material Testing lab to fulfil Quality Management System (ISO/IEC 17025, ISO 9001, ISO/TS 16949, etc.). What We Look For Education: Master or Bachelor in polymer chemistry, analytics chemistry, material science or other polymer or chemistry related. Working Experience: Master with more than 5 years’ experience or bachelor with more than 8 years’ experience in material analysis (more than 3 years’ experience on FTIR, DSC and TGA of EP materials) and material testing of polymer materials. Over 3 years’ supervisor or leadership experience. Over one year’s ISO 17025 experience will be plus. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Knowledge and hand-on experience on material analysis and material testing of polymer materials. Ability to deliver technical presentations in Mandarin and English. Ability to write comprehensive technical report by English. Knowledge on ISO 17025 system management will be a plus. Language: Ability to communicate with written and spoken English smoothly. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用

jobs.description: Account Manager Personal Care (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Account Manager Personal Care (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Als europäische Vertriebsorganisation der BASF-Gruppe vertreibt BTC Europe hochveredelte Spezial- und Feinchemikalien an internationale Kunden in zahlreichen Branchen. Wir profitieren von unserer Kundennähe, unserer Flexibilität, unserer langjährigen Erfahrung und dem geballten Know-how des BASF-Verbunds. WAS SIE ERWARTET Unser Vertriebsteam im Bereich Kosmetik braucht Verstärkung: im Rahmen einer Nachbesetzung suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen Account Manager, der unsere Kunden (vorwiegend in Deutschland) betreut und Lust auf Außendienst hat. Der Reiseanteil ist für die Stelle sehr wichtig und umfasst mindestens 50%. Ein Firmenwagen wird gestellt. Abgesehen von ein paar fixen Terminen, wie z.B. Messebesuche, können d ie Termine eigenständig koordiniert werden. Sie entwickeln und setzen Kundenpläne im Einklang mit der Gesamtstrategie um, treiben aktiv das Neugeschäft voran und nutzen Cross-Selling-Möglichkeiten. Die Analyse von Markt- und Wettbewerbertrends, die Umsetzung von Erkenntnissen in Wachstumschancen sowie die Unterstützung bei der Einführung neuer Produkte und Anwendungen gehören zu Ihren Aufgaben. Sie verwalten Ihr Kundenportfolio mit Fokus auf Wachstums- und Finanzziele, bauen starke Beziehungen zu wichtigen Stakeholdern auf und sorgen für eine transparente Dokumentation in Salesforce. Darüber hinaus übernehmen Sie die Verantwortung für das Preismanagement, bewerben das gesamte Portfolio und leiten Kundenverhandlungen. Als zentrale Schnittstelle koordinieren Sie interne und externe Stakeholder, sorgen für reibungslose kundenorientierte Prozesse und arbeiten eng mit lokalen und europäischen Teams zusammen, während Sie zur Digitalisierung beitragen und Ihr Fachwissen kontinuierlich weiterentwickeln. jobs.name: Industriemechaniker:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Industriemechaniker:in (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Industriemechaniker:in (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir bei Care Chemicals handeln aus Leidenschaft. Unsere Mitarbeiter:innen sind unser wertvollstes Gut und wir pflegen eine Kultur der Empathie, der Unterstützung und des Wachstums. Auf diese Weise befähigen wir unser Team, sich persönlich und beruflich weiterzuentwickeln. Mit vereinten Kräften engagieren wir uns für die Entwicklung nachhaltiger, digitaler und innovativer Lösungen für eine bessere Zukunft. Pflegende Hautcremes, milde Shampoos, Sonnenschutzmittel, schonende und umweltfreundliche Waschmittel und leistungsstarke Reiniger für Haushalt und Industrie – all diese Produkte nutzen Inhaltsstoffe aus unserem Bereich Care Chemicals. Darüber hinaus bieten wir beispielsweise Lösungen auf dem Gebiet des Pflanzenschutzes, für technische Anwendungen in der Bauchemie oder Rohstoffe für die Lackindustrie sowie digitale Services für unsere Kunden. Weltweit arbeiten wir gemeinsam mit unseren Kunden an innovativen Lösungen, die Endverbraucher:innen und industrielle Formulierer überzeugen – und das zunehmend auf Basis von natürlichen und nachhaltig gewonnenen Rohstoffen. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam eine bessere Zukunft entwerfen! WAS SIE ERWARTET Die Einheit Instandhaltung ist im Werk Grenzach für die Störungsbehebung, die Inspektion und Wartung sowie für Anlagenänderungen an Produktions- und Infrastrukturanlagen verantwortlich. Sie sichern den Zustand der Anlagen durch fachgerechte und kostentransparente Durchführung von Reparaturarbeiten und Störungsbehebungen. Sie führen eigenverantwortlich, wie auch im Team Störungsbeseitigungen, Prüfungen und Wartungstätigkeiten durch, dokumentieren diese in SAP und halten dabei alle relevanten Qualitäts-, Sicherheits-, Gesundheits- sowie Umweltvereinbarungen ein. Die Koordination von verschiedenen Gewerken und Beschaffung von internen und externen Materialen gehören zu Ihren Aufgaben. Des Weiteren begleiten Sie Projekte in den Bereichen Anlagenoptimierung, -änderung sowie an Neuanlagen. Sie arbeiten im Tagesdienst und nehmen an der Rufbereitschaft teil, um die Verfügbarkeit der Produktions- und Infrastruktureinrichtungen rund um die Uhr zu gewährleisten. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Anlagen- oder Industriemechaniker:in oder mit einem vergleichbaren technischen Schwerpunkt Erfahrung in der Instandhaltung verfahrenstechnischer Anlagen sowie Kenntnisse der Betriebs- und Verfahrenstechnik und im Sicherheits-, Gesundheits- und Umweltschutz versierter Umgang mit den MS Office Anwendungen Ambitionen zur kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung (intern als auch extern) sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (C1) WAS WIR BIETEN attraktive Vergütung: 44.000 - 57.000 € Bruttojahresgehalt, je nach Qualifikation und Berufserfahrung sowie ein variabler Bonus, der sich am Geschäftserfolg und Ihrer individuellen Leistung orientiert Rufbereitschaftsdienst wird zusätzlich vergütet 30 Urlaubstage, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle sowie je nach Tätigkeit die Möglichkeit mobiler Arbeit Sozialberatung, Unterstützung bei der Altersvorsorge, Gesundheitsförderungsangebote (Hansefit) und Aktienprogramm für Mitarbeitende ein sicheres Arbeitsumfeld, da wir Sicherheit immer Vorrang geben und Ihre Gesundheit an erster Stelle steht Ihr Beitrag für eine lebenswerte Zukunft, denn Nachhaltigkeit ist der Kern unseres Handelns und Wachstumstreiber SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Corinna Granterath (Talent Acquisition), corinna.granterath@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59302 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen (fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden). Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier ÜBER UNS An ihrem Standort in Grenzach, dem BASF-Kompetenzzentrum für UV-Filter, betreibt die BASF Grenzach GmbH die globale Entwicklung, den Technischen Service sowie die Produktion von innovativen UV-Filtern. Unsere Produkte kommen in Sonnencremes, in Kosmetika und Körperpflegeprodukten zum Einsatz. Am Standort befindet sich zudem die globale Entwicklung und der Technische Service von antimikrobiellen Wirkstoffen. Die einzigartige Lage des Unternehmens im Dreiländereck Deutschland, Frankreich und der Schweiz mit der Nähe zum Schwarzwald und den Alpen bietet neben den beruflichen interessanten Möglichkeiten auch attraktive und vielfältige Freizeitaktivitäten. Mehr über die BASF Grenzach GmbH erfahren Sie unter: www.basf-grenzach.de Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! jobs.name: Customer Service Officer, SEA
jobs.description:
Objectives of the position: The Customer Service Officer, SEA is responsible for executing end-to-end customer service and order management activities to ensure efficient and accurate order fulfillment, export shipment coordination and high customer satisfaction across the SEA region. The role supports both inter-company and third-party customer operations while ensuring compliance with trade regulations, operational procedures and customer service standards. Core responsibilities : 1) To execute end-to-end Order-To-Cash (OTC) processes including order entry, order processing, delivery coordination, invoicing support and shipment tracking 2) To manage both inter-company orders and third-party customer orders to ensure timely and accurate fulfillment 3) To coordinate export shipments including shipping documentation, booking arrangements and delivery follow-up activities 4) To maintain proactive communication with customers regarding order status, product availability, shipment schedules and delivery updates 5) To perform regular checks on customer orders, pricing, credit blocks and compliance requirements within SAP systems 6) To ensure compliance with export control regulations, trade compliance requirements and company operational procedures 7) To coordinate closely with Supply Chain, Logistics, Warehouse, Finance and Sales teams to ensure smooth operational execution 8) To support resolution of customer complaints and raise Non-Conformance Management (NCM) cases where required, ensuring timely follow-up and closure with relevant stakeholders 9) To monitor order fulfillment performance and support continuous improvement initiatives to improve service efficiency and customer satisfaction 10) To maintain accurate master data, customer information, and operational documentation within SAP systems 11) To support preparation and verification of shipping and export documents including invoices, packing lists, bills of lading and insurance documentation 12) To act as backup support for other Customer Service team members during leave or absence periods to ensure business continuity and uninterrupted customer support operations Job requirements and qualifications : Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or related discipline Minimum 2–4 years of experience in Customer Service, Order Management, Logistics or Supply Chain operations Experience in export shipment coordination and order management within a regional environment is preferred Has a strong knowledge of Order-To-Cash processes, export shipment coordination and customer service operations Experience with SAP ERP systems (SD/MM modules preferred) and Microsoft Office applications Good communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment with strong attention to detail and accuracy Good knowledge of shipping documentation, trade complianc, and export processes will be an advantage Strong problem-solving mindset with customer-focused approach Ability to manage multiple priorities and work collaboratively with cross-functional teams Position may be based in Singapore, Thailand or Vietnam (on local terms) . [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . BASF Group operates this platform solely as a technical service provider under a 
 Transitional Service Agreement following the divestment of the Coatings division, 
 effective July 1st 2026, now Surventis. The employer and contracting party for the advertised positions is exclusively . alone is responsible for the content of the job adverts and the 
 decision regarding applications. Data Protection The processing of your personal data is carried out by as the controller 
 within the meaning of any applicable data privacy laws. BASF Group acts as a technical service provider in this respect and processes 
 data exclusively on behalf of . Further information can be found in the privacy policy coatings privacy policy incl. Contact details .

Zeit für Deinen Start in die Praxis. Unsere Ausbildung im Industriepark Lausitz macht Dich fit für die Zukunft! Denn hier hast Du an einem Standort viele Möglichkeiten und lernst von Profis, die ihr Know-how in verschiedenen Bereichen und Unternehmen einbringen. Bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz arbeitest Du mit moderner Technik, bist mittendrin statt nur dabei und lernst Skills, die Dir wirklich was bringen. Keine trockene Theorie – echte Praxis, echtes Team, echte Zukunft. Starte Deine Ausbildung bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz – und profitiere von einem Erfahrungsschatz, der weit über einen Betrieb hinausgeht. Your move. Your future. DAS LERNST DU Während Deiner Ausbildung zum/zur Chemikant:in bist Du bei der Produktion vieler chemischer Erzeugnisse mittendrin statt nur dabei und bekommst einen genauen Einblick in die spannende Produktwelt der BASF! Du lernst unseren Fertigungsprozess für unterschiedliche chemische Produkte, z.B. Technische Kunststoffe, Schaumstoffe, Veredlungschemikalien, von A bis Z kennen. Dabei sorgst Du für die richtige Menge an Rohstoffen, fährst die Anlagen an und überwachst Druck, Temperatur und mehr. Direkt an der Maschine oder vom Computer aus überwachst Du den Produktionsprozess und meldest Abweichungen. Nicht zuletzt sorgst Du für die Einhaltung von hohen Standards bei Umweltschutz und Arbeitssicherheit. Egal, was Du bei uns herstellst, die Qualität der Produkte steht im Mittelpunkt Deiner Ausbildung. Zeit für Deinen Start in die Praxis. Unsere Ausbildung im Industriepark Lausitz macht Dich fit für die Zukunft! Denn hier hast Du an einem Standort viele Möglichkeiten und lernst von Profis, die ihr Know-how in verschiedenen Bereichen und Unternehmen einbringen. Bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz arbeitest Du mit moderner Technik, bist mittendrin statt nur dabei und lernst Skills, die Dir wirklich was bringen. Keine trockene Theorie – echte Praxis, echtes Team, echte Zukunft. Starte Deine Ausbildung bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz – und profitiere von einem Erfahrungsschatz, der weit über einen Betrieb hinausgeht. Your move. Your future. DAS LERNST DU Am liebsten hast Du alles immer ganz automatisch? Während Deiner Ausbildung zum/zur Elektroniker:in Automatisierungstechnik (m/w/d) wirst Du selbst zum Profi für Automatisierung. Du lernst unsere rechnergesteuerten Produktionsanlagen, z.B. für Technische Kunststoffe, Schaumstoffe und Pflanzenschutzmittel einzurichten. Dabei programmierst Du Prozessabläufe und packst in der Instandhaltung und Inbetriebnahme der Anlagen mit an. Du installierst elektrische Betriebsmittel, überwachst zudem die Regelkreise und stößt Optimierungen an. Du erfasst alle Maßnahmen und instandhaltungsrelevanten Daten. Sollte manchmal etwas nicht funktionieren, erweckst Du unsere Anlagen wieder zum Leben und weist Kolleginnen und Kollegen in die Nutzung ein. DAS BIETEN WIR eine persönliche Ansprechperson während Deiner gesamten Ausbildungszeit Ausbildungsvergütung: 1. Jahr: 1.115 € / 2. Jahr: 1.173 € / 3. Jahr 1.196 € / 4. Jahr 1.257 € 30 Tage Urlaub bei einer 38,5-Stunden Woche zahlreiche Zusatzleistungen wie Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld, Erfolgsbeteiligung, Betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme am Aktienprogramm und Sportangebote Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme an Azubiaustauschprogrammen finanzielle Unterstützung für Azubis von weiter weg Ausbildung findet in Kooperation mit unserem Verbundpartner TÜV Rheinland in Brieske statt DAS BRINGST DU MIT eine gute Mittlere Reife oder ein höherer Schulabschluss Spaß an Themen rund um Physik, Programmierung, Automatisierung, Robotik und im Umgang mit Daten technisches Interesse und ein motiviertes Auftreten analytisches Denken und eine zuverlässige Arbeitsweise AUSBILDUNG GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN? Als Automatisierungsathlet:in und Technikträumer:in sind Dir während Deiner Ausbildung einige Lichter aufgegangen - und diese solltest Du nicht unter den Scheffel stellen. Nachdem Du bei uns das vielfältige Feld der Elektrotechnik und Automatisierung beleuchtet hast, bist Du zweifellos bereit für große Aufgaben. Vielleicht bringt Dich eine Weiterbildung zum/zur Industriemeister:in oder zum/zur Techniker:in Elektrotechnik zum Strahlen? KONTAKT Du hast Fragen zu der Stelle? Wende Dich an Frank Porthun, Tel.: +49 1743481235, frank.porthun@basf.com . DAS LERNST DU Während Deiner Ausbildung als Industriemechaniker:in wirst Du ein echter Mechanik-Profi und sorgst für unser Herzstück – unsere Anlagen. Dank Dir läuft die Produktion von Komponenten für Schaumstoffe, Pflanzenschutzmitteln und Technischen Kunststoffen reibungslos. Ohne Dich gäbe es keine Maschinerie! Als Industriemechaniker:in hältst Du unsere Maschinen auf Stand und sorgst so für eine störungsfreie Produktion. Dazu montierst Du Baugruppen und technische Systeme und überwachst anschließend die Inbetriebnahme. Bei Bedarf stellst Du Werkstücke und Maschinenbauteile selbst her und bedienst dazu die entsprechenden Werkzeugmaschinen. Du erfasst alle Maßnahmen und instandhaltungsrelevanten Daten. Nicht zuletzt führst Du regelmäßig Funktionskontrollen durch und berätst Deine Kolleginnen und Kollegen in technischen Fragen. DAS BIETEN WIR eine persönliche Ansprechperson während Deiner gesamten Ausbildungszeit Ausbildungsvergütung: 1. Jahr: 1.192 € / 2. Jahr: 1.254 € / 3. Jahr: 1.279 € / 4. Jahr: 1.344 € 30 Tage Urlaub bei einer 38,5-Stunden Woche vergünstigtes Jobticket zahlreiche Zusatzleistungen wie Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld, Erfolgsbeteiligung, Betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme am Aktienprogramm und Sportangebote finanzielle Unterstützung für Azubis von weiter weg Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme an Azubiaustauschprogrammen Ausbildung findet in Kooperation mit unserem Verbundpartner TÜV Rheinland in Brieske statt DAS BRINGST DU MIT eine gute Mittlere Reife Spaß am Lösen von praktisch-technischen Problemstellungen handwerkliches Geschick und räumliches Vorstellungsvermögen analytisches Denken und eine zuverlässige Arbeitsweise AUSBILDUNG GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN? Nachdem Du das nötige Handwerkszeug erlernt hast, konstruierst Du deine Karriere als Maschinenversteher:in in einem unserer Unternehmensbereiche. Du inspizierst dort die Schwachstellen und wandelst die Maschinen zu einsatzfähigen Kraftpaketen um. Damit wirst Du ein unverzichtbares Bauteil in der komplexen Maschinerie im weltweit führenden Chemieunternehmen. Außerdem erwarten Dich interessante Entwicklungs- und Weiterbildungschancen, wie z.B. zum/zur Meister: in oder Techniker:in. KONTAKT Du hast Fragen zu der Stelle? jobs.name: Assistente Técnico Pleno
jobs.description:
Assistente Técnico Pleno - Curitiba PR Objetivo do cargo: Prestar suporte técnico aos clientes, assegurando a correta utilização dos sistemas de repintura automotiva das linhas Glasurit e Baslac, promovendo a na aplicação dos produtos, o aumento da produtividade das oficinas e a satisfação dos clientes. Sobre a vaga: Realizar instalação, ativação e acompanhamento de sistemas de pintura nas oficinas de repintura automotiva. Ministrar treinamentos técnicos sobre produtos, processos de aplicação e melhores práticas das linhas Glasurit e Baslac. Realizar visitas técnicas periódicas para acompanhamento da performance dos clientes. Prestar suporte técnico para resolução de problemas relacionados à preparação, aplicação, secagem, acabamento e colorimetria. Elaborar relatórios técnicos de visitas e registrar planos de ação para acompanhamento dos clientes. Auxiliar no desenvolvimento técnico de novos clientes e na implantação de oficinas. Suportar equipes comerciais em demonstrações técnicas e apresentações de produtos. Acompanhar indicadores de consumo, produtividade e qualidade dos processos de pintura. Garantir a correta utilização dos softwares de gerenciamento de cores e formulação. Zelar pela correta utilização dos equipamentos fornecidos aos clientes. Atuar como multiplicador de conhecimento técnico, promovendo treinamentos internos e externos quando necessário. Conhecimentos indispensáveis: Processos completos de repintura automotiva. Preparação de superfície. Aplicação de primers, bases, vernizes e acabamentos. Colorimetria e ajuste de cores. Polimento e acabamento. Equipamentos de pintura. Sistemas de mistura. Interpretação de fichas técnicas e boletins técnicos. Pacote Office. Formação obrigatória: Superior ou técnico completo em áreas correlatas com a função Nossos benefícios Assistência médica e odontológica Sul América Previdência Privada Gympass Programas de bem estar Flexibilidade de horário Refeição Seguro de vida Auxílio creche Programa Gestante Carro corporativo [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: Jefe de Aplicación Detalles del puesto
jobs.description:
Jefe de Aplicación Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Encuentra trabajos Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Perfil Ingreso para empleados Descripción del puesto Jefe de Aplicación LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Enviar candidatura ahora En BASF, somos una empresa que otorga igualdad de oportunidades. Todos los y las candidatas cualificadas que cubran el perfil para una posición se considerarán para nuestras vacantes sin distinción de raza, edad, ciudadanía, tono de piel, religión, sexo, estado civil, nacionalidad, discapacidad, identidad o expresión de género, orientación sexual, condición social o condiciones de salud, prohibiendo solicitar certificado de no embarazo, exámenes médicos; de gravidez y de VIH; así como cualquier otra característica o condición análoga protegida por la ley del país correspondiente. En BASF, estamos comprometidos con la inclusión, proporcionando siempre que sea posible, ajustes razonables a las personas con discapacidad o con alguna necesidad específica, para garantizar su acceso y el uso de servicios.  En caso de requerir algún apoyo, favor de comunicarlo durante tu primer contacto con Atracción de Talento. OBJETIVO GENERAL: Controlar y garantizar el funcionamiento de las cabinas en proceso de aplicación, de acuerdo a los parámetros establecidos en el plan de control, con el fin de garantizar la liberación de los productos de acuerdo a plan. FUNCIONES & RESPONSABILIDADES CLAVE 1.-Elaborar y coordinar procesos y procedimientos del área de aplicación, coordinando auditorías internas o externas de acuerdo con los sistemas de calidad y ambiental, con el fin de garantizar el cumplimiento de los requerimientos de los clientes. 2.-Garantizar la disposición y actualización de equipo de aplicación en el área de calidad, a través del programa de mantenimiento preventivo y correctivo para asegurar la liberación de paneles de acuerdo al plan de control. 3.-Establecer planes de apoyo técnico continuo que responden a la demanda de actividades del área de aplicación, mediante la coordinación de personal, insumos y equipos, asegurando el servicio de cabinas a los clientes internos y externos. 4.-Evaluar el cumplimiento de los objetivos relacionados con las competencias técnicas del equipo de aplicación, en conjunto con el líder de turno de laboratorio, con el fin de diseñar acciones que aseguren el desarrollo de las competencias. 5.-Establecer y estandarizar nuevos sistemas para el proceso de aplicación, a través de la participación en grupos globales de especialistas con la finalidad de optimizar tiempos en el proceso. 6.-Asesorar al laboratorio técnico en el proceso de aplicación para la introducción de nuevos colores, con el fin de asegurar una adaptación que cumpla con los requerimientos de fórmula NFI (New Formula Introduction). 7.-Garantizar que el proceso de correlación en cabinas corresponda al proceso de aplicación de la línea del cliente, a través de pruebas físicas de aplicación para asegurar la aprobación de los productos. 8.-Determinar la estrategia de operación del equipo de aplicación, a través del análisis de resultados (número de aplicaciones, FTQ) con el fin de optimizar tiempos en el proceso. 9.-Asegurar el cumplimiento de los procedimientos y prácticas de seguridad, establecidos por la Compañía, con el fin de evitar accidentes y contribuir a los objetivos del negocio. Administrar el presupuesto del área, con el fin de garantizar el cumplimiento de acuerdo a plan y necesidades del negocio. 10.-Administrar el presupuesto del área, con el fin de garantizar el cumplimiento de acuerdo a plan y necesidades del negocio. 11.- Cumplir con los procedimientos y las prácticas de Calidad (IATF-16949), protección ambiental (ISO-14001) y Seguridad e Higiene, con el fin de contribuir con los objetivos del negocio, la satisfacción de nuestros clientes y la prevención de la contaminación. 12.- Cumplir con las funciones y responsabilidades de Ecología, Higiene y Seguridad establecidas para la posición desempeñada, de acuerdo con el procedimiento P.T. 66-33 Funciones y Responsabilidades de EHS en Sitio Tultitlán. CONOCIMIENTOS / EXPERIENCIA Inglés avanzado Indispensable Aplicación electrostática y manual - Avanzado Procesos de aplicación de pintura en línea - Avanzado Funcionamiento mecánico y eléctrico de equipos de aplicación - Avanzado Interpretación de resultados de aplicación - Avanzado Mantenimientos de campanas y pistolas - Avanzado Control estadístico y Métodos de prueba - Avanzado [[LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ]] Notice regarding responsibility and data processing The job offers published on this platform relate exclusively to positions at . jobs.name: SAP Developer (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities : You will be responsible for ensuring the operation and project delivery across all phases You will do error analysis and corrections of Incident and Problems (Provide 2nd and/or 3rd level support). As part of our agile development team you will work on user stories, do the development and testing of new features. With your extensive experience on the subject matter, you will guide, coach and train other team members build competencies for the respective modules. You will be assigned projects where you will have end to end responsibility for all deliverables. Qualifications: Core technical profile with 4+ years SAP development and integration experience Application development experience in S/4HANA, Technical knowledge in OO ABAP, BAPI and CDS, cloud compliant development Preferably with experiences / knowledge with, integration patterns (e.g. APIs) OData services, optional BTP/RAP Preferable functional knowledge of SAP S/4 FI/CO Profession / Occupation / Studies : Completed university degree in Business IT, Business Economics, Information Science, Engineering, or Natural Science Years of experience : 3/6 WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Aerospace Industry Sales Specialist
jobs.description:
Industry Sales Specialist - Aerospace Company Description Surventis , is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable mobility and refinish coatings, as well as surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass across many industries. We share the skills, knowledge and resources of interdisciplinary global teams through a collaborative network of sites in Europe, North America, South America and Asia Pacific. Roles and Responsibilities: Account and relationship management Take ownership of the assigned account(s), sales, and pricing processes according to the company’s pricing guidelines and marketing pricing strategy for the industry. Develop and maintain accuracy of assigned customers to include organizational Power Map- and decision-making hierarchy. Responsible for ensuring all assigned account information is loaded into CRM (Salesforce) and is accurate and up to date. Negotiate and close sales deals with the client(s). Support Key Industry Sales Manager, GSM and Regional Marketing in developing pricing strategy Generate and manage commercial and technical approvals Prepare and manage bid/tender offers Assist the Key Industry Sales Manager and field sales in preparing and negotiating contracts. Maintain close business relationships with key decision makers of assigned accounts. Represent Chemetall in on-site visits and key meetings of assigned accounts. Identify new application and/or service needs within the industry and region Customer development activities Align account targets together with the Key Industry Sales Manager for assigned accounts. Continuously analyze and keep track of sales and identify up-/cross-selling opportunities within the account using Salesforce Understand technical and managerial requirements of the customer(s) Uncover and offer new products tailored to the customer’s needs Keep track of competitors’ offers and actions, initiate counteractions Engage clients with marketing & communication initiatives, e.g., events Other accountabilities and admin Advise Regional Marketing Manager on operational key account(s) management Advise Regional sales team on target accounts’ activities Support development of sales-related trainings Share market insights and trends with Regional Marketing management Provides input into industry segment strategy Requirements: Bachelor’s degree: Engineering, Chemistry or Business Administration preferred Knowledge of surface treatment 5 - 7 years of experience in Aerospace Familiar with entire product portfolio including sealants and CICs Experience with Airframe OEM and subcontractors Success in key account management Demonstrated consistent sales achievement Sales acumen (account & relationship management, value-based selling, etc.) Strategic thinking skills Strong cross-functional collaboration skills Organizational awareness Strong written and oral communication skills This position can be remote but needs to be located near a major airport: 50% travel required We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. jobs.name: Technical Account Manager - Refinery Catalysts (m/f/d)
jobs.description: The world needs innovations, and our team needs you! BASF is the world's leading chemical company #WeCreateChemistry and one of the best places to work and develop your career. We are looking for diverse, passionate, and creative talent who wants to make a difference. BASF Española S.L. markets more than 5,000 products to meet the needs of our customers and society across a wide variety of sectors. The chemical industry is changing; and so is BASF. Discover our new strategy: #WinningWays Do you want to boost your career to the next level and create chemistry with us? WHAT YOU WILL DO By joining BASF’s Catalysts division, with exclusive focus on Fluid Catalytic Cracking (FCC) applications within refinery customers across Southern Europe and the Mediterranean region, you will be: Serving as the primary technical expert for FCC catalyst applications at customer sites. Providing hands-on technical support, including troubleshooting, unit optimization, and performance improvement. Conducting FCC technical evaluations, simulations, and modeling to support recommendations and proposals. Advising refinery operations and technical teams on catalyst selection, performance, and operational strategy. Owning full P&L responsibility for assigned FCC accounts (volume, margin, pricing, receivables). Developing and executing account strategies aligned with BASF’s FCC business objectives. Managing pricing, contract negotiations, and commercial terms with refinery customers. Delivering accurate sales forecasts and managing CRM tools for account planning. Identifying and capturing new FCC business opportunities within existing and new refinery accounts. Expanding account penetration through relationship mapping across technical and commercial stakeholders. Positioning BASF’s FCC catalyst portfolio to maximize market share in the region. Building strong relationships with refinery engineers, operations teams, and decision-makers. Acting as the interface between customers and internal BASF technical, R&D, and business teams. Representing BASF in customer meetings, technical reviews, and industry engagements. WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU University degree (BSc, MSc or PhD) in Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, or related disciplines. Strong background (at least 5 years of experience) in refining with deep expertise in FCC operations and catalysts. Proven track record in a customer-facing FCC technical or commercial role with demonstrated success in pricing and contract negotiations. Ability to perform FCC process analysis, modeling, and technical evaluations. Familiarity with Southern Europe / Mediterranean refining landscape. Strong communication skills in English, both verbal and written. Knowledge on any Mediterranean language is a plus. Availability to travel ~30–50% of your working time. WHAT WE OFFER Healthy work environment : where your safety and health (both physical and mental) are always our priority. Attractive salary package : based on a fixed salary and a bonus based on performance. Flexible working model : you will be able to work remotely within the country and adjust your schedule. Flexible compensation plan : to take advantage of ser vices such as Health Insurance, Childcare, Transportation Card, and Training, among others. Professional development : we promote continuous training and access to internal vacancies to keep growing at BASF. Investments for your future: with a Pension Plan, Accident Insurance, Life Insurance, and even the opportunity to participate in the Stock Program after one year in the company. Work-life balance measures : as well as financial assistance for the schooling of children. Sports sections : where you can connect with colleagues and play football, paddle, basketball, cycling, yoga, etc. And much more. HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE PROCESS Send your CV through the job offer so we can get to know each other. If your profile is a match, we will call you from the Spanish Talent Acquisition team in Germany (prefix +49) and support you throughout the process. With each step, you will be closer to meeting your future team! If you have any further questions about this process, please contact: talent-acquisition-spain@basf.com * *Please note that we will not be able to accept your resume via this email. MORE ABOUT BASF We celebrate diversity and actively promote an inclusive work environment where every voice is heard, and every idea is valued, regardless of age, gender, marital status, ethnicity, religion, physical/mental condition, or sexual orientation. #BeYourself We are C reative, O pen, R esponsible and E ntrepreneurial people. This are o ur values , they guide us and describe who we are and how we act. If you identify with them, BASF is the place for you! Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.
