Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role -    Development of new application and/or new products based on business needs -    Technical support for Asia-Pacific customers, sales and product managers -    Support technology team with plants production troubleshoot, process optimization and analytics How You Will Do It -    Carry out lab innovation projects independently and update project status timely, including literature study, lab trial setup and support patents preparation (where necessary) -    Lead and support new products and new application development and launch -    Proper documentation of all activities, compile testing results and share report -    Support customer projects (on-site/ off-site) independently and update project status timely -    Work with country sales team and regional marketing teams to support business needs for new application development, product analysis, NCM and product training -    Work with technology team to support plant optimization process -    Ensure BASF safety standards are adhered What We Look For -    Bachelor's Degree or above, major in Chemistry, Polymer Science, Material Science -    At least 2 years working experience is preferred, ideally in PU, Epoxy, Coatings, Metalworking fluids -    Knowledge in laboratory techniques such as chromatography, spectroscopy, and titration -    Excellent communication skills, team player who is able to interact in multicultural & multi functional environment -    Good command of English and mandarin in speaking and written -    Strong analytical, project management, problem-solving, technical background -    Good knowledge of Microsoft Office

jobs.name: Lead Ingenieur:in Prozessleitsysteme (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Lead Ingenieur:in Prozessleitsysteme (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Lead Ingenieur:in Prozessleitsysteme (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Die BASF InfraService & Solutions Lausitz GmbH gestaltet aktiv die Strukturen und Abläufe im Industriepark Lausitz. Über 900 engagierte Mitarbeitende arbeiten hier täglich daran, Technik zu verstehen, Prozesse effizient zu gestalten und gemeinsam mit starken Partnern neue Maßstäbe zu setzen. Als Standortbetreibergesellschaft legen wir großen Wert auf kundenorientierte Services und maßgeschneiderte Lösungen – mit dem Ziel, nicht nur den Erfolg unserer Kunden zu sichern, sondern auch Ihre persönliche Weiterentwicklung aktiv zu fördern. Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – werden Sie Teil unseres Teams! WAS SIE ERWARTET Unser interdisziplinäres Team Automation & Digitalization Excellence treibt die digitale Transformation an einem unserer größten Produktionsstandorte voran. Wir implementieren innovative digitale Lösungen und automatisieren reale Produktionsprozesse. Dadurch schaffen wir einen erlebbaren Mehrwert für unsere Kunden. Als Führungskraft leiten Sie ein Team aus Fachingenieur:innen und steuern sowohl operative als auch strategische Aufgaben im Bereich Automatisierung und Digitalisierung. Sie fördern und befähigen Ihre Mitarbeitenden durch gezielte Motivation, Coaching und die Weiterentwicklung individueller Stärken. Darüber hinaus planen, priorisieren und koordinieren Sie komplexe Automatisierungsprojekte unter technischen und wirtschaftlichen Gesichtspunkten und entwickeln gemeinsam mit Ihrem Team geeignete Lösungsstrategien. Sie koordinieren die Schnittstellen zwischen IT und OT, Engineering, Produktion sowie externen Dienstleistern und stellen eine klare und effiziente Abstimmung sicher. Zudem verantworten Sie die Steuerung, Qualitätskontrolle und Abnahme anspruchsvoller Leistungen externer Partner. Sie treiben die konzeptionelle Weiterentwicklung des Fachgebiets Steuerung und Prozessleittechnik voran und fördern aktiv den Wissenstransfer sowie die Etablierung innovativer und digitaler Arbeitsweisen im Team. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Sie bringen nicht alles mit? Keine Sorge, wir suchen nach Persönlichkeiten, die etwas bewegen und unser gemeinsames Arbeitsumfeld gestalten möchten! abgeschlossenes Master- oder Diplomstudium in Automatisierungs-, Elektro-, Informations- oder Prozessleittechnik oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im industriellen Umfeld sowie fundierte Kenntnisse im Bereich industrieller Steuerungs- und Prozessleitsysteme Erfahrung in der Projektleitung sowie in der fachlichen oder disziplinarischen Führung von Mitarbeitenden von Vorteil ausgeprägte Fähigkeit zur strukturierten Analyse komplexer Aufgabenstellungen sowie zur Entwicklung kreativer und lösungsorientierter Strategien sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse sowie ausgeprägte Motivation zur Führung und Weiterentwicklung interdisziplinärer Teams sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeit im Umgang mit Mitarbeitenden, externen Partnern und Kunden WAS WIR BIETEN Sie sind vom ersten Tag an Teil unseres Teams, in dem MITEINANDER groß geschrieben wird und wir Herausforderungen gemeinsam meistern. Leben Sie Kreativität: Wir fördern außergewöhnliche Ideen und geben Ihnen Raum zu wachsen. Sie erhalten ein attraktives Vergütungspaket (u.a. marktgerechtes Grundgehalt, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Unfallversicherung, 30 Tage Urlaub, Möglichkeit zusätzlicher freier Tage). Ergänzend dazu erwartet Sie ein vom Unternehmensergebnis und von der individuellen Leistung abhängiger Bonus sowie die Möglichkeit zur Unternehmensbeteiligung durch den Erwerb von Aktien. Eine optimale Work-Life-Balance ist durch flexible Arbeitszeiten und anteiligem mobilen Arbeiten möglich. Zusätzlich bieten wir moderne Arbeitsplätze in Co-Working Atmosphäre in Dresden. Sie erhalten Unterstützung bei Ihrer persönlichen Entwicklung entsprechend der Lebensphase in einem kreativen und internationalen Team. Wir halten Sie fit durch umfangreiche Gesundheitsprogramme, die Möglichkeit zur Nutzung eines Jobrades sowie unseren Werksärztlichen Dienst vor Ort. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Nina Schülzke (Talent Acquisition), nina.schuelzke@basf.com , Tel. +49 30 2005-58790 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF in Schwarzheide, zwischen der Hauptstadt Berlin und der Metropole Dresden gelegen, produziert funktionale Materialien und entwickelt intelligente Systemlösungen für globale Kunden. Wir stehen für Innovation, digitale Transformation und Nachhaltigkeit. Mit unseren neuen Technologien und Arbeitsweisen übernehmen wir eine Vorreiterrolle innerhalb der BASF-Gruppe. Nutzen Sie die Chance und gestalten Sie mit uns zukunftsorientierte Lösungen. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: ustoms Asia Pacific (Manager)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position: Lead and oversee the team in delivering customs services, including Classification, Rules of Origin analysis, Certificate of Origin issuance, and customs verification and documentation review, ensuring alignment with service expectations. Foster strong relationships with internal business partners by understanding their requirements and delivering high-quality, collaborative, and responsive support. Coordinate effectively with external service providers to ensure seamless operations and alignment with service standards through proactive engagement and collaboration. Manage quality assurance and handle non-conformance issues (NCM) related to overseas delivery services, ensuring timely resolution and continuous improvement. Oversee risk management activities for the team, including access control, data protection, business continuity planning, and regular risk assessments. Drive knowledge management initiatives by maintaining up-to-date documentation, facilitating structured reviews, and assigning clear process ownership to ensure knowledge retention. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: 1.  Customs Services Define goals and target for the team to support business as usual Keep up to date of country’s regulations changes and requirement to ensure all compliance of all services Collaborate with Functional Expert and Global Digitalization team to promote automation, process enhancement Ensure all import/export activities comply with local customs laws and international trade agreements. Maintain up-to-date knowledge of regional customs regulations and free trade agreements (FTAs). 2. Project Management Lead/ Support and coordinate customs-related projects such as system implementations, process improvements, and regulatory change initiatives. Develop project plans, timelines, and deliverables in collaboration with cross-functional teams. Monitor project progress, manage risks, and ensure timely completion of milestones. Report on project outcomes and recommend continuous improvement strategies. .3.  Risk Management & Enforcement Identify and mitigate risks related to miscalculations, undervaluation, or non-compliance. Support internal audits and investigations related to customs activities. Implement corrective, preventive actions and process improvements as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement Act as the point of contact to support customs authorities inquiries. Provide training and guidance to internal teams on customs procedures and regulatory updates. Collaborate with cross functional team, business unit, supply chain, legal entity, and order management teams to ensure end-to-end compliance. 5. Reporting & Analysis Prepare regular reports on customs performance (quantitative & qualitative) and compliance metrics Analyze data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for optimization. Job Requirements: Education Bachelor’s degree in International Trade, Customs Administration, Supply Chain, or related field. Working Experience 3–5 years of experience in customs operations or trade compliance, preferably in the Asia Pacific region. Proven experience in managing cross-functional projects or process improvement initiatives. Good understanding of Preferential Rules of Origin, COO processes, and FTA frameworks. Familiarity with HS classification, customs valuation, and import/export documentation. Excellent project management, analytical, and communication skills. Proficiency in customs software, SAP systems, and Microsoft Office Suite. Technical & Professional Knowledge Good Customs and Foreign Trade knowledge and regulation Good knowledge of Import/Export processes and controls Good Supply Chain Operations knowledge Business Application Knowledge (e.g. S/4 Hana, SAP in OTC and GTS Functions) The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com .

jobs.name: AP Business Controller
jobs.description: RESPONSIBILITIES The candidate will join the Agricultural Solutions Team, responding to the Country Manager, and will support with the following responsibilities: o    Analyze data, identify operational trends and provide insights to optimize business performance. o    Develop and implement policies and procedures to drive operational efficiency. o    Prepare accurate and timely reports, including monthly, quarterly, and annual performance summaries. o    Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) that align with operational objectives. o    Conduct financial forecasting, budgeting, and variance analysis to assess operational performance and identify areas for improvement. o    Support operational leaders in decision-making by providing financial analysis and recommendations. o    Coordinate and foster compliance with relevant internal control processes. QUALIFICATIONS o    The ideal candidate is a graduate of Finance, Accounting, or a related field. o    At least 3-5 years of Experience in Controlling, Finance or a similar role, preferably in multinational companies. o    Good financial analysis skills plus the ability to understand and support operational objectives are required in order to translate complex data into actionable insights. o    An excellent knowledge of Greek and English language are required. BENEFITS Compensation includes attractive benefits. Excellent opportunities for growth in an international reality. We offer "on the job" training in a motivated and competent team.

jobs.name: Pack/Ship Operator
jobs.description: Now hiring! Pack/Ship Operator Vidalia, LA. (Onsite) We are looking for a Pack/Ship Operator to join our Catalyst team in Vidalia, LA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops and produces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recycling and related products and services. As a Pack/Ship Operator in our BASF Vidalia site in Louisiana, you will support warehouse operations and ensure safe and efficient packing and shipping of materials. During your day shift as a Pack/Ship Operator, you will Be responsible for movement of material in the warehouse. Load and unload trucks. Weigh material. Pack material in various packaging types, ensuring that product is appropriately labeled. Successfully complete all required EHS and quality training, including forklift driving test. Safely lift 50 pounds. If you... Have a High School Diploma or GED. Have 2-5 years related work experience. Forklift experience preferred. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF This position is part of the United Steelworkers Union and is covered under the collective bargaining agreement.  All benefits are outlined in this agreement. About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency The pay rate for this role is $20,93 per the Union's collective bargaining agreement. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Talent Acquisition Analyst - Internship
jobs.description: Job Purpose Support the Talent Acquisition (TA) team in day-to-day hiring operations by assisting recruiters with sourcing activities, interview scheduling, candidate communication, onboarding coordination, and maintaining accurate new joiner records in HR systems. Key Responsibilities ·       Assist recruiters in sourcing candidates through job portals, social platforms, and internal databases. ·       Coordinate interviews by arranging schedules, sending invites, updating calendars, and ensuring candidates are well-informed. ·       Communicate with candidates regarding interview details, required documents, next steps, or follow-up items. ·       Support onboarding activities such as collecting documents, preparing new joiner packs, and guiding new hires through pre-boarding tasks. ·       Maintain accurate new joiner information in HR systems, ensuring records are complete, updated, and audit-ready. ·       Help track candidate progress in the applicant tracking system (ATS) and flag pending items to the TA team. ·       Assist with posting job advertisements and refreshing job boards when needed. ·       Support general TA administrative tasks to ensure smooth and timely hiring operations. Requirements ·       Malaysian citizen, aged 18–30 years. ·       Minimum qualification: SPM/SPMV; diploma or degree holders (unemployed or seeking internship/apprenticeship) are welcome. ·       Comfortable using computers and basic tools such as Excel, Word, and online platforms. ·       Detail-oriented, organised, and able to manage multiple tasks. ·       Professional communication skills and strong customer-service mindset. ·       Able to work with minimal supervision and handle confidential candidate information responsibly. ·       Prior exposure to recruitment, sourcing, interviewing, or HR systems is an added advantage but not required. About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Global Business Expert - Transportation Management
jobs.description: Agriculture is all about balance. The balance that is crucial to everyone's existence - also applies to our Agricultural Solutions division. We connect innovative thinking with practical action to find the right balance for success - for farmers, agriculture, and future generations. With seeds and traits, crop protection and digital solutions, we help farmers produce food more efficiently, protect the land and meet the needs of society. To further develop and grow this ambition we are looking for a Global Business Expert Transportation Management. You will be part of the Global Supply Chain Process Excellence team with members located in Europe, Asia, North America and South America. Job Responsibilities: Implementing new organizational and / or technical concepts for Transportation Management processes, with a strong focus on standardization, simplification, and automation. Ensuring end-to-end integration and alignment with relevant stakeholders including Global Process Owners. Facilitating and contributing to change and test management activities. Driving innovation and automation initiatives using technologies e.g., RPA and AI. Continuously identifying high-value automation opportunities, assessing business impact, and partnering with IT to design and implement scalable solutions. Tracking and addressing adherence to business rules and processes through KPI-based monitoring, process transparency, and data-driven insights to increase efficiency, quality, and compliance. Leading regional, sub-regional and local key-user communities to enforce continuous communication and best practice exchange. Leading projects or project workstreams with cross-organizational or cross-functional aspects, such as S/4HANA implementation, digital process transformation, or supply chain integration of acquired businesses. Executing key-user and end-user support including issue resolution in day-to-day business, training, monitoring activities, and data maintenance, while continuously identifying opportunities to reduce manual effort through automation. Job Requirements: Have a Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree in Business Administration, IT, Supply Chain Management, Logistics or a related field. Have 8+ years of progressive experience in Supply Chain or IT, with a strong focus on Transportation Management processes and tools. Have deep knowledge of supply chain, operations, and logistics processes, with experience using analysis and monitoring tools such as SAP transactions, BW applications, and data lake queries to identify automation and improvement opportunities. Have detailed knowledge of Microsoft standard applications, SAP ERP, and related tools for Transportation Management purposes, as well as the capability to use tools such as Signavio for process mapping, design, mining, and optimization. Have strong knowledge of state-of-the-art automation technologies, including RPA, AI-enabled solutions, workflow tools, and process mining, and are eager to stay current with emerging trends and practical business applications Are fluent in English language (verbal and written) and have excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to interact effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders. Have experience contributing to projects or leading workstreams, with a strong commitment to enhancing customer and business partner experience throughout the Transportation Management process area by combining process excellence with digital and automation-driven improvements. Ability to travel up to 15% (domestically and internationally) About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Asset Reliability/ Maintenance Engineer
jobs.description: ABOUT US The BASF Ireland site based in Cork manufacturers specialty chemicals used in the mining industry for the solvent extraction of minerals from their ore bodies. At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, on sustainable actions, and on connected thinking and on you. Become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability, or belief. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Act as the site subject matter expert for asset management systems and tools, including SAP and Asset Integrity Programs, ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and compliance of asset records. Support compliance with all statutory, regulatory, and company asset management requirements while optimizing plant reliability and asset effectiveness through appropriate inspection and maintenance strategies. Develop, implement, monitor, and continuously improve preventive and predictive maintenance programs in line with the site's maintenance strategy. Support spare parts and equipment reliability strategies by analysing equipment performance, maintenance history, and criticality to ensure optimal inventory levels. For major shutdowns and partial shutdowns, you work with colleagues to define the maintenance scope and actively follow up on relevant topics during the turnaround. You take the lead in the gatekeeping process, including TAR Tie-in coordination. You manage inspection optimization and strategy. You prepare test and inspection programs and maintain them via periodic maintenance plans. Update the spare parts strategy. Use analysis to determine the optimum spare parts holding. Work closely with Maintenance, Project Engineering and Production teams to agree stocks. Demonstrate that equipment is fit for service (particularly safety critical equipment) including updating of statutory documents, e.g., EPD, P&IDs. Participate and support the site during internal and external audits. Drive asset driven operational excellence / reliability and Lean culture measures (identify and promote efficiencies and overall process improvements using relevant KPIs). Manage the development and delivery of relevant and appropriate technical and asset management-related training to site personnel. Participate as required in site Accident and Incident Investigations, SHE-Review/HAZOPs, RCFA’s. Liaise with external contractors, OEM representatives, etc. during maintenance, shutdown / project planning and execution. WHAT WE EXPECT Bachelor's Degree (Level 8 or equivalent) in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or a related Engineering discipline. Minimum of 5 years' experience within a petrochemical, chemical, pharmaceutical, or other process manufacturing environment. Strong technical knowledge of maintenance engineering, reliability engineering, shutdown planning, asset integrity management, relevant engineering codes, safety standards, and industry best practices. Proficiency in SAP, asset management systems, engineering software, reporting tools, and data-driven performance dashboards. Ability to analyze complex technical issues, make sound engineering decisions, and support multidisciplinary teams in achieving safety, reliability, and production objectives. Strong communication, problem-solving, teamwork, and stakeholder-management skills. Demonstrated focus on continuous improvement, operational excellence, customer service, and delivering cost-effective engineering solutions. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

To further develop and grow this ambition we are looking for a Global Business Expert Procure-2-Pay for Supply Chain. Have experience contributing to projects or leading workstreams, by combining process excellence with digital and automation-driven improvements. jobs.name: Senior Specialist Digitalization Digital Plant (m/f/d) 1
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF Digitalization is a true part of BASF's DNA — creating new customer experiences, driving business growth, and making processes more efficient. Global Digital Services drives BASF's digital transformation through innovative, global, high-quality digital products and a strong agile culture, and the Digital Hub Madrid is one of our key global delivery locations. We are seeking a hands-on AI Engineer for BASF's DevHub — the Internal Developer Platform (IDP) used by thousands of engineers and product teams across BASF. DevHub already ships an enterprise AI Gateway (50+ governed models, Entra ID, EU data residency, per-cost-center billing, Grafana observability) and a catalog that is a schema-validated knowledge graph of every product and its infrastructure. Your mission is to make AI a first-class platform capability: build reusable, production-grade AI services and developer experiences that help users discover, create, configure, operate, scale and govern their products, surfaced where they already work — the portal, the IDE (GitHub Copilot/MCP) and Teams. You will treat the platform as a product — shipping paved-road components other teams reuse, serving both humans and agents, with the multi-tenant scoping, cost-tracking, guardrails and governance an enterprise platform demands. RESPONSIBILITIES Treat the platform as a product. Build paved roads and self-service: reusable AI building blocks (shared retrieval/"context engine," guardrail & evaluation libraries, an MCP/tool layer), scaffolder templates, SDK/API access and stable, versioned interfaces — built once, reused across features. Ship AI experiences that delight developers. Grounded, well-cited assistants, copilots and wizards across the product lifecycle (e.g. a conversational knowledge assistant over our docs and catalog), meeting users on the portal, IDE (Copilot/MCP) and Teams via one shared API. Serve humans and agents. Expose platform capabilities through an MCP / SDK / API surface — read-first, RBAC- and tenant-aware — so internal and external AI clients can query and (later, gated) act on the platform. See the AI-Assisted Platform Strategy RFC. Own evaluation and quality. Build eval harnesses, golden tests and retrieval-quality metrics so features are correct, grounded and regression-tested in CI; invest in context engineering over model-shopping — the Gateway already solves model choice. Pick the right pattern. Prefer deterministic pipelines + structured outputs + human-in-the-loop where outcomes are structured; reserve multi-step/multi-agent orchestration (Azure AI Foundry Agent Service, LangGraph / Microsoft Agent Framework) for genuinely open-ended tasks, keeping state-changing actions gated. Strengthen MLOps / LLMOps. Improve prompt/version management, model adaptation, CI/CD and the path from experiment to production; treat prompts and retrieval as versioned, tested production assets. Build for multi-tenancy. Default to per-product / per-tenant scoping of context, tools and actions; bake in observability (OpenTelemetry, Grafana, distributed tracing) and per-product cost/FinOps visibility. Help advance security, safety & governance. Inherit platform RBAC (Entra ID / AccessIT), defend against the OWASP LLM Top 10, keep AI usage auditable, and respect BASF / EU AI Act and data-residency requirements. QUALIFICATIONS BSc or MSc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, AI, or related field. 4+ years in Software Engineering or Platform Development, with demonstrable recent experience in Generative AI and/or Agentic Systems. You don't need to tick every box. Strong Python + hands-on LLM application experience + a platform/developer-experience mindset matter most; we expect you to grow into the rest. AI / LLM engineering. Practical experience building LLM-powered applications; familiarity with RAG and agentic patterns (ReAct, plan-and-solve, multi-agent) and a clear sense of when not to use an autonomous agent. Evaluation & quality (core). Designing eval harnesses, golden tests and retrieval-quality metrics for LLM/RAG systems (grounding, retrieval precision, hallucination control); context engineering over model selection. Backend & API development. Python proficiency is highly desired (FastAPI, Pydantic, async); designing and operating production backend services and well-versioned APIs. Platform / Developer-Experience engineering. Building reusable, self-service components and paved roads (templates, SDKs, golden paths) and operating multi-tenant services in production (SLOs, observability, "you build it, you run it"). Software development across the stack. Enough context across frontend, backend, and infrastructure to contribute across DevHub's stack (with AI-assisted coding) — no need to be a full-stack expert in every layer. Cloud & infrastructure. Hands-on Azure and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes/AKS); infrastructure-as-code (HCL/Terraform, modular). Data & state management. Relational/non-relational databases (PostgreSQL) and vector stores (e.g. Azure AI Search); managing context and state at scale. DevOps & production operations. CI/CD (Git, GitHub Actions), monitoring/observability, and security best practices in production. AI / LLM ecosystem. LLM providers/APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral), managed AI services (Azure AI Foundry, Databricks), and the MCP standard. (At DevHub, models are consumed through the internal AI Gateway, not provider SDKs directly.) Security & multi-tenancy. Authentication/authorization, RBAC, tenant isolation, guardrails, auditability; awareness of the OWASP LLM Top 10. Agentic standards beyond MCP (e.g. A2A); spec-driven ("spec-kit") agentic development with AGENTS.md / skill conventions. Nice to Have Databricks / Unity Catalog — DevHub's core data platform; DevHub governs the Databricks account and environments (workspaces, Unity Catalog, blueprint lifecycle), not the Enterprise Data Lake or data distribution. Familiarity is a strong plus. TypeScript alongside Python; Grafana; MLflow and data pipelines. FinOps / cost attribution for AI features. Message queues / event-driven & data-intensive architectures (e.g. RabbitMQ, Azure Event Grid). Workflow engines / state machines; OpenTelemetry & distributed tracing. Experience with EnvoyProxy WHAT WE OFFER A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines.We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance.We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities

jobs.name: (Junior) Procurement Specialist with Spanish and Italian (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Join us with your own ideas and help us shape our processes as well as further development of our services! As (Junior) Procurement Specialist with Spanish/Italian and English, you are responsible for all operational purchasing activities. In detail, you ensure that our BASF companies in Europe are supplied with raw materials, technical goods and services. You play an active role: you implement and adhere to the company's procurement guidelines by conducting numerous Purchase-to-Pay process tasks. In collaboration and consultation with our internal business partners, you handle orders accordingly to the guidelines. Assuring timely deliveries and handling non-conformities are part of your daily tasks. In doing so, you are responsible for entering master data of contracts into our IT systems and keeping this data updated. In this context, you are the central point of contact for operational procurement processes and you answer questions from stakeholders (e. g. internal customers, suppliers, strategic buyers and colleagues from the finance departments). WHAT YOU OFFER bachelor's degree in commerce or a similar field, completed vocational education would be also possible initial professional experience in an area of business and commerce; preferably purchasing is a plus excellent communication skills in Spanish and/or Italian, as well as English good knowledge of IT standard applications, experience in working with SAP or other procurement related IT programs are a plus ability to identify customer needs and enjoy consulting WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 -39.000 € , depending on qualification and work experience -  In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the companys success 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Location-independent working models with defined days of presence in Berlin and residence in Germany - here a first impression Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts HOW TO REACH US Maximilian Mette (Talent Acquisition), maximilian.mette@basf.com, Tel.: + 49 30 2005 56084 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment . You can also reach our recruiting team on.basf.com/contact First information about our application process can be found here on.basf.com/application and in our Q&A video ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities at basf.com/servicehub-berlin . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us -- in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Transport Management (Executive) 3
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position: Responsible for ensuring transport management for inbound or outbound (as assigned) are as per stipulated guidelines, procedures and requirements Support process improvement, knowledge and risk management as per overseas delivery services M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Outbound Processing transports for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our customers in compliance with the required customer deadlines at cost-optimal conditions Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and customer-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation of all necessary transport documents, control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Inbound Processing transport for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our sites within EMEA Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and site-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates, and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation and monitoring of all necessary transport documents (e.g verifying draft documents), control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Job Requirements: Edu c a t i o n: Fresh graduates from programs such as Transport Management, IT, Business Studies, or other relevant fields who are interested in exploring a career in supply chain. W o r k in g Experience: 1-2 years in logistics Career starters or first professional experience in transport management T e c hn i c al & Professional Knowledge: Experience in supply chain operations, distribution and warehousing processes Knowledge of the processes of filling and shipping units Experience in handling of MS Office products and, ideally, SAP R/3 quality management and transport management Structured, independent and process-oriented working width High degree of customer orientation High proficiency in spoken and written English The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com.

jobs.name: Industriemeister:in Chemie (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Industriemeister:in Chemie (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Industriemeister:in Chemie (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir bei Care Chemicals handeln aus Leidenschaft. Unsere Mitarbeiter:innen sind unser wertvollstes Gut und wir pflegen eine Kultur der Empathie, der Unterstützung und des Wachstums. Auf diese Weise befähigen wir unser Team, sich persönlich und beruflich weiterzuentwickeln. Mit vereinten Kräften engagieren wir uns für die Entwicklung nachhaltiger, digitaler und innovativer Lösungen für eine bessere Zukunft. Pflegende Hautcremes, milde Shampoos, Sonnenschutzmittel, schonende und umweltfreundliche Waschmittel und leistungsstarke Reiniger für Haushalt und Industrie – all diese Produkte nutzen Inhaltsstoffe aus unserem Bereich Care Chemicals. Darüber hinaus bieten wir beispielsweise Lösungen auf dem Gebiet des Pflanzenschutzes, für technische Anwendungen in der Bauchemie oder Rohstoffe für die Lackindustrie sowie digitale Services für unsere Kunden. Weltweit arbeiten wir gemeinsam mit unseren Kunden an innovativen Lösungen, die Endverbraucher:innen und industrielle Formulierer überzeugen – und das zunehmend auf Basis von natürlichen und nachhaltig gewonnenen Rohstoffen. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam eine bessere Zukunft entwerfen! WAS SIE ERWARTET In unserer Einheit bei der BASF Grenzach GmbH sind wir für die Produktion modernster UV-Filter aus dem Bereich Care Chemicals verantwortlich. Als Teil des Prozessmanagement-Teams unterstützen sie prozessübergreifende Aufgaben (z. B. MOC-Prozesse, Durchführung von Änderungen und Maßnahmen) und stellen eine transparente Nachverfolgung sicher. Sie sorgen für eine einheitliche Qualität von Arbeitsprozessen über alle Schichten hinweg und unterstützen bei der Weiterentwicklung und Standardisierung von Abläufen. Sie unterstützen bei der Erstellung, Pflege und Qualitätssicherung von Betriebsdokumenten, Arbeitsanweisungen sowie technischen Unterlagen. Sie übernehmen unterstützende Aufgaben im Rahmen von Anlagenänderungen, Projekten und Inbetriebnahmen neuer oder geänderter Anlagen. Im Bereich Arbeitssicherheit erstellen und pflegen Sie Gefährdungsbeurteilungen, unterstützen bei Unfallanalysen und tragen zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung der Sicherheitsstandards bei. Sie unterstützen bei der Organisation und Durchführung von Sicherheitsunterweisungen sowie bei der Einhaltung gesetzlicher und interner Vorschriften (SGU). Zudem arbeiten Sie aktiv an der Verbesserung der Sicherheitskultur im Betrieb mit und fungieren als Schnittstelle zwischen Produktion, Technik und HSE. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN abgeschlossene Ausbildung zum/zur Industriemeister:in Chemie sowie mehrjährige Erfahrung als Meister:in (mindestens 5 Jahre) in der chemischen Produktion Kenntnisse im Bereich Arbeitssicherheit und Prozesssicherheit von Vorteil strukturierte, selbstständige und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Organisationsfähigkeit sicherer Umgang mit gängigen IT-Systemen (z. B. SAP, MS Office) selbstständiges und sicheres Arbeiten in einem komplexen Umfeld sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (C1) WAS WIR BIETEN attraktive Vergütung: 62.000 € - 86.000 € Bruttojahresgehalt, je nach Qualifikation und Berufserfahrung sowie ein variabler Bonus, der sich am Geschäftserfolg und Ihrer individuellen Leistung orientiert 30 Urlaubstage, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle sowie je nach Tätigkeit die Möglichkeit mobiler Arbeit Sozialberatung, Unterstützung bei der Altersvorsorge, Gesundheitsförderungsangebote (Hansefit) und Aktienprogramm für Mitarbeitende ein sicheres Arbeitsumfeld, da wir Sicherheit immer Vorrang geben und Ihre Gesundheit an erster Stelle steht Ihr Beitrag für eine lebenswerte Zukunft, denn Nachhaltigkeit ist der Kern unseres Handelns und Wachstumstreiber SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Corinna Granterath (Talent Acquisition), corinna.granterath@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59302 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen (fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden). Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier ÜBER UNS An ihrem Standort in Grenzach, dem BASF-Kompetenzzentrum für UV-Filter, betreibt die BASF Grenzach GmbH die globale Entwicklung, den Technischen Service sowie die Produktion von innovativen UV-Filtern. Unsere Produkte kommen in Sonnencremes, in Kosmetika und Körperpflegeprodukten zum Einsatz. Am Standort befindet sich zudem die globale Entwicklung und der Technische Service von antimikrobiellen Wirkstoffen. Die einzigartige Lage des Unternehmens im Dreiländereck Deutschland, Frankreich und der Schweiz mit der Nähe zum Schwarzwald und den Alpen bietet neben den beruflichen interessanten Möglichkeiten auch attraktive und vielfältige Freizeitaktivitäten. Mehr über die BASF Grenzach GmbH erfahren Sie unter: www.basf-grenzach.de Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. jobs.name: Operational Vendor Management
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Monitoring import export operations that are aligned with Company SOP and Working Instructions Managing operations and performance of Lead Logistic Provider (LLP) and Logistic Service Providers (LSPs) Supervision daily operation and communication of export process in BASF Indonesia to meet agreed performance requirement as per agreed target Meet performance targets and service levels and any assigned qualitative requirements relevant to import export Monitor and evaluate transport service provider’s performance referring to agreed KPIs and conduct periodic quality reviews. Ensure proper process and documentation in accordance to company and regulatory guidelines Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Export Operation Manage and monitor the daily export operations of LLP / LSP. Check the quality of import export orders and if required ensure adequate correction is in place Monitor box and ISO tank usage as per approved tender in monthly basis for each Business Units (BU) against the forecast from BU, analyze, provide inputs and mitigation if any deviation occurs. Monitor the carrier utilization by lane (port of loading/POL & port of destination/ POD) as per approved tender in monthly basis for each Business Units (BU) against the forecast from BU, analyze, provide inputs and mitigation if any deviation occurs. Fill in carrier issue log based on the actual event in timely manner. Managing the SPOT process in export activity, timely escalation to OVM lead for deviations and support Explore tools for digitalization for process improvement Continuous improvement for the export operation and process Manage the TM activity of pre-leg and overall export cost, ensure that the cost reflected in the system is correct and tally as per contract. Do analysis and solve if there is any discrepancy occurs. Support as back up to OVM Lead in absence or when required – as assigned by import export and customs manager etc. Import Operation Manage and monitor the daily import operations of LLP / LSP. Check the quality of import orders/documentations and if required ensure adequate correction is in place Continuous improvement for the export operation and process Monitoring daily report for import activities Liase with CnFT regarding permit, licences etc Documentation Support OVM Lead in knowledge management topics Ensure the document for export process are complete and accurate as per SOP and guidelines Ensure proper documentation and archiving process Managing of Lead Logistic Provider (LLP) and Logistic Service Providers (LSPs) Support OVM Lead in managing LLP and LSPs by providing data, analysis Facilitate Daily Performance Meeting (DPM) with LLP and LSPs and share Export related topics Monitor the performance and KPIs of the LLP and LSPs Raise NCM in timely manner to record any non-conformance from LLP and LSPs Check and verify the NCM closure with proper documentation, root cause analysis and CAPA (corrective and preventive action), monitor of recurrence of cases. Managing Vendor Performance Support OVM Lead during the performance review with vendors (Carriers, TCOs, etc.) by providing data, analysis and lead the meetings in the absence of the OVM Lead. Support OVM Lead during the vendor tendering and onboarding process. Monitor the performance of vendors (Carriers, TCOs, etc.) in monthly basis, document any deviation in carrier issue log and other tools provided, analyze and provide inputs to OVM Lead as well as Hub colleague. Communication Effectively communicate to the respective parties and stakeholders (BU, CSO, LLP, LSPs, Carriers, TCO, hub, regional / global SCM) in daily activities to ensure smooth operation in export area. Escalation of topics in timely manner to OVM lead, Import Export and Customs Manager to ensure smooth operation in export area. Job Requirements Education Degree in business management, supply chain management, international trade, transportation managements are preferred Working Experience and requirement Minimum 2-3 year’s experience in Import Export Operations are mandatory Working in chemicals industries or manufacturing is an advantage Required Language: Bahasa Indonesia and English Technical & Professional Knowledge Customs Certificate / Customs Training experience will be an advantage Working Independently with minimum supervision Strong logical thinking to understand process flow of import export Advance knowledge of Microsoft tools and experience using ERP SAP TMS

jobs.name: Global Functional Process Owner Book-to-Report | Asset- & Project Accounting
jobs.description:
WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Due to the strategic realignment of the Coatings business unit, a dedicated accounting department is being established. This presents an exciting opportunity to develop and shape the finance organization from its foundation. Therefore, we are looking for a Global Functional Process Owner Book-to-Report / Record-to-Report (B2R) | Asset and Project Accounting with the following objectives: You own the global B2R | Asset- and Project Accounting Finance processes, which include the project accounting lifecycle from the initial financial evaluation until the execution of the financial project start-up and project closure, as well as asset accounting processes and lease accounting according to IFRS 16. You establish and maintain global standards for B2R | Asset- and Project Accounting processes, ensuring these are aligned with organizational goals and regulatory requirements across the coatings division. Moreover, you oversee the asset and project accounting activities, identify and drive process improvement opportunities, leveraging technology and digital solutions to enhance automation, efficiency, and continuous improvement within the finance organization. Furthermore, you manage a third-party business process provider in the area of Asset- and Project Accounting and implement process performance KPIs to steer the service provider. You collaborate closely with related functions such as project engineering, Purchase-to-Pay and General Ledger Accounting, you coordinate integration efforts and ensure holistic alignment of accounting processes. In addition, you engage with internal stakeholders to ensure that Asset- and Project Accounting processes meet business needs and support strategic objectives. WHAT YOU OFFER university degree in finance, accounting, business administration, or a related field; additional certifications such as CMA or CPA are an advantage at least 10 years of work experience in financial process management and optimization, with a strong focus on Asset- and Project Accounting activities in a multinational corporation experience in the chemicals industry or a related industry, particularly during transitions or carve-outs, is highly beneficial strong understanding of accounting processes and process optimization methodologies proficiency in SAP R/3 and S/4, process mapping tools, and advanced MS Office skills excellent analytical, problem-solving, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse stakeholders in a multicultural environment fluency in English is required WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits. We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development. Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect. The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback. At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio “Freizeit” with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant “Auszeit” including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center “LackiKids” and a vacation program “LackiKids on Tour”. HOW TO REACH US Bekim Krasniqi (Talent Acquisition), bekim.krasniqi@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56136 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: Becario/a de Recursos Humanos
jobs.description: Estamos en búsqueda de un/a estudiante que tenga interés en desarrollarse como: Becario/a de Recursos Humanos en el área de Relaciones Laborales en la atención y soporte a becarios/as nacionales. Actividades: Gestión de altas de nuevos becarios/as Revisión de expedientes físicos y digitales Elaboración, gestión y seguimiento de convenios semestrales de becarios Gestión de bajas de becarios Envío de comunicados Incidencias de nómina Participación en el onboarding Requisitos Ser estudiante activo/a. Con oportunidad de entregar una constancia de estudios vigente. Estar estudiando las licenciaturas: Administración, Recursos Humanos, Gestion empresarial, Psicología Organizacional, Pedagogía, Ingeniería Industrial. Estar cursando de 4to semestre en adelante. Nivel de Inglés: Básico Manejo de: Paquetería Office Intermedio Interés por actividades administrativas Jornada Lunes a Viernes 6 horas diarias Nota: Becario ANIQ asignado a BASF. La participación se encuentra bajo convenio entre BASF Mexicana y ANIQ en la posición mencionada. BASF, es una empresa que otorga igualdad de oportunidades. Todas las candidaturas cualificadas que cubran el perfil para una posición se considerarán para nuestras vacantes sin distinción de edad, ciudadanía, tono de piel, religión, sexo, estado civil, nacionalidad, discapacidad, identidad o expresión de género, orientación sexual, origen étnico, lengua, identidad indígena, apariencia física, condiciones de salud, características genéticas, embarazo, religión, opiniones políticas, académicas o filosóficas, identidad o filiación política, por su forma de pensar, vestir, actuar, gesticular, por tener tatuajes o perforaciones corporales o cualquier otra condición análoga protegida por la ley del país correspondiente. Haciendo un compromiso con la inclusión, proporcionando siempre que sea posible, ajustes razonables a las personas con discapacidad o con alguna necesidad específica, para garantizar su acceso y el uso de servicios. En caso de requerir algún apoyo, favor de comunicarlo durante tu primer contacto con Atracción de Talento.

Estamos buscando un/a Especialista de Contabilidad (contrato temporal) para Ciudad de México. Tendrá como objetivo asegurar la ejecución de los procesos locales de activos fijos, proyectos de capital, inventarios, gestión y atención a auditoría, asegurar la gestión del costo de ventas para efectos fiscales y de auditoría y entrega de reportes gubernamentales a Banxico, INEGI y SE durante el periodo temporal asignado dentro de la transición correspondiente al negocio de AP. En este rol, estarás a cargo de: Coordinar las actividades de cierre mensuales, asegurando la integridad y exactitud de los resultados financieros locales. Comprobar que los procesos B2R (Book to Report) operados por el centro de servicios compartidos estén alineados con la operación y las regulaciones locales en el periodo de transición determinado. Comunicar eficiente y oportunamente cambios y mejoras en los procesos B2R; así como comunicar al Centro de Servicios Compartidos modificaciones a la normativa y necesidades locales. Coordinar junto con el centro de servicios compartidos la auditoría, asegurando el cumplimiento en tiempo y forma de los procesos de auditoría; así como dar seguimiento a la implementación de las recomendaciones formuladas por la auditoría. Ejecutar el cumplimiento de todos los requisitos gubernamentales, la contabilidad reglamentaria y la presentación de informes para cumplir con las normas contables locales. Facilitar la información fiscal para el pago de impuestos federales, declaraciones mensuales y anuales durante el periodo de transición Asegurar el cumplimiento de las obligaciones regulatorias, legales y de reporteo gubernamental en materia contable, alineadas con las disposiciones locales y requerimientos de entidades como INEGI, Banco de México, EMOE, INPP y Secretaría de Economía, durante la vigencia de la posición temporal. Coordinar el soporte y la gestión local de los procesos de auditoría interna y externa en México, asegurando el debido acompañamiento y cumplimiento de requerimientos durante la vigencia de la posición temporal hasta la finalización de la asignación. Si cuentas con: Formación universitaria finalizada en contabilidad. Experiencia de al menos 3-5 años en roles similares. Conocimiento de normativa de México contable y de impuestos. Manejo de paquete office (Outlook, Excel, etc.). Experiencia con SAP (deseable). Nivel de inglés intermedio o superior. Habilidades de organización, planificación, negociación y proactividad.
