jobs.name: Field Technical Service Representative
jobs.description: Now hiring! Field Technical Service Representative Remote. Territories: Southeast United States. ( Florida, Georgia, South Carolina, North Carolina, Virginia) Based out of the BASF Central, SC site. Pay Range: $75,000 - $100,000 We are looking for a Field Technical Service Representative to join our Performance Materials team. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction.These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. We are seeking a professional like you to have a major role in continuing the growth of the Autofroth Business by providing excellent technical support for Polyurethane Systems customers. You will be assigned specific identified target accounts and will be responsible to provide installation and on-going support for polyurethane applications as needed to grow and sustain business volumes. You will provide technical guidance and assistance to customers for application of BASF polyurethane systems products and dispensing equipment including optimization of use, troubleshooting, and training of customer's personnel. You must comply with all company policies, procedures and initiatives. This is an excellent opportunity for you to demonstrate your strong training skills and personal and business ethics.  You will also get to share your strengths in leading people and teams, interacting with colleagues and customers at all levels, and managing people without direct authority. This role requires that you be mechanically inclined and able to lift at least 50 pounds. Travel for the role will be extensive (75%+), including overnight travel. As a Field Technical Service Representative, you create chemistry by... Leveraging your education and experience with polyurethane chemistry and/ or polyurethane processing, you will be responsible for conducting foam trials at customers and communicating the results. You will be proactive in helping to determine the value proposition. Providing your proficiency in Excel, Word, Power Point, e-mail, and web-based platforms as this will be essential to document the foam trials. You will also use these skills to create and implement key account plans and strategies. Bringing your excellent verbal and written communication skills, as well as your experience in dealing directly with customers, to set up foam trials with Sales and the customer. Working independently with strong problem-solving skills, you will troubleshoot and maintain mechanical equipment. Having an unwavering commitment to workplace safety as you ensure and work in compliance with EH&S and ISO standards. Demonstrating your effective interpersonal, negotiation, and presentation skills, you will provide key effort in new product launches. Employing your analytical skills, you will provide competitive product analysis - technical analysis and how a product aligns with customer's requirements. Being a strong team player with robust technical skills, you will provide technical support for BASF Polyurethanes Specialties Business. Interfacing with and supervising contractors and engineers, you will help design chemical storage facilities as well as plant layouts for equipment placement. Being able to be focused on the vision of the business and provide customer insight, professional expertise and technical product knowledge as you understand and practice the highest levels of Product Stewardship. If you... Possess a education and experience in polyurethane chemistry and/or processing, with strong technical support skills for polyurethane systems, including installation and ongoing assistance for customers. Are able to conduct foam trials at customer locations, communicate results effectively, and document findings using Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, email, and web-based platforms. Have excellent verbal and written communication skills for customer interactions, coupled with strong problem-solving abilities and mechanical inclination for troubleshooting and maintaining equipment. Are commited to workplace safety, ensuring compliance with Environmental, Health, and Safety (EH&S) and ISO standards, along with effective interpersonal, negotiation, and presentation skills for new product launches. Possess analytical skills for conducting competitive product analysis and aligning products with customer requirements, with the capability to work independently and as a team player in providing technical support. Are experienced in interfacing with and supervising contractors and engineers for designing chemical storage facilities and plant layouts, with a willingness to travel extensively (75%+), including overnight travel, and strong leadership qualities to manage people without direct authority. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $75,000 - $100,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Technical Service Specialist - SoCal - Automotive Refinish
jobs.description:
Now hiring! Technical Service Specialist - SoCal - Automotive Refinish ** Field Role: Orange County, Riverside, San Diego, LA metro area / Company Car provided We are looking for a Technical Service Specialist to join our Automotive Refinish team in Southern California. Come create chemistry with us! Surventis Coatings (formerly BASF Coatings LLC) offers high-quality refinish paint brands and paint-related products to body shops from around the world. Our solutions are approved by the world’s leading automotive manufacturers for the repair of their vehicles. We provide collision centers with a vast array of solutions that help drive their business performance and efficiency.  At the heart of our business are our people and our trainers who take care of our customers’ needs including car painting skills, advanced body shop management solutions, and sophisticated management solutions. As a Technical Service Specialist in Southern California, you will be instrumental in providing customer support to automotive collision repair shop customers as well as distribution partners to grow our market share sustainably.  In this role, you will be responsible for the technical success of Surventis' automotive refinish products in the Southern California region. You will maintain high quality customer service standards at some of the largest Body Shop organizations in the market through process development, correct application of products through teaching and hands-on demonstrations, and helping the sales team convert new business. As a Technical Service Specialist - SoCal - Automotive Refinish, you create chemistry by... Providing technical assistance and training to our customer base and prospects to maintain and gain market share in the Southern California market Working with your Surventis colleagues in the region to accomplish regional sales objectives by training on our products, demoing new product lines and assisting the team in any customer requests Making process improvement recommendations within Body Shops Helping end users and/or distribution partners be more proficient in product application, selling and implementing new products and generating a positive experience with Surventis branded products Working and staying up to date on any product developments and technology to elevate your professional experience through training and reaching out to marketing, training and internal team members while adding value to our customers Communicating findings or any customer related activities to Surventis colleagues using our internal CRM or communication channels If you have... An Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree (recommended) or High School Diploma (GED) At least 5 years of experience of either training or working hands on with Automotive Coatings materials within the automotive market The ability to collaborate for achievement while working within a sales team to accomplish goals by training on our products, demoing new product lines and assisting Account Representatives and Business Development Managers A strong understanding of body shop workflow enabling you to make process improvement recommendations that can be highly valuable to end users The ability to communicate effectively which will help end users and/or distribution partners be more proficient in product application, selling and implementing new products and generating a positive experience with Surventis branded products Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. jobs.name: Assistant Manager, Sales (TPU)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position The primary function of this role is to implement Greater China TPU sales & marketing strategy in new business development and customers’ management by initiating contacts with existing and potential customers. Develop the business according to the regional strategy and objectives. How You Will Do It Sales Development . Conduct existing business and develop new projects in existing & new customers to ensure both volume and margin growth. Develop sales plans; secure and expedite orders. Supervise and consolidate the sales forecast in accuracy. Strategic Tasks and Implementation Identify key accounts and develop key account profiles. Implement the sales and marketing directions per aligned strategy. Product/Industry/Business Development Scout for new business opportunities in the respective sales areas / key accounts and develop a business plan. Suggest product developments and requirements to Sales Manager and support its implementation. Drive innovation & sustainability in key accounts Customer Management Conduct field visits, seminars, presentation and attend to customers’ inquiries. Provide prompt response related to quality, technical applications, inquiries, and other related issues; conduct customers’ assessments and analyses. Align with regional and function team to solve customers’ issues. Work with supply chain, CSO and logistic function to establish a reliable and high efficiency services. Credit Management Monitor the growth of the customers’ business, review and coordinate with credit control to make appropriate credit recommendations. Follow-up on payment collections and ensure on-time payment from the customers Marketing Analysis competitors and derive actions plan to defend our position. Provides China TPU market surveillance/market intelligence in specific area Conduct studies/analysis on market trend, competitors, products’ applications and technology, service needs. What We Look For Bachelor or Master of Science degree in Polymer, Chemistry, Material Science, Chemical Engineering, Material Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or equivalent. 5 years working experience above preferably with Thermoplastic material or Polyurethanes in Footwear industry. Good Plastic injection or extrusion processing skill; thermoplastic elastomer processing experience is preferable. Good communication skill in Chinese and English. Good presentation skill. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用

jobs.name: Specialist Cyber Security Culture (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. RESPONSIBILITIES : The Cyber Security Strategy product family provides the cyber security strategies and architectures for the BASF Group, the steering of Cyber Security project programs, remediation management for critical incidents, consultancy across BASF’s Cyber Security portfolio, establishes the key-pillars of a risk-based Cyber Security culture and supports the secure integration of AI within BASF. As a product team of this family, Cyber Security Culture is establishing a Cyber Security culture at BASF through regular communications, relevant awareness raising initiatives and training programs tailored to individual stakeholder needs. These activities are implemented via a global Cyber Security community, which is coordinated by the team. Develop and enhance WordPress plugins (backend & UI) Support SharePoint (SPFx) development using React for the Secure Together portal Build and style UI components (tiles, forms, dashboards) Work with APIs and integrations to connect systems and enable personalization Troubleshoot bugs and continuously improve performance and usability Support role ‑ based experiences, depending on the stakeholder groups and work on integrations with Cyber Security ecosystem Maintain and further develop PowerBI Dashboards with various data sources. QUALIFICATIONS : Degree in Information Technology. Alternatively, a degree in Business Administration or similar with a very high affinity for IT Basic JavaScript/TypeScript experience Some familiarity with React (or willingness to learn) Strong HTML / CSS General understanding of how web apps/APIs work Fluent in English, any additional languages are a plus Reliability, responsiveness and flexibility, excellent communication skills, high motivation, teamwork skills. WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Ob für Autos, Gebäude oder Sportartikel - wir suchen immer die ideale Materiallösung. Dafür arbeiten wir eng mit unseren Kunden zusammen: von Produktion und Logistik über Forschung und Vertrieb bis hin zu Bauteilprüfung, Designberatung und Simulation. WAS SIE ERWARTET Kommen Sie in unser Team Kommunikation und treiben Sie unsere Standortkommunikation sowie Public-Relations-Aktivitäten voran! Mit Ihrer Kreativität unterstützen Sie uns bei allen Kommunikationsaktivitäten am Standort. Kreativität ist gefragt: Sie recherchieren, verfassen und überarbeiten zielgruppengerechte Texte für die interne Kommunikation. Sie steuern eigenverantwortlich die Überarbeitung des Showrooms und treiben das Projekt aktiv voran. Nicht zuletzt unterstützen Sie uns bei der Planung, Gestaltung und Durchführung von Veranstaltungen am Standort und tragen zu einem reibungslosen Ablauf bei. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN immatrikuliert in einem kommunikations-, geistes-, sozial- oder wirtschaftswissenschaftlichen Studiengang Kenntnisse in der internen und externen Kommunikation, der Textformulierung und -gestaltung sowie der Online-Kommunikation Spaß am kreativen sowie eigenverantwortlichen Arbeiten in einem internationalen Team sehr gute mündliche und schriftliche Sprachkenntnisse auf Deutsch und Englisch WAS WIR BIETEN Praxisphase – Theorie aktiv in anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Aufgaben/Projekten anwenden Teambuddy – Ansprechperson für alle Fragen sowie regelmäßiges Feedback, eine persönliche und individuelle Betreuung sowie Einarbeitung durch unsere Fachexperten Netzwerke – unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Denkweisen und Arbeitsgebiete kennenlernen Vergütung – Die Vergütung erfolgt gemäß des Tarifvertrages der chemischen Industrie Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung – je nach Einsatzeinheit ist in Absprache eine befristete Vollzeitbeschäftigung in der vorlesungsfreien Zeit (Semesterferien) möglich SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Michael Wiesenack-Flick (Talent Acquisition), michael.wiesenack-flick@basf.com , Tel: +49 30 2005-58564 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH mit Sitz in Lemförde entwickelt, produziert und vertreibt maßgeschneiderte Polyurethan-Produkte und Problemlösungen, die das Leben komfortabler und sicherer machen. Unsere innovativen Spezialkunststoffe werden von Herstellern weltweit in nahezu allen Branchen erfolgreich eingesetzt. Mehr über die BASF Polyurethanes GmbH erfahren Sie unter: hier . Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.name: Payroll & People Data (Specialist) - Mandarin Speaker (Leasing, 12 Month contract) 1
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Understand business needs and provide expert advice, services and solutions on Payroll, HR data, systems and processes. Provide expert consultation in payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislation in local countries. Drive process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Ensure payroll and statutory reporting and payments are processed with high accuracy and timeliness according to our SLAs and local legal requirements. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Operational Excellence Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data maintenance processes. Manage full payroll portfolio (either in-house or via vendors) accurately, timely and in compliance with statutory requirements, business policies and company guidelines. Responsible for payroll issues resolutions, data analysis, payroll accounting activities, payroll taxation and legislation administration. Provides solutions to manage time and attendance processes for office and production workers, including the maintenance of time system configurations, time settings such as public holiday calendar maintenance, work schedules arrangements, time calculation rules etc. Provide actual payroll and compensation data based on SAPHR to the Compensation & Benefits and Mobility teams in support of the salary review management. Develop expertise in International labor and social standards and support relevant internal/external audit analysis (for example: Corporate Audits, external audits, FRC processes) Responsible for vendor management for Payroll, which includes service provider scouting, selection, negotiation, contract management, onboarding, performance monitoring and vendor invoice processing. Accountable to ensure finance processes with relevance to payroll is followed through with high accuracy and timeliness. Service Engagement Provide advisory such as manual payment calculation, pay slip simulation, gross up computations for payment, national service claims etc. Monitor and stay abreast with local payroll legislation which affects the HR climate and support business to manage changes proactively. Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and HR data to develop the formation of competence hub. Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target. Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience Develop and maintain the blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes to ensure the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time. Participate in merger and acquisitions activities pertaining to setting up or transfer of payroll and HR data maintenance activities. Ensure successful implementation of payroll and HR data maintenance processes for mergers and acquisition activities. Team Collaboration Works autonomously within established procedures and practices; seeks guidance in the more complex aspects of the process. Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously provide guidance and sharing knowledge with others. Guides and trains junior team members and newly joined team members. Job Requirements Education Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce, Business Administration and Finance Working Experience With minimum 3 years of related HR working experiences in Payroll and HR Operations Proficiency in Mandarin (speak, read, write) is required to support business operations in China. Experience in relevant payroll procedures, practices and country statutory Working knowledge of integrated payroll systems, such as SAP, is a must. Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrates a high level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location Understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. Strong processing skills and analytical abilities Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills. Functional knowledge and skills in using Microsoft applications

jobs.name: ESTGN - Transport Planner Officer (m/f/d) Temporal
jobs.description: ¡El mundo necesita innovación y nuestro equipo te necesita a ti! BASF es la primera empresa química del mundo #WeCreateChemistry y uno de los mejores lugares para trabajar y desarrollar tu carrera. Estamos en búsqueda de talento diverso, apasionado y creativo que desee hacer sus prácticas con nosotros/as, aprender de la mano de tutores/as experimentados/as y marcar la diferencia. BASF Española S.L. comercializa más de 5.000 productos para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros clientes y de la sociedad en una gran variedad de sectores. La industria química está cambiando; y BASF también. Descubre nuestra estrategia: #WinningWays . ¿Quieres poner en práctica tus conocimientos y crear química con nuestro equipo? LO QUE HARÁS La persona seleccionada deberá dar soporte en las siguientes tareas: Disposición de transporte para la carga de los productos consignados en las entregas a servir desde los Sites asignados. Seguimiento de los transportes hasta su cierre informando de las incidencias/cambios de los pedidos a sus gestores. Negociación de precios Spot con los transportistas cuando se requiera un servicio no cubierto. Soporte en la integración del nuevo sistema SAP. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Grado en Administración y dirección de empresas, Comercio internacional, Supply Chain o titulación académica equivalente Experiencia mínima de 1 año en funciones área de logística/ supply chain o similar en sector industrial Nivel alto de inglés y valorable conocimientos de italiano y/o alemán Conocimientos de SAP a nivel de usuario Habilidades comunicativas, de trabajo en equipo y resolución de problemas LO QUE OFRECEMOS Entorno de trabajo saludable: donde tu seguridad y salud (física y mental) son nuestra prioridad. Paquete salarial atractivo: basado en un salario fijo y un bonus según resultados. Plan de retribución flexible: para beneficiarte de servicios como seguro de salud, escuela infantil, tarjeta transporte y formación. Desarrollo profesional: promovemos la formación continua y el acceso a vacantes internas para seguir creciendo en BASF. Medidas de conciliación personal y familiar, así como una ayuda económica para la escolarización de hijos/as. Y muchos más. CÓMO PARTICIPAR EN EL PROCESO Envía tu CV a través de la oferta para poder conocernos. Si tu perfil encaja, te llamaremos desde el equipo español de Talent Acquisition en Alemania (prefijo +49) y te acompañaremos en el proceso. ¡Con cada paso estarás más cerca de conocer a tu futuro equipo! Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, contacta con nuestro/a compañero/a: Andrea López (andrea.lopez-gutierrez@basf.com)* *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. MÁS SOBRE BASF Celebramos la diversidad y activamente promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo donde cada voz cuenta y cada idea es valorada sin importar edad, género, estado civil, etnia, religión, condición física/mental u orientación sexual. #BeYourself Somos personas C reativas, A biertas, R esponsables y E mprendedoras. Estos son nuestros valores, que nos guían y describen cómo somos y actuamos. Si tú también te identificas con ellos, ¡BASF es tu sitio! At BASF, the chemistry is right. Because we are counting on innovative solutions, on sustainable actions, and on connected thinking. And on you. Become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Yeosu AEM Production Manager
jobs.description: BASF has been a committed partner to Korea since 1954. As a leading foreign investor in the chemical industry in Korea, BASF operates eight world-scale production sites in the country. The company also maintains the Electronic Materials R&D Center in Suwon. In addition, one R&D center for advanced Performance Materials solutions and two technical development centers are located across Korea. In 2023, BASF posted sales of approximately € 1.3 billion to customers in Korea and employed 1,209 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com . *Position summary: The AEM Production Manager is responsible for planning, directing, coordinating, and controlling all production-related activities to ensure safe, efficient, and cost-effective manufacturing operations. Reporting to the Plant Manager, this role oversees production planning, material management, maintenance coordination, project execution, people development, and EHSQ compliance to meet business and customer requirements. *Main Tasks of Production Manager: 1. Production Planning & Operations - Develop and execute production plans based on sales forecasts, customer requirements, and R&D demands. - Adjust production schedules according to operational constraints, equipment availability, and process issues. - Ensure production targets, product quality, delivery commitments, and cost objectives are achieved. - Coordinate production activities with Supply Chain, Procurement, Quality, Engineering, and other relevant functions. 2. Manufacturing Performance Management - Monitor daily manufacturing performance and identify improvement opportunities. - Conduct routine plant inspections and implement corrective and preventive actions. - Drive operational excellence initiatives to improve productivity, reliability, and efficiency. - Manage production budgets and support cost optimization initiatives. 3. Material & Inventory Management - Manage raw material planning, inventory control, procurement coordination, and material availability. - Ensure accurate production and material data management through SAP and MES systems. - Monitor material consumption and optimize inventory levels. 4. Maintenance & Reliability - Coordinate preventive and corrective maintenance activities to maximize equipment reliability and plant availability. - Support troubleshooting and resolution of operational and technical issues. - Prepare work permits and approve general maintenance work permits within assigned authority. 5. People Management - Provide technical leadership, coaching, and training to production personnel. - Foster a strong safety culture and promote continuous improvement practices. - Conduct first-level performance evaluations for operators and support employee development. 6. Capital Projects & Plant Modifications - Lead plant expansion and modification projects. - Coordinate with engineering teams, contractors, equipment suppliers, and service providers. - Support equipment selection, project execution, commissioning, and startup activities. 7. EHSQ (Environment, Health, Safety & Quality) Management - Ensure full compliance with BASF requirements, local regulations, and applicable international standards. - Lead implementation of EHSQ programs, procedures, and improvement initiatives. - Manage risk assessments, Management of Change (MOC), emergency response preparedness, and safety inspections. - Review and maintain SOPs, operating manuals, work procedures, and ISO-related documentation. - Support Process Safety Management (PSM), DCS/SIS documentation management, and proof-test follow-up activities. - Participate in SHE committees and promote continuous improvement in workplace safety and quality. *Education/Qualifications required: - Bachelor's degree in Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, or a related technical discipline. - Minimum 3 years of experience in a chemical manufacturing plant environment. - Strong knowledge of production operations, manufacturing processes, and EHS requirements. - Experience with SAP, MES, or equivalent manufacturing systems. - Strong problem-solving, leadership, and cross-functional coordination skills. * Competencies required: - Experience in semiconductor chemicals, electronic materials, or specialty chemical manufacturing. - Experience in project management, process safety management (PSM), or plant expansion projects. - Knowledge of Management of Change (MOC), risk assessment, and operational excellence methodologies. - Proficiency in English communication and native level of Korean. *Special Requirements if any: 1.Hazardous Materials Industrial Engineer certification preferred 2.Professional Engineer Chemical Engineering preferred 3.Professional Engineer Industrial Safety certification preferred 면접은 서류전형 합격자에 개별통지하며, 제출하신 서류는 일체 반환하지 않습니다. 입사지원서 내용에 허위사실이 판명될 경우 입사가 취소될 수 있습니다. 국가보훈대상자와 신체장애자는 관련서류 제출시 관계법에 따라 우대합니다. 취업지원대상자 증명서 (제출처 : 한국바스프 반드시 기입) 발급받은 후 입사서류와 함께 제출하여야 가점 부여 가능합니다.

jobs.name: Working student Frontend Engineering (m/f/d) - Remote GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF The Global Procurement division is responsible for BASF's purchasing activities worldwide. We negotiate contracts that provide BASF with tangible added value. Our focus is on the procurement of raw materials, technical goods and services as well as logistics solutions. Our comprehensive market know-how and deep understanding of value-adding chains make us avaluable partner for the operating divisions. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We are the AI & Analytics Group within the "Center of Excellence", an enthusiastic interdisciplinary group of engineers, game theorists, data scientists, and software engineers. With entrepreneurial spirit, we drive the global digital transformation of procurement. We are on the mission to make the lives of our colleagues easier through technological innovations. Our software facilities strategic decision making backed by innovative algorithmic solutions. You have the opportunity to support our team in the field of backend engineering for 6 months. Whether you have experience with React, Angular, Vue or any other framework, we know you can adapt to the development tools and operational platform currently in use. You quickly understand business problems, ask insightful questions, challenge assumptions, and proactively develop solution ideas. You take ownership of outcomes and ensure that solutions address the underlying user and business needs. Once requirements are clear, you independently turn ideas into high-quality frontend solutions, delivering at pace while maintaining a strong focus on usability and quality. Your way of working involves continuously inspecting and adapting, while constructively challenging existing solutions, processes, and assumptions. You collaborate effectively with business stakeholders, designers, and developers, communicating complex topics in a clear and pragmatic way. WHAT YOU OFFER currently enrolled in a university program in Software Engineering, Software Development, Computer Science, or a related field experience and passion for TypeScript and React and its core principles, including state management (e.g., React Hooks, Context API, Redux, Axios is a plus) basic understanding of REST APIs and how to integrate them into a React application experience in Material design, Ant design or styled-components is a plus fluent English communications skills, German is an advantage please include an up-to-date CV and Transcript of Records with your application, references we can contact will be very much appreciated members from states outside the European Economic Area and Switzerland are requested to send their residence/work permit, including the relevant supplementary sheet as well as the completed questionnaire WHAT WE OFFER Practical phase - actively apply theory in challenging and varied tasks/projects Teambuddy - contact person for all questions and regular feedback Networks - get to know different perspectives, ways of thinking and fields of work Compensation - compensation is based on the collective agreement for the chemical industry. Based on a 20-hour working week, the monthly gross wage is currently €1,784. Flexible working hours - depending on the department, temporary full-time employment during the lecture-free period (semester break) is possible by arrangement. HOW TO REACH US Katarina Borisov (Talent Acquisition), Tel: +49 30 2005-59269 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment. For technical questions please contact Alexander Jungwirth, alexander.jungwirth@basf.com. You can also reach our recruiting team here. First information about our application process can be found here. ABOUT US Chemistry for a sustainable future – that is what BASF stands for. As a group company located in Ludwigshafen, BASF Jobmarkt GmbH makes an important contribution to this mission. Our focus is on temporary employment as well as our training network. The business area of temporary employment includes recruiting and providing personnel to offer temporary support to the BASF Group. The goal is to meet short-term staffing needs and to offer qualification programs. In doing so, we create perspectives, promote flexibility, and strengthen the site sustainably. Visit here. Your assignment will take place at BASF SE. Learn more about BASF SE here. Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As part of our Global Technology Digitalization & Automation unit, you join an international team that drives digitalization and automation initiatives across the global Coatings business. Together, we develop global standards and technologies that enable innovation and operational excellence in our laboratories worldwide. In this role, you implement and continuously improve automation solutions across the full system lifecycle – from system integration and development through to operation and optimization. You develop and maintain software components, including frontend, backend, and interfaces, and integrate measurement devices, robots as well as PLC-controlled systems into digital workflows. Furthermore, you contribute to the evolution of automation towards data-driven and intelligent solutions by evaluating, conceptualizing and implementing Computer Vision, Machine Learning and AI use cases to enhance automation capabilities. Additionally, you independently drive automation projects, manage stakeholders, and coordinate with global teams to ensure effective communication and alignment across regions and functions. You manage requirements for new automation systems and coordinate implementation activities with internal and external stakeholders. Finally, you commission automation systems and support system rollouts at local and global sites. WHAT YOU OFFER master's degree in automation engineering, mechatronics or a closely related field practical experience in industrial automation, software development, and system integration solid knowledge in several of the following areas: C++ development, PLC, motion control, industrial robot programming, Python development, Computer Vision, database systems (e.g. PostgreSQL), REST APIs (e.g. curl, Bruno), Machine Learning, and AI very good language skills in German and English confident in using standard Microsoft Office applications WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio "Freizeit" with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant "Auszeit" including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center "LackiKids" and a vacation program "LackiKids on Tour". HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: Cloud-native Solution Architect
jobs.description: Who we are: We are BASF, the largest chemical company in the world, with over 150 years of experience. In Montevideo, we are a Hub made up of more than 1,000 employees who provide services for all BASF companies in the Americas. We offer professional solutions in finance, purchasing, reporting, logistics, technology, human resources, projects, communication, legal matters, environment, safety, health, and quality. Main Objective Act as a trusted advisor to product and DevOps teams by providing solution-oriented consulting, architectural guidance, and hands-on enablement to design and implement cloud-native solutions based on standardized cloud and infrastructure services. Tasks Consult product and DevOps teams on the design and implementation of modern cloud native solutions across AWS, Azure, Aliyun Cloud, and on-premises environments with a global focus. Translate business, application, and infrastructure requirements into implementable solution patterns and pragmatic architectural recommendations. Create and maintain reference implementations, reusable patterns, and technical guidance for cloud-native development using Infrastructure as Code. Advise teams on how to improve scalability, security, stability, and maintainability of their solutions by applying modern engineering principles and best practices. Contribute to the evolution of cloud and infrastructure service offerings by providing solution feedback, architectural input, and implementation experience from delivery teams. Support internal teams in navigating the end-to-end infrastructure demand lifecycle and in aligning their requests with BASF’s demand fulfillment processes. Drive requirement clarity and completeness in early demand phases to enable efficient evaluation, smoother implementation, and faster delivery. Act as a key interface between product teams, DevOps teams, infrastructure service providers, and request management stakeholders to align solution needs and delivery constraints. Facilitate effective collaboration between internal business partners, project teams, external providers, and internal IT teams to enable successful solution adoption and implementation. Requirements Education: Degree in Computer Science, Informatics, Business Informatics or in a comparable field. Working Experience: 5-8 years of experience in software engineering, solution architecture, or cloud-native application development. 5-8 years of experience in designing and implementing cloud-native solutions in enterprise environments. 35-8 years of experience working with DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and agile delivery models (e.g. Scrum). 5-8 years of experience in hybrid architecture environments, integrating cloud platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS) with on-premises solutions. 3-5 years agile project experience (nice to have). Language skills: Advanced level in English. Technical skills: Advanced skills in cloud-native architecture and modern application design. Advanced skills in solution architecture across applications, integration, and data layers. Advanced skills in cloud platforms and cloud services (e.g. Azure, AWS). Intermediate skills in container technologies, APIs, automation, and infrastructure architecture. Intermediate skills in DevOps methodologies and CI/CD tools and practices Intermediate skills in security principles for cloud and enterprise environments. Basic to Intermediate skills in programming and scripting languages such as Python, Bash, Java, or JavaScript. Soft skills: Advanced consulting and communication skills Advanced stakeholder management skills Advanced strategic thinking skills Advanced innovation and continuous learning mindset Advanced self-organized working skills with entrepreneurial mindset What we offer: Living BASF means being part of a culture where the focus is on people and on their professional and personal development. Our diversity, collaboration, and leadership style make the difference. When you join our team, we offer you: A hybrid and flexible working model Access to a learning ecosystem with on ‑ demand resources and tools Private medical insurance for you and your family from day one, as well as life insurance Additional flexible leave days, in addition to your statutory vacation days The possibility of remote work from abroad for up to 6 weeks a year A day off on your birthday A day off for moving Annual bonus (subject to meeting the requirements established by internal policies) Well ‑ being benefits, such as on ‑ site nutrition services and massages BASF considers Law N° 19.691 and Decree N° 73/019 in all its hiring processes. Every qualified candidate who meets the requirements established in the job description will be considered in this hiring process with no distinction. Moreover, BASF is an equal-opportunity employer. We enthusiastically accept our responsibility to make employment decisions without regard to race, age, civil and social status, national origin, disability, sex, gender identity or expressions, or any other candidate or employee´s characteristic or group that is not related to their aptitude and suitability for the position. Our management is committed to following this policy concerning hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, demotion, layoff, termination, recruiting, pay, and other forms of compensation, training, and general treatment during employment.

jobs.name: Lab Technician I
jobs.description: Now hiring! Lab Technician I- Monaca, PA Pay Range: $25.00-26.00/ hr. / Night Shift differential / Performance Incentive Bonus/ Full Benefits Package / 401k Options We are looking for a Quality Control Lab Technician to join our Dispersions team in Monaca, PA. In this role, you will be responsible for quality assurance testing of raw materials, intermediates, and finished latex products for the BASF Corp. Dispersions Plant. Providing accurate results in our software systems allows BASF to evaluate data which supports the continuous improvement of lab and plant processes to meet and/or exceed customer expectations. Come create chemistry with us! During your 12-hour shift as a Lab Technician I, you will Principal Responsibilities Actively supporting and promoting a safe working environment, participating in all safety trainings and programs, and supporting Environmental, Health, Safety, and Security plant initiatives. Using a variety of testing equipment including GCs, HPLC, FTIR, Spectrophotometers, Viscometers, Ovens, balances, pH meters, Microscopy, LIMS & SAP data systems. Using appropriate software to evaluate data based on internal and external specifications, procedure requirements, and LIMS programming, as well as entering information into SAP software. Quality assurance testing of in-process and finished latex products within the scope of the BASF Quality Policy and ISO 9001 Quality Management System which includes testing using pH, viscosity, gas chromatography, and other analytical techniques.  Other testing as required including waste water samples. Performing bacteria testing Supporting the continuous improvement of lab and plant processes through QA/QC activities, instrument troubleshooting and maintenance, and meeting/exceeding customer expectations. Interacting with the Supply Chain and Operations departments at the Monaca site to expedite shipments to customers utilizing SAP Available to work rotating 12 hour shifts 7:30am-7:30pm and vice versa and overtime when needed Process Safety Management Responsibilities Process safety roles and responsibilities across the organization are clearly defined and include an expectation to raise, and authority to respond to process safety concerns. Employees understand the importance of process safety as it applies to their jobs and are responsible for following and contributing to the work processes to achieve improvement in company process safety performance. Formula for Success... 2 yr Associates degree or technical degree in Chemistry/Analytical testing OR related field with lab experience OR High School Diploma/GED with minimum 2+ years of relevant industrial lab experience required. Experience working in a manufacturing environment on a 12 hour rotating shift Experience with Microsoft Office products; Word, PowerPoint, Excel and ability to operate various computer-controlled instruments. Knowledge of testing equipment found in laboratory setting; GCs, HPLC, FTIRS, Viscometers Basic understanding of general chemistry, techniques of quality assuarance and quality control Basic understanding of math, statistics, analytical methodology, insight into problem solving, and ability to record and report pertinent data. On occasion, must be able to lift up to a maximum of 30 lbs. Must be able to follow both written procedures, verbal instructions and follow all applicable safety procedures including correct personal protective equipment. Willing and available to work rotating 12 hour shifts (7:30am-7:30pm and vice versa) and overtime when required Preferred Skills Experience working in an ISO or GMP compliance driven lab environment Experience with emulsion polymerization products, laboratory testing and practices The preferred candidate will have minimum 2+ years’ experience in a lab position of increasing responsibility. #dispersionsresins&additives Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.name: Software Engineer SAP Environment Management EHS - (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Responsibilities : Support and continuously improve SAP Environment Compliance Solutions, including environmental permits management for sealed surfaces and emissions Work in an agile product team, contribute to iteration planning, and help deliver high-quality solutions with measurable business value. Create functional and technical solution designs and perform corresponding implementation activities that enhance business-critical applications. Apply high development standards to build robust, scalable solutions and deliver complex enhancements in an SAP S/4HANA landscape. Contribute to ongoing and implementation activities in a global SAP Product Safety & Stewardship environment based on SAP S/4HANA Requirements : Strong functional and technical expertise in SAP Environment Management and Product Safety & Stewardship (EHS) solutions, including permits management for sealed surfaces and emissions Proven experience in developing, operating, and supporting SAP EHS solutions, including incident analysis and sustainable issue resolution. Strong experience with SAP ABAP and SAP Fiori development including the analysis, enhancement, and support of custom developments Strong ability to build understanding of custom solutions and business processes, including their functional design and technical implementation. Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with global stakeholders, key users, and cross-functional teams. WHAT WE OFFER Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.

jobs.name: Product Manager Procurement Solutions - (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF At BASF Digital Hub Hyderabad we develop innovative digital solutions for BASF, create new exciting customer experiences and business growth, and drive efficiencies in processes, helping to strengthen BASF´s position as the digital leader in the chemical industry. We believe the right path is through creativity, trial and error and great people working and learning together. Become part of our team and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities. Purpose : The Product Manager Procurement is accountable for the end-to-end ownership of a digital product within Global Digital Services (GD). The role ensures that the product delivers sustainable business value for Procurement by aligning product strategy, delivery, operations, and people development with BASF’s digital strategy and GD priorities. Accountabilities : • Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap in alignment with GD, Procurement, and business objectives. • Hold full accountability for the complete product lifecycle (ideation, build, run, optimize, retire). • Decide on priorities, scope, and release content based on value, risk, cost, quality, compliance, and sustainability. • Ensure transparency on product status, roadmap, risks, and key decisions within GD governance. jobs.name: Payroll & People Data (Analyst) - Mandarin Speaker (12 month leasing) 1
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Seek to understand business needs and provide support, services and solution on payroll, HR data, HR systems and processes. Offer day-to-day support to service recipients on HR inquiries, work collaboratively with others and perform research on payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislations in local countries. Participate in process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Main Tasks Operational Excellence Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data processes. Receive, analyze, process and follow-up on personnel administration requests via multiple request channels, ensure accurate and timely interface to the respective internal/ external processing chains i.e. payroll, finance, data analytics, external service providers, local authorities etc. Manage entire termination workflow for all termination types which includes leaves calculations, information checks, letters preparation, systems updates, payment calculations and exit clearance. Responsible for time and attendance administrations, including manual calculations on time allowances/deductions, maintenance of work schedules, leaves, overtimes, and shift substitutions. Support the delivery of payroll inputs and administration i.e. employee subsidies, fleet management, long service awards etc. Support the salary review exercises on employee data preparation, letter generations and new salary processing. Responsible for the local labor administrative activities and scheduled statutory HR reporting, ensure timely and compliant deliveries as per legislation requirements. Responds to recurring or ad-hoc business needs to design and to deliver country specific or customized reports which could not be auto-generated by the analytics/reporting systems. Work closely with other teams and external service providers to ensure all service requests are completed accurately and timely. Service Engagement Providing advisory on employee enquiries, policy/guidelines clarifications, HR processes, HR systems guides, legislation processes etc. Monitor multiples service requests to ensure services delivered by the team are in accordance with established Service Level Agreement (SLA) and internal Key Performance Indicators (KPI). Perform research and information gathering on local market HR news, legislation updates and guidelines to maintain internal knowledge database. Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and People data to develop the formation of competence hub. Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case, and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target. Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience Identify deviations from the established blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes and escalate to Assistant Manager and Manager in ensuring the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time. Team Collaboration Works with moderate supervision within established procedures and practices. Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously sharing knowledge with others. Assist in orientation training and act as buddy to new team member. Job Requirements Education Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce or Business Administration and Finance Work Experience With 1 - 2 years of related HR working experiences in Payroll and HR Operations Ability to read, write, and communicate fluently in Mandarin is required due to business needs and support for Mandarin-speaking markets Preferably experience in relevant HR procedures, practices, and country statutory Knowledge of integrated payroll systems, preferably SAP. Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrates a good level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location. Preferably understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. Strong processing skills and analytical abilities (inclination for Mathematics/Finance/Problem solving). Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills. Functional knowledge and skills in using Microsoft application i.e. Words, Excel, Power Point. About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Import Export, Trade Documentation (Senior Executive)

jobs.name: Senior Officer, Intellectual Property
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Act as Case Manager: Coordinate internally with the Intellectual Property Community, legal Community, (Senior) Business Management / strategy, Research & Development units linked to technical area, Functional units (Communication, Finance, Tax, Regulatory) globally; and liaise externally with Intellectual Property agencies and attorney, worldwide Patent Offices, Trademark offices, Enforcement bodies (Administration Bureaus and Courts). How You Will Do It Case Management Training on eIPMS (working platform) to assume role as Case manager: Filing of priority patent applications; Managing deadlines during global patent prosecutions (Patent Cooperation Treaty); Supporting IP counsels for contract/assignment management; handling inventor remunerations, communication to inventors, law firms and patent offices; data administration; Archive Management Maintaining patent, trademark and contract filing system. Achieving and securing original IP legal documents. Keeping sensitive material in confidential. Assisting and preparing legal documents Helping with notarization and legalization for different legal actions, preparing instruction letters and notice letters for remuneration according to requests. Helping with registrations with governmental authorities according to requests. General Administration Performing standard office procedures including scheduling appointments and business trip, e-mails, telephone calls; checking, distributing, filing in/out memos, letters, faxes, etc. In order to facilitate staff in execution of duties and provide efficient information needed for the department's operation. Cooperating with Company Administrative Department to plan and organize hotel accommodation, and ticket booking to facilitate the trips of department staff. Communicating with travel agency. What We Look For Bachelor’s degree in English/Law/Administration/Science From Fresh graduate to experienced paralegal in IP field Fluent in English communicating and writing Excellent communication & influencing skills Resilient and highly self-motivated Interest in international working environment and mobility is a plus

jobs.name: Talent Acquisition Digital Intern
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position As part of the Talent Acquisition team for Asia Pacific (APAC) region, you will be reporting to the Talent Acquisition Manager and supporting the team in managing recruitment tasks. Through this internship, you will get to learn more about Human Resources related to Business, Marketing, Human Resources and Communications. You will also have the opportunity to take charge of projects, delivering well-rounded solutions in elevating the experience of our new employee onboarding processes as well as supporting the implementation of the strategic people agenda. Besides, you will gain hands-on experience in the recruitment field and international exposure to work with diverse colleagues from different countries in APAC. Main Tasks Depending on business needs, your day-to-day responsibilities may include the following: Support candidate sourcing through various channels, including job boards, social media platforms, and professional networks. Collaborate with team members to deliver a seamless and positive candidate experience throughout the recruitment process. Provide administrative support to the recruitment team, including file management, documentation, and data maintenance. Assist in organising and coordinating recruitment-related events, activities, and engagement initiatives. Participate in team improvement discussions and contribute to improvement plans, timelines, and follow-up actions. Track and report key recruitment metrics, such as candidates sourced, conversion rates, and other relevant performance indicators. Identify opportunities for process improvement, automation, and digitalisation; prepare business cases and support implementation of digitalisation and process optimisation initiatives. Continuously review, update, and maintain knowledge documents within areas of expertise to support the development of a competence hub. Design and review portal content to enhance the new employee onboarding experience, including researching new tool features based on user needs and requirements. Requirements Able to commit to a minimum internship period of three (3) months. Currently pursuing or completing the final year of a bachelor’s degree or higher in Human Resources, Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, IT, or other related disciplines. Good planning and organisational skills, with an interest in administrative support and coordination. Independent, proactive, and eager to learn, with the willingness to take on new initiatives and responsibilities as assigned. A strong team player with a flexible, positive mindset and good communication and problem-solving skills. Adaptable and technology-savvy, with a strong interest in learning and applying digital tools to support recruitment and HR processes. jobs.name: Senior Process Engineer
jobs.description: Now hiring! Senior Process Engineer Chattanooga, TN (Onsite) Come create chemistry with us! We are seeking a Senior Process Engineer to be part of the Engineering team and will be based in Chattanooga, TN to support the overall success of the Dispersions & Resins business in North America. The Dispersions & Resins business of BASF develops, produces and markets a range of high-quality resins, additives, colorants and polymer dispersions worldwide. These raw materials are used in formulations for coatings and paints, printing and packaging products, construction chemicals, adhesives, fiberbondings, nonwovens, and paper manufacturing. As a Senior Process Engineer, you create chemistry by... Serve as a manufacturing representative on capital projects and execute commissioning and startup of capital projects Manage process optimization, troubleshooting, data analysis, and technical support to the operations organization Lead new product scale up activities including recipe development, trial oversight, and commercialization activities Provide technical support to the Operations Team in the resolution of plant problems Utilize technical troubleshooting techniques to improve processes and product quality Provide Technical support for risk assessments on Management of Changes to ensure changes do not jeopardize safety of plant Identify and lead continuous improvement projects to reduce waste and minimize variation in support of site goals Execute site Process Hazard Analyses (PHA) and work closely with the EH&S Specialists to ensure site compliance to BASF and other regulatory requirements Support the BASF Process Safety and Site PSM/RMP Program through ownership/upkeep of process safety information If you have... BS in Chemical Engineering or other engineering discipline 6+ years engineering experience in a manufacturing environment Previous experience identifying and implementing process modifications to improve efficiency/reliability Previous experience in facilitation and coordination of root cause analysis Preferred: Experience programming in DCS and/or PLC platforms (e.g. Honeywell, Delta V, ABB) Experience identifying and developing cost/benefit analysis for potential new projects Background in dispersion manufacturing Working knowledge and experience with Lean, Six Sigma, Kaizen, or similar methodologies Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

As a Production Leader you will play a key role in optimizing daily production activities to ensure safety, quality, and efficiency. You will collaborate closely with operations, quality, engineering, and maintenance teams to help drive continuous improvement and support plant performance. jobs.name: Analista de Logística Jr - Afirmativa para pessoas com deficiência - Jacareí/SP
jobs.description: Negócio A equipe da Logística é responsável por gerenciar o fluxo de materiais e produtos dentro do site, além ser um grande parceiro dos negócios BASF oferecendo soluções inovadoras e sustentáveis. Queremos ser reconhecidos como os melhores parceiros pelos nossos clientes, por isso somos apaixonados por novas tecnologias, simplificação, além de   buscarmos constantemente eficiência e qualidade em nossos processos, sempre com a visão de cuidar da segurança de todos Área A área de Operações Logísticas em Jacarei tem como missão alavancar o sucesso dos nossos clientes, com performance e custos  competitivos com o mercado e um time comprometido e qualificado, guiado por melhoria contínua e simplificação, sendo assim parceiro preferencial de nossos clientes, de forma a assegurar o recebimento, armazenamento, movimentação interna e distribuição dos produtos, disponibilizando-os na quantidade, qualidade e prazos acordados, contribuindo para otimização dos resultados da BASF. Se você possui foco no cliente, excelência na execução e sabe trabalhar em equipe, junte-se a nós! Candidato(a) Você fará parte da equipe de Operações Logísticas do site, atuando nas atividades de Recebimento e Expedição, incluindo: Entrada de notas fiscais de recebimento, lançamentos de estoque, inventários, faturamento e entre outras rotinas operacionais. Buscamos profissionais que estejam cursando o último ano ou possuam formação superior completa em Logística, Administração, Engenharia ou áreas correlatas. Possuir conhecimento básico em SAP, além de entendimento sobre fluxos operacionais e rotinas administrativas na área de Supply Chain e Logística é mandatório. Também é necessário ter disponibilidade para atuar em regime de turnos. Serão considerados diferenciais: Vivência em ambientes operacionais ou logísticos, conhecimento em ferramentas digitais, como Power BI, Excel Power Apps e Automation, incluindo aplicação no dia a dia. Nível intermediário de inglês e espanhol será um diferencial. Nossa química valoriza o talento e a diversidade de gênero, raça, idade, nacionalidade, crenças, orientação sexual, identidade e expressão de gênero, deficiências e todas as demais características, diferenças e semelhanças que fazem parte da diversidade humana. Convidamos você a se candidatar em nossas vagas!  #BASF_EuRecomendo
