jobs.name: Officer, Order Management (Mandarin Speaker) - 1 year leasing
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position This position aims to provide end-to-end delivery services by efficiently handling customer service and logistics management to maintain business continuity, enhance service level and safety continuity in business. Job Responsibilities: - Manage end-to-end daily order process and operation By collaborating with multiple functions (internal and external) including but not limited to customers, sales, GB, suppliers, finance, controlling. The operation includes but not limited to: Sales order creation PO transaction. Payment following up and order release. Delivery schedule coordination. Delivery order creation. Shipment creation. Transportation arrangement. Billing and invoice management. Documents collection. Month end reconciliation. Import/export related coordination and operation. Monitor shipping and import/export status for overseas supply & delivery. Escalate any issues for on-time fulfillment. Capture market intelligence on supply chain related topics. From daily interaction with customers and sharing with the business as appropriate, includes but not limited to: Provide best possible service to customers and promote the establishment of long-term customers relationships to ensure a sound profitable future customers base. Ensure best logistic cost execution through daily transportation management operation. Continuously improve end-to-end efficiency with creative ideas and digital tools’ support. Complete any unstated end-to-end officer-related tasks assigned by the manager Manage customers’ complaints NCM operation and communication according to defined procedures / system. Escalate critical NCMs when needed for appropriate resolution. Operational Compliance Ensure that all operations (also at customer premises) are carried out in accordance with internal standards and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree or higher in International Trade, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field. Work Experience: Minimum 2–3 years of experience in order-to-cash processes; experience in the chemical industry is an advantage. Language Skills: Proficient in Mandarin (both written and spoken) for communication with customers in China, as well as fluency in English. Technical Skills: Strong SAP competency is required. Personal Attributes: Thrives in a dynamic environment, maintains a positive and solution-oriented mindset, adapts well to change, collaborates effectively across teams, and consistently demonstrates strong professionalism and work ethic. Note: This position requires flexibility to work according to China holiday arrangement About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my

jobs.name: Intern (화장품 연구팀 지원)
jobs.description: Global chemical company BASF has been a key player in Korea since entering market in 1954. As a leading foreign company in Korea’s chemical industry, BASF operates eight large-scale production sites across the country. Additionally, the company runs advanced Performance Materials and Electronic Materials R&D Centers, along with two technical centers focused on Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE), Polyurethane, and Personal Care. In 2024, BASF records approximately € 1.1 billion in sales to customers in Korea and employed 1,240 people as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com . *Position summary: -Internship as an assistant of Market Development Team (cosmetic industry) 인턴십 근무기간은 2026년 8월 ~ 2027년 1월(6개월) 입니다. 내부 사정상 근무기간은 변경이 있을 수 있습니다. *Main Tasks: -Documentation work to support customers -Archives management for the documents related to selling products -Market research, new cosmetic regulation follow-up, etc. -Email communication with global team -Lab work would be included *Education/Qualifications required: -Education: BS or advanced degree in chemistry or related disciplines Only students in school(attending/on leave) can apply (no graduates allowed) 재학생, 휴학생, 졸업유예생만 지원 가능 -Fluent English speaking & writing preferred and Native level of Korean. -MS Office skills *Competencies required: -Sincere & professional attitude 면접은 서류전형 합격자에 개별통지하며, 제출하신 서류는 일체 반환하지 않습니다. 입사지원서 내용에 허위사실이 판명될 경우 입사가 취소될 수 있습니다. 국가보훈대상자와 신체장애자는 관련서류 제출시 관계법에 따라 우대합니다. 취업지원대상자 증명서(제출처: 한국바스프 반드시 기입) 발급받은 후 입사서류와 함께 제출하여야 가점 부여 가능합니다.

jobs.name: Customer Service Officer, SEA
jobs.description:
Objectives of the position: The Customer Service Officer, SEA is responsible for executing end-to-end customer service and order management activities to ensure efficient and accurate order fulfillment, export shipment coordination and high customer satisfaction across the SEA region. The role supports both inter-company and third-party customer operations while ensuring compliance with trade regulations, operational procedures and customer service standards. Core responsibilities : 1) To execute end-to-end Order-To-Cash (OTC) processes including order entry, order processing, delivery coordination, invoicing support and shipment tracking 2) To manage both inter-company orders and third-party customer orders to ensure timely and accurate fulfillment 3) To coordinate export shipments including shipping documentation, booking arrangements and delivery follow-up activities 4) To maintain proactive communication with customers regarding order status, product availability, shipment schedules and delivery updates 5) To perform regular checks on customer orders, pricing, credit blocks and compliance requirements within SAP systems 6) To ensure compliance with export control regulations, trade compliance requirements and company operational procedures 7) To coordinate closely with Supply Chain, Logistics, Warehouse, Finance and Sales teams to ensure smooth operational execution 8) To support resolution of customer complaints and raise Non-Conformance Management (NCM) cases where required, ensuring timely follow-up and closure with relevant stakeholders 9) To monitor order fulfillment performance and support continuous improvement initiatives to improve service efficiency and customer satisfaction 10) To maintain accurate master data, customer information, and operational documentation within SAP systems 11) To support preparation and verification of shipping and export documents including invoices, packing lists, bills of lading and insurance documentation 12) To act as backup support for other Customer Service team members during leave or absence periods to ensure business continuity and uninterrupted customer support operations Job requirements and qualifications : Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or related discipline Minimum 2–4 years of experience in Customer Service, Order Management, Logistics or Supply Chain operations Experience in export shipment coordination and order management within a regional environment is preferred Has a strong knowledge of Order-To-Cash processes, export shipment coordination and customer service operations Experience with SAP ERP systems (SD/MM modules preferred) and Microsoft Office applications Good communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment with strong attention to detail and accuracy Good knowledge of shipping documentation, trade complianc, and export processes will be an advantage Strong problem-solving mindset with customer-focused approach Ability to manage multiple priorities and work collaboratively with cross-functional teams Position may be based in Singapore, Thailand or Vietnam (on local terms) .  decision regarding applications. Data Protection The processing of your personal data is carried out by as the controller

 data exclusively on behalf of . Further information can be found in the privacy policy coatings privacy policy incl. Contact details .

jobs.name: Accountant Accounts Payable (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Accounts Payable and Freight Cost Management Department provides professional invoice management for all affiliated European BASF Group companies. It cooperates with the procurement and local finance departments, actively involved in the creation of an optimized creditor process chain. You manage invoice processing for BASF Group companies, including invoice control, posting, payment runs and account clarification in line with internal and external guidelines. Furthermore, you handle internal and external requests across various channels and ensure timely, accurate execution of FRC controls while preventing fraud. You independently resolve complex accounting topics, clarify open items (e.g. vendor accounts, GR/IR) and drive continuous process improvements. In addition, you support and coordinate cross-team activities, including special cases, complex problem resolution and project contributions. You create and maintain documentation and deliver trainings, supporting knowledge sharing and capability building within the team. WHAT YOU OFFER commercial business training or a degree in Business Administration first experience in accounting and projects communicative team player who is open to changes first experience with SAP fluent in English; German, Turkish, and Hungarian are an advantage WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 40.000 - 46.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience - In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Team and company events in which we celebrate our achievements and live up our team spirit Location-independent work models with defined days of presence in Berlin and place of residence in Germany - Here a first impression Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks HOW TO REACH US Viktoria Pohl (Talent Acquisition), viktoria.pohl@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-58390 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an online appointment. You can also reach our recruiting team here . First information about our application process can be found here . ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Strategic Account Manager Paper Coatings - Dispersions & Resins - Remote
jobs.description: Now hiring! Strategic Account Manager Paper Coatings - Dispersions & Resins - Remote Come create chemistry with us! Strategic Account Manager Paper Coatings – Dispersions & Resins – Remote Where the Chemistry Happens We are seeking a professional like you to lead strategic customer relationships within the Paper & Carpet segment serving the Paper Coatings Industry. In this role, you will serve as the primary commercial owner for assigned accounts, developing long-term business partnerships, driving profitable growth, identifying new opportunities, and ensuring exceptional customer experiences. As the Strategic Account Manager, you will translate business strategies into customer-specific growth plans, coordinate cross-functional execution, and serve as the primary interface between BASF and the customer organization. You will collaborate closely with Marketing, Technical Service, Supply Chain, Operations, Customer Service, and Business Management teams to deliver sustainable value and strengthen BASF’s position with strategic customers. For further information about the Dispersions & Resins business in North America, please visit http://www.basf.us/dpsolutions . As a Strategic Account Manager Paper Coatings - Dispersions & Resins - Remote, you create chemistry by... Own strategic customer relationships and account plans for assigned Paper Coatings accounts, including relationship mapping, opportunity pipeline management, and execution priorities aligned with business strategy. Develop long-term business partnerships with key customer stakeholders to promote BASF’s Dispersions & Resins portfolio, increase share of wallet, improve customer experience, and deliver profitable volume growth. Understand market trends and customer needs in Paper Coatings applications, then translate insights into differentiated BASF solutions, innovation opportunities, and growth initiatives. Lead customer communication, pricing negotiations, contract discussions, deal-closing, and follow-up activities to achieve sales, margin, volume, and profitability targets. Coordinate cross-functional execution with Marketing, Technical Service, R&D, Product Management, Supply Chain, Customer Care, Logistics, and Business Management to meet customer needs and ensure account plans are delivered. Manage customer forecasts, demand validation, Salesforce/SAP/CPQ data quality, and account performance tracking to support forecast accuracy, operational planning, and disciplined commercial execution. Gather market intelligence, competitor insights, service feedback, and customer pain points, and represent the voice of the customer to internal stakeholders to drive action and continuous improvement. Drive Digital solutions in your space If you have... Relevant experience in strategic account management, sales, business development, or another customer-facing commercial function. Strong commercial acumen with demonstrated ability to drive profitable growth, manage account financials, execute pricing strategies, and deliver volume, sales, and margin objectives. Proven ability to build relationships, influence decisions, and communicate effectively across multiple customer and internal stakeholder levels. Strong negotiation, strategic thinking, problem-solving, and project-management skills, with the ability to operate in complex and dynamic environments. Self-starter with strong ownership, disciplined follow-up, customer focus, and the ability to work effectively across cross-functional and cross-cultural teams. Experience in Paper Coatings applications. Proficiency with BASF tools and systems such as Salesforce, SAP, CPQ, Copilot, and related digital tools preferred. Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

jobs.description: Becario/a Finanzas - Accounts Payable & Business Reporting (m/f/d) Detalles del puesto | BASF SE Saltar al contenido principal Buscar ofertas de empleo Idioma Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Acceso empleado/a Descripción del puesto Becario/a Finanzas - Accounts Payable & Business Reporting (m/f/d) LUGAR COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE TRABAJO N° DE REFERENCIA OPCIONES DE TRABAJO FLEXIBLE LUGAR COMPAÑIA ÁREA DE TRABAJO TIPO DE TRABAJO N° DE REFERENCIA OPCIONES DE TRABAJO FLEXIBLE Enviar candidatura ahora ¡El mundo necesita innovación y nuestro equipo te necesita a ti! BASF es la primera empresa química del mundo #WeCreateChemistry y uno de los mejores lugares para trabajar y desarrollar tu carrera. Estamos en búsqueda de talento diverso, apasionado y creativo que desee hacer sus prácticas con nosotros/as, aprender de la mano de tutores/as experimentados/as y marcar la diferencia. BASF Española S.L. comercializa más de 5.000 productos para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros clientes y de la sociedad en una gran variedad de sectores. La industria química está cambiando; y BASF también. Descubre nuestra estrategia: #WinningWays . ¿Quieres poner en práctica tus conocimientos y crear química con nuestro equipo? LO QUE HARÁS Durante tus prácticas, colaborarás con el equipo de Finanzas de BASF y adquirirás experiencia práctica en dos áreas clave: Accounts Payable y Business Reporting . Tus funciones y aprendizajes incluirán: Dar soporte en el proceso de Accounts Payable, colaborando en: Gestión de facturación y contabilización en SAP. Actualización de MasterData de proveedores. Participar en actividades de Business Reporting, como: Contabilidad por centro de coste y seguimiento de gastos. Análisis de Pérdidas y Ganancias (P&L) y cálculo de costes de producto. Valoración de inventarios y procedimientos financieros relacionados. Colaborar con profesionales experimentados/as para comprender cómo los datos financieros impulsan las decisiones de negocio. Desarrollar una base sólida en herramientas de contabilidad y reporting utilizadas en finanzas corporativas. LO QUE NECESITAMOS DE TI Estar cursando un grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas (ADE) o Economía, con posibilidad de firmar convenio de prácticas con tu centro de estudios, o recién graduado/a Nivel de inglés B2 o superior Mentalidad digital, habilidades de comunicación y ganas de aprender Disponibilidad para comenzar las prácticas a mediados de enero o inicios de febrero de 2026 LO QUE OFRECEMOS Nuestro programa de prácticas te ofrece el perfecto inicio en el mundo de BASF. Desde el comienzo acompañaremos tu aprendizaje. Además, contarás con: Tutoría individualizada : con una persona tutora a tu lado para apoyar tu desarrollo profesional, resolver dudas y darte feedback continuo. Proyectos reales : participarás desde el primer día en proyectos y actividades que impactan al negocio y a nuestros clientes. Prácticas remuneradas : contarás con un apoyo económico mensual proporcional a tu dedicación de tiempo durante tu estancia. Inclusión : queremos que seas uno/a más del equipo, y también podrás participar en eventos de empresa durante este periodo. Formación continua : además del aprendizaje diario, podrás realizar interesantes cursos para aprovechar al máximo tu tiempo con nuestro equipo. Flexibilidad : adaptaremos los horarios de las prácticas a tu carga lectiva para que puedas compaginarlas de la mejor manera. CÓMO PARTICIPAR EN EL PROCESO Envía tu CV a través de la oferta para poder conocernos. Si tu perfil encaja, te llamaremos desde el equipo español de Talent Acquisition en Alemania (prefijo +49) y te acompañaremos en el proceso. ¡Con cada paso estarás más cerca de conocer a tu futuro equipo! Si tienes más preguntas sobre este proceso, contacta con nuestro/a compañero/a: Andrea (andrea.lopez-gutierrez@basf.com*) *Por favor, ten en cuenta que no podremos aceptar tu currículum por este e-mail. MÁS SOBRE BASF Celebramos la diversidad y activamente promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo donde cada voz cuenta y cada idea es valorada sin importar edad, género, estado civil, etnia, religión, condición física/mental u orientación sexual. #BeYourself Somos personas C reativas, A biertas, R esponsables y E mprendedoras. Estos son nuestros valores, que nos guían y describen cómo somos y actuamos. Si tú también te identificas con ellos, ¡BASF es tu sitio! ¡La diversidad es nuestra mayor fortaleza! Conviértete en parte de nuestra fórmula ganadora para el éxito y desarrolla el futuro con nosotros/as, en un equipo global que abraza la inclusión y la igualdad de oportunidades independientemente de la edad, género, orientación sexual, discapacidad o creencias. jobs.name: Shift Supervisor - Hannibal, MO (Palmyra)
jobs.description: Now hiring! Shift Supervisor - Hannibal, MO (Palmyra) Palmyra, MO (On-site) Annual bonus / Overtime pay eligible We are looking for a Shift Supervisor to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Palmyra, MO. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental, and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. As a Shift Supervisor you will lead others toward the accomplishment of production targets in a safe and environmentally conscious manner. You also may be assigned to any one of five production units at the Hannibal Plant that produce insecticides, herbicides, or intermediate chemicals used in the production of insecticides and/or herbicides. As a Shift Supervisor - Hannibal, MO (Palmyra), you create chemistry by... Leading others toward the accomplishment of production targets in a safe and environmentally conscious manner. Safety of all personnel in his or her plant is the foremost concern of our supervisors and only individuals with the highest commitment to common safety will be considered for the role. Being a results-driven leader who can get things done by working through others. Initiating work orders, enters them into the maintenance work management system (SAP), and working with maintenance to reduce cost and minimize downtime. Working closely with engineers to recommend process improvements and to troubleshoot the manufacturing process and managing the daily activities of the chemical operators in the assigned area, while building a sustainable culture of safety awareness and responsibility. Ensuring that proper documentation is maintained, procedures for material and product handling and storage are observed, and that production reports are completed in a timely manner. Additionally, you will manage the job setup procedure for contractors, including confined space entry, lock-out, tag-out and the safety work permit systems. Conducting safety meetings, ensuring that subordinates are trained properly, developing unit staffing schedules, and administering discipline according to company policy and the collective bargaining agreement. You will also participate in emergency response teams and receive special training on hazardous materials, regulatory compliance, or other aspects of chemical plant operations. If you... Hold a High school diploma or equivalent education. Possess 3 years’ experience as an operator in one or more of the operating units at the BASF Hannibal plant OR 3 years supervisory experience in an industrial manufacturing environment outside of BASF. Show strong personal commitment to building and maintaining a safety culture. Demonstrate coaching and leadership skills, tact, and the ability to communicate effectively and build strong relationships with individuals at all levels of the organization. Have computer literacy and familiarity with Microsoft Office Suite, as well as commitment to continuous improvement of computer skills when required. Possess good problem solving and troubleshooting skills, with the ability to quickly address and resolve equipment, process, and people issues. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Wir ermöglichen BASF den Zugang zu erneuerbarem Strom und weiteren Energieträgern. Wir investieren in Produktionsanlagen, schließen langfristige Lieferverträge ab, handeln an Großhandelsmärkten und versorgen so die BASF-Standorte mit Energie zu wettbewerbsfähigen Preisen. WAS SIE ERWARTET Im Rahmen Ihrer Tätigkeit übernehmen Sie die Verantwortung für die Weiterentwicklung und Umsetzung der Einkaufs- und Vertriebsstrategie Erdgas für Europa unter Berücksichtigung der Anforderungen in den zugeordneten Ländern. Dabei sind Sie zuständig für die Verhandlung von Rahmenverträgen zum Weitervertrieb an Drittkunden und für den Aufbau von Kontakten zu Lieferanten und Drittkunden. Darüber hinaus erstellen Sie ein potenzielles Drittkundenportfolio und koordinieren die zur Entscheidung notwendigen Informationen mit den entsprechenden Fachstellen (Credit, Legal). Zu Ihrem Aufgabenbereich gehört zudem der Aufbau von Markt- und Preiskenntnissen inklusive Regulierungsrahmen sowie die Mitarbeit in zukünftigen Preissicherungsprogrammen (Hedging). Women in Business) Mentoringprogramme und Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Karrierewege als fachliche Weiterentwicklung auf andere Stellen im Unternehmen (auch Jobrotation) Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung (z.B. betriebliche Kinderkrippe, Ferienprogramme) Fitness – und Gesundheitsstudio, Medical Center mit zahlreichen Angeboten für Ihre Gesundheit Deutschlandticket als vergünstigtes Jobticket, Jobrad, Verfügbarkeit von E-Ladesäulen für Privatfahrzeuge SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Bonnie Gardt, bonnie.gardt@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59672 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. jobs.description:
Ausbildung Werkfeuerwehrmann:frau (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profil-Login Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Ausbildung Werkfeuerwehrmann:frau (m/w/d) STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) STANDORT FIRMA AUSBILDUNGSBEGINN (‑DAUER) Jetzt bewerben Über UNS Es ist Zeit fürs nächste Level. Zeit für Deinen Start in die Praxis. Unsere Ausbildung im Industriepark Lausitz macht Dich fit für die Zukunft! Denn hier hast Du an einem Standort viele Möglichkeiten und lernst von Profis, die ihr Know-how in verschiedenen Bereichen und Unternehmen einbringen. Bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz arbeitest Du mit moderner Technik, bist mittendrin statt nur dabei und lernst Skills, die Dir wirklich was bringen. Keine trockene Theorie – echte Praxis, echtes Team, echte Zukunft. Starte Deine Ausbildung bei BASF im Industriepark Lausitz – und profitiere von einem Erfahrungsschatz, der weit über einen Betrieb hinausgeht. Your move. Your future. DAS LERNST DU Während Deiner Ausbildung als Werkfeuerwehrmann:frau wirst Du zur furchtlosen Gefahrenexpertin oder zum mutigen Gefahrenexperten! Auf Deinem Stundenplan stehen elektro-, metall-, sanitär-, heizungs- und klimatechnische Basics, sowie Holzbauarbeiten für den Feuerwehreinsatz. Außerdem lernst Du die rechtlichen Grundlagen des Feuerwehrdienstes kennen und weißt anschließend alles über die Feuerwehrfahrzeuge, Geräte, Brandgeschehen, Löschmittel und Löschverfahren . Du erwirbst deinen LKW-Führerschein der Klasse CE, das deutsche Sportabzeichen sowie sowie die Qualifikation zum bzw. zur Rettungsschwimmer:in und -sanitäter:in an unserem Standort. Nicht zuletzt behältst Du in brenzligen Situationen einen kühlen Kopf und sorgst mit wachsamem Auge für die Sicherheit von Mensch und Anlage. DAS BIETEN WIR eine feste Ansprechperson während Deiner gesamten Ausbildung Ausbildungsvergütung 1. Jahr: 1.192 € / 2. Jahr: 1.254 € / 3. Jahr: 1.279 € 30 Tage Urlaub bei einer 38,5-Stunden Woche vergünstigtes Jobticket zahlreiche Zusatzleistungen wie Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld, Erfolgsbeteiligung, Betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme am Aktienprogramm, moderne IT-Ausstattung und Sportangebote Möglichkeit zur Teilnahme an Azubiaustauschprogrammen finanzielle Unterstützung für Azubis von weiter weg Ausbildung findet in Kooperation mit unserem Verbundpartner QCW in Eisenhüttenstadt sowie dem ÜAZ in Frankfurt/Oder statt DAS BRINGST DU MIT Du bist am ersten Ausbildungstag mindestens 16,5 Jahre alt. eine gute Mittlere Reife oder ein höherer Schulabschluss körperliche Fitness und Höhentauglichkeit technisches Verständnis und handwerkliches Geschick großes Maß an Verantwortungsbewusstsein, Engagement und Freude an der Arbeit im Team AUSBILDUNG GESCHAFFT - WAS DANN ? Nach Deiner Ausbildung kannst Du die stabile Seitenlage im Schlaf durchführen, riechst mögliche Gefahren bereits 10 Meilen gegen den Wind und gehst täglich mit der Gewissheit ins Bett, wieder einmal den Standort vor allem Unheil bewahrt zu haben. Mit der Lizenz zur Lebensrettung stehen Dir in unseren Bereichen, wie z.B. der Leitstelle oder technischen Hilfeleistung, die Türen weit offen. Außerdem erwarten Dich interessante Entwicklungs- und Weiterbildungschancen, wie z.B. zum/zur (Ober-)Brandmeister:in oder im gehobenen feuerwehrtechnischen Dienst. KONTAKT Du hast Fragen zu der Stelle? Wende Dich an Frank Porthun, Tel.: +49 1743481235, frank.porthun@basf.com . Du hast Fragen zum Bewerbungsprozess? Wende Dich an Paula Seez, Tel: +49 30200 556641, paula.seez@basf.com oder buche Dir hier einen Termin via MS Teams. jobs.name: Logistics Officer - temporary (1 year)
jobs.description: We are the world's leading chemical company because we offer intelligent solutions for our customers and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to advance. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, without regard to gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. We are looking for a new colleague to join our Supply Chain & Logistics team. Our future team member is professionally well-prepared, committed to high-quality work, and open to gaining new knowledge and continuous development. Do you recognize yourself? Join the BASF team! RESPONSIBILITIES Take ownership of end‑to‑end stock and warehouse operations, keeping inventory lean, available, and cost‑efficient Plan and coordinate shipments from order entry to final delivery, including transport mode and forwarder selection Drive freight and warehouse cost optimization through smart consolidation and cost control Collaborate with warehouse and logistics partners, ensuring compliance with BASF standards, EHS, and regulations Handle logistics documentation, invoicing, additional costs, claims, and NCM topics with confidence Support audits, physical stock counts, reporting, and data accuracy in SAP master data Prepare EPR and environmental product fee reports Perform EKAER registrations QUALIFICATIONS University / college degree in related field and/or Work experience within a similar sales/customer support / logistics role Advanced MS Office and SAP knowledge Fluent in English and Hungarian knowledge Growth Mindset, optimistic, „can-do“ attitude Demonstrate customer focus, collaborate for achievement Strong communication and interpersonal understanding skills BENEFITS We offer excellent working environment and conditions (flexible work arrangement and mobile working options), a compelling total offer and prospects of continuous development of professional skills and career. Join the best team with more than 110,000 other minds to connect with globally. Explore your BASF career options at basf.com/career .

jobs.name: Teamspecialist Trade Invoicing Services (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a Teamspecialist Trade Invoicing Services EMEA in our unit, you will be responsible for the subject-matter leadership of a highly motivated international team supporting BASF's Accounts Receivable process for outgoing invoices across EMEA. You ensure the operational provision of Trade Invoicing services, focusing on the creation, monitoring and compliance of outgoing customer invoices. You support and advise the team in resolving complex SAP-related issues in SAP R/3 and SAP S/4, with a particular focus on the SD module. You act as a key contact for business units, customer service, supply chain, IT and other stakeholders regarding complex invoicing topics within the Order-to-Cash process. You contribute as first level support to the implementation and support of countryspecific eInvoicing implemnttations across EMEA. You support the disciplinary manager in resource planning, employee development and knowledge management activities. You drive continuous improvement initiatives to increase invoicing efficiency, effectiveness and compliance. Furthermore, you will also take appropriate measures to ensure that the necessary knowledge for theprovision of services is available to the team at any time. In addition, you handle multiple optimization and service transition projects at once, dealing with ambiguity, tight deadlines and rapidly changing priorities in a fast-paced international environment. WHAT YOU OFFER degree in Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, Supply Chain Management or a comparable qualification several years of professional experience in Accounts Receivable, Trade Invoicing, Order-to-Cash or a comparable area excellent SAP R/3 and SAP S/4 knowledge, ideally with strong expertise in the SD module as well as understanding for process automation experience with digital invoicing processes, EDI integrations and eInvoicing solutions experience in project work, process optimization and stakeholder management strong communication skills in English, additional language skills are an advantage customer-focused mindset, openness to change, and a strong commitment to teamwork, knowledge sharing, and a constructive feedback culture WHAT WE OFFER Gross annual salary (full-time): 55.000 - 59.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience. In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success as well as individual performance 30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs Location-independent working models with defined days of presence in Berlin and residence in Germany - here a first impression Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks Modern sustainable office building in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg with flexible working options, rooftop terrace and cafeteria for innovative and team-focused collaboration - get to know our location Subsidy for public transport, personal employee benefit card and other discounts HOW TO REACH US Jennifer Weiz (Talent Acquisition), jennifer.weiz@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56454 will be happy to answer your questions for this position You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

jobs.name: Global Communications Manager, Pharma Solutions
jobs.description: Now hiring! Global Communications Manager, Pharma Solutions Florham Park, NJ (Hybrid) We are looking for a Global Communications Manager to join our Pharma Solutions team in Florham Park. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF Nutrition and Health, we develop, produce and market ingredients and solutions for the nutrition and health industries that fulfill the highest safety, regulatory and sustainability standards. We bring our passion and energy to work every day, knowing that through our cutting-edge research and global collaboration, we deliver products that support the health and wellbeing of humans and animals alike across the globe. Our areas of impact include Human Nutrition, Animal Nutrition, Aroma Ingredients, Pharma Solutions and Enzymes. Working in Nutrition and Health offers opportunities for you to develop and grow at every stage of your career. If you enjoy working in a truly inclusive, and highly collaborative environment where you will be valued, recognized, and rewarded for your contributions, we look forward to meeting you! In this position, you will be responsible for global communications for BASF’s Pharma Solutions business, shaping its reputation and driving strategic external and internal messaging across markets. Manage end-to-end communications, digital marketing, and crisis response while advising senior leadership and ensuring alignment with business goals. This is an individual contributor position that requires cross-functional collaboration. As a Global Communications Manager, Pharma Solutions, you create chemistry by… Leading global external, internal, and change communications for the Pharma Solutions Global Business Unit, aligning communication strategies with business, marketing, and organizational objectives. Serving as a trusted advisor to the GBU SVP and leadership team, providing strategic communications counsel and driving execution of key initiatives across Pharma Solutions globally and the Americas region. Developing and implementing integrated communication, digital marketing, and media relations strategies that enhance brand reputation, support business growth, and engage target stakeholders worldwide. Creating compelling content and communication campaigns across digital, social, trade, and business media channels, while building and maintaining strong relationships with industry media and journalists. Leading employee engagement and change communication programs, including town halls, leadership communications, video messages, and other initiatives that strengthen organizational alignment and culture. Managing end-to-end communication projects, including planning, budgeting, performance monitoring, and coordination of internal teams and external agencies to ensure measurable results. Developing and executing crisis and issues management communication plans, providing strategic guidance, media responses, and stakeholder communications in collaboration with business and corporate communications teams. jobs.name: DevOps Engineer
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role A DevOps Engineer is responsible for streamlining and automating the processes involved in software development, deployment, and maintenance. This role emphasizes collaboration, integration, and communication between software developers and IT operations professionals to enhance the efficiency of development and operational workflows. The DevOps Engineer focuses on building and maintaining the infrastructure and tools that facilitate continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), ensuring high-quality software delivery with minimal downtime How Will You It Infrastructure Management : Design, implement, and manage scalable cloud infrastructure and on-premises systems. Utilize Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools (such as Terraform or CloudFormation) to automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration. CI/CD Pipeline Development : Create and maintain CI/CD pipelines to automate the software build, test, and deployment processes. Streamline workflows to ensure quick and reliable software releases while minimizing errors and manual interventions. Monitoring and Performance Optimization : Implement monitoring and logging solutions to track system performance, application health, and user experience. Analyze metrics and logs to identify issues and optimize system performance for reliability and scalability. Collaboration and Communication : Foster a culture of collaboration between development, operations, and other stakeholders. Act as a liaison to facilitate communication and ensure alignment on project goals and timelines. Configuration Management : Use configuration management tools (such as Ansible, Puppet, or Chef) to automate the deployment and management of applications and services across various environments. Security and Compliance : Integrate security practices into the DevOps processes (DevSecOps) to ensure that applications and infrastructure comply with security standards and best practices. Conduct regular security assessments and vulnerability scans. Incident Management : Respond to and troubleshoot production incidents, working collaboratively with development and operations teams to resolve issues quickly. Conduct post-incident reviews to identify root causes and implement preventative measures. Documentation : Maintain comprehensive documentation of processes, configurations, and system architecture. Ensure that knowledge is shared within the team to support onboarding and continuous improvement. Continuous Improvement : Advocate for and implement best practices for DevOps processes, tools, and methodologies. Stay updated with industry trends and emerging technologies to enhance the organization’s DevOps capabilities. What We Look For Education -  BE, BTech, MCA Experinece - Min 5yrs of exp required Skills - Azure Clould, Azure Devops (CI/CD), Kubernet, Terraform, Keycloack, Finops, Azure Networking. Communication Skill -  Ability to communicate effectively and professionally with stakeholders across all levels.

jobs.description: Laborant:in Analytik (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Laborant:in Analytik (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Der Lausitzer Produktionsstandort ist auf Wachstum ausgerichtet. Mit dem Bau der Anlage für Batteriematerialien legt die BASF in Schwarzheide den Grundstein für eine europäische Wertschöpfungskette der Elektromobilität. Um unseren CO2-Fußabdruck zu verbessern setzen wir auf effiziente Produktionsverfahren, innovatives Kreislaufmanagement und eine nachhaltige Energiestrategie. Dazu zählt auch die Rückgewinnung der Rohstoffe durch Recycling um damit den Kreislauf zu schließen. Werden Sie Teil dieser Reise und entdecken Sie das vielseitige Produktportfolio von BASF. WAS SIE ERWARTET Unsere Geschäftseinheit Analytical Services unterstützt die Produktion der Batteriematerialien am Standort durch die produktionsbegleitende Analytik sowie Rohstoffeingangs- und Endprodukt-Analysen. In Ihrer neuen Rolle führen Sie Prüfungen entsprechend festgelegter Prüfpläne oder abgestimmter Analysestrategien durch und bereiten Proben unter Anwendung aktueller Analyse- und Auswerteverfahren vor. Mit Ihrem geschulten Blick erkennen Sie Unregelmäßigkeiten im Betriebsablauf, unterstützen bei der Identifikation möglicher Ursachen und leiten Maßnahmen zu einer effizienten Störungsbeseitigung ein. Qualität wird bei uns großgeschrieben: Sie dokumentieren Analyseergebnisse sowie Prüfberichte unter Einbeziehung etwaiger Qualitätsabweichungen in EDV-Systemen. Mit Ihrem Engagement lassen wir unsere Vision Realität werden und bilden das beste Team für unseren Standort in Schwarzheide. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Sie bringen nicht alles mit? Keine Sorge, wir suchen nach Persönlichkeiten, die etwas bewegen und unser gemeinsames Arbeitsumfeld gestalten möchten! abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Chemie- oder Physiklaborant:in oder eine vergleichbare Berufsausbildung idealerweise erste Berufserfahrungen in der chemischen Industrie umfassendes Wissen in der Durchführung von Prüfungen und der Dokumentation von Analyseergebnissen sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit WAS WIR BIETEN Wir bieten Ihnen einen spannenden Job an einem starken Wachstumsstandort, der Nachhaltigkeit und Innovation eng im Fokus hat. Arbeiten Sie in einer offenen Arbeitsatmosphäre, in der die Vielfalt von Menschen, Meinungen und Erfahrungen willkommen ist und geschätzt wird. Profitieren Sie vom starken Tarifvertrag der Chemischen Industrie (attraktives Gehalt, Urlaubsgeld i.H.v. 1.200 €, 13. Monatsgehalt) sowie unseren zusätzlichen betrieblichen Leistungen. Zusätzlich erwarten Sie eine jährliche Erfolgsbeteiligung, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, eine Pflegezusatzversicherung sowie ein Langzeitkonto für einen flexiblen Renteneinstieg inkl. Zuschuss von 550 € pro Jahr. Sie erhalten die Möglichkeit zur Unternehmensbeteiligung durch den Erwerb von Aktien. Die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf unterstützen wir mit zusätzlichen Familientagen (bis Ihr Kind das 6. Lebensjahr vollendet hat). Ihre Gesundheit liegt uns am Herzen, deshalb bekommen Sie außerdem ein jährliches Guthaben in Höhe von 200,00 € für individuell gestaltbare Gesundheitsleistungen sowie einen zusätzlichen freien Tag. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Nina Schülzke (Talent Acquisition), nina.schuelzke@basf.com , Tel. +49 30 2005-58790 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF in Schwarzheide, zwischen der Hauptstadt Berlin und der Metropole Dresden gelegen, produziert funktionale Materialien und entwickelt intelligente Systemlösungen für globale Kunden. Wir stehen für Innovation, digitale Transformation und Nachhaltigkeit. Mit unseren neuen Technologien und Arbeitsweisen übernehmen wir eine Vorreiterrolle innerhalb der BASF-Gruppe. Nutzen Sie die Chance und gestalten Sie mit uns zukunftsorientierte Lösungen. Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. jobs.description: Now hiring! Technical Service Manager, Industrial Specialties Market (Remote, US) Remote - US We are looking for a Technical Service Manager to join our Industrial Formulators, Care Chemicals team in the US. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Care Chemicals division is a leading global supplier of intermediates to cosmetics, detergents, and industrial manufacturing segments. We also offer solutions for technical applications and industrial process markets. Together with our customers, we create innovative solutions to meet the current and future needs of society more sustainably. We contribute to the long-term success of our customers’ brands with a broad range of products and via our global network of production and development sites. Industrial formulators deliver value by servicing high-value life-science and industrial specialty segments with high-performance ingredients used across diverse industrial applications.  We elevate everyday outcomes through superior chemistry, that boosts application and process performance, supplying surfactants, chelates, and water-soluble polymers to industrial customers and end-use markets including agrochemical; CASE raw materials (emulsion polymer latex manufacturing, aqueous resin emulsions, wetting and rheology modifying additives manufacturing, pigment dispersions); pulp and paper; water treatment; rubber/plastic; textile & leather; health, and contract/chemical processing manufacturing. We are seeking a results driven professional to join the Industrial Formulators, Care Chemicals team and lead field technical market development for the industrial specialties segment. You will own the segment’s technical pipeline and lead day-to-day field execution – building and nurturing technical relationships with customer R&D and technical teams through regular on-site engagement, troubleshooting, and application support.  You will provide hands-on technical service to solve problems, drive new customer project development, deliver technical trainings, and accelerate adoption of our portfolio by providing data-driven performance justification that supports commercial wins. As the end-market technical expert, you will partner closely with the commercial team to identify and pursue customer opportunities, coordinating with regional and global technical and development teams to deliver tailored customer solutions.  You will develop and optimize field tactics that translate technical capabilities into business value, lead technical project delivery, strengthen BASF portfolio position, and elevate BASF as the preferred surfactant partner for customer technical teams across industrial end-markets. Technical Project Leadership : Builds and own the technical opportunity pipeline; leads customer technical projects guiding technical teams on application testing and troubleshooting to meet customer needs. Product & Application Development Support: Identifies customer pain points and unmet needs; collaborate with R&D to modify or develop products and support new production introductions and commercialization Commercial & Sales Support: Collaborate with commercial and marketing teams to support account managers by developing segment-specific value propositions, technical briefs, and application data that demonstrate customer benefits and enable formulation success. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. jobs.description: Now hiring! In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis.

Se você gosta de desafios, pensamento estratégico e busca desenvolvimento constante, aqui é o seu lugar. Em nosso dia a dia, atuamos na defesa dos interesses jurídicos da BASF, gerenciando litígios e disputas judiciais e administrativas, mitigando riscos legais e apoiando o negócio na proteção de seus direitos e ativos, sempre com foco na segurança jurídica e na sustentabilidade das operações. A posição atua em estreita coordenação com a estrutura global de Litígios da BASF, realizando o reporte operacional dos principais casos ao responsável global da área nos Estados Unidos e contribuindo para a definição e execução de estratégias alinhadas às diretrizes globais da empresa.
