jobs.name: Yeosu AEM Process Engineer (Contractor)
jobs.description: Global chemical company BASF has been a key player in Korea since entering market in 1954. As a leading foreign company in Korea’s chemical industry, BASF operates eight large-scale production sites across the country. Additionally, the company runs advanced Performance Materials and Electronic Materials R&D Centers, along with two technical centers focused on Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE), Polyurethane, and Personal Care. In 2024, BASF records approximately € 1.1 billion in sales to customers in Korea and employed 1,240 people as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com . This position is a fixed-term contract role from September 2026 to February 2028 (1 year and 6 months). Please note that the employment period is subject to change based on business needs. *Main Tasks of new process engineer: 1.Carry out Electronic material plant CAPEX project as process engineer role -Supervise the installation and operation of new facilities to produce new products. -Participate to FAT and SAT for project items and correct the wrong implementation 2.Technology Management -Maintain and optimize process system and implement OpEx -Review and revise P&ID, operation manual, work procedure and standard(SOPs) -Prepare technical report to improve process, safety and quality -Implement MOC to improve process, safety and quality 3.Production process management -Make RM & finish good inventory plan & manage through S&OP meeting 4.SHEQ management -Following company and legal EHS requirements, implement the relevant safety and environment protection policies and procedures with EHS team -To prevent safety incident and environmental release, keep eyes on workplace by frequent patrol and field coaching 5.Production management - Report relevant production records / statistics to supervisor and relevant department in order to plan, set and update production schedules when necessary, regulate workflow to ensure maximum use of capacity and efficiency - Control the unit ratio and utility consumption and analyze production cost 6.Lead and train plant operators - Lead and train plant operators to ensure safe and stable plant operation 7. Maintain production facility with maintenance team : Corrective and Preventive maintenance plan, TAR 8. Manage production relevant consumable materials inventory * Education/Qualification: - BS or higher in chemical engineering or related field is preferred - Minimum 3 years of experience in a chemical manufacturing plant environment. - Good interpersonal communication skill and fluent in English include comprehensive listening, speech, reading and writing and Native level of Korean. - Ability to effectively communicate detailed and precise technical requirement, procedures, and results in written and oral format. - Team player, self-motivated and proactive attitude. * Competencies required: 1. Work Management/Planning and Organizing - Work Management/Planning and Organizing’ is the ability to plan, prioritize and organize work and ensure adequate allocation of resources. 2. Safety, Health and Environment Awareness - Safety, Health and Environment Awareness’ means to be aware of conditions that affect customers, the public and employees’ safety, health and environment. 3. Quality Orientation - Quality Orientation’ is the setting of high goals or standards of performance for self, others, and the organization. Once initial goals are achieved, it includes the identification of opportunities to continuously improve work processes, safety; and/or standards of excellence rather than having standards imposed by others. *Special Requirements if any: - Hazardous Materials Industrial Engineer certification preferred - Practical experience in chemical plant operations preferred. - Proficiency in document preparation using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word is required. 면접은 서류전형 합격자에 개별통지하며, 제출하신 서류는 일체 반환하지 않습니다. 입사지원서 내용에 허위사실이 판명될 경우 입사가 취소될 수 있습니다. 국가보훈대상자와 신체장애자는 관련서류 제출시 관계법에 따라 우대합니다. 취업지원대상자 증명서(제출처: 한국바스프 반드시 기입) 발급받은 후 입사서류와 함께 제출하여야 가점 부여 가능합니다.

jobs.name: OVM Support & Performance Management (Executive)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position Responsible for all activities associated with performance and service reporting to ensure a consistent high level of performance and focus on continuous improvement throughout the Global Operations Support unit. Responsible for organizing and coordination of meetings, collaborative initiative and manage follow up on assigned action items. Support intra-regional and inter-regional system reporting and analysis, utilizing both internal and external data and reports. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Run data extracts using BASF systems and databases on fixed intervals (daily, weekly, monthly…) and as required to support business requirements and during crisis management. Validate data accuracy and append necessary information to enable generation of reports. Provide monthly KPI reporting as well as other necessary data analysis and reporting to support the smooth and efficient operation of the Global Operations Support unit. Work closely with the local & regional teams to ensure consistent reporting of performance across all operations including reporting required for day-to-day operations. Support continuous improvement and digitalization projects for the group by conducting detailed data and performance analytics. Work closely with other regions to ensure accurate reporting of KPIs and adherence to target levels and to establish a clear understanding of what is driving the end-to-end delivery performance to customers. Coordinate regular meetings of the various groups and systematic and timely follow up on outstanding action items. Involvement in regular ODS operational meetings for updates from stakeholders (internal & External) and to share updates on improvements / enhancements in performance reporting. Operational vendor management activities support on performance reporting and analytics, training / support, etc. where assigned. Operational vendor management monitoring support, inbound status reporting & documentation, etc. Analyze and assess freight costs to identify areas of improvement and cost-saving opportunities. Job Requirements Education Tertiary education in Logistics/Supply Chain/Business Management or related field Working Experience Min 1-3 years in handling supply chain and/or import/export logistics operations preferred Entry level to be considered based on demonstrated excellent track record of performance reporting and data analysis know-how. Familiar with performance management of logistics service provider especially performance metrics and supplier management Experience in SAP/ERP TM module Technical & Professional Knowledge Proficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint, Word, MS Teams Knowledge in SAP system (Transport Management) and data analytics preferred. Familiar with dashboards and reporting tools SCI, Power BI, Tableau preferred. Have knowledge of Supply Chain & procurement Processes, Transportation Management and Import export operations Proactive team member who takes ownership and shows persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations. Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions. Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024.

jobs.name: Chargé de Communication (h/f)
jobs.description: BIENVENUE CHEZ BASF Le monde a besoin d’innovation et nous avons besoin de vous ! Nous créons de la chimie pour un avenir durable et nous cherchons des talents capables d'apporter des réponses aux questions de demain. Intégrez la division Industries et Services de BASF où nous commercialisons les quelques 8 000 produits fabriqués par le groupe et destinés à améliorer la vie quotidienne. Êtes-vous prêt pour une nouvelle étape dans votre carrière et contribuer au futur d’une chimie toujours plus durable au sein de nos équipes ? VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITÉS Rattaché(e) au Service dédié, vous accompagnez le déploiement de nos actions de communication. À ce titre, vous : Participez à la définition et à l’exécution de la stratégie de communication globale, sur les volets national et local ; Mettez en œuvre des actions de communication externe (relations médias, organisation d’évènements, valorisation des initiatives) ; Contribuez aux dispositifs de communication interne et à l’organisation d’événements Corporate ; Développez et animez les supports de communication digitale ; Apportez un soutien sur des projets transverses du service des Affaires Publiques et de la Communication et pouvez représenter BASF lors d’évènements. VOTRE PROFIL Nous recherchons un(e) candidat(e) rigoureux(se) et doté(e) d’un bon relationnel ; Titulaire d’un Master 2 en Communication, Sciences Politiques ou Lettres, vous justifiez d’expérience dans le domaine ; Enfin, votre niveau d’anglais professionnel vous permet de communiquer aisément à l’écrit comme à l’oral. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Situé au coeur de Paris dans le quartier de la bourse, l’établissement Industrie et Services est le siège social de BASF France SAS et compte plus de 200 collaborateurs. Ce site regroupe diverses fonctions support et commerciales qui couvrent différents domaines. Nos principaux clients sont les industriels de l’automobile, la construction, la cosmétique, l’hygiène, la santé, l’agro-alimentaire, l’emballage, ou encore l’électrotechnique. La diversité est notre plus grande force Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendamment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances.

jobs.name: Responsable Grands Comptes Europe - Tensioactifs standards et Alcools (h/f) 1 Détails du poste
jobs.description:
Responsable Grands Comptes Europe - Tensioactifs standards et Alcools (h/f) 1 Détails du poste | BASF SE Passer au contenu principal Trouver des offres d'emploi Langue Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Connexion employé Description de poste Responsable Grands Comptes Europe - Tensioactifs standards et Alcools (h/f) 1 LOCALISATION ENTREPRISE DOMAINE D'ACTIVITÉ TYPE D’EMPLOI CODE DE RÉFÉRENCE OPTIONS DE TRAVAIL FLEXIBLE LOCALISATION ENTREPRISE DOMAINE D'ACTIVITÉ TYPE D’EMPLOI CODE DE RÉFÉRENCE OPTIONS DE TRAVAIL FLEXIBLE Postuler BIENVENUE CHEZ BASF Chez Care Chemicals, nous portons la passion du soin. Nos collaborateurs sont notre atout le plus précieux et nous cultivons une culture de bienveillance, de soutien et de développement. Ainsi, nous donnons à nos équipes les moyens de s’épanouir tant sur le plan personnel que professionnel. Unis, nous nous engageons à concevoir des solutions durables, digitalisées et innovantes pour un avenir meilleur. Lotions nourrissantes, shampooings doux, crèmes solaires protectrices – ces produits, ainsi que des détergents respectueux de l’environnement et des nettoyants haute performance, contiennent des ingrédients développés par notre division Care Chemicals. Notre portefeuille comprend également des solutions pour la protection des cultures, des applications techniques en chimie de la construction, des matières premières pour l’industrie des revêtements ainsi que des services digitaux pour nos clients. En collaboration avec des clients internationaux, nous concevons des solutions innovantes avec une attention croissante portée à l’approvisionnement durable. Ensemble, dessinons un avenir meilleur ! VOS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILITES Au sein de notre unité G‑EM/OE, nous sommes responsables de la gestion de l’activité européenne des oléotensioactifs et alcools. Nous servons principalement les marchés de la détergence et de l’hygiène‑beauté avec des (co)tensioactifs standards qui constituent la base de la grande majorité des formulations présentes sur le marché, ainsi que d’autres produits répondant à des besoins plus spécifiques. Nous sommes une équipe dynamique, bien connectée, internationale et diversifiée, couvrant le marché EMEA, avec un siège basé à Monheim am Rhein. En tant que Responsable Grands Comptes Régionaux – Tensioactifs standards Europe, vous êtes responsable, au sein de l’équipe commerciale EM/O, de la gestion et du développement de clients stratégiques clés à l’échelle européenne. Le poste vise à stimuler une croissance rentable, renforcer des partenariats de long terme et assurer l’exécution réussie des objectifs commerciaux et stratégiques, en étroite collaboration avec les parties prenantes internes. A ce titre, vous : Assumez l’entière responsabilité d’un portefeuille de grands comptes régionaux, en tant que point de contact principal pour les tensioactifs standards et alcools ; Pilotez la croissance des ventes, la rentabilité et les parts de marché en ligne avec la stratégie de la Gestion Produits ; Menez et optimisez les négociations commerciales pour des contrats européens ou mondiaux, en étroite coordination avec la Gestion Produits et les autres régions ; Suivez les volumes et marges contractuels, challengez les écarts si nécessaire et fournissez un reporting régulier aux équipes internes ; Développez et mettez en œuvre des stratégies de comptes régionales et spécifiques clients, en collaboration étroite avec les Chefs de Produits et les équipes Grands Comptes ; Identifiez des opportunités de croissance et faites la promotion de manière proactive des projets d’amélioration de la rentabilité ; Identifiez les besoins clients et travaillez à des solutions à valeur ajoutée avec le support de la Gestion Produits, de la Supply Chain et des équipes techniques ; Fournissez des analyses structurées du marché, des clients et de la concurrence afin de soutenir les ajustements stratégiques ; Assurez un reporting précis et à jour via les outils et systèmes digitaux internes ; Vous vous déplacez régulièrement dans la région afin de maintenir des relations clients solides. VOTRE PROFIL Diplôme dans une discipline scientifique, commerciale, marketing ou équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation dans les tensioactifs / alcools gras ; 3 à 5 ans d’expérience professionnelle pertinente en vente, avec de solides compétences en négociation avancée ; Esprit d’équipe, excellentes capacités de réseautage, mentalité entrepreneuriale et orientée développement commercial ; À l’aise avec les applications informatiques standards et les outils digitaux avancés ; Compétences linguistiques : anglais courant indispensable, une bonne maitrise du français est souhaitable, l'allemand est un plus ; Mobilité et disponibilité pour des déplacements dans la zone de responsabilité. QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Situé au coeur de Paris dans le quartier de la bourse, l’établissement Industrie et Services est le siège social de BASF France SAS et compte plus de 200 collaborateurs. Ce site regroupe diverses fonctions support et commerciales qui couvrent différents domaines. Nos principaux clients sont les industriels de l’automobile, la construction, la cosmétique, l’hygiène, la santé, l’agro-alimentaire, l’emballage, ou encore l’électrotechnique. La diversité est notre plus grande force ! Rejoignez notre formule gagnante et venez développer le futur avec nous - au sein d'une équipe internationale qui encourage l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances indépendemment du genre, de l'âge, de l'origine, de l'orientation sexuelle, du handicap ou des croyances. Postuler Une offre totale unique: you@BASF Chez BASF, il n’est pas seulement question de rémunération. Notre offre totale inclut un large éventail d’éléments dont vous avez besoin pour être au meilleur de votre forme à chaque étape de votre vie. C’est ce que nous appelons « you@BASF ». Cliquez ici pour en savoir plus. Chez BASF, il n’est pas seulement question de rémunération. Notre offre totale inclut un large éventail d’éléments dont vous avez besoin pour être au meilleur de votre forme à chaque étape de votre vie. C’est ce que nous appelons « you@BASF ». jobs.name: Sales Representative - Vercelli- Agricultural Solutions Division
jobs.description: Job Description: WELCOME TO BASF Il mondo ha bisogno di innovazione e il nostro team ha bisogno di te! Siamo BASF Agro e tutto ciò che facciamo, lo facciamo per amore dell’agricoltura. Le nostre soluzioni sono progettate con un obiettivo per ogni sistema colturale, combinando sementi e caratteristiche genetiche, protezione delle colture, strumenti digitali e approcci alla sostenibilità. Lavoriamo per offrire i migliori risultati possibili agli agricoltori, ai produttori e agli altri professionisti che fanno parte della catena del valore. Facciamo tutto ciò che è nelle nostre mani per costruire un futuro sostenibile per l’agricoltura, connettendo innovazione, clienti e società. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Scopri la nostra visione e missione in Agricultural Solutions! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Il/la candidato/a garantirà il conseguimento degli obiettivi di sviluppo e di redditività commerciale per il territorio di riferimento, attraverso la presenza sistematica sul mercato locale e lo sviluppo dei clienti attuali e potenziali in coerenza con gli obiettivi definiti dai piani commerciali. In particolare: Assicurerà il monitoraggio delle opportunità di sviluppo del territorio affidato a livello di canale, cliente e prodotto. Si occuperà di garantire il conseguimento degli obiettivi di vendita condivisi con il proprio Area Manager attraverso un'efficace gestione dei clienti. Verificherà il raggiungimento dei target di vendita del territorio attraverso l'analisi dei risultati di forecast, l'individuazione di scostamenti e l'attuazione di eventuali azioni correttive. Gestirà il rapporto con influenzatori locali e aziende agricole opinion leader, mantenendo e sviluppando un efficacie posizionamento tecnico commerciale dei prodotti in portafoglio. Garantirà la gestione dei dati nel portale aziendale relativi ai contratti stipulati con i clienti. WHAT YOU OFFER Forte conoscenza tecnica della coltura del riso (agronomia e fitoiatria). Domicilio nel territorio di competenza, ovvero Piemonte occidentale/Lombardia orientale. Cesano Maderno sarà la sede di lavoro amministrativa. Laurea in Scienze Agrarie, Biologia, Biotecnologie Agrarie. Almeno 5 anni di esperienza nelle Vendite nel settore di riferimento. Conoscenza del territorio di riferimento: Vercelli/Novara. Interesse per il mondo dell’Agricoltura digitale. Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese. Completano il profilo spiccate capacità organizzative, dinamismo, proattività, forte orientamento al cliente e al risultato e capacità di interpretazione strategica del mercato e di lavorare in autonomia WHAT WE OFFER Opportunità di crescita in un contesto multinazionale e multibusiness. Offriamo benefit che soddisfano le necessità e le aspettative dei nostri collaboratori nelle aree formazione e sviluppo, ambiente lavorativo e retribuzione: clicca qui per saperne di più Cosa Offriamo HOW TO REACH US Hai domande sul processo di selezione o sulla posizione? Puoi rivolgerti a: talent-acquisition-italy@basf.com Le prime informazioni sul processo di candidatura sono disponibili qui: http://on.basf.com/applicationprocess . ABOUT US BASF Italia S.p.A. è la principale società del Gruppo BASF in Italia. jobs.name: Operations Design & Performance Management (Executive)
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position You will be responsible for implementing enhanced, efficient, and effective processes across all AP countries and business units, while continuously driving process improvements and harmonization of system workflows in the future. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities •    Support operations on preparation and generation of monthly/weekly report. •    Support on the resolution of operations issues raised by key users and teams leads pertaining to process or system. •    Support Projects & Initiatives to drive effectiveness & efficiency in service delivery. •    Coordination of User Acceptance Test and knowledge transfer to APTC users for projects, Business Release and change requests. •    Lead and support on the development and implementation of improvement initiatives across the teams in APTC. •    Support in the data preparation & analysis for KPI reporting and measurement to support the smooth and efficient operations of APTC. •    Support in the preparation, review and update of knowledge documentations in APTC. •    Work closely with country and Hub organization to ensure input to APTC are accurate and contain appropriate information for efficient and effective processing, ensuring compliance and minimizing of risk. Job Requirements Degree in Business Administration or equivalent, supply chain management, logistics 2-4 years relevant experience in supply chain management, including hands-on experience in order management and shipping document handling. Supply chain management / process knowledge, SAP (SD module) experience, operational order management, foreign documentary trade, analytical thinking, good communication skills. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my.

jobs.name: EHS Manager-Innovation Campus, India (GBA/UI)
jobs.description: We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Lead the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the Innovation Campus Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) management system by translating applicable regulatory requirements, corporate Responsible Care® principles, policies, and standards into effective site-specific programs and procedures. Ensure full compliance with environmental and occupational health and safety regulations, drive a proactive safety culture, enhance employee awareness of workplace risks, and implement measures to prevent injuries, occupational illnesses, environmental incidents, and property loss while supporting sustainable business operations. How Will You Do It Develop, implement, and continuously improve site EHS standards, procedures, and preventive programs in alignment with applicable legal requirements, corporate standards, and Responsible Care® principles; coordinate hazard identification and risk assessment activities across the Innovation Campus. Manage and sustain the site Management of Change (MoC) process to ensure risks associated with organizational, process, facility, equipment, and operational changes are systematically evaluated and Controlled. Maintain and oversee all statutory EHS permits, licenses, compliance records, and documentation in coordination with the Site Director; conduct periodic compliance reviews and proactively escalate compliance gaps and corrective action. Plan, coordinate, and execute EHS assurance programs, including routine inspections, audits, compliance assessments, and management reviews; analyze performance trends and provide actionable recommendations to management Conduct workplace exposure monitoring, risk assessments, and periodic safety inspections to identify hazards, evaluate controls, and drive risk-reduction initiatives across campus operation Perform periodic work permit and contractor activity inspections to verify compliance with permit-to-work requirements and communicate findings, corrective actions, and best practices to relevant stalkeholders Lead Responsible Care® implementation activities, support internal and external audit preparation, and ensure sustained compliance with Responsible Care® standards and expectation. Partner with the Central Responsible Care team to deploy corporate EHS and Responsible Care® initiatives, systems, and best practices at the Innovation Campus Facilitate monthly EHS and Responsible Care® review meetings to monitor performance, discuss key risks, track action items, and promote continuous improvement Culture Develop, coordinate, and evaluate emergency preparedness and response programs, including mock drills, emergency simulations, and business continuity readiness exercises. Design and deliver EHS awareness programs and targeted training to enhance employee understanding of workplace hazards, safe work practices, and regulatory requirements Lead incident investigations, root cause analyses, and corrective/preventive action programs to prevent recurrence and drive continual improvement in EHS performance Provide technical EHS guidance and subject matter expertise to site functions, project teams, contractors, and leadership to support safe and compliant business operation Manage contractor EHS performance by conducting prequalification reviews, safety inductions, site compliance monitoring, and ongoing contractor engagement programs Conduct EHS orientation and induction programs for all newly joined BASF employees and other relevant personnel to ensure awareness of site-specific requirements and expectations Serve as the site Technical, Documentation, and Safety (TDS) Advisor, conduct TDS audits, and support closure of identified improvement opportunities Support site leadership in developing EHS objectives, key performance indicators (KPIs), and annual improvement plans to strengthen overall EHS Performance Undertake special projects, initiatives, and assignments as directed by senior management in support of business, operational, and strategic objectives What We Look For Education : University degree in Science, Chemical Technology/Engineering, Chemistry, or a related feild More than 10 years of experience in EHS function Diploma or certification in Industrial safety Experience in EHS assessment and management system audits (e.g., ISO 14001, OHSAS 18001/ISO 45001) Knowledge of Responsible Care requirement Competency and knowledge of information protection requirement 2. Suggested Competencies: Core Competencies Result Orientation Strategic thinking and decision - making Clear verbal and written communication Interpersonal awareness and emotional intelligence Accountability and ownership of work Leadership Competencies Teamwork & Collobration People Development & coaching Decision-making under pressure Professional / functional Competencied Safety, health, and environment (SHE) awareness and best practice Certified/qualified safety professional Risk assessment and incident investigation Regulatory compliance and audit management Emergency preparedness and response planning Management of Change (MoC) and permit-to-work system Contractor and contractor safety management Occupational hygiene and workplace monitioring Authority Authorised to check and inform any safety non-compliances in Innovation Campus, Mumbai Authorised to check the implementation of Responsible Care system requirement Authorised to stop processes / activity in case of any safety non-compliance Responsibilities Prepare and maintain documentation related to RC, Responsible to cascade best practices and implement the same at Innovation Campus. Location Turbhe - Inovation Campus

jobs.name: Controlling Manager - Value & Cost Controlling (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As a newly standalone business, we are also adapting our Controlling organization with a strong focus on value creation. As Controlling Manager – Value & Cost Controlling, you will build and lead a team that ensures financial transparency, drives performance management, and supports value-oriented steering across our global business. Your team owns three key areas: functional cost controlling, the financial steering of the company's value creation initiatives, and the controlling of our Transitional Service Agreements (TSAs) and other external service providers during the first phase of our standalone journey. As the direct finance partner for senior management on all cost and value creation topics, you play a key role in achieving the company's financial targets. In your new role, you lead and develop the Value & Cost Controlling team, foster a collaborative, business-partner-oriented culture, and ensure the delivery of accurate, timely, and decision-relevant financial insights to senior management. You further enhance reporting on primary cost controlling, implementing new allocation models and driving process automation. In addition, you oversee the financial steering of value creation initiatives, ensuring transparency on the financial impact, milestones, and progress of the company’s strategic initiatives portfolio and providing management with clear insights into status, risks, and opportunities. Furthermore, you steer the controlling of all Transitional Service Agreements (TSAs) and other external service providers, ensuring transparency on scope, costs, service levels, and timelines while supporting a smooth and timely transition into the target setup; always in close collaboration with internal stakeholders. You design and implement a robust performance management framework, including KPI definition, target setting, variance analysis, and continuous monitoring of profitability and efficiency initiatives, and you play a key role in budgeting, forecasting, and long-term planning cycles, ensuring consistent alignment between corporate strategy, financial planning, and execution. Last but not least, you partner closely with finance leadership, business units, and operational functions to identify performance improvement opportunities, support strategic and operational targets, and drive the standardization, automation, and digitalization of controlling processes to enable data-driven decision-making. WHAT YOU OFFER university degree in finance, controlling, business administration, economics, or a related field; CFA or MBA is an advantage 8–10+ years of professional experience in controlling, FP&A, or performance management within a multinational corporation, including several years in a leadership role strong analytical background, ideally complemented by experience in strategy, corporate or business development proven track record in cost controlling, profitability steering, and performance improvement; experience in service agreements or shared services controlling is a plus, as well as experience in the chemicals industry or in business transitions strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and leadership skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse stakeholders in a multicultural environment proficiency in SAP S/4HANA, BI and planning tools such as SAC, Power BI, or Anaplan, as well as advanced MS Office skills very good language skills in English WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio "Freizeit" with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant "Auszeit" including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center "LackiKids" and a vacation program "LackiKids on Tour". HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. jobs.name: Ag Strategic Account Manager - Western Cornbelt to West Coast (Field)
jobs.description: Job Description: Now hiring! Ag Strategic Account Manager - Western Cornbelt to West Coast (Field) We are looking for a Ag Strategic Account Manager to join our Agricultural Solutions team to support customers in the Western Cornbelt to the West Coast of US. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental, and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. As an Ag Strategic Account Manager, you will be responsible for managing long-term relationships and growth at Strategic accounts. You will meet BASF specified targets by creating value for the existing customer base and prospecting for new opportunities with products, services, and/or solutions from BASF. You will be based in a Mid-West / West state (AZ, CA, CO, IA, ID, KS, MT, NE, ND, OR, SD, WA ), allowing you to service your local Strategic accounts. Your main geography will be regional in scope covering mostly the Western Coast of US and west region of the Midwest (and some other regions of US). As a Ag Strategic Account Manager - Western Cornbelt to West Coast (Field), you create chemistry by... Managing strategic customer accounts, delivering value through BASF’s products, services, and solutions. Managing business relationships with assigned channel partners, which may include Corporate and/or Regional Business relationships. Developing an approved 3 Year Business Plan as part of the business planning cycle with assigned channel partners and function as part of the Strategic Account Team that sets and implements channel and marketing strategy. Disseminating BASFs core strategy, channel goals, and objectives throughout all levels of the Channel’s organization, ensuring all key influencers fully understand and support the strategic direction and goals. Communicating and facilitating sales responsibilities with Regional and District managers, allowing for effective business solutions to be developed representing mutually successful and profitable results. Thinking strategically and with high self-motivation, you will ensure BASF is positioned as a preferred supplier. Identifying business opportunities for assigned distributor(s), working with relevant BASF groups to provide required resources to meet such opportunities and set and agree to stretch targets with the Channel and BASF Management that will be monitored monthly. If you... Have a Bachelor’s degree in Business, Agricultural Business, Science, or a related field, plus 15 years’ relevant experience (or an MBA with 13 years’ relevant experience). Have a minimum of 15 years of experience in Account Management, Field Sales Management, Marketing, and/or Business Development within the agricultural field. Demonstrate your understanding of distribution, the go-to-market systems, business cycles, and competitive relationships of channel accounts. Show strong business acumen and demonstrated understanding of the US Seed & Crop Protection market, specifically the relationship of the Distributor/Channel partner within the major US row-crop markets. Have account Management experience (particularly with Regional or National Accounts), persuasive selling and demonstrated negotiation skills. Possess a willingness and ability to travel a minimum of 70% of the time, primarily domestically. Reside in the Mid-West / West States of US (AZ, CA, CO, IA, ID, KS, MT, NE, ND, OR, SD, WA) to effectively support key geographies. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $154,400 - $212,300. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Job Description Footer:

jobs.name: AI Data Architect (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF Digital Hub Madrid attracts, grows, and develops passionate people who will meaningfully impact the digital future of BASF. Come join us and be a part of our digitalization journey. Describe your Product Mission here / objective of the role: The AI & Automation Enablement team designs and engineers scalable AI and automation solutions that deliver measurable business value. By combining strong architecture, engineering excellence, and industrialization capabilities, we enable BASF to turn opportunities into production-ready systems – consistently, reliably, and at scale. About the Job: AI Data Architect in a project-oriented team who designs scalable, enterprise-grade data architectures and leads the engineering of robust data platforms, collaborating across domains and teams to enable the development and industrialization of AI solutions that deliver measurable business value. RESPONSIBILITIES Define and own the end-to-end data architecture for AI solutions, from ingestion and storage to serving layers powering models and analytics (e.g., batch pipelines, real-time APIs). Design the data foundations for compound AI systems, including retrieval architectures (e.g. RAG), vector and knowledge stores, and feature/embedding layers. Establish data modelling standards, semantic layers, and reusable reference architectures across workloads (batch, streaming, real-time, analytical, AI-driven) across the organization. Define and enforce governance frameworks for data quality, lineage, security, and compliance, ensuring trustworthy and well-managed data products. Set architectural guidelines and best practices for scalable data platforms and pipelines, and ensure their consistent adoption Provide technical leadership and guidance to data engineers, data scientists, and business partners on data and AI architecture. Evaluate emerging technologies and define the strategic roadmap for the data and AI platform. Stay current with trends and best practices in data architecture, AI, and cloud platforms. QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor’s degree or Master’s in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field. 5+ years of experience in data engineering and data architecture, including designing data models, platforms, and end-to-end data pipelines. Proven experience defining data architecture for AI/analytics solutions on a major cloud platform (Azure preferred; AWS a plus) and Big Data architectures. Data architecture and modelling for relational and NoSQL systems, including modern storage paradigms (e.g., object storage, lakehouse, warehouse). Design of scalable data platforms and architectural patterns (e.g., Lakehouse, Data Mesh, Medallion), including governance, lineage, and quality considerations. Architecture of compound AI systems, including data layers for AI solutions (e.g., RAG, GraphRAG), vector stores, graph databases, and embedding pipelines. Strong programming skills in Python, including asynchronous programming (asyncio) and data processing. Apache Spark and Databricks platform Cloud data platform expertise, preferably Azure Could and Azure data platform services. Streaming and event-driven architectures, using streaming services such as RabbitMQ or Apache Kafka Big data and large-scale compute architectures, including batch and real-time processing patterns. Design of microservices and distributed systems, including API-based data services. Containerization and deployment practices using Docker (and Kubernetes as a plus) Data security, privacy, and compliance (e.g., GDPR), including encryption, access control, and data masking/anonymization Software engineering practices, including Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) and DevOps (CI/CD with GitHub Actions) Container orchestration platforms: Kubernetes Graph database technologies and data modelling Workflow orchestration and task management frameworks such as Apache Airflow and Celery AI/ML platforms and cloud AI services LLM and agentic solution patterns, including prompt engineering and orchestration of AI agents. MLOps practices, including model lifecycle management, CI/CD for ML, deployment, monitoring, and retraining pipelines. Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively in cross-functional teams. Excellent communication and stakeholder-management skills, with the ability to translate business needs into technical architectures and influence decision-making across teams. WHAT WE OFFER A secure work environment because your health, safety and wellbeing is always our top priority. Flexible work schedule and Home-office options, so that you can balance your working life and private life. Learning and development opportunities 25 holiday days per year 5 additional days (readjustment) A collaborative, trustful and innovative work environment Being part of an international team and work in global projects Relocation assistance to Madrid provided At BASF, the chemistry is right Because we are counting on innovative solutions, sustainable actions, connected thinking and on you, become a part of our formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces diversity and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.At BASF, we are committed to upholding and ensuring compliance with company standards related to quality, environment, health, safety, and energy, in line with our global guidelines.We actively promote a culture of prevention and continuous improvement, encouraging collaboration in initiatives related to quality, environmental protection, health, safety, and energy performance.We foster responsible energy use, promoting efficiency in daily operations and supporting the identification of improvement projects and energy-saving opportunities

jobs.name: Head of Operations Controlling (m/f/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description:
Head of Operations Controlling (m/f/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Profil-Login Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Head of Operations Controlling (m/f/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WELCOME If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT As we transform into a modern, business-partner-oriented Controlling organization, the Operations Controlling function plays a central role in steering the financial performance of our global manufacturing footprint and in providing transparency on production costs, CAPEX, and operational KPIs across our Business Units. In your new role, you take global ownership of the Operations Controlling function and lead its strategic development, providing functional guidance to 14 Production and Site Controllers across Europe, the Americas, and Asia within the matrix organization while fostering a collaborative, business-partner-oriented culture. You take central ownership of manufacturing controlling across all production sites of the global Coatings organization, with a particular focus on our Business Units, ensuring consistent standards, transparency, and decision-relevant insights for senior management, while steering manufacturing costs, including conversion costs and cost allocations. In close alignment with Group Controlling, you drive budgeting, forecasting, and planning for manufacturing costs, ensure integration into the overall financial planning cycle, and steer CAPEX and Technical Cost Controlling at group level, providing transparency on investment performance, project status, and value contribution. Furthermore, you design, build, and continuously evolve dashboards and KPI frameworks for the EVP Operations Mobility/Refinish, local site management, Plant Managers, and cross-functional stakeholders, leveraging modern BI and analytics tools to enable data-driven decision-making. As a trusted finance partner to the EVP Operations Mobility/Refinish, Plant Managers, Managing Directors, and Finance leadership, you provide high-quality KPI reporting, translate operational performance into transparent value drivers, identify performance improvement opportunities, and support the realization of strategic and operational targets across the global organization. Last but not least, you shape the reporting standards, tools, and processes of global Operations Controlling, drive their standardization, automation, and digitalization, and play a key role in ensuring scalability and efficiency across the global footprint. WHAT YOU OFFER university degree, preferably a master’s, in finance, controlling, business administration, industrial engineering, or a related field; CFA or MBA is an advantage 10+ years of experience in controlling, FP&A, or operations/manufacturing controlling in an international industrial corporation, ideally in the chemicals or process industry, including several years in leadership roles proven track record in global multi-site manufacturing controlling, including conversion cost steering, cost allocations, product costing, and CAPEX controlling in a multi-site environment demonstrated leadership experience in a global matrix organization, with the ability to engage, align, and influence diverse stakeholders across regions, functions, and management levels strong expertise in dashboards and KPI frameworks for senior operations stakeholders, combined with SAP S/4HANA CO, PP, MM, PM, PS, SAP Data Warehouse, and Power BI; automation and data tools are a plus very good language skills in English; German is a plus, combined with strong communication skills and willingness to travel globally to production sites on a regular basis WHAT WE OFFER We offer an attractive salary with individual benefits such as performance-related bonuses. Company-funded supplementary health insurance, accident insurance and pension related benefits We offer a wide range of local and global development and career opportunities for professional and personal development Surventis stands for diversity - we are proud of our Coatings employee networks WomenInCoatings, Color Pride and CultiConnect The feedback culture is firmly integrated into our corporate culture, e.g. through regular employee surveys and management feedback At our location in Münster, we have our own fitness studio "Freizeit" with a physiotherapy practice, permanently employed company doctors, a company restaurant "Auszeit" including a café bar, a bilingual company daycare center "LackiKids" and a vacation program "LackiKids on Tour". HOW TO REACH US Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel: +49 30 2005-59139 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. ABOUT US Learn more about our company here . Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. En BASF, estamos comprometidos con la inclusión, proporcionando siempre que sea posible, ajustes razonables a las personas con discapacidad o con alguna necesidad específica, para garantizar su acceso y el uso de servicios.  En caso de requerir algún apoyo, favor de comunicarlo durante tu primer contacto con Atracción de Talento. Estamos buscando un(a) Especialista de Planeación de Materiales para Lerma . Tendrá como objetivo Administrar y asegurar el proceso de planeación de materiales: producto terminado y materias primas, apego a las políticas y procedimientos vigentes de la empresa, mediante la correcta comunicación de los requerimientos a los proveedores internos y externos, determinando los niveles de inventario óptimos así como la llegada oportuna de los materiales de importación, para el cumplimiento de los objetivos dictados por la organización y asegurando el suministro de materiales hacia nuestros clientes internos y externos.. En este rol, estarás a cargo de: Consolidar y comunicar a las áreas involucradas, los requerimientos de materias primas, mezclas de Isocianatos y Resinas fabricados en Casa de Sistemas con base en el pronóstico, históricos de ventas e inventarios actuales a través de la planeación de materiales anticipando posibles fallas en suministros generando el programa de producción con una visión mensual a manera de cubrir las ordenes de cliente y futuros proyectos. Expedir las órdenes de compra necesarias para asegurar el suministro y disponibilidad de materiales: materias primas Inter compañía y con proveedores internos y externos, mediante la programación de órdenes en SAP, comunicando efectivamente con los proveedores internos, externos y planeadores de las filiales en USA, Europa, China, etc., con el fin de cumplir con el plan de ventas y los requerimientos de plan de producción. Coordinar la entrega y el dar el seguimiento de los materiales desde la colocación de la orden de compra hasta la llegada a bodega o planta, garantizando la disponibilidad del material físicamente y en SAP con el fin de asegurar la continuidad de la producción. Facilitar los pronósticos de materias primas, de acuerdo con las necesidades de materiales planeados con el fin de asegurar el suministro de estos. Supervisar los niveles de inventario y optimizar los recursos a través del análisis continuo y las políticas de movimiento de inventarios y depuración de partidas con apoyo de la herramienta de HOI, para alcanzar los objetivos de DIV, OIV y AGED establecidos por el Negocio. Coordinar los planes de acción para el inventario de materias primas y producto terminado, obsoleto, dañado, caduco, fuera de especificación y devuelto, con el fin de disminuir el impacto en el costo por reservas contables que afecten la rentabilidad del negocio. Mantener el maestro de materiales realizando extensiones y ajustes en SAP: ZHL (COBALT), que permitan su utilización para el manejo de materiales en todas las bodegas que requieran los negocios de PMU y PMO en México. Establecer planes de acción con enfoque de mejora continua, participando activamente en los procesos de investigación de NCMs, proyectos de ahorro/mejora continua que se referencien en plataformas como STAR u OPEX, generando ahorros y reducción de complejidad en los procesos de planeación y logística en general. Si cuentas con: Formación Universitaria en ingeniería, administración o similares. Mínimo 3-5 años de experiencia en roles de planeación. Conocimientos y experiencia en planeación de materiales, planeación de producción, manejo de inventarios, logística y distribución. Manejo de paqueterías Office (principalmente Excel). Experiencia de trabajo con SAP o ERPs similares (módulos de planeación). Habilidades de planificación, organización, proactividad y sentido de urgencia. Nivel de inglés intermedio o superior Esta vacante es para ti! Crea tu propia química: Lo que te ofrecemos ... Agregar valor a nuestros clientes comienza por agregarte valor a ti. you@BASF es el paquete de beneficios, desarrollo de carrera, ambiente de trabajo y compensación que ofrecemos para apoyarte en todas las etapas de tu vida y carrera. Las recompensas totales que recibes como empleado de BASF van más allá de tu pago. Desde planes competitivos de salud y seguros, hasta planes de pensiones robustos, creemos que invertir en ti es invertir en nuestro éxito. Trabajando para una gran organización global, tendrás la oportunidad de crecer profesional y personalmente, expandir tu red y construir una carrera profesional gratificante y dinámica. jobs.name: Operations Design & Performance Management (Senior Executive)
jobs.description: O bjectives of the Position You will be responsible for implementing enhanced, efficient, and effective processes across all AP countries and business units, while continuously driving process improvements and harmonization of system workflows in the future. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Support on the resolution of operations issues raised by key users and teams leads pertaining to process or system. Lead and support in the execution and implementation of projects & initiatives in APTC to drive efficiency and effectiveness in service delivery. Ensure successful knowledge transfer to APTC users and coordination of User Acceptance Test for projects, Business Release and change requests. Provide classroom training and hands on support to APTC team members. Lead, Develop and implement improvement initiatives across the teams in APTC by analyzing trends and patterns from periodic performance reports. Support in the data preparation & analysis for KPI reporting and measurement to support the smooth and efficient operations of APTC. Support in the preparation, review and update of knowledge documentations in APTC. Using the non-conformance system, identify areas of improvement and work with the concerned parties to ensure corrective measures are put in place. Work closely with country and Hub organization to ensure input to APTC are accurate and contain appropriate information for efficient and effective processing, ensuring compliance and minimizing of risk. Job Requirements Degree in Business Administration or equivalent, supply chain management, logistics 4-6 years relevant experience in supply chain management, including hands-on experience in order management and shipping document handling. Supply chain management / process knowledge, SAP (SD module) experience, operational order management, foreign documentary trade, analytical thinking, good communication skills. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/my.

jobs.name: Operational Excellence and Process Safety, Aroma Ingredients
jobs.description: Objectives of the Position Operations Aroma Ingredients is responsible for global production of our leading products. This position supports the operational excellence of assigned key technologies and value chains throughout their lifecycle by driving technical process optimization, collaborating with cross-functional teams, to achieve reliable, safe, and cost-efficient operations across all aroma assets globally. Main Tasks Responsible for Operational Excellence of assigned complex key technologies and value chains over their lifecycle (standard operation, start-up, shutdown, stand-by, turnaround, troubleshooting, quality, regulatory, etc.). Continuously drive technical process optimization and operational excellence in all assets (technical documentation, sharing of best practices, digitalization and AI implementation, etc.) to improve reliability and reduce product costs. Main contact for operations teams, technology, marketing, supply chain, quality and project management for assigned assets and products with focus on Asia. Support trouble shooting for Aroma assets globally. Manage regular communication with the respective production sites. Install and align reporting, communication channels and operational targets. Align and realize projects and lead teams to upgrade facilities according to business needs. Support new investments and project plans including milestones and deliverables. Participate in virtual communities to exchange experiences between Citral and Menthol assets. Support technical, regulatory and EHS compliance in the region incl. operational support of process safety related activities (e.g. HAZOP, RC Audit, etc.) Requirements Minimum degree in natural sciences or other related field Good knowledge of production and project management skills Expertise in Citral and Menthol technology as well as process safety Knowledge about processes in research and operational laboratories

jobs.name: Indirect Tax Specialist (m/w/d) Stellendetails
jobs.description: Indirect Tax Specialist (m/w/d) Stellendetails | BASF SE Zum Hauptinhalt Stellen finden Sprache Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Anmeldung für Mitarbeitende Stellenbeschreibung Indirect Tax Specialist (m/w/d) STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN STANDORT UNTERNEHMEN EINSATZGEBIET VERTRAGSART REFERENZCODE FLEXIBLE ARBEITSOPTIONEN Jetzt bewerben WILLKOMMEN BEI UNS Wir sind BASF in der Landwirtschaft und alles, was wir tun, tun wir aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft. Wir verbinden Saatgut und Traits, Pflanzenschutzprodukte, digitale Werkzeuge und Nachhaltigkeitsansätze, um Landwirten und anderen Stakeholdern entlang der Wertschöpfungskette die bestmöglichen Ergebnisse zu liefern. Wir tun alles in unserer Macht Stehende, um eine nachhaltige Zukunft für die Landwirtschaft zu schaffen, indem wir Innovationen, Kunden und die Gesellschaft miteinander verbinden. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Hier erhältst du nähere Einblicke in unsere Agricultural Solutions Vision & Mission ! WAS DICH ERWARTET Wir bei Agricultural Solutions bereiten uns darauf vor, ein selbständiges Unternehmen innerhalb der BASF-Gruppe zu werden. Um für diese eigenständige Zukunft bestens aufgestellt zu sein, suchen wir dich als Unterstützung für unser neues Corporate Center (siehe hier : build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In deiner neuen Position als Indirect Tax Specialist kümmerst du dich um die Lokalisierung von Anforderungen im Bereich der indirekten Steuern in komplexen Märkten bis ins Detail und stellst die vollständige Einhaltung lokaler Vorschriften sicher. Dein Fachwissen ist gefragt! Du führst umfassende Prüfpfade und Belegdokumentationen, um sicherzustellen, dass alle Positionen im Bereich der indirekten Steuern ordnungsgemäß begründet und vertretbar sind. Gemeinsam mit deinen Kollegen und Kolleginnen in den Bereichen Global Business Services, Controlling und den Geschäftsbereichen identifizierst und behebst du Unstimmigkeiten in Fragen der indirekten Steuern effizient und präzise. Du setzt regulatorische Änderungen durch die Aktualisierung von Prozessen und Kontrollen um und stellst so sicher, dass das Unternehmen den sich wandelnden Rechtsvorschriften im Bereich der indirekten Steuern in verschiedenen Rechtsräumen immer einen Schritt voraus ist. Darüber hinaus erstellst du fundierte Antworten an die Steuerbehörden und gestaltest den Austausch während Prüfungen oder Anfragen professionell und zeitnah. Nicht zuletzt berichtest du dem Group Lead und den zuständigen lokalen Steuerverantwortlichen über Fortschritte, Risiken und Probleme und trägst so zu einer transparenten und effektiven Steuer-Governance bei. WAS DU MITBRINGST erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium in den Bereichen Steuerwesen, Finanzen, Rechnungswesen, Betriebswirtschaft oder einem verwandten Fachgebiet, idealerweise ergänzt durch eine Zusatzqualifikation wie eine Zertifizierung als Steuerberater:in oder eine gleichwertige Qualifikation mindestens acht Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich der indirekten Steuern, vorzugsweise in einem internationalen oder länderübergreifenden Unternehmensumfeld fundierte Kenntnisse im Bereich der Mehrwertsteuer (MwSt.)/GST und anderer indirekter Steuerregelungen, einschließlich des Managements regulatorischer Änderungen und der Vorbereitung auf Steuerprüfungen fließende Englischkenntnisse sowie sicherer Umgang mit SAP und den gängigen MS-Office-Anwendungen; Erfahrung mit Steuertechnologie-Tools ist von Vorteil ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen und Lösungsorientierung, kombiniert mit effektiven Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und der Fähigkeit zur funktions- und kulturübergreifenden Zusammenarbeit WAS WIR BIETEN Eine dynamische Arbeitsumgebung, die dich dazu ermutigt, deine Ideen einzubringen, Verantwortung zu übernehmen und aktiv mitzugestalten. Ein starker Fokus auf kontinuierliches Lernen, moderne Technologien und den verantwortungsvollen Einsatz von künstlicher Intelligenz. So arbeiten wir zukunftsorientiert, effizient und nachhaltig – und treiben Innovation aktiv voran. Inspirierende Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung deiner Ideen (z. B. Women in Business). Vielfältige Lernmöglichkeiten für deine fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung. Karrierepfade für deine berufliche Entwicklung in andere Rollen innerhalb des Unternehmens. Wir unterstützen dich aktiv bei der Kinderbetreuung (z. B. Betrieblicher Kindergarten, Ferienprogramme) und bieten ein Fitness- und Gesundheitsstudio sowie ein medizinisches Zentrum mit zahlreichen Gesundheitsangeboten. Wir bieten nachhaltige Mobilitätsvorteile – ein vergünstigtes Deutschlandticket für den regionalen Nahverkehr, ein Jobrad-Leasingprogramm sowie E‑Ladestationen vor Ort für private Elektrofahrzeuge. So wird dein Arbeitsweg kostengünstig, gesund und klimafreundlich. SO ERREICHST DU UNS Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 beantwortet gerne deine Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne kannst du dir auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Fachliche Fragen können in diesem Termin nicht beantwortet werden. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichst du auch hier Erste Infos zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess findest du hier ÜBER UNS Die BASF Agricultural Solutions Deutschland GmbH bietet als zuverlässiger Partner der Landwirtschaft innovative, nachhaltige Lösungen speziell für verschiedene Anbausysteme. Unser Unternehmenssitz ist das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof, nahe der BASF-Konzernzentrale in Ludwigshafen. In der Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar erwartet dich beruflich wie auch privat ein attraktives Umfeld. Mehr über das Agrarzentrum in Limburgerhof erfährst du unter: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LI Mehr über unser Team bei BASF Agricultural Solutions findest du hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalte mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Wir duzen dich gerne – das gehört bei uns zur Kultur. Wenn du lieber gesiezt werden möchtest, sag einfach Bescheid. Jetzt bewerben Unsere Mitarbeitenden stehen im Mittelpunkt und setzen sich jeden Tag dafür ein, unseren Kunden einen echten Mehrwert zu bieten. Wir legen großen Wert darauf, unsere Mitarbeitenden heute und in Zukunft gezielt zu fördern, damit wir die Talente und Fähigkeiten haben, die für unseren Erfolg entscheidend sind. Vereint durch die Leidenschaft für die Landwirtschaft, entwickeln wir gemeinsam innovative Lösungen, arbeiten partnerschaftlich zusammen und streben nach den besten Ergebnissen – als ein Team. So schaffen wir Wirkung. Aus Liebe zur Landwirtschaft und dem wichtigsten Beruf auf der Erde. jobs.name: Indirect Tax Specialist (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Our solutions are purpose-designed for different crop systems, connecting seeds and traits, crop protection, digital tools and sustainability approaches. We strive to deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions vision & mission ! WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT We in Agricultural Solutions are preparing to be an independent, pure-play agricultural company within BASF group. To be ready for independence, we are looking for you to join us in our new Corporate roles (see here: build your career on our Standalone Transformation ). In your new position as an Indirect Tax Specialist, you execute last-mile localization of indirect tax requirements in complex markets and ensure full compliance with local regulations. Your expertise is in demand! You maintain comprehensive audit trails and evidence documentation, ensuring all indirect tax positions are properly substantiated and defensible. Together with your colleagues in Global Business Services, Controlling and Business units, you identify and resolve discrepancies in indirect tax matters efficiently and accurately. You implement regulatory changes by updating processes and controls, ensuring the organization stays ahead of evolving indirect tax legislation across multiple jurisdictions. Furthermore, you prepare well-founded responses to tax authorities and manage interactions during audits or inquiries in a professional and timely manner. Last but not least, you report progress, risks and issues to the group lead and relevant local tax leads, contributing to transparent and effective tax governance. WHAT YOU OFFER successfully completed degree in Tax, Finance, Accounting, Business or a related field, ideally complemented by further qualification such as a tax advisor certification or equivalent at least eight years of professional experience in indirect tax compliance, preferably in an international or multi-jurisdictional corporate environment in-depth knowledge of VAT/GST and other indirect tax frameworks, including regulatory change management and audit defense processes fluent English skills as well as proficiency in SAP and standard MS Office applications; experience with tax technology tools is an advantage strong analytical thinking and solution orientation combined with effective communication skills and the ability to collaborate across functions and cultures WHAT WE OFFER A dynamic work environment that empowers you to contribute your ideas, take ownership, and assume responsibility. A strong focus on continuous learning, modern technologies, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence, enabling us to work in a future‑oriented, efficient, and sustainable way while actively driving innovation. Inspiring networks for the development and implementation of your ideas (e.g. Women in Business) Career paths as professional development into other roles within the company. We actively support with childcare (e.g. company daycare, holiday programmes) and provide a fitness and health studio as well as a medical center with numerous offers for your health. We offer sustainable mobility benefits — a discounted Deutschlandticket for regional public transport, a job‑bike leasing program, and on‑site e‑charging stations for private electric vehicles to make your commute affordable, healthy, and climate‑friendly. HOW TO REACH US Lennart Boudgoust (Talent Acquisition), lennart.boudgoust@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-56521 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are also welcome to book an appointment online. Technical questions cannot be answered at this meeting. You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here ABOUT US As a reliable partner of the rural economy, Agricultural Solutions offers innovative, sustainable solutions for plant protection and health. With the agricultural center in Limburgerhof, close to the BASF Group headquarters in Ludwigshafen, you can expect an attractive place for both work and play in the Rhine-Neckar region. Learn more about BASF SE at: on.basf.com/BASFSE_LI Find out more about our team in Agricultural Solutions here Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.
