jobs.name: Quality Manager
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF We are BASF in agriculture and everything we do, we do for the love of farming. Connecting seeds and traits, crop protection products, digital tools and sustainability approaches, to help deliver the best possible outcomes for farmers, growers and our other stakeholders along the value chain. We do everything in our power to build a sustainable future for agriculture by connecting innovation, customers and society. #ForTheLoveOfFarming #TheBiggestJobOnEarth Take a look at our Agricultural Solutions: Vision & Mission WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT The Quality Manager is responsible for leading all quality assurance and quality control activities to ensure product quality, regulatory compliance, operational excellence, and continuous improvement across the site. The role oversees laboratory operations, quality systems, ISO compliance, team development, and quality performance while partnering closely with production, engineering, and other site functions to support business objectives. Duties will include the following: Lead and develop the Quality team to achieve quality, safety, and operational objectives Define and implement quality strategies, plans, and continuous improvement initiatives aligned with site goals Manage all quality assurance and quality control activities to ensure products meet required specifications and customer expectations Maintain and continuously improve the Quality Management System, including ISO 9001:2015 compliance and audit readiness Review quality results, approve product release decisions, and provide recommendations on quality-related issues Ensure laboratory operations are efficient, compliant, and supported by effective maintenance programs, procedures, and testing methods Drive root cause analysis, non-conformance management, investigations, and corrective actions to improve quality performance Collaborate with production, engineering, and other departments to implement process improvements and optimize efficiency Ensure compliance with all quality, EHS, regulatory, and company requirements across laboratory and site activities Manage quality records, certificates of analysis, trial samples, departmental budgets, and quality reporting WHAT YOU OFFER Degree in Biotechnology, Microbiology, or a related scientific discipline Minimum 8 years of quality management experience, including at least 4 years in a leadership role Proven experience managing quality systems within a manufacturing or production environment Sound knowledge of ISO 9001:2015 implementation, maintenance, and auditing; Certified Internal Auditor is advantageous Strong understanding of quality assurance, quality control, laboratory operations, and continuous improvement methodologies Experience with root cause analysis, non-conformance management, investigations, and process optimization Knowledge of EHS systems, regulatory requirements, and compliance standards Strong analytical skills with exceptional attention to detail and quality standards Proficient in Microsoft Office, SAP, and quality-related systems Excellent leadership, communication, organizational, and stakeholder management skills ABOUT US As a reliable partner of the agricultural sector, BASF Agricultural Solutions offers innovative solutions in the area of crop protection and pest control to improve agricultural production, increase the profitability of agricultural companies and thereby improve the living standards of a growing population. Our strength lies in supporting farmers in the face of the challenges presented by an increasingly complex agricultural sector and in working with them to secure basic human necessities, such as food, nutrition and energy. Learn more about BASF Agricultural Solutions at https://www.agro.basf.co.za/en/ Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief. Closing date for applications 17 August 2026 Join the best team with more than 110,000 other minds to connect with globally. Explore your BASF career options at basf.com/career .

jobs.name: Head of US Crop Protection Regulatory Affairs - Research Triangle Park, NC (preferred)
jobs.description: Now hiring! Head of US Crop Protection Regulatory Affairs - Research Triangle Park, NC (preferred) We are looking for a Head of US Crop Protection Regulatory Affairs to join our Agricultural Solutions team in [Location]. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. This position leads the regulatory function in the US, ensuring freedom to operate for the Crop Protection business units within the Agricultural Solutions business. The role requires a strategic, agile and highly collaborative senior manager with a deep technical understanding of regulatory affairs/science and demonstrated success interfacing with Federal and State regulatory agency senior leadership. As a key member of the APD North American Crop Protection leadership team and industry influencer, you will leverage your connections with management in the commercial, supply chain and development functions and apply your understanding of competitive threats, changing regulatory dynamics, and new technologies to set the regulatory direction that facilitates successful business outcomes. As a Head of US Crop Protection Regulatory Affairs - Research Triangle Park, NC (preferred), you create chemistry by... Synthesizing complex scientific and regulatory information in a continually changing regulatory environment into understandable, clear and effective regulatory strategies at the portfolio level. Ensuring maintenance and defense of existing products, and ensure new products have robust submission dossiers.  Provide active support and alignment on policies and procedures both within APN and globally. Setting expectations and reinforcing a culture that emphasizes empowerment and a drive to meet customer needs while meeting regulatory requirements. Ensuring appropriate roles and staffing levels to meet business needs, with clear responsibilities while meeting budget requirements. Serving as key interface between US Regulatory and key internal BASF stakeholders, proactively facilitating collaboration and coordination to assure strategic alignment and build relationships with stakeholder groups including EPA, USDA, and trade associations, including leadership. Ensuring compliance with all applicable regulatory standards and record-keeping requirements. Working with other key stakeholders (legal, supply chain, technical service, etc.), managing potential issues. If you... Have a Ph.D. with 5+ years, MS with 10+ years, or BS with 15+ years of agricultural chemical industry experience. Previous agricultural chemical regulatory experience required. Demonstrate expert understanding of US agricultural chemical laws and regulations, including FIFRA, TSCA, and state requirements, with a proven track record of successful registration of pesticide products. Have proven experience and ability to build and lead a high-performance team. Have experience working with US EPA Office of Pesticide Programs, USDA and other government agencies, and trade associations. Show ability to synthesize complex scientific and regulatory information into a coherent and effective regulatory strategy that provides clear guidance to the business. Display negotiation skills and ability to build relationships with key stakeholders and negotiate outcomes beneficial to the business, weighing both short and longer-term needs, both internally and externally. Decision making. Ability to manage through difficult situations to evaluate and provide optimal outcomes, managing relationships to ensure key stakeholder needs are met. Possess the ability to deliver challenging messages to stakeholders, willing to seek creative solutions, maintain focus and provide a regulatory perspective. Demonstrate strong interpersonal and networking skills and an ability to understand motivations of others and build and maintain key relationships. This includes key leadership at government agencies (US EPA, USDA, state lead agencies, etc.), with the BASF global regulatory team, global supply chain, and the US Crop, Seed Treatment, and P&SS businesses. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . jobs.name: Unified Communication Specialist
jobs.description: Who we are: We are BASF, the largest chemical company in the world, with over 150 years of experience. In Montevideo, we are a Hub made up of more than 1,000 employees who provide services for all BASF companies in the Americas. We offer professional solutions in finance, purchasing, reporting, logistics, technology, human resources, projects, communication, legal matters, environment, safety, health, and quality. Main Objective As part of the service delivery team your main role will be to support in the daily coordination, external operation and governance of Unified Communication services delivered by our external provider, while acting as a liaison between customers, internal stakeholders and the provider to ensure reliable and business-aligned service delivery. Tasks Support the Service Delivery global team in the day-to-day coordination of Unified Communication services delivered by the external provider, ensuring business continuity working closely with all regional service managers. As first line of operational contact you will assist the Service delivery manager to ensure the service delivery performance is aligned with the agreed SLAs, KPIs, and operational expectations. Support the organization and coordination of regular service review meetings with the provider for changes, incidents, requests, problems, etc. Follow up on open actions, service issues, improvement measures, and escalations to ensure timely resolution. Act as a supporting contact for the external UC provider in operational and service-related matters. Help ensure that incidents, service requests, escalations, and changes are properly tracked and followed by the provider. Ensure critical tasks receive the proper level of attention. Assist in reviewing provider performance, identifying trends, and highlighting risks or recurring issues to the Service Delivery Manager. Support the coordination of provider activities with internal teams and business stakeholders. Support in major incidents when required. Serve as a day-to-day liaison between customers / stakeholders and the external provider for service-related topics. Collect and consolidate feedback, requirements, and service concerns from business stakeholders, sites, and internal IT teams. Help to transfer the business needs into structured inputs for the Product Owner and the external provider. Communicate status updates, planned activities, service issues, and follow-up actions to relevant stakeholders. Support coordination with critical business stakeholders (Fire Brigade) Support the tracking of major incidents, recurring issues, problems, and escalations affecting Unified Communication services. Ensure that escalated issues are documented, followed up, and visible to the Service Delivery Manager. Help coordinate communication and follow-up during major service disruptions impacting end users or business operations, establishing contact with service desk, management, and other stakeholders. Support the Problem management process, RCA analysis and improvement actions for repeated service issues. Coordinate, analyze, review, and approval of change requests. Support and work in close contact with Contract value management to identify gaps in the CMDB ensuring the provider applies the needed corrections and permanent remediation to the identified process gaps. Support reporting activities related to service quality, incident trends, provider performance, and user feedback. Assist in identifying opportunities for service improvement, standardization, optimization, cost reductions and better user experience. Support improvement initiatives, rollouts, and service transition activities as required. Ensure governance artefacts and service records are accurate and up to date. Requirements Education: Degree in Computer Science, Informatics, Business Informatics or in a comparable field. Working Experience: 6+ years of experience in Unified Communications technologies and services. 3-5 years of experience in IT service coordination, service delivery, and provider management. 3-5 years of experience working with ITIL service management processes. 3-5 years of experience working with external service providers and managed services. 3-5 years of experience supporting global enterprise environments. 1-3 years agile project experience (nice to have). Language skills: Advanced level in English. Technical skills: Advanced skills in Unified Communications technologies and services. Advanced skills in IT service management and service delivery governance. Advanced skills in ServiceNow and service management tools. Intermediate skills in cloud solutions, virtualization, and monitoring tools. Intermediate skills in networking and telecommunications infrastructure. Intermediate skills in IT security and vulnerability management. Intermediate skills in Agile methodologies and DevOps practices. Soft skills: Advanced stakeholder management and communication skills. Advanced organizational and coordination skills. Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills. Advanced decision-making and prioritization skills. Advanced self-organized working skills with a proactive mindset. Advanced collaboration skills in multicultural and global environments. Advanced continuous learning and growth mindset. What we offer: Living BASF means being part of a culture where the focus is on people and on their professional and personal development. Our diversity, collaboration, and leadership style make the difference. When you join our team, we offer you: A hybrid and flexible working model Access to a learning ecosystem with on ‑ demand resources and tools Private medical insurance for you and your family from day one, as well as life insurance Additional flexible leave days, in addition to your statutory vacation days The possibility of remote work from abroad for up to 6 weeks a year A day off on your birthday A day off for moving Annual bonus (subject to meeting the requirements established by internal policies) Well ‑ being benefits, such as on ‑ site nutrition services and massages BASF considers Law N° 19.691 and Decree N° 73/019 in all its hiring processes. Every qualified candidate who meets the requirements established in the job description will be considered in this hiring process with no distinction. Moreover, BASF is an equal-opportunity employer. We enthusiastically accept our responsibility to make employment decisions without regard to race, age, civil and social status, national origin, disability, sex, gender identity or expressions, or any other candidate or employee´s characteristic or group that is not related to their aptitude and suitability for the position. Our management is committed to following this policy concerning hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, demotion, layoff, termination, recruiting, pay, and other forms of compensation, training, and general treatment during employment.

jobs.name: Lab Digitalization Specialist
jobs.description: Now hiring! Lab Digitalization Specialist Regional Laboratory Digitalization Specialist – Charlotte, NC Come create chemistry with us! The Regional Laboratory Digitalization Specialist enables dispersions and resins product and process development by serving as the regional lead for laboratory digitalization, LIMS user support, instrument integration, lab network hardware and regional automation coordination. The purpose is to improve consistency, accuracy, validity and usability of laboratory data while reducing manual effort, improving user acceptance and accelerating capture, analysis and transfer of experimental research information across regional laboratory sites. As a Lab Digitalization Specialist, you create chemistry by... Provide hands-on support for laboratory test methods, instrument integration and material ingredient management. Support harmonized testing protocols and standard applications testing. Connect laboratory instruments and data streams to EDvantage, LabCup or other approved systems where possible to improve data quality and user acceptance. Support instrument integration, middleware connections, lab networking and data capture. Serve as a regional application and user support contact. Coordinate Beta and Production LIMS updates, gather and communicate user feedback, advocate for desired upgrades, support UAT and build a strong Key User Community. Evaluate hardware standards and emerging user interface approaches. Support troubleshooting of lab networking, instrument integration and middleware connection issues while maintaining focus on reliability, cybersecurity and data integrity. Act as a dynamic digitalization point of contact for scientists, technicians, method owners, automation specialists, IT partners and external support teams. Maintain a wide network of enablers, develop citizen digitalization specialists in the laboratories and build the organizational awareness needed to work effectively across functions, sites and global communities. If you have... Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or a related technical field and 5 years of relevant experience is required. Experience with laboratory integrations, laboratory informatics, scientific data systems or instrument connectivity is preferred. Experience: Must have experience supporting laboratory digitalization systems, LIMS workflows, scientific applications or instrument integration in a laboratory, pilot plant, manufacturing technology or research environment. Experience with lab networking, hardware inventory, middleware, instrument connectivity, data acquisition systems, application user support, change coordination and user acceptance testing is preferred. Experience translating user feedback into requirements, actionable resolutions and practical implementation plans is required. Certification as a network engineer or comparable networking, infrastructure, automation, cybersecurity or systems integration credential is desirable. Relocation assistance may be available for eligible candidates if approved under applicable BASF policy and business requirements. Knowledge/Skills: A working knowledge of laboratory information management systems, scientific data capture workflows, instrument integration, middleware connectivity and digital application support is required. Knowledge of lab inventory management and lab information management systems is preferred. Must be able to communicate effectively with scientists, technicians, IT, automation engineers, method owners and global digitalization teams. Must be able to convert diverse user needs into practical solutions that improve data quality, user acceptance and laboratory productivity. Must demonstrate strong organizational awareness, customer focus, collaboration, problem solving, accuracy, integrity and attention to detail. Must be highly organized, flexible and capable of adapting to priority changes while maintaining reliable execution across multiple sites and topics. jobs.name: Technical Service Specialist - Maryland, DC - Automotive Refinish
jobs.description:
Now hiring! Technical Service Specialist - MD, Washington, DC Maryland ** Field Role: Company Car provided We are looking for a Technical Service Specialist to join our Automotive Refinish team in MD/Washington, DC! New finish, Proven expertise. Bolder ambition. If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact, and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships.  We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. As a Technical Service Specialist, you will work with some of the largest Body Shop and Distribution organizations in the market through paint application process development, correct application of products through teaching & hands on demonstrations and helping the sales team convert new business. You will work closely with the sales team to make sure our distribution partners are properly trained and can effectively sell and position our product offerings against competitive product lines. In addition, you will work with the Business Development and Sales team to gain new business and ensure identified Body Shop KPI’s are achieved.  Travel is expected to be 30-50%. As a Technical Service Specialist – MD and Washington, DC, you will... Be responsible for the technical success of Surventis Coatings products, in MD and DC with overlap support in VA, DE and PA markets. Work on coating technologies including solvent & waterborne base coat/clear coat systems, OEM paint approval process, new product and process development at the field level and affecting positive financial impact through product usage and application training. Train and develop our Body Shop and Distribution customers and make process improvement recommendations that can be highly valuable to end users. Lead new product and customer demonstrations, be proficient in product application, selling and implementing new products, and generate a positive experience with Surventis Coatings-branded products. Participate in the technical aspects of the sales process. Lead customer training to ensure Surventis product proficiencies and product conversions of new Surventis customers. Work with the Surventis team to help meet and exceed new business and financial targets. Add value to our customers through root cause analysis, trouble-shooting technical applications, and color match issues. If you have... HS Diploma (or equivalent) coupled with relevant experience. 7+ years of industry experience as a refinish technician. Strong understanding of competitive products and tools. Proficiency with Surventis Automotive Refinish products and technology tools (a plus). Ability to utilize Microsoft Office tools to lead presentations focused on introducing new products to the market and supporting a better understanding of Surventis coatings-branded products. CRM expertise is a plus. Bilingual English/Spanish is a plus. jobs.name: Representante Técnico(a) de Vendas Sênior - Lucas do Rio Verde/MT
jobs.description: Job Description: Representante Técnico(a) de Vendas Sênior - Lucas do Rio Verde/MT Negócio “Juntos pelo seu legado”. Esse é o compromisso da BASF com o agricultor! Aqui, o campo está em nosso DNA e, por isso, estamos conectados às mais novas tendências do agronegócio mundial, oferecendo um amplo portfólio de produtos e serviços, com foco na rentabilidade e sustentabilidade dos negócios. Área Posição de RTV Senior com base em Lucas do Rio Verde/MT, responsável pela gestão do território em região estratégica do agronegócio, com forte presença em soja, milho e algodão. Será responsável gestão Sell In e Sell Out, expansão de market share, rentabilidade, geração de demanda e conflitos. Também conduzirá planos de negócio no território, pipeline comercial e integração com áreas internas. A função exige relacionamento com canais parceiros estratégicos, visão de negócio, disciplina comercial e excelência na execução para acelerar resultados e fortalecer a parceria regional. Candidato(a) Buscamos profissional com experiência técnico-comercial robusta no agronegócio e vivência em distribuição principalmente com gestão de POG e contas estratégicas. Necessário histórico em negociações relevantes e construção de planos comerciais territorial. Esperamos conhecimento na gestão de conflito, expertises na soja e milho, algodão será considerado um diferencial. É necessário atuação com  canais de distribuição e indicadores de performance, além de experiencia anterior em Crop Protection e Seeds. Formação superior em Engenharia Agronomica ou áreas correlatas é um requisito. Procuramos perfil analítico, influente e organizado, com boa comunicação, capacidade de negociação, foco em resultados, gestão de conflitos e flexibilidade para viagens frequentes. Nossa química valoriza o talento e a diversidade de gênero, raça, idade, nacionalidade, crenças, orientação sexual, identidade e expressão de gênero, deficiências e todas as demais características, diferenças e semelhanças que fazem parte da diversidade humana. jobs.name: Instrument Control & Automation Engineer
jobs.description: Now hiring! Instrument Control & Automation Engineer Geismar, LA / Houston, TX / Wyandotte, MI (Hybrid) We are looking for an Instrument Control & Automation Engineer to join our Global Engineering team in Geismar, LA, Houston, TX or Wyandotte, MI. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. The Global Engineering team leverages our technical know-how and expertise to deliver solutions that enable safe, sustainable, reliable, and cost-competitive production. You will be responsible for supporting new chemical plants, research laboratories, major expansions, debottlenecking efforts, and infrastructure projects. You will also provide technical leadership to I&C engineers and designers, both internal teams and third‑party engineering firms, through all project phases and special initiatives. Your responsibilities will include reviewing design documentation, understanding how system designs interface with manufacturing operations, and identifying opportunities to reduce cost, enhance sustainability, and establish best practices. Participation in cross‑functional teams, internal committees, and ad hoc assignments will be essential to your success in this role. As a Instrument Control & Automation Engineer, you create chemistry by... Applying your expertise in process instrumentation, Safety Instrumented Systems (Triconex/HIMA), Distributed Control Systems (Foxboro, DeltaV, ABB), PLCs, plant automation infrastructure, and industrial networking systems to support site business units, safety initiatives, and capital projects across the North American region. Developing and maintaining instrument documentation, functional design specifications, P&IDs, control strategies, panel layouts, logic diagrams, and related engineering deliverables, while overseeing the design, specification, configuration, implementation, troubleshooting, and consultation support for Instrument & Control systems. Designing, implementing, programming, and commissioning PLCs, Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS), and Burner Management Systems (BMS) while ensuring compliance with BASF Technical Standards, industry standards (ISA 84, IEC 61508/11, NEC, NFPA), and applicable regulatory and design codes. Diagnosing instrumentation and I/O issues within PLCs, remote I/O systems, and Charms I/O, while ensuring reliable communication between DCS, PLCs, analyzers, and field devices. Collaborating with Process Engineers, Operations, and Maintenance teams by leading or supporting SIS training, SIS dossier management, Safety Requirements Specifications (SRS), proof test procedures, and the delivery of reliable and compliant automation solutions throughout the asset lifecycle. Applying local and national safety standards while participating in PHA, HAZOP, Functional Safety Assessments, and SIL studies to support safe and reliable operations. Utilizing reliability tools and methodologies including Equipment Criticality Assessments, Bad Actor Programs, FMEA, RBI, RBM, RCM, and Reliability Roadmaps to improve asset effectiveness, optimize costs, and drive continuous improvement. Providing Instrument & Controls engineering support throughout FEL, detailed design, construction, and commissioning by developing design bases, consolidating I&C manhours, preparing material take-offs, reviewing IFA/IFC packages, configuring HIMA and Triconex systems, and supporting FAT, SAT, commissioning, and startup activities. If you... Hold a Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, or a related engineering discipline with 10+ years of experience in Process Instrumentation or an Engineering Specialist degree with 15+ years of experience. Demonstrate experience with process instrumentation, Safety Instrumented Systems (Triconex/HIMA), Distributed Control Systems (Foxboro, DeltaV, ABB), PLCs, plant automation infrastructure, and industrial networking systems. Have experience developing and maintaining instrument documentation, control strategies, functional design specifications, P&IDs, logic diagrams, and related engineering deliverables, it is preferred. Bring experience designing, programming, implementing, and commissioning PLC, SIS, and Burner Management Systems, it is preferred. Are able to interpretate and adhere to BASF Technical Standards, industry standards (ISA 84 or IEC 61508/11, NEC, NFPA), and applicable regulatory and design codes, as applicable to specific projects and/or site initiatives. Possess strong troubleshooting, collaboration, and communication skills to support Engineering, Operations, and Maintenance teams throughout the asset lifecycle. Have experience supporting capital projects through FEL, detailed engineering, construction, commissioning, FAT, SAT, and startup activities, it is preferred. jobs.description:
Now Hiring! Customer Site Service Representative II Customer Site Service Representative II Location: Casa Grande, AZ If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! As a Customer Site Service Representative, you will support the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The role responds to customer requirements, enforces product quality/performance specification, and supports new product/application development. You will provide on-site expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. S/he will develop and support customer relationships. This role supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis and resolution. The position assures product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. The incumbent will demonstrate technical and professional expertise to enhance the Surventis reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. As a Customer Site Service Representative II, your responsibilities will include... Supporting the daily operation of Surventis products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including defect identification, system maintenance, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a Surventis sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. Acting as a capable leader and mentor to coach and develop team members as well as providing ready support for other facilities and customer locations. Being a subject matter expert in at least one technology of the coating process i.e.. Ecoat, prime, basecoat or clearcoat. Working in collaboration with e-coat tenders to maintain bath quality / parameters. Providing weekly samples to E-coat lab to meet targets. Maintaining material inventory and forecasting. Identifying opportunities for continuous improvement for presentation to management. If you have... High school diploma required, advanced degree preferred. 4-7+ years of automotive coatings or equivalent industry expertise; ability to utilize your advanced knowledge of the painting process and paint applications. Ability to develop and grow customer relationships, focusing on building a positive rapport with customers. Utilizing your inventory control/ERP knowledge you will collect and analyze data and apply results to make material and/or process recommendations. Your strong attention to detail will allow you to continuously inspect and evaluate pre-treatment defects. Your excellent interpersonal skills will help you with interactions with the team and management at our customer facility. Your strong leadership ability will allow you to run a shift on your own with minimal supervision. Prior experience in a manufacturing environment will help you complete instrument updates and calibrations as well as ancillary paint processes such as spot repair and workability trial processes. Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Pay transparency Surventis is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $71,600- $90,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. jobs.name: Chemist II - Automotive Refinish
jobs.description:
Chemist II - Automotive Refinish Southfield, MI If you're someone who inspires others, owns their impact and wants to elevate what they do, you'll fit right in. Until July 1, our business was part of BASF. As part of the current transition, all job listings and the application process are still hosted on the BASF platform and therefore fully presented in BASF branding. Please do not be confused by this: All roles are positions at Surventis, and your application will be submitted to us. At Surventis, we go beyond the surface - in everything we do. We put customers at the center, think one step ahead, and develop solutions that create real value and lasting partnerships. We inspire each other to think further, take ownership of our work, and constantly push to elevate what's possible. If you bring your full self, challenge the obvious answer, and want to grow with a team that means it - this is your place. We are looking for a Chemist II to join our Coatings team in Southfield, MI. In this role, you will be responsible for managing multiple projects’ and communicating deliverables to all key stake holders. The successful candidate must have high level of entrepreneurial drive with ability to think technically, strategically, and analytically while navigating through all functions, levels, regions within Surventis at the customer. This is an excellent opportunity for you to demonstrate your varied skills, including maintaining, contributing to, and leading EHS and 6S standards and continuous improvement. You will also need to be able to lift up to 50 pounds on an occasional basis. What you will do... Formulating paint based on established guidelines in collaboration with global teams. You will work in a lab to make paint samples, evaluate the samples for performance, and identify the adjustments necessary to match standards per customer specifications. Working in a lab to make and test paint samples to internal and customer specifications and evaluating formulas for workability and film performance to ensure they meet customer requirements. Collaborating with global customization technical teams for introduction of new technologies in the North America market Collaborating with Regional Key Accounts and Field service teams to address technical requests / trouble shooting projects related to new or existing products, by managing, initiating, and coordinating projects together with production sites. Driving and supporting the achievement of profitable growth through continuous improvement and executing of cost saving measures of existing products and raw materials. Working in SAP and other computer systems to establish codes, inspections plans, and make formulation changes. If you have... Bachelor’s Degree in a science major with 3 years of experience in Automotive color / paint or Masters with 2 years’ experience or Ph.D. in Polymers or Coatings Development of coating systems for automotive coatings (focus on base coat and primers). Comfortable using SAP. Experience and breadth in coating chemistries and with multiple paint systems and layers. Experience interacting with external customers. Experience working in a dynamic environment with ability to manage changing priorities. Project Management experience. Privacy statement Surventis takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Dann bieten wir Ihnen die Möglichkeit, sich berufsbegleitend zum/zur Chemikant:in weiterzubilden! Während Ihrer Qualifikation arbeiten Sie in der Produktion. Das vierjährige Programm beginnt jeweils am 1. Februar und 1. Juli. Wenn Ihnen Arbeitssicherheit wichtig ist, sind Sie bei uns genau richtig! Werden Sie Teil eines starken und engagierten Produktionsteams, das Ihnen zahlreiche Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten bietet und bei dem Ihre Sicherheit an erster Stelle steht. WAS SIE ERWARTET Sie sind ab Tag 1 ein wichtiger Teil unseres Produktionsteams. Erfahrene Kolleg:innen führen Sie schrittweise an die Aufgaben eines/einer Chemikant:in heran. Dazu gehören das An- und Abfahren, das Bedienen, Überwachen und Reinigen der Anlagen, sowie das Abfüllen von Produkten. Dabei halten Sie alle Sicherheits- und Umweltvorschriften ein. Sie nehmen an BASF-internen Qualifikationsmaßnahmen, sowie an einem IHK-Kurs während der arbeitsfreien Zeit teil. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Sie verfügen über eine abgeschlossene handwerklich‑technische Berufsausbildung oder eine abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung in einem anderen Fachgebiet mit mehrjähriger Berufserfahrung in der Produktion. Sie sind bereit und motiviert, sich in ein neues Berufsbild in der Produktion einzuarbeiten und die Prüfung zum/zur Chemikant:in vor der Industrie- und Handelskammer abzulegen. Sie sind handwerklich geschickt, packen gerne mit an und sind bereit in 12-Stunden-Wechselschicht zu arbeiten. Sie arbeiten gerne im Team. Angehörige von Staaten außerhalb des europäischen Wirtschaftsraums und der Schweiz schicken bitte ihre Aufenthalts-/Arbeitsgenehmigung inkl. zugehöriges Zusatzblatt mit. WAS WIR BIETEN Attraktive Vergütung nach Chemie-Tarifvertrag E02-E03 (je nach Berufserfahrung zwischen 3.270 € und 3.344 € monatliches Brutto-Tarifentgelt) Zuzüglich tarifliche Zulagen und Schichtzulage, sowie jährliche tarifliche Einmalzahlungen, wie Urlaubsgeld, Jahresleistung und Zukunftsbetrag Firmenfinanzierte Pflegezusatzversicherung Bei erfolgreichem IHK-Abschluss, fachlicher und persönlicher Eignung ist die unbefristete Übernahmemöglichkeit in die BASF SE das Ziel Lernangebote für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Weiterentwicklung Profitieren Sie vom Know-how unserer Fachausbilder:innen, einem Umfeld mit modern ausgestatteten Produktionsanlagen Unterstützung bei der Wohnungssuche Fitness- und Gesundheitsstudio, Medical Care Center mit zahlreichen Angeboten für Ihre Gesundheit Deutschlandticket als vergünstigtes Jobticket Netzwerke für die Entwicklung und Umsetzung eigener Ideen SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Antje Pfeifer (Talent Acquisition), antje.pfeifer@basf.com, Tel.: +49 30 2005-58153 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Buchen Sie einen Termin mit Antje Pfeifer: Fragen zur Stellenausschreibung und Rekrutierungsprozess Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF Jobmarkt GmbH ist eine Gruppengesellschaft der BASF und bietet Ihnen eine berufsbegleitende Weiterbildung zum/zur Chemikant:in an. Sie werden während des Programms in einem der Betriebe der BASF SE eingesetzt. Chemie für eine nachhaltige Zukunft – dafür steht BASF. Als Gruppengesellschaft am Standort Ludwigshafen leistet die BASF Jobmarkt GmbH hierzu einen wichtigen Beitrag. Unser Fokus liegt auf Zeitarbeit sowie unserem Ausbildungsverbund. Das Geschäftsfeld Zeitarbeit umfasst die Rekrutierung und Bereitstellung von Personal zur temporären Unterstützung der BASF-Gruppe. Ziel ist es, den befristeten Personalbedarf zu decken sowie Qualifizierungsprogramme anzubieten. So schaffen wir Perspektiven, fördern Flexibilität und stärken den Standort nachhaltig. Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und entwickeln Sie mit uns die Zukunft - in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengerechtigkeit unabhängig von Alter, Herkunft, Geschlecht, sexueller Identität, Behinderung, Religion oder Weltanschauung lebt. jobs.name: Internship / Thesis Automation and Information Technology  (m/f/d)
jobs.description: WELCOME TO BASF The Service Unit Global Engineering Services crafts and engineers a sustainable future with world class technical expertise. Our interdisciplinary, global, committed teams serve the BASF businesses to grow with our customers, products and markets. We add value by delivering competitive execution of investments. On construction sites across the globe, we deliver safe and reliable solutions to build and keep BASF assets operating at top performance. In doing so, we continuously improve and drive digital transformation. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT In the field of industrial communications technology, we support operations with industrial and hazard communication technology. In addition to conventional system technologies, we use innovative technologies for early hazard detection in the process industry. You will support us in the further development and implementation of our solutions. Possible topics include artificial intelligence (AI), PLC programming, computer vision, evaluation of innovative technologies. The focus of your work will be jointly determined depending on your interests and skills. At the Ludwigshafen site, you will work for this purpose in an interdisciplinary and cross-departmental team, in which you will present and discuss your results and implement them in the companies. WHAT YOU OFFER advanced bachelor's or master's degree in a STEM field of study good programming skills (e.g. Python, C/C++, Codesys) advantageous: knowledge in software versioning and MLOps (e.g. Git, DVC), machine learning, computer vision, network technology very good communication in German and English independent familiarization with new topics as well as the ability to actively contribute your ideas to the team we look forward to receiving your application documents, including a current certificate of enrollment. if it is a compulsory internship, please attach your study regulations or a corresponding document certifying the duration of the compulsory internship to your complete application documents WHAT WE OFFER Practical phase - actively apply theory in challenging and varied tasks/projects Teambuddy - contact person for all questions and regular feedback Networks - get to know different perspectives, ways of thinking and fields of work Internship compensation - a recognition of the commitment, more information here: on.basf.com/compensationBASFSE Flexible working hours - depending on the location and area of work HOW TO REACH US Yulia Haars (Talent Acquisition), yulia.haars@basf.com, will be happy to answer your questions for this position You can also reach our recruiting team here First information about our application process can be found here: on.basf.com/applicationprocess . ABOUT US BASF SE, located in Ludwigshafen am Rhein, is the corporate headquarters of the BASF Group and part of the Rhine-Neckar Metropolitan Region. BASF stands for competence in chemistry since 1865 and is represented today in the world's major consumer markets, with a wide range of products. As the largest employer in the region, BASF SE offers multiple career opportunities for students, university graduates and professionals providing also training in over 30 professions. Leisure, sports, culture and a good infrastructure characterize the Rhine-Neckar Metropolitan Region, in which BASF is also involved with numerous activities. Learn more about BASF SE at on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU Diversity is our greatest strength! Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

Bis zum 1. Juli gehörte unser Geschäft zur BASF. Im Rahmen der aktuellen Übergangsphase werden alle Stellenausschreibungen und der Bewerbungsprozess weiterhin auf der BASF-Plattform gehostet und daher vollständig im BASF-Branding angezeigt. Bitte lassen Sie sich davon nicht beirren: Alle Stellen sind Positionen bei Surventis und Ihre Bewerbung wird an uns übermittelt. Bei Surventis stellen wir die Kundinnen und Kunden in den Mittelpunkt, denken einen Schritt voraus und entwickeln Lösungen, die echten Mehrwert und nachhaltige Partnerschaften schaffen - we ge beyond the surface. Wir inspirieren einander, weiterzudenken, Verantwortung für unsere Arbeit zu übernehmen und ständig danach zu streben, das Mögliche zu erweitern. Wenn Sie Ihre ganze Persönlichkeit einbringen, das Offensichtliche hinterfragen und mit einem Team wachsen möchten, das es ernst meint – dann sind Sie hier richtig. WAS SIE ERWARTET Im Rahmen des juristischen Vorbereitungsdienstes bieten wir Ihnen die Möglichkeit, während der Wahlstation in der Rechtsabteilung mitzuarbeiten. Sie bearbeiten Fragestellungen des allgemeinen Wirtschaftsrechts sowie des Wettbewerbs- und Kartellrechts. Sie erstellen Gutachten zu konkreten Rechtsfragen unter Berücksichtigung der betrieblichen Besonderheiten. Ihre Expertise setzen Sie ebenfalls bei der Erstellung von Verträgen und Vereinbarungen ein. Die Mitarbeit im aktuellen Tagesgeschäft bildet eine weitere Facette Ihres spannenden Aufgabenspektrums. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN Rechtswissenschaftliches Studium mit mindestens befriedigendem Ergebnis der ersten juristischen Prüfung bzw. des ersten juristischen Staatsexamens idealerweise erste Praxiserfahrung in den genannten Rechtsgebieten sicherer Umgang mit IT-Standardanwendungen und verhandlungssichere Kenntnisse der deutschen und englischen Sprache Ziel- und ergebnisorientierte Arbeitsweise sowie sehr gute Arbeitsorganisation WAS WIR BIETEN Ein anspruchsvolles Aufgabengebiet mit hoher Eigenverantwortung. Ihre Einarbeitung erfolgt "on the job" in einem engagierten, kompetenten Team. SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Julia Kilias (Talent Acquisition), julia.kilias@basf.com, Tel.:+49 30 2005-59139 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen . ÜBER UNS Mehr über unser Unternehmen erfahren Sie hier . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen. Jedes START IN-Programm wird individuell Ihrer Entwicklung auf die Zielposition angepasst. Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit sich in einem dynamischen Umfeld zu entfalten und gemeinsam mit uns die Zukunft zu gestalten. Werden Sie Teil einer inspirierenden Community, die sich durch Zusammenarbeit und gegenseitige Unterstützung auszeichnet. WAS SIE ERWARTET Während Ihres 24-monatigen Traineeprogramms bereiten Sie sich a uf eine Tätigkeit im Aufgabenfeld elektrotechnische Anlagen, Automatisierung und Prozessleittechnik vor. In den verschiedenen Stationen werden Sie in unterschiedliche Technikteams integriert, lernen dort die Prozesse der BASF kennen und knüpfen wichtige Kontakte zu Experten und Entscheidungsträgern. Sie begeistern sich für neue zukunftsweisende Themen und möchten aktiv die Transformation mitgestalten? Gemeinsam mit uns treiben Sie die professionelle Planung, Montage und Instandhaltung von Änderungen an elektro- und prozessleittechnischen Anlagen sowie entsprechende Investitionsprojekte voran. Sie möchten einen spürbaren Beitrag in der Produktion leisten? Dabei erkunden Sie spannende Themen, wie Automatisierung von chemischen Anlagen und deren funktionale Sicherheit. Weiterhin geben Sie Ihren Input bei der systematischen und technischen Weiterentwicklung des jeweiligen Betriebes. Ein auf Sie zugeschnittener Karriereeinstieg steht auf Ihrer Wunschliste? Dann ist das persönliche Coaching genau das Richtige für Sie. Gemeinsam mit Ihrer Programmmanagerin gestalten Sie individuell Ihre persönliche und fachliche Entwicklung auf die Zielposition. Networking spielt für Sie eine zentrale Rolle in Ihrem beruflichen Alltag? Unsere internationale Trainee Community erleichtert Ihnen den Einstieg und bietet vielfältige Möglichkeiten zum Austausch. Sie sind neugierig geworden? Hier finden Sie einen Einblick in den Alltag unserer Trainees. WAS SIE MITBRINGEN überdurchschnittliche Noten im Ingenieursstudium - erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Masterstudium im Bereich Prozessleittechnik, Elektro-, Regelungs-, oder Automatisierungstechnik mit nicht mehr als zwei Jahren Berufserfahrung. Begeisterung zu verändern - Sie sind ein wichtiges Mitglied des Technikteams im Zusammenspiel mit den Fachstellen am Standort. Sie unterstützen zudem bei der professionellen Planung und Abwicklung von Projekten. Kreativität und Offenheit - Anwendung von unterschiedlichsten, fachlichen und methodischen Ansätzen , die Sie im Laufe des Programms erlenen werden. Innovative Projekte vorantreiben - eigeninitiative Mitgestaltung des digitalen Wandels im operativen Betriebsumfeld sowie Lust etwas zu bewegen und greifbare Ergebnisse zu schaffen. Kommunikationstalent - Offenheit für Networking und ein Gespür für adressatengerechte Ansprache auf Deutsch sowie Englisch (mindestens C1-Level in beiden Sprachen). WAS WIR BIETEN Coaching - persönliches Mentoring, das Ihre persönliche Entwicklung unterstützt Weiterentwicklung - vielfältige Trainings für eine breite fachliche Ausbildung Karriereweg - abgestimmte Trainings zur individuellen Weiterentwicklung Networking - exklusive Community Events, bei denen Sie Ihr Karrierenetzwerk erweitern können Perspektive - unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag ab Tag 1 und beste Karrierechancen SO ERREICHEN SIE UNS Katja Emich (Talent Acquisition), katja.emich@basf.com, Tel.: +49 30 2005-58810 beantwortet gerne Ihre Fragen zu dieser Position. Gerne können Sie sich auch einen Online-Termin buchen. Unser Recruiting-Team erreichen Sie auch hier . Erste Informationen zu unserem Bewerbungsprozess finden Sie hier . Wie geht es nach der Bewerbung weiter? Ein Video mit Informationen zum Bewerbungsprozess gibt es hier . Weitere Informationen zu unseren Traineeprogammen finden Sie hier . ÜBER UNS Die BASF SE mit Sitz in Ludwigshafen am Rhein ist die Konzernzentrale der BASF-Gruppe und Teil des Wirtschaftsballungsraums Metropolregion Rhein Neckar. Das Unternehmen BASF steht für Kompetenz in Chemie seit 1865 und ist heute mit einer breiten Produktpalette in den großen Consumer-Märkten der Welt vertreten. Als größter Arbeitgeber der Region bietet die BASF SE vielfältige Karrierechancen für Studenten, Hochschulabsolventen und Professionals und bildet in über 30 Berufen aus. Freizeit, Sport, Kultur und eine gute Infrastruktur prägen die Metropolregion Rhein Neckar, in der sich auch BASF mit zahlreichen Aktivitäten engagiert. Mehr über die BASF SE erfahren Sie unter: http://on.basf.com/BASFSE_LU . Vielfalt ist unsere größte Stärke! Werden Sie Teil unserer Erfolgsformel und gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft – in einem globalen Team, das Inklusion und Chancengleichheit unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderung oder Weltanschauung lebt. Und übrigens, wir überlassen es unseren Mitarbeitenden, ob und wann sie sich duzen.
